description:
A game for 6 players with 7 mysteries to solve independently.  Two pages present the mystery case and the back page has the solution provided on it.  
Cases include:

     The End of the Wealthy Playboy
     The Extortion Letter
     Romantic Interlude
     The Lunch Room Murder
     Check Double Check
     The Episode of the Two Ranneys
     Merril's Albi

In The Last Looters, a great battle took place. Several clans have teamed up to try to take the treasure of a dragon, unfortunately without success.
You play the role of two companions who have managed (cowardly) to hide during fights and who come to recover as many treasures as possible from gold coins, jewels or the weapons and armor of their dead comrades.
But beware, the dragon is certainly weakened by the conflict, it is nonetheless attentive to what it is taken ...

—description from the designer

Dans The Last Looters, une grande bataille a eu lieu. Plusieurs clans se sont associés pour tenter de s’emparer du trésor d’un Dragon, malheureusement sans succès.
Vous jouez le rôle de deux compagnons ayant réussi (lâchement) à se cacher lors des combats et qui viennent récupérer le plus de trésors possibles parmi les pièces d’or, les joyaux ou encore les armes et armures de leurs défunts camarades.
Mais attention, le Dragon est certes affaibli par le conflit, il n’en reste pas moins attentif à ce qu’on lui prend…

—description from the designer (French)

Designer's note: The images of the game come mainly from free sources, but unfortunately 7 images were created by AI (hoping for real illustrations soon!).

Seven Kingdoms is a fast and deep board game where four powerful factions battle it out for control of the Kingdom. Using the five most influential characters you can bribe, dispense Papal favors, start riots and wars and fight to conquer control of the regions. Be careful not to leave the provinces unguarded or the invaders will take advantage of it. Each player will be able to do only one action from those available and must be particularly careful which one to do because at the end of the turn the characters on the map will perform an automatic passive action that will radically change the balance in the game. It is a game with ZERO luck, only the most skilled will be the winner at the end of the game.

Each game turn has three phases:
Player action phase
Characters Passive action phase
End Turn phase.

Starting with the first player and then clockwise each player can only do ONE action among the available characters.
The action can be Normal or Enhanced, the enhanced action can only be performed if the player who intends to perform it has a Pope’s Favor which must be discarded immediately.
Characters must take the action ONLY after moving
Once a character has been chosen, he can no longer be chosen for that turn, the only exception being the army for whom two actions can be performed, one for the infantry and the other for the chivalry.
after all players have chosen their character and taken the relevant action, all passive actions of the characters are resolved.
finally in the final phase of the turn, the effects of the war are applied, it is checked whether the game is over and finally the invader's move is made.

Character actions (simplified)

The king
Basic action: place two of your own cubes and one of an opponent's
Advanced Action: Place two of your own cubes
Passive action: the player with the majority chooses the first player

The Pope
Basic Action: Place a Papal Influence
Advanced Action: square papal influence and a bishop
Passive action: the player with the majority obtains a papal favor

The Merchant
Basic action: Place a cube in the market
Advanced Action: Place two cubes in the market
Passive action: increases the economic value of a region

The spy
Basic action: Swap two cubes
Advanced Action: swap places of two cubes also in the market or between Governors
Passive action: places an invader cube

The army
Basic action: Converts an invader into its own cube
Advanced Action: Convert two invaders into two of your own cubes
Passive action: fights a war

Tour around the game board and create your very own creature bands. Hunt through flea markets to find instruments and recording equipment. Write and record heartfelt songs in multiple genres. Climb the charts and gain enough clout to save the Natural History Museum! Album cover opens into a game board! includes, game pieces, dice and 150 game cards.

Czech family game building the Babylon tower.
Age 8+ 2-6 players 10 minutes
The rules are easy to translate via Google.
Take turns throwing six dice to collect the resources to build all 8 levels of the tower. You can throw the dice up to 3 times.
The resources are: bamboo, brick, stone, wood, worker, bonus.
3 bonus dice automatically completes a level.
E.g., Level 1 needs 2 x stone, 1 x brick.

We are located in the Villa de Brihuega in the 17th-18th centuries. Each player represents a 'family' that competes to obtain the greatest fame and wealth by performing various tasks during the seasons of the year.

In each round, an event occurs and each player chooses one of the different characters. (Gardener, water bearer, farmer, priest, guard, mayor,...)

On their turn, each player moves their villagers, can buy actions from a market, play them and also buy 'postcards' that represent the places in the Village.

The winner will be whoever gets, at the end of the game, the most victory points for tasks and set collection.

—description from the designer

Moonlight Brewers: Triple Fermentation plunges you into a mysterious monastery where Trappist monks compete fiercely to brew the best beer... under the full moon! In this dark and fantastic world, one special ingredient makes all the difference: the werewolf.

On each full moon, you must hunt these mythical creatures—dangerous yet essential for your recipes. The game blends immersive strategy and resource management in a chaotic yet joyful atmosphere.

Gameplay: Players choose from several actions: gather ingredients, equip themselves to face the night’s dangers, hunt werewolves, or fight other monks to claim the best resources. Each action can be performed simply or with greater effort, depending on the level of risk you are willing to take. But beware—making too much noise attracts the werewolves, who will not hesitate to attack! Drinking beer can influence your dice rolls and actions, but be careful, as drunkenness might hinder your performance…

The game unfolds in turns where players craft strategies, face unexpected events, and take risks to brew the best full-moon beer.

—description from the publisher
The object of the game is to unscramble letters to form proper words or put words in correct order to recreate the headline. A code card is enclosed to check your answers.

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: Soil Enrichment Promo Card
description:
Spring Season Promo 2024 of FryxGames.

Promo X67

Soil Enrichment is an event card (red) with a microbe, plant and event tag
Cost 6MC
Spend 1 microbe from ANY of your cards to gain 5 plants.

In this modern day re-imagine of the classic game Raja Mantri Chor Sipahi, you compete with your friends to become the richest person of Rajasthan, India.

You get 2 secret characters and each turn you can either play the character’s abilities or bluff. But be careful, if others catch your bluff, you must pay the price!

Gameplay
Players take turns in a clockwise order. During each turn, a player selects a single action. Action is considered successful unless challenged or blocked by other players. Players could be telling the truth about their actions or bluffing! If their lie is caught with a challenge then they must pay the price of 3 Golds! 
The game concludes instantly when a player accumulates 20 gold.

Easy 2 Learn
Raja Mantri Chor Sipahi is a super fun, fast-paced game that is simple to learn and easy to play! Only takes 5 minutes to set up and each round lasts about 15-30 minutes.

Play Everywhere
Compact, travel friendly game that can be played at your house, office, parties and even road trips!

Perfect for Gifting 
Family friendly fun game with elegant, high quality material & packaging makes it perfect for gifting.

—description from the publisher

Creatures from all corners of the multiverse must work together to stop the time gods from destroying their world. But there's a catch. Once the time god is defeated, all who remain must turn on each other since the portal home can only fit one. Survive the realms. Collect epic loot to help along the way. Destroy the time gods. And get back home. No matter the cost.

Old-school rules-lite board game featuring the art of Skinner ? An epic combination of loot collection, boss battles and battle royale showdowns.

Trail Markers is a 1-2 player game that task you with finishing your hike before the elements stop you.

Each game you will pick one of four trails, and a set of equipment to help you along the way.

Each round you will roll the dice, pick a die from those rolled, and use its value to either move along the trail to complete your goal or assign it to your camp to gain access to abilities from your chosen equipment.

You need to be careful when picking dice, because if there are any dice of a lower value than what you choose, they become "exhausted." This gives you fewer dice to use in future rounds.

Manage your time, equipment, and dice to finish your hike in the given time, and receive the best possible score!

All gameplay content, all meeples upgraded to miniatures, and all Stretch Goals stored in a single "Chest of Plenty" big box. PLUS get an exclusive Myth Scenario and miniature chosen by the backers (Charon)!

Long Live Backyardia! is an expansion to the mancala meets trick taker, Bug Council of Backyardia.

The expansion includes the following four modules, each one offering a new quirk to spice up the base gameplay.


     The Peacekeeper: Rein in the chaos of Backyardia with the Peacekeeper, a new token that prevents a certain section of the mancala from being manipulated.
     Nectar: Introduce negotiation with Nectar, a resource that can be traded among the players for favors and nets end game bonus points.
     Prosperity & Turmoil: Secretly affect allegiance bonuses with Wealth tokens, which can either grant positive bonus points (Prosperity) or negative penalty points (Turmoil).
     Leaders: Aid your cause by recruiting Leaders, special action cards gained by collecting suit majorities among the tricks you’ve won.


These modules can be mixed and matched in any combination you desire.

—description from the designer

Rubava Hospital is on its feet. Doctors will have to defend their certification. There's even talk of layoffs. All doctors will have to prove their quick judgment and sharp minds! Who will prove to be the best diagnostician?

Modrý kód is a deductive party game in which you take on the role of doctors from the popular Czech TV series. Your task - to correctly diagnose five patients before your colleagues can! This is the only way to make sure that there will be room for you in the hospital.

boardgame: Star Trek: The Next Generation Collectible Dice Game – USS Enterprise NCC-1701-E Booster Set #3
description:
Expansion for the Star Trek: The Next Generation Collectible Dice Game – USS Enterprise NCC-1701-E Starter.
Contains:
2xd8 Repair Damage Control dice
4xd6 Special Sensor dice
1d8 Command Character die
1d8 Crew die
2d6 Crew dice

Each booster set contains 10 new dice to expand your ship and rulebook with rules for multiplayer battles and explaining new dice effects.

You play as foxes in search of a new home, and the way to your destination is treacherous. Avoid the hunters, their dogs and the predators. You'll need to cross tumultuous rivers, dangerous roads, snowy mountains and perilous swamps. And keep an eye on the terrifying wolf!

To win, find the best way and make strategic halts while watching your rivals' progress and managing your health gauge. Cross the map and reach the goal first to win the game.

Párty Alias: Pro děti is a children's version of the popular game Party Alias, in which you find yourself in the role of visitors to the amusement park. Using describing, acting and guessing the pictures on the cards, you have to get through the park and get to the circus as soon as possible.
 Describing the pictures will be varied with some fun activities like jumping or holding your nose. The game plan is dominated by the Ferris wheel, which helps to advance faster along the marked path.

Rodinná trefa is a word game in which one player guesses and the others, as shooters, try to hit as many words as possible that fit the word. They count points for correct answers, and the player who guessed gets points as well. The words are rotated each round and players take turns as the guesser.

A solo roll & sketch map building game.

Roll Corvus dice!

Collect Nimbus!

Build Structures!

Win the Game!

Corvus Nimbus is a roll & sketch game for children & adults.

All you need to enjoy the game is these rules, a pencil, 6 regular dice [d6] and the map page of the game.

In this game your purpose is to roll the dice, collect nimbus, build structures and win!!!

In this game you control Corvus (represented by 6 regular dice or 6 d6- called Corvus dice) and collect Nimbus (the currency of the game) to build structures that bring benefits during the game.
Each round consists of three phases, the rolling phase, the action/building phase and the point phase.
The game is won if you build 9 structures or collect 100 nimbus and it is lost if you lose all your Corvus (dice).

boardgame: ラミーのトリック (Rummy Tricks)
description: Rummy Tricks is a rummy-like must-follow trick-taking game, in which players want to set the best combination of cards winning tricks to score at the end of the game. Each card has a combination of two different numbers, that can be from the same suit or different suits. When you play a card, you can choose which of the two numbers and/or suits will be used, but when the trick ends, players collect cards and also can choose which number they will use to create sequences or groups of the same number. But beware, lone cards are worth negative points, but you can also play cards in your opponents melds. Which is better?

A game of cards to complete to form funny sentences and win the game. The theme is rurality. Unlike other similar games, it can be played with the whole family (no sex jokes).

Played with 9 cards in hand, the funniest phrase wins. A blank sentence is read by a player, the other players play a card to complete the sentence, and the top card of the deck is added (the deck plays). The funniest sentence earns a point (if the deck wins, no point, a rule is added for the next card, and a new sentence is read, the deck plays).

—description from the publisher

Jeu de cartes à completer pour former des phrases drôles et remporter la partie. Le thème porte sur la ruralité. Contrairement à d'autres jeux du même tonneau, il peut être jouer en famille (pas de blague de sexe).

Se joue avec 9 cartes en main, la phrase la plus drôle l'emporte. Une phrase à trou est lue par un joueur, les autres joueurs posent une carte pour compléter, la carte du dessus de la pioche est ajoutée (la pioche joue). La phrase la plus drôle rapporte un point. (si la pioche gagne, pas de point, une règle s'ajoute pour la prochaine carte, et il lis une nouvelle phrase, la pioche joue)

—description from the publisher (French)

The year is 2182. You and your fellow Legendary Stratos League stunt drivers have been abducted by the alien Twisted foOk. A rescue helicopter is headed to the portal home, but only one of you can escape. Will it be you?

Escape Deathrace 2182 is a dynamic car-battling adventure through treacherous, alien terrain. You’ll have to drive and gun your way to victory through boss battles and against your previous allies, all in order to make it back home.

Create unique tracks from a deck of 20 Road Tiles.

Move through obstacles like Fire Zones & Hazards.

Seek & destroy 3 sub bosses before taking on the Final Boss.

Engage against your fellow racers in Drive-Bys & Battle-It-Outs.

Harness the power of 4 types of Weapon Drops to succeed.

Carefully utilize your Power Cards to gain the upper hand.

Watch out for special Event Cards that will help or hinder your progress.

Choose from 6 Characters with different Special Abilities and their own pre-painted cars.

This tile-placing, racecar-themed dungeon crawl board game is for 1 to 4 players and takes about 10 minutes per player to play.

boardgame: ジャックたちと豆の木レース (Jack and the Beanstalk Race)
description:
Jack and the Beanstalk Race is a tile placement competitive game for 2 to 5 players based on the fairy tale "Jack and the Beanstalk."

Place beanstalk cards so that your starting beanstalk connects to the clouds and race to find the hidden treasures, which are worth a variable number of victory points.

The player with the most points at the end of the second round wins.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Jack and the Beanstalk

description:
Xenolanguage is a game about first contact with alien life, messy human relationships, and what happens when they mix together.

Play centers on the "Platform": a custom channeling board of mysterious origin. Use a planchette-like lens to channel over alien symbols as you receive and interpret messages. Players will progressively discover meanings for the alien symbols on the board, grapple with what they learn, and experience how it changes them.

The base game of Xenolanguage includes a custom channeling board with thirty alien symbols, planchette-like lens, story deck, and digital soundscapes.

Hidden role game where you must discover who is a Villain or a Fairy and depending on that, get the gems or avoid it. Generate your best strategy to be the winner!
Discover if you belong to good or evil with the role cards, being a Villain get the power gems with the help of the path of thorns and magic potions, but it will not be so easy since the fairies will try to avoid it.
Includes: 1 buildable castle with base, point coins, 1 start card, 49 path of thorns cards, 24 action cards, 9 role assignment cards (fairies and villains), 3 plastic gems and 1 instructions.

Juego de roles ocultos en donde deberás descubrir quien es Villano o Hada y en funsión a eso consigue las gemas o evitalo. ¡Genera tu mejor estrategía para ser el vencedor!
Descubre si perteneces al bien o al mal con las tarjetas de roles, siendo Villano consigue las gemas de poder con la ayuda del camino de espinas y pócimas mágicas, pero no será tan fácil ya que las hadas tratarán de evitarlo.
Incluye: 1 castillo armable con base, monedas de puntos, 1 carta de inicio, 49 cartas de camino de espinas, 24 cartas de acción, 9 cartas de asignación de roles (hadas y villanos), 3 gemas de plástico y 1 instructivo.
This is the Kickstarter promo pack for Angel Fury, containing three archangels, and three archdemons, with their unique cards. It also features a couple of level 3 cards.

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Onslaught – Tendrils of the Lichen Lich Starter Set
description:
Dungeons & Dragons: Onslaught is a competitive skirmish game in which each player controls an adventuring party from one of the powerful factions of the Forgotten Realms. Parties delve into dungeons, battle rival adventurers, and confront fearsome monsters on a quest for treasure and glory.

Two new factions join Onslaught with the Tendrils of the Lichen Lich Starter Set: the Lord's Alliance and the Emerald Enclave. Battle for control over the city and forests, which is represented by a new tile system that allows each scenario's map to be unique. Over the course of six scenarios, you'll fight each other as well as more and more powerful monsters, leading up to a final confrontation with the horrifying lichen lich...

This starter set is playable on its own, or it can be combined with other Dungeons & Dragons: Onslaught starter sets or expansions.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
12 Suit is a word that refers to the marks of clubs, hearts, diamonds, and spades drawn on cards such as playing cards.

How to play
1. Each player is dealt 6 instruction cards and 6 answer cards. Place the remaining cards in separate piles in the center of the table.
2. Turn over the instruction card at the top of the deck and play in turn clockwise.
3. When it's your turn, play the answer card as specified by the previous player's instruction card.
If you are able to play an answer card, continue by selecting one of the instruction cards for the next player from your hand.

     If you can't answer, you will get a pass and draw one card from both decks.


4. The first player to play their hand is the winner.

The world is infested with zombies, knocking humans down a notch on the food ladder. In this merciless dice game, you'll have to fight to survive or to feed yourself if by some misfortune you find yourself infected.

Rolling Dead is a game of dice combinations, speed and self-control. At the start of the game, everyone chooses their colour and starts as a human with the associated dice. A virus die is rolled and determines who will be the first infected in the game. If you become infected, you turn into a zombie and swap your dice.

When the first person is infected, the dice combinations determine who attacks and who defends. The attacking zombie or human must quickly touch (or slap) the hand of the target. If a human is hit, they become a zombie. If a zombie is hit, they are eliminated. But there's no room for error; if a player removes their hand for no reason, hit the wrong target or attack when not supposed to, they go from human to zombie and from zombie to permanent death.

The last human standing is declared the winner. Zombies can also win by being the last ones standing, in which case they decide whether they share the victory or go hungry again and become cannibals to the last survivor.

An adventure game combined with a paragraph game. Full of puzzles, RPG elements, exploration, managing too small equipment, fighting for life and a plot that is followed out of curiosity rather than duty. Try to escape this nightmare as part of the campaign or play it as a regular board game outside the campaign mode.

boardgame: Power Rangers: Heroes of the Grid – RPM Ranger Pack
description: The RPM Rangers are here! This expansion adds the RPM team to Heroes of the Grid! Add the 5 RPM rangers (Red, Blue, Yellow, Green, Black) to your game. Includes 5 Zords and 2 Megazords.
family: Game: Power Rangers: Heroes of the Grid

boardgame: Power Rangers: Heroes of the Grid – Arsenal Pack
description:
Introducing a brand-new type of card: Arsenal Cards! This expansion incorporated some of the larger ranger equipment such as Battilzers.

Also included are four new characters, Void Knight, Izzy Garcia, Trini Kwan, and Tyler Navarro, each with a figure, character card, Zord card, combat deck, along with an array of the new Arsenal cards.
family: Game: Power Rangers: Heroes of the Grid

boardgame: Movie Maker: The Print & Play, Roll & Write Game
description:
The objective of this game is to make your Studio the most successful movie-making studio of the lot. 
You have 20 years in the game to create and release the most highly rated movies, which will generate income and experience for your studio. 
You will then be able to use that income and experience to improve the quality of your directors, actors, screenplays, and ultimately your movies! 
Don't forget awards ceremonies, which happen every 5 years! Awards provide you with much needed rewards!

Game Boards & Rule Book may be found in the file section.

The Sexpert game tests your knowledge of sex in a playful way. The player who knows the answer to the most questions can proudly use the title Sexpert! The questions are very varied and you may be surprised how many things you don't know yet. On the cards, you will find juicy details that will give you more interesting details from the erotic and sexual environment. The game will be both entertaining and educational. There are still a lot of preconceptions about sex. With this game you will remove all taboos!

The game contains different types of questions. Some you will answer yourself, others are for all players. Even if you blush, know that you will have a great time and learn something.

This is a historical based 2-4 player pirate game. You are a 1700's pirate attacking merchant ships moving across the Atlantic. Beware of the Navy and maybe even a sea monster that will easily destroy your vessel.

Attack merchants, each other, the navy, upgrade your ship and claim victory by having the most treasure at the end of the game. Where you will be named King of the Sea.

2 Extra Gin Cards that were available with the Kickstarter campaign. These cards are not included in the regular package.

The Extra Gin Cards will include CraftGin 9148 #0101 from Benizakura Distillery, a craft gin distillery located in Hokkaido, Japan and Lazy Master Silky Citrus from Numazu Distillery, a craft gin distillery.
publishers: JUGAME STUDIO (ジュゲムスタジオ)

boardgame: War of the Ring: The Card Game – Against the Shadow: Promo Cards
description:
Set of two promo cards for the expansion. Given out at SPIEL '23, and with pre-orders for the expansion. Later available as part of the Game Boy Geek Season 12 (2024) Kickstarter.


     Gothmog
     Whip of Many Thongs


Same named and looking promo cards are part of the main game’s promos, but these versions are used in the solo mode. Both cards come with the eye symbol of the expansion.

boardgame: Heroscape: The Grove at Laur's Edge
description:
Heroscape: The Grove at Laurs Edge Terrain Pack Expansion includes 3 Laur jungle trees, 6 Laur jungle underbrush pieces, and a setup guide to maximize your use of these striking new terrain features.

This set is fully compatible with all Heroscape Master Sets and Expansions!

Look at yourself with different eyes. Exploring the human body is endless fun. Did you know that the human body produces enough heat in half an hour to bring about 4 litres of water to the boil? That our heart pumps up to 10,000 litres of blood a day? That we need 17 kinds of muscles to smile, but 43 to frown?
 The human body is such a fascinating machine that it still offers us hundreds of mysteries and curiosities. The board game Mysteries of the Body reveals some of them through nearly 900 questions.
 The board game Body Secrets is based on the popular Czech TV show of the same name.

Welcome to TEPfaktor! A lot of tricky tasks await you, which will test not only your creativity and ingenuity, but also your knowledge and logic. Can you be the first to solve the mystery behind the clues hidden in the TEPfactor rooms?
 TEPfajtor is a team party game where players apply a variety of skills. Challenges the game puts in front of players include memory tests, pantomime, drawing or finding answers to tricky questions. Join forces and be the first to solve the mystery!

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Ravnica: Clue Edition
description:
Wizards of the Coast joined with Cluedo to celebrate their 75th anniversary. Reimagined versions of the infamous cast are incorporated in a brand new "Whodunnit"-experience, combining the evidence-gathering sleuth gameplay of Cluedo with Magic: The Gathering's strategic depth and the Ravnica setting.

This is not a reskin of the boardgame, but it is a new way of playing Magic designed by R&D that weaves the core concept of Clue with the playing of Magic.

If you love playing trivia games anytime, anywhere, then the Trivial Pursuit Mini Pack 2000s game is for you! This quick-playing game features trivia questions from the years 2000 to 2010. Since it includes cards only, you can take it to parties, get-togethers, on vacation, and more. On your turn, another player will ask you a question; get it right to keep the card. The first person to earn 5 cards wins! Answer fun trivia questions like "In 2001, scientists discovered that an octopus has eight additional brains, one for each arm. True or False?" and "Which country gave up the escudo and adopted the euro as its official currency on January 2, 2002?" Here's your chance to prove your prowess of 2000s pop culture!

—description from the publisher

This quick-playing game features trivia questions from the years 2010 to 2020. Since it includes cards only, you can take it to parties, get-togethers, on vacation, and more. On your turn, another player will ask you a question; get it right to keep the card. The first person to earn 5 cards wins! Answer fun trivia questions like "What hybrid pastry, fried like a donut and flaky like a croissant, became a delicious craze in 2013?" and "What Internet craze of the early 2010s involved participants lying face down in unusual locations?"

—description from the publisher

Bingo game with matching cursive words to everyday and domestic objects, stimulating initial learning with a simple and well-known playful dynamic.

Gioco di tombola con abbinamento delle parole corsive agli oggetti di uso quotidiano e domestico, stimolando il primo apprendimento con una dinamica ludica semplice e ben nota.

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 100 wins the game.

—user summary

Hollow is a capricious game of heightened strategy for two players, red and blue. The objective of Hollow is to have more points at the end. You receive one point for each piece that remains on the board and something called the button exchanges hands throughout the game and awards the player who has it at the end of the game half a point. The game ends when no more moves of any kind remain.

Definitions: Hollow: A hollow is any set of cells partitioned by stones of either or both colors and possibly board edges.

Button: The button is a piece off the board worth half a point that the last player to create a hollow always receives or retains.

On a turn a player may either place a stone of his color, such that it does not alter the number of hollows or he may create a new hollow by removing the maximal group of stones, regardless of who they belong to, and leaving just the stones outlining the new region, often in conjunction with board edges.

Special thanks to Luis Bolaños Mures for creating a much simpler way of preventing hollows from being filled in with some classic efficient technical writing. My original plan was that players would always play in the uniquely largest region and that placements could never create hollows, but he instead suggested the current wording that seems so obvious now. My original approach would have led to a much less interesting game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Morgan The Savior
description:
She traveled in a land of vivid auroras, hanging in successive curtains of heavy red and voltaic blue. She drifted through them, her aura washed in strobing power until she passed into the land beyond, her destination. At the end of the aurora there was a meadow of negative darkness, she passed like a shadow, darkness on darkness until she reached the cairn of shining stones. Their light, flickering and sputtering, absorbed by the treasure within. She plucked the dream, waiting in the center. It shone as nothing for as far as she could see, punching through the shifting sheets of light like a beam of void. Clutching the dream, she was born, at the moment of extinguishing that marked the passing of the lantern year. A new lantern ignites in the settlement, an ominous violet aura glowing within.

—description from the publisher

This is a single miniature that can be used in the game, together with four cards.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Vignette of Death: Killennium Butcher
description:
Vignettes of Death are a great diversion from the intensity of Monster campaigns or an easy way to introduce new players, dipping their toes into the fathomless ocean of Kingdom Death: Monster.

Play as four wish seekers pursuing the Killennium Butcher to steal a wish granted by the frozen star. The awakened monster will not give up its boon easily, attacking the survivors with an intensity of rage that freezes the very air. The new larger-sized vignette rules reveal branching outcomes of your showdown determined by who's left standing when the snow clears.

Using ready-to-play gear grids, survivor vignette cards, and limited components from your Monster core game makes the vignette a quick way to dive into a brand new story.

—description from the publisher

Tvoje tvář má známý hlas is a team party game inspired by the TV Nova show of the same name.

Players use drawing, explaining, singing, humming, and other activities to suggest to their teammates a performer or concepts from the world of music. The more cards a player guesses, the closer the team gets to victory. The Your Face Has a Famous Voice game also includes special transformation cards where players must guess not only the show's protagonist, but also who they are currently portraying in the photo.

Three routes lead to the destination. The fastest is the red one, the green route is longer and more difficult and very careful drivers rock along the longest, yellow route.
The game is played with a die. Everyone moves their car forward by the number rolled. If a driver reaches exactly one intersection with his throw, his car is positioned in the direction of the arrow and he must continue driving on the new route as soon as he throws again. If a driver comes to a stop on a brown curve, his car must return to the previous O-curve on the same route. Drivers who accurately reach the first brown corner must return to the start. Intersections and curves that can be passed with one throw are ignored. The winner is whoever drives their car to the finish line first.

—user summary

The original Two Faced game. A hilarious card game of acting challenges. But this ain't charades! No hand gestures, no body movements, just a lot of silly faces.

Perfect for a party night or choose a quick game for a fun filled half hour.
Gloups the cat wants to catch sea animals in groups of 2, 3 or 4. Turn over your cards and be the first to spot the identical animals! A game of observation and speed!

Spellcraft is a competitive team-based game, where teams of mages split into two sides, each fighting for their throne while also crafting powerful spells through elemental management (by gathering elements to craft spells from the available menu).


     Each player controls a single Hero.
     All players move and perform actions on the same map, whilst having their personal boards containing foraged elements, status and crafted spells.
     Both teams have a 5/6/7 HP Throne they defend whilst attacking the opposing one.
     Each team has 5/6/7 Life Points that are shared across the team.
     A Flag is positioned at the center of the map, granting significant buffs (and potential victory) to the team that manages to capture it.
     Players forage 4 available elements (Air, Fire, Water, Earth) from Rune Spawn tiles.
     Players use a combination of 3 elements to craft Spells.
     There are 20 possible combinations (and thus spells) in total.
     Each spell has its own Spell Type.
     There are 5 Spell Types: Air, Fire, Water, Earth and Neutral (no dominant element).
     Crafting 2, 3 or 4 spells of the same type grants the Hero appropriate Elemental Blessings (passive perks).
     This gives players an option between choosing to commit to one spell type or more flexibility.
     A player can craft the same spell twice, enabling him access to Enhanced version.
     This gives players an option between choosing to focus on stronger spells or more flexibility.
     Players use spells to battle each other and support their teammates.


—description from the designer

Work together with your fellow dinosaurs to escape from the attacking carnivorous dinosaurs! A huge volcanic eruption, a giant meteorite collision, and a plunge into an ice age that will bring about extinction... Only you who can protect Triceratops will become the ruler of this world!! The world is truly in the age of dinosaurs!!!

Sgt. Drake Alexander
Drake Alexander has always been a man of principle. On Earth, he fought to defend the rights and liberties of his fellow humans from the encroaching Reich. During a fateful battle, moments before his certain death, the Valkyrie Jandar intervened. Drake’s noble heart now beats in the strange and distant land of Valhalla.

Jandar promised Drake that if he fought against the vile Utgar, he would one day be returned to his homeland. As have so many before him, Drake agreed; and though time and countless battles have since passed, Drake is no closer to seeing Jandar’s promise fulfilled. Through it all, one of his closest friends remains the Kyrie Raelin. She may appear now to have joined the enemy, but Drake knows better, and he remains at the ready to aid her however he can.

Sgt. Drake Alexander is available as a pre-order promo for Wave 1 of Heroscape in 2024. Obtainable with a purchase of Master Set, Battle Box, or both from the Renegade Webstore. Or available at participating local game stores. Sgt. Drake Alexander will be available for purchase on the Renegade webstore after the promotional period ends.

Shiori
Einar is one of Valhalla’s first Valkyrie Generals. He is known above all for his stoic enforcement of discipline. His army is more coordinated than Vydar’s Soulborgs, more loyal than Jandar’s stalwart knights, and more efficient than Aquilla’s wizards.

Shiori, by contrast, is none of these things. Einar’s summons plucked her from her home in Earth’s feudal Japan when she was barely in her teens, and she has been belligerently operating outside of his maxims ever since. Now a warrior grown, Shiori has spent her time in the mystical Valhalla honing her craft, and can blend Earth-style martial arts seamlessly with magic.

Einar may not approve of Shiori’s methods, but after her success in the Marro swamp, he knows his fate and hers are intertwined. And Shiori, though she could care less about Einar himself, will not fail those friends she does hold dear in this rising age of destruction.

Shiori is available as an organized play promo, obtained from Launch Tournaments or Gen Con 2024 events! Shiori will be available for purchase on the Renegade webstore after the promotions she is associated with have passed.

Melee Grande 2 Player Fighting Game in which players take control over a team of Luchadores and Luchadoras to pit them against the opposing team in tactically challenging matches until only one fighter remains.

Players simultaneously place cubes from their fighter's pool to define the quality of their attack this turn. The attacks are then resolved whereby each combination of cubes has its advantages as well as hard counters. Assessing your opponent's capabilities and anticipating their moves is what will give you the edge.

The game features two game modes. The duel mode is focused on mastering the battle interaction and intended as an introduction to the game, whereas the tournament mode adds more depth to the gameplay by giving choices outside of the battle more weight, like substitutions and support cards.

20 different Luchadores and 20 different support cards will make every match feel different and guarantee high replayability.

—description from the publisher

ananas is a strategic board game mixed with the simplicity of a social game. Each player starts with a series of tiles and competes in exciting and fun challenges in order to connect their tiles to the board and win.

Named after the most common word for pineapple in over 43 languages, ananas' theme is centered around pizza, where each tile is a topping. Successfully complete as many of the challenges as you can to win the game. Losing a challenge means your topping (tile) gets flipped to its burnt side.

Challenges are divided into three categories: mild, medium, and spicy, which correspond to the size of tile placed down (ie large 3x3 tiles are spicy, 1x3,2x3 are medium, and 1x1, 1x2 are mild).

A coin is used to determine where the tile gets placed. Flip or bounce the coin as close as possible to any tiles in play. As long as your chosen tile can reach the coin, it can be connected in.

First player to place all their tiles ends the game! A points scoring system will determine the winner based on who has the most tiles placed (and not burnt).

And that's it.

—description from the designer

This promo card for Axe-A-Lot-L is a once-per-game card you can choose to shuffle into the main deck. The card text reads:

"Flip the top card of the deck. If it is an Axe-A-Little, immediately target all opponents with an Axe-A-Little. Otherwise, all players draw one card."

This shiny foil card was available as an exclusive during the game's Kickstarter campaign.

Toot Toot is a game about hoplite battles of Ancient Greece.

Players arrange their available units, assign them orders (move, attack, hold and like) and then begin the game.

Assigned orders can be changed throughout the game but it is meant to be not guaranteed. Each order mode comes with certain restrictions that limit what units can (or even have) to do.
The feature of this design: it tries to represent the influence of noise on the battlefield. Our units create volumes of noise that make communication more difficult and eventually even impossible. Even issuing orders create new levels of volume.

This means that direct orders, issued by leaders leading from the front (and risking Leader Casualty game effects), can be of relevance.

Four armies are present in the game: Athenians, Thebans, Spartans and Persians.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
An expansion for Dutch Resistance: Orange Shall Overcome!

This expansion adds 2 extra scenarios, extra cards for the different decks in the game including one extra character card for each character, and many modules to adjust the game to your liking. These modules include different upgrades, for example, stronger Morale Action tokens, a higher maximum Alibi or Morale, as well as ways to make the game tougher to win such as the officer that can be added to any scenario and other more dangerous Occupiers on the roads.

—description from the publisher

An army of Alices from alternate dimensions defeated the Void and then fought a war over who would rebuild Wonderland. They live the carefree but disruptive lives of heroes, killing monsters for experience points and opening treasure chests in private homes and castles. The Queen of Hearts has recruited you to reorganize the population of Wonderland and divide the Alices evenly among the different territories.

In Alicematic 畢竟世界 (hikkyō sekai / In the End, The World), you take cards from a shared area to your own territories (or column), like a multiplayer game of Freecell. The player may decide whether each territory is ascending or descending with the second card placed. Cards you don't want to play or are unable to play are collected are discarded, creating a penalty at the end of the game.

Alice cards act as nuisances in the game, but they are also the only way to score points. Each Alice is unique and features a special power that may be used for one turn after the card is placed. Territories may only have one Alice, and territories with an Alice may not be added to or reorganized. The territory will score at the end of the game based on how many cards match that Alice's preference. Players must carefully choose when to add each Alice, or they may be left with territories that don't score at all when the game ends.

boardgame: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Flying Dutchman's Treasure Hunt
description:
The wind is perfect, the tide is right. Let's hunt for treasure.

Roll the dice and move around the board, searching for treasure map clues to get to the X in the center of the board. Remember, the X marks the spot, and that's where the treasure is buried! But watch out! Your fellow scallywag crew mates may betray you and leave you shanghaied by playing cards against you. So, what are you waiting for, lads? Dig up some treasure and maybe you'll be lucky enough to win a few gold doubloons! Or will you be stuck with plastic!?

Kill the King is a quick playing card game of opportunity, timing, deception and tactics. Players each have three cards face down on the table. Players take turns playing a card for its attack power or its ability.  The winner of the game is the person who kills the king, or the person who has the king when all other cards have been killed.

In Root Bound, living plants expand across the board in a struggle for territory. With each advance, future options dwindle, and careless growth can leave a plant tangled, cut off, and withered.

Root Bound won the BoardGameGeek Abstract Games Forum’s “Best Combinatorial 2-Player Game of 2024”.

As an abstract strategy game, Root Bound requires only a hexagonal board and pieces in two colors.

Root Bound draws inspiration from the game of Go, Mark Steere’s Take, Michael Amundsen’s Cavity, and Michael Amundsen and Luis Bolaños Mures’s Lifeline.

—description from the designer

A slightly asymmetric board games where 4 teams (French, Spanish, and British Navy ships as well some pirates) are trying to to travel the board to reach the treasure island and retrieve the treasure. After retrieving the treasure the team must travel back to the starting point again. If Any two teams meet at any cell on the board they can fight. Each team has 50 main soldiers and other soldiers they can collect while travelling the seas. If any team  decreases beyond the 10 soldiers threshold they cannot crew a ship anymore. There are elements of sneaks and ladders, Risk, strategy and just luck.

—description from the designer

Play as Turing, du Châtelet, Ramanujan or Pythagoras in this printable braniac battle game, much like Bananagrams for math.

Each player starts with 7 random numbers or symbols in their "unproven" pile. There are no turns; anyone can move at any time.

To attack, arrange tiles from your unsolved pile into a true statement in the "proof" row, e.g. “1 = 1” or “28 / 4 = 7”. Then read the statement aloud and dump all those tiles on any other player’s unproven pile.

The first player to have no tiles in front of them wins.

—description from the publisher
A simple set rules for re-fighting the naval battles of the Napoleonic Wars with miniatures ships on a square grid.

Solace is a co-operative science-fiction game set in a world of immortals in which players are agents of a futuristic SWAT called FTO. Players have to accomplish increasingly dangerous missions, which are playable independently or as part of a campaign. In each mission, as a team you raid a building or a palace, with the goal of saving hostages, killing a target, retrieving data, or pursuing other objectives as stated on the mission cards.

Each turn, you have to plan your actions in a co-operative mode to move between palace floors, move inside rooms of the same floor, hack electric elements, fight enemies, and so on. Once you've planned the actions, you execute them and see the feedback of the palace: enemy movement, enemy attacks, new room cards being triggered, etc.

Roll the dice, plant the fruits, and cleverly manage the numbers to get the sudoku-style bonus!

Game Play
Players take turns to be the active player and roll the five dice. In clockwise order, each player chooses one of the five dice and writes down the number on an empty square of the matching color on their field board.

Special rules In 2-player games:
The sequence of choosing dice is as follows: Player A plants one die, followed by Player B planting two dice.
Player A then selects the fourth die, and there will be one remaining die.

After all players have chosen one die, there will be 1 or 2 dice remaining.


     In a 2- or 4-player game, all players simultaneously plant this remaining die.
     In a 3-player game, each player selects one of the two remaining dice to plant.


The game ends when all rounds are completed. Each player scores points from the following:


     Points from the four colored fields. ("Fruit Field Score" table at the top of the board)
     Points from the "Sunshine Blessings" areas. (Total value in the area)
     Points from the "Carefully Planted Seed." (15 points per column/row with 6 distinct numbers)


The player with the most points wins!

Join us for a musical party! 
This is a music-themed party card game suitable for 2-6 players which is perfect for families to play together and also great for teachers to engage students in a fun experience! There are five distinct interactive games at the party for you to enjoy.


     In the lively "Note Rush," see who can quickly play all their cards first by following the music notes with lightning-fast reactions. 
     In the cooperative "Mic Challenge," work together with your teammate to complete challenges before the opposing team. 
     Then, there's the music rhythm test of "Sing Along with the Beat," where accurately singing the right beats and keeping up with the speed is no easy task. 
     Test your melody memory in the "Goldfish Brain Showdown," where you might just forget the lyrics while singing. 
     Finally, there's the puzzle-based scoring game of "Pinyin Five by Five," where you'll strategize to score as high as possible within the square formed by 25 cards!

This expansion adds the possibility to earn extra points at the end of the game.
This is an 8 card expansion.

boardgame: Jokkmokk: The Winter Market – Straw Goat Promo Set
description:
An additional set of special cards for Jokkmokk: The Winter Market. This set of cards can be used in combination with the existing sets in the game.

Straw Goat card rules:
When claimed, take turns doing the following actions, starting with you and proceeding clockwise: Move one of your cards from in front of you to the middle of the table. The card must be from a set that no one has added yet. If unable to do so, instead add the top card of the deck.

Then, starting with you and going clockwise, each player chooses and gains a card from the middle of the table.

Reminder: Any cards gained do not activate their effects.

The cards in this set score the points in the top left corner as all cards do. No special scoring.

boardgame: Vesuvius (fan expansion for Age of Steam)
description:
Age of Steam - Vesuvius is a free print-and-play expansion for Age of Steam, designed for 2 or 3 players.

You are engineers around the years 60-80 AD, building the Roman road network around the Bay of Naples, and also assisting building the great aqueducts throughout the region to link towns and cities to the fresh water supply from the aquifers at the top of the various mountain ranges. Manage your Denarii effectively to impress the emperor.

But beware: At some point during the game, Vesuvius may erupt, destroying some of the nearby roads and cities!

Normal Age of Steam rules apply, with the following main changes:

     Blue cubes represent Water from Aquifers, and can be delivered anywhere (except other aquifers), but reward money, not income.
     Turn Order Pass is replaced by Aquarius' Bonus, which allows a 3rd delivery, which must be water.
     Vesuvius erupts if it ever runs out of water on the hex, "destroying" a number of tiles in the wind's direction.


—description from the designer
A 7 cards bonus set for "Claim Kingdoms (Royal Edition)".
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Little Mermaid

description:
Murder Mystery Motel Nobody
One summer day in Florida, there was everything and yet nothing.

Motel Nobody is a motel with no guests.
No one can come and no one knows.
Five people gather in such a lodging.
Some people have curiosity, some have malice, and some have dreams.

There is nothing at Motel Nobody.
Still, they had no choice but to head there.
For them, Florida was the best and worst street in the world.

Like reading a mystery novel, players aim to uncover the truth of the case based on various clues.

Additionally, the criminal is also played by the player, and the player who is the criminal lies or acts so that no one knows about his or her crime.

Full of rain, radical politics, cultural diversity, & questionable streetwear, Keep it PNW is your guide to the upper left.

This card pack includes 300 prompt & answer cards based on Pacific Northwest experiences & culture. Each round, the designated judge reads a prompt card, & the remaining players choose the best answer card to fit the prompt. The winner of the round, as chosen by the judge, receives a point. Whoever reaches 10 points first wins.

May the best hippie win.

—description from the designer

A card game for Seattleites, by Seattleites.

Want to put your Seattle experiences to the test? College in this city comes with some special memories. Challenge your friends to an unfiltered card game and remember those sweet, sweet campus days.

Enjoy game night with the same great, authentic Seattle college experiences, but with a post-grad perspective. Down the Ave cards are based on student life in Seattle’s U District.

Each deck comes with a pack of 255 Purple Answer Cards and 45 White Question Cards. 3 Question Cards are gold foiled and are worth double the points. Included in every pack, is also a Rule Sheet with instructions on how to play Down the Ave, along with terms that give more detail about the Seattle experience.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Onslaught – Expansion: Sellswords 2 – Gold and Glory
description:
Bolster your Dungeons & Dragons: Onslaught games with exciting new characters for hire! Four new mercenaries apply their skills and offer up new options, abilities and tactics when assembling your party, and can join with any faction! New characters include a Wizard, a Sorcerer, a Barbarian, and Onslaught's first Artificer!

This expansion also unlocks the Sellswords as a playable faction on their own! Typically, when you assemble your party from one of Onslaught's other factions, you can also include any one Sellswords character. Now, if you have this and another Sellswords expansion with different characters, you can build an all-sellswords 5-character party!

—description from the publisher

On a wooden board 48 holes form a circle. Depending on the number of players, a starting position is chosen with colored sticks in the holes. Each player has 4 sticks in each of his 2 colors. On your turn, move one of your sticks to an adjacent empty hole or jump over one stick (no restriction) or several adjacent sticks (but not of the same color as the jumping stick). The player who exchanges all of his sticks of one color with the sticks of the other color wins.
The game comes with a small box containing the sticks and separately the board and the rules.

boardgame: Catan: The Helpers – Leader of the Flock
description:
New character card to be added to the Helper expansion. 
Jasmin is helper number 13. During set up, add the card to the deck of helpers, and prepare the deck the usual way.
Jasmin advantage is: 
Between your turns, when any amount of sheep is traded between players or the supply, place one wool from the supply on this card. This card holds a maximum of 2 wool cards. On your turn, you may activate this card to collect all held cards.

Promo card was made available as a prize at CATAN official tournaments.

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons: Onslaught – Scenario Kit: Grasp of the Mind Flayer
description:
You have received a letter of unknown provenance, offering a hefty reward for clearing a pack of kobolds from a local dungeon. But who is this mysterious benefactor—and can they be trusted?

Dungeons & Dragons Onslaught: Scenario Kit – Grasp of the Mind Flayer features six new scenarios that can be played individually or as an interlinked story adding value and replayability to your collection of D&D Onslaught and D&D Miniatures!

It also features new monster cards, adding iconic enemies like Mind Flayers, Ropers and more! You can use minis from your collection or recommended products, and the pack also includes tokens to represent them if you don’t have the miniatures at hand.

This pack adds even more to your skirmish experience, with new legendary items, swamp items, terrain tokens, and the new Terror deck!

—description from the publisher

Welcome, brave adventurer, to the Shadow Dimension, where the Skeleton King rules supreme. As the God of all Concepts and Ideas, the Skeleton King can grow quite board. Ironic that the power behind all ideas is fresh out of ideas for ways to spend his time. So like many powerful figures throughout history, he simply enjoys a clash between heroes.
​
In Shifting Shadows 8 mighty heroes, well seven mighty heroes and a stray accountant named Dave are called to fight in a mighty battle for the entertainment of the Skeleton King. Players take on the role of these heroes from different multiverses and enter the battle royal. The last person standing is the victor.

Shifting Shadows is a deck builder game where players use an in game currency called power to buy more cards to deal damage, gain power, and use magical effects. Players also have a hero power, action and bonus action, so every round offers some way to improve. This fast paused game has over 300 cards that can be added to you deck in the fight before the skeleton king.

Win and gain eternal glory, a lifetime supply of fictional fantasy Applebees, and a Boon from the Skeleton King. But beware, the consequences of losing are dire, as the Mighty Skeleton King will turn your hero into his little Skeleton Servant. Will you become a legend in your realm or be transfigured into a tiny little guy?

—description from the publisher

To celebrate the summer ahead and to honor an old temple soon to be demolished, the game of Hyakumonogatari Kaidankai (A Gathering of One Hundred Supernatural Tales) will be held. Danjo, the village's figurehead, puts out the invitation. Six men and women from all walks of life gathered together in the old temple. Danjo tilts his head at this strange group. “Huh, I thought I called on five people… or did I invite six by mistake?” Danjo felt uncomfortable, like small bones were scraping against each other in his throat. He suddenly recalled urgent business that required his immediate attention, so he excused himself. He ran out of the temple gates as fast as he could. Thus, a hundred stories will be told by the remaining six people. It's time to begin.

In 乏月百物語 (Bougetsu Hyakumonogatari/100 Tales Under the New Moon), Each player becomes one of the six storytellers who have come to the old temple to join in the telling of 100 stories. The more ghost stories they hear that are to their liking, the deeper and deeper the character becomes lost in the story, and the more Ghost Story Points they will be rewarded. If, on the other hand, a ghost story is not to the storyteller's liking, the player will pull their hair out and drop out of the game.

As the number of players gradually decreases, the last remaining player becomes the Ghost in the Ghost Story and loses the game. However, this ghostly player invokes a curse when they are revealed, and any player killed by the curse also loses the game.

Summer of 1985, hardline members of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union who were opposed to the new reform program, take control of the country from Soviet President and General Secretary Mikhail Gorbachev.

The new leaders, faced with the economic and social crisis unleashed, decide to invade Western Europe, and for this they begin to prepare all the economic, diplomatic and military resources of the Warsaw Pact. The new political bureau has to choose between starting a war immediately or waiting to accumulate resources and troops in greater numbers, hoping that the NATO countries do not become stronger in the same way.

1987 focuses on a hypothetical but possible third worldwide large-scale military conflict. Players control land, air, and naval forces of the various sides engaged in the conflict. The scenarios provided allow players to examine multiple aspects of how a World War 3 could develop. NATO and WP are challenged to win quickly and decisively, as both forces and munitions downgraded rapidly in the face of modern combat operations.

The game is divided in a PreWar phase and a War Phase. In the PreWar phase players consume resource points to improve their military forces or their diplomatic or technological positions. During the War phase players are given a hand of cards to play out seven segments of each turn. The deck of cards forces players to constantly make decisions as they need to do a little bit of everything (move units, reinforce the armies, build resources points...) but they can only do one thing at a time. How and when players play the cards will decide to a large degree the outcome of the war.

—description from the publisher

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 100 wins the game.

The game was published as a campaign tool for the German federal election in 1972.

—user summary

Teech for Two is a climbing/shedding game for exactly two players. It was designed by Larry Levy to meet the need for a two player climbing game when there were none readily available.

The goal of the game is for a player to shed out their entire hand before the other player.

The gameplay is standard climbing play. One player leads with a combination. The other player must follow and play a higher version of the exact same combination.

Teech for Two was inspired by David Parlett's "The Bum Game", which was Parlett's preferred way of playing Daihinmin.

What makes Teech for Two original and exciting is the way that it introduces wilds that are always dealt to each player every hand. With wilds available like this, many more hands are playable and it's always an exciting puzzle to figure out how to best play out a hand.

The dark and gritty action of Warhammer 40,000: Darktide slips from the screen to the tabletop in a new card game by Cubicle 7 – publishers of tabletop RPG Imperium Maledictum and more. Darktide: The Card Game is a fast and deadly cooperative caper for one to four players, battling hordes of Chaos-warped monsters and traitorous Guardsmen across six missions decreed by their merciless Inquisitor.

The set includes over 260 cards, six player boards, and all the rules and tokens you need to play out missions packed with nerve-wracking decisions – after all, you’re merely Rejects, and the mission must succeed at any cost. If you’re up for a shorter experience, you can also tackle the terrors of Hive Tertium in solo and single mission game modes.

—description from the publisher

Expansion 1 add a new crew and five new modules to include in the game in any combination. The Suffragettes crew allows for five players to compete in the race. With seasonal changes come weather changes.  The post office may ask you to deliver valuable mail to your next destination.  Driving through so many nations will bring up diplomatic issues.  Patronage will bankroll your efforts.  And paddocks will add new scenarios to experience through vehicle customization.

boardgame: Starmada: The Universal Game of Starship Combat
description:
The Core Rulebook celebrates STARMADA’s 30th Anniversary with a complete structural overhaul: every rule is now integrated into a single, logical flow — no more flipping between pages to find that elusive option.

Those who have been playing the Unity Edition for the past seven years will feel right at home; while some updates have been made, care has been taken to ensure existing ships can operate as they have before. To emphasize this point, the "Unity" tag has been removed entirely: going forward, the game is simply STARMADA.

Promo Card for 30th birthday of '6 Nimmt'

The Jumping Cow doesn’t have a value of its own. When you place a card in the row with the Jumping Cow, use the card to the left of the Jumping Cow as your reference.

When you place your card in the Jumping Cow’s row, it jumps to one of the other three rows – to the one that has the lowest value as its last (rightmost) card.


The Jumping Cow may not have a value, but it counts towards the row’s card limit. A row counts as full if it has four number cards and the Jumping Cow in it.

If you take the row with the Jumping Cow in it, it jumps. First, take the number cards (but not the Cow) as usual, put them in your bull pile, and make the card you played the first card of the row. Then, the Cow jumps to another row as described above.

boardgame: SAS: Rogue Regiment – KS extras set
description:
This set includes a 36 card event deck, and 3 punch boards of tokens including new vehicles and other tokens AND the new operative: Henri. These items were unlocked as part of the 2023 Kickstarter campaign and are also included in the Black Box edition.

—description from the publisher

In Gnome's Garden, you will become a gnome farmer that was given a task to feed whole gnome village. You will grow your plants, but you can not place some of them next to some others. As a gnome, you must follow some rules to make your veggies have the most taste. Besides that, some of your neighbours have very defined tasted and like some foods but dislike others so you have to plan accordingly.

Gnome's Garden is a Roll and Write game designed for 1 to 4 players. On the game board, you have various parcels on your gardening area, each working differently. You will gain points when you meet the parcel requirements. You will use various tools that will help you with your gardening, allowing you to mitigate dice results.

—description from the publisher

Parkour competitions, which are characterized by the use of urban environments as obstacle courses, require participants to complete complex courses by running, climbing, jumping, and other techniques to overcome obstacles.

To take part in the competition, you will need 10d6 dice and a copy of the game.

Play as an urban traceur and engage in epic stunts with your friends or in solo mode! You face 80 challenges in 100 pages and have to add up all the points scored at the end. Each challenge consists of three parts: the name of the parkour trick, instructions, and an example of how to perform the trick.

—description from the designer

Tromi is an abstract flip & write for 1 to 4 where players fill a grid with trominoes based on their own viewpoint of a shared card. Each turn a card is drawn which assigns each player one of four symbols (the symbol closest to them). The card also shows four trominoes (shapes made up of 3 squares) in each of the four symbols. Each player, looking at the card from their own point of view, must draw their symbol's tromino along with at least one other tromino to their player sheet, without rotating or separating the trominoes. When a player cannot place their trominoes, the other players finish their turn and the game ends. Players score points for the longest string of trominoes in each symbol, and lose points for the largest empty area on their sheet. Player are also aided by three 1-time actions they can take once a game. These are: Rotate their chosen trominoes, Ignore their assigned tromino, and Play one tromino only.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
HAVOC is a tactical card battler set in the perilous world of Port Terminus. In this game, you will compete against other players in one-on-one or two-on-two turn-based matches. Both players will deploy powerful combatants and utilize surreal alien technologies to gain the upper hand.

The game is a battle of both resources and position, featuring a dynamic board controlled by Reactors which, upon activation, grant the ability to play devastating Biome cards. Embrace strategy and maximize the advantages of the unique 6x6 card placement and streamlined combat-movement systems, providing players with an extraordinary degree of freedom.

Players construct their decks and battle for control of the field of play.

Core set is for 1v1 play, Nyxwalker Set includes 1v1 and 2v2 play.

—description from the publisher

A quick, fully self-contained card battler game.
The game comes with 20 "Thing Cards", which are drafted out to each player until each player has 10. Each has an attack and defense stat, as well as an effect.
The players then shuffle their decks and set their life to 20.
On their turn, up to 3 cards on their 3 spaces. Then, choose opponent spaces to attack. If the opposing center space is empty, their life is directly attacked. First to reduce the opponent's life to 0 wins.

From the creators of Ticket to Ride, a fun game where kids can learn about Countries, Capitals and Landmarks as they travel across Europe! Players use Country and Transportation Tiles to chart a course across Europe. The first player to complete a 10 trip journey, where each trip connects to the next, is the winner!

In Save Yogino we should help the alien Yogino to find the spheres of his device and recall his ship. There are two modes of play: cooperative or competitive. A pinch of memory will help us finding the spheres in the woods which will come out on the dice from turn to turn. However, Agent M will try to prevent us from activating the device by chasing us relentlessly, it will be a race against time, will we be able to save Yogino?

In save Yogino dovremo aiutare l’alieno Yogino a trovare le sfere del suo dispositivo e richiamare la sua nave. Ci sono due modalità di gioco cooperativo o competitivo. Un pizzico di memoria ci aiuterà a ritrovare le sfere nel bosco che di turno in turno usciranno sui dadi, L’agente M però cercherà di impedirci di attivare il dispositivo inseguendoci senza sosta, sarà una corsa contro il tempo, riusciremo a salvare Yogino?

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons Chainmail: The Ghostwind Campaign
description:
The Might of Stratis Lies within Your Grasp. Legend and rumor have driven a handful of warlords to the Ghostwind Plateau. There, they will battle tirelessly to gain the powers and abilities they need to seize Bonebreak, the first weapon ever wielded by the God of War. The material in this book includes:

A complete campaign system that allows warlords to gain levels and win magic items while battling through an epic story.
Access to 3rd-level spells, including classic D&D spells, such as fireball and searing light.
Fourteen new scenarios that offer fresh challenges to battle-tested players.

You must choose your allies and devise your strategies well. Only a warlord who commands a truly formidable warband will survive Ghostwind and take the first step on the path to godhood.

To use this accessory, you also need the official Chainmail miniatures game rulebook, the Blood and Darkness guidebook, and the Fire & Ice guidebook.

—description from the back of the box

There were seven main books for the Chainmail Miniatures Game:

Chainmail Miniatures Game sourcebooks
Product #    Title    Year
88339    Starter Set: Rulebook & Model Description Booklet    2001
88000    Core Rulebook    2002
88010    Blood and Darkness - Set 2 Guidebook    2002
88580    Fire and Ice - Set 3 Guidebook    2002
88960    Shadow of the Drow - Set 4 Guidebook    2003
17861    The Ghostwind Campaign    2002
17620    Dragons' Flight    cancelled

At the very end of the product line, the Core Rulebook (2002) was released for digital purchase, for a brief period—not to be confused with the Starter Set: Rulebook. It included all the newer rules and clarifications made in previous books. It was released in September 2002 after the line had been cancelled in August.

As the god of war died, his blood fell from the sky and burned through the earth into the lost caverns beneath. Bold warbands willing to brave the perils of this ancient place have found the remains of a mighty civilization.

And something even greater. The blood of Stratis still burns hot in the deep earth. Those strong enough to claim it fight with his fury on their battlefield.

Every faction has come here to claim this lost power, bolstered by new forces. The Crazed Minotaur Cultist, the Ogre Delver, the Pulverizer, the Stonechild and more, all fight to possess that which was lost.

If you are to succeed in the unending darkness, you must perfect new tactics. This guidebook will begin your training. Master it, and be victorious.
Time to throw down.
Underground.

—description from the back of the book

LIFTOFF! 2.0 is the definitive, epic space-race to the moon board game, which updates the successful (1989) Liftoff!  The Race to the Moon board game and its computer game, Buzz Aldrin's Race Into Space (1994). LIFTOFF! 2.0 is packed with additional content based on actual and proposed technologies that make this the most highly researched and complete simulation of man’s journey into space. Relive the Apollo 11 landing on the Moon!

Up to 4 players compete to be the first nation to land a man on the moon, and successfully return them home again.

During the activity turn all players receive their current income, an event card which may help or hinder, and can buy new hardware (rockets / satellites / capsules etc), try and develop the reliability of systems they already have (by buying dice and adding the total to the current safety), hire astronauts, and secretly plan future missions.

The launch phase sees players carrying out their missions in turn (as being first to achieve certain mission goals such as 'first man in space' is rewarded). The relevant hardware must be purchased, including a rocket powerful enough to lift the weight of the other items involved. The mission is carried out by following a series of steps, and rolling percentiles against the safety rating of specific items each turn - e.g. liftoff roll against rocket, orbital burn roll against space capsule, space walk roll against space suit etc.
If a player fails a safety roll, the advanced game contains several possible failure outcomes (requiring one or more further rolls) ranging from a bulb failure to a catastrophic explosion, which will reset the safety factor of your equipment!

Once missions are completed, players’ incomes are adjusted depending on successes / failures, and the next year begins.

Great Power Lies Amid the Bones of Dragons. Ages ago, great legions of dragons battled in the skies above the Plain of Edora. Their skeletal remains cover the ground now known as Scalebane. Brave warbands have converged on the draconic graveyard, seeking powerful magic and formidable allies. The material in this book includes:

New recruits, such as the Gallowsgaunt, Ice Paraelemental, and Half-Dragon Mage.
Two new terrain cards - the dragon claw and the dragon skull.
Rules for using the Set 3 models and statistics for all the one from Set 2.

If you're to survive the day in Scalebane, you must adapt your warband. This guidebook will give you the tools you need. Master their use, and you will emerge victorious.

—description from the back of the book

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons Chainmail: Shadow of the Drow
description:
Deep beneath the surface of the Sundered Empire, warbands clash upon magical battlefields. As the exiled House of Kilsek enters the fray, every faction seeks out new allies, abilities, strategies, and spells.

The material in this book includes:

New recruits, such as the Drow Wizard, Werebear Trooper, Zombie Minotaur, and Drider Trooper.
New scenarios, such as the Cave of Pain, as well as new special abilities and spells, including Veil of Shadows, Displacement, and hold person.
Rules for using set 4 Models and statistics for all models from Set 3.

If you're to survive the day, your warband must bolster its forces and devise new strategies. This guidebook will give you the tools you need. Master their use and you will emerge victorious.

—description from the back of the book

In Mage Knight: The Apocalypse Dragon, the first major expansion for Mage Knight since 2015, you'll meet a new playable hero: Coral, who wants revenge on the Apocalypse Cult. You'll also find new enemies to fight, including the four horsemen and the fearsome Apocalypse Dragon. You'll explore new tiles with new locations and challenges. This all comes together in a story-driven campaign mode featuring new scenarios and more.

—description from the publisher

Elements is a spell-slinging card game for 2-8 players. Players can either win by reaching 20 gems or by being the last one standing. Players start the game with 20 life and 0 gems and cast spells of the four Element types.

Fire spells deal damage, water spells restore health, gem spells craft gems, and electric spells counter other spells. Spells have a power and a target. The power is the size of the spell's effect and the target is who is affected.

Spells of the same Element type can be combined to produce a spell that has the power of the combined spells and one of the targets of the original spells (chosen by the caster). A spell can be overpowered by a more powerful spell of the same Element type, which also counters the less powerful spell.

Game play uses a simple draft system: players are dealt 4 cards and keep 2 and pass 2. Players take turns (clockwise) casting one spell or passing the turn without casting. Rounds end when every player passes without playing a spell. Between rounds, players can purchase a Greatspell for 8 gems, which is a powerful spell that can only be purchased once per game (per player).

—description from the designer
