description:
The Destroyer's Box is a Kickstarter exclusive storage box that comes with the Jörmungand, Surt, and Nidhogg expansions. 
The box also has room for the Ymir expansion, while that expansion has to be purchased separately as it is not included.

Jörmungand - The Snake of Midgard.
Prophesied, like his brother Fenrir and his sister Hel, as the deliverer of the worst misfortunes to come, Odin, hoping to be rid of the serpent threw him into the seas of Midgard.
A exercise that had little success since Jörmungand continued to grow, so much so that it ended up encircling the world of the Humans.
Unleashed, the Midgard Serpent is wurming his way towards the battlefield!

Surt - The Lord of the Flames.
Guardian of the ardent world of Muspellheim, one of the oldest life forms in existence, probably present before the birth of the Gods themselves.
Now that the Ragnarök has come, he will blaze through Midgard towards Asgard with his Sons of Muspell, incinerating everything in his path!

Nidhogg - The Corpse Eater.
Dwelling between the roots of the World Tree, this strange creature spends his time attacking the bark and draining the sap of Yggdrasil, devouring Oathbreakers' corpses in the snake walled-fortress of Naströnd and waiting for the end of time—when he will carry the dead on his wings.
But, of course, the end of time has arrived, and Nidhögg intends to enjoy himself as the worlds collapse.

Includes:
2 Titan & 1 Monster Miniatures
2 Titan & 1 Monster Dashboards
3 3D Tidal Wave Tokens
2 3D Burning Tokens
15 Activation Cards
6 Adventure Mode Scenarios

This storage box will hold and arrange all your cards, dashboards and other game accessories for Mythic Battles: Ragnarök and its expansions.

Content (from the Base Game):
2 Double-sided Boards
45 Dashboards
39 Tokens
15 Runes
10 Dice
20 Stat Clips
40 Stat Clips Backs
35 Art of War Cards
15 Rune Cards
32 Futhark Cards
211 Activation Cards
45 Recruitment Cards
11 Troop Cards
8 Jarl Cards
6 Polemarch Cards
14 Divider Cards

At dawn, under the hot desert sand, a few lizards line up their offspring before they hatch. The lizards then engage in a tough competition to survive...

J'emme is a strategic game for the whole family!

The principle is simple, be the first to align your gems before having them swallowed and try to swallow those of your opponents to prevent them from triggering the end of the game. Betrayals and alliances will be there.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Similar strategic gameplay to 5-in-a-row (gomoku), but with major differences that make the game greatly addictive and replayable unlimited number of times!

    Multiplayer (up to 6 players, each playing with his color and competing with others)
    Multiple winning conditions (depending on the number of players):
        First to reach a line of 6
        First to reach X lines of 5 (X depends on number of players)
        First to reach X lines of 4 (X depends on number of players)
        First to reach X swallowed opponent's pieces (X depends on number of players)
    


—user summary

Dès l'aube, sous le sable chaud du désert, quelques lézards alignent leurs progénitures avant qu'ils n'éclosent. Les lézardeaux s'engagent alors dans une rude compétition pour survivre...

J'emme est un jeu stratégique pour toute la famille !

Le principe est simple, soyez le premier à aligner vos gemmes avant de vous les faire gober et tentez de gober ceux de vos adversaires pour les empêcher de déclencher la fin de partie. Trahisons et alliances seront au rendez-vous.

—description from the publisher (French)

boardgame: SAS: Rogue Regiment – They Who Dare
description:
An expansion for SAS Rogue Regiment that adds the SOE agent, Nancy and a whole new way to play the game as a result. The set includes a new event deck, support SAS operatives, aircraft and AAC guns. Games can also increase from 9 to 12 boards with the addition of 3 new double sided tiles.

This is a Kickstarter Exclusive expansion that also featured in the Black Box edition of SAS Rogue Regiment.

Journalists were on the frontlines during WW2, and this mini expansion is a homage to all the men and women who risked their lives to bring the news to the public. Without daredevils like Robert Cappa, Ernie Pyle and Martha Gellhorn, our knowledge of this conflict would be much, much poorer.
 In addition to offering exciting original content, the cards included in this pack will allow players who don't have access to D-Day Dice: Overlord to fully enjoy the campaigns presented in To Do or Die.

Soul-biting is a one-of-a-kind journey. It is an engaging adventure, in which your choices will decide the destiny and the shape of the story. It is a mix of mazes to explore, games to solve, and key decisions to take in a mysterious dream world.

To play, you need a pencil and three six-sided dice.
By exploring the maps in the book, you can find bonuses that increase your Life Points and Skill Points, necessary to influence the results of dice rolls.
The maps and routes are interspersed with roll and write minigames, puzzles or crossroads routes.
When you encounter an enemy, roll both dice and compare the results to determine the winner of the duel: whoever gets the highest score wins. If you have special abilities, you can manipulate the dice results to your advantage. By defeating enemies, you also gain new abilities that you gradually unlock on your player card.
As you progress and find resources on your path, you unlock new parts of the main story until you complete it and discover the ending.

—description from the designer

Specifically designed for the Festival des Jeux Cannes, this promo card adds another tree species to Forest Shuffle (Forêt Mixte in France):

The palm tree (palmier in French) allows you to play a bird for free and adds one point for any bird in this players forest.

The high value of this card is symbolized with a golden tree icon for this card.

Be the first to wake all your sloths in this fun performance game.

Rise and shine! Be the first to make enough noise to get all of your sloths out of bed in this fun performance game for toddlers.

Children take turns to pick cards - they could be miaowing like a cat, barking like a dog and even making the noise of a police car or a trumpet to wake up their sloths!

If they turn over a 'Shh!' card every players sloth remains asleep. There's lots of fun to be had as other players can join in making the sound too.

Sleepy Sloths encourages sound recognition, turn taking and even being quiet! Once the game is complete the adorable sloth characters are a great way to encourage conversation from little learners.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Necromolds: Monster Battles – Monster Pack 5: Kracklings & Magmass
description:
Mold and squish new terrifying monster miniatures out of Spell Clay by adding this set of evil spellbooks to your Necromolds board game collection!


     Hail missiles of molten lava upon your enemies and erupt the top of Magmass to power its fierce close-quarters attack!
     Deploy claws and hooks to fight off swarms of smaller enemies using Kracklings—a crustacean curiosity of cruel creation!

boardgame: Necromolds: Monster Battles – Monster Pack 6: Gorgoden & Tormurack
description:
Mold and squish new terrifying monster miniatures out of Spell Clay by adding this set of evil spellbooks to your Necromolds board game collection!


     Transform smashed monsters into gems to empower your golem army using the unique reclamation ability of Gorgoden!
     Keep your friends and enemies close by snaring them in the barged chains of Tormurack—a living dungeon of no escape!

boardgame: Necromolds: Call to Arms Expansion Set
description: Take ultimate control over your Necromold armies by becoming one of six legendary necrocasters—each with the power to unleash devastating abilities through their chosen champion! Equip your golems with powerful soul-haunted weapons of war and leave your enemy's monsters wounded in battle. The player with the last champion standing wins the game!

Welcome to the 20th Annual Street Food Fair! Every year Food Truck vendors from all across the state arrive ready to sell delicious delights -- but none of them know where to park their vehicles. Several of the Food Trucks have asked for your help in finding the best parking spaces. But beware! There are a lot of Food Trucks to remember, and some are hidden behind other trucks. Even worse, your opponent is trying to find the best spaces for their group of Food Trucks and just might frustrate your plans.

In The Great Food Truck Assembly, each player uses their Meeples to move among the Food Truck cards arrayed in a 3 x 3 grid . This is called the Parking Lot.  Using the specials of the Food Trucks, strategy, and a good memory, the players will move Food Trucks to match the locations depicted on their Parking Receipt cards. The winner is the first player to complete all of their Parking Receipt cards.

FEATURES:

UNIQUE AND STRATEGIC MEEPLE MOVEMENTS

Players can move their Meeples in 8+ different ways. Each move and, more importantly, the timing of the move is crucial to your next move and opponent’s move.

NO DOWN TIME AND HIGH REPLAYABILITY

Players interact with each move whether they made the move or it was their opponent’s move.  The game is consistently changing after each move.

LOTS OF FUN IN A SMALL BOX

The Great Food Truck Assembly is very portable and requires very little playing space. With only 26 components fitting in a mint tin box, you could play this on a cutting board in the back of a food truck!

—description from the publisher

Objective:
get most money within agreed time
- acting by turns -

a classical Monopoly, taking place in Vienna, Austria with all the well known activities like buying, trading..
typically 3-6 players, including banker 7

There are some differences to Monopoly or DKT:
There are no train stations.
A group of lots may consist of up to 4 lots.
You may already build houses if you own at least two lots.

The Story
An unknown Pharaoh, having built a pyramid ordered a ritual to celebrate its creation. 
Unbeknownst to him, one of his many conspirators desecrated the ritual, angering the gods, who cursed the pyramid. 
After escaping the 4 vengeful Inhabitants (his former servants), the Pharaoh ordered the immediate burial of the pyramid, entombing the 4 Inhabitants.
It’s now the 1920’s and at the height of Tutmania; Professor Abiana Lawanda has discovered the pyramid. Excited and unaware, she has failed to notice other interested parties have been observing her movements. 
Seeing her entering the pyramid, they follow her in, oblivious that the path behind them has closed. They are all Entombed in the center of the pyramid. They must now survive until the pyramid exits open and be the first to escape.

"Who Do I play As?"
You control one of the 4 Explorers (or their corresponding Lost Soul) AND and Inhabitant. Use both characters to gain a strategical advantage or to slowly trap a fellow Explorer. Be warned, the Pyramid is only wide enough for 1 person and you cannot pass through anyone.

How To Play
Each round is split into 4 phases, Explorers, Lost Souls, Inhabitants, and next round preparation.
Explorers - Each alive Explorer may move up to 3 squares.
Lost Souls - (Explorers who have met an unfortunate end) - Reveal a Lost Soul Movement card. Move that number of spaces.
Inhabitants - Reveal a Who and a Where card. The Who card tells you which Inhabitants are moving and in what order. The where indicates the number of spaces.
Prep - If appropriate move the turn tracker cube down one square and pass the 1st turn card clockwise. In a 3 player game move the spare inhabitant clockwise.

The Lost Souls and Humanity
If an Inhabitant (or Lost Soul) lands on the same square as an Explorer they become a Lost Soul. If a Lost Soul lands on the same square as an Explorer, they steal that Explorers humanity, thus rebecoming an Explorer.

The Walls Move
Landing on a Wall Movement Space allows you to rotate up to 2 pieces of wall 90 degrees, so long as an area isn't fully blocked off (i.e. inaccessible), or both of it's point of origin move (cant move to the space opposite).

What Makes Entombed Unique  
- Entombed allows you to play as both an Explorer / Lost Soul and an Inhabitant, rather than one or the other, allowing for greater depth of strategical play.

- Wall Movement Tiles constantly change the layout during the game giving players a tactical dilemma; defend yourself (or others); attack to eliminate the competition; or a combination of both.

- Inhabitants and Lost Souls are immortal, therefore it’s a survival experience as you are constantly being chased. Having only 1 piece of health also places greater emphasis on survival.

- In survival games you tend to be eliminated once you die. In Entombed, Lost Souls can regain their humanity by stealing an Explorers soul. The game is not over until either no Explorers remain or one Explorer has escaped.

The 1 Player Experience
Entombed can also be played as a 1 player game. There are several modes available which can make Entombed feel like more of a puzzle game, especially if you are playing the mode where you have to keep all 4 Explorers alive.

The Customisable Game
Entombed is extremely customisable. Although 15 turns are recommended before the exits open, the board allows for anything up to 30 turns, allowing game lengths to be shortened or extended depending on how you feel.
Also, as the walls are moveable, the start layout is completely at your discretion. A total of 9 maps have been included within the rulebook to get you started.
The Wall Movements, changeable game length and adaptable starting layout keeps Entombed feeling fresh.

—description from the designer

"Château Aventure" is a cooperative game in which you will experience extraordinary adventures in various worlds. It uses Jared A. Sorensen's Parsely system to mimic the play of a text-adventure computer game and features several of his original adventures.

This new release proposes 6 new scenarios :

     Les Vengeurs Masqués
     Île Aventure
     Robot Aventure
     Super World
     Princess Aventure
     Frigo Go Go

boardgame: The Cauldron: Promo Pack #4 (fan expansion for Sentinels of the Multiverse)
description:
Promo Pack #4 from the unofficial fan expansion The Cauldron, intended for use with the Sentinels of the Multiverse card game by Greater Than Games, contains alternate character cards for Cauldron hero and villain decks.

These decks — which come from The Cauldron: Base Set, The Cauldron: Experimental, and The Cauldron: Adrift — are available separately and required to play.

The following cards are included in this promo pack:


     1609 Drift
     1789 Drift
     Test Subject Halberd-26 Drift
     Dragon Ranger Gargoyle
     Infiltrator Gargoyle
     Captain Gyrosaur
     Last of the Forgotten Order Necro
     Expedition Oblask Pyre
     Test Subject Harbinger-1 Quicksilver
     Area-51 Starlight
     The Stranger in the Corn
     Creed of the Sniper Tango One
     Oni Titan
     Tomb of Thieves Vanish
     2199 Tiamat

boardgame: The Cauldron: Unexpected (fan expansion for Sentinels of the Multiverse)
description:
The Unexpected set for the unofficial fan expansion The Cauldron, intended for use with the Sentinels of the Multiverse card game by Greater Than Games, includes new environments but no new heroes or villains.

The following decks are included in this set:

Environments:

     Dungeons of Terror
     Oblask Crater

Itamae San is a tabletop memory-style game for kids and grown ups featuring colorful, minimalistic illustrations, iconic shapes and patterns, and easy-to-learn gameplay.
In Itamae San you play as a sushi chef (itamae) creating beautiful sushi orders for your customers. On your turn you try to turn over only the ingredients needed for any face up sushi order on the table. If you do it without making a mistake, you get to take the Sushi Order.
Every sushi order you collect is worth a certain amount of money. Earn the most money by the end of the night to become the most revered chef behind the board.
Ingredient tiles never leave the table, and are used for multiple Sushi Orders, creating a completely unique take on the classic memory game genre.

—description from the designer

Akhedena: Destiny of The Throne

This board game can be played with 2-4 people.

Akhedena is a medieval strategy board game that requires clever tactics to complete with opponents. Each player owns generals and armies with a variety of strengths and abilities, building their own force to be the ruler of the lost kingdom.

A dragon flies overhead, making things unpredictable. While its fiery breath could destroy your lands or shatter your legions, with cunning and courage, you might be able to bend this winged beast to your will, and claim its dominion as your own.

Victory in Akhedena hinges on strategic planning. Players must carefully choose locations for their castles, determine the right moments to fortify with walls, and meticulously craft a powerful and well-balanced army in order to dominate the battlefield.

Akhedena is approachable for new players, thanks to its intuitive rules that capture the essence of combat. But don't be fooled by its easy-to-grasp core. Mastering the battlefield requires strategic cunning, as true victory hinges on outmaneuvering your opponents.

Shinobi: The Ninja Scroll

A test of wit, stealth, and skill awaits!

Shinobi: The Ninja Scroll is an interactive puzzle adventure that transports players into the clandestine world of ninjas. Designed for 1-4 players, this escape-room-style game challenges you to unravel the mysteries of an ancient scroll while mastering key ninja principles.

The game features a stunning, Japanese-inspired scroll as its centerpiece, adorned with intricate artwork and subtle clues that immerse players in its rich, thematic world. As you progress through nine unique puzzles—including logic challenges, codebreaking, and object manipulation—you'll unlock the secrets of the scroll and face a final meta-puzzle to prove your worth as a True Warrior.

Enhancing the experience is an intuitive online portal, complete with tiered hints, engaging videos, and an evocative soundtrack. Choose between Standard or Expert modes, depending on your experience level, and enjoy a satisfying balance of challenge and fun.

With its tactile components, immersive narrative, and replayable format, Shinobi: The Ninja Scroll offers a refreshingly original puzzle game experience. Whether you’re tackling it solo or teaming up with friends, it’s perfect for family game nights, date nights, or thoughtful gifting.

Players: 1-4
Ages: 12+
Playtime: 2-3 hours

A wargame from Panzerfaust Magazine #66, 1975 by John Van Devender.
This is the first game based on the Battle of the Pelennor Fieds. Few simple rules with a lot of units.
Skill game where you have to take turns placing 1 or more astronauts on the board while keeping it in balance. Whoever drops the board or the astronauts loses.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Inheritance is a City engine building card game, set in the City of Dreams. You take on the role of a person in the city, who was randomly selected to be elevated to the very top of society. The benefactor of the city has granted you infinite wealth and influence, with the opportunity to take it all.

In Inheritance, you will play as one of 24 unique characters. You will start the game with a few resources and access to the most basic locations around you.

Explore the city to gather resources to gain influence in new locations.
Draw cards to find new locations to add to your districts. Engage with socialites and stock traders to gain influence and gamble with debt.
The benefactor will not wait around and time is limited, so make your mark quickly.

—description from the publisher

The Emperor Keralon is mad. His own daughter Lemron is starting a rebellion against him. Throughout the land, Leaders of the five most powerful houses of Oshra are gathering their armies. They want to extend their realm in the power vacuum of a civil war. Will you be victorious in battle and emerge as the most powerful dynasty of Oshra? Yield is set in the world of Oshra. and its story plays out 43 years prior to the events in Hidden Leaders.

2-5 players represent Leaders of powerful houses and try to conquer (and keep!) as many fortresses over 3 game rounds or 12 player turns as possible. You do this by commanding 3 armies across a tactical map by selecting action cards from your hand. Recruit units, conquer fortresses and defeat opponents in battles.

After 3 rounds the player with the most victory points in form of controlled fortresses wins the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Murder Mystery: A World of Dreams and Secret Love
description:
Murder Mystery Game is an experiential mystery game where the player becomes a strange character and aims to solve riddles and cases.

Each player has a character, and setting materials containing secrets unique to that character are provided. Through information exchange with other players, the truth of the incident was revealed.

Antique "Fox Borrows a Trick" reappears as Torakaru Kitsune by Ginga Kikaku! It is a work where animals compete for the fruits of the forest.

Torakaru Kitsune is a trick-taking game where each player plays an animal card face down, and everyone counts the numbers from one. When your animal's number is called, you reveal your animal card. The key is being able to use special effects unique to each animal upon reveal, such as brainwashing. The number on the animal indicates its strength, and the strongest animal wins the fruit with the lowest point value on the field. As the game progresses, the points become higher, so by deducing the opponent's hand, you can aim for a comeback.

You’re the hosts of a dinner party, and your guests are people of history. Convince characters like Marie Curie, Cleopatra or Gandhi to join your table, use their special abilities and treat them to drinks from the bar. Send Adolf (who came uninvited and insists his invitation “got lost in the mail”) to the table of an opponent to ruin their evening, and make sure to keep your own guests entertained so other players won’t be able to charm them away from you. Only one can have the greatest party ever when the game ends and dinner is served!

Dinner Party is a quick, dark-humoured, dice-driven game for 2 to 6 players.

—description from the designer

Just like water turns into ice at 0°C and into steam at 100°C, at certain fixed temperatures, the behavior of substances dramatically changes. This is called a phase transition.

The Phase is a game that combine this universal physical phenomenon of three states of matter with trick-taking mechanics for 3 to 6 players. Just like ice turns into water, the game features changing states, referred to as "phases," during which the rules also change.

Starting with the dealer, each player plays a card in turn. The person with the lowest number is "1st," and the second lowest is "2nd." The "1st" player wins the card with the highest number present, while the "2nd" player wins the card with the lowest number. This round of play is called a "trick." Keep the cards you win in front of you. After a trick is completed, the "1st" player becomes the dealer for the next trick.

Mix over 30 ingredients to create unique 4-card combos in this fast-paced game of cards. Compete using random ingredients, and score as many points as possible before the main deck runs out of cards!

How to play

Hambre presents a simple mechanic of drawing a card and playing a card.


     Each time it is your turn, you will get a new random ingredient card.
     Each player has two boards that store up to 4 cards each (refrigerator and table). 
     Each time a player collects 4 cards on a board, he will have created a combo. Add up the points for each card and add them to your score sheet.
     Finally remove the cards from the game and keep cooking combos until you run out of ingredients.
     If you manage to collect 4 cards from the same menu (same color) the points are doubled.

Two imposing cake towers loll upwards in front of you. To get to the top, you have to choose the dice wisely and combine them to form a two-digit number. How far will you dare to climb? Whoever lasts the longest without failing will be rewarded with a hefty score at the end!

Gameplay:
On your turn you must decide whether you
a) play or
b) pass.

Play: Choose two (out of five) dice from the general display and use them to form a two-digit number. For example, you can use dice with the numbers 2 and 5 to form 25 or 52. Like this, you try climbing up your cake as high as possible. The next number must always be higher than the previous one.
Pass: if you cannot use any dice, you pass and re-roll all dice. You can only pass once on each level.

The round ends as soon as someone has reached the last level of his cake OR if someone has blundered (= not able to write a valid number after "passing")

A game consists of two rounds.

Secret Slasher is a murder mystery board game where players have to use clues, deduction, and strategy to figure out who the Slasher amidst them is, before they get slashed.

A player can participate in the game as either an everyday Citizen or a Slasher (Killer). No one knows who the Secret Slasher(s) is or who the Citizens are, this is assigned randomly and discretely at the start of the game.

The objectives are simple, if you are an innocent Citizen, you need to complete your list of errands or figure out who the Secret Slasher(s) is to win the game. If you are a Slasher, you need to attempt or pretend to complete your Errands, all while discreetly and secretly Slashing the other players to eliminate them from the game, just don't get caught!

The Nutcracker is based on the story of the same name by E.T.A.  Hoffmann.  It tells the story of Klara, who received a nutcracker from her godfather.  Imagine that, like Drosselmejer, you are a toy designer.  Create beautiful nutcrackers with colorful pieces to win.  The winner will be the constructor who makes as many grandfathers as possible from parts of the same color!

Game Goal:

Players try to score as many points as possible for the complete nutcrackers they have assembled.  The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.


Dziadek do orzechów to gra na podstawie opowieści o tym samym tytule autorstwa E.T.A. Hoffmanna. Opowiada o Klarze, która otrzymała od ojca chrzestnego dziadka do orzechów? Wyobraźcie sobie, że podobnie jak Drosselmejer jesteście konstruktorami zabawek. Stwórzcie piękne dziadki do orzechów z kolorowych części, aby wygrać. Zwycięzcą okaże się ten konstruktor, który wykona jak najwięcej dziadków z części w tym samym kolorze!

Cel gry:

Gracze starają się zdobyć jak najwięcej punktów za ułożone przez siebie kompletne dziadki do orzechów. Wygrywa gracz z największą liczbą punktów pod koniec gry.

boardgame: B17 Twenty Five Missions: Avro Lancaster
description:
Avro Lancaster is an expansion for the game B-17 Twenty five missions.

Content


     In this pdf you will find the new British AVRO LANCASTER bomber with the corresponding modifications for the game.
     Five exclusive missions to play with the Bomber.
     Mission Report Sheet.
     Sheet with information on some small modifications to the game.


—description from the designer

boardgame: Cat vs Rat: 奶酪陷阱 (Cat vs Rat: Cheese Trap)
description:
Cat vs Rat: 奶酪陷阱 looks to be a complete copy of Gobblet Gobblers, but with another graphical theme.

As in Tic-Tac-Toe the goal is to be the first one to get 3 in a row. With the additional ability to place pieces on already occupied squares, as long as the piece is big enough to cover the piece on the square.

boardgame: El Gran Juego De Las Cuatro Reglas
description:
Old game of the year 1973 the great game of the 4 rules, from the collection mathematics is a Distein game, totally educational game for egb, ref. 305, Price in pesetas, complete and with instructions printed on the cover of the box.

From 2 to six players/team compete to arrive first at the finish line. They roll the four dices, discard one and with the remaining three count the possible solution by using a basic math formula, choose one and advance; then next players do so and so on until the end.

Tales of Evil Final Chapter is the adventure that will lead us to the final showdown against the Demon-Puppet-Mistress!

Includes 6 new tiles, 3 new adventures and 48 new cards.

Puppets in High Places is set in the same universe as With a Smile & a Gun, but it is a standalone game, not an expansion.

After gaining control of the streets of Gattory City, mob bosses are setting their sights to the next level: getting a foothold in City Hall.

Puppets in High Places is a cutthroat game of underhanded power. Players will go through the Streets to obtain the resources they need to bribe VIPs and gain their favour. They will then proceed to the Speakeasy, where they will fight over these VIPs through a dynamic tug-of-war mechanism. Of course, mob bosses that they are, they have tricks up their sleeves and goons working the back alleys.

Puppets in High Places uses an evolving rondel, which the players will modify throughout the game, and a tug-of-war mechanism where players try to score VIP's represented by dice, whose value the players will also influence.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer: The Horus Heresy – Mechanicum Taghmata Army List
description:
The Horus Heresy Mechanicum: Taghmata Army List collects Mechanicum units and rules from The Horus Heresy Books One to Five in a 120 page, hard back book.

It includes background and profiles for all of the units available to the Mechanicum Taghmata, Legio Cybernetica and Ordo Reductor armies as well as support aircraft, seven classes of Knight, the Warhound, Reaver and immense Warlord Titans and introduces powerful new units such as the Ordinatus Macro Engines.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Horus Heresy – Age of Darkness

boardgame: The Great Race: Wild West & Far East expansion
description: This expansion provides USA and Asia mapboards, tokens, and a draw bag, plus certain stretch goals.  It is not a complete game, and you need The Great Race to use it. This content is already included in The Great Race 2.

boardgame: Warhammer: The Horus Heresy – Campaigns of the Age of Darkness: The Siege of Cthonia
description:
The planet of Cthonia, once home of the Sons of Horus, is consumed by a rolling war and under siege by the Imperial Fists. The True Sons of Cthonia vow to retake their world before the Warmaster claims Terra, with the aid of the Word Bearers and the Alpha Legion.

Standing between the traitors and victory is a garrison of Imperial Fists, reinforced by warbands of the Shattered Legions and even a cadre of Loyalist Thousand Sons.

This 240 page hardback rulebook includes eleven new missions based on the events of the siege of Cthonia, and campaign rules to fight the siege from beginning to apocalyptic end.

Also included are rules for new loyalist and traitor characters, and for new units including Astartes Inductii, Legiones Decurions and Cults Abominatio.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Horus Heresy – Age of Darkness

description:
In From the Abyss, a new expansion for Unfathomable, the SS Atlantica and its journey to Boston are submerged in even deeper peril. New, horrifying monsters emerge from the depths of the Atlantic Ocean to assail the ship, and the waters of the ocean itself have grown violent enough to sweep the passengers overboard. New treacheries are here to sabotage the ship, and with the mythos deck doubling in size, fresh dangers will threaten the Atlantica at every turn.

Fortunately, the humans aboard this ship have some new tools at their disposal. In addition to eight new playable characters, players now have access to ally cards that move around the ship independently and help keep it afloat. New prelude cards can alter the conditions at the start of the game, providing even more variety so that no two games ever play out the same way. With new skills, items, and the brand-new boon cards, the struggle for the SS Atlantica has never been this fierce!

A new expansion for Unfathomable, featuring expanded content and several new mechanics:

    Rescuing passengers now grants players access to allies, who can provide aid to the humans in all sorts of useful ways.
    Three new monsters known as “horrors”—the Shoggoth, the Drowned Spirit, and the Grasping Tendril—assail the ship, each of them incredibly dangerous and difficult to defeat.
    New prelude cards come in three different varieties and change the conditions at the start of the game, adding unfathomable levels of replayability.
    With additional playable characters, items, skills, and more, this expansion builds upon the base game to bring players more of everything that makes Unfathomable such a thrill to play!


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer: The Horus Heresy – Legions Imperialis: The Great Slaughter
description:
Contained within this book you will find a comprehensive history of the Second Battle for Nyrcon City and the origin of the Great Slaughter that would engulf the Garmon Cluster, detailing the Loyalist and Traitor forces that took part in that terrible conflict.  As well as this complete narrative, this book also includes new detachments for both the Legiones Astartes and Solar Auxilia Army Lists, providing new weapons of war for the forces of the Horus Heresy.

It also includes a complete Campaign system for linked battles.

—description from the back of the book
family: Game: Horus Heresy – Age of Darkness

description:
The Quick Delivery Board Game is a game in which a driver who delivers food ordered from Tower M quickly wins.
It is a board game that delivers food quickly by moving the elevator up and down using numbers from two dice.

The game player must be the first to complete the delivery of all the food to be delivered.


     Roll the dice.
     Add or subtract the number of elevator floors with dice numbers.
     Move the elevator.
     Deliver food.


The game is over when anyone finishes delivering all the food.

—description from the designer

Micro Mycelium invites players to delve into the intricate world of a vibrant fungal forest, where they must strategically place tiles to expand their territory and maximize their points.

In this tile-placement strategy game, players must carefully grow, connect, and conquer as they navigate the dense mycelial network. With each turn, players strategically place tiles to expand their mushroom kingdom, earning points for their clever placement and strategic connections. The goal? To cultivate the most prosperous mushroom kingdom and emerge victorious as the ruler of the fungal forest.

During each round, players lay one tile down. When the last tile is placed, scoring begins.  The player with the highest score wins.

—description from the designer

The Race Game is an Australian Horse race game where players race by the roll of a dice along a track until they reach the square 100. If a player lands on certain spaces showing the race hurdles then the player has to go back and miss a turn.

First player to reach 100 is the winner.

—user summary

boardgame: Rail, Road and Air Race Game
description:
Rail, Road and Air Race Game is an Australian roll and move race game where players begin in three different Australian cities (Melbourne, Brisbane and Adelaide) with each player choosing either car, train or plane to race to Sydney Central Train Station.
Each separate route has squares that can aid or slow player progress. First player to reach Sydney Central Train Station is deemed the winner.
Several versions of the game exist beginning in the 1930s to the 1960s.

—user summary

D100 Space Galaxy Builder (Book 2) adds to D100 Space a way to create and explore new and fascinating galaxies. Your captain will travel the galaxy, interacting with star systems, space stations, and military docks; and explore unique and dangerous planets in search of adventure. They will discover missions and may even have events sprung on them as they explore the darkest reaches of space.

—description from the designer

At the start of the game, the meeples are outside the game board. Each player strives to place one of his three meeples on three adjacent spaces in a row.
Each player rolls a die once in turn. On the first roll the player places a meeple on the space that shows the number rolled, this continues until all 3 meeples are on the board. Then, according to the roll, any meeple is moved forward. Anyone who leaves the board on space 24 immediately moves back to space 1. There is no capture; there may be two or more meeples of different colors on one space.
The winner is the first player to form a “trio”, that is, to have their meeples in a horizontal row, whereby none of these spaces may be occupied by other meeples.

—user summary

Discover the diversity of grape varieties grown in France and bet on their strengths to win the battle! Each grape variety has 3 characteristics: "surface area cultivated, change in surface area over 60 years and bunch size".

The principle of the grape variety game is simple: play against your friends to win as many cards as possible and win the game.

First, the homeowner couple distributes the 10 rings to any black diamonds on the game board.
The 1st burglar starts, followed by the 1st homeowner, then the 2nd burglar and the 2nd homeowner. The homeowners start at A, the burglars at B. B is also the end space for the burglars. Each player can play with one or two dice as he sees fit.
The burglars try to roll the black squares to get possession of the rings lying there. If this succeeds, the ring is placed on the burglar meeple. If a burglar has captured 3 rings, he tries to leave the house at B, and the winner is whoever manages to do so.
The homeowners catch a burglar when their meeple lands on the space he occupies. This means that the burglar is eliminated from the game. The homeowners win, if if they capture both burglars.
No burglar is allowed to enter or pass the space on which a homeowner stands. He must choose a different direction, if such an option was still open to him.

—user summary

Duration: 90 minutes, 2 halves of 45 minutes each.
The playing pieces of both teams are placed in the red or blue bordered spaces(team “Red” or team “Blue”) with numbers 1 to 11.
In any case, the blue team starts. Two dice are rolled and the parties take turns. For every normal roll of 2 dice, 2 pawns are drawn, corresponding to the number of points on each dice. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.
The team against which a goal has been scored has kick-off after the pieces have been brought to the starting position. The red team starts the second half in any case.
Two dice are rolled and the parties take turns. For every normal roll of 2 dice, 2 pawns are drawn, corresponding to the number of points on each dice.

The game was produced as a promotional for Blendax toothpaste.

—user summary

Dive into an underwater adventure with Poulp Party!

Head to an underwater world vibrant with life, where colorful fish swim around a mischievous octopus.
The mission cards guide the children on their adventure: each card indicates the fish to recover, transforming the game into an underwater treasure hunt.
Children will have to show skill and ingenuity to make the fish fall into the holes by making the octopus move using the string. But be careful! You must only collect the designated fish, otherwise the next player will benefit! Children learn to plan their movements, anticipate the actions of other players and react quickly. This way they develop their skills and their ability to think, while having fun.

The game includes a box illustrating the seabed, 20 mission cards, 30 fish, an octopus with its card and a dice.

—description from the designer

Abracadabra! Are you ready to lift the curtain and discover the animal hidden behind the smoke?

This observation and deduction game is designed for children aged 3 and up.
The game includes a cardboard theater with its curtain, 20 illustrations to guess, 18 magnetic clouds of smoke, a magic wand and a dice.

The clouds of smoke hide an illustration that had previously been slipped into the room. By rolling the dice and using the magic wand, the players gradually remove the clouds, each in turn. The illustration slowly reveals itself... The first one to guess the right animal wins the round!

Children learn to observe carefully, infer from visible clues, and respond quickly. Thus they develop their ability to observe and deduce, while having fun.
 —description from the designer

Solve the puzzle to open the door…
Take the 4 puzzle cards of the color of your choice.
At the top, the coded message of the riddle card is revealed.

Arrange your cards cleverly to reproduce the right combination.

Place the Pharaoh grid on your cards to check. Did you find the solution?

—description from the designer

The Legend of Aquiny is a cooperative, evolving campaign with 10 chapters. Two to four players can play as one of the four heroes and advance through the Adventure Book to solve the mystery of the Legend of Aquiny.

Each chapter requires you to achieve several objectives in order to succeed. If one of the objectives is not achieved, you will have to start the chapter again until you have achieved all the objectives in order to move on to the next one. Some chapters are in two parts. You will be invited to read the next section once you have achieved specific objectives.

The material is evolving! When you successfully achieve a chapter, you can open a new envelope to unlock new materials... and new rules!
The number of players can vary from one chapter to the next. So you can start the adventure with four players, move up to two, and finish the story with three.

—description from the designer

This is a print-and-play only crossover character for Millennium Blades featuring a duelist from the TTRPG Perfect Draw!

Elena favors smaller star cards and powers them up by combining them, much like her Building Block Battalion signature deck!

description: Do you know which is the longest cave system in the country? And how long has Túró Rudi been produced? In the Terra Magyarország board game, you can encounter similar questions! Compete with each other while getting to know this amazing country!

A must-follow trick-taking game where Martin Luther rebels against the sale of indulgences by the church.

In this game, the red suit (hearts and diamonds) represents money, and the black suit (spade and clubs) represents sin. Game includes a separate phase after trick-taking, where players may use 1 Indulgence card. Red, Black, and Indulgence Cards will be worth different points, depending on whether Martin Luther is present (i.e. a player has no trump and no Indulgence cards) during end-game scoring.

You need a standard playing card deck, in addition to the game-specific components (13 indulgence cards, 5 player aids, and 1 Japanese rules card).

In INYO, you will demonstrate your mastery of balance and unbalancing by knocking your opponent out of the arena or forcing them into submission. To do so, take steps with your dice as if they were your feet. Manage your footwork effectively, striking a calculated balance between gentle agility and explosive power. Engage in measured collisions to throw and grapple your opponent.

Gongdo! 拱斗 is a novel, engaging and meaningful four-player competition game. Inspired by the formidable stars in the sky, it’s also known as "Supreme Star Mahjong". Players must collect the tiles they need to win the game: gather 15 tiles of the hand comprising 2, 3, 3, 3, 4 - Win!

Gong 拱 means stars come together to pay tribute to the supreme winning status.
Do 斗 (pronounce as "Dou") - in Chinese it has two meanings - Fighting, Conquer or “Dou” are the Stars themselves!

The "Gongdo!”engraved tile set comprises 160 tiles in total. The tiles are categorized into five star echelons. You don't need to memorize them, just glance at the tiles’ differences to start the game.

"Gongdo!" can be easy to get started (since you can win with a fixed combination), while it also can be complex and in-depth (i.e. the combinations of 4 pattern tiles are unpredictable and changeable along the game.)

"Revealing your pattern/prospects": When you reveal your pattern/prospects, you can declare it loudly and get good luck! Each 4 pattern tiles, are formed by those “heavenly bodies combinations”, they can have a relatable good connotations in many aspects of life!

"Couples" are vital! "The matched 2 tiles and couples" are essential! Actively look for the "best match of stars" among tiles.

Friends of Gongdo!, let's play!

—description from the designer

Reclaim the Land. Reshape the Legend.

The Han Empire has fallen.

Dong Zhuo rules from the shadows of the throne. The Yellow Turban Rebellion spreads like wildfire across the provinces. And as the realm descends into chaos, the great forces of Wei, Wu, and Shu-Han rise to claim their place in the storm to come.
But not all stories are written in the scrolls of history.
In ASSERTION: The Three Kingdoms players step into the turmoil of a fractured China, drawing from the legendary Romance of the Three Kingdoms to explore a bold, re-imagined path through one of history’s most turbulent eras. New warlords rise. Loyalties shift. The fate of the land may yet be shaped by unexpected hands.

Command armies, court favour, reshape alliances and navigate the ambitions of kingdoms and emperors alike. The path to power is yours to carve, in battle, in diplomacy, and in shadows. The world is at war. This is your story. What part will you play in the legend?

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: Adventure Is Nigh!
description:
The Red Dragon Inn: Adventure Is Nigh! is a card game for 2-4 players featuring the characters from the Adventure Is Nigh YouTube series.

After another wild adventure, it's time you kicked back with your buddies for a party at your favorite nightclub. Brawl, gamble, and drink your way through the night as a unique character with its own deck, traits, and mechanisms. The last adventurer standing wins!

All of the characters in this standalone game can be mixed with characters from any The Red Dragon Inn game or expansion.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Reverse Charades Junior Edition Demo Pack
description: Set of 8 cards and 2 rules/advertising cards for Reverse Charades Junior Edition
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

boardgame: Skeleton Stories: Geister in den Wänden
description:
Sarah's world collapses after the death of her mother. Her father tries to suppress the loss of his wife and flees with his daughter to the small town of Radenau, where she gradually loses herself in the world of necromancy in order to make contact with her mother. Three months later, Sarah is found dead in the woods. Was she in contact with more spirits than just her mother? Or is there an earthly cause behind it all?

With the help of parapsychologist Dr Corvus, embark on a search for the truth about the eerie events. Examine the documents and evidence, investigate paranormal events and shed light on the darkness.

The aim of the game is to solve the murder and put the paranormal events to the test.

Can you successfully communicate with the ghosts?

ARE YOU READY TO WORK WITH DR. CORVUS? ARE YOU SUITABLE AS A MEDIUM?
As an experienced medium and doctor of parapsychology, Dr Corvus is contacted by people from all over the world to investigate unexplained occurrences, allegedly cursed sites or demonic possession. Become part of his team and investigate exciting criminal cases whose victims usually had contact with the afterlife before they died.

Skeleton Stories is a modern crime game in which you not only have to solve the murder case, but also investigate the case for paranormal events using interactive and multimedia research. The physical case file is accompanied by a digital companion app.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Nach dem Tod ihrer Mutter bricht für Sarah eine Welt zusammen. Ihr Vater versucht, den Verlust seiner Frau zu verdrängen und zieht mit ihr in die Kleinstadt Radenau, wo sie sich nach und nach in die Welt der Geisterbeschwörung und des Übernatürlichen verliert, um Kontakt mit ihrer Mutter aufnehmen zu können. Drei Monate später wird Sarah tot in einem Waldstück aufgefunden. Wurde ihr der Kontakt in die Geisterwelt zum Verhängnis? Oder steckt am Ende eine irdische Ursache dahinter?

BIST DU BEREIT FÜR DIE ARBEIT MIT DR. CORVUS? BIST DU ALS MEDIUM GEEIGNET?
Als erfahrenes Medium und Doktor der Parapsychologie wird Dr. Corvus von Menschen aus der ganzen Welt kontaktiert, um  unerklärliche Vorkommnisse, angeblich Verfluchte Stätten oder Dämonische Besessenheit zu prüfen und zu erforschen. Werdet Teil seines Teams und ermittelt in spannenden Kriminalfällen, dessen Opfer meist schon vor Ihrem Ableben Kontakt mit dem Jenseits hatten.

Skeleton Stories ist ein modernes Krimispiel, bei dem es nicht nur gilt den Mordfall zu lösen, sondern dabei auch durch interaktive und multimediale Forschungen den Fall auf paranormale Ereignisse hin zu untersuchen. Begleitet wird die physische Fallakte mit einer digitalen Begleitapp.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer: The Horus Heresy – Campaigns of the Age of Darkness: The Battle of Beta-Garmon
description:
Campaigns of the Age of Darkness: The Battle of Beta-Garmon is teh second campaign expansion for Horus Heresy: Age Of Darkness. The book covers the narrative and battles of the Beta-Garmon, and provides a complete campaign to fight from its opening skirmishes to its cataclysmic climax in the Titandeath.

The 240 page hardback book also contains rules for the Shattered Legions and Blackshields forces, characters including Little Horus Aximand, Tybalt Marr, Shadrak Meduson and Endryd Haar, and rules for Onslaught team campaigns.
family: Game: Horus Heresy – Age of Darkness

boardgame: Warhammer: The Horus Heresy – Exemplary Battles of the Age of Darkness: Volume One
description:
The Horus Heresy spans innumerable desperate battles and bitter grudges – many lost to history, other the subject of grim and terrible rumours.  The champions who fight these far-flung battles are unlike any in the galaxy's history, warriors armed with inimical strategies and esoteric weaponry, locked into struggles across sprawling arcologies, towering citadels, and claustrophobic tunnels.

In this book, you will find five accounts of battles from the Age of Darkness - The Defence of Sotha, The Siege of Hydra Cordatus, the Battle of Armatura, The Atttack on Calth and The Purging of the Invocastus Sector - along with the campaign and missions rules to recreate them on the tabletop in games of Warhammer: The Horus Heresy.

This 112 page hardback book also includes new rules for five notable Legiones Astartes units unique to Legions that fought in these battles – the Night Lords, Iron Warriors, World Eaters, Ultramarines, and Salamanders – as well as expanded rules for the Emperor's Children Traitor Legion, and complete rules for their ascended Daemon Primarch, Fulgrim Transfigured.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Horus Heresy – Age of Darkness

description:
Franklin 1864 allows gamers to refight the historic battles of Spring Hill and Franklin, Tennessee in 1864, using the popular Civil War Brigade Battle Series of rules.  Franklin 1864 is Volume V, following Antietam, Shiloh, The Seven Days Battles, and Gettysburg.

The Battle of Spring Hill was a trap laid by Confederate General Hood in his drive on Nashville, trying to trap part of the Army of the Ohio under Union General Schofield. The historic battle did not go as Hood hoped with the Union forces escaping to Franklin relatively unscathed.

The Battle of Franklin the following day, November 30, was a last ditch by Hood trying to catch the Union forces with their back to a river and the bridges damaged.  A lack of time, artillery, and planning led to a vicious Confederate defeat with 14 Confederate generals killed and 55 regimental commanders.  The battle was a close run affair for the amount of time involved a Union deployment mistake almost giving Hood the victory he so desparately sought.

Fight each battle separately or combine to refight the 2 days together in a linked mini-campaign.

Players will command either the Confederate or Union armies as they battle to destroy opposing forces and capture key terrain objectives to determine victory.

—description from the publisher
