family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
PolyDots is an abstract strategy game and brainteaser for 1-4 players, aged 5+. Using 13-sided tiles called polykites, players align and connect tiles to claim territory with coins matching simple math outcomes.

COMPONENTS 

     30 polykite tiles
     80 coins
     2 dice.


GOAL:
Score the highest points by claiming tiles.
    
SETUP:

     Shuffle the tiles and place face down between the players.
     Give each player 20 coins of the same colour.
     Each player takes five tiles and places them face up beside themselves.
     Place a random starter tile between the players.
     Decide upon the winning condition using either adjacent or non-contiguous territory.


GAME PLAY:
Playing in turns, a player drops one of their tiles in an attempt to connect colored dots of their colour. A connection can be complete or partial. A complete connection creates a dot of one solid colour, while a partial connection has a dot of mixed colours. Each tile has four sets of 3 dot fragments of the same colour.
If a connection is made, the player makes an attempt to claim one of the adjacent tiles with a coin. This is accomplished by rolling the dice and forming an equation. The equation dictates which coin to place. A coin can be placed on a territory claimed by another player only if the equation is higher than the value of the coin already in place.
Claiming a Tile:
A tile can only be claimed when an adjacent tile has formed a dot with the same colour. The subsequent coin can be placed on either tile.

Stealing Territory:
A player can only steal a territory (tile) by forming an equation whose outcome is mathematically higher than the existing coin.

Stacking Tiles:
A tile can be stacked on an empty tile if an appropriate dot can be formed.

Equations (Outcomes):
Upon rolling the dice, a player chooses the outcome of a mathematical equation from the two numbers. Only 20 outcomes are possible between 1-36. The outcome or solution is from addition, subtraction, multiplication or division of the two numbers. For example, rolling 3 and 5 can yield 2, 8 or 15 from subtraction, addition or multiplication of the two numbers.

WINNING:
There are two winning conditions based on the initial decision of claiming territory:
Adjacent: add up the value of coins in the largest contiguous area.
Non-Contiguous: add up the values of all coins.
The player with the highest coins claimed wins. In case of a tie, the youngest player wins.

STRATEGY:
Try to claim an important tile using the highest mathematically outcome. Such a tile links adjacent tiles to form a larger contiguous area.

SOLO GAME PLAY:
This mode requires you to solve the brainteaser by perfectly aligning as many tiles as you can to form complete dots. The perfect solution uses all tiles.

POLYKITE BACKGROUND:
Polykites were the miracle mathematical shape discovered in 2023. These 13-sided polygons allow a pattern to be formed that never repeats. 
In the math world, this kite- or hat-like shape is known as an "aperiodic monotile", also called an "einstein" (or the German phrase for "one stone").
Slotted together, it's impossible to find a matching arrangement or orientation.
On March 20, 2023, a group of computer scientists (David Smith, Joseph Samuel Myers, Craig Kaplan and Chaim Goodman-Strauss) revealed their discovery nicknamed the "hat polykite”.

—description from the designer

Each player is paying 10 tokens into the toll. The order is determined by placing the round tiles face down on the table and each player draws one. The die is then rolled in the order of the colors at the bottom of the guns. The players roll a die and push their grenades further according to the number they rolled. Whoever gets on a red number has scored a hit and will receive 3 tokens from the toll as a reward. However, if one gets to a white number, he has to move 1 number back. On the roll of more than 15, the player has to go back as many spaces from 15 as he rolled too many. Whoever gets to 15 first wins the game and gets 2 thirds of the contents of the till, the next person gets the rest.

—user summary

Plum-Plum consists of 32 coins (8 each red, green, yellow and blue discs). Red is worth 30 points, yellow is worth 10 points, green 20 points, and blue 0 points.
The player who deals, places the coins on the table with the colored side facing down and shuffles them. Then, like in a card game, they are distributed evenly among the players. Nobody is allowed to see what color the coins are! The next player on the left comes out by turning over any of his coins. The other players follow in turn. Now the rule applies that the highest color played first wins. So if, for example, green-yellow-red-blue is played, red wins. When played: yellow-blue-blue-green, green wins.

Exception: If all players have the same color, the last player wins. Whoever wins comes out for the next turn!
After the game, the coins are added up and the result is written down by each player. The first person to reach 1010 points wins.

—user summary

On the violent and deadly streets of the Frozen City, feuds and vendettas are commonplace. Wizards may start out focused on discovery and exploration, fighting when necessary for survival or to win possession of some long-lost artefact or grimoire, but such battles can swiftly become intensely personal. Competitors become adversaries and, as the cycle of violence continues, some wizards lose sight of their original goals and focus their efforts on the elimination of their hated foes – their mortal enemies.

This 96 page paperback supplement for Frostgrave presents rules for creating recurring villains in both competitive and solo campaigns. These characters are unique individuals who return over the course of a campaign to plague the players' wizards, gaining experience, recruiting allies, and becoming more deadly with each appearance. Against such threats, a wizard's base becomes as much hideout as headquarters, and an expanded range of options for enhancing bases is provided, as are rules for defending your own... and attacking those of your rivals!

—description from the publisher

Embark on a daring quest to recover the lost Ace of the Covenant, while outwitting ninjas, spies, and Nazis, and putting together clues along the way.

Navigate through the shuffled deck 3 times. If you recover the Ace of Spades during the 3rd round, and collect enough cards in the game (defeated enemies, collected artifacts) you succeed! Otherwise the Ace of the Covenant has fallen into the wrong hands.

Inspired by Solitary Journey.

—description from the designer

boardgame: ただ今の決まり手は！(Tadaima No Kimarite Wa!)
description:
In ただ今の決まり手は！(Tadaima No Kimarishu Wa!) the players are assigned a name of a sumo wrestling move, such as "Drunk Rap Battle" or "Dirty Tickling". One player acts out the match using puppets on the end of clips, and are only allowed to make the sounds of the animals they've chosen.

Once their demonstration is complete, the other players, in turn order can guess the combination based upon the adjective and noun cards shown on the table.

The player with the most points after two rounds wins.

You are managing a new coaster park. Build paths, rides and stalls and attract guests to your park. Each ticket sold is more money for construction, or hold on to your cash to grab victory.

Over 20 rounds roll two dice for everyone to use. One dice determines the type of action, and the other determines the size or effectiveness, and each player can decide which dice to assign where.

Several types of buildings are available:
Stalls allow more guests into your park and increase ticket prices.
Gentle Rides, Thrill Rides and Rollercoasters make space for guests to stay in your park, each with their own polyomino shape.
Corporate buildings give access to special abilities.

At the end of the game, whoever has the most money available wins.

—description from the designer

You're the manager of a traveling youth baseball team that competes for Fun Points by winning tournaments and meeting objectives set by your board of directors.
Start by choosing players who can cover every position on the field and who will maximize the talents of your coaches. Improve your squad by holding practices and paying for trainers and gear, but don't get too low on morale or money you'll need to get into the tournaments.
Managers take turns picking from a variety of face-up team improvement cards that refresh every two turns.
They have 16 weeks (turns) to improve their players or raise money or morale. Fun Points are awarded at four tournaments and from Board Goals achieved or failed at the end of the game.

—description from the designer

General d’Armee 2 is designed for Divisional and Corps size actions in the Napoleonic period.  Dynamic and fast-paced, General d’Armee uses the ADC command and control system seen in the first edition to put you in the shoes of the commanding General, deciding how and when to use your influence to control and win the battle.

Packed with Napoleonic flavour, General d’Armee uses the battalion as the smallest manoeuvre element, the Brigade the lowest tactical command. A typical club night action involving several Brigades per side and a full day or weekend handling a Corps or two with ease.

116 pages in length, General d’Armee 2 includes rules for using figures from 6mm to 28mm in size, details on organising your armies and getting them in the table. Two introductory scenarios are included to get you playing with the latest edition of these classic rules from the master of the big battlefield.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 1813: The Battles for Germany – Six Wargame Scenarios for General d'Armee
description:
1813. The Battles for Germany is a scenario supplement for General d’Armee penned by game designer Dave Brown.  Here Dave provides us with six iconic actions from the decisive campaign which saw Napoleon fight to preserve his German possessions after the disastrous 1812 campaign in Russia.  Beginning with Weissenfels, this scenario supplement goes on to cover the actions at Lutzen, Bautzen, Dresden, Mockern and finally Liebertwolkwoitz.

This 40 page PDF contains an illustrated historical introduction to the campaign followed by six scenarios which are easily printed out for the game you wish to play and contain complete army lists with unit and commander ratings as well as deployment notes and a breakdown of the terrain.  What’s more the six scenarios can be played individually or as part of a “campaign’, with the result of each scenario affecting the next.

Can you save Germany for the Emperor of the French or will you be the liberating forces of Europe combining to liberate the continent from the terrors of the Corsican Ogre?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 1941 to '43 Eastern Front: Barbarossa to Citadel
description: This PDF supplement for ‘O’ Group covers the war in the East from Barbarossa to Kursk, combining army lists for Germany, Italy, Finland, Romania, Hungary and the Soviet Union with nation specific rules and an array of support options. Also presented are eight scenarios which may be played individually or as a linked campaign.

This PDF supplement for ‘O’ Group covers the 1944 campaign in the Normandy region of France, combining army lists for Britain the USA, Canada, Germany, the Free French along with forces from Poland and an array of support options. Theatre specific rules are presented alongside six scenarios which may be played individually or as a linked campaign.

boardgame: 1940: The Fall of France & the Low Countries
description: This sixty page PDF supplement for ‘O’ Group covers the 1940 campaign in France and the Low Countries, with army lists for France, Britain, Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, and Italy with core forces and an array of support options. Theatre specific rules are presented as are six scenarios which may be played individually or as a linked campaign.

boardgame: Black Rose Wars: Rebecca Personal Spell – Cursed Blood
description:
This spell was created as part of a contest by the community. The files were later shared for print and play.
Its an alternative personal spell for "Rebecca", a Mage from the Black Rose Wars base game.

Cursed Blood
A spell inflicts one damage on you.
Spell Type: Protection, Profane 
Effect: If you have been defeated target the room where the spells owner is, summon a Bone Knight and it activates.
Otherwise: target the room where the spells owner is, summon a Landsknecht and it activates.

boardgame: Black Rose Wars: Spurchia Christmas Scenario
description:
This special Christmas themed scenario was released for print and play by Ludus Magnus Studio. 
It contains cards, terrain and the Spurchia miniature.

A Legendary Scenario is a special game that allows you to play an event from the world of Black Rose Wars: Rebirth by applying a specific set of new rules.
In Spurchia's Carol, the malicious spriggan Spurchia wants to ruin the holidays.
Can the Mages stop this evil and mischievous villain?
Or will they go home with their pockets full of coal?

Test your skills as a magician with the extraordinary Harry Potter The Magic Tournament Board Game for witches and wizards! Use the magical cards to cast spells with each duel you face. Each card features an iconic character with a different score that will depend on the roll of your magic dice. This unique, high quality board game is full of magic and will delight any Harry Potter fan!

—description from the publisher

Fire in the Blue transports you to the strategic crucible of the Mediterranean, where iconic battles raged between the Axis and Allied forces. Witness the critical role of aircraft and submarines in scouting and unleashing devastating attacks. Experience the awesome hail of anti-aircraft fire and the fury of carrier-launched fighters as air formations engage in epic clashes.

Will you... 

     Secure vital supply lines for the British and defend Malta from invasion?
     Cut off crucial resources and cripple the Allied war effort as the Axis?
     Master the Art of Combat, balance your air formations, employing fighters and bombers for strategic strikes and aerial supremacy.
     Command powerful fleets, employing cunning tactics and careful maneuvering to outwit your opponent?


 
Components:

    400+ counters
    1 A3 mounted map
    2 A4 maps
    9 player aid cards
    12 airport cards
    1 rules booklet (24 pages)


—description from the publisher

This 100 page full-colour rulebook contains all basic rules for Freebooter’s Fate, garnished with entertaining stories, artwork, miniature photography and concept sketches.  a handful of scenarios and many valuable advice. Also included are scenarios and rules for naval combat.

Grab the rules and an opponent and try it! You will like it. How could it be otherwise, with these ingredients: Tussling pirates! Scary goblins! Mysterious assassins! Skillful Amazons! Marching Debonner! Occult mystics! Steadfast Imperials! Tough mercenaries! Parrots! A world full of villains, scoundrels, heroes, ladies, femme fatales and much more. With a thousand wooden legs!

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
Splinter Quad is an abstract strategy game for four players, and a variant of the two-player game of Splinter. The object of the game is to eliminate your opponents’ pieces by sliding, pushing and fracturing the battlefield.  The last player with at least one square, one circle and one cross on the board wins the game.

Each player starts the game with 9 pieces, grouped into 3 different shapes – 2 squares, 2 crosses and 5 circles.  Play begins with the pieces arranged in a checkerboard pattern at the center of the board.

Assign a color to each player and decide who goes first.  Then continue playing, with all players alternating turns.  On your turn, you may slide any of your pieces one square in any direction (orthogonally or diagonally), pushing along any pieces that lie in the way.

If a piece is pushed off the edge, it is removed from the board.

In addition, if the pieces are splintered into two or more disconnected groups (so that no piece of either group shares a side or a corner with any piece of the other group), the largest group remains on the board and all other groups are removed. If a splinter results in two groups of equal size, the player who caused the splinter decides which group is removed.

To remain in the game, a player must have at least one square, one cross and one circle on the board.  If a player ceases to satisfy this condition, that player is eliminated from the game, and that player’s pieces are removed from the board.  The last player with at lease on square, one cross and one circle on the board wins the game.

Click here for official rules and diagrams: https://splinterboardgame.blogspot.com/2024/03/splinter-quad-object-of-game-knock-out.html

—description from the publisher

Giraffe Chess is a 68 square result oriented board game of India where an additional 4 squares are added to the standard 8 x 8 chessboard: 2 behind each King and Queen: these squares are occupied a pair of Giraffes (these have the move of the fairy chess Zebra, or the Bannerman in 3-sided Chinese chess).

There are a number of ways to win:


     By checkmating your opponent's King.
     By "Crowning": getting your King into one of your opponent's Giraffe squares.
     If your opponent does not defeat you in 70 moves you win.
     If your opponent does not capture one of your pieces within 50 moves you win.
     If you stalemate your opponent, you win.

boardgame: Freebooter's Fate: Tales of Longfall #7 – Scenarios 2
description:
The second scenatio book for Freebooter's Fate, collecting fifteen new scenarios in a 40 page softcover book.

For the first time these include single player scenarios, and in addition optional rules and variants to add challenge and variety to your games of miniature piracy.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Freebooter' Fate: Tales of Longfall #5 – Scenarios
description:
Tales of Longfall #5's 28 pages are packed with fourteen new scenarios for Freebooter's Fate, each accompanied with little stories that describe a fight, a skirmish or a washed out battle and in which your teams are the main actors.

There are treasures to find, stupid birds to catch, fire or water to escape, and gold to find and lose. Of course, the enemy will try to stop you, mostly because he has exactly the same objectives.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Freebooter's Fate:  Tales of Longfall #6 – Shadows
description:
Shadows have fallen over Longfall! They squirm out of darkened doors, a swirling mass of darkness.  The streets of Longfall are shrouded in dark, impenetrable fog and the whole town is threatening to sink into chaos.

And that’s because power-obsessed Brotherhood members and curious Amazons have opened two ancient doors a little further than was wise. Doors to another world. A dark, formless, thoroughly evil world. Through this rift they emerge again, and the heroic efforts of a centuries-old secret society have been thwarted, for the shadows are back! And they are not kind. Suddenly the darkness on Leonera has become even more dangerous…

This 36 page paperback supplement for Freebooter's Fate provies all the new special rules and traits to create and fight a dark bunch of new shadow characters, and a first “shadow scenario” to start right away.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Coast Watchers: Allied Field Intelligence in the South Pacific, 1942-1943
description:
Coast Watchers takes you into the World War II South Pacific’s struggle between Allied intelligence teams and Japanese security forces. Standup blocks hide coastwatching stations, guerrillas, refugees, and stranded Allied crew. Facedown counters hide the buildup of Japanese forces, which the coastwatchers seek to observe and report to headquarters. Other counters show where Japanese patrols are searching for coastwatchers or go into and get drawn from cups to run Japanese searches and Allied delivery missions.

Each side has Mission cards. Allied Missions assign side tasks to the coastwatchers. Japanese Missions lay out secret military objectives for victory points, including air and sea operations against which the coastwatchers are to warn Allied forces. By hunting coastwatchers while building up military readiness and guiding operations, the Japanese player seeks to slip through the enemy intelligence net. Players also draw Asset cards: special abilities to augment actions and perhaps add victory points.

Coast Watchers offers 15 standalone “Situations” plus 4 campaigns to tie them together. The Situations span the early-1942 Japanese incursion into the South Pacific to the neutralization of their great base at Rabaul by the end of 1943.

A solitaire system is included.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The Queen of Hears wants to make jam tarts. Play cards beneath a row of "berry" cards (clubs). Try to make a suitable set of three to collect the corresponding card. Collect all ten berry cards to win.

—description from the designer

A didactic card game, meant to teach children about the planets in the solar system. You have a 3 card hand and must play out a Sun card, then each planet in sequence, doing nothing but draw/discard if you cannot.

Advertised as part of the "small one's series"

Boardgame rules: Each player draws a snake from Cyclops' Cave and places it on the board to match the dark snake figuers. A player scores by matching color segments on snakes.

As noted, instead of a dice, the unique Cyclop's Cave is used to pick up randomly the snakes. The other interesting and novel part is that the snakes may be placed in two different ways adding to a combination of memory and strategy game. There are also special snakes that have special mathematical actions.

Solo rules: Place all 24 snakes on the outlines of medousa’s head by matching the color segments on the snakes when they overlay another snakes. Depending on how many colors are matched, the player reaaches a corresponding score.

This product is strictly available in German from Mirakulus. Otherwise, My Little Scythe and the Pie in the Sky expansion are--and will continue to be--provided separately by Stonemaier Games and all other localization partners.

This box contains the base game My Little Scythe and the expansion Pie in the Sky.

My Little Scythe is a competitive, family-friendly game in which each player controls 2 animal miniatures embarking upon an adventure in the Kingdom of Pomme.

In an effort to be the first to earn 4 trophies from 8 possible categories, players take turns choosing to Move, Seek, or Make. These actions will allow players to increase their friendship and pies, power up their actions, complete quests, learn magic spells, deliver gems and apples to Castle Everfree, and perhaps even engage in a pie fight.

Some of My Little Scythe’s mechanisms are inspired by the bestselling game, Scythe. It caught the eye of Stonemaier Games as a fan-created print-and-play game in 2017 (it went on to win the BoardGameGeek 2017 award for best print-and-play game).

—description from the publisher

Pie in the Sky begins on the eve of the 3000th Harvest Tournament, where stories are retold of Pomme’s ancient animals venturing into distant lands to establish their own kingdoms. To accomplish this, Pomme’s founders worked together to build the legendary Airship Kai, imbuing it with the best knowledge from all nine animal species. Sharing the ship’s powers and speed, each kingdom established its foundations. But one year, the airship and its Fox and Owl passengers journeyed into the far frontiers and were never seen or heard from again … until now.

As animals gather for the milestone tournament, the fabled lost airship emerges from the horizon, carrying Seekers from the Fox and Owl kingdoms. As if this reunion isn’t reason enough for celebration, Pomme’s Seekers realize that Airship Kai still responds to each animal species. The stage is set for the greatest Harvest Tournament in 3000 years!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games
Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description: Game of attention and strategy. The player who gets a pirate family first wins. You can buy cards with coins or lose them to the bomb!

Pacto is Mortem et Gloriam small game, but in no way feels small as it keeps all the main features of Maximus that players like. Armies still have 10-20 “units” - so again this is why it keeps the feel of the larger games. But it can be played with armies of just 20-30 bases, making it good for travel and easy to build.

Our garden is full of delicious critters, get ready to feast on only the yummiest bugs that the dice can offer you. Hide your hands under your sleeves and surprise those unsuspecting bugs.

Objective of the game:
To score the highest number of points before the deck runs out, trying to keep your favorite Bugs to earn more points. Each caught card will give you one point at the end of the game, but if you manage to get cards with your favorite bugs, they will score double. Get your sleeves ready because it's time to dine!

All players must have one hand hidden under the sleeve of their coat and wait for someone to roll the dice. When it stops rolling, each person will take their hand out of their sleeve and quickly place it, with their palm extended, on one of the 6 cards on the table, completely covering it. That card must contain a Bug of the same color as the top face of the die (there can only be one hand per card). If there are no cards on the table that have that color, a seventh card will be placed on the table and the die will be rolled again (this will be repeated until the die color matches the color of one of the Bugs on the table). But that's not all, before lifting the hand from the chosen card, each person must name at least one of the Bugs on that card (it can be a Bug different from the color of the die).

Once all players have revealed their cards if they won any, they will have to replenish as many cards from the Bugs deck to complete the 6 and roll the die again to continue playing.

—description from the designer (translated)

Nuestro jardín está lleno de deliciosos bicharracos, prepárense para comerse solo los bichos mááááás deliciosos que los dados les puedan dar. Escondan las manos debajo de sus mangas y sorprendan a esos incautos bichos.bichos mááááás deliciosos que los dados les puedan dar. Escondan las manos debajo de sus mangas y sorprendan a esos incautos bichos.

Objetivo del juego.
Conseguir la mayor cantidad de puntos antes que se acabe el mazo, intentando quedarse con sus Bichos favoritos para que les den más puntaje. Cada carta atrapada te dará un punto al final del juego, pero si consiguen cartas con sus bichos favoritos les puntuarán el doble. ¡¡¡Preparen sus mangas que llegó la hora de comer!!!
Todas las personas deben tener una de sus manos escondida bajo la manga de su abrigo y esperar a que alguien lance el dado. Cuando deje de rodar, cada persona sacará la mano de su manga y la colocará rápidamente, con su palma extendida sobre 1 de las 6 cartas sobre la mesa tapándola completamente. Aquella carta debe contener a un Bicho del mismo color de la cara superior del dado (solo puede haber una mano por carta). Si no hay cartas en la mesa que tenga aquel color, se colocará una séptima carta en la mesa y se lanzará el dado nuevamente (esto se repetirá hasta que coincida el color del dado con el color de uno de los Bichos de la mesa). Pero esto no es todo, antes de levantar la mano de la carta elegida, cada persona deberá nombrar por lo menos uno de los Bichos que esté en aquella carta (puede ser un Bicho distinto al del color del dado). 
Una vez que todas las personas hayan sacado su carta si es que la ganaron, tendrán que reponer tantas cartas del mazo de Bichos hasta completar las 6 y volverán a lanzar el dado para seguir jugando.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dragonbond: Lords of Vaala – Epics of Valerna
description:
This expansion includes an array of modules to increase player options:


     Bring narrative scenarios to Valerna.
     Develop your character with Epic Player boards.
     More miniatures for existing factions and Denizen cards
     A new creature to roam in Valerna


Narrative Scenarios:
At the beginning of the game, reveal 3 random scenarios that affect the regions of the board throughout the game. 
After every Event card is revealed, a stage card is triggered one of the scenarios. These greatly impact the decisions from all players.

Epic Player boards:
Your board has different slots for your Power tokens. Each grant a bonus or some sort of ability. From increasing your Combat Value, to permanently reducing the amount of Power you need to cast Vaala cards, and more! Customize the growth of your Dragon or play with a General that rely more on their own skills than on their army.

—description from the publisher

Booty on Deck is a swashbuckling board game where players take on the role of pirate captains, sailing the high seas in search of treasure and glory. The game combines strategic gameplay with real-time battles, challenging players to claim forts, sink rival ships, and bid for more resources at the Marauders Market.

Players must navigate treacherous waters and unpredictable events, such as the appearance of the Kraken or volcanic eruptions, all while managing their resources and crew. The goal is to accumulate 30 pieces of booty, which can be earned by claiming forts, plundering wrecks, and outwitting opponents in bidding wars.

With its blend of strategy, chance, and player interaction, Booty on Deck offers an exciting and immersive gaming experience that will have players on the edge of their seats until the final piece of treasure is claimed. Are you ready to set sail and become the most notorious pirate of the high seas?

—description from the designer

WITS (What Is This Sorcery) is an immersive digital trading card game (TCG) that brings together the classic appeal of card collecting with the dynamic interactivity of online gaming. Set against a backdrop of enchantment and strategy, WITS challenges players to master the art of magical combat, using a diverse array of cards that represent mythical creatures, arcane spells, and cunning tactics.

The heart of WITS lies in its strategic gameplay, where players build personalized decks from their collection of digital assets. Each card is a key to new strategies, offering unique abilities that can turn the tide of battle. Players engage in turn-based duels, where smart planning, anticipation of the opponent's moves, and adaptability are crucial to securing victory.

How to play? The unexpected child of Hearthstone, Yu-Gi Oh, MTG and other favourites. Turn based battle against your opponents Hero, getting them to 0 HP to win the game utilizing your deck of 40 cards crafted by oneself.

Each Physical card has chip technology embedded into the card itself allowing players to claim their physical cards in the digital game to compete and use in competitive game modes to become the #1 ranked player in the world. Digital Rewards versions of the cards will live online and enable players to earn rewards for every game that someone uses their digital rewards version card. EXAMPLE, User 1 owns card abc, User 2 collects card abc in the game and adds it to their competitive deck, User 2 wins the competitive game and earns ELO and rewards, some of those said rewards are given to the owner of the digital rewards card version.

Beyond the thrill of the duel, WITS aims to foster a strong community of enthusiasts. Players are encouraged to trade, collect, and compete in a vibrant ecosystem. The game rewards participation and achievement, offering players the chance to earn exclusive cards and other digital rewards through gameplay, tournaments, and special events. This system of rewards adds an extra layer of strategy, as players must decide how best to use their earned assets to enhance their decks or trade with others for the perfect strategic addition.

WITS is more than just a game; it's a continuously evolving adventure that grows with its community. With a focus on strategic depth, player ownership, and a rewarding ecosystem, WITS offers a rich and engaging experience that appeals to both competitive gamers and collectors alike, promising endless possibilities for exploration, strategy, and competition.

boardgame: Pokemon Pokemon Board Game Get Battle Adventure
description:
Get Pokémon and win in battle!
Mix and match the field according to the number of people and the time of play!
Choose your favorite field from the 4 fields and play alone or in combination!
There are 15 patterns in total.
You can play it in a short time, and you can also enjoy the mini game of battle games and evolution games.

How to Play
When you turn your turn, sprinkle the dice and proceed with the number of pieces you put out!
Go through the field and get the Pokemon Chips! There are 200 different Pokemon chips!
Pull "Battle" on your event card and battle with the collected Pokemon Chips!
You can also use item cards to power up.
When you get more than 20 points of the Pokemon chips you get, it's the last battle on the Rival Road!
Who can defeat their rivals first?

boardgame: FABFOUR Escape: Im Labyrinth des Minotaurus
description: Finally vacation in Greece, but there the 4 detectives get involved in a particularly exciting case. An ancient gold treasure has been discovered in the Minotaur's labyrinth, which a gang of grave robbers is now after. Will the FabFour be able to find the treasure and bring it to safety by dawn?

Now 2 money and at the end of the game 2 points

Originally planned as replacement art for the original Harem.

Choose your path and explore the ruins of the giant robot. Disable the traps and defeat the small enemy robots that come your way. 
If you manage to deactivate the evil AI before the time runs out you have won the game.

To play you need a pencil and 3 6-sided dice.
By exploring the map you can find bonuses to increase your Life Points and Skill Points, necessary to modify the results of the dice rolls.
When you encounter an enemy you have to roll the dice for both and compare the results to find out who won the duel. Whoever obtained the highest result wins. If you have special skills you can change the dice results in your favor.

By defeating enemies, you also collect resources that will allow you to access the otherwise inaccessible AI room.
Now you can always fight against the AI using the dice rolling mechanism. Use all your energy to win the duel and finish the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Fog: Escape from Paradise – Deluxe Edition
description:
In The FOG, you are mystical guardians of an isolated island who must help panicked islanders reach their boats and escape a rapidly approaching, life-threatening FOG.
Doing so will require guiding and coordinating islander paths toward their boats. Each turn, you use point allocation to run, swap, push, squeeze, cross, and jump your self-drafted islanders toward escape ... and survival! When the FOG reaches the shore, the boats depart and each player receives or loses points in several categories. The player with the most points defeats The FOG and wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Fog: Escape from Paradise – Uncertainty Expansion
description:
This expansion to The Fog enhances the excitement even more when playing. It consists of 60 cards in 5 decks.


    Guardians: You play one of the 6 available Guardians with a special ability (asynchronous)
    FOG Uncertainty: While before the FOG consistently advanced by 1 it now advances between 0 and 2 steps
    Unforeseen Events: In each round you will have to take into account an event happening when moving your islanders the best possible
    2 Mission Decks: You will have to fulfill a mission to achieve additional Rescue Points


—description from the publisher
A Monopoly version themed after the English football club Blackburn Rovers.

A Meal Time Game for Two Fussy Eaters

In Creature Crunch, players take turns to Feed, Roll, and Trade food until their fussy creature is full with their favourite food. You'll need to be quick, for there are other ravenous creatures about who just might eat your lunch!

A game of acting challenges. But this ain't charades! No hand gestures, no body movements, just a lot of silly faces.

Teams challenge each other to act out an action and emotion card, at the same time, using only their face. There are tactics at play as you choose the trickiest combination for your opponents.

In this adult edition the challenges are too hard, or too rude for children. So you might be find yourself performing an 'ecstatic bikini wax' face, or 'depressed with pubic lice'.

The aim of the game is to guess the most correct answers, and of course to have a good laugh at your friends' weird faces along the way.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Undaunted: Normandy – Promo Scenario 14: Night Owls
description: Late in the evening a squad of US soldiers on a reconnaissance mission deep in the Normandy countryside are cornered by an enemy patrol and pinned down inside a small farmhouse. Under sustained fire, the unit has taken multiple casualties, including their squad leader. The remainder of the American platoon have been tasked with relieving the embattled squad and taking control of the nearby village.

The German counterattack is in full swing. In their bid to reclaim the gains made by the Americans under Operation Cobra, they lay siege to a vital intelligence post outside Mortain. With no reinforcements within range to support them, the besieged US forces need to repel the assault so that vital intelligence doesn’t fall 
into German hands. 
This is a fictional scenario, designed by Carlos, the winner of our scenario contest.

boardgame: Undaunted: Normandy – Promo Scenario 17: Battle of San Pietro
description:
Battle of San Pietro Infine
San Pietro - 15 December 1943
Between December 8th and 17th 1943, the small Italian village of San Pietro Infine became a focus for the Italian Campaign of World War II, with the Germans to the North and the Allied forces, joined by Italian soldiers, to the South. The heavily fortified German ‘Winter Line’ ran through the town and was a significant obstacle for the Allies, as it protected an important highway and 
blocked their advance on Rome.
The ten day engagement began with a direct attack led by the Fifth Army. After securing the peak of Mount Sambúcaro, an assault on Mount Lungo was planned for the 15th of December. After four successive Allied attacks and German counterattacks, the German soldiers were forced to retreat to more defensive positions to the North. At 12:30 on December 16th, for the first time during the war, Italian and US flags were waving on top of Mount Lungo.

Embark on a riveting journey as an audacious seeker of fortune, aiming to outshine your rivals in the relentless pursuit of glory, treasure, and unparalleled fame. X: Seekers of Fortune invites you into a world brimming with daring quests and the allure of the unknown.

This strategy card game, designed with the essence of a trading card game (TCG) yet encompassing an all-in-one box experience, is meticulously crafted for two players, with adaptability for up to four intrepid adventurers. Dive into a realm where maps serve as your guide and whispers of lore lead the way. As you traverse this captivating landscape, you will unearth ancient relics, achieve feats of heroism, and unveil sites of legend.

At the heart of X: Seekers of Fortune, lies a competitive spirit where you and your adversary vie to be the first to complete five thrilling adventures. However, the journey is fraught with peril as you must also navigate the cunning strategies of your opponents, who are just as determined to impede your progress and claim victory for themselves.

With every card played, you're not just making a move; you're weaving a narrative of ambition, strategy, and exploration. Prepare to immerse yourself in a game where each decision could lead to triumph or tribulation. Whether you're a seasoned strategist or a newcomer to the realm of card games, X: Seekers of Fortune offers a richly rewarding experience that challenges your mind, sparks your imagination, and tests your resolve on the path to becoming the ultimate seeker of fortune.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Замес: Русы против ящеров (Smash Up: Lizards Must Die)
description:
The wicked Lizards have invaded our native land. It's time to show them who the real Rus' People are!

About the expansion Замес: Русы против ящеров ("Smash Up: Lizards Must Die"):
This box is full of heroic spirit, because 2 new factions collided inside it: the legendary Rus' People and the formidable Lizards! Yes, yes, you've definitely seen these guys somewhere, and now they're ready to come together in an uncompromising Smash Up with each other. Tip over a cup of Baikal water, and off you go!

Combine the Rus' People with the Bear Cavalry, and the Lizards with the Kaiju. Or make an unexpected alliance of the Lizards with the Rus' People and send them to fight other factions! Show your enemies how the slavonic clamp by balls is made!
—description from the publisher (translated)

•••
Ящеры нечестивые на землюшку родную вторглись. Самое время показать им, кто такие настоящие русы!

О дополнении:
От этой коробки так и пышет богатырским духом, ведь внутри неё столкнулись 2 новые фракции: легендарные русы и грозные ящеры! Да, да, вы точно где-то видели этих ребят, и вот теперь они готовы сойтись в бескомпромиссном "Замесе" друг с другом. Опрокиньте чарку водицы байкальской, и понеслась!

Объедините русов с казаками, а ящеров с кайдзю. Или заключите неожиданный союз ящеров с русами и отправьте их бороться с другими фракциями! Покажите своим недругам, как делается славянский зажим яйцами!

—описание от издателя

Dice Stackers is fast-paced dice stacking game for two players. Flip a card over and race to stack the dice as depicted on the card. Not only do players need to match the die’s color but they need to match the value of the die as well.

Key Features:


     Fast-paced
     Dexterity
     Competitive
     Simultaneous Play
     Add more dice to accommodate more players!


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Birdie Pro: Maple Hill Expansion Course
description:
Birdie Pro expansion: Disc Golf Destination: Maple Golds, Leicester, MA

Maple Hill is one of the premier disc golf courses in the United States and home of the MVP Open presented by OTB.

The course runs through the fields and woods of the Maple Hill Christmas Tree Farm. It uses elevation change, heavily wooded fairways, and iconic water hazards to create a magical and unique challenge. The Christmas tree nurseries that span the open fairways, make even the open shots a challenge.

Components: Two game boards.
Board 1: Holes 1-9
Board 2: Holes 10-18

boardgame: Birdie Pro: The Preserve Expansion Course
description:
Birdie Pro expansion: Disc Golf Destination: Timberwolf, Clearwater, MN.

Located directly on the banks of the Mississippi River, The Preserve is a challenging and fun track that plays through a vast, beautiful landscape. Five different bodies of water come into play requiring a solid variety of shots. The par 5 18th may be the most exciting finishing hole in disc golf!

You will need to focus to keep the disc in bounds. Be smart about when to utilize your skills and make sure to save a little for the signature 18th hole.

Components: Two game boards.
Board 1: Holes 1-9
Board 2: Holes 10-18

In the game 9 in a Net, players try to collect nine different types of bugs. Each bug card has a different number value, so that the higher the number the more points it is worth. There are seven different spots where bugs are placed in the center of the playing area. Each player has nine spaces in front of them that are different traps that can only be filled with bugs from specific spots. Players take turns collecting bugs, so they have as many different bugs with the best points possible. When one player has filled in all their traps the game ends and whoever has the most points wins.

—description from the designer

Introduction:Esgrad (from Scottish Gaelic: Sgaradh, meaning "partition") is a drawless game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty hexagonal board with triangles. The recommended board size is 7 points per side, but boards of 5 or 9 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     A partition of a group is each of the areas separated by the group that cannot be connected to each other except through said group.
     The value of a group is the number of partitions it contains based on triangular scoring, which is calculated by summing consecutive natural numbers from 1 to the number of partitions in the group.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, do one of the following actions:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point.
     Pass your turn.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass their turns consecutively. At the end of the game, points are counted by summing the value of all friendly groups, plus an extra half point to the player who passes his turn first.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player chooses an integer number as the komi value and then the second player chooses a side. This balancing method is called komi pie.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Hungarian Kingdom 1899 (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
Embark on a thrilling journey through the heart of Europe with this unofficial expansion that takes you back in time to the border of the 19th and 20th century, taking place in 1899.

Includes:

     Historical City Names
     7 Historical Regions of the Hungarian part of the Austro-Hungarian Monarchy + few foreign cities
     Passport Cards that are based on real life passports and include people that existed
     Tons of Fun!
Given a colored border of a rectangle, lay down square mosaic tiles with three different border colors themselves. Players who cannot place a tile are out. The last one standing wins.

Ecosfera Baltic Sea is a version of the Ecosfera game.

A cooperative game in which you must restore the Baltic Sea.

A version with faster games and more unpredictable scenarios that will put you on edge.

Jointly designed between Julibert Games and Helcom. a intergovernmental organization bridging policy and science on matters related to the environment of the Baltic Sea.

Each round, each player pays one point and forecasts the numbers of 6 dice thrown. The better you guess, the more you get points (up to 8). If you don't possess any points, you are out of the game. The player who reaches 64 points wins, if the game doesn't end earlier because the bank is bankrupt.
Except for the dice (plastic) and the rules (paper) all components are made of wood. The points are represented by colored sticks and are stored in holes of a personal table. You forecast by putting small dice into the large hollow of the table.
40-card Ecosfera Expansion. Face unpredictable situations that will change the rules of the game.

A collection of three expansions for SpaceShipped:


     The Dynasty Seal
     Debts and Damage
     Out of Route
An additional crew allowing you to play with five or more players (with the expansions).

Each player starts with either the black or white cards, and half the red cards. Play cards one at a time within a 5×5 grid to create of-a-kind sets or sequences of your colour. Sets do not have to be in a straight line, but must be connected.

When you play a red card, you can't score a set that turn.. red cards can be used by either player and are worth double the points!

Continue until all cards have been played, then count how many points you have in scored sets.

—description from the designer
family: Components: Dice Deck by We Heart Games

description:
A frantic and fast-paced game inspired by the likes of Dutch Blitz, Bananagrams, and Speed; Dice Blitz is a 2-player realtime game where both players play as fast as they can.

Add cards to a crossword-style grid where every row is a sequence of the same colour, and ever column is the same number of any colour.

Be the first to play all your cards to the grid to win!

—description from the designer
family: Components: Dice Deck by We Heart Games

description:
Two people or two teams will compete to get four cards (one per color).

Yellow cards will challenge something refered to music.
Black cards deal with foods.
Red cards invite you to talk about games.
Blue cards suggest something about sports.
Use the spinning top as a dice to know the color of the challenge.
publishers: Departament de Cultura. Generalitat de Catalunya

boardgame: Inventors of the South Tigris: Inventors 2024 Kickstarter Promo Tiles
description:
A set of new tiles for Inventors of the South Tigris, consisting of:


     Three research tiles
     Two pass tiles
     Four workshop tiles
family: Series: The South Tigris Trilogy (Garphill Games)

description:
Step into the world of Bubble Stories with two adventures where you are the hero!

Discover and explore 3 wonderful tales with Bubble Stories: Tales:

     Look for the characters
     By jumping from bubble to bubble
     Help them in their adventures to have wonderful dreams!


—description from the publisher

In Die drei ??? Labyrinth: Team Edition, the famous trio of investigators and a supporter work together against Victor Hugenay, who has struck Rocky Beach again and stolen the most popular cover paintings of the Die drei ?? cases.

Round by round, the players turn the pages of a Die drei ??? book to find out what obstacles Victor puts in their way. Can they manage to use their skills to move the walls of the labyrinth and find all the stolen paintings before the final page of the book is turned and Victor’s plans succeed?

—description from the designer

You don’t know what team you’re on, but you might be able to figure out from talking with others. Can you eliminate the players on the other teams and protect your teammates?

Each player draws a card, but can't look at their own card. Everyone has two minutes to ask questions of others to deduce their team (colour). When the time is up, everyone points at someone they want to eliminate!

Turn order is by number, lowest to highest. Once all eliminated players are removed from the game, count your team's score by adding up the pips on all remaining cards of your colour.
family: Components: Dice Deck by We Heart Games

boardgame: The Collector: A Dice Deck game
description:
Dig through an archeological site to uncover the bones for a new species of dinosaur!

In this tripeaks-style solitaire game, uncover and collect as many black and white cards as you can. If you get stuck, you can use a red card, but when the red cards are gone, the game ends! Can you collect every card?
family: Components: Dice Deck by We Heart Games

description:
Play as space truckers harvesting resources from asteroids across the galaxy and hauling them home. In an act of friendly competition, you challenge one another for who can deliver the most valuable cargo!

Use cards from your hand to move to upgrade your three harvesters; then play cards to move to asteroids of different colours, harvesting resources based on the efficiency of the harvester for that colour.

Resources are used to fulfill contracts of a variety of point values. Fulfill the most valuable contracts to win!

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dice Deck by We Heart Games

description:
Ofertu is a combination bidding + deckbuilding game; bid with limited resources to add red cards to your hand. On subsequent turns, bid using red cards to score the card you win as points, instead of adding it to your hand.

Ofertu is a light bluffing game for two, full of interesting mind games.

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dice Deck by We Heart Games

description:
Odyssey is a game that blends strategy with combat, where players must strategically weaken opponents' teams while planning the development of their own. The goal is to thwart rivals from completing the development of mythical weapons while successfully advancing their own weapon development in the game."

Odyssey: Legendary Arms is a tabletop game that combines luck and combat. With over 200 game cards and a competitive map, players collect coins and Vail crystals from the map. Coins can be used to purchase more game items, and collecting a specific number of Vail crystals allows players to upgrade their main character. By collecting specific Vail crystals and three designated attribute partners, players can win the game. It's a game suitable for playing with family and friends, with simple gameplay but endless variations in each game.

Gameplay: At the beginning of the game, draw 3 action crystals. Action crystals can be used to roll dice, move tokens, use action cards, and initiate battles. Players need to build their army from over 200 cards, with each weapon granting new abilities. Victory is achieved by assembling three designated stage 2 partners with specific attributes and collecting a specified number of Vail crystals from the map.

boardgame: Однажды в Чикаго 1930: Новые времена (Once upon a time in Chicago 1930: New times)
description:
New Times is an additional case to the detective game Once upon a Time in Chicago 1930. This case is based on a new game principle: two teams investigate it in parallel, competing with each other. You can also play with one team in the "brainstorming" mode.

Новые времена - это дополнительное дело к детективной игре Однажды в Чикаго 1930. Это дело основано на новом игровом принципе: две команды расследуют его параллельно, соревнуясь друг с другом. Можно играть и одной командой в режиме "мозгового штурма".

The deck of cards containing four special "Spin!" cards are laid out in a circle, then the players take turns spinning the bottle to select and draw a random card.
The players earn points by answering questions and performing challenges intended to build connection and intimacy between partners. 
Once all four "Spin!" cards are drawn, the player with the most points wins.

A heavy blizzard sits above the Outlands. Sudden snows and cruel winds change the landscape in the blink of an eye. Along with the cold comes new alchemical opportunities to explore.

FantasyForm: Frozen Over is a 6-card expansion for FantasyForm. Each multi-use card, like those found in the base game, adds multiple elements to the game. Master the new Ice Form, wear the Cloak of Fog, hire a Scout to help you navigate, and more. Some of the new Encounters also allow for limited manipulation of the Ethereal Row, giving players an additional factor to consider as they race to defeat their Rival!

CookieRun Braverse Trading Card Game is a trading card game based on the Korean mobile game IP CookieRun.
In this 2-player game, players use a 60-cards deck composed of Cookie cards, Item cards, Trap cards and Stage cards to compete against their opponent. The goal is to defeat your opponent's cookies. Each cookie card has a Level (1, 2, or 3) and a number of Health Points (usually 1 to 6). The cookie will faint if their HP is reduced to 0, and placed in their owner's Break area. When a player has cookies in their Break area whose combined levels are equal to 10 or more, they lose the game.

You know rock-paper-scissors from your sleep. You are quick with your fingers and know exactly which symbol you can use to beat your opponents. Your eagle eyes recognize the right colors in a flash and you form "paper" with your hand and win hands down.

But the game is not yet won. Can you survive all the rounds and take home the victory? You collect one card after the other, but it remains exciting until the end. You've never played this classic game like this before.

Experience the classic game in a whole new way! Rock-paper-scissors gets an update with this exciting game for on the go. If you already love the finger game, you won't be able to stop playing this version, because it gets really tricky here. Prove your speed and combine the symbols to beat your opponents.

Rock, Paper, Scissors contains 56 cards on which the three basic symbols of the game (rock, paper, scissors) are depicted in three different colors. In each round of the game, try to recognize the symbols of the same color as quickly as possible and then show the beating symbol with your fingers. If identical symbols are revealed, clap your hands quickly.

Word reached the spacestation Nexus-9 about a tiny planet called Earth. They have invented the one thing all lifeforms in the galaxy need. The five biggest corporations in the galaxy want the invention, and they want it first.

In Mission to Earth players play as one of five alien corporations trying to get to Earth first. Players have to buy three different upgrades for their spaceship in order to make it to Earth. Plus they need two pilots to fly through the tricky parts of space.

You mine, steal or trade crypto currency and you use this currency to gain the favour of pilots. But beware, the pilots are not the nicest people in the galaxy and they just might go with someone else. In the meantime try to hinder the other corporations by spying on them, seducing them or use "diplomacy" to sabotage their plans.

The first to have the three upgrades and two pilots on their side gets to Earth and wins the game.

—description from the publisher

A cooperative trivia game based on the UK's game show by the same name. Over a series of questions players try to guess the correct answers which are always numbers. Guess a number higher than the correct one and you are out of the game, guess too low and you're out of lives.

Welcome to the exciting world of animal care.
Gather food plus care plus medicine and get your animal. If the opponents fall on your animal, charge for the visit and collect victory points to win.
With luck and care, you will get them to breed and you will be able to collect more. But beware of hunters, traps, predators and the dreaded coronavirus that will slow you down or cause you to lose your winnings.

—description from the designer (translated)

2036, the American elections are approaching. Thanks to you, Mars Inc. has fallen. But another enemy awaits you: the Foundation. A tentacular organization that influences the highest spheres of the States.

Once again, Colonel Jackson calls upon your team to face this new challenge. Travel the world, investigate discreetly, and prepare for the worst. Humanity needs you!

Features:

     8 episodes of 30 minutes
     Two different endings
     A new career: the Corporatists
     Enhance your character with Nanotech cards


—description from the publisher

The Council is a standalone expansion for Mini Rogue. You can play it with or without the base game.

- If you don't own the base game: you can play The Council solo or in pair (competitively, with the Overlord module).
- If you own the base game: you can play The Council solo (with a much greater variety of cards), cooperatively (with two or three players), or competitively (with two, three or four players, thanks to the Overlord module).

In The Council, play as an adventurer who arrives in a doomed city, lost in the hands of a power-crazed regent, and his council who orchestrates dark manipulations in the shadows.

Wander the dark, randomly-generated alleyways, card after card, encounter ever more terrifying encounters, manage your resources and upgrade your skills by gaining levels to meet the greatest challenge of all: defeating the King's Remains!

All Council content is compatible with the Mini Rogue base game.

The game also includes a campaign, which follows on from the base game campaign (but can be played without having played the previous campaign).

5 new word cards to add to the game for more variety.
Pack found in Plato Magazine 163.

Trees, Trees, and more Trees! Create your dream arboretum with this expansion for Botany. Arm yourself with information to gain an edge over your fellow Botanists.

—description from the publisher

Poisonous, Carnivorous, Parasitic, and Bizarre Plants! Even more opportunities for sabotage and new plants to collect that have developed unique survival abilities.

—description from the publisher

House Climber is an original climbing/shedding game system.

There is a basic game for 3 players that operates as an introductory climbing game that is still satisfying for climbing game veterans.

Following the basic rules are a series of variant ways to play that apply the base game rules to different types of traditional climbing game. There is a bidding game, a partnership game, a social game, and other ways to play. Each game is intuitive and easy to learn once one has learned and mastered the basic game.

The basic gameplay is simple and standard for a climbing game. One player leads with a combination. The other players must follow with a higher version of that same combination, play a bomb (a special combination), or pass.

—description from the publisher

Urban Legend Night is a multiplayer cooperative card library building game.

The game is based on the production of a supernatural TV program. The game is composed of many segments, and the content of each segment is taken from classic supernatural program activities and well-known urban legends. In each segment, there will be several supernatural events, some of which are even accompanied by urban legends. When these supernatural events are triggered, they will bring negative effects to the field, such as weakening the character's mental value, giving the character  negative states, or eventually causing the character to fall into madness.

During the game, players can play topic cards to represent various topics on the show. Different topic cards have different effects, such as restoring mental value or drawing cards. The characters played by each player also has exclusive abilities that can help build their card library or remove effects. Players must also gather specific combinations of topic cards as quickly as possible to eliminate the supernatural events on the field.

The ultimate goal of the game is for all players to work together to eliminate the supernatural events and end the recording safely with everyone alive.
 —description from the publisher (translated)

Gameplay:
Everyone starts with equal chances of winning.  The better you can predict someone's card, the better your chances of winning!
Goal:
Be the player with the most points through collecting fish.
Difficulty:
If you can play a card, you can play the game!

To Play:
All players play a single card from their hand at the same time.
Fish that are duplicates get combined.
Fish chains get created and collected
Repeat till all cards are played.

boardgame: Stonespine Architects: Shrines and Fountains Mini Expansion
description:
Mini expansion containing 7 new chamber cards with new abilities.

Chamber types: Some chamber cards in this mini-expansion are both stone and cave type. During final scoring, each chamber card with multiple chamber types must be scored as either stone or cave, but not both.

Rules: Each card in this mini expansion contains a shrine or fountain at its center, which does not count towards the limit of four elements per chamber. Players may perform its effect once, immediately after placing the chamber.

Fountain: Move a market token or secret passage token to another chamber. Do not exceed four elements in a single chamber.

Shrine: Swap the position of and two chamber cards in the player's dungeon. Any market tokens on the card also move. Secret passage tokens do not.
Players get 1 of 4 scavenger hunt find lists, then spin and move through the charmkins playhouse to be the first to find all their items and win the game.

Blokopolis is a cooperative game for 1-4 players. Players construct blocks by placing cards on the table. Will you be able to successfully complete the blocks before running out of cards?

Oh, and don't forget to achieve additional challenging goals before the completion!

Once upon a time, passionate Fire, hardworking Earth, wise Water, and wayward Air spontaneously decided to build a kingdom. The Spontaneous Kingdom. However, the elements are very competitive—each wants to build the wealthiest part of the Kingdom. Join them in the battle for riches and influence!

Capitalia: Spontaneous Kingdom is a competitive family board game where each player takes on a role of one of the elements. The goal of the game is to be the first to earn 100 million.
There are 4 actions players will perform on their turns: move, visit, sell a building (optional), and discard (or build).
To move around the board, players will roll the dice and move their meeple the corresponding number of track spaces. When the movement is complete, a player must visit all the adjacent buildings around the hex where they stopped. To visit a building means applying the effect written on the building’s tile. When visiting Town Square, players trigger various events; be it a good or a bad event, players cannot refuse to apply it. There is also a Magic Tower that can help players with magic effects throughout the game. After performing the visit action, players may sell a building if they wish to and gain as many millions as stated in the current building’s price. Finally, players must choose between discarding a building from the market and getting the corresponding resources or constructing a building available in the market and spending resources for it.
To invent the best winning strategy, players must constantly analyze the situation on the board and decide when it’s time to buy and sell buildings. The game ends immediately when one of the players reaches 100 million in their supply.
It's time to become the most influential of the elements and the new ruler of the Spontaneous Kingdom!

La Reconquête de l'Empire Romain d'Orient (Reconquest of the Eastern Roman Empire)

Romanitas 528-628 is a Strategic level solitaire board-wargame simulating the history of the Eastern Roman Empire from 528 to 628 AD.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Payoff Pitch Baseball: 2022 Cards is a set of 1,500 cards — including players and parks — printed on high-gloss heavy cardstock for use with the Payoff Pitch Baseball base game.

L/R splits on batter cards as with all Payoff Pitch Baseball sets.

Shuffle is a play-and-replenish word game, where a player plays as many letter cards from the hand as they can and wish during their turn, and then draws cards to give themselves a 7-card hand. The deck has 72 cards including 2 jokers (wild cards).

A player's first play of the game must be a word of 4+ letters from the hand (junior players: 3+ letters). After that they may play words of 3+ letters (juniors: 2+ letters) from the hand, and they may also do the following: 

     Swap a joker with the letter that it stands for from your hand. 
     Take letters from words on the table (yours or other players'), while leaving each of them as a valid 3+ letter word, and adding at least as many cards from your hand to make a new valid word. 
     Steal an entire word from another player by adding at least as many letters from your hand to make a new valid word. The new letters may go anywhere in the new word, but the order of the previous word's letters may not be changed.
     Add letters from the hand to an existing word (yours or others') to make another valid word.


If you play all the cards in your hand in a turn, draw 7 more and take another turn. 
If you can do nothing with the cards in your hand, draw another card and pass.

After the deck runs out and one player plays all their cards or no one can do anything more, everyone adds up their score, which is the value of each card in your words on the table, plus bonus points based on the length of each word, minus the value of the cards left in your hand. The highest score wins.

Collect all four crown parts from each corner and bring them back to your Castle to form the whole crown.
The first player to form the crown wins.
Buy assets on squares to improve your chances of collecting the crown.
Set up land fields and units to expand your movements. Set up a market and let your workers generate income. Set up houses to get working pawns to generate coins when others move by.
Allow your strategic decisions to shape your game so that you can collect the crown before your opponents do.

—description from the publisher

In A Very Merry March Madness you'll use card values, special abilities, and bonus gifts to complete your bracket and help your team win the final game by the biggest margin! As a bonus, your completed game sheet can be used to fill out your actual March Madness bracket!

The game sheet features a tournament bracket, but unlike traditional brackets, you have much more control over the outcomes! Each matchup in the first two bracket columns is associated with a card suit. Each turn, you'll flip over two cards from the deck, and find a matchup with same suit as one of the cards. Then you'll choose a card value to write in one of the two boxes for that matchup. Numbers are limited, so you'll need to be strategic about when and how you use each number!

The numbers for each matchup are either added or subtracted and the total contributes to a power score for each suit/team. When all of the matchups are complete, the power scores are used to determine the bracket finalists and winner. Achieve the highest possible score by correctly picking the finalists and the winner, maximizing the win margin of the final game, and minimizing penalties.

—description from the designer

In this fast-paced family card game, you align sardines in your fish cans. Play wisely to create combos in order to outrun your opponents and prevent them from stealing fish in your open cans.

The winner is the first to close 3 fish cans, a can is complete when a lid is laid on its third sardine.

You are a company development team trying to finish projects on time. Assign team managers, developers, designers and other resources to get the work done. You have three weeks to succeed or fail.

You start out with 3 available tasks out of 8 needed to win the game. Players play clockwise around the table usually either by playing a card from their hand on a task, and thus fulfilling its conditions, or moving a task whose conditions have been met further down the production line. Most tasks need two cycles of fulfilment before they can be scored. Some cards can also be played to upgrade production line or as onetime effects. After everyone has played their one action, a new round is played. After fifteen rounds (three weeks) the game is over, ideally you fulfilled all your tasks, if not you score those you did for their point value. To increase difficulty, the downloadable app will assign random events at the start of some or all weeks.

The Perfect 10 Word Game is part of the Smethport Hip Hugger game series, with a foldable vinyl-covered metal board that can fit in your hip pocket, and magnetic pieces, which in this case are 98 8mm x 8mm letter tiles.

At the game start all the letters are placed face down, and players take turns revealing the letters one at a time. After revealing a letter, if a player can make a 2+ letter word with that letter and other visible unused letters, they take the letters involved and make the word on their part of the board. On your turn you can also take another player's word by combining it with one or more unused letters to make a new word, scrambling the letters if you want.

The first player to have 10 words wins.

Fast Blast is a game of speed and discard. Be the first to get rid of your cards to win the game. Everyone plays simultaneously with their 4 cards revealed. Discard your cards if they match the same color or number as the card in the center of the table.

Get ready for the ultimate race! Fast Blast is a game of speed that will get your heart racing! You'll need to hurry to outpace your opponents and be the first to get rid of all your cards. A game lasts only minutes, packed with sensations, ending with the glory of being first. Fast Blast stands out with its constant frenzy, no downtime; you won't have time to catch your breath.

—description from the publisher

Trigger Happy is a mix of Crazy Eights and Russian Roulette. Survive this game of chance by matching numbers or colors of cards. If you cannot match the cards then roll the die. If the die matches the card then you lose. It’s just that simple but be careful: trick shots, cards that can hinder other players, can be lethal. This game can last for ten minutes or even ten seconds; it depends on the luck of the draw and your wits. Be the last player standing to win.

—description from the designer

Base game is the same as Pachisi but with four different additions that change things up. Now you can protect your pieces from being eaten, you can teleport around the board, impede movement of figures and reroll.

Spella comes with 100 Mah Jong-type tiles that each have a letter of the English alphabet on the front. [The set could be used for any language that uses the Latin alphabet.] The game is set up and played like traditional Mah Jong: each player starts with a hand of 16 tiles; a turn consists of drawing a tile and discarding a tile; you can claim the recent discard if it helps you meld; you go out if you can meld all 17 tiles -- your hand and the tile you just drew.

The difference is that the melds are not sets and runs, but valid words of 2 or more letters each. When one player goes out, each player scores 1 point per letter in the valid words they can make. The winner (the one who went out) thus gets 17 points, but they also get 1 point per letter in their longest word. This bonus can be multiplied: 

     Clean Out: If the winner never drew a discard tile, their bonus is doubled. 
     Fast Out: If the winner goes out with the first drawn tile, their bonus is times five. 
     Sentence: If the winner's words make a valid sentence, their bonus is times ten. (In this case, the words "I" and "a" are allowed.) 


The winner is the player with the highest score after an agreed number of rounds, or the first to reach a target score.

Ten vowel tiles have small B on them, indicating they are Bound and must be played next to another vowel within their word.

If desired, a round's words can be limited to a certain part of speech (e.g. noun, verb, adjective), or a certain theme (e.g. sports, movies).

—user summary

boardgame: Reluctant Friends, Bitter Enemies: The Battle for Sicily 1943
description:
In Reluctant Friends, Bitter Enemies: The Battle for Sicily 1943, three players take on the roles of the Axis commander, the American commander, and the Commonwealth commander.

The objective is to either reach the port city of Messina first (as one of the Allies) or to preserve your forces to fight another day by escaping the island (as the Axis).

Can you, as the Commonwealth player beat the Americans to Messina? Or can you as the Axis player prevent the loss of territory and troops long enough to claim victory?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Limits of Glory: Santa Maura & Capri
description:
The third and forth campaigns in our Limits of Glory series are two games in one box. Each game is designed to be a stand-alone shorter game, playable in approximately one hour.  The games use the same unique Limits of Glory system, with the same Event clock and Combat system but on a smaller scale.

Campaign III in the Limits of Glory series looks at the attempt by the French and Neapolitans under their new King, Joachim Murat, to re-capture the island of Capri just off the Bay of Naples in 1808.

This game represents the two week period that covers Murat's invasion of Capri.  The game introduces weather effects for the first time in Limits of Glory, as weather was crucial to the outcome of the campaign. The French player must get their troops across the bay, land on Capri and take back the island by capturing Capri Town and Ana Capri. The British must hold on, making the French task as difficult as possible while trying to negotiate favourable surrender terms, hoping for windy weather to bring help from the Royal Navy and perhaps reinforcements.

Campaign IV in the Limits of Glory series looks at the British attempt to capture the Ionian island of Santa Maura, modern day Lefkada.

The game represents the four week campaign, starting on 21st March, by the British to capture the significant fortification of Santa Maura fortress and consequently, the entire island. Greek patriotism and the desire for independence played a major role in persuading the Albanians to desert the French and the game reflects this with the ability of the Greek commander Kolokotronis to entice Albanian units from neighbouring spaces to desert. Also, for the first time in Limits of Glory, the morale of Camus, the most senior French commander, plays a key role in the game. Once the British are adjacent to the fortress of Santa Maura, Camus must take a morale test following each assault or round of siege. This reflects his nerve breaking and him surrendering the fortress, even though the French forces were still able to resist. The French player must therefore do everything they can to keep the Allies away from the fortress by clever use of secret causeways for manoeuver and the considered positioning of shore batteries.

The games use the same single mechanic used in Bonaparte's Eastern Empire and Maida 1806, with the only difference being that each player has three dice rather than four. There are less events, the games are shorter, the maps are smaller but players face all the same difficulties in strategic decisions and balancing when to push and when to wait.

—description from the designer
