family: Series: Limits of Glory (Form Square Games)

boardgame: SolForge Fusion: Battle for Whitefang Pass
description:
In SolForge Fusion, all decks are algorithmically generated so that no two decks are alike. Take any two decks and shuffle them together, then you're ready to play. You and your opponent compete in strategic lane-based combat. When you play a card, it levels up, allowing you to access new powers and abilities to defeat your opponent and damage their creatures. The first player to bring their opponent down to zero health wins.

SolForge Fusion: Battle for Whitefang Pass features new Forgeborn abilities and over ten thousand new cards, including new card types like exalts and solbind, the latter being able to summon their counterparts from outside the game.

Word Challenge was designed by a teacher in remedial reading to be an aid to strengthening reading skills in students, but the games it includes are fun in their own right.

It includes a deck of 108 cards (104 letters and 4 wild cards), 100 plastic chips, and a rules book with rules for 16 different games, including:


     Word Challenge. A form of contract rummy. Each of the 7 hands requires a player to meld words of a certain size (from two 3-letter words to three 4-letter words) before laying off the rest of their hand. 
     Word Rummy. Normal rummy, where players draw, meld words and discard. When one player empties their hand all stop and score according to the length of the words they made. 
     Cincos. Each player deals out a 5x5 grid. For each row and column, the player who makes the longest word in their grid gets chips from the others. 
     Barter I. Each player has 5 cards and 10 chips. Until a player can spell a 5-letter word, cards are turned up 1 by 1 and players can bid on them with chips. 
     Read My Mind. In a turn, one spells a word and the next player tries to recreate it given its letters, getting points for how well they do. 
     The Chips are Down. A variant on Spoons.
     Memory Game. A variant on Concentration/Pelmanism, where players turn up 3 cards and try to spell a word of 3 or 2 letters. 
     Go Dig. Like Go Fish with word making. 
     Quick Trick. One player quickly reveals cards 1 at a time in a row; when anyone can spell a word from the revealed letters they call out the word, play pauses, and they collect the letters in that word. 
     Chipper Snitcher. Each player gets 5 cards, and estimates how long a word they'll be able to make after exchanging up to 3 cards. If the can spell a word that long they get its length in chips, unless another player can spell a longer word from those letters, in which case they get those chips.

The cards know your secrets, in this party-game version of Truth or Dare.

The game includes Fear cards featuring a variety of challenges designed to push the players' cringe levels to the limit. These cards are dealt face-down to the table.

Players then take turns drawing Truth cards and asking the personal questions to others in the group. If the person asked can't answer, or chooses not to answer, they can still earn the point by completing a random Fear challenge instead.

Guaranteed to get conversation going and laughter flowing, Truth or Fear is the awkward love child of the other popular CUT titles Truth or Drink and Fear Pong.

—description from the publisher
Stand alone card game, or expansion pack for Smart A** board game.  Where were you in the '80s? If you lived it, you know it!  Hear the clues and guess the people, places and things from this decade.

boardgame: Revenge: The Diplomacy Sandbox Card Game
description:
Players compete to be the sole survivor in a no-holds-barred series of power plays, standoffs, negotiations, and assassinations. Victory is gained by forcing other players out of cards and jealously guarding one’s own hand. Each player begins with a hand of cards each representing one of 15 different actions like stealing a card (Sword), countering/reversing effects (Mirror), or eliminating a player (Death). On a turn, players can play multiple of any single card effect before drawing to recuperate. Eliminating opponents is rewarded with a bonus turn.

This is accompanied with the legalization of every form of table talk including extortion, threats, bluffs, and the facedown gifting and exchange of cards (not always those agreed upon). The game is a diplomacy sandbox where player’s can creatively maneuver their opponents to their favor. Players have to make and break alliances and complete tense exchanges to gather the power they need to win.

At its core, Revenge is about outsmarting opponents through a mix of strategic card play and shrewd negotiation.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: To the Strongest!: Medieval Army List
description: This PDF contains 152 TtS! medieval army lists, including background information on the armies and images of painted wargames figures. The lists are mostly set in the period 550 CE to 1500 CE.

An exciting card game for all secret agents!  Covert mission is perfect for all fans of strategy games.  What's special: There are only zeros and ones cards.  On secret missions you have to keep your own cards hidden, while in nasty counter-missions cards from other players can be stolen.  The simple rules ensure a quick start to the game and let the game evening begin without complicated game rules!

Here's how it's played:

     There are only two different cards in the entire game: 0 and 1
     All players try to play certain combinations from these cards in front of them and thus win the game.  Multiple strategies lead to victory.
     If it is your turn, you may play a card from your hand face down in your display or reveal a card in your own display and try to steal a face down card in someone else's display.  But beware!  If you steal a 0, you have to pay dearly for it.
     The first person to reveal three more 1s than everyone else wins - unless someone else can steal the victory by revealing five 0s in their own display one after the other.


—description from the publisher (translated)

Ein spannendes Kartenspiel für alle Geheimagent*innen! Verdeckte Mission ist perfekt geeignet für alle Fans von Strategiespielen. Das Besondere: Es gibt nur Nullen und Einser Karten. Auf geheimer Mission gilt es, die eigenen Karten verdeckt zu halten, während in fiesen Gegenmissionen Karten von anderen Spieler*innen geklaut werden können. Die simplen Regeln sorgen für einen schnellen Spieleinstieg und lassen den Spieleabend ohne komplizierte Spielregeln beginnen!

So wird's gespielt:

     Im ganzen Spiel gibt es nur zwei unterschiedliche Karten: 0 und 1
     Alle Spieler*innen versuchen, aus diesen Karten bestimmte Kombinationen vor sich auszuspieeln und so das Spiel zu gewinnen. Mehrere Strategien führen zum Sieg.
     Wer am Zug ist, darf eine Handkarte verdeckt in seine Auslage ausspielen oder eine Karte in der eigenen Auslage aufdecken und versuchen, eine verdeckte Karte in einer fremden Auslage zu klauen. Aber Vorsicht! Klaut man eine 0, muss man teuer dafür bezahlen.
     Wer zuerst drei 1en mehr aufgedeckt hat als alle anderen, gewinnt - es sei denn, jemand anders kann den Sieg stibitzen, indem sie*er nacheinander fünf 0en in der eigenen Auslage aufdeckt.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dungeons & Dragons Onslaught: OP Kit – Store Support: Skeletal Warriors and Spectral Warriors
description:
The Dungeons & Dragons Onslaught: Store Support Kit Skeletal Warriros and Spectral Warriors includes incredible prizes for in-store tournaments for Dungeons & Dragons Onslaught! Each kit is designed for 8 players, including a Skeleton Warrior mini and foil Skeleton card for each participant, and 4 special Spectral Warrior minis for the top finishers.

The kit includes 4 unique sculpts each in 2 different versions, creating 8 unique minis for players to collect! Each sculpt is a version of a favorite character from the original four Onslaught factions! It includes 2 of each in the Skeleton Warrior version, and 1 of each in the Spectral Warrior version.

Players can use their Skeleton and Spectral Warrior miniatures to upgrade the tokens in the Tendrils of the Lichen Lich Starter Set and other Onslaught products!

These Store Support Kits provide prizing and incentives for new and exsiting Onslaught players to participate in events and play in your stores! If you've never bought an Onslaught store kit before, this is a great place to start!

This expansion contains 10 cards. Only one card chosen at random is used during each game. Each card grants different bonuses to the player who loses the battle. The bonus depends on difference between battle power of the winner and battle power of the loser.

Each player is trying to be the best Vtuber out of all the players.

First, players attract a crowd and bid on their activities, which would earn them a spot on the shared schedule board. By the end of the game, players receive points based on who occupied the most time slots on the schedule board. Be careful not to get cancelled, or you will not receive new crowds for a turn.

boardgame: Similo: History – Yi Sun-Sin Promo Card
description:
Promo card at Spiel '23.

Yi Sun-Sin
Text: Korean admiral who heroically defended his country in the Imjin War (1545-1598).

boardgame: Similo: History – Heo Nanseolheon Promo Card
description:
Promo card at Spiel '23.

Heo Nanseolheon
Text: Korean painter and poet of the Joseon Dynasty. She left poems in Hangul and Chinese verse (1563-1589).

Promo card given out at SPIEL 2023.

Rules:
This card can be earned by any person that scores 21 points or more, while taming dragons.
The dragon treasure is worth 12 points at the games end.

boardgame: Million Dollars But... The Game: Anime Pack
description:
Million Dollars, But... The Game Anime Booster Pack comes with 30 new cards to either play standalone, or combine with your core game to expand the fun.

Million Dollars, But... The Game (based on Rooster Teeth's popular comedy series, Million Dollars, But.) is a card game that puts your morals and imagination to the test, posing the question, "What would you do for a million dollars?"

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Million Dollars But... The Game: Aussie Pack
description: Million Dollars, But... The Aussie Expansion Pack comes with 100 new cards to either play standalone, or combine with your core game to expand the fun. 75 new gold rule cards, 25 new black trigger cards, and one game rules card for standalone play. Each card is labelled with a kangaroo symbol for easy sorting.

Unicron, the Planet Eater, threatens the multiverse! One by one, planets fall prey to the mighty Chaos Bringer, who gains more and more power as he consumes or destroys everything in his path. Both Autobots and Decepticons must put aside their differences to face this doom together and stop it, before Cybertron itself is devoured!

In this new expansion for the Transformers Deck-Building Game, you can play the likes of Rodimus Prime, Wreck-Gar, and Galvatron, and join forces against the all-powerful Unicron in two exciting modes of play: Co-op and One versus Many! Automata-Unicron gives up to 4 players a menacing threat to overcome in a fully cooperative experience, while player-Unicron comes with his own set of dangerous Starter cards and a massive Character card, ready to take on all comers in an epic One versus Many battle!

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – Rivals: Martial Law
description:
The Martial creed are masters of the equipment needed to hunt monsters. Their skill with guns, explosives, vehicles, and even drones are second to none. They will need all this and more when they also have to face off against the vampires that skulk about in the shadows.

Vampire: The Masquerade – Rivals: Martial Law includes a pre-constructed Martial deck, as well as new vampires and cards for the six latest clans: The Banu Haqim, Caitiff, The Ministry, Ravnos, Salubri, and Tzimisce.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Power Rangers Deck-Building Game: It's Morphin' Time
description:
A relic of millennia ago, Cyclopsis is a tool of war created by Rita and rebuilt by Lokar for one purpose: to destroy the Rangers! The War Zord, piloted by Goldar, serves as the flagship of Rita's army and rallies her forces to battle the Rangers.

But as evil Masters gather in the Power Rangers Deck-Building Game: It’s Morphin' Time expansion, the Rangers are enabled to meet force with force, bringing in more Zords to combat their foes. Will the combination of Cyclopsis and the Masters overwhelm the Rangers, or will the presence of additional Zords tip the scales in the favor of good?

—description from the publisher

Dungeon War is a game for 1-4 players, set in an expanding dungeon. Each player controls a group of heroes searching for treasure, and a band of monsters to attack their opponents. The winner of the game is the player who has accumulated the most treasure at the end of the game.

The game ends when there is only one player in control of all the Hero and/or Monster Tokens in play, or when the last Dungeon Card is drawn. On a Turn when the last Dungeon Card is drawn, play continues until it is the First Player’s Turn again.

When a game comes to an end each player determines how much treasure they have found in the dungeon by counting up the value of their Treasure Tokens, and any unused Relic Cards they have. Then, they add up all the  treasure value of each Monster and Hero Token they still have in play. The player with the highest treasure value has won the game.

Dungeon War also has a solo mode.

—description from the designer

Two players take turns placing stones on empty spaces of an initially empty hexagonal grid of hexagons, one stone per turn. There must never be a stone adjacent to six other stones on the board. You win if, after your placement, there is an empty space whence the edge of the board cannot be reached through a series of steps onto adjacent empty spaces. The empty area including that space is then said to be looped.

If you play several rounds, note after each round the size of the looped area and add it to the winner's running score. Whoever reaches a predetermined score first wins the match. The loser of one round plays first in the next.

Like all impartial games, Luup is exquisitely boring. To make it partisan (if you must), fill a looped area with markers of the looper's color and keep playing until there are no more legal moves available. If you loop an area, you must make another move on the same turn. Whoever has more markers in the final position wins.

As an alternative to the standard placement restriction, one may forbid non-perimeter placements that complete minimal triangles of stones around a vertex of the grid. This is a bit less elegant but possibly more fun and accessible. Loops are guaranteed in both cases.

—description from the designer

boardgame: My Little Pony: Adventures in Equestria Deck-Building Game – Scholarly Shenanigans Expansion
description:
Put on your thinking caps and line up your quills because it's time for a new curriculum for My Little Pony: Adventures in Equestria!

The new cards included in the Scholarly Shenanigans expansion will open up new adventures in My Little Pony, adding more locations, situations, hurdles, and challenges, while also introducing Twilight Sparkle's School of Friendship. This new set of locations will help you expand your horizons and spark your imagination. What's the first lesson? Befriending these six spirited students: Gallus, Ocellus, Sandbar, Silverstream, Smolder, and Yona!

—description from the publisher

Symptomatic is a light, card-based game about serious diseases. From Chicken Pox to the Bubonic Plague, getting sick has never been so fun.

Manage your cards and combine various symptoms to infect others with deadly diseases, while utilizing cures to protect yourself. However, be careful, as cures can have consequences too! The goal is to be the healthiest player in the group when the first player dies. The diseases in game are based on real life ailments, and require specific symptoms to infect another player. Assemble your hand to give a set of symptoms to your opponent, and watch out for the sets your opponents give you! Use both your real world pathology knowledge and your in-game strategy to dish out more diseases than you take.

Symptomatic, much like real life, requires critical thinking, strategy, and a bit of luck to stay healthy as everyone else suffers. Featuring plenty of brightly colored original art and goofy puns, Symptomatic is the mix of disease, decisions and dad jokes you never knew you needed. 

—description from the designer

Get ready for an electrifying, time-traveling escapade with Sharjah: The Board Game! Be transported to the bustling heart of Sharjah's Central Souq in 1978, where the race is on to become the ultimate merchant tycoon! Dive into a cunning strategy and high-stakes objective gave that thrusts you into the race to be Sharjah's wealthiest merchant. On the illustrated game board, Sharjah's Central Souq comes alive, beckoning players to harness dazzling Dirhams Cards, deploy game-changing Action Cards, and pursue glory with rewarding Objective Cards, crafting their unparalleled trading empire. Will you rise above the competition to become the richest merchant in Sharjah?

Players acquire, position, and remove stores, trying to dominate the souk board. Once the last empty souk space has been filled, the game ends and points are tallied for completing objectives and buying stores.  The person with the highest number of points wins - but ties can also happen.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Ancient Shadows: The Shaman in the Demon Village
description:
The young shaman must face dangers of all kinds in the village of NewCastle overrun by demons. The search for magical items to activate the powerful talisman is crucial, and time is running out. A map-based, dice and pencil exploration adventure.

How to play
The game is a "roll and write and click" (the device is an integral part of the game, along with the use of physical dice and the printed map). Each level involves exploring a map of the demon-infested village, where essential items for casting spells, opening doors and fighting demons are hidden. For each room, we have to roll the dice and choose which action to take, between opening a chest, taking an item, facing a pitfall and breaking down doors.
The fights are the most interesting part, as we have to compare our dice roll with that of the device that represents the demons. To make things even more complicated, there are 3 levels of flooding for each map, which will force us to use the freeze points we have been given wisely. A challenge in which the device sets the exit point for each game and determines where to hide magic items and demons, making infinite combinations possible. We win when we have found all the items that make up the powerful talisman and chased the demons out of the village forever. There are different endings and different combinations of objects, chosen each time by the device. A unique adventure, but always different.

—description from the designer

Step into the fast-paced world of coffee culture with Expresso, a thrilling card game for 2-5 players where speed and strategy brew the perfect blend of excitement. In Expresso, players race against each other to fulfill coffee orders and become the ultimate barista.

Set in a bustling café, players take on the role of skilled baristas, vying to complete orders before their opponents. Each turn, players have 2 options, Play a Card OR Guess a Card. Every card you play gives out information about your hand, allowing other players to deduce and steal cards from your hand. Play cards strategically and carefully because only the most efficient and cunning barista will claim victory.

Expresso features a unique blend of card mechanics, including hand management, set collection, and a dash of luck. With a variety of ingredients each sporting their own unique rules, this game is a fast, fun, and fresh experience.

Are you ready to take on the challenge and race to caffeinated glory in Expresso?

—description from the publisher

One night, a superstitious teen named Bert Hudson ventured a bit too far into Brightview's dark past. As he stumbled across strange markings in the forest, Bert inadvertently broke a seal from an ancient Elder Tree that would awaken a demon known as the Whisperer.

The Brightview Haunting is a one versus many cooperative horror game. Play as either Exorcists, working together to save Bert and banish the Whisperer, or as the Whisperer, tracking them down to spread corruption and attempting to possess them all.

Experience asymmetric gameplay as each Exorcist utilizes different passive actions, and the Whisperer uses a set of unique abilities that get upgraded as the game progresses. As Exorcists, trigger random Events through key actions like invoking ritual items, or using corrupted artifacts.  Determine when to use special items, manage your corruption, and strategically move around the map to outwit the Whisperer. As the Whisperer, possessing Exorcists turn them against their allies. When this happens, the game becomes team based, and a possessed Exorcist coordinates with the Whisperer. Any Exorcist who gets possessed during the game is able to be saved in different ways, in which they'd switch back to their original team.

Will you become vessels for the demon or banish the Whisperer back into the Elder Tree for good?

—description from the publisher

Welcome to your first day at our innovative and exciting recruitment agency, explicitly tailored to our hardworking rat colleagues. As new employees, your mission is to assist our fellow rats in finding their calling in diverse industries—each with its own unique appeal. From the bustling sector of food scraps to the rustling sector of leaves and yard waste, you will guide your recruits toward their dream jobs in one of our esteemed trash-related industries. As you navigate this dynamic workplace, always remember that at our agency, every rat has the potential to become the Big Cheese!

In Rat Race, you’ll assume the role of a recruiter who tries to put your rats in the best position to succeed. Expand industries by building the trash pile. Assign new recruits to entry-level jobs. Encourage them to climb the corporate ladder (to earn a bonus when they become the Big Cheese). Or undermine the competition by reaching full employment before your opponents reach their full potential. You can win either way.

Rat Race is a highly interactive game with simple rules that yield a complex and dynamic game space. Filled with strategic and tactical depth, this game might leave your brain feeling like Swiss cheese.

—description from the publisher

Players race tokens from their base and must successfully navigate to the centre and back on an ever-changing board. The first player to successfully reach their base again wins! Cards determine actions that can be taken on their or their opponent's tokens and tiles to help, block or hinder progress.

—description from the publisher

Continuing the Tales of Danger story, First Footholds picks up with the initial villages established on the uninhabited archipelago discovered in the first game in the series.

Players take on the roles of village leaders, each building a village on their own island. On each turn, they will assign the people of their village to gather building materials and food, provide defense, build buildings, store supplies, and respond to events. At the end of each turn, new people arrive to help for the next turn.

The game takes place over 6 turns, spread across a Prelude and 3 Acts, with game features added and removed for each Act. Points are scored on buildings, events, supplies, and a secret scoring goal for each player, and the player with the most points wins.

Gameplay is centered around worker placement and resource production and spending. It is playable solo, and also with 2 to 5 players.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Land of Danger (Matt Worden Games)

description:
In Oh My Goods!, first released as Royal Goods, players are European artisans during the Middle Ages who produce tools, barrels, glass windows, and many other goods. Only if you make clever use of your production chains will you have the most victory points at the end of the game.

This fine card game was complemented by two editions, Oh My Goods!: Longsdale in Revolt (2016) and Oh My Goods!: Escape to Canyon Brook (2017). All three are part of Lookout Games' #greatgamesinsmallboxes series.

For the very first time, all the good-ness of Oh My Goods! series wrapped in one small big box.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
DIMOI games are crafted to encourage conversation among players, making them perfect for discovering, rediscovering, or simply sharing memories with loved ones.

The Couples edition allows you to (re)discover yourselves as a couple, to nurture your relationship, and to cultivate your bond.

This card game offers a chance to take a break from connected devices, allowing for sharing, introspection, and heartfelt exchanges.

With questions designed to deepen intimacy and provoke reflection, laughter, desire... Each question serves a purpose!

On average, we spend nearly 5 hours a day in front of a screen. What if we break the routine? In an era where new technologies and daily stress dominate our lives.

It's on these values and desires for connection that DIMOI Couples Edition was born, the favorite couples' game in France.

Crafted to spark engaging discussions, our questions help you to better understand each other and enjoy moments together filled with closeness, laughter, and tenderness.

DIMOI aims to strengthen our relationships and return to the essentials: authentic exchanges.

A set 10mm Sci-Fi miniatures wargames rules for Mech combat.

The rules have been design for futuristic combat between war machines like Mechs (or Mecha) and Supersize Tanks, which you can add your own storyline too.  Miniatures are available from a number of manufactures.

The game uses a points allocating system, which you placed counters on to a card. which are allotted to a system, which allows you to move, shoot evade missiles etc.

You will also need dice (D6s), three types of colour counters, enough for all players, damage markers for the infantry and print out enough Elements Cards for all Mechs and AFVs.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Race & Write is a dynamic filler racing game. In just 15 minutes, you will experience the full excitement of a high-stakes race, making it the perfect game for those short on time but looking for a quick adrenaline rush! It will give you the unpredictability of racing with the need for tactical thinking.

Navigate through a diverse track full of twists, turns, and unexpected surprises. You’ll face a series of challenges and unknowns, making each playthrough unique and exciting. Game is prepared for 1-5 players in mind.

Each round lasts five turns.  During each turn, players choose their gear (based on their position in the race) and move using speed points.  Bonuses are  available at various points of the track. After 5 turns, a new round begins with a fresh gear box.  First car across the finish line, wins!

Buckle up for a high-octane experience, start your engines, and may the best driver win!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: City of Spies: Enigma Promo Card
description:
A Promo Card to be used with "City of Spies: Estoril 1942".

At the start of the 1st Round, the Enigma card is placed in a random Reward Space face-up. All cards in this Location must be face-up at all times (including the Reward Card).

The player who wins the Reward Card for this Location also wins the Enigma Card and places it next to him or her.

At the Start of each Round after the Reward Cards are placed the player who controls the Enigma card MUST place it in a Reward Space of his or her choice. If no player wins this card, place it in the next Round in the same way it was done in the 1st Round. At the end of the game, this card awards 2VP to the player who owns it.

Professional Baseball Card Game DREAM ORDER is a one-on-one competitive trading card game with a baseball theme!
Become a manager, appoint players, play against each other with your own "Dream Order", and enjoy collecting cards of your favorite players!
The center of the game is the battle between the pitcher and batter!
Predict your opponent's actions and win the game!
Players gather their favorite players to form a "dream order" and play a game.
Become a manager, appoint players, decide the batting order, and win with your own "Dream Order"!

—description from the publisher (translated)

「プロ野球カードゲーム DREAM ORDER」は、野球をテーマにした1対1の対戦型トレーディングカードゲーム！
監督となって選手を起用し、自分だけの「ドリームオーダー」で対戦したり、好きな選手のカードをコレクションしたりして楽しもう！
ゲームの中心は投手（ピッチャー）と打者（バッター）の勝負！
相手の行動を予測して、ゲームに勝利しよう！
プレイヤーは好きな選手を集めて「ドリームオーダー」を組んでゲーム（試合）を行います。
監督となって選手を起用し、打順を決めて、自分だけの「ドリームオーダー」で勝利をつかもう！

—description from the publisher

boardgame: STAVKA: HQ Operations on the Eastern Front
description:
STAVKA is a 2-player wargame, played on a very high-level (only 40 hexes) map of the East Front. It focuses heavily on Reserve Management.

The game uses dice to represent Infantry armies and counters for  Tank armies, Air assets, Cavalry corps and Security Divisions. Its major innovation is the absence of a movement phase - advance after combat is the only way the frontline moves. Demonstrating a functioning frontline is mandatory in the game; failing to do so at the end of a turn leads to your opponent's victory!

Playable in a single evening.

—description from the designer

Welcome to the Insectarium, a museum of insects! You are tasked with creating a board of insect specimens for display in the Insectarium's main hall. You must use your gear, gather insect specimens, and collect tools to precisely arrange and pin your specimens in your display.

On your turn, you will choose a card and token from the yard and pin an insect specimen to your board. Precisely pin your insects, collect display tools for bonuses, and swap tokens for gear abilities.

During the game, each player will take exactly nine turns to choose specimens and gear, and pin cards to their display board. At the end of the game, each row and column of specimens will be scored based on type and color categories.

Will you have the most precise specimen display that will be chosen for the Insectarium?

—description from the publisher

Quiltable: Paper Quilts is a thinky game where players simultaneously compete in a grand quilting challenge to design the highest scoring quilt! Points are scored for completing quilt patterns, quickly finishing your quilt, and designing a symmetrical quilt. Completing rows and columns in a quilt unlock Beeline Bonuses that grant players special abilities and enable valuable dice mitigation. Will you focus on filling your quilt, claiming valuable patterns, or racing for the most valuable bonuses?

As all players use the same dice rolls to take each action, you cannot rely on luck to help you win.

The game is a PDF file that can be printed on a standard sheet of paper, either A4 or Letter size. Rules are available on a second page so that each copy of the game can be printed double sided with the game on one side and the instructions on the other. All you need to play is a pencil or pen and two standard dice! For players without a printer, the game can also be played digitally on a computer or tablet using a JPG file.

The game can be played with one or more players, and is infinitely scalable.

The solo game comes with three levels of difficulty as you face off against master quilters with increasing levels of skill.

How will you ensure that the quilt you plan is the most Quiltable?

—description from the publisher

It's time to collect the loot and wake the hoot! Take turns checking in with the owl to see how he's feeling while racing around the forest gathering tokens that match your cards. Move your mouse around the board until you reach a stump. Then gently press down on the owl's head to see what you do next. If the owl is feeling happy, then you continue your turn toward your loot. But if he wakes up grumpy, you cannot move forward, and your turn is over. Don't forget about that fast track — avoid the owl if you can! When the first player returns to the starting space with all their loot, they win!

—description from the publisher

This Board Game/Puzzle is built on Plato's Cave Allegory and the diagram from Carlo Cipolla's essay "The Basic Laws of Human Stupidity".

It is about gaining experience of the human nature, becoming a better person, to then escape the cave and become enlightened. In the process, each player must help others to become “enlightened enough” and accumulate an accepted number of Brain-Cells to also gain applied intelligence. That way, everyone becomes efficiently useful, for the whole society to benefit.

This game was not created to target or to mock people with the words Nifty, Stupid, Egoist, Warm-Hearted, Super-Genius or King of Fools or other stereotypes. The behaviors have been already experienced by us (especially by the creator of this game) and by playing Plato’s Cave, we get to experience them again to think critically and creatively.

The IQ scale used in this game is a playful one and has nothing to do with other (subjective) IQ scales used around the world.

And in contrast to some other games, where we are taught to harm others in order to win, in this game we win by helping others.

Experience and knowledge are combined to become intelligence.

The game is played by 2 to 4 Players, while there is also a 1-Player Puzzle.

—description from the designer

In February 1942, the German Navy prepared a top-secret mission, known as Operation Cerberus, to move the battle cruisers Scharnhorst and Gneisenau (known to British sailors as the “Wicked Sisters”) and heavy cruiser Prinz Eugen from Brest in western France to Norway. Without special training operations or any other moves that might alert the British, they would suddenly dart up the narrow English Channel. The daring “Channel Dash” caught the British by surprise, and all three ships made it to German though Gneisenau suffered damage that would never be fully repaired.

As a cover story for the preparations, the German Navy put about the rumor that the three ships would head south, around the Cape of Good Hope and across the Indian Ocean to join the Japanese in the Dutch East Indies. Crews were told of this objective, tropical helmets and uniforms were loaded, and rumors spread through the French dock workers.

Wicked Sisters is a Second World War at Sea Campaign Study making this deception a reality. It’s a set of thirteen scenarios tracing this cruise that never happened. The German squadron is pretty much on its own until they reach safe harbor. The Royal Navy has no intention of letting them get there, and the U.S. Navy has no intention of allowing them to survive.

You’ll need Bismarck Second Edition or Playbook Edition, South Pacific, Horn of Africa, Eastern Fleet and Java Sea to play the scenarios.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Vampire: The Masquerade – CHAPTERS: Epilogue Expansion
description: The Epilogue Expansion adds 8 new chapters to the story, after the events of the main game. The story adapts to the choices you've made, testing your ability to persuade and lead. It brings three new clans into play (Tzimisce, Ravnos, and Salubri) expanding your strategic options.

The ramshackle descendants of a once-great civilization seek to prove themselves worthy of their ancestry. By uncovering the lost knowledge of this world's spirit-powered technology, they hope to follow their forebearers deeper into the depths.

Standing in their way: the darkness. Exposure is forbidden, and those touched by it are cast out forever. Preserving them is the safety of the Slab - a massive stone construct imbued with spirit fire - the last refuge of light against the corrupting dark. Equipped with spirit lanterns, the people search the ruins for the understanding they seek.

Of late, a mysterious voice has been heard echoing up from below, drawing people into the dark, never to return. What lies beyond the security of The Slab, and what strange secrets does the darkness hold?

Every realm comes with 9 resealable realm packs, 1 blister pack of cards, 7 PVC dividers, and a dual function display box.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Spirit Fire: Hidden Depths Pack Bundle
description:
This bundle includes a display box and 7 10-card character packs to add variety to the 12 character packs included in the character box. Use them to master new disciplines and earn advanced spark of life cards with triggered abilities.

—description from the publisher

Personal quests are standalone storylines and quest chains that can be added to your character's campaign. Each unfolds across two 10-card packs that will take you to the far corners of the world, solving mysteries, overcoming challenges, making connections, and discovering things long-thought lost.

This set features four quest lines centered around enigmatic creatures who, if you play your cards right, may become stalwart companions.

Includes: one display box and 8 resealable packs (each personal quest is 2 packs filled with story quests and character development rewards)

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Spirit Fire: Winds of Wisdom Quest Bundle
description:
Personal quests are standalone story arcs that are independent of those that can be discovered in specific realms. These quests travel with you, regardless of what realm you are in. Each personal quest consists of two 10-card packs filled with tasks, objectives, and rewards, some of which will ask you to seek answers down paths that may by unfamiliar, or even dangerous.

This set features four quest lines centered around seeking wisdom in the unassuming people around you, the obscure places you might find yourself, and the pursuits that drive you from within.

Includes: 1 display box and 8 resealable pack (each personal quest is 2 packs filled with story quests and character development rewards)

Quests: The Wisdom of Instinct, The Wisdom of Experience, The Wisdom of Culture, The Wisdom of Music

—description from the publisher
A complete crew representing India and its will for self-determination of its people. This was a KS Exclusive additional crew allowing you to play with five or more players (with the expansions).
Put avocado on every dish to earn extra points.

A hexagon tile based game with more than a dozen upgradable characters, monsters that you can tame and then some really hard monsters that will tame you!

Just shuffle and deal the cards each time for a different game experience.

In Treasure Hunters, all you need to do to win is find the treasure.  But that means traveling through the safer plains to the much harder caves and not dying along the way!

boardgame: North Africa '40: The 1940 Expansion to North Africa '41
description:
The legend continues with North Africa ’40, the prequel expansion to North Africa ’41. This expansion covers September 1940 to December 1941. Scenarios cover both Operazione E (the Italian invasion of Egypt; 12 turns) and the Allied counter-offensive, Operation Compass (6 turns). Both scenarios can be extended through December 1941 for an Operazione E full campaign (32 turns) or an Operation Compass full campaign (26 turns).

Can you as the Allied commander bag the Italian 10th Army? Will you be ready for theater whiplash, the Middle East Command operational shift to Greece in February 1941? Will you be prepared for the entry of Rommel and the German panzers?

Can you as the Italian commander find an answer for the enemy heavy armor, the Matilda tanks? Will you find solutions to the grave errors your historical counterpart made? Will you have the coastal road open for the arrival of Rommel and the DAK blitzkrieg to the Suez Canal?

The answers to those questions you will find in a journey filled with tension, excitement, and rich World War 2 history!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: God of War: The Board Game
description:
God of War: The Board Game is a co-operative dungeon crawler filled with epic fights and puzzles for players who will encounter various iconic monsters in the form of highly detailed miniatures. The game also features a unique combat and leveling system based on the bonding of characters and co-operation. Take on the role of Kratos and Atreus, and follow a series of adventures that imagine scenarios during the years between the 2018 God of War video game and 2022's God of War: Ragnarök when Fimbulwinter descends on Midgard.

To play God of War, players must first choose a Scenario and follow any setup instructions and exceptions described. The game is played through a series of Rounds. Each round is separated into 2 Game Phases, starting with the Hero Phase and followed by the Enemy Phase. Once both phases are complete, a new Round begins.

To win any Scenario, unless otherwise specified, players will need to complete the Scenario Objective and kill the Boss. To complete the Scenario Objective, players will have to complete the Scenario Quest Tokens for each Scenario that are placed during the setup or have specified rules for it on the special rules of each Scenario (see Scenario book).

The game is lost if either Hero has its life reduced to 0 or if the Tracker Cube reaches the end of the Menace Track.

—description from the publisher

Introduction:Dehil (from Irish: Deighilt, meaning "partition") is a drawless game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty hexagonal board with triangles. The recommended board size is 7 points per side, but boards of 5 or 9 are also valid. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a set of stones of the same color connected to each other. A single stone is also a group.
     A partition of a group is each of the areas separated by a group that cannot be connected to each other except through said group.
     The value of a group is the number of stones it contains.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, do one of the following actions:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point. You can create a triangle pattern if exactly one of its stones is on the edge of the board and the rest or all of them (not on the edge) are adjacent to (i) a friendly stone that is not part of a triangle pattern or to (ii) a center stone of an hourglass pattern.
     Pass your turn.


It should be noted that the above restrictions apply to all triangle patterns.

				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass their turns consecutively. At the end of the game, points are counted by adding the value of all friendly groups with partitions minus the value of all friendly groups without partitions, plus an extra half point to the player who passed his turn first.

To ensure fairness, before the game begins, the first player chooses an integer number as the komi value and then the second player chooses the sides. This balancing method is called komi pie.

—description from the designer

Wave right! Wave left! Put your fan up and rumble rump!!

8-Legged Peacock is quick draft-and -match card game about the mating dance of Maratus spiders, a.k.a., peacock spiders.

Match male dance move cards to the color and moves of your Maratus female card. Each round, players draft eight cards, then score points depending on symbols and colors. Mate tokens are awarded for getting specific dance combos. After a set number of rounds, points are added up and bonus points are awarded for collected colors and sets of mate tokens.

—description from the publisher

Each player tries to be the first to reach the destination, the palace, with their camel caravan, whereby their water supply (water cards) must not be used up. If you run out of water cards, you die of thirst and have to return to the start. The winner is the player who gets both camels to the finish area first.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Jeder Spieler versucht, mit seiner Kamelkarawane als erster das Ziel, den Palast, zu erreichen, wobei seine Wasservorräte (Wasserkarten) nicht verbraucht werden dürfen. Wer keine Wasserkarten mehr hat ist verdurstet und muss zum Start zurück. Gewonnen hat der Spieler, der zuerst beide Kamele ins Zielfeld führt.

—description from the publisher
This expansion contains 14 cards. These cards allow other players to support one of the parties who are in battle right now (something like betting). It is an alternative way to get victory points.

A trading card game based on famous Japanese Anime/Manga Detective Conan as 30th anniversary of Detective Conan series.
In this game, players use their own partner card and character cards to collect evidence to solve event. By reasoning and action, the player who collect enough evidence to solve the event will win the game.

When considering the current state of our politics, what can you do but laugh? Love 2 Hate: Politics is here to help! This 108 card expansion adds a slew of new sentence Starter and Finisher cards to the game. Mix them in with your standard Love 2 Hate cards or play the expansion on its own for a themed experience. You may hate politics, but you'll love this expansion!

This expansion includes an expansion rule book, a new scenario with set up aids and a set of new counters to play the scenario. The scenario is called Erasing the Bulge and starts with the Ger Dec 30AM turn and ends with the allied Jan 4 PM turn. This also comes with a double sided players aid with rules and optional rules on 1 side and a battle map and Order of Battle on the other side.

Come ye young men, come along
With your music, dance and song
Bring your lasses in your hands
For ’tis that which love commands
Then to the Maypole haste away
For ’tis now our holiday
Then to the Maypole haste away
