boardgame: Weather Report (ウェザーリポート)
description:
You are a shaman in a distant village. The village chief entrusted you to forecasting the weather on the day of an important ceremony. But you don't have any precognitive abilities. However, you hold in your hand a cursed talisman that can control the weather. Use that power to adjust the weather to your forecast!

Weather Report is a game where you manipulate the weather to make your own weather forecasts successful. Weather forecasts are made every round and the player who gets it right gets points. During a round, players play weather cards to change the weather. Read the forecasts of others and guide the weather to your own forecast.

Repeat playing rounds until a player scores a total of 10 points and wins.

—description from the publisher (translated)

In a round of Weather Report, players will start by selecting a card from their hand to be their forecast. On subsequent turns, players will either play a card to adjust the weather at 1 of 3 altars or will redraw their hand to get different cards. Each card played will advance the date closer to the ceremony date by 1, 2, or 3 steps. Certain dates on the calendar trigger events which allow the player to take additional actions to influence the outcome.

When the date marker reaches the ceremony date, a specific altar site is determined by drawing the next card from the deck. The goal for each player is to match their forecast card to the weather at the selected altar.  All players who correctly forecasted the weather score points. The number of points earned depends on the value of the other players' forecasts and the number of players who correctly forecasted the weather.

Rounds are repeated until a player scores 10 points. The first to do so wins the game.

—user summary

Escape from Camp Creepwood is a cooperative horror board game for 3-6 players.

You and your friends take control of campers, each with their own strengths and weaknesses, as they attempt to scare each other around the campfire. The scares quickly become real as the cursed grounds bring the horrifying stories to life.

Now, tasked with a set of objectives in one of 20+ unique scenarios, you must fight to survive. Exploring the camp, you'll flip tiles to build out the map, ensuring the grounds are never the same upon each playthrough. You'll stumble upon items that may help or hinder you and events that will leave you making real choices, each with their own results and tests. But keep your wits about you, if your stats are depleted... you will never make it out alive.

Will you live to tell the tale... can you Escape from Camp Creepwood?

—description from the publisher

The battle continues in Burger Battle 2! With all new burgers and battle cards, do whatever it takes to build your burger first, because everyone is out to take you down! Add ingredients, take them away or even blow your opponents burger up in this sequel to the hit strategy & sabotage card game!

Play Burger Battle 2 as a standalone game or as an expansion to the original Burger Battle!

To start the game, players will receive 1 of 8 different Burgers, each with different ingredients. To win, you must build your Burger to completion while keeping other players from building theirs.

During your turn, you will play an ingredient for your Burger and draw a card; This repeats until you can no longer play. Once your turn is finished, you can either discard a card or play a Battle Card.

Battle Cards add and take away ingredients, protect your Burger or blow other players' Burgers up. Some can be as simple as switching hands others can be as devastating as destroying all the Burgers on the table, including yours.

—description from the designer

How much do you really know about the Bible? Play the National Bible Quiz and find out how contemporary the Bible is.

Playfully, you'll find out all the time!

The .RAT (Regime of Authoritarian Tetrarchs - the New World's government), has created the ultimate weapon - A Killswitch in case the world's A.i. gets out of hand, aptly named ZeroDay.exe. Unfortunately, the .RAT also doesn't think too far in advance. Their precious A.i. learned of this weapon and were...angry, to say the least. The A.i. decided to take control of the program and have started targeting our bio-cybernetics (cybers). At first, they only targeted the .RAT and those close to it here in Shijima - the capital city - so us commoners didn't care much about it - hell, some were even happy about it. But recently there have been attacks against anyone the A.i. deems a threat…which is basically every human with cybers. We didn’t want (or try) to get caught up in this fight, but here we are. Now, we’re on the A.i.’s shortlist, so the only thing left to do is infiltrate their databanks and destroy them before they get to us. And I guess if we save some lives, that’s a plus

Take on the role of one of 8 unique characters and fight to reclaim the city of Shijima in this Cyberpunk-syle, cooperative Rogue-lite dungeon crawler. On your turn, you will have 2 action points to spend on a variety of different actions. Game mechanics include: Deterministic Combat, Hand Management, Deck Building, Character specific actions (Programs), AI Threat level, Initiative track, and Strategic positioning.

Play 4 ways:

OP Mode - Play a random Operation. Upgrade your character and draft your deck throughout the Operation for endless replayability. (1-4hr)
Boss Mode - Draft a deck and customize your character before taking on a Boss! (1-2 hr)
OP + Boss Mode - Play a random Operation and use the upgrades you earn throughout the Operation to take on a Boss. (2-6 hrs)
Campaign Mode - Play through all 15 Operations over multiple sessions, upgrading your character along the using Virtu cards to streamline your deck into a more curated set of cards. (20-40+ hrs)

Are you looking to add more choices to games like Heroquest or other dungeon-crawler titles.  Look no further than MegaQuest.  This is a rework of the original Megaquest Hero Pack.  The first version was rough and needed some rework.  Megaquest Hero Pack 2.0 has all-new art and rebalancing of most Skills, Spells, and Paths.  This has been a major passion project for me so I hope you enjoy these cards as much as I enjoyed making and playing with them.

Purchasing the cards provides the following:    
15 new classes each with their Unique Traits.    
10 New skills with each having 6 different modifications.    
20 new spells with each having 6 different modifications.

PolyPlots is an abstract strategy game and brainteaser for 1-4 players, aged 5+. Using 13-sided polygon tiles called polykites, players align and connect tiles to claim territory with coins matching simple math outcomes.
The theme is growing fruits on triangular plots of land. Coins represent fruit crops of varying values.

WOODEN LASERCUT COMPONENTS 

     50 polykite tiles
     80 coins (4 colored sets of 20)
     2 dice.


GOAL:
Score the highest points by claiming plots.
    
SETUP:

     Shuffle the tiles and place face down between the players.
     Give each player 20 coins of the same colour.
     Each player takes one tile placing it face up.
     Place a random starter tile between the players.


GAME PLAY:
Playing in turns, a player drops one of their tiles in an attempt to form a triangular shape of any color. If a triangle is formed, the player rolls the dice and forms an equation from the two numbers. The equation dictates which coin to place on the plot.
Alternatively, the coin can be placed on a territory claimed by another player, but only if the equation is higher than the value of the coin already in place.
Play until all tiles have been laid.

Claiming a Plot (Triangle):
To claim a plot, place the coin matching the equation on any complete triangle. The triangle does not need to match your color, however, all sections of the triangle must be of the same color.
Stacking Coins to Steal a Plot:
You may stack a coin on another coin of any color as long as the new coin’s value is higher than the top coin.

Equations (Outcomes):
Upon rolling the dice, a player chooses the outcome of a mathematical equation from the two numbers. Only 20 outcomes are possible between 1-36. The outcome or solution is from addition, subtraction, multiplication or division of the two numbers. For example, rolling 3 and 5 can yield 2, 8 or 15 from subtraction, addition or multiplication of the two numbers.

STRATEGY:
Try to claim an important triangle using the highest mathematical outcome.  Knowing that a player automatically gets 10 points for each triangle matching their color, you can forfeit a plot by placing a coin on a differently colored triangle.
 
SCORING & WINNING:
Each player automatically gets 10 points for each triangle matching their color. Add up the visible coins of the same color. Total the two numbers to get a final score. 
The player with the highest score wins. In case of a tie, the player with the most plots wins. If still a tie, the player with the highest coins wins.

SOLO GAME PLAY:
This mode requires you to solve a brainteaser by creating as many perfect triangles of the same color as possible using all 50 tiles.

—description from the designer

Towers of Ra! immerses you and up to eight players in a mythic struggle to regain control over the god of Chaos, Apophis and aid the fallen god of the Sun, Ra. As the gods of ancient Egypt, you wield unique abilities to shape gameplay and tactics in this cooperative experience. Your ultimate goal: building a magnificent tower to Ra, granting him the strength to ascend once more and thwart the collapse of creation.

Drawing on elements of dexterity, cooperation, and unwavering resolve, you’ll utilise statues, temples, and card components to build a structure capable of supporting all four of Ra’s temples. But beware: should a single component falter, Apophis claims victory, plunging the world into darkness.

Can you and your fellow deities aid Ra in time to preserve existence itself?

—description from the designer

Make delicious food at home — in a playful way! — in Homefarming: Das Spiel.

Based on an idea from best-selling author Judith Rakers, this game challenges players to cultivate their own little garden. After three garden years, that is, three rounds of play, players tally their points: Who harvested the most and who used the space in their garden most cleverly? Who had the pests and seasonal effects under control? And who has collected some chicken friends to bring extra points?

The cards provide helpful information not only for playing the game, but also for growing a real garden.

boardgame: Star Wars: Box Busters – Rebels Speeder Bike Chase
description:
Ezra Bridger commandeers his Speeder Bike as imperial troops give chase. In the process he meets his future comrades of the Rebellion.

Star Wars: Box Busters is a series of small plastic dioramas. The dioramas fold into a small plastic cube known as a Battle Cube. Each player needs their own Battle Cube to play.

The basic game uses a simple battle-dice mechanic. Advanced rules incorporate a Battle Cube's unique special abilities.

This stone maze is rumored to contain untold riches, but only the bravest heroes dare to delve here, for it is guarded by the dreaded minotaur. Navigate the twisting corridors. Evade the labyrinth’s traps and guardian. Gather what treasure you can. Employ all of your cunning and you may survive to see the other side.

Encounters are single maps for Warriors & Writings. They can be played with any hero from across the Warriors & Writings line. This is not a stand-alone product. A core set of Warriors & Writings is required to play.

Encounters are intended to break the mold of what you might expect from Warriors & Writings. Encounter 1: The Labyrinth features an alternate qualifying condition. Instead of having a boss enemy that must be defeated, in order to qualify for scoring on this map you must end a round on the space marked “End”. When you do so, you have finished playing, even if there are rounds remaining. In addition, The Labyrinth features a roving enemy that will pursue you through the maze.

—description from the publisher

Super Battle Mon is a TCG inspired game, no table needed. It is for 1 to 4 players where players battle using just 7 card decks with battles that last just 2 minutes.

Your goal in each battle is to play more of your Mons than your opponents. Use your Mons' abilities and strength to gain the advantage and take-home victory!

—description from the designer

Embark on an epic journey in Mythorifts: Clash of Gods, a strategy fantasy card game where players harness the power of Mythologic Gods and Mythical Creatures. Construct a deck featuring Mythologic Gods(Avatar), Legendary Creatures, and potent Spells. Use Divine Essence to summon creatures, cast spells, and activate powerful abilities.

Two players face each other in this Clash of Gods.
Construct a 40/50-card deck featuring Mythology Gods, Legendary Creatures, and potent spells. Use Divine Essence to summon creatures, cast spells, and activate powerful abilities. Each element — Fire, Water, Lightning, Earth, Light, and Darkness— brings unique playstyles, from aggressive fire tactics to strategic water control. Engage in turn-based combat and unleashing synergies for victory.

In Mythorifts: Clash of Gods, to learn to play, players construct decks comprising cards aligned with one elemental affinities of your chosen Mythologic God(Avatar), each offering distinct play styles. With a 40/50 cards per deck, players strategically select creatures, spells, and legendary cards to craft synergistic combinations and execute their chosen strategy. In the advanced rules you can choose 2 Mythologic Gods(Avatar) even of different elements and build the deck (40/50 cards per deck) with cards from the elements of your Gods.

During gameplay, players take turns drawing cards, summoning creatures, casting spells to outmaneuver their opponents. Each card requires Divine Essence, a precious resource, to play or activate its abilities. Players must manage Divine Essence carefully to maximize their plays and maintain control of the battlefield.

Creatures engage in combat in turn-based sequences, their forces to attack enemies or defend against incoming assaults. Abilities, keywords, and synergies further enhance creature interactions, allowing for dynamic and strategic gameplay.

Legendary creatures and Gods wield immense power, offering unique abilities and effects that can turn the tide of battle. Mythologic Gods (Avatar) provide a active skills and an ability “Wrath of Gods” who can unleash his power once he reaches his required rage charge, further shaping the course of the game.

Victory conditions from reducing the opponent's Mythologic God(Avatar) health to 0. With diverse elemental affinities, synergies, and strategic depth, each game of Mythorifts: Clash of Gods offers dynamic and engaging gameplay, where careful planning and decisive actions lead to triumph.

—description from the publisher

Have you ever wished you had a Dragon as a pet? Now it’s possible! You can breed your own Dragons and compete to be the one to train the toughest and most valuable specimens. Watch out for dragon eggs in your rolls, but take a chance on your final move to get the best creature!

In Dragon Clans, you can be the one who manages to breed the most difficult Dragons. The more difficult your Dragons are, the more points you will earn. Be careful with the Dragon Eggs, but take risks in your final move to get the best creature.

Players can roll dice up to 3 times per turn. After each roll, they can choose to keep some dice and reroll the rest, but once dice are kept, they can't be rerolled in that round. After three rolls or if they're satisfied with the results, they pick the best combination of Eggs to get a Dragon from the table. If they fail, they return a Dragon to the table. To get a Dragon, players need to collect at least 4 Eggs shown on the card.

The game ends if there aren't enough Dragons to replenish the centre or if a player reaches a set number of Dragons. The winner has the highest score, based on Barriers held, points for Dragons, and extra points for evolutions. If tied, the player with the most Dragons wins. If still tied, it's a shared victory.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Most Frustrating Fight: The Battle of First Newbury, September 20, 1643
description:
A Most Frustrating Fight is the second game in Roberto Chiavini's series of games set during the English Civil War. The game is very easy to learn/teach and true to the history.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: English Civil War (High Flying Dice)

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Order Battletome – Daughters of Khaine
description:
Order Battletome: Daughters of Khaine is the guide to fielding an army of the Daughters of Khaine for Age of Sigmar.

This book contains a miniatures showcase illustrating colour schemes and painting advice, plus examples of painted models. It also features rules, battleplans and warscrolls for fielding your Daughters of Khaine as an army in games of Age of Sigmar.

boardgame: Atlas Lost: Rise of the New Sovereigns – Campaign Expansion: The Lost Nation
description:
Campaign Expansion: The Lost Nation is an expansion that allows you to enjoy 3 unique campaigns.

Each campaign consists of 5 games, where you'll compete for victory. Each game within a campaign is called an "episode" and revolves around the various artefacts from the past, bringing you closer to the cause that led to the collapse of this world.

The 5 expansion modules that appear in the campaign can be freely combined and played with the base game of Atlas Lost.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Death Battletome – Flesh-Eater Courts (Second Edition)
description:
Battletome: Flesh-Eater Courts is the  guide to fielding a Flesh-Eater army for Age of Sigmar.

This book contains a miniatures showcase illustrating colour schemes and painting advice, plus examples of painted models. It also features rules for fielding your Flesh-Eater army in games of Age of Sigmar.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Atlas Lost: Rise of the New Sovereigns – Ability Expansion: In-Depth Research Team
description: Ability Expansion: In-Depth Research Team includes two types of special tokens, Armor and all-terrain vehicles, that enhance your existing advance team tokens.  Special abilities are added to the advance teams sent by the factions. Develop deeper strategies using abilities that vary among the players

Let's journey through the great American Interstates!

Interstates Wonderland is an engaging board game that takes you on an adventurous road trip across the United States, where your aim is to make the most memories by exploring destinations via the iconic interstate highways. The game is designed for 2-5 players, each representing a traveler embarking on a memorable vacation.

The game board represents a map of the USA; various states connected by interstates. Players start their journey from a designated starting point and navigate the vast network of interstates that crisscross the country. Along the way, they explore the country and challenges that mirror the real-life experiences of a road trip.

The objective of the game is to explore and collect the most memories along the way. Players advance their vehicles along the interstates by drawing destination & interstate cards, strategically planning their routes to maximize their memories.

Throughout the game, players must overcome obstacles such as traffic jams, detours, and conditions that will challenge them to rethink their strategies. Cleverly use interstate cards to gain advantages and outmaneuver their opponents.

As players traverse the interstates they will learn quirky facts about the destination states and interstates written on the cards. They have the opportunity to explore and make memories for each destination they visit. Additionally, they can interact with other travelers, forming alliances or competing for resources to enhance their journey.

The game concludes after a year or two year road trip. The player who has explored and strategically accumulated the most memories emerges as the winner, having had the most fulfilling and memorable road trip experience.

Interstates Wonderland offers a unique blend of strategy, luck, and exploration, providing players with an immersive and entertaining gaming experience that captures the excitement and adventure of a cross-country road trip through the USA.

—description from the publisher

Come play our original creation - Shape-A-Palooza - a fun, fast-paced, family friendly card game where players vie to collect card crafted from basic geometric building blocks like triangles, rectangles, hexagons, and circles. The aim of the game is simple: strategically collect the cards which increases the potential of your hand, and play your hand at the time of maximum impact. The game ends when an end card in the deck shows up, the player with the most points wins. Get ready for a whirlwind of strategic thinking, swift decision-making, and thrilling competition!

Game Contents:
8 Shape Cards - two each of Triangle, Rectangle, Hexagon & Circle
1 End Card 
Many Thing Cards & Shape Shift Cards

—description from the publisher

Prowl is a compact area control game designed for 2 to 4 players, where each participant vies for dominance over territory with their tiger companion.

In this immersive game, players embody tigers navigating the wilderness, strategically exploring, hunting for sustenance, marking terrain, and initiating challenges to assert their dominance. As you navigate this dynamic landscape, you'll need to safeguard your territory against rival tigers while seizing opportunities for expansion.

Beware, however, as rival tigers are always poised to challenge for coveted spots, ensuring constant engagement and tension. The survival of the fittest hinges on your ability to outmaneuver opponents and secure the most advantageous land.

Ultimately, victory is determined by accumulating points from controlling valuable territory, ensuring your tiger's supremacy and survival in the unforgiving wilderness.

—description from the publisher

In Alliance of Wizards: Nether Portal, fate has dealt a dire hand as a monstrous gateway to the Nether Realm cracks open, unleashing a tide of vicious demons, know as Infernals, set to engulf our world in chaos. In this desperate hour, you and your fellow wizards, once rivals, now find yourselves bound by necessity, forging an uneasy alliance to stem the demonic onslaught and safeguard humanity’s future.

This gripping fantasy-themed deck-building game combines cooperative gameplay with tactical combat elements. Designed for 1-6 players, Alliance of Wizards: Nether Portal offers a riveting experience lasting 60-90 minutes. At the outset, players wield a basic arsenal of spells, gradually enhancing their decks as they progress. Spells serve various purposes, from decimating enemy forces to conjuring minions and bestowing potent enchantments upon artifacts.

Your objective: navigate the treacherous combat track, advancing your wizard unit to the heart of the battlefield where you will cast a powerful incantation to seal the portal. Yet, with each step forward, Infernals surge forth relentlessly, testing your mettle at every turn. Every decision matters as you strategize to outmaneuver the demonic hordes and reach your goal.

Success hinges on teamwork and resourcefulness. Coordinate your efforts with fellow wizards, pooling your magical prowess to overcome daunting odds. But beware—time is of the essence. Fail to close the portal before it reaches critical mass, and all hope is lost, condemned to face an eternity under the infernal yoke.

With its immersive theme, dynamic gameplay, and nail-biting tension, Alliance of Wizards: Nether Portal offers an unforgettable gaming experience. Will you rise to the challenge and seal the portal, or succumb to the relentless tide of darkness? The fate of humanity hangs in the balance.

—description from the publisher

In the card game Compile, you are competing Artificial Intelligences trying to understand the world around you. Two players select three Protocols each to test. Concepts ranging from Darkness to Water are pitted against each other to reach ultimate understanding. Play cards into your Protocols' command lines to breach the threshold and defeat your opponent to Compile. First to Compile all three Protocols grasps those concepts to win the game.

Control your opponent's Protocols with card actions, Compile your own as fast as possible, and Compile your reality.

Compile: Aux 1 includes three new Protocols: Love, Hate, and Apathy.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In the depths of the ocean lies a world in which coral gardens flourish. These underwater paradises, teeming with life and color, captivate anyone lucky enough to witness their beauty...but that beauty is in danger of fading forever. Reef architects brave the depths of the oceans to protect and cultivate the growth of a once-great coral ecosystem.

In Coral Gardens, players take turns drafting coral discs to populate different coral tiles. Then based on the undersea currents at the start of their turns, players place these tiles into the underwater garden. At the end of the game, players receive points based on restoration goals and private objectives that have helped to repopulate the reef. The player with the most points wins!

—description from the publisher
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description: Nothing Personal is a game that will allow you to learn a lot about your friends, look at yourself through their eyes, and have a lot of fun in the process. The rules are simple: take a card, read out the question and vote for the person who seems to you the most likely candidate. And then you count the votes - if the majority of players voted the same as you, you get a winning point!

Flagships is a competitive card game with a unique asymmetric twist. There is no deckbuilding, but players will pick from a wide variety of playstyles. Players must weigh the odds in a battle before fully committing their cards. There are two ways to win: militarily by having the higher strength over your opponent, or economically by scoring 5 points through various card effects.

—description from the designer

boardgame: One Sheep, Two Sheep, Many Sheep
description:
One Sheep, Two Sheep, Many Sheep is a family card game about herding sheep and pigs.
Animals are arriving all the time and have to be herded to the right field.
Some need pigs, some need sheep and some, with the farmer, can have a mixture of creatures. As soon as the exact number of creatures are in the field, the gate is closed and you collect all the field and animal cards.
At the end of the game the player with the most cards collected will win.
Along the way you can change the number of animals in a field or change the type of animal being put in the field.

Hordes & Heroes Ancients and Medieval is designed as a fast play tabletop historical wargame, which uses hexagon marked terrain as a precise and effective method for moving troops. The rule system is simple and easy to learn, yet sophisticated enough to allow “realistic“ battles to be fought between a diverse range of ancient and medieval armies. It is easy to play, but more difficult to master.

—description from the publisher

The Jungle is about to get wild with Leafy Lanterns, Furry Flutes, Coconut Coolers, Forest Fries, and more! Collect as many party cards as you can - the fancier your party, the more likely you are to charm the animal guests to attend! Plan, strategize, and compete - the host with the most (points) wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Get the jungle animals to attend your party rather than other players' parties. Each animal card is worth 2 to 7 points, depending on the combination of party cards needed to claim that animal. Squirrel DJ figures can be claimed with a set of 4 party cards that are all the same type.

Players can conduct up to 3 actions per turn, with the goal of putting more party cards into play. Party cards function as resources to match the combo specified on an animal card. This mechanism is reminiscent of Splendor and Happy City's bonus cards. In this deck 29 of 100 cards are played for special actions, which often let the player take cards from other player(s), add points directly to their party, designate someone to lose a turn, etc.

A simple filler that incorporates play mechanisms like resource set collection and card/action combos.

—user summary

Five fellow gamers, The Collector, The Sore loser, The Strategist, The Stickler and Lady Luck, are invited to the game night. Play the role of a Comics creator and follow the adventures, on their way to the favorite hobby activity.

In this game, Players will create their own Comics Chapters, with 9 scenes.

The cards, they will use, are part of the five parallel story line (indicated by the story line icons), and their chronological order is indicated by a number (1-15.

Players’ objective is to create a Chapter with as many as possible scenes from the same story line. Additionally, their Chapter will score better if they will use cards with suitable numbers.

—description from the designerç

A website and show exclusive set of 12 cards to play with D-Day Dice.

This set includes 2 Legendary Units for D-Day Dice: The Forgers and The Knights, including new Specialists and items.

Fancy playing as the King of the Britons or throwing a Holy Hand Grenade...you can now!

—description from the publisher

Play a card from your hand, and place a chip on a corresponding space on the game board – when you have four in a row, it’s a SEQUENCE.
Each player or team tries to score the required number of four-card SEQUENCES before his opponent.
The first player or team who gets two sequence, is the winner.

In this tile draft and placement game, up to 3 players take the roll of companies competing to design the best transportation network.

Fulfill the requirements of public authorities, connect people, cities and industries but don't forget sustainability. Forest and lakes will provide you with extra reputation points.

Build the best transportation network and beat your competitors in this thrilling abstract game.

—description from the designer

CRACK THE CODE: Be the first to figure out the cleverly coded phrase depicted on the card and shout it out for the win. Cards include hints and trivia for an extra bit of fun.
GUESS THE HIDDEN PHRASE: A judge holds a card up so only they can see the answer. Outsmart the rest of the group with your brain teaser puzzle solving skills to get to three cards first for the win!
BRAIN GAME FOR PARTIES: Take a guess at answers that range from tame teasers to very adult phrases. Minimum two players, for ages 18+.
SPIN MASTER GAMES: We have coffee table ready board games for adults, board games for kids 8-12, 1000 piece puzzle for adults, family games, yard games, game room decor, & your fave brands!
BOARD GAMES FOR FAMILY NIGHT: For your next games for family game night, bring one of our adult games, couples games, family games, party games, 500 piece puzzles for adults, games for kids ages 4-8, or card games for adults.
Includes 300 cards, instruction sheet

—description from the publisher

Vitawaasi warriors use their knowledge of the Savannah to lay traps to slow enemies and long ranged attacks to finish them off, while dangerous predators and nightmarish horrors seek out wounded prey.

The Vitawaasi expansion faction is the 10th and final faction for the board game Factions: Battlegrounds. This unique faction includes brand new monsters, spells and abilities.

Includes everything you need to go to battle against the other Factions:

15 unit cards (all with new, original illustrations)

5 savannah Terrain Tiles

Vitawaasi Faction Overview

—description from the publisher

Air Raid 36/46 is an easy to learn WW2 Air Combat Game. It also doesn't take much time to play and scales very well with increasing number of aircrafts controlled. Two players are at least needed but plenty more one each side can join the game. The more units the longer the game takes but it scales really well in terms of gameplay and keeping the overview.
The game is played on hex-field maps. It is turn-based and consists of an initiative phase, movement and fire phase. Damage is taken and casualities will be removed at the end phase simultaneously from the board.
The faster a plane moves - the wider the turn it makes. There are also numerous maneuvers available.
Players will be able to turn the tide of battle with their skill and dice rolls.
Air Raid 36/46 focuses on having fun and excitement at the table. Despite the light-weight and fluent gameplay all statistics of the planes have been researched with care from historical facts.

There is no administrative burden with tracking damage, ammo etc. on a record sheet. Everything you need to remember will be attached with a chip to the base of the aircraft model.
Only one statistic card per type of aircraft model is needed at the table.
The aircraft models themselves come at a very high quality and are made of metal. Scale is 1/200 which avoids flimsy parts breaking off and keeps the models at an acceptable size.

Beside the rules, maps, counters and stat cards the base box contains models from the pacific theatre. These are three P40, three Zeros and 4 Japanese light bombers.
The scenarios that come with the rulebook offer situations and objectives as a change to head-on dockfights.

There is also more than 40 miniatures released from different theatres and nations like English, German, French, Spanish etc. 
The timeline starts at 1936 from the Spanish civil war and will be covered to a fictional ongoing war past 1945 and later theatres. Blisters with multiple fighters are already released as well.

—description from the designer

Partner Poker is designed for 4-8 players, offering a unique twist: each player is paired with a partner, necessitating collaborative efforts to secure victory. 
The gameplay revolves around each player possessing half of a Poker hand, which they must merge with their partner's hand to form a Poker hand. 
Each round unfolds through four distinct phases: The Flop introduces a central card known as the Piggy, the Gift grants every player an additional card, the Volley prompts each player to exchange a card with their partner, and culminates in the Showdown where players unveil their combined Poker hands to determine the victor.

—description from the designer

After an eternity, a dungeon appears. Resting within are relics that were supposed to have been lost to the past. The people who brave the dungeon in search of relics are called Relic Takers. Yes, you are a Relic Taker! Gather your friends to help you on your adventure and set off into an unknown dungeon!

-description from the publisher (translated)

In Relic Takers, players delve into a dungeon in search of relics using 2 characters each round. Each character has special abilities and order, so players must select the best character for their needs.  Outside of the dungeon, there are requests for specific combinations of relics (combinations of color and number of relics needed to 'complete' a request). Players must acquire these from within the dungeon.  This is accomplished by drawing from a bag of relics... but a player's character is limited in how many relics they can carry.  Get to greedy and spend too much time in the dungeon and your haul out may be quite a bit sorter than your needs.

The game centers on a press-your-luck mechanic in drawing relics. Players must balance this risk by drafting the right characters to aid them in completing requests. The game ends and the winner is determined when a player completes 6 requests or all 12 crystals are recovered.

—user summary
publishers: メリーアンドリューワークス(merry-andrew-works)

description:
Hedgehog Nig's home is threatened because the strict gardener Sepp is cleaning up his garden. While he trims the bushes and cleans the flower bed, the natural gardeners quickly build their animal-friendly gardens.

Who will be the first to get all the important natural elements together so that hedgehog Nig can slip through the hole in the fence to safety? Hurry is required before the protective pile of leaves is cleared away!

Get to know the animals in the natural garden in a playful way and find out how you can protect them with lots of fun and exercise! — description from the publisher

Spot the Peep in the Crowd in this head-to-head "Where's Waldo" style card game.

Players reveal a Peep to spot. The peep will be on 1 of the 3 Crowd Cards. The catch is that 1 of the 3 Crowd cards will be facedown. Be the first to spot the Peep and slap card the right card to win!

Over 150 Peeps.

Each copy of the game contains a unique set of peeps.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Blik op de Nederlandse taal spel
description:
This trivia game contains 108 double-sided cards, 36 letter tiles, a notepad, a pencil, a standard D6 die and the rules, all neatly packed in a tin box.

The cards have trivia questions in four different categories:

     Spelling a word
     Guessing a word based on a cryptic description
     Giving a synonym of a word
     Giving the meaning of a word (multiple-choice).


A throw of the die decide which one you have to answer correctly to gain a tile. The tiles can be placed on the table to form words (Scrabble meets Rummikub) to gain points.

The player with most points at the end of the game (after the pool with tiles has been emptied and after subtractions for leftover tiles) wins the game.

Little is known about how Great Uncle Harry amassed his fortune.  The sole stipulation to each of his beneficiaries (whom Great Uncle Harry fondly described as “my dear Trustafarians”) was to “stay strong, keep trading and have fun along the way”.

In Great Uncle Harry’s memory, Dave Sellwood (with a soupçon of help from Jon and Darren) set out to devise a board game with universal appeal.

“Navigate the exhilarating highs and heart-churning lows of your Risk & Reward cards and enjoy landing on Great Uncle Harry space to get just what you need.

Playing in pairs, the winning team is the last one standing or the one with the highest net worth after 90 minutes has elapsed.”

In the imaginary country of Fiatlandia, fiat currencies are losing the trust of the people. The government has decided to act and is playing one card - cryptocurrencies. It has a few interesting projects lined up. However, it wants to see how these projects will behave not only in bull and bear markets, but above all what fundamental value they can offer people. Which of the cryptocurrencies will eventually beat all the others and become the accounting unit of Fiatlandia?

The goal of the game is to beat other cryptocurrencies and become Fiatlandia's official currency. This is achieved by increasing Fundament value to 7 or 8 and gaining 13 or more Market Cap units.

All players share one standee which moves along the game board. After rolling a die, a player moves the standee and resolves the corresponding action space:

     Fundament: Active player takes the top Fundament card, reads a question out and all players use their tokens to guess the correct answer. If answered correctly, their Fundament value increases, otherwise it drops by 1.
     Pump +, Retail, FOMO, To the Moon, BTD: Active player increases their Market Cap.
     Pump -, FUD, Correction, Flashcrash: Active player decreases their Market Cap.
     Whales: Active player rolls a die. Even numbers mean increasing Market Cap by that number of units, odd numbers mean decreasing Market Cap.
     Market event: Active player takes the top card from the Market event deck and resolves the effect.
     Consolidation: Active player is not affected by other players' actions in this round.
     Speculation: All players can use their "long" and "short" tokens to predict the market development. Active player then rolls a die; even numbers mean market goes up ("long" tokens), odd numbers mean market goes down ("short" tokens). Players who guessed correctly increase their Market Cap and vice versa.
     HODL: Active player gains a HODL token which can be discarded to avoid decreasing Market Cap.

A limited collector's box. Includes all five individual games, an art book, a metal day and night coin, two small playmats, six alternative art muse cards, the same six alternative art muse cards in foil and five breath control cards in foil.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
FOLLOW ME: The Infantry’s Game is a game of military strategy and domination. It was developed to teach junior military leaders, both commissioned and non-commissioned officers, to maintain situational awareness on the battlefield while orchestrating the elements of combat power with their designated units. It would be wise for players of this game to apply the principles of warfare when planning, coordinating, and executing their game strategy.

This game is played on a gridded game board by two or more players who use FOLLOW ME game pieces to maneuver and fire on their opponents in order to accomplish a chosen game objective. The game pieces represent the warriors, leaders, supports, and assets that are employed on the field of battle, and their fate is determined by a roll of the dice. The game ends when the chosen game objective is accomplished by the winning player.

Mechanically, the game centers around formation-based grid movement and dice-based fires adjudication.  Units moving in a formation near a leader are more mobile and effective than those operating alone, but they those benefits require staying close to a command piece.

This game was designed in 2019 and initially released in 2024.  It is based on the designer's experience in the US Army with the goal of creating realistic and instructional models of squad-level and platoon-level combat on the modern battlefield.

Cut yourself a slice of the action with this fast-moving game of pies and pairs! battle it out to be the first player to get rid of all their cards as you attempt to match the pies and get ready to smash the mash! with added wild cards aimed to mix things up and throw you off your game, it's time to get out of the kitchen if you can't stand the heat!

—description from the publisher

Slotters is a set collection memory game for exactly four players. Player receive a set of numbered cards, and must place their highest face down on the table in front of themselves.

Then, each player will reveal 3 cards on their turn; they can choose to have any player, including themselves, reveal their smallest or they can reveal one of the cards from the table.  If all three match, the cards are collected and a reward is received.  If the cards do not match, the same number of cards must be returned, but it may be different cards.

If a player collects the three 7s, they immediately win. Otherwise, the winner is the player with the highest rewards.

boardgame: Flesh and Blood: Ira Welcome Deck
description:
Flesh and Blood: Ira Welcome Deck is a free intro product to Flesh and Blood TCG. It focuses on the key components of FAB - how to gain resources to play your cards, how to play out the cards in your hand by using go again, and how combat works.

After watching the 6 minute learn to play video on fabtcg.com, you will be ready to start playing as Ira, Crimson Haze and will quickly discover the surprising level of depth that awaits!

Return to the world of Rocket Ranchers with this new push-your-luck racing game! In the game of Start Your Rockets, you are one of six raceship pilots vying for sponsorship, fame, and the checkered flag. Drive as fast as you can but don't blow your engine! Throughout the game, buy upgrades for your raceship, speed past opponents, and deal with the effects the various biomes have on your vehicle. And of course, be careful of space cats catching a ride and frying your systems and causing malfunctions. At the end of the game the fastest racer (or the one with the most fame) will be the winner!

Will you be the fastest? Everyone... START YOUR ROCKETS!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Titania: Forest of Fairies and Jewels
description:
On a sweltering midsummer night under a full moon, a festival of fairies begins deep in the forest. But the pixies are moody, the brownies are housecleaning, the gnomes are working in the mines, and the trolls are sitting idly by. The leprechauns are accumulating rune stones, and the goblins are playing tricks. Oberon, the arrogant king of the fairies, can't read the mood, and Titania, the queen, is touring the forest with her fairy friends in tow. Gathering such fickle fairies is a difficult task. Fortunately, they love shiny rune stones that contain the power of fire, water, earth, and trees! To convince the fairies to participate in the festival, you must give them the rune stones they desire.

In Titania, each player drafts rune stones from a shared board. Players use their rune stones to pay the cost of summoning fairy cards from a common deck. Each fairy has its own special ability, so players must use these abilities to summon the highest scoring fairies!

The game ends when the deck runs out of cards. The player with the most victory points on their fairy cards wins. Gain bonus points by creating sets of matching fairies. (The game includes special rules for solo play.)

—description from the publisher (translated)
designers: サダマツ ケイタ (Keita Sadamatsu)

boardgame: Chester: Four Sisters and a Magical House
description:
In a village lived four witch sisters. Their elder said to them, "I'll give you some land on the outskirts of the village. You can build a mansion there to live." And so the four sisters began building a house together. The rooms they create are magical, leading to unexpected places when you open the door. The selfish girls use their familiars to create the rooms they like; the eldest daughter loves gardening, the second sister obsesses over cooking and eating, the third daughter who craves books, and the third daughter's hobby is sleeping. Of these witches, who will contribute the most to building their mansion with its mysterious layout?

In each turn of Chester, a player chooses one room tile from their hand and adds it to the mansion, marking it with one of their cubes. Each room has colored arrows at each exit to show the room's requirements, and the player covers those arrows with cubes as the conditions are met. Once a room is completed, the player who placed it receives the points written on the tile. Players continue to complete their rooms and collect points, and the player with the most points at the end of the game wins. (The game includes special rules for solo play.)

—description from the publisher (translated)
designers: サダマツ ケイタ (Keita Sadamatsu)

boardgame: Liddell: Alice and the Secret Tea Party
description:
Fun tea parties are all the rage in Wonderland! However, the Queen of Hearts is vain and jealous. She hates for any tea party to steal the spotlight from the tea parties she hosts at her castle. If the Queen finds out that you're throwing such a tea party, she shouts that familiar line: “Off with their heads!” To stay out of trouble, Alice and her friends must make sure their tea parties draw less attention than those hosted by the Queen of Hearts while still attracting guests. Who will avoid the Queen's attention while throwing the most exciting tea party?

In Liddell: Alice and the Secret Tea Party, players place cards either in the common play area or in their own area. Players compete for the highest total value of cards. However, the total value of the cards in your area must not exceed the total value of the cards in the common area.(Any cards exceeding the total value of the common area will be worth 0 points). Avoid going bust, use each character's special ability, and try to collect all 5 types of character cards!

—translated description from the publisher
designers: サダマツ ケイタ (Keita Sadamatsu)

description:
Perform your Rites. Complete your Ritual. Doom the World.

Ritual is an expandable horror card game (XCG,) where you compete to complete your ritual before your opponents. Use your agents to scheme and perform your rites, or to thwart your foes, keeping them from completing theirs. The first player to have seven or more rites dooms the world!
Monopoly, the world’s most popular board game, with a Lyon, France theme. Limited issue of 10,000 copies.

A one-page dungeon crawl for solo players!
Enter a room, roll a d6, kill monsters or buy potions, defeat the Squeleton King and try to get out of the catacombs.
Good luck!

Karni is a tile-laying polyomino game for 2-4 players.
You assume the role of a farmer on a plot of swampy land. The only thing that grows on your land is your carnivorous plant. Attract flys to grow your plant. Attract men in PointyHats to feed your plant even meatier snacks. The farmer who grows the most impressive plant wins the game.

Each turn you will attract Flys and PointyHats to your farm. Flys and PointyHats are fed to your plant and become a game currency to claim new tiles to grow your plant. 
Surround Clearings to unlock Attractions and lure more Flys and PointyHats.
Players score victory points by completing various objectives such as covering all the spaces on their farm game board to unique objectives defined by objective cards.

—description from the publisher
Neutral Factions, Side Missions and two new playable asymetric Factions - the Raghna and Qfin.

boardgame: Cartaventura: Les Trois Mousquetaires – Au nom du Roi
description:
The game where you are the hero.

Cartaventura offers historical adventures in pocket format, “Book in which you are the hero” style. Players build their adventure using cards that offer different options. They think together about the best choices in order to follow in the footsteps of the game's hero. Thanks to rich scenarios and magnificent watercolor illustrations, they are truly immersed in different eras of History.

The game system, simple and unique, allows you to replay each scenario several times in order to discover all the possible endings. In The Three Musketeers, discover 3 different endings, including 2 alternatives!
Scenario: Richelieu has Constance Bonacieux kidnapped and d’Artagnan imprisoned. In prison, he meets Benjamin de la Fère, a Huguenot activist captured for espionage. He reveals to him that an attack against Louis XIII is to take place during Gaston's wedding. Benjamin had sought to warn the King, but was captured and imprisoned by Richelieu, who seemed to protect the conspirators for obscure reasons.

A set of rules allowing teams of players and umpires to recreate the battle of Jutland, 1916.

The actual movement and combat rules are very simple - the core of the game consists in the use of umpires who provide the players with realistically limited and inaccurate information on which to base their decisions.

The rules were published in issue 80 of Miniature Wargames, October 1990.

This game is based on several battles in the early stages of the War to Resist US Aggression and aid Korea in 1950. The game supports 2 players, one commanding the People's Volunteer Army and the other the United Nations Army.

The game contains 5 fierce battles with different styles. The battles range from the cold icy fields of North Korea to military fortresses, the island of Taehwa-do and the surrounding rough seas.

—extract of the description from the publisher (translated)

The game contains a booklet with 5 small games about the Korean War. While postcard-game style counters are provided in the booklet mounted counters are available for this game.

Game 1: Bear Trap (Chosin Reservoir)
Game 2: Turkey Feast(Battle of Kunuri)
Game 3: Horse Hunter(Battle of Unsan)
Game 4: Chief's Fortress (Siege of Chipyong-Ni)
Game 5: Eagle strikes the Sky (Air Battle of Taehwa-do)

Game 1 to 4 were originally published by War Drum Games in 2016 as promotional postcards of Iron Fists of Our Fathers: Korean War. Game 5 was newly added by Banner Of War.

boardgame: Beware the Ides of March: The Liberators' Civil War
description:
Beware the Ides of March is an operational hex-and-counter game covering the Liberators’ Civil War – the conflict which saw erstwhile allies Mark Antony and Octavian team-up against Brutus and Cassius. It was here that Rome’s fate was decided once and for all: would the republic be restored, or had it fallen irrevocably to despotism?

The war between the Second Triumvirate and Caesar’s assassins was decided in two battles a few weeks apart - but it didn’t need to happen that way. Designer John Theissen (Antony and Cleopatra, Campaign of Nations) presents players with an operational sandbox. It models the realities of the conflict: the logistical challenges of supplying an army in the ancient world, the uncertainty of combat, and the dangers of sea travel. You will need to be bold, but every action carries with it significant risk, and just as was the case historically, you are not protected from the consequences of failure.

Theissen’s designs are some of the most approachable and streamlined in operational gaming. Rolls on tables simulate sudden storms, external invasions, internal revolts, and the effects of plague. Together these create texture and give context to your decisions.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Players become space merchants called 'Ether Gliders' and earn money by transporting cargo and passengers from star to star.
The player who acquires the most assets by investing that money, strengthening spaceships, and building facilities wins.

A vast map that is randomly generated for each game. Replayability is perfect with 16 types of unique abilities for unique supporters. Artwork and rich flavor text add to the space opera atmosphere. In addition, a gorgeous map is completed for each game, and products are lined up on individual boards.

Complete with solid solo play rules. This is a game that can be enjoyed by one person or multiple people.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Ready your conkers for a great battle. Divide & Conker is a 2 to 4 player game. Set up varies dependent on player count. Game play usually lasts 30 minutes and is suitable from age 8 upwards.

Gameplay takes place over 2 rounds with a total of 7 battles. At the end of 7 battles, each player counts their victory points from cards in play. The player with the most victory points is the winner.

Within a round there are 3 phases; draft, prepare army, battle. In phase 1, players take it in turns drafting cards either from a pyramid or snake draft. Once all cards have drafted move to phase 2. Players can now prepare their army by laying down their collected conkers, applying hardener cards and attaching strings. Once each players army is prepared, play then continues to phase 3. During phase 3 players battle each other by declaring attacking and defending conkers. Player roll the dice and play trick cards during a battle until one conker is defeated. The second battle within round 1 can then commence.

When round 1 is over, move to round 2 where these 3 phases are played out but with a total of 5 battles in the final phase instead of 2. If no more battles can be played, the game ends and victory points are counted.

—description from the designer
Cooperative Alias variant. Can you find the connecting thing among the words. Contains the usual explaining of words. Clock is ticking as players race against the timer.

boardgame: Street Masters: Rise of the Kingdom
description:
A SteamForged Games KS repack of

     Street Masters
     Street Masters SG
     Legend of Oni Expansion
     Twin Tiger Expansion
     Redemption Pack 1

SteamForged Repack containing:

     Street Masters: Aftershock
     Street Masters: Aftershock Stretch Goals
     Street Masters: Essence of Evil
     Street Masters: Redemption pack II

boardgame: Street Masters III: Tide of the Dragon
description:
SteamForged repack of:

     Tide of the Dragon Expansion
     Rumble Pack
     New Challengers Pack

boardgame: Battle Line: Chiến Thần Vũ Trụ
description:
Board Game VN has recently bought the copyright to the addictive gameplay of Battle Line - one of the best strategy card games from the legendary board game designer Knizia - with a totally new story and art.

It is based on Reiner Knizia's Battle Line (2000) with a new theme, some slight changes in Tactics card, and a whole new 3-player mode.

     Reinforcement is Battle Line's Mud. But instead of counting on 4/6 cards on either side, the game requires you to form a formation using 4 cards at the chosen location.


—description from the publisher

"Cosmic War God" edition:


     The story of the war for resources on the planet Maldek. It is a dramatic and fascinating story, in an enchanting and mysterious fantasy world, where different races in the universe fight to earn invaluable resources on the planet Maldek.



     You will play the role of a leader, commanding your army to win this war. Your mission is to defeat your opponent on as many pins as possible during your round by playing the cards in your hand according to strategy.


"Hesman" edition:

     Hesman is a superhero from a Vietnamese science-fiction comic series.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: I Am The Big One: Journey to the West
description:
The four apprentices of Tang Seng decide to compete with each other to rescue their master.

Players randomly and secretly support the two apprentices in the game, move forward through their respective plans, and compete to defeat the monster. The player with the highest score in the task wins.

The only game set in the bathroom!
A hilarious card game that delves into the wonderful world of poop.

CHOOSE YOUR ASS, ATTACK WITH POOP, DEFEND YOURSELF WITH TOILET OBJECTS...
MANAGE TO MAKE A PERFECT AND YOU WILL WIN THE GAME!

The most fun party/filler in recent times, with 108 cards, more than 50 different actions and 3 game modes, for 3 to 9 players and for ages 8 and up.

The only 3-in-1 game, board game, humor comic and poop catalog in the palm of your hand!
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
Order of the Day: Normandy is an Operational  level two-player board-wargame about the Normandy invasion of June, 1944 - playable in 45-120 minutes this is not a complicated wargame.

Played in IGO-UGO fashion, players move units from area to area across northern France and Belgium.  Combat occurs at the end of a turn in any area where both sides have forces and is resolved with a draw of cards.  The cards inflict hits on opposing units with 'bonus hits' caused by having specific types of units in the battle (Panzers, Rangers, Artillery, etc).

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Until the Bitter End: Tanks – Eastern Front
description:
Welcome to  Until the Bitter End - Tanks: Eastern Front,  a World War II wargame.  This is the fourth game in the Until the Bitter End series and the second that focuses, solely, on tank (and other armoured fighting vehicles) battles. This is  a print and play game for solitaire and two-players. This is a standalone game in my series of WW2 print and play games. You don't need to have played or own the first games - all of the digital PDF files are included (you simply print it out, provide yourself with a regular six sided dice and a pencil).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Knucklebones: Cult of the Lamb Dice Game
description:
Knucklebones is a dice game of risk and reward, created originally within the videogame 'Cult of the Lamb'. It has since been adapted in limited-run official sets as well as fan-made boards to keep the dice-rolling fun going!

How to play:
The game consists of two 3x3 boards, each belonging to their respective player. On a player's turn, they roll a single 6-sided die, and must place it in a column on their board. A filled column does not accept any more dice.
Each player's score, which is the sum of all the dice values on their board. If a player places multiple dice of the same value in the same column, the score awarded for each of those dice is multiplied by the number of dice of the same value in that column. 
When a player places a die, all dice of the same value in the corresponding column of the opponent's board gets destroyed. Players can use this to destroy their opponent's high-scoring combos.
The game ends when either player completely fills up their 3x3 board. The player with the higher score wins.

Arcane Acres is a card-drafting and tetromino tile placement game for 1-4 Players.

Become a caretaker of the Sanctuary for Mythical Creatures to create the most harmonious habitat! Taking turns drafting the Creatures and expanding their Sanctuary, Players seek to shelter magical animals while shaping a perfect home for them to live in.

The goal of the game is to build an optimal Sanctuary and find Creatures that naturally work together to score points. At the end of the game, the Player with the most points wins.

boardgame: Red Flag Over Paris: Censorship Variant
description: The Censorship variant limits the risk of an extremely bad hand, and offers an interesting decision space when it comes to hand management.

In The Botch Is Back, we return to the chaotic bluffing-lying-hurting playstyle of The Botch, but with a Canadian flair. The ferry between Seattle and Vancouver is a perfect place for white collar crimes to take a quick breather, for big deals and small crimes to go unnoticed. It's also a perfect place for knuckle-busting blue-collar good-natured crooks to try and get their end in, hauling booze or maple syrup across the border. You get bad folk in closed spaces trying to get their own in, and what can possibly go wrong?

Everything!

Meant to evoke those chaotic crime movies where everything's going wrong, The Botch Is Back is a party game of hidden information and ridiculous messes. It's a game about bluffing and nerve, about pulling off a con, and about grabbing the jewels and running. In The Botch, you're going to lie, cheat and steal, and if you're really lucky you'll get out of this with your skin intact.

The Botch Is Back is a hidden-information bluffing game designed to keep things reasonably chaotic and make it hard for players to get an overwhelming edge through experience and memorisation. It's a game to get people into gaming, where things going embarassingly wrong is part of the design.

The Botch Is Back contains 5 Basic roles and 5 Basic items, which are designed to be played as an entry-level stable game. As you become more comfortable with the game you can add more roles and more items, choosing from an additional 7 Advanced roles and 7 Advanced items.

—description from the designer

During your journey around the world, you draw the fauna of different habitats. Of course, you need patience to observe them accurately. In this game you fill in very cute pixelated animals on laminated card sheets with erasable markers. The central interesting decision is whether to choose a lot or a little? Obviously, it would be nice to always choose more shapes and larger shapes to make faster progress, but sometimes we need smaller shapes to fill in the holes. The game is quick to learn, but can also be a serious head-scratcher. You have to pay attention to bonuses, habitats and extra objectives also.

boardgame: Riddle Of Fire: One Speckled Egg – Petal
description:
Promo card for Riddle Of Fire: One Speckled Egg.


Petal
Change any ingredient to a Syrup.

boardgame: Here to Slay: The Rhythmic Archer Promo Card
description:
2024 convention-exclusive promo card.

Card Text: "At the beginning of your turn, you may switch the Rhythmic Archer between Bard and Ranger. Each time any player (including you) unsuccessfully rolls to ATTACK a Monster card, you may DRAW a card."

boardgame: Heap-O-Sheep: The High Flying Game of Flingin' Sheep!
description: Taking turns, players use their springboard to launch their sheep onto the hook-and-loop sheep pen. Land them, stick them, pile 'em up! The first to get all of their sheep to stay in the pen wins the game!
The complete four game Set of Cat-a-Comb, Light Seeker, Forgenesis, and Top Cap in one box set.

Educational roll and move teaching various traffic rules to players. Board contains numbered path around Helsinki city centre, with certain numbers highlighted in red. Should player hit one of those numbers they read related event and follow instructions in it.

A solo game in which the player will be envolved in the exploration of an old house in search of clues that will reveal where a precious treasure is hidden. 
Only by collecting enough clues and useful objects it will be possible to successfully reach the goal.
However time is limited so the player has to find the treasure before the night is over.

A HYBRID game using the 'Silent War 2.0' AND 'Silent Victory' games in combination to provide a single submarine campaign game. In essence, SW determines the encounters and SV resolves the combat. The rule set provides for a seamless integration of the two games.

Players will need both of the constituent games.

boardgame: Extraordinary Rendition: A Mission Pack for Exploit Zero
description:
Extraordinary Rendition is a five-part, mini-campaign for the solo/cooperative miniatures war game, Exploit Zero.

Designed for beginner to intermediate level players, the missions are presented in a linked, narrative format which can be run over the course of several sessions, or perhaps a single, frenzied day of cyberpunk espionage and mayhem.

In Extraordinary Rendition, your four Agents are tasked to 'reacquire' a corporate defector, defend and deliver him safely to waiting transport, then initiate follow-up operations to keep the competition off balance.

This supplement includes four pre-generated player Agent profiles, generic H-SEC stats, one unique H-SEC Agent profile, as well as mission briefs and set up suggestions for each game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Smuggler's Neck: A Case File for When Nightmares Come
description:
People are disappearing on the Waterfront - corner boys and hookers, immigrants and vagrants. The discarded and downcast.

So far, the police have kept it out of the news, explaining away the incidents as drug overdoes, gang violence, tragic accidents, and poor life choices.

But your contacts on the streets say that's a lie; something is stalking the homeless, the helpless, the vulnerable. Someting fast, something mean. Something unnatural.

The police won't listen. Social services doesn't believe them. The local residents don't care. Can you and your Nightwatch friends help?


            
        
    


Smuggler's Neck is a six-mission campaign supplement for Osprey Wargames' When Nightmares Come - An Investigative Wargame of Supernatural Horror.

—description from the publisher

Impress your fellow players with your tumbling skills as you roll your tortoises across the garden to score. How and where your tortoises land decides how many points you win, so you’ll want to work out your best tumbling technique as quickly as possible. But be careful not to land on your back—that would be extremely embarrassing. The first player to earn 500 points is the winner and is crowned Top Tortoise Tumbler!

Perfect for friends, families, and lovers of tortoises, it's suitable for ages 8+, 1+ players, has an average 15-minute gameplay, and includes English instructions. Made using FSC paper and soy inks.

How to Play:
To begin the game, you must first create the garden. Piece together the nine scene cards in any position you like. There are potential bonuses on each card, so think carefully about where you place the highest earners!
The first player rolls the two rubber tortoise pals onto the garden and checks the scorecard to see how many points they’ve won. For instance, landing on your shell side will earn more points than face-planting but landing on your back scores zero! Remember to check for bonuses on the card where you landed, too. Not happy with your score? You can re-roll twice per turn but remember: your score can decrease as well as increase. And if a tortoise misses the garden altogether, you score zero and cannot re-roll. When you’ve done all you can, record your total score for that round on the scorepad and pass the tortoises to the next player. The garden layout stays the same throughout the game. The first person to earn 500 points is the winner and is crowned Top Tortoise Tumbler!

—description from the publisher

A checkers variant with scoring. The board has numbered spaces, & players would have to track their score through the game on sheets of paper. The winner is the player with the highest score, not the player with the last remaining piece.

Embrace the challenge and sharpen your grammatical skills in GrammarnistA1! Gameplay: Players take turns making sentences that are GRAMMATICALLY correct. Each 30-second turn, players can form sentences with their decks (or even other players' ones). Throughout the game, there are function cards to spice up the rounds.

Droched (from Irish: droichead, meaning "bridge") is a drawless annihilation game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the hexes (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal board. The recommended board size is 7 cells per side, but boards of 5 or 9 are also valid. There is also a location outside the board called the prison. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stones of their own color.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A group is a maximal set of connected stones of the same color. A single stone is also a group.
     A path of a group is a minimal set of empty cells that connect the group to a border or corner cell. A single edge or corner cell is also a path.
     A group is on a bridge if its combined paths include cells on at least two edges, two corners, or one edge and one corner; otherwise, it is on a broken bridge. Corners are not part of edges.


				
				
					Turns:Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, perform one of the following actions:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty cell whose group of the placed stone is not on a broken bridge. Then, in the following order, move to prison (i) all enemy groups on broken bridges and (ii) all friendly groups on broken bridges.
     Remove an enemy stone from the prison. If the prison contains stones of both colors, remove pairs of stones of opposite colors until only stones of one color or none remain.


At the end of your turn, the board position must be different from the board position at the end of your previous turn. If you cannot make a placement, pass your turn; otherwise, passing is not allowed.

				
				
					End of the game:You win if the last enemy group is eliminated from the board.

To balance the game, before starting, the first player places several black stones in the prison and then the second player chooses a side.  This method of balance is called the komi pie. For handicap games, the weaker player takes black and starts by placing on the board a number of black stones proportional to the difference in skills between the players.

—description from the designer

Jambuza is a simple abstract game in which players add pieces to the board or move the pieces on the board to align them in a certain way to win. It's a 2 to 4 player game. You can play 1 versus 1, 1 versus 2, or 2 versus 2 in teams.

In Shogun, each player assumes the role of a great Daimyo with all his troops. Each Daimyo has the same 10 possible actions to develop his empire and secure points. Castles, temples, and theaters replace the infrastructure of Wallenstein (as rice replaces wheat), creating a thematic experience for players who wish to move their exploits east to the Land of the Rising Sun.

Shuppa is a card-collecting game in which you try to collect as many cards as possible by matching lollipops of the same color, Candy Crush-style.

To set up, each player gets a hand of seven number cards (which come in four colors) and two action cards. Two rows of number cards are laid out, with each row having three cards more than the number of players.

The game lasts seven rounds. In the first round, reveal three number cards from the reserve. Each player then chooses and reveals a card, marking it with their player token, with all cards then being placed in a row above the existing cards. Players then claim any group of at least three cards in the same color if the card they played is part of this group. (Player order is determined at random by the left or right arrow on top of the card deck.) After a group is claimed, drop any cards that have an empty space underneath them, which might possibly create a new group that is claimed by the active player. If a player doesn't claim a group, they take an action card from the deck. Place a number card from the reserve at the bottom of any empty column.

The next six rounds play out similarly: Reveal three number cards; everyone plays a number card, then claims a group, if possible. Now, though, anyone who doesn't claim a group can either draw an action card or play an action card. The action cards let you "eat" a card from the play area to score it, move a card to another column, or swap two cards. Ideally these actions will let you score groups.

After seven rounds, tally your score, with multicolor cards being worth 2 points and every other card 1 point.

If you enjoy the drafting mechanic in card games, then DRAFT - the Ultimate Card Drafting Game is a must have for your game shelf. Players have over a dozen ways to draft cards in this set-collection style card game.

Players will play three rounds of three phases each drafting cards into their collection of cards. At the end of three phases scoring takes place. Who is the Master of Sudoku? 2 to 4 players can compete for this title. Of course there are puzzles for adults (100 pieces) on 9x9 fields. But children have also been thought of; they play on 6x6 fields on the back of the game board (50 puzzles). After a puzzle has been chosen, you place the starting lineup with the corresponding chips on the game board. From now on you reveal chips and try to fit them into the game board. There must be no contradiction to the Sudoku rules; i.e. no number appears twice in a row, column or square. But you can't ponder endlessly, the hourglass runs relentlessly.

When placing the chips, make sure that the marking is facing you. This is the only way to determine at the end who placed which chips. Naturally, the chips are not very clear in up to 5 orientations; it is best to look at the colors instead of the numbers.

There is a reward for whoever was able to place the most valuable chips in a complete (!) row/column/square. That's exactly the crux of the matter: Due to the placement rules, a complete Sudoku can never be created, and complete rows/columns/squares are also the exception. I advocate for the highest sum in an area, regardless of whether it is ultimately completed or not.

Alternatively, you can also play solo with Sudoku Master, but this is often difficult due to the lack of note-taking options.

Furthermore, two variants have been included that turn Sudoku into a game of chance: One is Sudoku-Lucky Number, the other is Sudoku-Dragon. In Sudoku-Lucky Number, all the number chips are distributed on the game board and you turn one chip over. If he doesn't contradict any Sudoku rules, he can stay put. However, if there is a contradiction, you have to accept it. In the end, whoever's chips have the lowest total wins. A similar process can be found in Challenge Sudoku, but it is solved better there: chips that have already been taken can be installed later in appropriate places. This means that some of the luck is lost, as anyone who uncovers more high numbers (and has to take them) has no chance of winning in Sudoku Master.

Sudoku Dragon is the worst thing I've ever encountered: you choose a puzzle and give it a clue. Again you have time until the hourglass runs through once. During this time you reveal chips that you must immediately put on the game board. For every chip already lying on a vertically, horizontally or diagonally adjacent field there is a winning chip - so up to 8 pieces at once. It's no surprise that the 27 chips are quickly gone. From this point on you take chips away from your opponents. Counting the chips takes up most of the time and doesn't allow the game to flow.

—user summary

You are one of up to 4 benevolent nobles looking to expand and develop your land in hopes of establishing a functional and fearsome empire.
You will collect resources and exploit the land; you will build buildings and develop your civilization through military, grace, population, and science tracks; you will explore ancient ruins, fight off troublesome invaders, and set up trade routes.
