boardgame: Tiny Epic Game of Thrones: Ice & Fire
description:
Embark on an exciting cooperative gaming experience with the Tiny Epic Game of Thrones: Ice & Fire expansion. In this unique twist on the original game, players must work together to unite the Seven Kingdoms against the menacing threat of the Night King and his army of White Walkers. While the core mechanics remain mostly the same, players must now strategize and collaborate to outsmart and outmaneuver this formidable foe. As fear spreads among the non-player houses due to the Night King's relentless advance southward, building alliances becomes crucial. The fate of Westeros hangs in the balance, and without the support of these houses, all hope may be lost. As long as the Wall stands, the White Walkers are contained. However, once the Wall falls, they will be unleashed upon Westeros, wreaking havoc as they march southward.

The expansion introduces two new playable houses: House Targaryen and The Night's Watch. Players can embody iconic heroes such as Daenerys Targaryen with her three dragons, and Jon Snow. The map of Westeros is expanded to include new regions beyond the Wall. The expansion also introduces a new element of exploration to the game. Throughout the domains on both sides of the Wall, players will encounter various exploration tokens with unique effects. It is essential for players to collaborate in searching these domains for Dragonglass and Valyrian Steel.

Each round begins with players receiving a report from the Rangers of the Night's Watch. These reports, represented by a deck of cards, reveal where the Night King and his army will strike next. This mechanic, along with the Night King's Ice Dragon, adds an element of suspense and strategy to the game, driving the movements of the Night King.

The challenge of defeating the Night King requires seamless coordination among all participants. The ultimate outcome of their collective efforts will be clear-cut: either successfully eliminate the Night King within six rounds of gameplay, or face the catastrophic loss of Westeros.

boardgame: Gear Krieg: Fighting Vehicles of the Modern Age
description: This supplement for Gear Krieg takes a look at the many types of fighting super science vehicles fielded by the armies of both the Axis and the Alliance. Each vehicle is detailed with background information, variants and modelling guide.

Humanity is a trading card game that celebrates the vast spectrum of human achievement. Players take on the roles of rival civilizations competing to determine who will have a greater impact on future cultures. The cards are inspired by our shared heritage, taking the form of ancient rulers, great works of art, philosophical breakthroughs, and mythical beings.

Key Features:
Civilizational Conflict: This box contains three civilization cards, each with its own strengths, weaknesses, and unique abilities.

Multifaceted Gameplay: Humanity offers multiple paths to victory, encouraging players to explore different strategies and tactics to outmaneuver their opponents.

Cultural Enrichment: Each card in Humanity is a testament to the richness and diversity of human civilization.

Gameplay
Each player starts with a civilization card that has a unique victory condition.
Add cards to the bank to generate suns. Spend suns to play cards.
Challenge your opponent's cards to send them to the dustbin of history.
Win by attacking your opponent directly, meeting the victory condition on your civilization card, or forcing your opponent to draw all of their cards.

—description from the designer

The evil mastermind Kronoxator has erased the hours of the Cosmic Clock! Space and time will collapse unless you repair the Clock. You also need to rescue the Time Jokers and defeat him before the Timer reaches 0!

Cosmic Clock Solitaire is a fast, high-score solo game of hand management, multi-use cards and circular movement. Mostly built around the classic 54 deck.

—description from the designer

Six players vie to stay alive and wipe out their opponents in this stand-alone expansion to the classic Nuclear War card game. Nuclear Destruction reimplements the Weapons of Mass Destruction game, and adds enough cards for a full six player game. It comes with 6 large playmats and a comprehensive rulebook covering all the Nuclear War family of games.

boardgame: Green Island (Fan Expansion for Age of Steam)
description:
A global warming inspired semi-modular expansion map for Age of Steam.

Due to the climatic impact, the island undergoes changes. As the game progresses, the glaciers in the northern part of the island melt and grassy fields with small ponds take their place.

Forests start to spread in the southern parts of the island, which need to be cleared for tracks or from tracks.

The Chasquis were young people who traveled extensive roads of the Inca state to carry messages from one post to the other. They were people trusted by the Inca and were well prepared for this task.

Memoria de Chasqui challenges you to travel through paths trying not to forget a word of the messages you have been given.

HOW TO PLAY?
Each player follows their own path, carrying different messages and overcoming adversity. The winner will be the one who arrives at the Inca first and remembers exactly every message that was given to him during his journey.

WHAT IS IT FOR?
Helps stimulate memory and concentration. It immerses you in the fantastic world of chasquis while you have fun playing.

Two games in one: with Pukkum you must use your visual memory to form chains of figures. The more figures you remember, the more points you will earn, but be careful! Your opponent can alter your strategy at any time.

Tottam' is a fast and competitive game that combines the visual-spatial intelligence of Pukkum with the dynamics of card games: form shapes with your pieces, block your opponent's pieces, or steal them. You can play as a team.

Remando En Dulce De Leche is the experience of living in Argentina brought to a board.

You can study, work, save, invest, but the entire environment conspires against you, and the rules of the game change all the time. Hyperinflation, unemployment, taxes and other crises change your plans, and you will come across corrupt people, leaders, kids, businessmen and even hippies with prepaid bills, all willing to complicate your life.

To win, you're going to need to be flexible, fast, but also a lot of luck... Or some contact with the right people.

It is not the one who wins the most who wins, but the one who loses the least. But, yes, we take it with a lot of humor!

Get ready to laugh a lot, and sweat too!

Rota Rota Pelota is a card game with a very simple dynamic, but with a special seasoning: the first to collect four identical cards takes a ball from the table... And scatters the rest. And there, run!, because there are not balls for everyone, and whoever is left without theirs loses.

In Dicey Diggers players take on the role of experimental miner robots, digging tunnels underground to collect riches. These robots are still in prototype phase: they move a bit erratically, and sometimes their pieces fall off for no reason. To make matters worse, the area is peppered with explosive landmines! Try to get the most riches without breaking down!

A roll&write game of drawing tunnels, resource collection and push your luck. Free to print, allows for 1 to 6 players to play simultaneously in about 40min.

—description from the designer

El Árbol de las Aves invites us to learn more about these beautiful species while we have fun trying to place them on a tree defending each place. Each participant has 7 birds and must go around a board to find a place on the tree to place them. Eye! When there are no free spaces you must compete for the place. The first to place all his birds on the top of the tree is the winner.

El Gran Sabor Argentino is a question and answer game about customs, flavors and foods of the Argentine Republic.

It is the first board game in the country that is written in Easy Reading.

Contains: 150 cards (450 questions) – 1 regulation with explainers – 1 Argentine recipe as a gift!

A mini-expansion available from the original KS.

1 Character card: Miss Elizabeth Price

7 Specimen cards: 

     Rose of Philippe Noiselle (2 pts)
     Red Cabbage Rose (3 pts)
     Cabbage Rose (4 pts)
     Rosa Persica (3 pts)
     Austrian Copoer Rose (2 pts)
     Cuspidate Rose (3 pts)
     Roses (England) (1 pt)


1 Title/ Rules card
Two groups of exhausted castaways crawl on a precarious raft or hang from its edges, trying to survive. There is no place for everyone, so they will have to fight to keep La Balsa.

The proposal of this game is to give a voice to History. That the stories are impregnated with the places and landscapes of the Islands, their flora and fauna. "That the stories generate links, that they enable exchanges and encourage reflections, aspects that we consider fundamental and necessary for the strengthening and construction of citizenship.

As usual in the Contame series, the objective of the game is to create and tell a story where the Malvinas Islands are the protagonists.

Includes 4 game suggestions.

Michis is a game of skill and strategy with some chance. In this one, we are going to play gangs of kittens who fight in a house for control of a toy.

The objective of the game is to throw your cat tokens as close to the toy as possible, which will be transformed into points that will allow you to win the game. Michis implements the dynamics of shuffleboard transferred to a board game, with the characteristic of having a dice that will define how to throw the pieces, in addition to each jack having its own effect or special ability.

It rotted and now you have to decide who is going to cook, or worse, who does the dishes. This is a game that will solve a couple of problems for you. Yes, a trivia game to have fun, learn and that also solves questions between your family, group of friends or people you are going to eat with.

Delicatessen is a board game with cards that is especially useful for having fun talking and laughing, with themes and questions about cooking, all in one.

With this game you bring together a group of people who want to have fun, learn or simply decide issues related to cooking, such as who is going to cook today.

This is not a classic board game with questions and answers. This is a game in which all the questions are linked to cooking. What better plan for a rainy afternoon than a cooking board game? There is no, there is no better plan. With this trivia game you will create fun memories and spend unforgettable moments, with guaranteed laughter with your friends, or family.

Shima ("water" in Yagan) invites us to discover a wonderful world, close but at times unknown: our sea.

The species that inhabit the Beagle Channel are presented in this game and invite us on a journey to learn about their ways of life and their importance in the ecosystem. The cards allow us to learn about their interactions as we play!

El Equipo Azul and the Beagle Foundation came together to think up this game that is part of the Educational project "A Window to the Sea", in the province of Tierra del Fuego.

It's two games in one!

On one hand, the different modalities for players +5 years old, which are: Kombaíbi, Yela and Miakasha. Where the objective is to beat or equal the score of the card played by the person immediately before us.

The other is Kaiuala, intended for childhood and first players. With the dynamics of the memo, the winner is whoever finds in the cards that are arranged face down on the table, their four cards with details of the species.
This new bonus pack contains 36 cards, and new solo rules for a single player. Follow the road guideline specs and aim to build multiple highways alone to achieve the best score.

boardgame: Catacombs: Wyldlands KS Playmat for Catacombs 3E
description:
Travel deeper into Wylemuir with this exclusive double-sided neoprene playmat which serves as a companion to the Wyldlands expansion. This map places obstacles and such in different locations unique from other maps resulting in a different playing experience.

Playmat dimensions in inches: 26.7″ x 12.6″
Playmat dimensions in metric: 68 cm x 32 cm

This new playmat was first made available as part of the Catacombs 3E playmat version Kickstarter in 2018.

Logos is a Bible-based solo or cooperative trading card game. Play through the faith timeline with your Faithful, gaining faith to persevere through the places and spaces and complete the locations and events revealed upon the timeline.

Each player starts with 3 Faithful cards, adding their respective Faith token amount to their faith pool. Play happens across the 5 location cards set out on the Faith timeline.

Each round, players reveal a card from the encounter deck on the Faith timeline. These cards either add to the cost of Faith tokens to clear the location or are added to the players hand as a support card to be played during their turn.

Players draw 1 new Faithful from the Faithful deck at the start of each turn, adding to their pool and allowing to play cards to refresh Faithful faith tokens.

Navigating the cards encountered, play ends when player(s) are able to clear the timeline with their acquired Faith tokens or the limit for each location of event/Faithless cards is reached without players successfully clearing respective locations & encounter cards.

Can you persevere across the faith timeline or will you succumb to the faithless?

—description from the publisher

Embark on this game and discover the most incredible places in our world!

Take a card and try to find out what territory that location is in. If you get it right, you will be able to answer a question on the different topics related to geography and culture in general that the letter contains. If you get it right, you get the letter that serves as a stamp in your passport.

In an intelligent and enjoyable way, participants discover and recognize cities, great monuments and important landscapes as if they were traveling the world and experiencing all the culture it offers us.

Discover the world in 90 photos!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Embarque neste jogo e descubra os lugares mais incríveis do nosso mundo!

Pegue uma carta e tente descobrir em que território aquele local está. Se acertar, poderá responder uma pergunta dos diversos temas ligados à geografia e cultura em geral que a carta contém. Acertando, você ganha a carta que serve como carimbo em seu passaporte.

De uma maneira inteligente e gostosa os participantes vão descobrindo e reconhecendo cidades, grandes monumentos e paisagens importantes como se estivessem viajando pelo mundo e experimentando toda a cultura que ele nos oferece.

Conheça o mundo em 90 fotos!

—description from the publisher

While the bulk of the imperial army begins its movement from Olmütz to reach the heart of Bohemia, the  I Austrian Corp of Count Clam-Gallas is ordered to guard the border and collect the retreating Saxon divisions in accordance with the plan drawn up by the general staffs of the two countries. He will then be able to begin his withdrawal to join the rest of the Austrian corps concentrated for the decisive battle envisaged in the purest Napoleonic tradition.

But this is without taking into account the energetic Prussian advances of the Elbe Army and the First Army which, during the battles of Müchengrätz and Gitschin, jostled and disorganized the imperial troops in their retreat.

New module in the “Battles in the Time of Napoleon III” series, Müchengrätz-Gitschin is the third and final game in the series of preliminary battles of the Bohemian campaign of 1866 leading to the major confrontation between the two German powers: the battle of Sadowa/Königgrätz which will be the subject of a big game later.

The “The Great Battles of Napoleon III” series (VaeVictis n° 55, 73, 138, 157 and 165), offers a simple and dynamic system, emphasizing troop engagement in the face of deadly defensive fire.
 Scenarios by Arnold Giraud-Moser on a system by Luc Olivier.
    Game turn: one hour
    Units: regiments and battalions
    Map: one hex. = 750m
    Complexity: 5/10
    Solitaire: ​​7/10
    Playing time: 2 to 4 hours depending on the scenario

Third volume of the campaign of 1866 which also includes Trautenau/Soor and Nachod/Skalitz.

family: Series: Les Grandes Batailles de Napoleon III (Vae Victis)

description: Based on the children's song, a cooperative game that incorporates environmental knowledge into a social-emotional game surrounding an infant whale's effort to reach open water alongside companions.

I'll Huff and I'll Bluff!
Get ready to build your house THEN watch it Blow Away while being deceived by the only person you thought you could trust!

The Big Bad Wolf is an exciting twist on The 3 Little Pigs! Secretly pass the "Big Bad Wolf" then Entice and Persuade players into flipping it, to Blow Down Their House.

Draw 3 then Pass 1 card Face-down to any player of choice.
Get in their heads! By adding face-up materials as incentives.
Chosen player MUST now decide to either flip it or pass it. Is it Materials(points) or is it a Wolf?...

—description from the publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Three Little Pigs

description:
A brain-bending battle of buttons on bridges!

--- TWO PLAYER GAME ---

Place a button from your supply on an empty space next to one of your opponent’s buttons. Or alternatively, move one of your buttons on the board to another space, then remove any button already there and place it on the space you vacated.

Each of your buttons moves along the bridges by the exact number of spaces it indicates, one space at a time. A button cannot move along any bridges in your opponent's colour. Backtracking is also not permitted.

You and your opponent alternate taking turns until one player creates a line of four of their buttons. That player wins the game!

--- SOLO GAME ---

Begin by randomly placing all sixteen buttons on the board.

Your goal is to rearrange the buttons to form two black lines of four and two white lines of four. (For a harder challenge, form the lines in sequence.) Each line must match a line in the opposite colour, using the same values in the same order from the same perspective.

Move any button on the board to another space, using the same movement rules as the two player game. Continue doing this until you achieve your goal!

boardgame: Datos No Opiniones: Drogas Legales (Y de las Otras)
description:
Datos No Opiniones is that crazy and bizarre trivia game that you deserve as an adult. In Drogas Legales (Y de las Otras) edition you will have data, humor and a little irreverence to talk about one of the biggest taboos in our society. Find all kinds of information about drugs, those sold in the pharmacy and those obtained elsewhere, and about the fauna that surrounds them.

The deck has 102 cards with questions about drugs that are answered with some number. To start, they grab a random card, read it aloud and reveal the answer, that is going to be the starting point in the data row.

On your turn, you are going to grab the first question from the deck and, without seeing the answer, you are going to place it to the left or right of the card you used as your starting point. If you think the answer is a greater number than the one in the data row, you put your card on the left and, if you think it is smaller, on the right. When you have chosen where to put the card, you turn it over to see if you were right. If the card is placed correctly, it's the next player's turn, if it's wrong, you take it out and keep it. If you place two cards wrong, you are immediately eliminated.

Alert! As the game progresses there will be more cards in the data row and you will have to take them all into account when placing the question that comes your turn.

IMPORTANT: You can mix the three thematic editions of Datos No Opiniones and play purely Sex, Drugs and Rock n' Roll.

Some others that you had no idea about and will awaken your curiosity while playing.

Bounders is a territorial game played on a hexagonal board of side length 7 or 9, and some light and dark stones. Players decide who goes first, and alternate turns. Each turn, players put a stone of their color on an empty space, adjacent to an equal number of light and dark stones.

When no more moves are available, the game is scored. Players get points for surrounding territories: for each empty region, the player with more stones bounding that region is awarded points according to the number of empty spaces there. Regions next to an equal number of light and dark stones are not counted. A half-point is awarded to the player who did not place the first stone. The player with the higher total score wins.

Bounders was designed by Alek Erickson in March 2024. The game was inspired by Mark Steere's game Hadron and Markus Hagenauer's game Cairo Corridor, and accidentally bears some similarity with Luis Bolaños Mures's game Quinten and Phil Leduc's game Midgard.

—description from the designer

For years, Prohibition has poured Americans a tall glass of austerity and condemned the whole country to a life of secret transgressions. In Chicago Dry, players look for a way around Prohibition to bring bottled fun to Chicago's hidden pubs and speakeasies.

In this game, 2-4 players secretly distribute alcohol across city districts as they face off against rivals and fight for territory, achieving higher scores as they occupy the Chicago central area. The player with the most influence points in both phases of the game wins and becomes the most infamous gangster in town!

Kickstarter exclusive expansion pack which brings 10 new HEROes, 2 new consultants and their corresponding standees.

The cards are available in English, German, French, Italian and Spanish.

What's Kraken?
.... Ghost Captain!

Your ship survived the Kraken, do you have the fortitude now to defeat the Ghost Ships that have set their cannons towards you? Or will your vessel be the next recruit for their fleet?

This is a stand-alone expansion to What's Kraken? a solo roll-and-write, with a clear win/lose condition. At the beginning of each turn you will roll 4 dice. 2 for your Pirates and 2 for the Ghosts. Use your two Pirate Dice to perform actions and use your Crew to give boons. Will you Board the Ghost ships for helpful items and rescue prisoners, Repair your ship, or even Attack the Ghosts? Sink the 3 Ghost Ships to claim you victory! Be careful though - when each ship sinks, so does their unclaimed loot!

Each turn, after your dice are spent, it's time for the Ghosts to Attack! Using the 2 Ghost Dice and polyominos, you'll fill in the two sides of your ship with damage. Be prepared for more damage the more ships there are! If you ever run out of space for the shape you need to place, you sink! And the Ghosts claim their next spectral crew!

—description from the designer

Nightmares and horrors await behind these doors.
You will face many disturbing challenges: each step may be your last.


     Delving cards test your risk-taking tendencies in between areas.
     Torments grant you a small bonus in order to smother the evergrowing madness flooding you. Contain it or you'll face your tormentor!
     Cursed Jewelry give you access to extra abilities that you can refresh at a cost.
     Scrolls are very powerful tools, but you cannot control which one will come to you.


Finally, the new Character, Rooms and final Boss will bring even more hype and replayability to your games!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: '85 Afghanistan: Graveyard of Empires – Alone In The Mountains
description:
Alone in the Mountains is a solitaire expansion for '85 Graveyard of Empires. Utilizing an updated version of the same solitaire system used in Night of Man and '65 Squad Battles in the Jungle of Vietnam, Alone in the Mountains is a boxed game that comes complete with a full sheet of counters, a new map, rules, solitaire action cards, a Player Aid Card and 6 new solitaire scenarios for playing Graveyard of Empires solitaire.

—description from the publisher
This expansion adds 20 new cards to the game.

Le Banquet pour Louis is a simple, fast and highly competitive game in which the players are chefs who try to satisfy every request of King Louis XV. before the big banquet.

In a series of separate rounds, the dishes you cook will be worth a different number of points, and it's up to you to maximize them towards victory. However, you must regularly watch out for other chefs at the table, because each dish activates special, sometimes insidious, mini-actions. This way you can run out of cooked dishes or someone can steal a dish that is just being prepared, but there are also ways of 'defense', for example if you cook a fish dish that gives you a short immunity to the wickedness of other teammates.

In the game, everyone is sweating about the random arrival of the king on a tour of the kitchens. As soon as he knocks on the door (jumps out of the deck of cards with dishes), the game stops, and the king goes from one table to another and tastes the finest dish - i.e. the dish that is worth the most points that particular round. The only way to save your most valuable dish is to distract the king with the king's cat, a card that rarely appears in the deck, and which is equally valued by chefs and the king.

Le Banquet pour Louis is primarily intended for families, has simple rules and is very intuitive. Replayability is guaranteed with a number of different cards with scoring dishes, and the always random appearance of Louis on a tour.

Step into a realm where real animals and nature come to life in the form of trading cards. Let your imagination roam free in this unique game that celebrates the wonders of nature. Embrace the challenge, savor the victories, and connect with the essence of the wilderness. Gaia Cards TCG invites you to embark on an adventure like no other!

Build your decks with your favorite animals and battle against friends by building ecosystems! Change the game with Event & Condition Cards, making the game everchanging and challenging. Each Animal card has educational information and a fun fact- they are truly fun to collect and trade with!

—description from the designer

Assimilation is an asymmetric zone-capture/battling game. Each round, 4 war zones will be up for the taking, but the points you score aren’t only dependent on the zones you capture, you also score points based on the number of troops that have been played to capture the zone, both yours and opponents! Quick-paced thanks to single action turns, each decision you make must build toward your strategic plan for victory.

In Assimilation you’ll battle rival commanders as you strive to outmaneuver whatever tactics come your way with your own clever strategies. Will you hammer down on a single zone or spread your resources wide in an attempt to trick your opponents into sacrificing their valuable resources? Whatever strategy you use, one thing is always true: the commander with the highest number of points at the end of the game wins and there is no prize for second place.

—description from the publisher

Players take turns rolling, moving and placing dice on the gameboard with the goal of creating groups of four or more dice to earn tide-pool animal tokens.

The first player to place 20 tokens on their scoreboard wins.

Evening is created as a family game of Alexander Brady's Interleave.

Like Interleave, it is a turn-less game, and instead of the sightline placement restrictions of the original game, it uses placement restrictions based on group size superiority.

Setup
Use a regular hexagonal board of any size (size 5 is preferred) and a sufficient number of black and white stones. The white player begins the game by first placing one white stone in any empty cell.

Groups
A subset of stones of the same color that are adjacent to each other on the board is called a group. The size of a group is the number of stones it contains. A single stone is also considered a group of size 1.

Gameplay
You can only place your stone in an empty cell where the sum of the sizes of your group adjacent to it is smaller than that of your opponent's group. The number of cells available for placement is recalculated each time a piece is placed.

Game End
The owner of the largest group wins. If the largest group is tied, the next largest group continues to be compared until a difference occurs. If all groups are tied, the black player wins.

Variant: Odd-Even protocol
The white player is responsible for the "odd" pieces and the black player for the "even" pieces.
If all groups are tied in size, the number of pieces of one color (whichever is chosen is equal) is counted. If there are an odd number of pieces, the player responsible for the odd number wins; if there are an even number of pieces, the player responsible for the even number wins.

Control is a finite, draw less territory game based on SNORT. This game is also relevant to Glass Bead, Stigmergy and Tumpletore (a variant of Tumbleweed).

Components

     Hex grid regular hexagonal board of any size (Size 5 or larger)
     Sufficient quantity of white and black pieces (A majority of the number of hexes is sufficient)


Terminology
Control
The player responsible for the majority of the colors of the pieces placed in hexes adjacent to a given hex controls that hex (If there is a tie, neither player controls the hex).

Setup
Each player decides which color he/she is responsible for.
The game begins with the board empty.

Gameplay
First, the white player places a piece of his/her color in one of the hexes bordering the perimeter of the board.
The next black player and onward take turns.
On his or her turn, the player makes one of the following two types of move. Passing is not allowed.


     Place a piece of your color in an empty hex not controlled by the opponent.
     Remove a piece of the opponent's color in a hex controlled by you.


Game End
The player with no legal move in his or her turn loses the game.

If both players are experienced in the game, the game may be terminated by mutual agreement. In that case, count the number of empty hexes under your control and thereby determine the player who loses.

An updated printing of a modern classic! Players will plant peppers, harvest them, cross-breed them, sell them, and make delicious chili to score points!

This edition includes colorblind-friendly plastic peppers, new art, and The Labs Expansion.

—description from the publisher

Archwynd is a competitive, asymmetrical fantasy card game for 1-4 players, age 13 and up. Archwynd offers Solo, Team and PvP modes.

In this game, you choose a Hero who sets forth to save the land of Archwynd from a monstrous invasion. The King will pay a bounty for each monster killed.

Along the way, you will collect magic and treasure to help you buff up for fights, heal lost health, or avoid traps and damage from encounters. The bounty you collect may also be used to make your Hero stronger.

You win the game by being the last Hero standing. There can be only one! If your Hero dies, you lose. But all may not be lost, as special cards, like Raise Dead, can help your Hero live to fight another day.

—description from the designer
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
You have recently created a golem from the finest mud and stone in the land, but have accidentally inserted a scroll for a shopping list instead of your originally planned evil deeds. The golem now is only interested in procuring slices of pie and will not stop crashing through walls until they 1) get all the pie or 2) run out of steam. Can you ensure that the golem, made from your hands, leads a fulfilling life?

How to Play

Game Start: Start your game on the Golem space.
Movement: Cross off an available Steam to roll 1d6. You must move the full amount rolled. You may not backtrack on any spaces moved through on this turn.
Pie: If you move through any number of Pies, mark that many in the Pie scoring area. There are 8 Pies per game board. 
Bonus: If you end your movement on a Pie, mark the Bonus scoring area in addition to the Pie scoring area.
Breaking Walls: You may cross off an available Steam to break a wall while moving. Sometimes this may be the only option you have to ensure you complete your full movement.

Game End

The game immediately ends if you have run out of Steam, all the Pie has been collected, or you are unable to complete your full movement. If you are unable to complete your full movement, your score is 0.

—description from the designer

Fysh is a two player abstract strategy game. Four balls randomly roll into holes that determine the shape needed for victory. Position your fish and rotate the whole board to achieve that shape in this two player abstract strategy game that will have you thinking upside down, sideways, and outside of the fishbowl.

—description from the designer

Ahoy there captains! Seas of Dread is a supplement book for Mantic's game of epic naval warfare: Armada.

Add extra levels of fun and excitement to your games - With all new scenarios, rules for sea monsters and random events as well as interactive terrain ~ and more! The book also expands on the naval background in the world of Pannithor and introduces the next wave of fleets to be released for the Armada game.

—description from the back of the box

boardgame: Path of Civilization: Queen of Sheba Promo Card
description:
Level II Leader

Cost 4 Wisdom

Immediate Effect: Gain 4 Wisdom

Counts as a Spiritual Card

Card number GL-45

boardgame: Path of Civilization: Bruxelles Promo Card
description:
Level II Wonder

Cost 4 Builders

2 VP and counts as a Spiritual Card

Immediate Effect: Gain 1 Population, 1 Military, I Wisdom

Card number GW-41

Rabusie ("Robbers") is a card game in which you play the role of treasure hunters competing to find valuable items in the heart of a forgotten jungle. The game is intended for 2-6 players over 7 years of age, and the average playing time is 20 minutes.

By playing appropriate cards, robbers try to keep the treasures they have collected so far, steal loot from their opponents, and take over those that have not been tried yet and are still in the common pool. Each loot here is equally valuable! In the end, the only thing that will count among robbers is the number of treasures looted. The players' goal is to collect as many cards as possible, because each of them gives 1 point at the end of the game.

Weather in the mountains can change faster than you can bomb a blue run. Better be ready for anything!

Snow Report 6-card expansion adds effects that must be resolved when a Snow Report comes in. Players need to think fast to respond to the changing weather.

Shuffle two Snow Report cards into the deck after setup. Each Snow Report has an effect, either to be resolved immediately by all players or ongoing until another Snow Report comes in. Players may need to alter their planned runs as the weather changes.

boardgame: Mauseschlau & Bärenstark: Wissen & Lachen – Abenteuerliche Tierwelt
description: The players discover the world of animals with their cleverness and strength. They move through the desert, into the rainforest, through Antarctica, into the depths of the ocean and through our local forests on the lovingly designed game board. Along the way they get to know animals that live in these different climate zones. When the players reach a mouse-smart field, they can draw a quiz card and put their knowledge of animals to the test. Whoever can answer the question correctly wins the card. When players reach a Bärenstak space, they can show off their talent at imitating the animals!

You call the terrain park home. Stomp that landing for big points!

Park Rat 6-card expansion adds a new scoring opprtunity. Make sure your run passes through the new features in the right direction, or face the penalty.

Park Rat cards add a new scoring opportunity. They are all numbered 0, so they are always placed at the bottom of the shared column, and have features with arrows which affect scoring. Whenever a player’s scoring run passes through a feature in the direction of its arrows, they gain the number of points shown. If it passes in the opposite direction, they lose that many points.

This part of the mountain is for expert skiers only! Scout out the run before beginning your descent.

Black Diamond 6-card expansion adds the Scout ability to reserve a card to play later.

Black Diamond 19-24 cards add the new ability, Scout, which allows you to reserve a card and add it to your map at any point later in the game.

LAG is a two-player abstract strategy game. Based on transversal games, your pieces move in a straight line a number of steps equal to the number of groups of your pieces. The goal is to have fewer groups when the active player runs out of legal moves.

LAG is named after the lag caused by process failures in digital games.

Setup
This game is played on a regular hexagonal board of appropriate size (size 4 for practice, size 5 for regular play).
Each player decides which color they are responsible for and places pieces of their color in the two rows on their side.
For example, in the case of size 5, the pieces are placed as shown in the diagram.

Terminology
Object - When pieces of the same color are adjacent and connected together, they form a single "object" as a whole.
(Defined in the same way as so-called groups, the name is just a flavor)

Gameplay
The White player moves first and then turns alternate.
The active player moves one piece of his/her color. Passing is not allowed.

-Movement
Your piece moves in a straight line along the grid for a distance exactly equal to the number of objects of your color on the board at the beginning of the turn. Pieces cannot be moved off the board or into cells already occupied by pieces of friendly color.
Pieces can move to any cell with a piece of the opponent's color, and then that opponent's piece is captured by replacement and removed from the game.
On the move, a piece can jump over any number of other pieces, regardless of color.

-Restrictions
Moving a piece forward is always allowed.
If a piece is moved sideways, the number of objects of its color must be reduced after the move.
Moving a piece backward is not allowed.

Game end
The game ends when there are no legal moves left for the active player.
The player with fewer objects wins.
If there is a tie, the player who ended the game, i.e., the player who no longer has a legal move, wins.

boardgame: Aetherstream: Interceptor – Callistonian Empire Squadron Set
description:
The Imperatrix has made the call and Callistonian's are answering. New fighters need pilots and crews to keep the Commonwealth at bay. The Empire will need all of its resources and ingenuity to fend off their military might. New tactics and resources will help but it will be those on the front lines who will make the difference and pay the price.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Awaken Realms Vault: Story Dice – AR Board Game Dice Bundle Box
description: Contains all 5 AR Board Game dice sets.

A card game in which your objective is to roast the perfect mallow by placing it on the campfire. Watch your gooey points stack up as the longer your mallow is on the fire the more points it's worth. Beware however this makes your mallow vulnerable to attack from the other players and you must be clever when you take off your mallow to keep it from getting too crispy !

Players have the chance to destroy your fluff pillows and you must be carful of unsavory characters. You will have a chance to defend yourself with the mighty mallow knight but fate can be a cruel mistress so be warned. There are plenty of special cards including some naughty bears that come to steal your food, a very sexy ladies man mallow who steals your precious chocolates and several other misfit mallows.

The player with the most gooey tokens at the end of the game will be declared the winner.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Path of Civilization: Woody Promo Card
description:
An additional level 1 leader card

Card GL-47

When played, draw a level 1 wonder

New York Noir is a film-noir, narrative mystery detective/deduction game, set in New York City, and spanning several decades from the 1930s through the 1950s. It's a cooperative/solo game in the spirit of Sherlock Holmes Consulting Detective, with a focus on longer cases meant to be played over several virtual days. Investigate crimes, identify evidence, and bring justice to the victims.  Along the way you’ll need to make tough moral choices that affect how the story unfolds.  Our focus is on supporting fan made cases.

Additional credits: Logan Patrick Hill (map art); Adam Hall (box art & design; rulebook co-design); Debbie Levy (case editing); Ani (rulebook co-design); Daria (park art); Lux (criminal history directory, etc.)

Navia is a wild downstream race where rival clans compete for control of the forest.

Every spring, with the thaw and the rains, the Northern rivers turn into dangerous water racing tracks. Otters, badgers, hedgehogs, and foxes compete to win the favor of the goddess Navia and extend their dominion over the territory throughout the year, until the next spring.

Lead your clan through fast currents and dangerous obstacles. Steal, sabotage, use hidden powers, attack your rivals, and earn the favor of the goddess Navia. Anything goes to win. The problem is that your rivals will do the same.

—description from the publisher

---
Navia es una alocada carrera río abajo en la que los clanes rivales compiten por el control del bosque.

Cada primavera, con el deshielo y las lluvias, los ríos del Norte se convierten en peligrosas pistas de carreras acuáticas. Nutrias, tejones, erizos y zorros compiten por lograr el favor de la diosa Navia y extender su dominio sobre el territorio durante todo el año, hasta la próxima primavera.

Lidera a tu clan a través de rápidas corrientes y peligrosos obstáculos. Roba, sabotea, usa poderes ocultos, ataca a tus rivales y logra el favor de la diosa Navia. Todo vale para ganar. El problema es que tus rivales harán lo mismo.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

An abstract educational board-game that combines Filipino checkers or “Dama” and basic Mathematical operations. A tabletop game used in schools around the Philippines.

Players alternatingly move their numbered chips from 0-11 forward diagonally in a checkerboard filled with operation symbols. Pieces are required to capture opponent's pieces that are adjacent to them and can be skipped upon by a vacant square diagonally across it; the player must them compute the resulting answer between the two numbers using the operation in which their piece landed on. Numbers which reach the end of the board are called “Dama” pieces, and has the power to move as many spaces diagonally as they want, scores made with this piece are doubled after resolving the operation, two “damas” capturing one another are then multiplied by four.

The game ends when there are no more moves for a player, a player loses all of their pieces, or if the time runs out, and the player with the higher score at the end of the game wins.

Hidden roles game, a kind of Argentinian Mafia.

There are Chantas between us. Among the art that is seen in the graffitied streets there is hidden one or more chantas. We must burn them, before they burn us all!

Objective: there are two teams; that of the trout letters (including those of chantas) and that of the posts. The posts win when they burn (leave out) all the black cards and the trout win when there is a black card among the last 3 unburned cards.

Ideal for gatherings, super transportable.

Chamuyo: Expansión ESI is a tool to address Comprehensive Sexual Education (ESI) with young people and adults. Once again, Chamuyo offers you the opportunity to talk about everything that interests you. We choose words that are triggers and enable a playful experience crossed by ESI.

Take a position and seek to defend it to win!

Includes 23 cards (4.5×6.4cm) and Rules.

This expansion includes definitions that frame the words in the curricular guidelines of Law 26,150: National Comprehensive Sexual Education Program.

You need Chamuyo: A full! or Chamuyo: De una! to be able to use them!

This is a Kickstarter-exclusive version of Momiji that contains within the box: Momiji base game, the Ancient Garden expansion, the Squirrel module, the Golden Leaves module, and component upgrades (cardboard tiles and wooden tokens).

The box is noticeably larger than the retail version.

The Deluxe version contains the following enhancements to the components and gameplay:

     Recessed player mats
     Shaped and screen-printed meeples
     Gametrayz insert
     5th player components
     Robotic Wanderer mini expansion
     Orca reintroduction mini expansion
     Underwave Academy mini expansion
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: According to the Kickstarter campaign, includes: A Kickstarter edition box, Solo mode (Gutenberg) expansion, Mini Expansion 1 - Secret contract, and Mini expansion 2 - Gold and leather

Li Shizhen (李時珍) is a character promo made available during the Taiwan crowdfunding campaign and again during the Kickstarter campaign.

Li Shizhen was called a boy genius for passing the college exams to become a Xiucai (秀才) at the young age of 14. But despite his best efforts, he could not pass the triennial provincial exams to become a Juren, and quit after three tries.

However, he eventually became a well-regarded doctor, and after three decades of extensive research, he authored the largest encyclopedia of oriental medicine and herbology ever written, the 本草綱目 (Bencao Gangmu) or translated as Compendium of Materia Medica.

When utilized as a player's Scholar, Li Shizhen will grant them double the Discard Bonus on their turns. As an Assistant, a player may spend $1 to activate a Discard Bonus on their used or drafted tiles. All together, Li Shizhen is a highly adaptable and practical character to use.

—description from the publisher

Climate Crisis is a two player card game about saving the planet for two players. To win the game, be the first player to save Earth's three domains land, air and water.

At the start of a battle, each player puts down a number card from their hand (face-down) and declares the card’s suit and number. When they do, it can be the truth or a lie. The game rules determine which card wins a battle along with strategy, wit, bluffing, and accusations. After the battle, the winner draws an Action card. If a player collects three action cards that could be used to claim a domain, they must show the cards to their opponent and explain how each card relates to saving that domain. Players can trade their action cards if they follow certain rules. The first player to save 2 domains wins the game!

—description from the designer

Win the favor of the Geishas with all new tiles and tactics!

Your favorite Geishas are back with more talent and graceful performances! With the "Mini Expansions", you may play this award-winning 2-player game with even more tactics! The "Mini Expansions" regroup the 7 mini expansions (#1 to #7) of the kickstarter for the retail market. The material is different from the kickstarter version : you have only 2 sets of 4 Action Markers (4 for each player) numbered #1 to #4, and 7 cards - each card give a different combination for the Action Markers #1 to #4.

Mini Expansions may only be played with "Hanamikoji".

Use the base gameplay rules with the Action Markers.

Changes to Setup: When playing with an expansion, each player take a set of 4 markers and play with the following modifications:
- At the start of a round, you no longer remove a card from the Item Card Deck.
- Put discarded cards face down in the game box and keep hidden throughout the round.

Thematic game about Bitcoin mining, in american style. The game can be played in two modes: as a simple party game or as a standard boardgame. Both modes can be played from 2 to 8 players, but for 6 or more players the party game mode is suggested.

Players are miners that start to mine Bitcoin at home, by betting energy and selling Bitcoin to get more energy. When played as a party game, the game requires very little room for the game board and just few components: players can sit around the board, or even few meters away, since the main mechanics are just bidding energy to get Bitcoin cards and sellig cards to get energy. A complete game is played in 4 eras, where each era represents one 4 years cycle (Bitcoin's halvings). Players can agree in advance to play just 1, 2 or 3 eras, for a shorter game.

The party game and standard game modes shares the same mechanics, the only difference is that when the game is played as party game the Bitcoin cards are used just as victory points, that is: the text printed on the cards is ignored. On the opposite, when played as a standard boardgame, an Action phase is added to end of each game turn. During this Action phase players can play the cards to get benefits, and trigger some special actions like stacking and upgrades. This adds more strategy to the game experience and reduce luck dependances.

The standard boardgame leads miners to choose their favourite approach to Bitcoin. Depending by cards and strategy, each player can decide to use Bitcoin just as an economic asset, to improve it as software developer, to mine more Bitcoins improving his rigs, or even act as a human science expert, exploiting the social and psychological aspectes of Bitcoin. These choices are offered by the four types of cards: Economics, Engineering, Human Sciences or Informatics. Specializing in only one approach it's not common, therefore usually each players will build a mixed profile with all these skills.

Because investing time it's often better than investing money.

The players take turns to roll and move. The race track is formed by eight wooden planks. On some variable positioned spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to complete one or more laps wins the game.

—user summary

Play at reconstructing the chronological timeline of events and put history in order! From the creation of the world to the last judgment, find 60 biblical events from the Old and New Testament. Chronicle - The Bible is a learning game in which we have fun memorizing the chronology of events gradually and durably. Ideal for the curious who want to discover or rediscover the Bible while having fun.

—description from the publisher

New expansions bring deeper strategy and enhanced deduction to a game that asks one not-so-simple question — can you hide in plain sight?

The Tribunal expansions supercharge both the strategy & deduction in Veiled Fate.


    The Hadria expansion is focused on introducing more strategic variables to the game and adds a new miniature, renown tracker, and favor cards.
    The Celestials expansion is focused on introducing more deduction and misdirection to the game and adds a new miniature, renown tracker, spell cards, and 7 celestial cards.
    The Servants expansion adds 3 new miniatures and cards. Servants can be commanded by all players.
    The Renewal Dice replaces the permanent 'refresh card' effect that normally activates in the Pools, creating more dynamic gameplay and ever-shifting strategies surrounding how players use the Abyss.

Check 10  is a two-player, abstract strategy board game. You play with either the white or black pieces. Each player has 16 pieces, numbered 1-8. The aim is to score more points than your opponent. You can score points by capturing pieces of your opponent and by promoting your own pieces.

You capture pieces if the combined value of your own pieces and your opponent's pieces is exactly 10 when making groups. A group consists of any pieces that are connected diagonally or orthogonally.

You can promote pieces by reaching the opponent's end of the board. Then, your piece can no longer be removed and is worth points at the end of the game. Moreover, you can remove a piece of the same value from your opponent if it is available.

#SocialDevastation the game where words have power and the internet is your canvas! In #SocialDevastation, players unleash their creativity by filling in the blanks on social cards, then posting them online to see what unfolds. With hilarious social prompts and potentially devastating results, #SocialDevastation turns social media into a daring adventure.

—description from the designer

If you find a cat and its costumed companion, keep the cards and take another turn.

Are you fe-line lucky? Take it in turns to pick two cards to reveal. If a cat looks fur-miliar, match it with its costumed counterpart! The player with the most matches win...

A more advanced version of the classic memory game. 
Instead of revealing two matching cards, the goal is to reveal and match a plain cat with another card of the same cat wearing a costume.

boardgame: Casting Shadows: Cheeky Chinchilla Promo Card
description: Card Text: "Companion - Each time you Collect a Counterspell card, look at the top 2 cards of the Counterspell deck. Add one face down to your Spell Book, then return the other card to the bottom of the deck."

boardgame: Tic Tac K.O. 2024 Convention Exclusive Objective Promo
description:
The Tic Tac K.O. 2024 Convention Exclusive Objective Promo card is given away at the Unstable Games convention booth with purchase of a Tic Tac K.O. game or game related item. It has the "CON" expansion/set symbol that Unstable Games began placing on Convention cards in 2024.

Card text:
When you take this Objective card, reveal it immediately. Draw 3 cards, then return this Objective card to the bottom of the Objective deck and take a new Objective card.

It is not unheard of for some combatants to make a life of fighting daily in the Pits. Linzara 'The Cyclops' is such a combatant.

The rules of the pits are not written anywhere and there is nothing that suggest that the combatants must outright challenge the Pit Lord when their trials are complete, but this is often the last mistake that fresh combatants make.

Lovingly referred to as 'The Cyclops' ever since she lost one eye from a strike by a Clawfoot's tail stinger. She used the very stinger to impale the beast in the same combat trial. Linzara is content to enter the Pits each day, emerging from her hideout deep in the decrepit depths of the pit tunnels. From here she makes short work of the enemies that emerge and puts on a fine show for the people of the Megopolis. After her three challenges are complete, she opts not to attempt the fourth and instead disappears back into the tunnels only to emerge the next day. Deep within depths of the pit tunnels thrives a secret city where only the most brutal survive. This is the place Linzara calls home.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel Dice Masters: The Dark Phoenix Saga
description:
Dice Masters brings Collectible Dice Building to the tabletop with this award-winning competitive game set in the Marvel universe! Dice Masters is a global hit from WizKids. It uses the same proprietary Dice Building Game platform designed by Mike Elliott and Eric M. Lang. Each Countertop Display Contains 8 Draft Packs. Draft Packs contain cards and dice that can be added to your collection as normal but are ALSO optimized for draft.

Brand new teams will make their Dice Masters debut! Players will get to collect Xavier’s students, intergalactic allies, and cosmic enemies in all new ways! Even if they’re already in your Dice Masters collection – these heroes will play like you’ve never seen them before.

With over 150 different cards (and their dice!) to collect, this set will help you bring some Uncanny excitement to the tabletop.

boardgame: Exiled Legends: Crane Sensei Promo Card
description:
November 2019 Convention Exclusive

Card Type: Light
Max HP: 3
Strength: 2

Champion Ability: When Crane Sensei is attacked, roll 1d6. On 5-6, the attacking Champion takes the damage instead.
Support Ability: Remove 1 damage counter from your Champion.

NOVA (means Narrative of visual actual) is a Japanese Trading Card Game. In this game, two players construct their own 40-to-60-card main deck and a 10-card character deck to fight with each other, the player who makes the opponent's life point from 2000 to 0 will win the game.
A special point of this game is the character deck. Each player can choose a character from this deck to play once per turn, and the level of character to choose will decided by player's life point, when a player's life point reduce to 1500 then he/she can use lv.2 character and when a player's life point reduce to 1000 then he/she can use lv.3 character.

Stuck on an ice flow, floating aimlessly in the frigid ocean, two opposing factions of animals have been stranded on this piece of ice for days now. The leaders from both factions, sly foxes, have realized that the ice flow is melting and before too long the ice flow will tip, leaving one side under water. Sneaky and self-serving, the fox leaders of both factions have concocted a plan to send their clueless faction members to the opposite side of the ice flow to weigh down the opposite side. The question is, whose factions will implement the plan quicker?

Its a race to send your animals to the opposite side of the ice flow to save your side from plunging into the frigid waters. Each animal has their own particular way of moving and special actions. As you work animals to the other side, be sure not to have your fox stray onto your opponent‛s side, for they are trying to preserve their lives.

If you can get three groups of animals to the opposite side, while keeping your fox on your side of the ice flow, you win!

Tipping ice is a two player, area control game where you are trying to move your game pieces to the opposite side of the game board. There are five groups of animals that you can score with.

—description from the designer
A 2 players card game based on chemistry. Use atom cards to build molecules and score points based on their molar mass.

boardgame: 鱼的回忆 (The Sea of Memory)
description:
My memories are fading away. After many years, I called upon those old friends to once again gather around, just like when we were young, and chat about the distant past.

We try to recount memories, to rediscover the bonds between us, racing against the clock before we forget ourselves.

The Sea of Memory is a cooperative narrative memory game. Players take on the role of an elderly person whose memory is declining, recalling past events through photographs from their youth. By playing cards representing these photos according to the rules, and selecting the most memorable ones to include in their memoir, players work towards the goal of recalling every photo and its accompanying story by the end of the game.

我的记忆，快要消失了。时隔多年，我喊上那些过去的伙伴，再一次围坐一起，像少年时那样，聊起遥远的日子。
我们试着讲述记忆，找回彼此的羁绊，赶在忘记自我之前。

《鱼的回忆》是一款合作叙事记忆类游戏。玩家将扮演一名年事已高、记忆衰退的老年人，通过年轻时的照片回忆过去的往事。按照规则打出代表照片的卡牌，并选出印象最深刻的照片放入回忆录。在游戏结束时需要将所有的照片与故事一一回忆起来。

boardgame: 宋韵西湖 (West Lake: A Piece of Song)
description:
You and your friends have finally arrived at this enchanting place, the West Lake, which has inspired countless poets and scholars with its poetic beauty, where the lake's shimmer and the mountains' grandeur vie for attention. As you marvel at the breathtaking scenery of the West Lake, you embark on a quest to gather photographs of the legendary "Ten Attractions of the West Lake," while inquiring about the myriad stories that have unfolded here, immersing yourselves in the charm of the Song Dynasty. The goal is to return home filled with fascinating anecdotes and insights, ready to share your newfound expertise as a West Lake know-it-all with your companions.

你与小伙伴终于到达了这个地方，这个文人墨客赋诗赞美，湖光山色争奇斗艳的地方——西湖。现在你需要一边欣赏西湖的绝美景色，收集传说中“西湖十景”的照片，一边打听这里发生的各色故事，体验宋朝韵味。好在返程的时候跟你的小伙伴分享你收集到的有趣见闻，成为西湖万事通。

boardgame: 京杭大运河 (Grand Canal: From Beijing to Hangzhou)
description:
The Grand Canal, stretching from Beijing to Hangzhou, traverses China from north to south, connecting five major river systems. It is the longest artificial river in the world. The Grand Canal has always been a vital channel for grain transport and commercial travel in China. In this game, players take on the role of the Grand Canal Administrator in the Ming Dynasty, tasked with fulfilling the grain transport mission assigned by the government while also establishing connections with local areas to open up trade routes. The game's primary mechanics consist of action point selection, engine building, and area control.

京杭大运河，北起北京，南抵杭州，纵贯中国南北，沟通五大水系，是世界上最长的人工河。 大运河一直是中国漕运和商旅往来的重要通道。在本游戏中，玩家扮演明朝的漕运总督，需要在完成官府的运粮任务的同时，也与地方建立联系，打通商路。游戏的主要机制为行动点选择+卡牌引擎构筑+区域控制。

boardgame: 自在西游 (Monkey King: Arena of Heroes The Board Game)
description:
自在西游 "Monkey King: Arena of Heroes The Board Game" is a Chinese art-style card game set against the backdrop of classical Eastern mythology,Journey to the West, adapted from a mobile game of the same name. Players embark on a journey reminiscent of the classic tale, where they subdue demons and monsters by dispatching divine generals from four races: Saints, Immortals, Spirits, and Demons. Traverse mountains and seas on a thrilling adventure, striving for combat rewards.

This game boasts a fast-paced rhythm and straightforward rules, making it an ideal gathering card game that values both strategy and luck.

《自在西游》是一款以东方古典神话为背景的国风卡牌游戏，改编自同名手游。玩家将在游戏中重走西游降妖伏魔之旅，派遣圣、仙、冥、妖四大种族神将，穿越山海冒险，争取战斗奖励。
游戏节奏明快，规则简洁，策略与运气并重的聚会类卡牌游戏。

A civilization driven to war.

An entire Clan brought to ruin.

And the course of human history forever changed by…

CLAN SPANIEL!

For generations, every freeborn and trueborn in the Kerensky Cluster was raised in the Way of the Clans, learning their culture’s traditions, taboos, caste roles, and philosophies through The Adventures of Clan Spaniel. Clan children were engrossed with the Spaniels’ missions to defeat the evil Monkey Tribes and restore the Star League, subconsciously forging their own identities in the process.

But darker secrets lurked behind the unblinking lens of the holoprojector. Now, for the first time, learn the true story behind the critter crusaders and the sinister agenda beneath their wholesome façade.
Read on, if you dare!

—description from the publisher

Tetroku is a tetromino-based roll-and-write game where you score points using a simple sudoku-style system. It plays in about 20-40 minutes, depending on the number of players. There is no limit to the number of players who can play (from 1 to 999 or more!).

The only components required are a six-sided die for the group, plus a single game sheet and pencil per player.

—description from the designer

Mighty Meerkats – Memory Roulette

				
				
					OverviewA clever twist on a classic, with surprises, hidden abilities, and heroic meerkat families buried in the sand.

In Mighty Meerkats: Memory Roulette, the meerkat families of Mirkatopolis are trapped under the scorching desert sands. It’s your job to dig them up and reunite them, while dodging distractions, hidden tricks, and the occasional flying sandal.

At its core, this is a memory game, but with a Fried Potatoes twist. Nine cards lie face-down on the sand plane, and each round you draw a new card from the pile, placing it face-up. Then, you flip one card already on the sand plane. If they match, you rescue that meerkat family and get to dig up two more cards as a bonus. If not, both cards are flipped back and your turn ends.

Hidden among the cards are Mighty Cards, powerful specials that can change the game in an instant. Some are helpful, like Visionary Vantage, which lets you peek at and reorder the top three draw cards, while Earthquake reshuffles the entire sand plane. Just hope you don’t hit Desert Danger, it ends your turn and skips your next!

The more mistakes players make, the more the desert fills with cards, and the harder it becomes to remember what’s where. Smart placements, sharp memory skills and focused attention are the keys to saving the most families before the desert takes over.

For the bravest adventurers, there’s the Mighty Meerkats Challenge. In this solo mode, your mission is to reunite every meerkat family before the sand pile runs out. Only if you rescue all the families in time can you claim victory.

				
				
					Key Mechanics
        Flip cards to find matching meerkat families hidden under the desert
        Watch out for tricks, traps, and surprise cards that change the board
        Sharpen your memory, strengthen your focus, and improve your recall with every turn


				
				
					Gameplay Facts
        1–8 players | Ages 7+ | 20 minutes
        Includes a premium suede playmat
        Learn in under 2 minutes. Easy enough for kids, clever enough for adults
        Plays differently every time: no two deserts are alike


				
				
					Components
     54 Meerkat Family cards
     12 Mighty Cards
     Premium suede playmat
     Rulebook


				
				
					Part of the Fried Potatoes UniverseA connected series of light, fast-playing card and board games, each with its own flavour and twist.

Archwynd Vale is an expansion set for Archwynd  which provides support for up to 6 players. Archwynd Vale provides additional Hero cards, Monsters, Traps and all-new magic items -- as well as advanced rules that enable more extensive customization of each Hero.

Dive into the world of math like never before with the new and improved Multimania! This latest version of the beloved multiplication board game brings a whole new level of fun, learning, and quality to your fingertips. Ideal for kids aged 6+, Multimania transforms number crunching into an epic adventure where speed, strategy, and smarts lead to victory.

With its enhanced gameplay, vibrant components, and high-quality materials, this edition introduces fresh challenges and more ways to play. Navigate duels, leverage handicaps, and enjoy individual 30-second sprints with our newly included components, ensuring that each round is packed with excitement and educational value.

Whether it's a family game night, a classroom learning session, or a solo quest for math mastery, Multimania is your go-to for interactive and engaging multiplication practice. Experience the upgraded game where every flip of a card, tick of the clock, and placement of a pawn immerses players in a world where math is endlessly entertaining. Embrace the new Multimania – where learning meets joy and every calculation counts!

—description from the publisher

1099 A.D. Crusaders conquer Jerusalem, setting the worlds ablaze. 
This city, sacred to all, instead of becoming a symbol of unity it became a symbol of endless war. The armies of two Gods rally their strenghts. 
The Christians hold the newly formed Crusader states but the Muslim warriors, fierce and unrelenting, are ready to reclaim their lands. 
But the outcome of the battles in the Holy Lands cannot be easily forseen. 
Will you emerge victorious or will you be forgotten in the sands of time?

Crusades is a card-driven (semi cooperate) light wargame depicting the series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims to control the holy lands. Each player takes the roles of Christian and Muslim leaders with objective to siege, conquer cities and fight to control the christian kingdoms of Cilician Armenia and Eastern Roman Empire (Byzantio). Also at the same time they must defend their imperial cities from their enemies.
Τhe game lasts 7 rounds and in each round two historical event cards determine the conditions and the battles of the game.

At the end of the game

     the control of Jerusalem 
     the number of objective cities conquered
     the number of winning battles 
     the influence/control of the christian kingdoms of Cilician Armenia and Eastern Roman Empire 


are compared and the side with the most wins the game!!!

Strategize wisely, it's the only way to control the Holy Lands !!!

description:
Napoli 1799 is a non-simulation-based card wargame included with issue number 34 of the Italian gaming magazine IoGioco.

In January 1799, the Republic in Naples was proclaimed after tumultuous days of uprising, involving Jacobins, a division of the French army, and Lazzari. By June, a papal army counterrevolution, supported by the English fleet, ended the Republic and executed the Republicans.

Each side fought along with its own vision of freedom, and the game focuses on these tumultuous dynamics.

In this game, the designer and artist is a famous italian cartoonist, known with the nickname of Pera Toons. 
There are 20 cards depicting a funny joke involving two or three characters. Then there are all cards with a single character depicted on them that are drawn by players. When a player has the two or three characters belonging to the target funny-joke card, he or she can claim it and score points. In addition there are special cards that allow better interactions among players. 
A "bomb card" is shuffled in the deck and, when its' revealed, the game ends and the player with most points wins.

Metti insieme gli elementi giusti per una battuta, gli altri giocatori ti ostacoleranno, ti anticiperanno, ti faranno arrabbiare... ...ma tu farai lo stesso! Una sfida esplosiva... perchè prima o poi... TIC-TAC-TIC-TAC...

There are 50 cards depicting either an animal or an action, with nice and funny drawings perfectly understandable by very young players (3 years old). The first player secretly look at the top card and try to mimic the animal or the action to have the other players guessing. The player which guesses the card wins it and in turn look at the following card and mimic.

After all cards are played, the player with the most cards wins the game :-)

Il tradizionale gioco dei mimi fatto su misura per i più piccoli. Vince chi indovina il maggior numero di imitazioni. E tu sai imitare una rana?

Snow White, The Little Mermaid, Cinderella, and many other legendary princesses are celebrating a five-day party. The prince charmings of the land, who have not been invited, have infiltrated the ball to propose marriage. As an independent princess, you have to avoid marriage proposals and remain single. Avoid the most proposals and become the Rebel Princess!

Rebel Princess takes place over five rounds, representing the five days of a party, and each round has a special rule that makes each game totally different. The general mechanisms are those of trick-taking games, in which each player plays a numbered card into each trick, following one of the four suits in the game. The player with the highest number of the suit that started the trick takes all the cards of that trick — but that's not necessarily good as players want to avoid taking cards with prince charmings, who each bring one marriage proposal, aside from the enchanted frog who brings five proposals. The player with the fewest marriage proposals after five rounds wins.

Importantly, each player assumes the role of a different princess and has a special ability that they can use once per round.

Compared to the original edition, Rebel Princess Deluxe Edition features:

     Two new Princesses and six new Round cards
     Princess cards upgraded to thick punchboard tiles
     Round cards upgraded to Tarot-sized cards with rules text on each card
     Balance tweaks to existing rules, rounds, and princesses
     A new "Rebel of the Ball" mechanism to allow players to catch up if they are falling too far behind
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Sleeping Beauty
