description:
Forge of Fists is a two-player deckbuilding card game set in the land of Almokon, which is ruled by an old and wise emperor, Manfred the Most Loved. Unfortunately, Manfred is dying (for he is old) and upon his death he knows his empire will crumble (for he is wise). To maintain peace in Almokon, Manfred must decide, now, who is to wear the crown of the empire upon his passing.

You, the player, are one of many monarchs vying for Manfred’s favor. Your ability to rule, to protect, and to grow your realm will determine if you will be the next emperor of Almokon.

Each player begins the game with a unique monarch, city, and deck. Players then take turns playing cards from their hand - improvements, characters, cults, and events - to build up their realm and launch attacks on their opponent by land and sea. The first player to win Manfred's favor or obliterate their enemy is the winner.

—description from the designer

Inspired by the best-selling book Masala Lab by Krish Ashok, you will prep various Indian dishes while trying to rack up the highest score.

Masala Lab Mini is a set-collection game, where you have to collect food "Items" to make your "Prep", in a way such that the Items match the recipe of the Prep. An interesting card-overlap mechanism means you need to collect the Items in a particular sequence shown on the recipe. The game ends when the one player collects 7 Prep cards, but the winner is the one who scored the highest! Watch out for penalties for incomplete Prep. A game lasts 20-30 minutes.

This is a "mini" version of the game Masala Lab, and is sold separately.

—description from the publisher

This quick-playing game features trivia questions from the years 1980 to 1990. Since it includes cards only, you can take it to parties, get-togethers, on vacation, and more. On your turn, another player will ask you a question; get it right to keep the card. The first person to earn 5 cards wins! Answer fun trivia questions like "What kind of laceless shoes were sometimes worn with a coin in the tongue?" and "What hairstyle was described as 'business in the front, party in the back'?"

—description from the publisher

Silhouette junior - 10th Anniversary Edition - the creative memory game adapts to every stage of the child's development. Whether searching for couples, assigning colors, bringing details together or finding terms. Everything is possible! Without being overwhelmed or under-challenged, but with even more fun, Silhouette junior promotes observation skills, color understanding and language development.

When finding terms, it is easier to find the pairs of cards if they have the same color combination as well as small, repeating details. If the cards are placed with eye contact, the correct order of the searched term is automatically determined.

The Anniversary Edition includes 72 cards (12 more than the original edition)
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
The famous and eccentric paleontologist John Jamón has entrusted you with a mission. You must find the fossils of four dinosaurs and move them to the museum playing your cards wisely. Whoever gets it first will become your assistant, with the prestige that comes with it...

If you have completed an excavation, at the beginning of your turn you will be able to move the fossils to the museum.

Additionally, you must do one of two actions:


     Play a card
     Discard as many cards as you want


At the end of the turn you will have to draw as many cards as necessary to have 3 again.

—description from the publisher (translated)

El afamado y excéntrico paleontólogo John Jamón os ha encomendado una misión. Debéis encontrar los fósiles de cuatro dinosaurios y trasladarlos al museo jugando sábiamente tus cartas. Quien primero lo consiga se convertirá en su ayudante, con el prestigio que conlleva... ¡E incluye dietas!

Si has completado una excavación en tu turno anterior, al inicio del siguiente podrás trasladar los fósiles al museo.

Además, deberás realizar una de dos acciones:


     Jugar una carta
     Descartar tantas cartas como quieras


Al final del turno tendrás que robar tantas cartas como sean necesarias para volver a tener 3.

—description from the publisher

Inspired by the game For the Queen, Alibis, is a card-driven story-building game that you can begin playing in minutes, without any RPG experience.
The gameplay relies of a simple mechanism: draw a question card, read it out loud and answer it out loud.
One question at a time, create a criminal investigation (as silly or serious as you want it to be).
Being prompt driven, the game is infinitely replayable. It comes with 6 crime scenes, 15 illustrated characters, 8 causes of death, 14 clues, 25 questioning cards, 12 newspaper front pages and 12 jury reactions.

The first player reveals a crime scene, the second reveals the identity of the victim; the next, the cause of death.
Then in clockwise order, everyone chooses a clue or a suspect until there is as many suspect as there are people playing and 3 clues.

The questioning of each suspect is driven by card prompts.
The players secretly attribute culpability tokens of different values to the suspects.
The players then vote for the suspect they think has the most culpability.
The players can then introduce the classification of the crime and mitigating factors before the suspect is sentenced.

The players now play as journalists writing about the case by using 2 out of 3 cards to present an event occuring during the trial.
Only then will the culpability tokens be revealed and the truth exposed.

The world was at peace until the discovery the "weapons". Strange mechanical titans made of stone and metal that lay dormant depths of the world. Now the tribes are in disagreement in what should be done to these mysterious artifacts.

Ancient Future is a competitive card game for 1-4 players with no direct combat. Players will create a deck of 30 cards and compete to be the first to either destroy or awaken 2 of the artifacts that are in the center of the table.

Ancient Future features an innovative depleting resource system that has players start with 10 resources to spend and each turn refresh all resources and remove one resource from the game, slowly depleting their pool from turn to turn. The game also ends when the last resource is removed at which point the player holding the highest value artifact is the winner.

—description from the designer

SOCIALISTS
STEALING IN A PANDEMIC IS A GAME

This board game is ideal to give to that left-handed friend who doesn't want to work and needs to pass the time while waiting for his pay.

Play without complexes, because they have been playing with you for a long time.

—description from the publisher

SOCIALISTOS
ROBAR EN PANDEMIA ES UN JUEGO

Este juego de mesa es ideal para regalar a ese amigo zurdo que no quiere trabajar y necesita pasar el tiempo mientras espera su paga.

Juega sin complejos, porque hace tiempo que ellos juegan contigo.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Immerse yourself in the epic grandeur of ancient Greece with Epireia, a captivating board game where you command one of six cities: Sparta, Athens, Elis, Delphi, Corinth, or Larissa. As the city's Administrator, you're tasked with a monumental challenge: to elevate your city-state to unparalleled heights of power and influence.

Epireia is designed around three scenarios, leading to a final confrontation.

A round in Epireia is segmented into three phases to mirror the complexities of ancient leadership. The development Phase is where your visionary planning comes to life, allowing you to construct buildings that are the envy of your rivals and develop your prestige to your citizen. 
During the Diplomacy Phase, your strategic acumen is tested as you deploy influential figures across the game's map to secure military victories or establish key trade routes. The journey concludes with the Production Phase, where you reap the fruits of your labor through resource collection.

Victory in Epireia is not just about the glory of a single city but the triumph of an alliance. The first league to win the final battle will be crowned the ruler of the land, achieving a legacy of dominance that will be remembered for ages.

Epireia invites you to a world where strategy, diplomacy, and resource management intertwine, challenging you to claim your place in history as the greatest leader of ancient Greece.

—description from the publisher

description: A stripped down 'basic' form of the game, with a plethora of optional rules and ideas that can be added or ignored to customize Bin'Fa to be any sort of game you would like, all without violating the basic principles and concepts that make Bin'Fa what it is: a beautiful game of Oriental Strategy and Conquest!

This is the game where strategy, luck, and fun come together as you build your own beehive and gather honey.

In Honey Bees, it's all about scoring the most points by pollinating flowers, building your hive, and filling it with honey. It's an exciting game where every roll of the dice can bring you closer to victory!

What makes this dice game unique?

- Strategic choices: Wisely choose your action cards and dice to maximize your chances.
- Competitive and interactive: Each turn offers opportunities for all players, keeping everyone engaged.
- Beautifully designed: The game is a tribute to the wonders of the bee world.

Goal of the game: Score the most points by building your hive, pollinating flowers, and collecting honey. But be careful—the end of the game is triggered as soon as someone unlocks the second black die!

Each turn in Honey Bees you will be choosing an action card at the start of your turn. After that you roll the dice to see what actions you can take. Unlock more dice or build the beehive so it can be filled with delicious honey. Gain bonuses while building the beehive or spreading pollen. Other players get to perform actions during your turn.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Horror on the Orient Express: The Board Game – Investigators Unveiled Expansion
description:
Investigators Unveiled Expansion turns every character you meet in the base game into a playable character. Suspects from the main game can now be played as Investigators, bringing it up to a total of 10 playable Investigators for you to choose from. Moreover, it works both ways - with this expansion every Investigator can be also turned into a Suspect.  Every game can have a completely different set of possible cultists!

The Investigators Unveiled Expansion contains the following new components: 

     78 Skill tiles 
     6 Investigator portraits
     6 Investigator Profession tiles
     4 Suspect boards

Choose your team, either Dolphins or Seals, and try to make 3 in a row by bouncing the beach balls and landing them in the waves. For each 3 in a row, your team can place their flag on the scoring island. The first team to raise 3 flags on the island wins!

Furan (”Stealing”) is a two player competitive board game with two roles: Fons (“the Source”) and Furem (“the Thief”). Players play twice, once in each role, aiming to capture the most beans overall.

At the start of the game, the area belonging to Fons is filled with ten beans, and the area belonging to Furem is empty. Fons and Furem take it in turns to place and remove beans. Fons plays two beans per turn, whereas Furem can take any bean which is orthoganally adjacent to an empty space. The round ends either when the Fons area has no more than two beans in it, or when the Furem area has no fewer than two beans in it.

You have just taken over an old coffee shop. There’s plenty of supplies; enough for all the orders you'll ever need—but it’s all stuffed away in different cupboards, leaving you scrambling every time a customer comes in!

Will the shop thrive under your ownership, or crumble beneath your feet?

During this game, you’ll transform an old store into a great place to serve coffee. To win, you must upgrade all eight cupboards, while juggling day-to-day customers and paying your rent on time.

You'll use pen and paper to track your time and gold. Customer orders are dictated by rolling a d8. A deck of cards (including jokers) is split into eight decks, each representing a cupboard. Different suits are different ingredients, and the value of the card indicates how much gold you'll receive from making coffee with that card. You'll search cupboards to fill orders and make gold, eventually upgrading all your cupboards to win.

Expansion (scenario pack) for Advanced Squad Leader

The pack contains 8 brand new scenarios, using DTF special rules:

DTF-9 Mao's Iron Army - Japanese vs Chinese Communist
DTF-10 Five To One - Finnish vs Russian
DTF-11 A Greek Tragedy - Greek vs Italian
DTF-12 Not One Step Back! - Russian vs German
DTF-13 The Living Dead - Russian vs German
DTF-14 Pillbox Pains - Japanese vs American
DTF-15 Storming Lommel - British vs German
DTF-16 Panther Cull - Allied vs German

A lot of cards, half of them with pictures and the other half with english words. 
Every pair of picture and word card has exactly one match, and the players have to find the matches. Their are several game modes, for single players, cooperative or competitive play.

Educational memory game - match a picture with the english word for it.
Words from A1 basic language level English.
contains additional vocabulary list

boardgame: Ninja All Stars: Ninja Practice Demo Kit
description: The Ninja Practice Kit features a full-color poster playmat which includes a play space and fully guided demo. Nine fully-assembled Void Shrine team Ninja All-Stars models, six custom elemental dice, and a mini-token sheet allow players to see the quality components which make up the Ninja All Stars experience. Exclusive Void Shrine art cards are included.

A ROVE’s exploratory nature causes no end of trouble. These curious little bots are suckers for even the most humdrum attractions. With a near-infinite number of alien worlds to visit, every ROVE is guaranteed to get stuck in a tourist trap eventually!

This 6-card expansion for ROVE: Results-Oriented Versatile Explorer introduces new Movement cards which remove the bonus movement points for matching a specific pattern. Instead, each of these new cards require you to move a specific Module, using at least one of the card’s movement points, before using the rest for any movement you like.

A collection of expansions for ROVE: Results-Oriented Versatile Explorer:

     ROVE: Fascinating Flora
     ROVE: Reconfigured
     ROVE: Tourist Traps

Nothing can stop a ROVE from its task. Once they reach the shore, it’s time to activate their aquatic descent protocols and continue their mission!

ROVE: Dive Mode is a 1-card amphibious expansion that combines the core ROVE game with Aqua ROVE. Once you’ve completed a normal game of ROVE, place this card over your last mission to initiate Dive Mode, then continue your game using the Aqua ROVE rules and cards. If you can manage to complete 7 Missions of each, you’ve earned the title of Ultimate Amphibious Explorer!

3OFF is an n-in-a-row/tic-tac-toe based strategy game with a twist.

In 3OFF, each player must use their wits to solve a paradox involving eight tokens. Specifically, to win 3OFF, a player must remove all eight of their game tokens from a 4x4 game board. However, before doing this, tokens must be (strategically) lined up vertically, horizontally, or diagonally, in sets of 3 or 4 in a setup phase. Then starting with White(0) and alternating with Black(X), all eight tokens must then be removed, 3 at a time, before your opponent accomplishes the same. The paradox itself, involves the need to reintroduce removed tokens to the board to ultimately win. To make matters even worse (or perhaps more fun!), this paradox must be resolved in the context of what your clever opponent does.

After the setup phase, each player chooses 1 of four actions on their turn that include:

-REMOVING 3 OF YOUR OWN TOKENS (that are lined up horizontally, diagonally, or vertically)

-REMOVING 1 OF YOUR OPPONENT'S TOKENS (via a one-space, diagonal displacement of your opponent's token with your unmarked token)

-MOVING 1 OF YOUR OWN TOKENS (to any empty space lined up horizontally, diagonally, or vertically from the position of the token you wish to move; no jumping over other tokens allowed except when moving your unmarked token to an empty space)

-ADDING BACK 1 OF YOUR REMOVED TOKENS (to any empty space)

3OFF can be played as though it were a solitaire puzzle via deciding whether you are going to win with White(O) or Black(X) beforehand. To make solitaire mode interesting, to the best of your ability, play until no alternative counter moves exist for the opposing side that would stop you from winning.

Air & Armor: V Corps, Operational Armored Warfare in Europe is the second game in the Air & Armor series. Air & Armor is a grand tactical simulation of a hypothetical Soviet attack on West Germany in the mid-1980s. V Corps depicts the collision of the Soviet 28th Army and  1st Guards Tank Army with the U.S. V Corps in the Fulda Gap. Three maps cover the sector from the Inner German Border all the way to Frankfurt.

Air & Armor employs a highly interactive game system with asymmetric rules modeling the different command & control capabilities of each alliance. The game simulates all major aspects of modern warfare, including reconnaissance, combined arms operations, air, helicopter, and artillery strikes, chemical and electronic warfare, and combat engineering. Multiple levels of hidden intelligence produce a true “fog of war” providing plenty of opportunities for deception, surprise, traps, and ambushes. Play this game to experience what it would have been like to command a division in battle if the Cold War had gone hot.

Air & Armor: V Corps features twelve different scenarios, each a complete game by itself. Each scenario covers a different battlefield operation, including a meeting engagement, a delaying action, a frontal assault, an attack on a fortified line, a blocking action against a Soviet OMG, an armored cavalry covering force, two NATO counterattacks, a desperate defense of a broken line, and a surprise attack with NATO still in barracks.  Extensive Scenario, Player, and Designer Notes provide advice on play and historical detail.

Air & Armor is ideal for solitaire play.  The hidden intelligence system provides a high level of tension during solitaire play because, even though a solo player is commanding both sides, he never knows what he will hit when he conducts an operation.

Product Information:
Complexity:  High
Time Scale: 1 Turn = 2 Hours
Map Scale: 1 Mile/Hex
Unit Scale: Companies to Regiments
Players: 1-7 Players
Solitaire Suitability: High
Playing Time: 3 to 12 hours based on Scenario

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pro Wrestling Titans: The official Pro Wrestling Card Game
description:
Pro Wrestling Titans is a card game with a pro wrestling theme. It's a fast paced fighting game that uses only cards to have a full wrestling match. This game is for 1 to 4 players. The only way to settle a feud is to fight it out in the squared circle. Build your feud to a steel cage match.

Styles make fights. Each superstar builds a custom 40 card deck with a specific fighting style. Choose a Brawler, Showstopper, Powerhouse, Dirty, Technician, or Highflyer style. Superstars are also either Heel, Babyface, or Conflicted(a little bit of both).

With these custom decks, many match types are possible, 1 vs 1, 3 man matches, Battle royale, tag team, table, ladder matches, steel cage, submission, street fights, and more. Each match has a different win condition to increase replay ability. A 1 vs 1 match is won when one wrestler has reduced one of their rival's health stats to zero. The three body health parts are limbs, body, and head. Zero head health equals a knockout, zero limb health equals a submission, and zero body health equals a win by pinfall. Another example of a match is an ironman match, which is won by reducing all 3 body health stats to zero.

Achieve combos attacks by building up stamina. Your superstar has eight powerful individual cards that are only used by the superstar you choose. These cards include finishers and signature attacks, managers, special weapons, arena areas, and individual talents. Attacks are achieved by playing Stamina (the resource used in the game) to play attacks like, body slams, punches, kicks, flying attacks, and submission holds.

—description from the publisher

RIVALES is a board game about football (soccer) that uses cards and a dice, combining chance, mathematics and strategy. Two people over fourteen years of age compete, each one representing a club. Eight fixtures are simulated to define who has hegemony in four seasons, with two fixtures being held per football year. A game lasts approximately 120 minutes, depending on the skill and concentration of the participants.

Each player plays different roles: owner, sporting director, manager, footballers and referees. The footballers and the actions of the referees are cards. Footballers have different types (categories) and monetary values in relation to the goals they can score or avoid. Cards that give or take away money, cause inconveniences (sanctions, injuries) or allow the situation to be unraveled or the score changed are also influential. Monetary values are indicated in millions without specifying the currency (M¤).

—description from the designer

People love traveling. We want to meet new cultures, see famed landmarks with our own eyes and taste the world cuisine. One of the most pleasant ways to explore a new city is... just going for a walk. But how to chart a tour route that satisfies everyone? There’s an answer!

En Route is a flip-and-write game in which you create tour routes to the most interesting landmarks of the real-world cities (London, New York City, Hong Kong, Paris, Cairo, and Rio de Janeiro) and the made-up First City packed full of geek references. Every city features basic and specific mechanisms, so all of them play and feel differently.

Cards are not flipped randomly — instead, an active player plays one card face up, and two players next to them secretly choose one card each. Then, an active player selects one of the two and discards the other. After that, all players use coordinates and other information from the two cards left on the table to draw their tourists and routes.

During the game, route segments are drawn, connecting different types of tourists to various landmarks and attractions. A game of En Route lasts exactly 10 rounds. At the end of the game, each place of interest on the final route will earn players Prestige Points according to its rules. Whoever creates the most satisfying tour route, wins!

The game features constant and quite intense player interaction, and players have a fair degree of control and can strategize. It plays fast, dynamic, and makes you constantly think about what your opponents want to do (and sometimes even negotiate so that they play cards that are beneficial to you!).

En Route. Special (Kickstarter) Edition features four additional cities, new mechanisms and game modes, plus Kickstarter-exclusive cards.
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description:
Biomass is a tense, area control wargame. Become one of four asymmetric Biomorphs, each uniquely adapted to hunt and survive in this beautiful and strange alien landscape. Gather Biomass by hunting prey and investigating strange monuments, spend your resources on a wealth of rotating powers and units, and fight tooth and claw for territory and ultimately, victory!

—description from the publisher

40 Bridges is an abstract strategy game played on a grid of squares and octagons. Players try to win by either being the first one to move a piece into the opposing corner square, or by capturing all of the opponent's pieces.

A piece may move to an adjacent space of the same type it started on (square or octagon) or jump over any adjacent friendly piece. Capture occurs by surrounding an enemy piece on three separate sides.

Ever dreamt of running your own bar?

Picture this – a lively medieval tavern filled with quirky customers, fantasy brews, and unusual events.

Become a medieval tavern owner with Spells and Brews Tavern - a 2-6 player card game. Greet eccentric customers, concoct brews, and watch the profits roll in!

Your goal? Get the most valuable customers by expanding the tavern, serving brews, and navigating the world of unpredictable events; it’s an immersive experience where strategy meets fantasy.

Best served with 6°+ drinks!

HOST CUSTOMERS

The Spells and Brews Tavern boasts 96 customer cards, each featuring a funny and unique character with their own special quirks. These patrons will frequent your tavern, endowing you with special abilities, and ultimately leading you to victory.

SERVE BREWS

There are 5 different brew types in the game, each catering to specific tastes of our customers.

With each patron having their preferred brew, you must ensure to stock up on their drink of choice to accommodate them in your tavern.

COLLECT COINS

With each round, you'll amass profits from customers indulging in your tavern's offerings.
The fancier the brews and the clientele, the more coins you'll rake in.

EXPAND YOUR TAVERN

Utilizing your earnings, you can expand your tavern by purchasing more seating space to accommodate new customers. Additionally, invest in events to influence gameplay and throw a wrench into your opponent's tavern management.

WINNING THE GAME

The player who hosts the most valuable customers in their tavern emerges victorious.

—description from the publisher

Pura Vida is not only the life motto in Costa Rica, but also stands for the country's enormous biodiversity and its stated goal of living in harmony with nature and wildlife. The approximately 30 national parks, which make up around 26% of Costa Rica's area, also make an important contribution to the protection of its biodiversity. 
All kinds of landscapes can be discovered here: from dense rainforests and mangrove forests to rocky volcanoes, dry savannahs and sandy coasts. The wildlife is equally diverse: toucans, sloths, snakes, butterflies and many other species live here – not least the red-eyed tree frogs, which are so iconic for the country.
As a ranger, your task is to create a balanced animal population in the numerous nature parks. The particular challenge is to take the animals’ individual and natural movements into account. Besides taking numerous tricky decisions, you will also get to know Costa Rica's cheerful and relaxed attitude to life - Pura Vida!

Gameplay:
On your turn you must choose between these two actions:
a) Move
b) Rest 
Move: Move your ranger along the path and take the tile where you end your movement. Place the tile in your own national park, trying to align animals and/or landscapes the best way to get points.
Rest: Move your ranger to the rest area. You are not allowed to make any more moves this round.

Scoring: Every time you place a tile in your national park, you check whether you meet one of the biodiversity criteria and receive points for it:
For 3/4/5 tiles directly next to each other in a row or column with different animals and the same landscape, you get 2/2/3 points. 
For 3/4/5 tiles directly next to each other in a row or column with different landscapes and the same animals, you get 2/2/3 points. 
For 3/4/5 tiles directly next to each other in a row or column with different animals and different landscapes you get 1/1/2 points.

The game ends as soon as someone has reached at least 20 points. This person wins.

The river is rising and the only road connecting your settlement to the world is threatened. Responding to the call to action, your team is assigned a section of the riverbank and a wagonload of sandbags. You must build a levee before the river rises to flood the road!  
Flash Flood is a cooperative card game for 2 to 4 players. The participants are counting on each other, but your communication is hindered by the roaring river. Will your fellow players to repair a leak while you add an intact sandbag, or should you help with stop the leak?
The deck of cards represent the sandbags, and players will arrange these cards, turn-by-turn, to form a levee on the table. Hidden in the deck (in semi-random intervals) are cards which indicate the rising water level of the flooded river.
Working together, there's still a chance that the flood will overcome your efforts. Good luck!

—description from the publisher

In this case of Crimen en Casa, a police officer named Laura was found dead in her room of the shared apartment where she lived.

To discover what happened to her, you will have to become a true detective. Access the police database to investigate criminals, analyze fingerprints, and obtain information on vehicles and flights.

Additionally, you will need to investigate Laura's notebook among other personal objects.

Prepare to face the infamous German Luftwaffe Messerschmitt 109s and relentless German Flak as you head towards your target in B-17 Solitaire. Command an American B-17 Flying Fortress bomber during the 1942-44 bombing campaigns of World War II.

Your B-17 missions will take you to various locations in Europe during the war, from the skies of France to the heart of Germany. Your plane and crew will face bad weather, chance circumstances, Axis air defenses, and fighter planes determined to stop your bombing missions. The Axis forces are an autonomous artificial intelligence (AI) BOT player. The goal is to earn victory points from successful bombing runs and downed enemy fighters, all while keeping your crew safe.
The Axis forces have stacked the deck against you. Still, success and survival can be achieved with careful planning, good flying, and keeping the target in sight. Good luck!

To play the B-17 Solitaire Bookgame, you will need to provide a pen and at least one six sided die, we recommend two. Each set of Map and Plane pages have 3 different missions, with 30 unique missions in total. There are two chances to play each mission, in Book 1 and Book 2 which provides a total of 60 missions.

—description from the publisher

description:
A simple race game where one player takes the role of the Fox chasing the other players as the Geese.

After paying a deposit into a central pot, players spin the teetotem and move along the numbered tile course (from 1 through 80) with 

     the fox attempting to catch the geese (and gaining from the pot); and,

Some of the spaces on the course are pictured and have associated benefits/forfeits noted in the Rule book.

Scrapbots is a bot battling deck builder, drawing inspiration from card battlers (Magic, Pokemon, Yugioh) and combining it with deck building mechanisms (Star Realms, Ascension, Nightfall). Scrapbots takes place in a post-apocalyptic robot junkyard arena where you, as a Scrapjack, will compete for glory. Build up your bots, power em up with gadgets, and overwhelm your rival with malfunctions to win.

boardgame: Sonic The Hedgehog: Sonic Battle – The Search for The Chaos Emeralds

Sonic and Friends must compete to find the 7 Chaos Emeralds and harness their powers to defeat the notorious Dr. Eggman!

Play as Sonic, Amy, Tails or Knuckles and be the first to find the Chaos Emeralds.But beware.... Dr Eggman's robots are lurking behind every corner and will try to sabotage your mission! Its time to find out if you have what it takes to win this epic battle!

The aim of the game is to be the first player to reach Dr Eggman at the centre of the board with all 7 Chaos Emeralds (one of each colour) in your possession. Once there, you must face Dr Eggman in a final battle to win!

Bro! Surfing is a quick playing solo game. Go for a casual surf or compete in competitions. Link competitions and lay down a Legendary surf career.

Each run will be different from the last. You will have decisions to make along your run.

Manage your "FLOW" to prevent dings in your board. These dings will lower your overall score at the end of the run.

Easy trick system make Bro! Surfing very easy to pick up and play. Three levels of tricks that can be performed on any wave. These tricks will add to your overall score at the end of the run.

Gain sponsors by performing tricks on Sponsored waves. You sponsors will provide you with funding that can be used to purchase items that will help you throughout your career.

In ALLEGRA Grande, based on the traditional card game „Golf“, you want to keep the score in your display as low as possible.

The deck consists of cards numbered from -2 to 13. 
In each round, each player starts with 16 unknown cards facing down. The cards are placed in a grid of 4x4.
Before the game starts, each player randomly turns around two cards. 
The left player from the dealer starts the game.
In each turn, we draw 2 cards (either both from the drawing pile or both from the discard pile, or one from the discard pile and one from the drawing pile; here, the order mentioned is binding—first from the discard pile than from the drawing pile) and play them one after another. 
But if one player has just one closed card left in the display, he can just draw one card.
We want to reduce the score in the display and/or clear rows or columns.

In ALLEGRA Grande, with its 4x4 (or even 4x5 with 2 players) display, one can clear 4 (or even 5) of the same cards in a row or in a column. Here, you must be careful how to place the cards because you must immediately clear a set of three cards as soon as they are next to each other in a row or column—even if you wanted to collect four cards and clear them later. 
To clear four cards, there first must be a gap between them, for example: „3, 4, 3, 3“. 
As soon as the 4 in this row is replaced with another 3, all four threes can (must) be immediately cleared. 
With 5 cards, it could look like this: „3, 3, 4, 3, 3“. Here it's the same; as soon as the 4 in this row is replaced with another 3, the complete row can (must) be cleared.

This makes chain reactions possible (as seen in the images). It is possible to clear all 16 cards through one chain reaction, but trying it is risky.

We have 3 action cards. The cards could be used as a normal card with just their value or can be activated to use their ability. 
The activation just works when the action card is drawn from the drawing pile and is immediately discarded in the same turn to the discard pile. (no activation when drawn from the discard pile or opened in the display!).
After the action, one must open one closed card from the display; it could be any closed card.

The -2:
It's the lowest card, and it's just 4 times in the deck. The -2 allows you to look secretly at all the closed cards in one row or column. Clearly, the next player can now draw this -2 from the discard pile, and he probably will.

The 5:
It is the most common card; it is played 16 times in the deck. Here, one is allowed to look at one closed card secretly.

The 13: 
It's a little bit more special than the others. It is the highest card and 10 times in the deck. The action is to discard one open card from the display and replace it with one card from the drawing pile, and this card must stay closed. After that action, no closed card is allowed to be turned open. This is the only possible way to get one more closed card than before. For example in the end of the game when one player has just one closed card left and is just allowed to draw one card out of this reason he can with the 13 get back one more closed card and is allowed to draw 2 cards again.

The Joker:
This card simply stands for every card, but not their actions. If the Joker is next to two identical cards, he immediately mimics them, and one must clear that set. For example „6, J, 6, 1“ the Joker mimics the sixes next to him and you must clear the three cards. So be careful where to place the Joker.

The Swap: Swap 2 cards in your display: 
Instead of drawing two cards, one can also swap two cards in their own display. After that, one must open a closed card. Do it before it's too late.

The swap is also perfectly suitable to swap the Joker to the perfect place.

Sometimes one needs to do the so-called „dodging“. 
This allows you to replace a card two times at the same place on the display. This also allows you to „defuse“ the action of an action card (especially the 13) when you place the action card first in the display and then again replace the action card with the other card. That means you are allowed to indirectly discard an action card and of course it just works as long as you can draw two cards.

Contrary to the first „ALLEGRA“, we play just in our own display (no column that belongs to a neighbor, and no knocking to switch a card with a player).

These devices, once opened, create rips in the fabric of reality and connect the mortal world to the Void, birthing Nightstalker horrors into reality. boardgame: Я где-то это видел (I saw this somewhere)
description:
This is a word explanation game. In it, you will have to draw a card and explain the word or picture using words, gestures or pictures. Other players must find this item on cards in their hands.

Внутри этой игры вас ждёт шесть картин, на которых художники спрятали самые разные слова. Их вам и предстоит объяснить. Здесь нужно не просто отгадывать, а ещё искать на поле и хватать игрушку-сквиш. Чего же вы ждёте? Скорее собирайте друзей, раздавайте поля, объясняйте слова разными способами, ищите их на картинках и побеждайте!

The game that takes place at a festival. With music, bars, food trucks, fun tasks and visitors who sometimes get in the way...

With your Festival Card you plan your optimal walking route through the Festival. During the festival, you earn tokens which you place in the right place of your Festival Card.

The winner is the one who first leaves the festival grounds with a full Festival Card.

Do you win the game by outsmarting everyone, working best together and completing your assignments the fastest?

—description from the designer

Fluid Fealty is a 1-4 player trick-taking game. The king is dead—killed by his own advisors—and many are rushing to fill the void left by his passing. To take the throne, and hold onto it, you need allies—but who can you trust when everyone is out for themselves?

At the beginning of each round, players will choose a card from their hand to represent their ambition--this card will dictate both that player's bid for how many tricks they expect to win, and that player's favored suit for the round. Each round comprises 13 tricks; however, there is no single trump for a round. Instead, a played card is automatically considered a trump card if it matches that player's ambition suit--so different players have a different trump suit!

At the end of a round, the player who came closest to matching their bid adds their ambition card to their alliances. Once a player reaches a certain number of alliances, they automatically win the game; but, the more alliances you have, the more your opponents will know about your hand...

Fluid Fealty also comes with a solo mode, where you play against two automated rivals.

—description from the designer

Stick it is a family friendly, abstract, tile-placement game where players are strategically placing stickers on a water bottle and a phone.

Players earn points in several ways as they create set collections of stickers.

Stick it has 100 unique sticker illustrations that feature designs in 15 different categories. There is something for everyone in this colorful and charming game.

—description from the designer

Dwarves: Reclaim a Kingdom is a cooperative board game for 1-6 players where each player assumes the role of a Dwarven noble who leads a group of workers and warriors to reclaim their lost kingdom and defeat the enemies who seek to claim it as their own!

Throughout the game you’ll travel around your city, search ruins to uncover long lost buildings, train new and advanced workers and root out the enemy who lurks in your city!

—description from the publisher

Reservations is a trick taking game that shares DNA with traditional contract bidding games, such as Königrufen, Vira, Doppelkopf, etc. but with a "modern" feel.

In the traditional games, the scores are almost always zero sum, meaning if I am gaining points, you are losing points. In Reservations, the scoring is based on an area majority in the 4 different levels of contracts you can complete. Most of the contracts are 1 vs All (solo), however, when playing 4 players you can have 2 vs 2 (team) contracts as well. Completing a solo contract will let you place two cubes, where as a team contract lets each player place one cube. Be careful, any failed contracts will count against you at the end of the game!

Contracts also change how the card play system works. One contract might have you play Talon Exchange/Must Follow/Must Trump/Must Win while another contract will have you play Talon Reveal/Must Follow/May Trump/May Win.

There is also an advanced mode that includes a side board full of additional bonus contracts.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Paper App Dungeon: Free RPG Day 2024 Expansion
description:
We are providing 4 unique levels for our upcoming game Paper App Dungeon, along with custom P6 pencils, for players to experience some fun dungeon delving and pencil rolling on Free RPG Day 2024!

—description from the publisher

Cartographers: Map Pack Collection includes three maps featuring thematic twists and gameplay additions to expand your game of Cartographers or Cartographers Heroes. The three maps (4: Frozen Expanse, 5: Ketrha's Steppe, and 6: Hornhelm) were previously sold separately in their own packaging but are now combined into a single boxed collection.

Frozen Expanse includes 75 double-sided new map sheets featuring pre-scouted spaces and a frozen lake, two new ambush cards, and one new hero card. Players must plan carefully while adhering to new terrain placement requirements and can earn a large bonus for surrounding a lake.

Kethra's Steppe includes 75 double-sided new map sheets featuring twin mountain spires and their beacon ranges and 3 new scoring cards, specifically for use with this map pack. Players light beacons by surrounding them and score points based on a 5th type of scoring card introduced in this map pack.

Hornhelm includes 150 double-sided new map sheets featuring a market to purchase items and 6 merchant cards, a new card type specifically for use with this map pack. Players receive two sheets - one on the map side (front) and one on the market side (back) - and purchase items for ongoing effects and reputation stars at the end of the game.

Welcome to your first day of Genie school! Before you can become a certified Genie, you need to master the art of granting wishes...remember to always grant exactly 3 wishes. No more, no less!

Third Wish is a trick taking game that has players aiming to win exactly 3 tricks each round. The deck is made up of 3 asymmetrical suits and special Genie cards. It is a must follow trick taker where the highest card, regardless of suit, wins the trick and the lowest leads the next trick. After a set number of tricks (10 for 3 players and 13 for 4 players) any players that took exactly 3 tricks will gain a gold lamp. If you take more than 3 tricks, you will gain a bust cube, which can help you avoid tricks in future rounds, but will have a chance to count against you at the end of the game. After a set number of rounds (short game is 6 for 3 players and 4 for 4 players) the player with the most lamps wins!

—description from the designer

In Moratia Card Quest Game, play as adventurers going on quests to build their own legends and maybe even save the world! Gather your equipment, go on quests, and triumph over your enemies! Will you be Moratia’s next legendary hero?

At its core, Moratia Card Quest Game is an inventory management style game. To succeed you must balance your utilization and possession of three point types: Quest Points to open Quests, Power to beat Quests, and Renown to win the game. Each card you gain has various benefits or can be discarded for its Quest Point value to open new Quests. So it’s up to you to decide how to best utilize the Spoils you gain along your journey to legendary status.

—description from the designer

This is a FUN Social Strategy game mixed with Chance and highlighted with Trivia.  Designed for 2, 3, 4, or 6 players the game is played with tokens on a hexagonal shaped board.  Three dice are thrown to start a round, cards are drawn and played, and tokens are moved based upon the player's strategy.  The game takes 45 minutes to an hour for most games played primarily for strategy purposes, but when played primarily for social engagement the time passed with others normally lasts a couple of hours.  The 108 unique starting cards included in the box have eight topics (1 topic for each suite with 2 decks included) and each card has a QR code to source the cards' statements.

The goal and setup of the game are simple.  The advancement of pieces on the board is easy, and the fun new knowledge gained is wild.

Players move their tokens based upon the number of turns rolled for the round, what the bonus color cards say they can do for the round, and what level token they have to play.

—description from the designer

Villain's Veil is a thrilling card game that can be played entirely in your hands. Inspired by the popular Yu-Gi-Oh TCG, this game fulfills the dream of being able to play Yu-Gi-Oh TCG solo, without the need for a playmat.

The rules of "Villain's Veil" are simplified, ensuring a dynamic and fast-paced gameplay experience. Players take turns drawing cards from their decks, strategizing their next move, and unleashing devastating spells and abilities upon their opponents. The goal is to outwit and outmaneuver your adversary, while carefully managing your resources and anticipating their moves.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Marvel Dice Throne: Captain Marvel, Black Panther, Doctor Strange, Black Widow
description:
In Marvel Dice Throne, you become one of eight of Marvel's most famous heroes, including Black Panther, Captain Marvel, Black Widow, and Doctor Strange! Every Marvel Dice Throne hero was painstakingly designed and balanced to provide the most thematic experience possible, allowing you to truly embody your favorite heroes like no other game. Featuring all-new mechanisms and asymmetrical designs, these are our most innovative and exciting heroes yet.

Marvel Dice Throne is a heart-pumping, fast-playing game of skilled card play and dice manipulation supporting multiple modes of play, including 1v1, 2v2, 3v3, 2v2v2, or free-for-all.

Attack your opponents and activate abilities by rolling your hero's unique set of five dice. Accumulate combat points and spend them on cards that have a large range of effects, such as granting permanent hero upgrades, applying status effects, and manipulating dice directly (yours, your teammate's, or even your opponent's).

All 4 heroes are compatible with the entire Dice Throne ecosystem.
Use upgrade cards to improve your hero abilities as the game progresses.
Play action cards strategically to manipulate dice and surprise your foe.
Features a deluxe, highly functional, Battle Chest storage system.
Each hero in the Battle Chest has their own beautiful set of custom swirl dice.
—description from the publisher

Dice Throne to szybka gra karciano-kościana, która sprawi, że twoje serce zabije mocniej. Gra opiera się na mechanice bestsellerowej serii „Dice Throne” i pozwala wcielić się w bohaterów Marvela: Czarną Panterę, Kapitan Marvel, Czarną Wdowę, Doktora Strange'a. Każdy bohater ma swój zestaw kości, talię i planszę. Rzucaj kośćmi, aktywuj potężne zdolności i atakuj przeciwników. Zdobywaj punkty walki i wydawaj je na ulepszenia lub karty akcji.

Szybująca wśród gwiazd Kapitan Marvel rozbłyska na niebie niczym supernowa. Przewagę w grze zyskuje dzięki kosmicznej energii, która wykorzystuje do zadawania przeciwnikom obrażeń. Niech mają się na baczności ci, którzy wejdą jej w drogę, gdy będzie w trybie Binary!

T’Challa, jak na króla Wakandy przystało, walczy z honorem! Jego kombinezon z vibranium nie tylko pochłania obrażenia, ale przekształca je w energie. Dlatego zastanów się dwa razy, zanim podniesiesz rękę na Czarną Panterę – odda mocniej.

Mistrz Magii posługuje się zręcznie swoją księgą zaklęć, a dzięki mistycznemu przeczuciu tworzy swoją rękę kart lepiej niż inni wojownicy. Jeśli to nie pomoże, Doktor Strange zawsze może cofnąć czas, wybierając pewniejszą drogę do zwycięstwa.

Czarna Wdowa jest specjalistką od planowania tajnych operacji. Krok po kroku realizuje swój plan, stajać się silniejsza z każdym ulepszeniem. Jeśli usłyszysz tykanie bomby zegarowej… może być już za późno!

—description from the publisher (Polish)

description:
Fish Off is a 2-4 player competitive dice and tile fishing game where you catch and steal fish from players through a tug-of-war dice mechanic. Players compete to catch and steal point value fish to earn the highest score over 3 matches. Most of the game is played synchronously, meaning that players all take their turns at the same time allowing the game to be played at a good steady pace with the occasional dice war needed to settle the inevitable tug-of-wars over high value fish.

The game board displays a 5 by 5 grid of water with hook icons arrayed along each row and column of the grid. Players sit along each edge of the board and are given Hook and Seagull tokens of the same colour, matching their edge of the board. Fish tiles are randomly shuffled and dealt face down on the grid, discarding all leftover tiles. Players are now ready to start their first round of game.

One Round of play is broken into 3 steps: Positioning, Casting and Catching. Players select a Position along their edge of the game board by placing their Hook token on a hook icon which will be beneath a column of tiles. Players then roll their dice to Cast their hook and resolve where their hook landed. Players then reveal their hooked tile and decide whether they want to place a Seagull token on an opponent's tile to try to steal it off them. Uncontested tiles are collected first, then any remaining tiles with multiple players contesting them must enter a dice base tug-of-war to see who keeps them. Once this has all been resolved, one round of play has concluded, and a new round can start. Players spend multiple rounds catching and stealing fish until all players have collected 5 fish, ending the Match. Players tally the face value of their fish and can now setup the board for a new match. The player with the most points collected over the 3 matches is the overall winner of Fish Off.

The game board is double-sided with Calm Waters on one side and Strong Currents on the reverse side. The Calm Waters side is suitable for first time players and younger kids who want to play a simple fun game. The Strong Currents side is suitable for more experienced players and adults who want to add another level of complexity and strategy to their turns. The currents help direct a players hook through the waters granting them a greater chance to hook a better fish tile. Players can also choose to add Fish Set Bonuses to either side of the board which act like poker rules. For example: Players are rewarded bonus points for catching unique sets of fish at the end of a match i.e. A Full House, Four of a Kind and a 5 Fish Straight to name a few.

—description from the publisher

If you love playing trivia games anytime, anywhere, then the Trivial Pursuit Mini Pack 1990s game is for you! This quick-playing game features trivia questions from the years 1990 to 2000. Since it includes cards only, you can take it to parties, get-togethers, on vacation, and more. On your turn, another player will ask you a question; get it right to keep the card. The first person to earn 5 cards wins! Answer fun trivia questions like “Which U.S. president, elected to two terms in the ’90s, was born in Hope, Arkansas?” and “What computer bug did companies around the world fear would cause mass chaos when the digits flipped to the year 2000 on January 1st?” Here's your chance to prove your prowess of 1990s pop culture!

—description from the publisher

1889 Berlin, an 18xx game in the series originated by Francis Tresham's 1829, is set in Berlin Germany.

In 1889 Berlin, 2-5 players will use money to purchase shares in railroad companies during Share Dealing Rounds. Between subsequent Share Dealing Rounds, railroad corporations then conduct operations on the game board, with the decisions being made by the player who is the major share holder. The game simulates the progression of railroad development during an historic period in Berlin (1889 - 1989). Old trains become obsolete while the revenue earnings from cities grows over time. Players will try to purchase the most profitable shares at the right times of the game to maximize earnings in dividends and stock value gains. In the end, the player with the highest value of personal cash and held stocks is the winner.

Unique mechanisms in 1889 Berlin:

     At the beginning of phase 1945 (end of WWII) players remove some brown tiles - simulating bombardment of the city
     At the beginning of phase 1961 (building the wall) the map is split by wall tiles (functioning as "edge of the map")
     Companies in West-Berlin can't operate in East-Berlin (phases 1961 - 1989)
     Companies in East-Berlin can operate in West-Berlin (phases 1961 * 1989) but had to end in their station token or loop back to East-Berlin
     Companies in East-Berlin use a common pool of trains an had to reach a given target value ("Planwirtschaft") to get a new train or loose a train
     Phases: yellow (1889), green (1900), brown (1933), green (1945), brown (West-Berlin 1961), green (East-Berlin 1961), gray (1989)


—description from the designer

An additional airport for Sky Team

Contain one postcard with two additional missions (Red and Green) .

Note: Available as a Print and Play

Kids were walking along the river and stumbled upon a sleeping dragon. A rough river separates them from home but the power of friendship, quick wits and a little magic will help them overcome all obstacles!

In this cooperative game you will roll dice and decide the best way to use them together. The Bridge dice will tell you how the kids can move forward and the Spell die will help them go faster and lull the dragon to sleep. To win, lead the kids across the river back to their village before you run out of Dream tokens and the dragon wakes up.

Every turn you roll 3 dice: 2 of them indicate what kinds of bridges the kids can walk on, the 3rd one allows you to cast a spell. Spells help you reroll the dice, choose bridges and walk silently without disturbing the dragon.

If at the end of your turn at least one kid has reached the village, the dragon keeps seeing the same dream as before. If no one has reached the village during your turn you have to discard a dream token. When you run out of dream tokens, the dragon wakes up. Get all the kids back home before that to win!

—description from the publisher

Animals have fled and hidden in the bushes, try to find them! In this game you will be training your memory – you are going to look for a pair of identical animals or a group of different animals. The player who gets most victory points for completed tasks (both simple and difficult) becomes the winner.

There are two types of tasks:

     simple (1 point), that require to find a pair of identical cards;
     difficult (2 points), that require to find a specific group of 4 cards.


On your turn, you open cards one by one. If the first two cards you've opened match each other and a simple task, you can complete this task by taking the Task card.
However, if you decided to continue opening the cards, it means that you are aiming at a difficult task now and cannot gain a simple Task card even if the cards will be matching.

After finishing your turn, close all the cards without changing their position.

—description from the publisher

Objective: collect as many bonus points as possible, but don't miss to be back home on time
- tactical (geographical) quiz
3 phases - moving by turn - each player starts on his marked/coloured area

     move around collecting cards/images
     answer questions regarding images/cards you hold - if you fail discard card

Forty Nine Football was originally designed to play your favorite team through the current season. This quickly progressed into a game that makes replaying any season a lot of fun due to its statistical accuracy, and each game playing like a real football game with many of the coaching decisions needed to be successful. I have played through multiple seasons playing my favorite teams in the CFL and NFL and I'm very happy with the results.

One thing you will find is all of the player and team ratings come from statistics, opposed to subjective ratings. This does make it a challenge with older seasons where statistical data is not as available.

If you own Forty Nine Football, all of the new seasons will still work perfectly with your original game. But, it is being rebranded into two games, Quick Decks Football, and On The Turf Football. Quick Decks is a perfect standalone quick play game where you play through all the plays in a game with realistic stats. Plot:
Welcome, Detective, to Rain Mané, a small town on the outskirts of France about 200 km from Paris. Rain Mané is celebrated for its rich artistic heritage and renowned art community.

In this seemingly idyllic place, the truth needs uncovering. Tonight, you find yourself in the middle of a story where a crime has been committed. The year is 1926. Eleanor Leroy, a prominent figure in the art community, has been found dead. The police are overwhelmed by the motives surrounding the death of Eleanor.

Considering your impressive past as a detective, you are appointed the role of lead investigator on this case. The police need your help figuring out who among the characters of Rain Mané is responsible for this particular crime. You need to trace the web of relationships, uncover motives, and get to know the suspicious characters who inhabit this town. The storm is brewing, Detective. And the truth awaits your intuition.

Every time a demigod enters The Pools when being moved out of the Abyss, the player moving them activates the current modifier on the Renewal Die. That player then re-rolls the renewal die, resulting in a new modifier for the next player to move a demigod from the Abyss to the Pools.

The Renewal Die replaces the permanent 'refresh card' effect that normally activates in the Pools, creating more dynamic gameplay and ever-shifting strategies surrounding how players use the Abyss.

The SYW Battle Series (SYWBS) games are playable grand-tactical portrayals of battles of the Seven Years War. The series’ intent is to show how command operates on the battlefields, using the weapons and tactics of the period. The system emphasizes command & control and playability.

     Scale = 200-220 m/hex (219-240 yrd/hex)
     Time = 30 min/Turn
     Counter = 1 Brigade, 1 Battalion, 1 to 3 Squadrons, 1 Battery, 1 Leader


Each unit's historical flag and uniform are displayed on Counters, Orders/organizational Displays, and Orders of battle (in Playbooks).

Torgau 1760 simulates the battle fought on the 3rd of November 1760 in northwestern Saxony during the Seven Years War. King Frederick II the Great commanded the Prussian Army: 48,500 men (62 bns, 102 sqns) and 246 guns in total. F.M. Leopold Daun commanded the Austrian Army: 55,500 men (67 bns, 116 sqns) and 275 guns in total.

description:
For its 20th anniversary, Dungeon Twister is back with a whole range of improvements.

In Dungeon Twister 20th Anniversary Edition, players take on the role of a team of heroes imprisoned by the Mad Archmage in an ever-changing dungeon riddled with deadly traps. Will you be up to the challenge of surviving and entertaining your jailer?  Players compete by strategically exploiting their adventurers' skills and the opportunities created by the maze's ever-changing shape. To win the game, eliminate your opponents by magic, force or cunning, and activate the mechanisms that will make them fall into a trap. Bring back precious treasures or escape alive to earn victory points.

With Dungeon Twister, strategy is at the heart of each and every one of your actions. The game features :


     A tactical action system: plan your turn and choose the best action card to optimize your moves. Keep track of the cards played by your opponents to anticipate their next actions.
     Multiple ways to win: take advantage of the enemy heroes' weaknesses and defeat them, control strategic points or dodge the battle and escape the dungeon to earn victory points.
     An evolutive and interactive board: adapt your tactics with each tile revealed. Activate mechanisms to rotate them to your advantage, open up new opportunities or block other players' strategies. Strengthen your heroes with magic items scattered throughout the labyrinth.

description:
Two field camps face each other in trenches. There are obstacles between these entrenchments, so that the advance can only take place in zigzags.
Each field camp has the same number of divisions and regiments, represented by large and small colored pyramids that tilt as you move. In doing so, they push enemy companies (red dots on the game board), which are captured by covering up. The goal is to take as many prisoners as possible and conquer the enemy entrenchment.
The starting position is indicated by the red border line, where the two large pyramids are first placed on the triangles that are outlined with a white double line. A small pyramid is placed to the right and left of each large pyramid, also on the triangles that are bordered with a white double line.
Germany moves first by tilting one of its pyramids and placing it on an adjacent field. The opponent does the same, so the game is played one move at a time. The pyramids can only be tilted by folding over one of the three sides in such a way that the rotating edge remains on the original line, so it must not be tilted over the top.
Whoever has occupied all 8 bright squares marked with points in the opponent's fortifications with his or her own or someone else's pieces can call “Stop”; the game is then over.
The party that has taken the most prisoners, i.e. can add up the most points under their pyramids, wins.

The game was published during WWI.

—user summary

The game is played alternately by pushing all cruisers and pushing an ironclad. The cruisers are all set in motion with each turn; They may advance up to three spaces at will, but only in a straight line. Every cruiser must be moved as long as it is not on one of the small crosses within the large red crosses. In this case, it can be set in motion again from there at the player's discretion.
The ironclads are only set in motion one at a time and can travel as far as they want in one direction, i.e. unless it encounters another ship and is forced to stop.
Ironclads or cruisers can drive over the minefields, but are lost as soon as they stop or have to stop on them.
If a cruiser sinks a mine ring when leaving one of the harbor bases, which can only happen if the chimney was vertically on the base during the move, the 9 spaces that the mine ring touches may no longer be used by both parties.
No ship is allowed to jump over another one of your own or enemy fleet. This only happens when an ironclad is drilling a cruiser into the ground; it must do so as soon as the cruiser is within one of the ironclad's four directions of travel.
A ship that is wedged in such a way that it can no longer move will be taken away by the opponent on the next move. But as long as one of the ironclads can still move, the other ironclads that may be wedged in cannot be taken away.

Germany has won

     if it has occupied the three crosses within the large red crosses with three cruisers and has also occupied the base closest to the coast with a cruiser or occupied it with a mine ring;
     when the three ironclads have been put out of action.


England has won

     if two of its ironclads come to rest in such a way that they completely or partially cover at least four of the former cruiser locations;
     if at least 5 cruisers are out of action and one ironclad is still maneuverable.

description: Choose one of your favorite characters from the popular Pin and Pon toy series and run around the farm running away from the wolf.

Kill Switch is an abstract boardgame for two players. What makes Kill Switch unique is that the players decide how their pieces move and how the board looks like. As a matter of fact, setting up the board and pieces – and changing them continuously during the game - plays a key role in the strategy. Chance and luck do not play any role in Kill Switch! No dice or chance cards: it’s entirely up to you to win the game.
Contrary to chess and - as a matter of fact - most games, Kill Switch will start differently every time. There are over 1028 possible positions to create in Phase 1 and hundreds of actions to choose from each turn in the Combat Phase! 
Goal of the game is to disable the opponent. That is no surprise, is it? The way to do that is by hitting the opponent’s Kill Switch. If you succeed in that, all enemy units are turned off immediately and you win. The challenge is to program your Bots in such a way that enables them to break through enemy lines. But you also have to keep up your own defenses against your opponent who is after your Kill Switch!

—description from the designer

RAF Bomber Command: Lancaster (Expansion 4) is a tactical-level, narrative-style solitaire wargame expansion using a tables-based game engine simulating the British RAF Bomber Command nighttime bombing campaign against Germany during 1943-1945.

As the pilot of an RAF Avro Lancaster heavy bomber, your ultimate goal is to survive a 30-mission tour of duty. You and your crew will be assigned with taking part in nightly area bombing raids against various German cities from 1943 to 1945 in an attempt to break the will of the German people. But it won’t certainly be a cake walk, as Luftwaffe night fighters will be hunting your bomber streams, not to mention navigation challenges over open water and deadly FLAK you will encounter over the target cities.


     One D6, one D12, and two D10 dice are required to play.
     5 minute game setup and breakdown times.
     A medium size table is recommended for game setup.
     Total play time to complete a full 30-mission tour of duty takes approximately 10-15 hours, with a typical mission averaging 20 minutes.
     Adjustable starting difficulty based on player's skill level.


With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. And with detailed damage modeling, fuel management, numerous crewmen skills, mission weather effects, random events, and a multitude of table modifiers, RAF Bomber Command: Lancaster (Expansion 4) offers memorable experiences and high replay value. Are you up to the challenge of surviving a 30-mission tour of duty?

Underbranch is an abstract push-your-luck set building game of 64 multi-use cards.

The cards have two halves: a number half (numbers 1 to 10 in six suits) and an objective half (Flush, Straight, Even numbers, etc). The numbers are played on top of matching objectives, and the sets form columns where subsequent sets must fulfill the combined requirements of all earlier objectives in the same column.

On a turn, you have four action options:

     Play a number card from a queue
     Play a number card from the deck
     Play an objective card from the deck
     Draw 3 objective cards: play one, discard one, return one


The game ends when you cannot do any action, or do not want to do any action, or if the discard pile has 11 cards or more.

At game end, the sets that have been built between objectives are scored. You gain victory points based on the number of cards in a set and the set's level in the column.

Although mainly a solo game, the rules include competitive and cooperative variants for two players.

—description from the designer

The most awaited night has arrived, and you know it!
The grand royal banquet of the year is being celebrated, and all the leftovers, which won't be few, will be yours.
Who will get the best morsel in their mouth?
Pigs, to the royal sty!
Devour the finest delicacies of the island!

At the beginning of the game, all players will be dealt 3 feast cards, once looked at (privately), they will be placed face down in their play area.

After that, players will take turns clock-wise, playing cards in order to discover what feast other players have, and swapping them in order to be able to - when an event instructs the player to do so - eat whatever they have collected, scoring the corresponding points.

Once three scoring events have been resolved, the player(s) with the highest score are eliminated from the game, and from the remaining ones, the player(s) with the highest score are proclaimed the King of Pigs, winning the game.

Corner a stock by gaining possession of a preference share and four ordinary shares. The winner takes all of the counters in the pool.

The game is played with up to six sets of shares to match the number of players. Each player is randomly dealt five shares and pays 20 counters into the pool. A separate deck of exchange cards is used to buy and sell shares. Players can only hold up to 5 shares in their hand.

The player to the left of the dealer (or previous player) names a share to deal in. An exchange card is then turned over. The exchange cards give three outcomes - quoted, call, or dividend.

With “quoted”, that player may sell any of the named shares to the pool at the quoted price. 
With “call”, every player must put a specified number of counters into the pool for each share of the named share. 
With “dividend”, every player takes a certain number of counters out per share held and preference shares take double that amount.

—user summary

The royal court is full of danger and deception. Will you unite the warring factions, or bring the court to its knees with trickery and might? Combine poker hands with powerful skill cards to exert your will and rise to the top. But beware the tyrannical red queen and the mysterious black knave…

Duel of Whispers and Knives is a fast-paced duelling card game for two players, with games typically taking 10–15 minutes.

There are two ways to win: either reduce your opponent to zero life by playing stronger poker hands, or acquire three out of four faction leader cards by playing four cards of the same suit. You can also use thematic skill cards, burning your playing cards as fuel, to trigger effects like stealing your opponent's cards or doubling your damage output.

description:
It seems the Quar have always been at war. When the first wandering clans of insect gatherers built their low, mud-walled buildings, it was as much to keep out other Quar as the wild animals. As they first learned to make tools to get food, they quickly learned to make tools to defend themselves. The mud walls quickly became fortified citadels, and then city-states. In five thousand years of civilization, the only constants of their culture have been the changing of the seasons and the threat of war.

Quar are just like you and me - perfectly normal creatures, bereft of magic or supernatural powers or other-worldly technologies. They do eat bugs, though, and lots of them. Most quar, when they’re not fighting each other over slights real or imagined, are farmers, or traders, or merchants. Mostly they live simple, happy lives full of hard work, ale and the occasional overeating of mothcakes.

But there are other quar - ambitious, striving quar - for which a mere warm bed and full belly are not enough. These are the ones that scheme, that connive, and their machinations bring whole nations and kingdoms into conflict. Constant conflict, brutal conflict, for the history of Alwyd is written in blood.

The Long War is entering a new phase. Gather your weapons, choose your side, and prepare to begin your chapter in the history of this grand conflict.

This is a two-player game that features 24 hard plastic Quar (12 Crusaders and 12 Coftyrans), 24 hard plastic dual-use bases, 10 activation cards, 6 D6 dice, and a 48 page rulebook. There is also a selection of cut-out terrain on the box sides to help bring your battlefield to life!

description:
To win SlideAscope, you need to collect the most valuable tiles from the 'scope, with each tile having a value from 1-4 in its center and four (of six) half symbols on its four edges.

To set up the game, draw forty tiles from the bag and place them in a 5x8 grid inside the included frame, with the one open edge on the short side of the grid. Each player gets a tile at random, with the remaining tiles being placed in face-down stacks.

On a turn, place your tile in an empty space at the top of a column. If the symbol on the left or right edge of your placed tile matches the symbol on the adjacent tile, remove the matched tile — not the one you placed — then slide down any tiles above the one just removed. If the symbols match again, remove that tile as well! Next, check whether the symbol on the bottom edge of your placed tile matches the symbol on the tile below it; if so, remove that matched tile, then slide down the tile you placed, which will possible create more matches. Place all of these removed tiles in front of you.

Once on your turn, you can discard a tile in front of you to rotate the tile you placed that turn. If you make more matches, remove the matched tiles and slide columns as necessary until your placed tile has no more matches. (If two half symbols make a completed symbol elsewhere in the grid, leave them in place. Only matches with the tile you placed this turn matter.)

When at least one column is empty, continue taking turns until everyone has had the same number of turns, then sum the values of the tiles you've collected. Whoever has the highest sum wins.

Tentenruten is a set collection game using cards with different numbers on the top and bottom. On your turn, you can choose to score a set, keeping one to show how many points you've earned, and passing the others, upside down, to your neighbor; or flip your hand, passing any one card to your neighbor, and they may choose the orientation.

The game end is triggered when either one player has played all of their cards or scored a certain number of points. The player who played all of their cards or scored the most points wins.

boardgame: つめつめモンスターハウス (Tsume Tsume Monster House)
description:
Tsume Tsume Monster House is a game in which the players will cut out monsters from a piece of paper in their player color, aiming to successfully fit them on a central board.

Each player is given a colored piece of paper, that shows the values of 9 monsters on the back. They draw the shapes of their monsters on the back and then cut them out.

Then, players take turns placing their monsters on a central board according to the placement rules, possibly having to remove monsters already present.

The game ends after all players have placed their monsters and the winner is generally the player who had to remove the fewest monsters.

Songoku is a card game heavily steeped in the story of the Monkey King and buddhism.

In the game, players reveal virtue cards, compete for sutra cards, and trade various goods.

The player with the most points wins the reward card stack is exhausted wins.

Contents:
Contains 52 new cards for Millennium Blades.  Featuring a new character and sets from newer Level 99 games.


     L'oree, Harbinger of Artabayaxnias Extra Character
     Bullet ♥ Bronze Promo Set
     Cool Animal Facts Bronze Promo Set
     Bullet ★ Silver Promo Set
     Space Chase Gold Promo Set
     Adventures At Argent Gold Promo Set
     Extra Core Set Accessories

boardgame: The Ghost of Crimson Sky Ranch
description:
Welcome to Crimson Sky Ranch, nestled on the outskirts of dusty Texas. The ranch, once known as a quiet and uneventful place, now holds an air of mystery and unease due to a recent incident. The young owner of Crimson Sky Ranch, Samuel Blackwood, was recently found lifeless in his residence with no visible wounds, and the scene was devoid of any conventional clues. With Samuel having no reason to die this way due to his young age and good health, the officers at the local police department are left scratching their heads. With no evidence linking anyone to a committed crime, they’ve had no choice but to write the case off as an unfortunate event of natural cause, thereby closing the investigation.

The townsfolk however, having a history of telling myths and legends around the ranch, believe otherwise. They whisper of something supernatural among them. The people were quick to dub the potential assailant as “The Ghost of Crimson Sky Ranch" - a phantom that struck without leaving a trace.

Upon learning about this case, you feel a sense of unease and a suspicion that there's more to the story waiting to be uncovered. Being the detective you are, with a strong determination to solve difficult cases, you decide to reopen the case to pursue the truth. As the ghostly tales echo through the town, you arrive at Crimson Sky Ranch to figure out what truly happened.

—description from the publisher

Agents of M.A.R.S.: Objective Initiative is a small optional expansion for the core game, which adds a series of objective cards that can be drawn at the start of the game to guarantee that both players don’t have the same setup.

The expansion includes two 24 card decks.
The only difference between these two decks is the colour scheme used for the cards.

Solo rules are available on the website.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Horror on the Orient Express: The Board Game – Consumed by the Void Expansion
description:
Consumed by the Void Campaign Expansion invites you on a scenario-based campaign journey through a real nightmare. Face new, powerful enemies, work hand in hand with a mysterious detective, and see for yourself what awaits you in the grand finale!

Also contains (non-exhaustive list):

     1 new detective mini
     3 new starting landscape tiles
     some new monster minis

The issue is similar to Le Franc Tireur, #12 and is focused on the Pacific Theater. 
14 of the 15 scenarios feature the Japanese vs various opponents, the 15th is French vs Chinese. The period covered is 1941-1946.
