A fun, fast paced game of Trick-Taking and saving all humanity from cosmic horrors!

Based on the familiar mechanism of trick taking, Dark Trickery turns the genre in strange, cyclopian angles by making the game play cooperative.

Players work toward a common goal of closing the 5 gates through which the Great Old Ones seek to enter our reality to devour our world and all mankind.

—description from the publisher

The modern high-tech world is full of digital perils. The internet age has brought a cornucopia of knowledge and technological progress. However, we are prey to digital predators. Cyber Defense thematically explores concepts in cyber security. Modern computer systems are vulnerable to cyber attacks, and there is a constant vigil to keep computer systems safe against cyber attacks through cyber security measures. Common types of cyber attacks include Phishing, Man-in-the-middle and Distributed Denial of Service (DDOS) attacks. In Cyber Defense, one player plays as the Hacker who is trying to crack a password. The other player plays as the Defender trying to keep their password safe.

OBJECTIVE – The objective for the Hacker is to try to deduce the Defender’s password. The objective of the Defender is to try to protect his password.
GAME PLAY – Each turn, players develop battlegrounds, play card effects, and resolve battlegrounds. Winning battlegrounds allows the Hacker to try to deduce the Defender’s password. When the Defender wins battlegrounds, they can strengthen their password and reduce the effectiveness of Hacker attacks.
GAME END AND WINNING –  The game ends after three rounds or if the Hacker has cracked the Defender’s password.
THEME – Cyber Security

—description from the designer

boardgame: Road From Disunion: The American Civil War
description:
Die Rolling game of conquest simulating the American Civil War in a casual yet historically based manner. In this game, two players take the sides of the Union and the Rebels, vying for control over the Thirteen Colonies. The map contains 42 locations some of which count toward victory conditions. Dice are rolled to conquer each location and its defenses. Event cards vary play. This is a follow up game to Road to Independence and uses similar game mechanics.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Searching for Legends is a competitive cryptid-centric board game where 2 – 6 players collect supplies, travel the world, and hunt local and legendary creatures!

The number of creatures catalogued in our game allows for near-limitless replayability, and features many obscure and lesser-known cryptids; sure to educate and entertain!

Gather supplies and weapons, thwart your friends with Plot Twists, and complete your Legendary Assignments in our easy-to-learn, one-of-a-kind game! Compete with your friends to become a Legend! Happy Hunting!

—description from the designer

Tramways Engineer's Workbook volume 2 is the second game-book that uses the Tramways Engineer's Workbook concept: A solo-duo game with erasable pens. Draw lines, connect buildings, move passengers, meet the goals or beat your opponents.

The volume 2 is fully independent from the volume 1: It uses all the rules, which are fully explained in the rules book, but with a twist because you are traveling through 24 different cities.

One city = one new twist!

—description from the designer

This is the first stupid Clinic Deluxe Expansion with adult content. It must be taken with a big dose of humour. This expansion requires the base game and for some of the new modules, Clinic Deluxe: The Extension, as well

It contains 6 new big modules, 98 new tiles, and  56 new Wooden Meeples! Visit the Sperm Bank, use the Condom Vending Machine, treat your sexual Addiction, smoke Therapeutic or not Cannabis, make some Genetic Research to solve the Zombies case, or use your new risky Cryogenic tanks and treat the patients at the end of the game!

—description from the publisher

This is the second stupid and bad taste expansion for Clinic. Released with the 69th Blue Expansion, it must be taken with humour.
It contains adult themes like Cosmetic Surgeries with your new Pop star meeples, Gynecologists and contraceptive pills, the new Obesity Institute. Discover the secret of Inside the Clinic with lustful doctors, lubricious nurses, and seductive secretaries.

The 69th Pink Extension contains 5 new big modules and an additional deck of randomizer cards. We also added Blue Pills and Pink Pills tokens for the fans who don’t like playing with the real pills released in the Clinic 6th Extension. 17 New Solitaire Goals end this new set of rules. Forget everything you’ve learned about Clinic through all the expansions. With this expansion, your goal is not to treat patients, nor even sustain them; rather, you strive merely to satisfy your most primal instincts.

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Fearless and Inventive Squad Pack
description:
Brave heroes set out to rescue one of their comrades! Once a simple farmer on Tatooine, Luke Skywalker is now a Jedi knight ready to fight the Empire and free his friend Han Solo from the clutches of Jabba the Hutt.
Luke is assisted in his efforts by Leia Organa and Lando Calrissian, who have daringly infiltrated Jabba's palace under false identities before Luke's arrival. With the help of R2-D2, Luke's astromech field droid, this intrepid group sets out to put an end to the evils of some of the most evil ruffians and channels in the galaxy...

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Fear and Dead Men Squad Pack
description:
The iconic Darth Vader helps usher in a whole new era in Star Wars™: Shatterpoint! Wielding the full power of the dark side of the Force, Vader enters the game as one of the first Primary Units from the Galactic Civil War era, opening even more squad-building possibilities. He is flanked by a trio of fearsome Imperial Stormtroopers, including Lt. TK-289 as a Secondary Unit and two standard Stormtroopers as a Supporting Unit ready to bring the weight of the Empire to bear against their opponents. Rounding out this pack are all the Stat, Order, and Stance cards players need to add these characters to their squads.

With the full power of the dark side of the Force, Vader is one of the first Primary Units of the Galactic Civil War era.

One of the Galactic Empire's most terrifying commanders, Darth Vader destroys any enemy at the behest of his dark master, the Emperor. Known by many nicknames, Lord Vader's authority is almost absolute among the forces of the Galactic Empire. Using the full power of the Dark Side of the Force, Vader is able to quickly and efficiently eliminate even the most skilled of adversaries.

When "Darth Vader, the Emperor's servant" takes to the battlefield, he does so commanding the most experienced and competent stormtroopers in the ranks of the Imperial army. The mere sight of Darth Vader accompanied by his stormtroopers strikes fear into those who dare oppose the power of the Empire...

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Clone Force 99 Squad Pack
description:
In a force of soldiers cloned to be identical, a few stand out as unique!
Once known as Clone Force 99, this experimental unit of clone troopers defies all expectations due to the extraordinary capabilities granted by their genetic mutations. Adopting names that describe their role in the unit, the Remittance is led by Hunter, whose heightened senses make him an unparalleled tracker. Tech is the technology expert, using his superior intellect to hack computers and bypass security systems. The group is completed by Crosshair, the sniper, Wrecker, an unstoppable force in close-range combat, and Echo, a former CAR soldier transformed into a cyborg.

As the Republic transforms into the Galactic Empire, the Remittance is forced to improvise to survive, becoming fugitives. A young medical assistant named Omega joins their ranks, who is also the result of a cloning experiment. Protecting it gives the Bad Batch another reason to find its own way in a hostile galaxy.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – You Have Something I Want Squad Pack
description:
Tremble before the authority and ruthless determination of one of the most powerful officers and politicians in the Imperial Remnant! It is not wise to underestimate anyone who holds the title of moff, as they are granted considerable authority and dominance over Imperial forces. Moff Gideon would end up proving to be an especially tenacious adversary for the Mandalorian, as he spared no resources in his search for the boy named Grogu, whom he referred to as "the asset."

In pursuit of this goal, Moff Gideon has activated numerous dark troopers, powerful and sophisticated battle droids that are nearly invulnerable to conventional weapons. With the venerated Dark Sword in his possession, Moff Gideon will not give up his efforts until he has achieved what he wants...

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Certified Guild Squad Pack
description:
The members of the Bounty Hunters Guild are very diverse, and each of them has their own concept of honor. Born Din Djarin and raised by Mandalorian warriors, he makes a living in the galaxy as a bounty hunter after the fall of the Empire. Hired by a suspicious individual to recover a supposedly vulnerable asset and deliver it to him, he decided instead to risk his life to protect what turned out to be an orphan.

Among those affected by this decision were Greef Karga, the Guild representative who had contacted the Mandalorian, and the skilled assassin droid turned hired gun called IG-11. The careers of both would be disrupted by the Mandalorian's actions and the subsequent consequences of his confrontation with the Guild...

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Ee Chee Wa Maa! Squad Pack
description:
The smallest rebels achieve victory against all odds! During the decisive Battle of Endor, the power of the Empire was tested by the Ewoks of the Bright Tree Village and their rebel allies.

Accepting into his tribe the rebel assault team sent to destroy the Imperial shield generator, Ewok chief Chirpa organized bands of warriors to combat the stormtroopers. In these bands fought the skilled explorer Paploo and numerous Ewok warriors armed with simple bows and spears, who demonstrated that there is no small enemy...

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Yub Nub Squad Pack
description:
Unexpected heroes appear on the eve of the Battle of Endor! The Ewoks of Shining Tree Village played a vital role in that battle thanks to C-3PO, whose tales of the Rebellion encouraged the brave Ewoks to stand up to the Imperial forces on the forest moon. Convinced that these stories came from the mouth of a god come to guide them, warriors such as the shaman Logray, the explorer Wicket and many cunning Ewok hunters joined C-3PO and his comrade R2-D2 to become small but brave heroes of the Rebellion...

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Lead by Example Squad Pack
description:
A wizened Jedi Master and his famed squad of clone troops joins the battles of Star Wars™: Shatterpoint in this new Squad Pack! Plo Koon is respected throughout the Jedi Order for his level-headed demeanor and calm approach to battle. As the Jedi General of the famed “Wolfpack,” he enters Star Wars: Shatterpoint as a Primary Unit alongside his trusted lieutenant Clone Commander Wolffe as a Secondary Unit. They’re joined by two 104th Battalion Clone Troopers from the famed “Wolfpack” as a Supporting Unit to form a complete squad that can be fielded on its own, or players can choose to add these characters to their own custom squads. Rounding out this pack is the Padawan Ahsoka Tano originally released as a Hobby Next promo who represents an alternate Secondary Unit option.

Kel Dor Jedi Master Plo Koon has earned a reputation for undertaking dangerous missions in the service of the Galactic Republic, fighting alongside legends like Mace Windu. Among his many accomplishments is discovering Ahsoka Tano when she was still a child and bringing her to the Jedi temple to begin her training.

His leadership skills earned him promotion to general and placed him in command of Battalion 104, nicknamed the "Manada Battalion." With the help of clone commander CC-3636, better known as Commander Wolffe, this famous battalion participated in many important engagements, including the search and destruction of the Separatist warship Malevolence. Not even the loss of his right eye at the hands of a Sith apprentice stopped Commander Wolffe from continuing to help Plo Koon in carrying out his vital missions for the survival of the Republic...

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Sabotage Showdown Mission Pack
description:
Players can embark on a new Star Wars™: Shatterpoint mission with this pack! Featuring the first new set of Mission cards outside of the Core Set, Sabotage Showdown adds more variety to games of Shatterpoint with new objective layouts and new mission special rules that pose new tactical challenges for strike teams to overcome.

What’s included
• 10 Mission Cards
• 1 Punch Sheet

Star Wars: Shatterpoint - Sabotage Showdown Mission Pack adds a new set of missions to your games of Star Wars: Shatterpoint, offering you new objectives that pose unique tactical challenges.

—description from the publisher

A small-scale abstract strategy game inspired by Mancala. Rondol provides simple concept with surprising depth. Play is simple: pick up stacks of chips that you control and play them out either clockwise or counter-clockwise, placing each chip on a space. You can only pick up stacks that have your color on top. The goal? Control 4 stacks of chips in a row, or block out your opponent by controlling all available stacks of chips. Outwit your opponent to seize victory! Then play again.

Taking up a small amount of table space and easily carried, Rondol is great to take along and play most anywhere.

Create 6 stacks of alternating colored chips. 
Place them in a circle.
On your turn, select 1 stack that has your color chip on top. 
Drop all chips, 1 at a time in each space, going around until all chips have been dropped. 
Objective: control 4 stacks in a row OR control all stacks on the table.

Use alternate rules and setups to increase depth of strategy.

—description from the publisher

A team-based expansion for Magic Trick. The gameplay is the same as the base game, but now players are on two teams of two players each, trying to collectively take tricks equal to their mid-hand bids.

There is also a variant to score extra points for deducing some cards out of the game.

Copies were handed out at The Gathering of Friends 2024.

Do you like shiny things and puzzles? Gemblems is a solo puzzle game that fits in the palm of your hand! Draw a few cards, then twist, flip, and move the cards to line up all of the Gemblems, BUT, be sure to follow the rules!

Complete a set of cards by following every rule listed on each card drawn. Fan the cards the other way or even rotate them to obtain access to different Gemblems. Flipping the card over to the other side will reveal new Gemblems, but will also a new rule to follow! Either discard the completed puzzle or draw a new card to complete a new high score streak!

Gemblems was created with the thought of reducing time spent mindlessly scrolling on your phone. Perfect for waiting in doctor's offices, car rides, and breaks at work. Gemblems fits in your pocket and plays directly in your hands for a great portable game!

Shuffle the deck and draw a few cards. We recommend 5 for a standard puzzle. 
Fan the cards in your have so either the top right or left corner of all cards is visible. 
Reorder, rotate, or flip cards until the Gemblems in the card corners meet all the rules listed on the cards. 
Once the puzzle is completed; discard your hand and draw a new set of cards OR draw a new card and try to add it to your puzzle.

—description from the publisher

You are scientists tasked with creating THE CURE. The cure to what? Not important! Work together with your fellow scientists; however, rumor has it that a rival company has attempted to bribe someone to sabotage your efforts... Of course, it is just a rumor, right?

The Cure is a small hidden role game where you are working together to reach your goal. Players are dealt a hidden role and 12 numbered cards with the rest set to the side. Then, players will take turns adding 1 card face down to a center pile while declaring aloud the supposed total of the cards. If suspicion gets too high, a vote may be taken to use a microscope to check the last played card, but only the active player may look. Once players believe they are close to the goal, they may vote to stop. If the goal is reached, the good players win! If not, the bad player wins. There may be games with no bad player, so if the goal is not reached, all of the players lose!

Carefully selected truths and lies are the main strategy of this game, whether good or bad, a well made lie can guarantee victory. Just be careful not to be caught.

—description from the publisher

This is a kind of distillation of my previous hex-and-counter Barbarossa games, onto a standard US wargaming 22x34" mapboard.

Refined Disruption mechanics, including a Both Disrupted (BD) result which reminds me of the old Engaged result in some past games.  If the Axis player does not want to exchange, he can accept a Disruption result against the defender instead of a 0.5 exchange result, or a BD result in the event of a 1.0 exchange.
Accept the losses to get a major breakthrough?  Luck can be pressed on offense or defense.

Basically corps to army level with selected panzer divisions for small change.

Half-month turns which should play 33% faster than for Death Struggle.

Hidden Russian units.  Overall Russian strength may not be known until game's end, and that can affect victory requirements, up or down.
The big if slow Russian mechanized corps are just as clumsily dangerous as ever ... if they exist.
How hard to push?  How big a risk to take? ... as historically!
Some solitaire potential on the German side.

—description from the designer

description:
Dastardly Dog's Dogpocalypse is a 2-8 player card game for families and parties, it takes 5 minutes to learn and 20 minutes to play. Race to the Winners Bone with Rainbow-Farts, Dogapults, Go-karts, jet packs, and more or you can attack the other dogs with UFOs, Zombies, Mini-Nukes, Lightning Strikes, dog catchers, and more but don't forget to yell woof or you'll have to restart.

A family-friendly card game that's perfect for all ages, Adults, Teens, and Kids. It's 2-8 players so it's great for small get-togethers or it doubles as a fun party game to break the ice and stay cool!
With fast-paced action, each round only takes about 20 minutes to play, so much fun that you will. Be Dastardly, Play Often!

Learn to play in as little as 5 minutes with easy-to-follow full color illustrated instructions.
This edition contains all 144 cards from both the Original Game and Wave 2 Expansion Pack

—description from the publisher

boardgame: No Ni Ma! Krakowiacy i Górale
description:
No Ni Ma! is a bit of a sentimental trip back to the world of childhood. It's one of those card games that we played with friends in the stairwells or under the carpet beater. Back then, playing Huj or Gówn because that was the name of the original game in our slang had something magical about it. Here we learn the dialect: Krakow and highlander.

No Ni Ma  is a traditional, popular card game - based on memory and bluffing. In the new version, it refreshes - for the older, teaches - for the younger, brings closer - to the immigrants - two beautiful Lesser Polish dialects!

During the game you are collecting sets of cards. The player with the most of the sets wins.



Przypomnij sobie czym jest borówka, ceper czy dudkiNo ni ma! to tradycyjna popularna gra karciana - oparta na pamięci i blefie. proste zasady! Dynamiczna rozgrywka i świetna zabawa! Gra przybliża również gwarę krakowską i góralską

Fairy Day is a card-based placement game in which 2-6 players take on the role of a fairy and tend a magical garden.  Over the course of the game, the garden grows and flourishes and the player with the most points wins at the end. The game is a good mix of tactics and luck.

Fairy Day ist ein kartenbasiertes Lege-Spiel bei dem 2-6 Spieler in die Rolle einer Fee schlüpfen und einen magischen Garten pflegen.  Im Verlaufe des Spiels wächst und gedeiht dieser und es gewinnt am Ende er Spieler, der die meisten Punkte sammeln konnte. Dabei findet das Spiel eine gute Mischung aus Taktik & Glück.

Enjoy this beautiful version of the classic game of Scrabble with a National Parks spin! Enhance your score with special features such as playing National Parks Cards and earning bonus points for playing National Parks words.

Note: This game has cards and extra scoring rules which are not found in Scrabble: The National Parks Edition.

This mini expansion replaces one random card in each row during setup. Each individual card only indicates the row or column where a treasure or curse can be found. Only by finding the pair of special cards can you uncover the exact location of a treasure or curse; the pair will tell you the row and column of the item — the intersection of the row and column is the location of the item.

Because these treasures are harder to find, each one has a value of 36! But beware, these cards also introduce an extra curse introducing the possibility of a fifth cursed location! At the end of the game, the new treasures are awarded as in the base game (evenly split between the surrounding players).

In the mythical realm of Elemental Dragons, players embark on a journey to harness the powers of the four elemental suits: earth, water, fire, and air. The goal? To amass a hoard of precious dragon gems, the coveted currency of this world. But beware, for amidst the riches lie hidden dangers in the form of dragon eggs, which bring negative scores to those unfortunate enough to acquire them.
In Elemental Dragons, players engage in a battle of wits, employing elemental powers to outmaneuver their opponents. Each round, players compete to win tricks by playing cards of higher value and utilizing the unique abilities of the powerful dragon cards.
At the outset, a dominant suit is randomly determined. Then, the first player to play a card sets the lesser dominant suit if their played card does not belong to the dominant suit. Thus, the order of precedence emerges: dominant suit, followed by the lesser suit, with the remaining two suits only useful in this round for their powers. The dragon's powers will enable you to change the dominant suit, steal gems from the trick, and poison the trick with eggs among other things. Each player plays a card either to try to win the trick, or to play a useful power for them, or both. Then there is a second round of card exchange, offering players the opportunity to exchange their initial card for another, only if the new card can win the trick. Players also can choose to "downgrade" their card for an egg card, poisoning that trick for the winner by nullifying gems from the final score.
This mechanic injects an element of bluff and double-bluff into the gameplay, as players must carefully consider when to reveal their most potent cards and when to conceal their true intentions.
As the game unfolds, players must carefully balance their pursuit of dragon gems with the risk of acquiring detrimental dragon eggs. Enter the world of Elemental Dragons and unleash the power of the elements in this thrilling trick-taking adventure.

—description from the publisher (translated)

En el reino mítico de los Dragones Elementales, los jugadores emprenden un viaje para aprovechar los poderes de los cuatro palos elementales: tierra, agua, fuego y aire. ¿El objetivo? Acumular un tesoro de preciosas gemas de dragón, la codiciada moneda de este mundo. Pero cuidado, porque entre las riquezas yacen peligros ocultos en forma de huevos de dragón, que otorgan puntuación negativa a aquellos desafortunados que los adquieren.
En Dragones Elementales los jugadores participan en una batalla de ingenio, empleando poderes elementales para superar a sus oponentes. En cada ronda, los jugadores compiten para ganar bazas jugando cartas de mayor valor y utilizando las habilidades únicas de las poderosas cartas de dragón.
Al principio, se determina aleatoriamente un palo dominante. Luego, el primer jugador en jugar una carta establece el palo subdominante si su carta jugada no pertenece al palo dominante. Así, surge el orden de precedencia: palo dominante, seguido por el subdominante, con los dos palos restantes solo útiles en esta ronda por sus poderes. Los poderes de los dragones te permitirán cambiar el palo dominante, robar gemas de la baza y envenenarla con huevos, entre otras cosas. Cada jugador juega una carta ya sea para intentar ganar la baza, o para jugar un poder útil, o ambos. Luego hay una segunda ronda de intercambio de cartas, que ofrece a los jugadores la oportunidad de cambiar su carta inicial por otra, sólo si la nueva carta puede ganar la baza. Los jugadores también pueden optar por "rebajar" su carta por una carta de huevo, envenenando esa baza para el ganador al anular gemas de la puntuación final.
Este mecanismo introduce un elemento de engaño y doble engaño en el juego, ya que los jugadores deben considerar cuidadosamente cuándo revelar sus cartas más potentes y cuándo ocultar sus verdaderas intenciones.
A medida que el juego avanza, los jugadores deben equilibrar cuidadosamente su búsqueda de gemas de dragón con el riesgo de adquirir perjudiciales huevos de dragón. ¡Entra al mundo de los Dragones Elementales y libera el poder de los elementos en esta emocionante aventura de captura de bazas!

—description from the publisher

Overflight! is a solitaire aerial reconnaissance wargame about the CIA’s TOP SECRET U-2 spy plane. It is set in the Cold War from 1956 to 1960. This is the second book of the Special Mission Series from Historic Wings, a publisher whose mission is to publish aviation games that are true to history. Not only will you learn about the CIA's covert air force and the U-2, you will have the chance to see how you would have fared had you been in charge.

The importance of the U-2 program to the US and Allied intelligence communities cannot be overstated. U-2 overflights provided 90% of all intelligence regarding the Soviet military during one of the most critical periods of the Cold War. The program was indispensable to US national security.

This is a wargame book -- a full-featured wargame presented in the format of a book. This provides buyers with two options: a) to save some money and get the wargame book from Amazon and make your own maps, play aids, and game counters; b) to spend about what you'd spend for any boxed wargame by purchasing the book from Amazon AND purchasing the ready-made game assets (all maps, play aids, and game counters) from Blue Panther.

Amazon Link: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0D1QKXK74/
Blue Panther: https://www.bluepantherllc.com/products/overflight

In the game, you play both in the role of the CIA’s U-2 Program Director -- historically, Richard Bissell -- and, when you fly missions, you take on the role of the pilots themselves as you navigate over Soviet-controlled territory to photograph your targets and, if all goes well, return safely. All of the pilots in your CIA Pilot Roster are recruited from the US Air Force and “sheep-dipped” – thus, they’re “officially civilians”. Two other pilots are drawn from the ranks of the Royal Air Force (RAF), just as in history.

In 1956, your missions will seem easy. The Soviets have little chance of intercepting your U-2s as they soar beyond the reach of even the highest-flying Soviet interceptors. However, with each passing year, the Soviets will deploy newer, better aircraft like the MiG-21 and Su-9. Ultimately, you will face SA-2 “Guideline” Surface to Air Missiles (SAMs), the very missile that shot down Francis Gary Powers on his U-2 flight on May 1, 1960, an event that ended CIA’s overflights of the Soviet Union.

Among your assignments will be photographing important targets, such as Tyuratam, where the Soviets tested Intercontinental Ballistic Missiles (ICBMs) and the facilities at Aralsk-7, a secret Soviet biological weapons research site, and more.

Be prepared to sweat out your missions -- will you succeed or will one of your pilots die trying?

The game was developed from declassified TOP SECRET and SECRET documents sourced from the CIA. It is a narrative-style game with extensive decision-making and features a mid-sized tabletop footprint and easy-to-learn rules.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Air War Book Games (Historic Wings)

description:
Cohors Cthulhu: Tactics is a horror-themed, solitaire/co-operative adventure wargame set in the Cohors Cthulhu universe during the height of Imperial Rome. You begin your heroic journey as one of a handful of survivors of an ambush, desperately trying to escape the Mythos-ridden mists of a Germanic forest. As your heroes grow in experience and power, you will fulfill your destiny, becoming the leader of a powerful legion and facing the avatars of the Outer Gods themselves in full-scale war.

The game is meant to fuse the strategic nuance of tabletop wargames with the immersive narrative of role playing games. Cohors Cthulhu: Tactics will feature a wide range of 28mm miniatures in both resin and 3D print at-home STL files. The miniatures range features Roman Centurions, scoundrels, nobles, hunters, priests, druids, assassins, soldiers, and warriors. Facing them will be the full might of the Outer Gods: The Cult of Mormo with their Priests, Servitors, Ghouls and Overlords of Mormo, Teufel Hounds, Fluttering Fiends, Sheehad, Elder Things, Chosen and Die Draugr.

Players start the game from the corners of the board.
Players take turns rolling the dice and moving around the board in any direction. When a player stands on a space with a mushroom marker, he removes the marker from the board and draws a mushroom card. If the mushroom is edible, its card is kept. If the mushroom is poisonous, the card is discarded. In addition to the description, the mushroom cards contain the number of kilograms that have been collected. The person who collected the most kilograms of edible mushrooms wins the game.

—user summary

Embark on a thrilling spy adventure with The Man From Sector Six, The Mystery Agency's latest home escape room by Olivier-winning writer Henry Lewis.

This fourth instalment in the acclaimed series opens with players discovering a newspaper cutting and a locked, official-looking document portfolio left by a mysterious disappearing agent. A dire warning about a monumental peril facing humanity sets the stage for an urgent and immersive challenge.

Navigate through multiple brainteasers and locks, uncover a hidden website, and hack into the agency's secrets. Real-world objects, including a brass rotating cipher wheel and a complex data chip, serve as authentic clues, ensuring an engaging home escape room experience.

Designed for 1-6 players aged 12 and above, this escape room encourages teamwork or solo adventures. With nothing damaged during play, the fully reusable experience can be shared after for others to try - reset instructions provided.

—description from the publisher
Monopoly version based on the Belgian town of Lier.

Beyond the Event Horizon is a diesel-punk inspired 4X space tile game that focuses on larger, multi-player combats and customizable ships. It focuses less on civilization building and focuses more on fast-paced, competitive gameplay.

Enter through a black hole into uncharted space. Discover new planets to capture them for resources and tech. Use this tech to upgrade your ships with basic upgrades and special upgrades found in space. You can then customize your three different ship types to create a superior fighting force and dominate your opponents in large battles. Battles can have dozens of ships, from multiple players all fighting for control of an area, and the battles move so quickly you'll have lots of opportunities to come back for a second chance at winning.

You can use planetary enhancements to develop and defend your planets. During each round marauder ships will enter through new placed wormholes to attack and when defeated, they drop new special upgrades to enhance your ships. These wormholes open up the battlefield as you can travel through them to access different parts or the map. The tiles are placed in a new spot every game, so find new strategies to win.

Beyond the Event Horizon features streamlined rules with complex, engaging strategy. This is a great game for beginners of 4X space tile games, with still so much for long time lovers of the genre. Fast paced and action-packed, it's not "Space...The Final Frontier" it's "Space...THE FINAL FRONT LINE!"

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Doctor Esker's Notebook IV: The Moroccan Affair
description:
The fourth game in the Doctor Esker's Notebook puzzle card game series, this decks pages come from Doctor Esker's long-time friend and companion, Abellona. Having led Dr. Esker to Morocco in previous games, she leaves behind only a mysterious book full of puzzles written in her own hand. Nobody has cracked her cryptic codes yet. Are you up to the challenge?

The game provides a deck of 82 cards which contain ten devious puzzles to solve. You can play through the puzzles solo or together with family and friends. Great for a party, a game night, or a lazy afternoon.

Each puzzle is composed of a set of cards. You can solve the puzzle by reading, comparing, arranging and pondering the messages, drawings, and artwork on the cards. Solving the puzzle reveals a sequence of numbers. When you think you’ve got it, gather the solution cards corresponding to the number sequence and flip them. If you see a message or image, you’ve solved the puzzle. Persevere to the end and uncover Abellona's mysterious discovery.

Clash between a cunning submarine commander and a ruthless hunter in a thrilling duel in the Atlantic Ocean during World War II. The hunter searches for and destroys the submarine with underwater charges, while the submarine commander hides and evades.

This exciting strategy game is 'print and play', meaning you can easily download and print it at home. To play, laminate the game and use a whiteboard marker to mark the movement of the submarine, draw the ship's loads, mark turns and depleted resources, such as sonar, hydrophone and loads.

Will you be the hunter who triumphs or the hunted who escapes in this exciting strategy game?

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Pawris, the bustling city of entertainment. Here, the greatest bakers gather at Le Délice bakery where the flames of fierce competition fuel the ovens. As one of the bakers, you'll be competing in the culinary arts by baking and selling the most delicious desserts; the best bakers will not only be able to fuse together the finest ingredients to perfection, but also strategize against their competition and sabotage their work.

Start each turn by rejuvenating your pantry with new ingredients. Players will then use action points to prepare, bake, present, and sell their delicious desserts. Amid the baking chaos, players will have the opportunity to sabotage other players or improve their own position through the power of effect cards. Each character also has a unique set of abilities which will influence their strategy during the game.

The skilled bakers have a selection of recipes they can bake, these recipes are categorized in four different tiers of difficulty. The more difficult the recipe, the greater the bonus a baker will attain. The baker with the best strategy and a bit of luck will be the one who claims the title Virtuoso!

So, whisk up, knead down, and bake your way to glory!

—description from the publisher

This game is a card game for 1 or 2 players launched by the famous Japanese game publisher DOMINA GAMES. Players will transform into powerful witches and engage in astrological duels with their opponents. This game is a spin-off of the "Blade Rondo" series. Players will play the game from the perspective of Veronica and other witches. Win the grand astrological ceremony and realize your wishes.
In this game, players can use a combination of card with a total number of 10 to generate a star dust, use a star dust to attack, use 3 star dusts to sublimate cards and use sublimated cards. The player who makes the opponent's life point from 20 to 0 will win the game.

A 2 to 4 player game where the objective is to be the first to make two sequences of five connecting tiles. In order to do so, players must make use of their cards strategically, placing their tiles in a square that matches the shape and color of the card they played that turn.
Special cards with game-changing effects are also included, incentivizing players to use them at the appropriate time to thwart the opopnent's plan and gain the upper hand.

A team of archaeologists discovered a cave in the Inca civilization that had never been explored by the human race. People from all over the world flocked to the site to investigate, and you are no exception. Since it was an uncharted territory, danger lurks in every corner of the cave. As you studied the cave, the ground suddenly began to tremble beneath you. Rocks began to fall, and your group scattered to seek for safety. Fortunately, no one was hurt. Unfortunately, everyone is now trapped in separate places. You found yourself surrounded by a bunch of strange symbols on the wall, and with your communication devices still functional, you tried to exchange messages to decipher the symbols, hoping for a chance to escape among the gibberish writings.

—description from the publisher

Originally sold at Pax Unplugged 2023

A new enemy for CoraQuest plus a QR code for a linked downloadable quest, a family dungeon crawling game.

Special ability: At the end of Every Enemy Phase roll one white dice to make a teleport check. If the result is a sucess then Slippery Jim teleports.

A minimalist solo fantasy game where you play using only three cards and one die. Each quest have a map card, a quest card and an event card, composing a complete adventure. You'll choose different paths and enter locations, where you'll roll the die to face challenges, also you have a single atribute value, your modifier, which will alter throughout the adventure and affect your results.

—description from the publisher

100 years of Edelberger Schokolade. That’s 100 years of customer loyalty, ambition and tradition.
A perfect occasion to celebrate the anniversary as a private function. And it would have been the best anniversary in the company’s history, hadn’t it been for the insidious murder of the sales manager.

Who killed Oliver Borgmann?
It’s most likely that the murderer was among the guests. But who was cold-blooded enough to commit such a crime while colleagues were happily celebrating next door?

Take a look behind the scenes of a chocolate manufacturer located in Germany and untangle a web of intrigues, blackmail, nepotism and excess in Oliver Borgmann’s Last Celebration.

—description from the publisher

Survival of the Fittest is a wildlife themed, fast paced, strategy game which will see you square off against your friends and family as you battle it out to see who will survive. As with all things wild, there is an element of luck involved, as the roll of the dice will prompt your next move, but then comes the strategy; do you attack or defend, run and hide, or stalk and pounce? Every decision has a consequence, every move could be fatal, so keep your wits about you as you prepare to strike.
Survival of the Fittest can be played with 2, 3 or 4 players, and each variation requires a new strategy and a new way of surviving. Depending on the type of lion you are, the game plays between 30 - 45 minutes and keep in mind that there are obstacles that are introduced during the game, so be careful not to get blocked in… or out!
The third big element to the game: can you convince the other players to help you when you need it, even though you both know you are doing your best to try to eat each other’s prey? Survival of the Fittest is an interactive game where having alliances will definitely help you…but look out for the double cross!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Through Hell and Back: B-17 Expansion
description:
Climb up and strap into the B-17.
This expansion for Through Hell and Back includes;


     9 new missions with custom artwork and new locations. A V-1 rocket facility is in France, and refinery and chemical plants are in Germany.
     9 Historical bomber squadron cards from the 447th BG.
     Escort cards. P-38s make their entrance into the game.
     10 Novice crew cards.
     10 Veteran crew cards.
     A new system for having squadron mates fly with you.
     1 game board featuring a B17 from the 447th bomb group.


—description from the designer

Black Sonata: Strange Shadows includes several expansions that may be added to the base game. These should work separately or in various combinations, including with the earlier Fair Youth expansions. The new content includes:

The Darkest Ladies - Unlike those in the base game or Fair Youth expansion, this alternative set of Dark Lady cards has no suits, and you must deduce the Dark Lady’s symbols in their correct order. Features more card-flipping hole-peeking antics.

Indirections - This expansion adds two different types of stealth cards to be mixed into your deck. Cards with compass symbols show what compass direction the Dark Lady traveled from her last location, while others show the potential colour of her current location.

Blazonry - The Dark Lady is hiding behind the shield of her heraldic blazon. To access her you must first deduce the symbols of her blazon using special fog cards.

The Ferryman - The ferry is the quickest way to get around London, allowing you to travel from any riverside location (marked with the boat icon) to any other riverside location in one turn, possibly heading off the Dark Lady as she flees. But this comes at a cost – the ferryman protects the Dark Lady, preventing you from searching for her at any riverside location. In addition, you must collect the location cards in a specific order.

Cat and Mouse - This is an asymmetric two-player game of cat and mouse, where one player is the Dark Lady and the other is the Historian trying to track and unmask her.

Tarry cards - This small expansion allows you to temporarily pause the Dark Lady’s flight, potentially letting you catch up to her, but you must pay a later cost...

Extra fog cards - Four additional fog cards are provided, each with a beneficial effect. You may use these to: (a) Increase the size of the fog deck to allow more searches in a game – especially if including the harder Dark Ladies from either expansion, as these may require more clues to solve; (b) Replace detrimental fog cards in the base deck to make it more appealing (but still risky) to use fog cards that arise; or (c) Randomly replace fog cards from the base deck to increase the variety of fog card actions.

—description from the publisher

A mysterious Ichor has infected the world of Suros, changing the alien life into mutant beasts. Take control of 3 science driven players to investigate into the Ichor, kill the mutants and discover what secrets it holds. Spend action points to make moves, use abilities and roll attack dice. Gain mutations that improves abilities and equip new gear to become stronger and face more powerful foes. Defeat 3 mutants to face the final boss and unlock even more secrets!

—description from the designer

Kemet: Rise of the Gods brings various new and long-awaited features to Kemet: Blood and Sand.

This expansion includes: Gods mode, Team mode, a 6 player mode, a solo mode, and two new tile colors, Amber and Amethyst, with various other modules, content and quality-of-life components.  The Rise of the Gods expansion will come with a new rulebook (including the core rules and all the rules for the new modules) and 6 new player aids.

—description from the publisher

Have you always dreamed of beating Bandido but never managed to? Managed to win a match in 20?

Your time has come. Now go!

To give you a little help in your matches, the "Go Now!" they only have 4 exits (as opposed to the original game where there are 5 or 6 exits) and greatly improve your chance of capturing the bandit.

Use one of the 5 "Go Now!" as the starting card of your game, in place of the original card and try to win with this "little help".

Play with children? Use the cards to simplify your games!

Numbered from 1 to 5, these cards are dealt separately. Collect them all!

You're recruiting for an epic quest but must avoid having redundant leaders on the team. You'll score points by collecting cards during this trick-taking game but will lose points for collecting duplicate face cards (Jack / Queen / King).

Remove 1 joker. Deal 13 cards to each player. Dealer may declare gameplay variations while dealing. Display 1 card to determine the trump suit. With 2-3 players, discard up to 4, draw to replace; with 4 players, pass exactly 4 cards to your partner (across the table).

You must follow the led suit if possible. If you can’t follow suit, play any card. Joker is a "super-trump" and takes the trick. Otherwise, the highest trump takes the trick. Otherwise, the highest in the led suit takes it. Aces are high.

Score the hand: 1 point per card taken. For each face rank (J / Q / K), lose 5 points if you took more than 1 of that rank. With 4 players, don't combine partner cards (or scores) until the end. Play 4 hands.

—description from the designer

Charlie Brown, Lucy, Sally and Snoopy are excited for Valentine's Day and they believe the perfect way to celebrate is by giving and receiving valentines and chocolates.

In this Peanuts valentines edition game players move around the board, collect valentine action cards and chocolates and deliver valentines to their friends.

The first player to share their love and mend their broken heart is the winner!

Sweet valentine fun for you and your favorite Peanuts pal.

—description from the back of the box

Introducing LikeU Cards, the ultimate card game for breaking down barriers and fostering meaningful connections between people. Whether you're at the office, on a date, or spending time with family and friends, our game is designed to put an end to awkward conversations and help you connect with others in a fun, engaging, and meaningful way.

120 curated questions split into two groups, the yellow questions explore what makes us fascinating, and the blue questions uncover what makes us human!

Our game features a variety of thought-provoking prompts that encourage players to share their own stories and experiences, creating a safe and inclusive space where everyone can feel heard and valued. From lighthearted questions to deeper, more reflective prompts, our game is designed to help you connect with others on a level that goes beyond small talk.

What sets LikeU Cards apart is its versatility. Our game is perfect for any social situation, from intimate gatherings to larger parties. It's also a great icebreaker for new colleagues or clients, helping to break down barriers and foster a sense of connection from the get-go.

—description from the publisher

Bread upon the Waters is an asynchronous resource-trading game. Players take the role of rival shipping tycoons managing resources, sending out trading ships, and fulfilling contracts. Each player has a wharf with his own ships and warehouses. Players will also bid for space on other players' ships. At the end of the game, the player with the most money wins.

—description from the designer
A card game with 2 decks and rules. A stick-taking game played in rounds until 3 sticks is reach by the winner. The theme setting is a school with a headmaster and 10 iffy looking misbehaving pupils.

In Ancient Mesopotamia, no other copper merchant was more feared than the great Ea-Nasir. This is what we believe, at least, as demonstrated by complaints eternalized on clay tablets made circa 1750 BCE, some of the oldest customer complaints ever found. Ea-Nasir's skill at selling really bad copper and kicking out any messengers sent to him weren't, to our relief, lost in time.

Your goal in The Copper Trickster, which debuted as CharLatão, is not to be taken in by Ea-Nasir - except when you're playing him, of course. In this competitive trick-taking game, each player has partial knowledge of the quality of the copper being offered. Your goal is to make the most points each round, acquiring good quality copper and avoiding the bad.

Over the course of a round, you play multiple tricks, contesting for victory or avoiding winning according to your knowledge of the copper's quality. The game ends when every player has been Ea-Nasir once, and whoever has the most points wins.

Ultimate Thumb Master is a 74 card party game for 3 or more players that is quick to learn and easy to play. Players take turns drawing a card and completing the task to determine if they will give or take drinks. Once played, cards are stacked atop an overturned cup (not included with the game) with the exception of “Master” cards, which are kept face up in front of players. Players with “Master” cards can use their abilities at any time until the same card is drawn again, at which point they must stack their card. When being stacked, cards must have two corners overhanging any previously stacked cards which is indicated by lighter colored corners printed on the card backs. The game ends when the stack is knocked over, and the player who causes the cards to fall loses, and must finish their drink! Inspired by traditional party games, Ultimate Thumb Master introduces many new and exciting elements making it the ultimate party game.

An exciting investigative game set in a Maltese landscape during the era of the Knights of St John. Through the elimination process of the Knights, Places and Objects, solve The Knight’s Riddle by uncovering who did the act, where and using what. The first player to solve the case, wins.

—description from the publisher

Logħba investigattiva eċitanti f’ambjent Malti ta’ żmien il-Kavallieri ta’ San Ġwann.
Bi proċess ta’ eliminazzjoni tal-Kavallieri, il-Postijiet u l-Oġġetti, solvi l-Każ tal-Kavallier billi tiskopri min hu l-ħati, fejn u biex sar l-att. L-ewwel plejer li jirnexxilu jsolvi l-każ, jirbaħ.

—description from the publisher (Maltese)

Fortune Heroes is an original, captivating, and intriguing game where you and your friends will embark on an epic quest that will test your endurance.

Navigate perilous trails, uncover forgotten treasures, and outmanoeuvre rival heroes in a world of mysticism and danger. With each hero wielding extraordinary powers and the impending Great Flood threatening to wash away all hope, destiny beckons. Will you rise above the tide and claim your rightful place among the Fortune Heroes?

Embrace the unknown. The adventure of a lifetime awaits!


     beautiful card illustrations and artwork in this original exciting game
     combine both skill and luck to create the best possible path without being washed away by The Great Flood
     ideal to be played with family or friends in a cosy atmosphere or even outdoors


—description from the publisher

One player is chosen as a dictator, the others are senators, either loyal or traitors.

A game round starts with senators making proposals which are shuffled and revealed and cause the dictator possibly lose life; after which the dictator can use their cards to question the senators and punish them. The round ends with the senators having option to switch side and revolt.

48 cards grouped together into 12 sets of 4. Sets are called 'Books' in such games by the publisher.
Rules are similar to Quartet games nowadays.
When asking for a card from another player, the questioner must state a country, the Book it comes from AND the population in that country. If the questioned player has that card he must pass to to the questioner. If he does not have the card he must draw a card from the deck and end his turn.
The winner is the player with most books when all have been collected.
Game must be made for trivia buffs with the population of countries in 1896 in their heads!!

In German, the term "dunkelflaute" refers to a period in which energy demand is high but electricity production is low, as in winter on windless days with little sunlight. This expression underlines the importance of planning and diversification in our energy mix.

Dunkelflaute is a competitive game in which players try to build a resilient energy park to guarantee sufficient electricity production on each turn. To achieve this, players must invest wisely in different energy sources and allocate their budgets to maximize production while limiting risk.

On each round, players have three discs of different values to allocate to three different purposes: to purchase new tiles for their park, to purchase new houses to power, and to purchase dice to be used that round. Players then roll the dice and try to place them on the tiles they've bought to power the houses they've purchased. The number of houses a player has managed to power in his park will determine the value of his discs for the following round, and so on until a player ends the game by buying a twelfth house. The round then ends as normal, and the player who has powered up the most houses wins the game.

—description from the designer

Embark on a riveting journey of strategy and fortune in Dice of Steam! Dice of Steam is a roll and write print and play strategy game prepared for 1-4 players in mind. It takes about 30-60 minutes to play, and suits players of age 12 and up.

Become a railroad investor. At the turn of the 20th century many train companies are fighting for expansion of their tracks and stock values. That is a great moment to turn the opportunity to your advantage! You have the power to influence expansion strategies and acquire shares. You will lay the tracks for various companies, gain investment from the cities reached and exchange them into shares of various train companies.

By controlling the track expansion, you influence stock values. Try to predict which companies will be worth the most and invest in their shares while they are still cheap! Help your favorite companies to expand, so your shares will reach even higher value! But watch out to not overinvest as the shares can lose value at some point.

Forge your destiny, outsmart your competitors, and watch your fortune rise with Dice of Steam, a game where strategy meets tactics.

Finally, you can now experience the true war of total annihilation against Australia's greatest threat!  Giant birds!

This box contains all units used by the army in the great emu war and shed load of terrifying emu!  Its everything you need to start recreating the emu war, so that it is never forgotten!

Solo or two player game, contains miniatures and rules.

—description from the publisher

Gather loose elements and build an engine of element producing resources to attain the highest score and win the game on round 21 using the magnetic turn counter built into the lid. The game is played with diamond-shaped cards in a single 84-card deck, held in a fold-open box insert that includes the six draw/discard areas.

This is a compact game for travel and table space, loaded with five play modes. It can play solo or up to five players with built in accelerators for higher player counts to speed the game along. In most game modes you are changing the purchase and draw options when you make your own purchases, laying new cards on top of the ones accessible to players atop the six draw-discard piles.

Varied and largely unique costs for permanent resources challenge players to find buy-path patterns and make strategic choices on wether to reserve cards as part of a draw or hope to buy them before someone else snatches it up. There are many paths to victory, and winning strategies may change as the purchase options alter. The cards have vital information clearly showing along with colorful imagery and art. Each of the seven element colors also has a unique symbol to aid color-blind players.

There are five play modes, from a very fast and easy intro-mode called "Initiate", to "Novice" (adds upgrading), to the standard mode of "Magi" (adds purchases and an economy engine); then "Journeyman" (changes format) and "Alchemist" (adds a few more rules to boost scoring even more). So that the game can be learned quickly and progressively. Play time ranges from 10 minutes to an hour depending on mode and player count.

—description from the publisher

The goal of SG Gwint is to offer the opportunity to play Gwent, which we all love, in a form worthy of the title "well-balanced card game." Unfortunately, the old good Gwent is not that, and it's no wonder. In The Witcher 3, players collect their decks on their journey towards the title of Gwent Grandmaster, and the variety of cards ranges from unusable to absurdly strong.

We approached the new version with respect for the original version and the preservation of the spirit of the game. However, we also utilized the space for innovation - in addition to balancing the strength of the cards and their abilities, we added some of our own, created a few new cards, and replaced the card illustrations with those from the online game GWENT to give them a fresh touch. Furthermore, we replaced the existing interface with our own for enhancement.

How to play SG Gwint

Objective:
The objective of Gwent is to win two out of three rounds by having more points on the board than your opponent.

Setup:
Each player begins with a deck of cards that consists of unit cards representing different factions, abilities, and values. A standard deck contains a minimum of 25 cards. Player takes 10 cards and can change 2 of them.

Gameplay:

Round Structure: The game is played over a series of rounds. Each round allows players to play one card per turn until both players pass consecutively.

Board: The board consists of three rows for each player: melee, ranged, and siege. Each row can hold a certain type of unit card.

Unit Cards: There are different types of cards: melee, ranged, siege, and special cards. Unit cards have a power value and sometimes an ability.

Leader Cards: Each player has a leader card that can be used once per game, usually having a powerful effect that can influence the game significantly.

The Hand: Players draw a certain number of cards at the beginning of each round. Players can mulligan (exchange) a few cards from their hand if they wish.

Scoring: The player with the highest total power on the board at the end of each round wins that round.

Winning a Round: Winning a round does not mean winning the game. Players need to balance winning rounds with conserving cards for future rounds.

Round Length: Players can play as many cards as they like in a round until both players pass. Passing means the player ends their turn and cannot play any more cards in that round.

Card Effects: Many cards have special abilities that can influence the game, such as boosting other units, damaging enemy units, or affecting the game board.

Tiebreakers: In the event of a tie at the end of a round, the player who won the previous round wins the game. If neither player has won a round, the game continues with another round.

Strategy:
Gwent requires strategic thinking, bluffing, and careful resource management. Players must decide when to commit powerful cards and when to hold back to secure victory in later rounds.

description:
Gather 'round the campfire everyone. Time to make some tasty, gooey treats. Grab a stick, toast those marshmallows just right, and smoosh them between some graham crackers with some melty chocolate. But you'd better make 'em right, or you might just end up with a hot mess!

In S’mores Galore players compete to score the most point. You score points by making S'mores. To make S'mores, you'll first have to grab graham cracker, chocolate, and marshmallow cards from a grid on the table. You'll have to stack them to your line-up in the order you pick them up to create the perfect S’mores. With some clever hand management, you can score a lot of S'mores and win the game.

—description form the publisher

boardgame: A Dutiful Fight: The Battle of Chateauguay, October 26, 1813
description:
“…that now they had fulfilled their duty to their God, they would fulfil that to their King.”—Remark by British Captain Longtin, Commander of the Beauharnais Militia, after leading his men in prayer before the Battle of Chateauguay.

The US capture of Canada was assumed in Washington, DC “to be a mere matter of marching”. However, the fight for the British colony was severe and prolonged. The US mounted a double-pronged offensive from New York to take Canadian capital city of Montreal in the fall of 1813 planned by US Secretary of War, John Armstrong, Jr. However, the two US forces were commanded by Generals James Wilkenson, who had a particularly dubious and corrupt history, and Major General Wade Hampton, who loathed and despised Wilkenson.  With little to no cooperation between the US forces, their British and Canadian opponents were able to deal with each US force separately.

With most of the British regular forces devoted to dealing with Wilkenson, it was left to Canadian militia and First Nation warriors, under the command of Lieutenant Colonel Charles de Salabarry, to stop Hampton. The combined Canadian and First Nation force skirmished repeatedly with the Americans as soon as they crossed the St. Lawrence River, frustrating Hampton’s hopes that he could beat his rival to Montreal. The weather and sparsely settled region meant that the few trails were muddy quagmires that frequently mired the American supply wagons and artillery caissons, slowing the American’s advance even more. Along the Chateauguay River Hampton learned that the enemy had built a substantial defensive position along the main route to Montreal, and he divided his force in two. One would pin the Canadians and First Nation warriors in place, while the other crossed the river to flank the enemy and capture fords that were to the rear of their defensive works. If successful, the enemy would be trapped and the way to Montreal open to the Americans. However, dividing one’s force in the face of an undefeated and determined enemy is a very dangerous gambit in warfare.

Can you do as well, or better, than your historical counterparts?

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Battles for Canada (High Flying Dice)

description:
Do you like museums? In this game, you will run a new museum from the Phoenician period. Starting from scratch, you must manage to group the different Phoenician objects in their corresponding display cases. The showcases will display the pieces that are shown to the public who come to visit your museum, so you will have to try to collect beautiful pieces from the Phoenician past to win the game. The more different pieces in your showcases, the more points you will score!
However, this won't be an easy task, be prepared! many things can happen to disturb the peace of a museum. You must be careful with school visits, including building works and renovations, as they may prevent you from working that day. And don't forget to maintain good relations with other museums! Sometimes they may ask you to lend them an object temporarily for display... and this could leave your display case without the most important piece!

—description from the publisher (translated)

¿Te gustan los museos? En este juego tu papel será dirigir un nuevo museo de época fenicia. Al empezar de cero, deberás conseguir agrupar los diferentes objetos fenicios en sus correspondientes vitrinas. En las vitrinas se exhibirán las piezas que se muestran al público que acude a visitar tu museo, por lo que tendrás que intentar reunir hermosas piezas del pasado fenicio para ganar la partida. Cuantas más piezas diferentes haya en tus vitrinas, ¡más puntos conseguirás!

Sin embargo, esto no será tarea fácil ¡prepárate!, pueden pasar muchas cosas que alteren la tranquilidad y el silencio de un museo. Tendrás que tener cuidado con las visitas de centros escolares, ¡incluso con las obras y remodelaciones en el edificio!, ya que podrían impedirte trabajar ese día. ¡Y no te olvides de mantener buenas relaciones con otros museos! A veces pueden solicitarte que les prestes temporalmente un objeto para que sea expuesto… ¡y esto podría dejar tu vitrina sin la pieza más importante!

¿Estás listo? Pues ve eligiendo qué vitrinas prefieres y ¡a trabajar!

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Fine Art and Art Museums

description:
This is a fixed set of 12 expansion cards for use with Cosmic Frog. The pack contains six new Ability cards, five new Action cards, and one new Splinter Strike card.

—description from the publisher

This is a fixed set of 18 expansion cards for use with Cosmic Frog: Find Muck. The pack contains six new Ability cards (one set of six cards for the dice-based deck; one set of six cards for the chip-based deck), five new Action cards, and one new Splinter Strike card.

—description from the publisher

Offworld Empires is a light 4X space strategy game for 2 to 4 players. Each player chooses a unique space civilization with its own strengths and weaknesses and builds their space empire in a far flung galaxy.

At the head of your galactic empire, you will explore an unknown galaxy full of various planets, asteroids, wormholes, and much more. Use colony ships to settle on new planets and gather their resources, or send your marines to pacify those that resist your expansion.

Erect buildings to harvest food and grow your population of workers, search for new technologies from a unique tech wheel, assemble spaceships to secure your own airspace or battle enemy fleets, and appoint senators to vote military or economic laws that will give you an edge over your opponents.

The customized worker placement mechanism is a key point of the game. Every player can build the buildings of their liking – provided they have the necessary resources – and assign their workers to them to maximize their efficiency. You need to find the proper balance between population growth, scientific research and raw production, or choose to specialize in a single domain at the expense of others.

Will you manage to lead you galactic civilization to victory?

—description from the publisher

Mode 1: «The Couplets»

You and your brothers-in-law have bought some semi-detached houses, but upon entering, you realize they are filled with rats. It’s time to roll up your sleeves and clean the houses to be able to boast of being the best rat exterminators!

Objective of the game: Get rid of all the rats in your house as quickly as possible and compete with your brothers-in-law to see who is the most efficient in the task of rat extermination.

Mode 2: «The House» (Solo Game)

You have bought a beautiful countryside house, but when you enter, you discover with horror that it’s full of rats. You don’t want your brother-in-law to find out about this problem, so you must get rid of as many rats as possible before his visit.

Objective of the game: Eliminate the maximum number of rats within a limited time and prevent your brother-in-law from discovering the infestation.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Puzzle X Crime: A Murderous Birthday
description:
The Thonbach family is one of the wealthiest families in the region. Yesterday, Eduard Thonbach was found dead at home on the day of his birthday! There is a lot at stake and the clock is ticking. Who killed him and why? The investigations begin with the puzzle. Piece by piece, the decisive note is decoded. The truth is out there waiting to be revealed.

Are you ready to become the master of the puzzle and detectives? It's time to solve the case!

Puzzle X Crime is a combination of exciting mystery and puzzle fun! First, the crime scene is assembled from 406 puzzle pieces, then the puzzlers slip into the role of investigators. Which objects in the motif should be examined more closely? The numbers on the back of the puzzle pieces lead to playing cards and envelopes with important evidence. Is it possible to combine all the clues correctly and get on the trail of the perpetrators? Puzzle meets crime game! The popular game principle, in which you examine a case file with evidence and solve a criminal case, is now also available as a Ravensburger puzzle - Puzzle X Crime! The highlight: the puzzle is also printed on the back with numbers that provide valuable information on how to solve the case. Then intuition, combination skills and criminalist sense are required. Together with the enclosed playing cards and the evidence in envelopes, the puzzlers gradually determine the murder and can find the perpetrator, murder motif and murder weapon. Also ideal as joint puzzle and investigation fun! No game material has to be destroyed and the Puzzle X Crime can be played again.

Contents:
- 408 puzzle pieces with printed back in two different colours
- A suction cup for flipping puzzle pieces
- 72 playing cards with valuable clues
- 10 envelopes with different evidence material

boardgame: Puzzle X Crime: The Gift of Murder
description:
The venerable Lord George McAllister is dead - shot with an old duel gun on the sofa of his hunting room. Everything points to a suicide - if it weren't for Chief Inspector Paul Higgins, who is plagued by doubts.

And so he asks you to search the crime scene...

Can you confirm the thesis of suicide or is there perhaps something even more terrible behind it?

Are you ready to become the master of the puzzle and detectives? It's time to solve the case!

HitPoint is a multi-purpose card game for players to create characters, build decks, and battle in a standalone trading card format or adventure in a tabletop RPG campaign.

Your deck represents your health, which is also your main resource to play cards. The more powerful the card, the more it will exhaust your character. You win when all opponents’ HP is reduced to 0 or they run out of playable cards, and you have cards remaining in your deck or your hand.

This keeps the game fast and simple, while giving a satisfying visual effect to your character’s exhaustion.

HitPoint features meaningful strategy in combination with random dice rolling. This allows each player to make choices that affect how the game progresses but the dice rolls can influence the potency of cards played. Even the rarest cards can underperform and the common cards can overperform.

—description from the designer

In this semi-cooperative game of strategy and deduction, the fate of the friends lies in your hands.

Embark on an extraordinary adventure in the whimsical world of Perspectópolis! When Alice and her friends stumble upon a mysterious wooden box bearing the inscription "Perspectópolis," they are transported to a dimension where reality bends and twists in fantastical ways.

As players, you must take on the roles of Alice, Ethan, Mark, Olivia, and even Whisper the cat as you navigate the mind-bending streets of Perspectópolis. Your mission? To find the elusive amulets, rescue Whisper, and unlock the portal back home. But beware, Perspectópolis is filled with enigmatic inhabitants and perplexing puzzles. Each turn, you'll explore the pentagonal-shaped board, seeking clues from the locals to uncover the whereabouts of the amulets and your companions.

Will you unravel the secrets of Perspectópolis and escape back to Villa Willowbrook, or will you be forever lost in this surreal dimension?

—description from the publisher

A card game where the objective is to be the last ninja standing.
Players draw a card from the deck each turn and are eliminated if the drawn card is a trap and they have no "escape" card in hand. The rest of the cards provide ninja tools and skills that players can use to avoid losing and to hinder their opponents.

A two-player game using the principles of L'Attaque (and, later, games such as Stratego) where your playing pieces are hidden after secret, initial placement and then moved - turn by turn - to try and 'take' your opponent's pieces.

When attacking, both Attacker and Defender are revealed and the piece with the higher value wins - removing the other from play. Ties mean the pieces remain in situ.

The ultimate goal is to take your opponent's "Treasure" piece before they take yours!

—user summary

When you were invited to a weekend at Dalrymple Hall, you expected wining and dining in the lap of luxury. Instead, you got a murder.

Someone has killed Sir Eustace and locked all the guests in the house, trapping you inside with a body and a murderer. The only way to escape is to find the killer before they start bumping off the witnesses. Could it be the widow, the cold and forbidding Lady Anne? Or perhaps the victim's son, the scheming and money-hungry Benedict? Or maybe the butler did it? Anyone in here could be a murderer, and it's up to you to find out who. No-one's leaving until you do.

Play as either a traditional escape room, or across an evening as a dinner party, for a night you'll never forget... As long as you get out alive.

—description from the publisher

Introducing Furylands - Ashes of Avalon from Warrios Games, a cooperative fantasy roleplaying board game for up to four players.  Forge your hero, embark on quests, challenge dangerous foes and find incredible items to boost your power.
Create a totally customized character using an innovative skill pairing system. There are no restrictive, predefined classes in Furylands - you create your class. Play a massive campaign with over 40 hours of content and virtually endless replayability.

Play one of twelve characters, each with their own special traits and starting items. There are no restrictive, predefined classes in Furylands. Characters start as a blank slate for you to mold into your ultimate adventurer.    Mix any two of the nine skill lines together to create a totally unique character. Customize your character even further as the game progresses, adding life, mana, skill power and skill variety.

An extensive skill system is one of Furylands' defining features. There are 120 skills to master across 9 skill lines - Fire, Frost, Chaos, Shape Change, Necromancy, Poison, Archery, Martial and Spirit Magic. Mix and match skill lines to make a completely unique character.   Any character can learn any skill.Combine Fire and Chaos to build the ultimate ranged damage dealer. Combine Martial and Shape Change to make a hybrid tiger, bear, dragon or wolf melee character. Combine Poison and Frost to make a poisoning, freezing assassin. Summon warrior or mage minions to do your bidding. Learn special skills by defeating bosses and use them against your foes. The possibilities are endless.

Play over 60 highly-detailed, full color maps while playing through an epic campaign storyline.  Explore haunted ruins, cryptic dungeons, frozen landscapes and more.    There are no reusable tilesets in the game - each map layout is unique and every quest challenge is different.  Scenario setup is fast and easy.

Combat in Furylands is fast-paced and fun.   Each of the game's five zones has a different set of monsters for your party to challenge.  Monsters and bosses have unique abilities, ensuring dynamic and unpredictable combat. Enemies are controlled by game rules and don't require a GM.

—description from the publisher

Explore unique, tricky strategies in the Arcs base game with new Leaders, and add wild twists to both the Arcs base and campaign games with new Lore.

—description form the publisher

Initially known as the "More to Explore Pack" during the Kickstarter campaign.
