description:
The monkeys are on the loose!

In Monkey Fun, you try to claim a connected area as quickly as possible with your gang of monkeys. The cards you play indicate on which square you can place a monkey, but the other monkeys are already waiting to snatch this space from you...

Blood Suckers is a competitive card based game that sees each player take on the role of a different Blood Sucking critter in the animal kingdom. Using each Blood Suckers bonus, each player in turn order will choose from the available Hosts to suck blood from. But before they can successfully Attach to that Host, they must draw a begone card and roll a dice to see if they pass. At the end of each round, every Blood Sucker attached to a Host draws Blood from that Host.

boardgame: Horror on the Orient Express: The Board Game – New Monstrosities Expansion
description:
This expansion comes with three completely new types of Monsters. During setup you can decide which Monster you want to use—the green one from the core box or the green one from the New Monstrosities expansion. You can mix and match, but you should alway stick to having three different colors in the game.

In the New Monstrosities expansion, you will find:

     7 flying Monster minis on transparent bases
     5 abducting Monster minis
     3 Flower Lady Monster minis
     3 new Monster cards with new Monster abilities


There are 3 types of Monsters in the core game — each with a unique color and a name. They attack the train during the game and they are dangerous to the Passengers, but each in a different way. Monsters appear alongside the train—when they are placed next to the car with no Passengers, they will move forward with the aim of reaching the Locomotive. When the Monsters attack, you will notice very quickly the unique kind of threat each of them pose to your journey.

The new red monster looks to be a very calm woman with a strange haircut, but don’t let its appearance fool you! As soon as this creature notices potential victims, it becomes clear that the woman's body is a twisted disguise for the real creature hidden underneath. She metamorphosizes during the game and reveals her true face—a bloodthirsty plant-like beast that will try to kill everything in its path.

The new blue monsters are winged creatures that spread insanity among the Passengers. How do they do that? They kidnap people from the train and take them on a very unpleasant ride, during which their victims are treated to life-like visions of their own darkest memories… The Passenger is then returned to the train, but never their previous state of mind..

Green insects move in swarms, searching for a warm and cozy space to begin their reproductive process… That’s why they can enter the train cars! It’s quite inconvenient for the passengers, because these monsters cause the train to become even more crowded and dangerous.

—description from the publisher

A strategic card game with a modular board in the Caribbean sea!

The ship of the famous captain Black Sam has just been sunk on the high seas and the remains of its cargo are floating adrift on the waters of the Caribbean. As a renowned pirate roaming this area, you cannot miss such a boon! Master the fearsome sea currents, foil your enemies traps and make your way to the coveted treasure!

- Play as one of the 4 pirate captains and set sail! Find your way through sea currents to reach a chest or attack an enemy ship.

- Open the chests and find out if you are rich or if you have been set up by Black Sam!

- Manipulate your destiny by crafting a relentless strategy on the high sea to ensure your victory, by being the first to collect 3 gold chests.

Master the treacherous sea currents, outwit your opponents’ traps and set out to claim the coveted treasure and secure victory!

—description from the publisher

Great civilizations built great things.  Most of these great structures took years to build.  Now you can take on the role of these great civilizations and build something great in just a day!

Built in a day is a competitive tile laying game for 1-4 players.  You can play as Greece, Egypt, Babylon, or China as you compete with the other players to build one of ten floor plans.  The end of the game is triggered when one player completes their building but the player with the most uniform building will win.
This pack contains 15 additional missions (76-90) that can be played seamlessly with the B 17 Twenty five missions base game.

Guess the words from strange questions!
A conversational guessing game that can be played with 6 cards!

What kind of power does an apple have? How much does a deserted island cost?
Ask questions to identify the secret word.
When you get the correct answer, Atetango! Win by guessing the most words!

へんな質問から単語を当てよう！
6枚で無限に遊べる会話型推理ゲーム！

りんごはどんな力を持っていますか？ 無人島の値段はいくらですか？
質問をしながら、親だけが知っている秘密の単語を特定しましょう。
正解にたどり着いたらアテタンゴ！ たくさん単語を当てたら勝ちです！

boardgame: じゃんけんノーボーダー (Janken No-Border)
description:
じゃんけんノーボーダー (Janken No-Border) is a fast 2-4 player game where each person simultaneously plays one of the illustrated Rock, Paper, Scissors from their hand. The player with the most wins at the end of 5 rounds is the victor.

The biggest feature of this game: the fact that you cannot tell for sure what card (Rock, Paper, or Scissors) you are playing until it’s too late. There are a total of 16 numbered cards and while Card #1 and Card #16 are clearly defined as Rock and Paper respectively, it’s impossible to say what range is defined as Scissors until the end of the game.

After five rounds of placing out your cards, you use the two remaining cards left in your hand to figure out what range of cards actually count as “Scissors” for each individual player — and therefore how many of the five rounds you actually won.

If you play with 3 players, there will be 3 rounds.

A playful game of psychological warfare that combines funnily illustrated cards with serious strategy as you guess what cards your opponent could possibly have left in his or her hand and play your own hand accordingly.

グーやパーなどの手が描かれたカードを同時に伏せて出し、じゃんけんをする２～４人用ゲームです。じゃんけん勝負を５回行い、勝った回数が多い方のプレイヤーが勝者となります。

このゲームの大きな特徴は、５回の勝負に使うカードをすべて出すまで各カードのグー・チョキ・パーが確定しないことです。最終的にじゃんけん勝負に使わなかったカードによって各カードのグー・チョキ・パーが決まります。
3人戦なら3回の勝負を行います。

人をからかっているかのような変わったカードの図柄と、相手がどんなカードを残しているのかを推理しながら手札を出していく心理戦が特徴的なゲームです。

boardgame: Vs. System 2PCG: Marvel Crossover Vol. 5, Issue 14
description:
In this issue, Crossover Vol. 5 expands on the popular teams within the Marvel Cinematic Universe's Infinity Saga and includes new and reimagined characters. This 55-card set includes Iron Man, Thor, and more starts for the Marvel Cinematic Universe.

55 cards

Vocabulary word game for the whole family.  The object is to have the greatest number of points after a set number of rounds.
The game is composed of 1000 unique and fascinating words.  Each word is printed on one card which is followed by 3 choices for its definition.  The word and its three choices are read aloud to an opposing player who will choose one of the definitions, after the player has made a choice the correct answer is read from the back of the card.  There is also at least one sentence illustrating the proper use of the word.  After all players have had one chance at a word, that completes one round, the game consists of 3-5 rounds.  There are multiple rule variations to play depending on difficulty and and number of players.  Including a solitaire version.

Risk! on the Russian Front!
This is a VERY fast, simple, playable - abstracted - Barbarossa areas game using a very abstract roster of units - infantry vs. armor - in 8 Jun41-Jan42 monthly turns.
10 German 2-dice panzer korps, 24 1-die infantrie korps, 3 1-die Romanian armies and other Axis allies group vs. 12 1-or-2-factor Russian tank korps and 24 1-factor armies. No Finns.
3 battle rounds per battle and depending on the weather with leftover battle rounds, armor can advance farther and possibly attack for for one more round.
No movement factors - terrain difficulty is simulated by just more areas in those regions - and fortress( citi)es are the only terrain affecting combat (other than a 2-unit limit attacking across the Narva River when it is unfrozen.
Airpower not specified - assumed to be implicit in the stronger armor units, although each group of 3 UnDisrupted German infantry korps get an additional "army"/artillery bonus shot/die on both attack and defense
.And as in many of my games, Germans have a Disruption(-recovery), qualitative advantage.
Hidden Russian units are an option to bedevil the Axis player.  Strong, "Historical," or Weak armor arrays can have the Axis player intimidated by weak Russian tank units or reckless at the mercy of un-revealed strong Russian tank korps.

Although areas are just disguised point-to-points, the implications of their configuration can be far easier to overlook and downright sneaky, I've been reminded.
Estimated 90-120 minutes to play.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dust: Moment To Turn The Tide
description:
The Dust is a rapid duel between two players or two teams.
It happens in a manga-styled world. Players choose their weapons and accessories to use in the battle. These items provide different abilities and styles. Many items have two ways to use. Switching the way at the right time can be vital to win the duel. Some items provide powerful one-time use ability but require dice to be consumed to charge it.
Every turn, players manipulate dice to trigger skills and perform attacks on their opponents. Whoever knocks down his or her opponent first is the winner.

In Do Not Disturb Players search for ancient treasures defying the Pharaoh's curse.
Setup: randomly lay 7 cards face down on the table (doors), one for each symbol on the back. The other cards are dealt among players to form their hand. By deduction, players have partial information about the contents of the 'doors' (because the cards in their hand, are not on the table).
Game: It is a cooperative game. Without speaking, on their turn, players can move the 7 cards on the table, either up (STOP) or down (OK), to communicate with other players which doors they would enter and which doors they would not enter. The 'expedition leader' of the round must take responsibility for entering the chosen doors, revealing the contents of the cards, hoping to find maps that will lead them to the treasures and trying to avoid Pharaoh's curses that may stop their search.
End: Players win if they can reach the treasure halls (by winning three consecutive rounds) and find at least one treasure (each map not found, because it was hidden behind doors players decided not to enter, reduces the treasure halls they can reach).

—description from the publisher

In Meteo, players try to find the best weather conditions for last-minute holidays.

To set up, lay out six face-up weather cards (worth +2 to -2), then place one of six color cards at random face down under each weather card. Each player secretly looks at one face-down card.

Shuffle the deck with sky cards in the six colors. Reveal sky cards one by one face up next to one another on the table. When 2-5 sky cards have been revealed, any player can shout "Off I go", collecting all of the cards present as they take off on a vacation. As a reward, they get to peek at another face-down color card. If no one has claimed the cards after five have been revealed, the weekend has arrived, and all of those cards are discarded before a new week begins.

Each player can claim cards at most three times. When all players have done so or the deck is empty, the game ends. Everyone tallies their score after revealing the face-down color cards to determine how much each sky card is worth. Whoever scores the most wins.

Amos: Němčina v kostce is a distinct entry in the Amos game series, focusing on the German language. This particular version involves rolling dice that are marked with letters from the German alphabet, distributed based on their typical frequency of usage. Players aim to form words from the letters displayed after a roll, organizing them either separately or interconnected like a crossword puzzle. Each letter is assigned a point value, and the game's objective is to gather the highest score by creating the most valuable words possible.

Amos: Angličtina v kostce is a distinct entry in the Amos game series, focusing on the English language. This particular version involves rolling dice that are marked with letters from the English alphabet, distributed based on their typical frequency of usage. Players aim to form words from the letters displayed after a roll, organizing them either separately or interconnected like a crossword puzzle. Each letter is assigned a point value, and the game's objective is to gather the highest score by creating the most valuable words possible.

Amos: Slovenčina v kostce is a distinct entry in the Amos game series, focusing on the Slovak language. This particular version involves rolling dice that are marked with letters from the Slovak alphabet, distributed based on their typical frequency of usage. Players aim to form words from the letters displayed after a roll, organizing them either separately or interconnected like a crossword puzzle. Each letter is assigned a point value, and the game's objective is to gather the highest score by creating the most valuable words possible.

Amos: Matematika v kostce is a distinct entry in the Amos game series, focusing on basic mathematics. This particular version involves rolling dice that are marked with digits and basic operations' symbols like addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, lower and greater. Players aim to form equations from the numbers and symbols displayed after a roll. Each correct equation is assigned a point value, and the game's objective is to gather the highest score by solving the most equations possible.

An adventure game: The search for the Crystal Spider!
A Secret Government Organization has managed to bring together the most intrepid adventurers and archaeologists for the most risky mission: the theft of the CRYSTAL SPIDER, the most valuable "stone" of all time, a spider-shaped meteorite fragment that is kept in its interior the Secrets of the Universe.
Those who dare to undertake this adventure will have to infiltrate a lost jungle, full of dangers, beasts, poisonous spiders, traps, fearsome cannibals... and gather the four fragments of the MAGIC AMULET, the key to open the crypt of the TEMPLE OF CRYSTAL, sanctuary where the strange jewel is hidden, guarded by the magic of the Sorcerer, a being abducted by the power of the meteorite.
Once in the crypt, the adventurer must reach into the SPIDER'S NEST and take out the CRYSTAL SPIDER. But beware! You can be bitten by the poisonous spiders that guard the nest.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Un juego de aventuras: ¡La búsqueda de la Araña De Cristal!
Una Organización Gubernamental Secreta ha conseguido reunir a los más intrépidos aventureros y arqueólogos para la misión más arriesgada: el robo de la ARAÑA DE CRISTAL, la más valiosa "piedra" de todos los tiempos, un fragmento de meteorito con forma de araña que guarda en su interior los Secretos del Universo.
Los que se atrevan a emprender esta aventura tendrán que infiltrarse en una jungla perdida, llena de peligros, fieras, arañas venenosas, trampas, temibles caníbales... y reunir los cuatro fragmentos del AMULETO MÁGICO, la llave para abrir la cripta del TEMPLO DE CRISTAL, santuario donde se encuentra escondida la extraña joya custodiada por la magia del Hechicero, un ser abducido por el poder del meteorito.
Una vez en la cripta, el aventurero deberá introducir la mano en el NIDO DE LA ARAÑA y sacar la ARAÑA DE CRISTAL. Pero ¡cuidado! Puedes ser picado por las arañas venenosas que vigilan el nido.

boardgame: Puzzle X Crime Kids: Stolen Time
description:
A mysterious letter arrives at the Scotland Yard post office - a former art robber claims that there are four art objects stolen by him a long time ago in his summer house.

The police know about your intuition and ask you for help...

What can you expect in the house? Are you perhaps on the verge of the recovery of art treasures that were long thought to have been lost and thus facing an investigative success of historical significance?

Puzzle X Crime Kids is a combination of exciting crime and puzzle fun for young detectives aged 9 and up! First, the crime scene is assembled from 264 puzzle pieces, then the puzzlers slip into the role of the investigator. Which objects in the motif should be examined more closely? The numbers on the back of the puzzle pieces lead to important evidence. Is it possible to combine all the clues correctly and get on the trail of the perpetrators? Puzzle meets crime game! The popular game principle, in which you examine a case file with evidence and solve a crime case, is now also available as a Ravensburger children's puzzle - Puzzle X Crime Kids! The highlight: the puzzle is also printed on the back with numbers that provide valuable information on how to solve the case. Then curiosity, combination skills and detective feeling are required. Together with the enclosed playing cards and the evidence material in envelopes, the young puzzlers gradually determine the murder and can find perpetrators, murder motif and murder weapon. Also ideal as joint puzzle and investigation fun with the whole family. No game material has to be destroyed and the puzzle X Crime Kids can be played again.

Contents:
- 264 puzzle pieces with printed back in two different colours
- A suction cup for flipping puzzle pieces
- 72 playing cards with valuable clues
- 8 envelopes with different evidence material

description:
In Bounty Board, your objective is to outscore your fellow hunters by capturing the most valuable monsters before the hunt ends. Each monster requires a specific combination of Tool and Location to be successfully captured, so strategy and timing are key. Along the way, Action Cards can help tip the odds in your favor—or sabotage your rivals’ plans.

Every hunter takes their turn in three distinct phases:

Draw Phase – Draw one Action Card.

Action Phase – Play any number of Action Cards to influence the game.

Hunt Phase – Change your Tool or Location if needed, then attempt to capture a monster.

The game consists of 155 cards, including Monsters, Tools, Locations, and powerful Action Cards. Use them wisely to secure your cache and stake your claim on the Bounty Board.

—description from the publisher

An economic and cultural semi-cooperative game starting with the settlement of Iceland and ending with it gaining independence. Only one player can win, but the players as a whole have to develop Iceland sufficiently to reach the independence threshold, or no one wins. Currently in Kickstarter (July 1- July 30).

—description from the publisher

Illustrated  Tarokk, also known as Palatine Tarokk, is a four player, variable partnership, trick taking tarot game. As with most games in this genre, the base objective of each round is to capture a certain amount of card points (in this case, more than half) in tricks. However, each round of Illustrated Hungarian Tarokk starts with a peculiar round of auctions and announcements that impart a lot of information about the players' hands, which make it a very skillful, challenging, yet surprisingly accessible game.

It uses a shortened pack of 42 cards: the trump suit consists of 22 cards while the other four suits consist of only five cards each. The size of each player’s hand is only nine cards (with a talon of six cards).

Each round starts with a short auction in which players bid for the right to choose a partner that holds a particular high ranking trump card, usually the XX. The bid itself determines the number of cards the winner of the auction may exchange (higher bids mean less cards exchanged) and the base value of game (to capture more than half of the card points in tricks) in points.

Players have restrictions on how they may bid; in particular, a player may only bid only if he or she holds an honour (the lowest trump, the second-highest trump, or the highest trump). In addition, there are bidding conventions that, unlike Bridge, are part of the rules. These conventions, or "invitation bids," indicate if a player holds a particular high ranking trump.

After players exchange cards with the talon, there is a round of announcements. Partnerships can pursue additional objectives other than game:


     Winning three-fourths of the card points
     Winning all the tricks
     Winning all three honours
     Winning all four kings
     Winning the first 5 tricks, the 5th with XX
     Winning the first 6 tricks, the 6th with XXI
     Winning the first 7 tricks, the 7th with Skiz
     Winning the trick before the last with the lowest trump
     Winning the trick before the last with the 2nd lowest trump
     Winning the trick before the last with one specific suit of Kings
     Winning the last trick with the lowest trump
     Winning the last trick with the 2nd lowest trump
     Winning the last trick with one specific suit of Kings
     XXI-catch: Capturing the opponent's second highest trump with the highest trump


During the round of announcements, a partnership may announce that they will accomplish one or more of these objectives, which doubles their value. A partnership may also challenge or kontra an announcement, which also doubles its value. These objectives are scored independently; a team can fail at accomplishing game but still accomplish (and score for) other objectives. For example, the base value of game ranges from 1 to 4 points, sometimes it can be higher with the additional objectives.

boardgame: Blood and Oil: The 1985 Persian Gulf Tanker War
description:
“If they help Saddam Hussein attack our economic resources they will not see oil.” –Ayatollah Khomeini, Supreme Leader of the Islamic Republic of Iran.

January 1985 marked over four years of bloody war between Iraq and Iran. The previous year Saddam Hussein, leader of Iraq, initiated a new campaign in the war with Iran by having his air force target the Iranian Kharg Island oil installations and any shipping caught loading there. Saddam realized that if Iran’s ability to sell oil was successfully brought under attack, that nation’s ability to wage war would be severely impacted. In response, Iran constructed a floating terminal at Sirri Island that was beyond the range of Iraqi warplanes. Iranian tankers ferried oil from Kharg Island to Sirri where the Iranian oil could then be safely off-loaded. What ensued was an escalating war in the Persian Gulf in which Iraqi warplanes targeted any vessel suspected of heading for any of Iran’s ports, and similar efforts by Iran’s warplanes, ships and Pasdaran militia attack boats targeting ships they believed were heading for Iraq and ports in Arab nations sympathetic to their enemy. 1985 promised to be a year in which the violence of the growing “Tanker War” could spread throughout the region and threaten the economies of nations all over the globe.

In Blood and Oil players take on the roles of the leaders of the Iran and Iraq military forces fighting the 1985 Tanker War in a moderate complexity level wargame. Can you do as well or better than your historical counterpart?

—description from the publisher

Monotheism is a solitaire in-hand deck destruction game. Players will help a small cadre of worshipers discover the deities in their local pantheon, and then, through the strategic use of deck manipulation, pattern building, and special abilities, the worshipers must trick these deities into consuming each other until only one god remains.

—description from the designer

Whimsy the chimera is sleeping and if you wake him up, he might be annoyed and bite ! Use items to try and wake up Whimsy safely and get him to bite your opponent until he loses all of his Health Points. Roll several dices to set up the initial state of the game, prepare accordingly and start guessing. Very simple game you can explain and play within five minutes.

—description from the designer

The realm of Westarland is in turmoil. In the wake collapse of the Permon empire, the power vacuum has left nations scrambling to exert local control. Some previously minor kings now aspire to the dragon-skull throne of Westarland.

The King of Avondell is making his bid for the throne and has discovered that powerful magic relics were hidden deep beneath his castle in ages past.  To that end he has set his followers the task of unearthing these relics to help him in his bid for the throne.

Use your knights and wizards to shift, swap, flip, and exchange, trace cards on the board to solve clues and unearth fragments of ancient relics.  Be the first to bring one of these completed relics to your King and win the game.

—description from the publisher

A game based on Jan Brzechwa's book "The Travels of Mr. Kleks" and its film adaptation from 1986. At the beginning, each player must equip themselves with a button because no pawns were added to the game. (In those days, all Mr. Kleks's fans collected buttons, like Mr. Kleks, looking for a button for a magic hat, needed to turn his enchanted friend into a starling back into a human.) Players roll the dice. If the result is even, they move the number rolled. If the player rolls 1, he draws a card from the "surprise" deck, if 3 - from the "obstacle" deck, if 5 - from the "adventure" deck. Each of these decks consists of 10 cards and one whose content you can come up with yourself. The cards describe situations such as: you lose 1 turn, you gain 2 throws, you move 10 spaces, stand 20 fields away from the finish line, return to the start, etc. The player who reaches the finish line first wins.

This Issue (Children of Lilith) will expand on the Underworld team which plays two roles, that of the underworld of crime and the more nefarious super natural underworld. This issue will explore the latter and bring the Darkhold Redeemers, the Nightstalkers, Lilith and the Lillin into the forefront of Vs. System 2PCG action. Images are sourced from the Marvel Comics themselves and feature art from the Midnight Sons storylines.


     Explore the darker side of the Marvel Universe with Children of Lilith.
     Join Morbius, Blade and Johnny Blaze as they take on the forces of Evil.
     Play as Lilith for the first time as a Main Character!
     Expand your Underworld and Defenders teams with 17 new characters!
     Epic competitive gameplay to pit your team of Allies against your opponents


—description from the publisher

2231 AD… Humanity has embarked on a full-fledged space migration plan, and you, as a scientist belonging to one of the four research institutions engaged in a fierce research battle, are involved in the “Human Settlement Planet Design Project”. Your mission is to create the longest-lived planet. The idea is to produce better simulation results than other research institutes at the once-a-month planetary research presentations.

To create a harmonious long-lived planet, you need to venture into space to collect materials, then conduct research to create the refined materials used to build planets. The key to creating a planet with a long life is to create a large amount of high-quality planetary materials.

Now, compete with your rival scientists to create the best planet!

------

What is PLANET LAB? Players repeat [Gather] and [Research] actions to collect space materials that are then researched to recombine into planetary materials. Various types of materials are required to make these "refined materials", but the rarer the material, the longer it will take to bring it back to the laboratory. If you are absent from your laboratory or take a break from carrying materials, a rival researcher might secretly come to use the materials you worked so hard to transport! Once you have finished building your planet, you can simulate the planet using your smartphone! The resulting planets will change depending on the combination of planetary materials used. Who can create the longest living planet? Determine the materials you need, predict your rival's actions, and sometimes sneak into their laboratories to obtain the best planetary materials!

— Description from the publisher (Translated)

PLANET LAB is a simultaneous action selection game in which players draft material cards in an effort to collect the right material sets to convert them into planetary materials. Higher-valued raw materials take longer to return to your laboratory - which can expose you to others using your materials for their own gain! Each round consists of a Morning, Midday, and Night activity. Your action selection takes place in the morning and based on your selected action, your Midday and Night action follow.  The game ends when all the raw materials cards are collected and players assemble their planet simulation using their converted planetary materials.

The game advertises that this last feature (scoring of the planet) is done with a smartphone app; however, the base game can be played entirely without this functionality. If players DO use the application, the extended game play allows material cards to have a variable range of values, resulting in holding the determination of final winner until the very end of the game.  The app reveals the winner by creating a hologram of each planet and then destroying them from last to second place. Only the winner's planet remains.

The New Dangers Expansion features two new modules:

Assist, offering a third decision for players each round.

Events, which affect the gameplay in each round while exploring the temple ruins.

This expansion also includes 8 Explore & 8 Exit cards with updated art to match the New Assist cards.

Additional game play content backers received. Comes in a tuck box separately packaged from the base game.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Outlaws of Thunder Junction
description:
Saddle up for MTG Outlaws of Thunder Junction, the thrilling Wild West showdown you've been waiting for. This Magic set unleashes a stampede of notorious villains from across the Multiverse, converging on this dusty frontier plane for riches untold. Join forces with the cunning outlaw Oko and his gang as they execute a heist that could shake the foundations of the entire Multiverse.

Rumour has it an ancient vault lies hidden in Thunder Junction, overflowing with treasures ripe for the taking. But every outlaw worth their salt wants in on the action, including Oko's estranged son, Kellan. Buckle up for family feuds, double-crossings, and a thrilling Outlaws vs. Outlaws showdown to decide who will claim the ultimate prize.

Outlaws of Thunder Junction delivers a whole new bag of tricks for seasoned Magic players and newcomers alike. Wrangle trusty Mounts for daring escapes, and unleash the full potential of the Plot mechanic to turn the tides in your favour. Draft decks overflowing with desperados, magical artefacts, and maybe even a card or two that will literally let you get away with murder.

Whether you're a hardened veteran or a greenhorn just getting your spurs, Outlaws of Thunder Junction welcomes you to the wildest, most unpredictable ride in the Multiverse. So, what are you waiting for? It's time to break the law, gamble for glory, and make your mark on the history of Magic: The Gathering!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Unlimited – Shadows of the Galaxy
description: Shadows of the Galaxy is the second set of Star Wars: Unlimited. It set takes the focus off the conflict between the Galactic Empire and the Rebel Alliance and puts the Outer Rim on center stage.

Create cities as you explore them, one building at a time, with uniquely detailed inhabitants, ripe with danger and adventure. You can become a ruler, partake in conspiracies, burn heretics, haggle, buy houses, lead revolutions, hunt villain masterminds, marry into nobility, raise children, and much more.

Treacheries of the Troublesome Towns is divided into two tomes:

     Tome 1 covers rules to generate towns.
     Tome 2 collects all the tables and secrets to be discovered.


You need both books to play.
Requires Four Against Darkness and Four Against the Abyss.

Welcome to "collateral" a roll and write adventure print -n-play game.

In this game you will be the leader of a tribe that has been hiding for centuries in the vastness of the prairie. Your mission is to manage the tribe's resources and activities to ensure its survival and prosperity. You will hunt, cut wood, fish and cultivate the land to feed and grow your tribe. But the prairie is not only a place of abundant resources; it is also a world full of mistery and danger.

When you are ready, you can send explorers to venture into the surrounding lands, solving puzzles, fighting enemies and uncovering ancient secrets. Each region hides a mysterious portal.

In the end, you will have to make a crucial decision:
Which portal will you cross to face the Collateral? With six possible bosses, each choice will lead to a unique and enthralling adventure.
Prepare to experience an epic saga of adventure and survival, where every choice counts and every risk can lead to extraordinary rewards.
Collaterals awaits you!

—description from the designer

Pataquada is a fast-paced card game by Airton Braga, in which the players are the leaders of a group of ducks at migration time, trying to get the best positions with each new flight.

Each player has a set of ducks from the same team, with values from 1 to 9 and each with a different special action.
With each new round, a migration leader card is opened on the table, each with a special power that alters one of the basic rules of the game.
Over the course of three turns, the players will choose 3 of their ducks to place in the flight pattern, according to their numerical order. On the fourth turn, each player chooses another of their duck cards, but it will not be added to the flight pattern, only using its special power, which will try to alter the flight pattern in their favor.
If two or more players choose the same card in this phase, none of them take place.
After the flight pattern is complete, points are awarded for the positioning of the ducks that managed to avoid being in the same position as others and that managed to use the bird vacuum further ahead.
The cards furthest from the flight pattern leave the game completely and a new round begins, with some players having fewer cards due to the discarded cards.
At the end of 3 flights, the player with the most points wins.

No one can notice that the museum's most valuable paintings are gone!
Reproduce them with the utmost speed and perfection, because in a few hours the gates will open and the first to save the exhibition from embarrassment will be named the best replicator in the world!

Each player has the same set of hand cards showing abstract patterns.
In the basic game, each round a card showing one of the exhibition's missing paintings is revealed and players must combine their own hand cards to try and reproduce the pattern shown. The first player to succeed receives 1 point and the game ends after any player has scored 5 points.
In the advanced game, each player competes for a different painting, from 3 different difficulties. The easiest are worth fewer points and the most difficult are worth more points. The last player to finish their board, however, receives no points, so choosing a difficult board, which can bring more points, can be risky if you don't finish it in time.
There is also an expert game mode, in which extremely complex challenge cards are added, but with consistently higher rewards.

description:
Princes is a medieval-themed deck builder and the first game developed by German board game influencer Johannes "Hunter" Jaeger.

Die Fürsten ist ein Deckbuilder mit Mittelalter-Thema und das erste selbstentwickelte Spiel von Brettspiel-Influencer Johannes "Hunter" Jaeger.

Prepare to unlock new realms of divine power with this captivating expansion. the pilgrims’ road introduces two powerful new gods and fifteen devoted Followers, offering fresh opportunities for strategic gameplay.

Within this expansion, you will encounter the awe-inspiring gods of dreams and blood, who bring their unique abilities and blessings to the game. These celestial beings will guide you on a path of devotion and enlightenment as you navigate the realms of Altar. But that’s not all! The pilgrims’ road introduces a brand-new gameplay system, allowing you to harness the power of the Followers who embark on pilgrimages to the divine realms.

These devoted individuals, with their unique skills and talents, can be employed strategically to further your objectives and shape the course of your journey.

Are you ready to embark on a celestial pilgrimage and forge your path among the gods? The pilgrims’ road awaits you, with boundless wonders and divine secrets to uncover. Will you answer the call?

Experience the thrilling world of Altar like never before with this groundbreaking expansion.

One must prevail revolutionizes gameplay by introducing a solo mode, allowing you to embark on an epic adventure all on your own.

No longer bound by the constraints of multiplayer, you can now delve into the realms of Altar at your own pace and immerse yourself in a captivating solo experience.

But that’s not all. Alongside the introduction of solo play, One must prevail brings forth a new deity – the enigmatic and unpredictable God of Chaos. This deity’s abilities will test your strategic thinking and adaptability like never before.

Prepare to navigate the twists and turns of chaos itself as you harness its immense power to shape the fate of Altar.

Are you ready to face the challenges of Altar alone and confront the chaos that awaits? Prepare to embark on an unforgettable solo adventure. One must prevail beckons you, with boundless possibilities and the thrill of unruly unpredictability.

Will you find order in the chaos or succumb to its whims? The choice is yours. Will you prove yourself worthy?

—description from the publisher

Step into the role of a Roman noble and engage in a strategic tug of war for influence! Gather your wits, plan your moves, and exert your influence as you vie for the loyalty of the citizens of Rome. Will you emerge as the victor in this thrilling contest for influence in the grand city of Rome?

Furmation of Rome is a quick to play area majority game where players either play cards into their own area to contest for influence tokens or use card effects to disrupt the board.

There are over 20 unique card effects, and new mechanics are introduced into the game for different player counts.

—description from the designer

In this game the each of the two players have a balloon under their clothes simulating a belly. In your turn you usually "eat hamburgers", so the balloon simulating the belly is pumped. If the balloon explodes you lose the game.

In your turn the other players draws a card. Most of them are invitations to eat burgers, but some can have no effects (vegs burgers) or ever reduce your belly (go running).

Each player, in turn spins the spinner and moves any one of the three fish the corresponding number of spaces along the trail. A player may move only one fish per turn, but may move a different fish each time. Players may move in any direction, but in only one direction per turn. Play continues with each player in turn trying to 1) hook a large "keeper" fish on his own line, OR 2) hook a small fish on an opponent's line.

To land on a hook, players must spin the exact number needed. When a fish lands on a colored hook, the player whose fishing pole matches the color of the hook must move the t fish up his pole. He does this by continuing to spin, in turn, moving the fish up the pole. Each knot on the fishing line is counted as one space. He may not move or hook another fish until the fish on his has reached the top knot and is landed. If the fish is a "keeper" the player adds it to his collection. If It is a small fish, it is discarded to the game box.

Immediately after a fish is hooked, another fish of the same size must be placed on START.

HINT: Players use strategy by hooking a small fish on an opponent's fish hook. The opponent must then utilize his turns by moving the worthless fish up his pole.

WINNER

The winner is the first player to land two large "keeper" fish.

—description from the rules

Stargeek Comic Expo is one of the top comic conventions in the country. With over 50,000 attendees converging on the Collins Convention Center, the expo appeals to a wide range of fandoms. With a childlike sense of glee, you temporarily discard your fedora and trench coat in favor of superhero spandex. For this weekend only, you're no longer a Will Street detective. You're just one more geek looking to find an escape from all the death and depravity you see in your line of work. The only crimes you hope to see this weekend will be drawn in four-color panels and accompanied by tag lines like "Zap!" and "Pow!" Yet, you've read way too many comics in your life and know that the hero rarely gets a break.  The criminal element never sleeps. When it’s discovered someone is killing con attendees who are dressed as the popular comic book hero, Knight Detective, you get pulled into the case. Now that a comic book villain has come to life and is loose at Stargeek Comic Expo, is anyone really safe? It’s up to you to find and stop this super villain before more tragedy can unfold.  Who is the Comic Book Killer?  Grab your utility belt, adjust your tights, and find out!

As with all Deadbolt Mystery Society games, you step into the role of the detective. Examining the evidence provided, visiting different locations and using the password protected QR mechanic to uncover the truth.

Players win once they catch the killer, find what's missing or whatever else is needed to solve the case at hand.
A 2- player game with 2 decks of cards.  Play against and try to defeat the Mets!

boardgame: Admiral of the Fleet: Naval Annual 1907-1921
description:
This volume of the AOTF NAVAL ANNUAL Series provides over 500 pre-formatted, printable ship logs.  It covers the many ship classes of the 1907-1921 period for the navies of Argentina, Austria-Hungary, Brazil, Chile, China, Denmark, France, Germany, Great Britain, Greece, Italy, Japan, Netherlands, Norway, Peru, Portugal, Russia, Spain, Sweden, Turkey, and the United States. The ship logs are divided into sections by country, each preceeded by summary sheets listing basic information for each of the ship classes included for that country.

The AOTF Naval Annual Series is an obvious play on Brassey’s & Jane’s but with actual ship logs for playing AOTF. There will be three volumes in the series: 1885-1906, 1907-1921, and 1922-1944.  Covers will be color-coded similar to Conway’s (blue, green and red respectively) and each volume will be comprised of ship logs for each country (alphabetic by country, then ship type, then completion date) preceded by summary pages for each country.  A single ship log for each class (using the data of a representative ship of the class for the time period) is provided and for ship classes spanning volumes (i.e. all the WW1-era battleships and cruisers like HOOD, CAVOUR, PENNSYLVANIA, etc.), updated versions will appear in the subsequent volume.

The first two volumes are limited to battleships, battlecruisers, armored cruisers and light cruisers but the third volume will also contain individual ship logs for classes of destroyers.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Black Card Revoked: Jollof and Fufu
description: West African flavored Black Card Revoked expansion pack covering back home, entertainment, pidgin, history, food & family life.

In Cogs and Carts you and up to three of your friends race to collect diamonds in a maze of rotating interconnected cogs.

All cogs are mechanically linked and turning a single cog will rotate all the cogs on the board - changing the maze in dramatic and meaningful way. At any point in time there are 8 versions of the board that exists simultaneously; they are accessible by simply rotating the cogs.

A game of Cogs and Cart begins with empty board; each turn a player flips over an empty cog to the track side, growing and creating a unique puzzle every time you play. The location of the diamonds also get randomized at the beginning of the game to shake things up.

Cogs and Carts is a culmination of two distinct goals - one is to play a game that make use of a gear or a cog in a meaningful and mechanical way just like one of my favorite Tzolk'in: The Mayan Calender. The other is the desire of wanting to play a more tactical and less luck dependent version of Tsuro; an aesthetically beautiful tile laying game.

The final result being a quick to learn fun little game that takes about 5 minutes to learn and plays in about half an hour. With each turn revealing a new fun puzzle to solve but not so disruptive that you cannot plan your move in advance. Finding clever route that net multiple diamonds per turn feels rewarding, just as snatching the ones your friends are aiming for right from under their noses.

How-To-Play

To setup a game of Cogs and carts flip all the cogs so that the empty side is facing up.

Place all the diamonds inside the bag. Randomly draw the diamonds out one by one and place in around the board in clockwise manner with the numbered side facing up until all 28 spots are occupied.

The number on the diamond indicates how many cogs from the edge of the board to place the diamond when adding it to the board.

Starting from the youngest player and going around clockwise each player choose which of the middle four cogs to place their cart.

The last player then chooses which of the diamonds to start adding the board. Add three diamonds to the board.

The first player is now ready to take their turn!

Each turn a player can do any, all, or none of the followings in this order:

1) Rotate the cogs by 45° any number of times you like.

2) Pick up one of the cog with its empty side facing up. Orient and place is back down with its track side showing. You may not flip a cog that is occupied by one or more cart.

3) Start moving your cart along a path that connects to the cog your cart currently occupies. Your cart will continue following this path until it encounters an unflipped cog, or your cart has fallen off the board.

- If you encounter an unflipped cog - your cart stop there for the turn.

- If you have fallen off the board, on your next turn you must enter the board from the track where you've fallen off.

As your cart travels along the path you will collect the diamonds on the cogs your cart entered. Any time a diamond is collected pause the cart and immediately add a new diamond to the board if one is available.

Remember that your cart picks up diamond when it arrives at a cog - so a diamond added to a cog that already has a cart on it will not be picked up. It is also possible to have multiple diamonds on the same cog.

Once your cart has come to a stop, your turn has ended.

When all diamonds has been collected, the player with the most diamonds is the winner.

If there are ties - the player who started later in the turn order amongst the tied players is the winner.

—description from the designer

If you had the strength not of one, but of all the monsters on Thyera, you could put an end to this nightmare for mankind.
- Nemon, Prime Seeker

This first campaign expansion introduces 3 new monsters, as well as the Nightmare variant rules. It includes all components for the new monsters, 5 new quest (#31-35) that expand the campaign mode, and all the cards you need to play the Nightmare variant.

The new monsters can be used in either Campaign mode, using scenarios provided in the Campaign book specific to this expansion, or in Expedition mode, playing the scenarios featured in this expansion rulebook.

description:
The Galactic Republic's elite Clone Commandos enter Star Wars: Legion in this pack! As the preeminent soldiers of the Grand Army of the Republic, Clone Commandos are deployed to the most dangerous missions and this pack adds a unit of four Clone Commandos to players' collections. Two unit cards allow these miniatures to be deployed as either a standard Support Unit or as the renowed Delta Squad. Additionally, two upgrade cards make the Clone Commandos even more hearty with their signature Katarn Pattern Armor and give them even more tactical flexibility with two configurations for their DC-17m ICWS Blaster Carbines.

A simple push-your-luck game for 2-6 players featuring dice with uneven value distributions.

If you roll two different non-number symbols you bust, otherwise you get to keep going.

First to 150 points wins.

Kanastic is a mythology theme game for 2-4 players where you score points by making melds or golden suits in four progressive stages.

Gameplay Features
– A interactive game for 2-4 players in two partnerships. 
- The game consists of several rounds, usually 3 to 4,in which players take turns clockwise, drawing, melding, and discarding the cards in their hands to score points. 
- The gameplay will keep you focused, constantly engaging your reasoning, memory and intuition in intense psychological moments and extreme overturnings.

Art Features
– Hand-drawn digital artwork.
– 108 playing cards, each featuring a unique mythology – themed illustration.
– Includes all major mythologies including Arabian, Greek, Persian, European, Japanese, Norse, Irish, English, Scandianavian, North American, Jewish, and African.
– 88 „natural” cards divided into 11 ranks, 8 cards forming one puzzle realm: Necromancy fours, Inferno fives, Sea sixes, Lake sevens, Haunted Woods eights, Swamp nines, Desert tens, Ice World Jacks, Magic Forest Queens, Cave Kings, Heaven Aces.
– There are 8 cards in each rank. Every single one of these 88 cards features a different mythical creature.
– The deck also contains: mythical Swords, Shield, Artifacts and Treasure Pot bonus cards.

12 стульев (“12 Chairs”) is based on the novel of the same name by Ilf and Petrov. You have to find a chair in which jewelry is hidden.  The talent to properly manage a small amount, the ability to win bids, the ability to outperform opponents in ingenuity and memory, the willingness to put a spoke in the wheels of competitors, and, of course, a little luck - this is the key to victory in this dynamic and exciting adventure.

Вам предстоит найти стул, в котором спрятаны драгоценности. Талант правильно распоряжаться небольшой суммой, умение выигрывать торги, способность обходить противников в смекалке и памяти, готовность вставлять палки в колеса конкурентам, и, конечно же, немного удачи — вот залог победы в этом динамичном и увлекательном приключении. «12 стульев» принесет много удовольствия людям любого возраста. Игра не требует долгой подготовки и изучения правил. Ее всегда можно взять с собой и поиграть в дороге, на природе, в кафе, институте и других местах.

boardgame: Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game – Weapon X
description:
The 40th expansion in the Legendary line! 4 new heroes, including Weapon X before he was Wolverine of the X-Men! 3 new Masterminds and 2 Villain groups! This set also contains 10 new "Enraging Wounds" Cards. 100 new cards featuring original art, detailing each hero, villain and attack!

Electric Card is a card game that challenges your logical thinking and problem-solving skills by creating electrical circuits. With minimalist cards representing electrical components such as batteries, switches, and bulbs, you must strategically combine them to complete circuits. The game promotes practical learning of electricity fundamentals and fosters critical thinking through real-time challenge solving.

Mich in the Downgeon is a Flip and Write game that combines figure drawing with resource management.

Players must explore the Downgeon, defeat enemies, get loot and use all their items to advance faster than others.

—description from the designer

Green Team Wins is back with Holiday Party, a 105-card expansion that decks the halls, lets it snow, and jingles all the way. Mix (and mingle) these new questions with your other Green Team Wins cards — or tear off the gift paper and play with them right out of the box.

Finally, something fun to do at a holiday party.

A cooperative deck-based game where players are aliens who have crashed on Earth. Our scout craft landed and suffered damage; we must dig through solitaire-rows to find good parts and avoid both junk parts that won't help our ship in the long run, and obstacles which require our special skills to overcome.

Collect all six high-quality parts and then collect your toolkit and summon the Mothership, or else fail and be stranded on earth forever...with all its unfriendly obstacles.

On the distant planet of Praxxis 5, giant mecha called Battle Supremacy Robots wage war over goods and resources.  Mercenary commanders use assorted units to make their way in a world of conflicting mega-corporations.

Big Stompy Robots (BSR) is a fun and easy to play miniatures game that features mixed unit tactics.  It is meant to be accessible but still be full of features.  It is fast playing with games often being less than an hour.

Players cooperate as small house-gnomes, performing charitable favours while the humans sleep. Each task on the 9-tile map merits a reward, but there are dangers lurking, including meanies who will attempt to capture a gnome who is not careful.

All the gnomes must survive until morning, with their tasks completed, to collectively win.

Challenge your friends to Kick Off, the football card game that simulates the actions of a real match. Just like in football, based on actions built to get to the shot, Kick Off will allow you to score goals through combination of cards. The mixing of these two mechanisms, choosing the right cards to play with unpredictable reactions makes for a tense and engaging game. The Deluxe edition provides a multitude of new options, cards, and variant, which provide players with new paths to victory.

Match the cards to shoot, score a goal and get the famous Poker, that is, 4 goals. Each player will be able to choose the best cards to get to the shot, trying not to let the action be intercepted by the opponent. The player who starts the action is the Attacker, while the other is the Defender. The playable offensive cards are Action Cards and Special Cards, and each of them increases the opportunity to play the Shot Card and score a goal. Each offensive card corresponds to one or more defense cards which allow the defender to recover the ball and become the new attacker, as if a counterattack were triggered. To get to the Shot, the Attacker must play 3 cards consecutively without being intercepted by the Defender. To avoid the goal, the Defender can play the Block Card and start the action again as the new Attacker. Kick Off also allows you to combine Action Cards and Special Cards to create lethal actions. Each Special Card allows the Attacker to get to the shot more easily and, in some cases, to bypass the opponent’s ability to intercept cards.

—description from the publisher

This a new twist of the classical word game Scrabble aimed for kids.

In this version your get your letters throwing rings on a board using a flipper. The board has all the letters of the alphabet, so depending on the place where the ring lands you get a letter.

When you make a word, you remove all the rings on the used letters and add them to your "word pool", and will turn into points at the end of the game.

Interstellar Glory is focused on space exploration and combat, where players aim to increase the size of their alliances while defending against hostile races. The rules are structured with a detailed breakdown of various aspects, including set-up, turn sequence, phase actions, victory points, optional rules, advanced rules, card types, and designer's notes. Up to 6 players can play, each with a unique race with a unique benefit. Exploration is key, finding new planets increases your income which allows you to buy ships, and buy weapons for those ships. Ships help you explore and look for new planets, weapons help you protect those planets and the ships looking for them. The rules are organized into different sections, such as Core Rules, Optional Rules, and Advanced Rules, making it easy for players to find specific information. Additionally, the rules provide detailed explanations and examples, ensuring that players have a clear understanding of how to play the game. Overall, the rules are well-structured and provide a solid foundation for gameplay.

Attackers! is the definitive fighting game. Play your cards right, perform unstoppable combos and take on all your opponents to be the last player standing.

Discover its three game modes and enjoy the most fun and dynamic games. Up to 9 players to enjoy with family and friends.

In Attackers there are 6 different characters printed on the cards, each of them has a different ability, in your turn, you can play as many cards as you want or can, you can even combine cards to make even more powerful abilities.

The main objective of Attackers is to survive, and be the last player standing. Try to eliminate your opponents while keeping your lives safe.

Do you dare to face the craziest flies in the world?

In Breaking News, each player is a journalist trying to find the most interesting news that happens in the South Tyrolean region to report on the Salto portal. The players will play cards to explore elements of the region, and when they match the symbols of a goal card in sequence, an interesting fact will be found. To score the points, you must report breaking news involving all the elements in the respective cards. The player who could report the more interesting news is the winner!

—description from the designer

Fill your week of dungeon creatures, scoring the most points in the process. Each creature has unique rules of play, with traps and spells cancelling scores and removing creatures, as well as lots of interaction between creature types.  A short take-that strategic game.  One deck is needed per two people playing.

—description from the designer

Necklace is a drawless connection game for two players: Red and Blue. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored red; the left and right edges are colored blue.

A crosscut is a 2×2 area containing two diagonally adjacent red stones and two diagonally adjacent blue stones. A point is secluded if there is no free path along the lines of the board between that point and the edge of the board.

Red plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty point. After your placement, there must be no crosscuts and no secluded empty points on the board. Passing is not allowed, but, if you have no legal moves available, your turn is skipped.

You win if there is a chain of orthogonally connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

To make the game fair, Blue will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Red instead of making a regular move.

—description from the designer

DIMOI games are designed to foster conversation among players, providing the perfect opportunity to discover, rediscover, or simply share memories with your loved ones.

The Spicy version is a spin-off of the Couples edition, offering intimate challenges to be undertaken as a couple.

DIMOI Spicy Couple Edition is the ideal couples' game to explore new horizons and continue to thrive and reinvent yourselves!

Share your desires during a game created to spice up your couple life!

Whether you've been together for a few months or for years, DIMOI Spicy Edition is the perfect game to keep the spark alive in your relationship and enjoy quality time together to reconnect and break the routine!

In this dungeon crawler you take on the role of two adventurers looking for the tomb of the fabled robot god Teknor.

You’ll enter an underground maze of rooms roamed by ancient dangerous machines. You will fight them with meaty dice combat and rad power moves. Slaying them will yield you loot which you can bring and upgradable gear . Or you could also delve deeper and rack up your loot multiplier. But beware: if you die you lose all loot you have not brought back.

Start with a Paycheck, Manage Daily Bills, Invest Smartly, from Startup to Business Empire. ----A Game Changer, not only in the Game.

How to Play
setting up the game start by putting the game board in the middle of the table
Shuffle the event and Shop cards and put them in their spots on the board 
every player picks an Eye of Providence token and places it at the start space 
each player gets $500 paycheck and then picks five event cards but keeps them a secret by placing them face down
during the game you can choose a car and a medical insurance card but once you choose you can't change them 
having a car allows a player the option to roll one die or two dice on their turn

own and use a venture
if you land on an unowned Venture you can pay the designated amount to Own It by taking its Venture Card 
next time you land on your Venture you can pay the same amount to upgrade
by adding an upgrade token if you land on someone else's Venture you have to pay the bill based on its level you can sell your Venture on your turn to the bank at the net asset price or at the negotiated price to another player 
use a card if you land on a event card space take the top card from the event card pile 
if you land on the card shop space pay $100 and take the top card from the card shop pile but remember you can only use one card per turn and you have to use it before you roll the dice 
if you get a recurring card keep it out so you remember to pay or gain every time you pass or land on start you get $500 but also have to pay any recurring costs and add any recurring income use cards go to the bottom of their pile except for recurring cards which stay in front of you

helicopter
if you land on helicopter space or use a helicopter card you can go to any space on the board landing on start by helicopter gets you a paycheck

Hospital
landing on the hospital space means paying a $2,000 bill but if you have insurance it costs less stop sign token use a stop sign card to place a stop token on any space the first player who pass or land there has to stop at the space upgrade token 
you can use upgrade and downgrade cards on any venture to add an upgrade token or remove an upgrade token a venture could upgrade up to level five

—description from the designer

Dragons love two things.

Sleeping on huge piles of treasure and eating delicious treats... Especially cupcakes!

Frosted. Chocolate. Cupcakes.

As the mayor of your village, you are duty-bound to provide your citizens protection. And as it happens, the absolute best way to secure that protection is to use cupcakes and loot to entice a dragon to come live in your village.

BUT REMEMBER:
Dragons eat quite a lot and your treasure better be piled high for maximum comfort!

—description from the publisher

An easy to play, competitive road building table top card game suitable for families and fun for everyone.

Each player tries to build a road from one end of the playing area to the other. The deck is made up of a variety of road cards that must connect, and action cards which have different effects.

The game is combination of chance and strategy and suitable for all ages.

—description from the designer

In Train Trek, players experience travel by placing landscape cards each turn. The tracks accumulate into a path for plenty of scoring options. Enjoy this relaxing and carefree game with friends and family, or play it solo. Train Trek is all about that travel vibe and can be enjoyed at home, abroad, at the pub, a restaurant, even on a train!

Players lay a card then decide how to best score it. Should it cover up part of another card, be placed adjacent, maybe turn it upside down to continue a really good track, or even tuck part of it underneath another card. The challenge with finding the perfect placement depend a lot more on what your longer goals may be.

Most scoring options require the connection of two Stations, but each of the four score sheets has a different track scoring. Connect two of the preferred landscape and you’re on your way to using your special power to move any one card that doesn’t have any other card on top of it. This tricky bit of strategy is going to keep your opponents guessing as you place it in just the right spot for those extra big points!
Let’s take a ride on this Train Trek!

Each turn the player will play 1 card from their hand or the face up card in the offer. The placed card can be played partly under another card or two, partly over a card or two, or even next to other cards. Players are encouraged to continue the track, but this is not necessary. 
 After the card is placed, the player will assess the path they formed with the tracks on the card to another card or more cards that continue the path. In the event of two stations being connected on this path, the player will be able to score in any area of the player sheet as long as they meet the criteria. Or they can ignore the stations and aim for the path to connect multiple landscapes of the same type. Doing so will start to fill a track which allows the player to move a "top" card. Top cards are cards that do not have another card over them. The player can move a Top card to another spot on the landscape of cards to create another scoring opportunity for themselves.  
 Players will find plenty of strategy with placing cards and then moving cards to create those scoring paths. There are cards for multiple player counts. In solo play the player will use 18 cards, in two player there are 27 cards that are used giving each player 13 turns. In 3 player there are 36 cards and for4 players 45 cards. No two cards are the same allowing for plenty of variation. Players may which to use the starting card and then shuffle the rest of the cards to form a different deck depending on their player count.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Pete the Cat: I Love My White Shoes Game
description:
Pete the Cat goes walking down the street wearing his brand-new white shoes. Along the way, Pete steps in piles of strawberries, blueberries, and other big messes! Does Pete cry? Goodness, no! Pete keeps walking along, singing his song while strumming his guitar. Help Pete change his favorite white shoes to new colors. Be the first player to collect each color token to win!

Scatter all color tokens across Pete’s neighborhood in the gameboard. Give each player their own Pete board. Pass Pete’s guitar to the youngest player to begin play.

On their turn, players push the button on the guitar to determine which color token to collect from the gameboard. Then turn Pete's color wheel board to match the collected token. This wheel will change the color of Pete's shoes. Keep playing until one player has collected each color token.

—description from the publihser

The Founders’ Pack contains 14 promotional cards for the Splintered Lands deckbuilding game. This pack consists of new, unique characters, and alternative art for base game cards.

Many of the characters featured in this pack were created using the likeness of one of our backers and were designed with their input and support!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Three Battles for the Jacobite Rebellion
description:
A short set of miniatures rules for refighting the battles of the 1745-5 Jacobite Rebellion, published in Wargames Illustrated 134. 
These rules are developments of the author's previous ruleset for the mid C18 but with period focus on the '45, so feature modifiers specific to the campaign, e.g. the effect of the 'Highland' charge. These rules are basing and figure size agnostic, although the author uses small units of 8 x 15mm figures for foot regiments. 
The rules included 3 scenarios: Prestonpans, Falkirk and Culloden. None of the scenarios is particularly big. The first two scenarios favour the Jacobites, the latter the Government forces.
This expansion contains 40 cards. They are shuffled in the main deck at the begging of the game. New warriors and new type of tactic cards have different and more complex effects during battles.

boardgame: The British Way: Enemy of My Enemy
description:
The British Way: Enemy of My Enemy is an expansion for The British Way, including two entirely new games on “prequel” conflicts using the Malaya and Palestine maps, and also expanding the base game experience with new variants for all four conflicts originally covered.

The British Way: Japanese Occupation puts the British and the Malayan Communist Party (MCP) on the same side in their efforts to resist the Japanese occupation of Malaya during World War II.

The British Way: Arab Revolt allows players to explore the brutal suppression of the Arab Revolt (1936-1939) that preceded the Jewish insurgency covered by the original Palestine game.

The two new games involve entirely new mechanics to capture the unique features of each conflict and can each be linked to the original Palestine and Malaya games, which also allows them to feed into the “End of Empire” Campaign Scenario. The two new games in the expansion allow players to explore how British attempts to combat earlier threats to colonial rule involved collaborating with local armed groups that they would later end up fighting against (Jewish paramilitary groups in Palestine and the MCP in Malaya), ironically strengthening and preparing these groups for two of the conflicts featured in the The British Way.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ASL Action Pack #19: Roads to Rangoon
description:
ROADS TO RANGOON, the 19th “action pack” for Advanced Squad Leader, follows up ACTION PACK #9 TO THE BRIDGE! with more action in Burma between Japanese (and allied Burmese Independence Army troops) and British Commonwealth forces in March and April 1942. ROADS TO RANGOON presents several “tournament-sized” offerings featuring Japanese infantry forces, occasionally supplemented with light armor and guns, against Commonwealth infantry (including units of the Gurkha Rifles) supported by Stuart tanks of 7th Armoured Brigade the famed “Desert Rats,” fresh from the North African theater. The Chinese even make an appearance in one scenario, all of which were designed by Gary Fortenberry.
Three double-sided 11˝ × 16˝ maps, painted by Charlie Kibler, round out the package. Each board is completely geomorphic and compatible with previous ASL mapboards.

Action Pack #19: ROADS TO RANGOON contains:

three 11˝ × 16˝ double-sided geomorphic mapboards (20a/b, 21a/b, 22a/b)
    10 ASL scenarios:
        AP205 Kick in the Teeth – 5½ turns, 3 March 1942, Kyaikhla, Burma (Japanese vs British)
        AP206 Jungle Rats – 6½ turns, 4 March 1942, Payagi, Burma (Japanese vs British)

boardgame: Tåg till Rydsgård: andra upplagan (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)

Tåg till Rydsgård is a fan-made expansion for the Days of Wonder board game Ticket to Ride. It is a partially almost entirely correct reproduction of existing, or previously existing, train lines in the southern-most part of Sweden (Scania and its closest neighbours).

This second edition ("andra upplagan") have added components and gameplay, most notably the addition of a second map "Øresund Runt".

List of changes from the first edition:

description:
Almost everyone has experienced the irritating feeling of waiting in a never-ending amusement park line. Coaster Cutters scraps the traditional first-come, first-serve line advancement system. All riders battle to solve riddles and dodge obstacles to ride the rollercoaster first.

Riders advance through the line by solving riddles. 2 teams face off against one another. Both teams alternate solving a riddle per turn in 1 minute. If a team answers the riddle correctly, they can advance farther with the ability to roll two dice. If the team answers incorrectly, they advance at a slower pace by rolling only one of the dice. Either team is subject to encounter a setback by landing on an obstacle!

Gather your crew for a thrilling adventure as you navigate through challenging obstacles in an effort to ride the rollercoaster before the other riders. Test your thinking skills along with luck and see who will emerge victorious in this fun-filled game.

—description from the designer

Hidden Agendas is a 3-6 player game all about political shenanigans which parodies the US Congressional system. In Hidden Agendas players take on the role of a political operative, each with their own secret Agenda. Play as the outspoken Activist, the cunning Ambassador, the hot-headed General, the slick Lobbyist, the seasoned Party Leader or the conniving Opposition Party. By creating, debating and voting on Bills, players compete to see their Agenda achieve victory by the end of the game with careful strategy, political maneuvering and a healthy helping of bribery, blackmail and deceit. 
Gameplay is a mix of strategy and party-game elements with players having to make calculated decisions about which Legislation cards to play when creating Bills so as not to give away their true intentions. During the debate stage of the game, players will argue for which Bills to Kill and which to Pass into law. Either by enticing rhetoric, or a well-placed bribe of sweet Taxpayer Dollars, players will try to Pass enough Legislation matching their Agenda to win them the game. 
 —description from the publisher

This game has the theme of 4 adventurers competing to climb a mountain, where the players try to reach the finish line at the 64th box. Each player spins the roulette to determine the number of moves, with positive numbers making them go forward and negative numbers making them go back. There are obstacles that must be overcome, such as ravines and mud, while creeping plants can provide benefits. Players will also get items that are useful for harming other players or benefiting themselves. The first player to reach the finish line will be the winner, with the game ending when 3 players have reached the finish.

Permainan ini bertema 4 petualang yang berlomba untuk mendaki suatu pegunungan, di mana para pemain berusaha mencapai garis finish di kotak ke-64. Setiap pemain memutar roulette untuk menentukan jumlah langkah, dengan angka positif membuat mereka maju dan angka negatif membuat mereka mundur. Terdapat rintangan yang harus dilewati seperti jurang dan lumpur menghadang, sementara tanaman menjalar dapat memberikan keuntungan. Pemain juga akan mendapatkan item yang berguna untuk merugikan pemain lain atau menguntungkan diri mereka sendiri. Pemain pertama yang mencapai garis finish akan menjadi pemenang, dengan permainan berakhir ketika 3 pemain sudah mencapai finish.

boardgame: Frozen II: Path to the North
description: A collaborative game where players have to work together to create a path of leaf cards without using their hands. Instead, players must try to direct the cards into place by blowing each card individually. The path is completed when the leaf cards connect with the start and finish cards and all three character cards are included in the path. If the players succeed in doing this before running out of action cards, they win the game together.

Hockey Hardware is a tabletop game made for hockey fans, gamers, and fantasy hockey GMs, but anyone can play, have fun, and win. No hockey knowledge or experience is needed.  This game is socially interactive, competitive, and unpredictable. No two games are the same, and no lead is safe. Players must use a combination of luck and strategy to win.

At the start of the game, take on the role of a hockey player. You will start as either a Forward on Line 2 or as a Defense on Pair 2 in either the East or West Conference.

On your turn, collect as many hockey stats as you can by rolling dice, avoiding Adversity, and strategically playing Game Cards. Game Cards reflect different hockey actions that can help you get ahead or push your opponents back.

The more hockey stats you collect, the more likely you will earn different Trophy Cards. Players compete for 11 Trophy Cards throughout the game, which consists of 15 rounds divided into 3 periods and 2 intermissions in a Game Schedule.

The player with the most Trophy Cards by the end of the Third Period earns the Champion's Cup and wins the game!

—description from the publisher

In this game, you have a limited time to find a card from a deck of cards with ingredients and characteristics that matches the dish presented to you, and ask your friends to guess which dish it is.
You must manage to tell your friends which dish you are looking for even though the ingredients you want may have been taken by other players or you can't find them.
Be tactful and express your dish!
