description:
Dungeon Pen unfolds in the mythical world of Scythius—a medieval fantasy realm created by Compagnie du Yucca for our live-action role-playing games (LARPs).

Step into the roles of daring adventurers or nefarious underworld scum, all cast into the dreadful dungeons of the infamous Ursula, necromancer of the endless mountains.

For too long, you've languished in a cramped cell within a vast, damp hall. On the rare occasions you're allowed out, you've managed to gather a small treasure, which you guard meticulously beside your bed.

Mostly, your days are filled with nothing but the taunts of fellow prisoners and the sneering laughter of the jailers.

Then, on one dark and indistinguishable night, when the jailers have neglected to bring food, the gates of two cells swing open. Your heart pounds with adrenaline—not to escape, as any sane person might, but driven by all those insults endured, to snatch your rival’s treasure.

Gameplay Overview
Players find themselves on opposite ends of the Dungeon-Prison map. The objective is simple: navigate through the dungeon, seize the opponent's treasure, and return to your cell first to claim victory.

Each turn, maneuver your character with a strategic pencil flick, then draw an event card that needs immediate resolution. The dungeon throws challenges at every turn, from monsters and magical barriers to perplexing walls, testing every player's resolve.

As you navigate the map, any element you encounter (be it debris or treasure) can be collected:

     Debris: Draw from a deck filled with various equipment and advantageous items that could tilt the scales in your favor.
     Treasure: Capture your opponent’s treasure and make a swift return to your cell.


But tread carefully—the dungeon is fraught with peril, your adversary is unyielding, and your life points are precious. Every move could be your last. Navigate wisely to survive and triumph.

—description from the publisher

Imagine you can control the forces of a noble family, guild, or religious order on a barren planet which is the only source for the most valuable substance in the known universe.

Imagine you can rewrite the script for one of the most famous science fiction books of all time. Welcome to the acclaimed 40-year-old board game which allows you to recreate the incredible world of Frank Herbert’s DUNE.

In DUNE you will become the leader of one of six great factions. Each wishes to control the most valuable resource in the universe - melange, the mysterious spice only found at great cost on the planet DUNE. As Duke Leto Atreides says “All fades before melange. A handful of spice will buy a home on Tupile. It cannot be manufactured, it must be mined on Arrakis. It is unique and it has true geriatric properties.” And without melange space travel would be impossible. Only by ingesting the addictive drug can the Guild Steersman continue to experience visions of the future, enabling them to plot a safe path through hyperspace.

Who will control DUNE? Become one of the characters and their forces from the book and . . . You decide!

Skull of Freedom is Pirate/Navy themed in the 17th Century era. There's six faction to choose from. 3 of them are pirates factions and 3 of them are navy factions. Each player chooses each factions they want to play. The faction has no effect at the game mechanics in current version. Each player have 6 cards and dices to roll either to attack or to repair their ship. This action is determined by the cards they put out and if they do nothing, they pick one card. The last ship standing wins.

—description from the designer
Lure: Deep Waters adds 15 new, harder-to-catch fish and a new d4 die per player to roll more precisely.

boardgame: Traveller Customizable Card Game: Type HT Bounty Hunter Mini Ship Pack
description:
Sleek, maneuverable and deadly, the Type HT is equally suited to crime or constabulary.  But it’s as a bounty hunter that the ship truly shines.  Whether motivated by justice or credits, these intrepid captains scour known space for their mark.  And they rarely fall short.

—description from the publisher
Includes 30 new cards! 2 new classes, 8 new sword pieces (2 full new swords), 6 monsters, 5 tavern cards, and 9 quest cards: 3 action, 3 gear, and 3 curses.

boardgame: City of Blood: The Battle of Khorramshahr, September-October, 1980
description:
“Iran has bared its hostile designs against the Arab nation and its pan-Arab revolutionary movement” –Saddam Hussein, April 1980.

When the conflict between Iraq and Iran erupted into full scale war with Iraq’s invasion in late September 1980, the control of the Shatt al-Arab was a major goal for Saddam Hussein’s forces. Key to an Iraqi victory was capturing the crucial Iranian port and oil refinery center at Abadan just a few miles east of the city of Khorramshahr. Due to the earlier clashes along the border, that included artillery and air strikes as well as commando raids, much of the Khorramshahr’s populace fled earlier, and those that remained were being hastily evacuated by the Iranian government once full-scale war broke out. The Iraqis believed they would find the city abandoned or only lightly defended, and gave orders for their forces deployed further north to strike deep into Iran to secure their objectives and win a quick victory. However, the Ayatollah declared that the forces of the “godless Baathists” were to be resisted everywhere, and so units of the Iranian Army, including elements of the newly formed Pasdaran and Basiji militia, were rushed to defend Khorramshahr. What ensued was one of the most protracted and bloody city fights since the end of the Second World War.

Can you do as well or better than your historical counterparts?

Combat units are mostly battalions or their equivalent sized battle groups. Each day turn of the game represents 4 days of time with each turn’s Operations Phase representing two days.  Each hex is approximately 100 meters across.

—description from the publisher

Midnight Hero enables couples to find greater pleasure by using competition to reconcile different preferences and re-introduce novelty.  You will take a journey with your companion to learn what makes them excited and what they shy away from.  This game is not for the timid, because the real-life outcome of your evening is shaped through competition rather than the standard formula.

While this game cannot solve all barriers to intimacy (health issues, sleepless nights with kids, or wider relationship issues  as examples), it is designed to help break down common barriers to sexual intimacy in healthy relationships.  Some of those common barriers are:

Initiation of sex can be an awkward song and dance
Switching from everyday life to being intimate can be a difficult transition
Trying new things can be intimidating, and for some it is hard to even know what to do
Sexual preferences often differ and those differences are frequently ignored rather than explored.

The game is played in 3 phases

In phase 1 you play a game of would you rather.

Step 1:  Deal 10 cards to each player from the foundation building deck

Step 2:  Player 1 reads 2 cards at a time from their deck to player 2 (tip do not show cards while reading)

Step 3:  Player 2 selects their preferred card and the remaining card goes to player 1.  The cards should be distributed face down.

Step 4:  Player 1 repeats steps 2-3 until all cards from their hand are distributed.

Step 5:  Player 2 now follows the same steps 2-4

Step 6:  Both players turn their cards face up and organize by color

Phase 2 you’re playing to get to 9 points first.

The board needs to be set up with the bonus cards up top while the scoring cards are in a pile with 5 cards turned over just below that.  The moonlight tokens are stacked below the scoring cards and 2 blocking tokens are given to both players.

The player who has the next birthday starts.  If this isn’t your first time playing, the loser from the previous round starts.

A player has 2 options for their turn.

Option 1:  Select 3 moonlight tokens (or 2 of the same color if there are 4 or more of the same color available)

Option 2:  purchase a scoring card

The Scoring cards and foundation cards subsidize future purchases by discounting the purchase price by the number of cards you have.
 When a scoring card is purchased the point value purchased is in the upper right hand corner.  The first player to 9 points wins the phase.  
 Players may also use their blocking token during their turn.  This does not count as their turn, however it removes the scoring card from the line up.

If a wild card is drawn the scoring cards that have not been purchased need to be reshuffled.

Bonus cards contain no point value and are earned when a player obtains the minimum of scoring cards displayed on the bonus card.  The bonus card holder may be different than the Midnight Hero (winner of phase 2) however the Midnight Hero collects any remaining bonus cards at the end of phase 2.  These do not require an extra turn to obtain but do require to be picked up during the turn of the player securing the bonus card.

Phase 3 is all about rewards.

The Midnight Hero gets to select one foundation card and 3 points worth of consequences from the scoring cards.  The bonus cards are performed before the foundation and scoring cards are performed.

—description from the designer

In Amberlight, take the role of a brave hearted Apprentice in the world of Nous; gathering a retinue of friends and followers as you seek adventure in procedurally generated hexcrawls in 3 dimensions!

With a focus on hexcrawl exploration, roguelike adventures are supported by light deckbuilding and crafting/resource management with over 400 illustrated cards that allow for significant replayability and different means of approaching each scenario.

Modular hexcrawl rules allow for solo, cooperative, competitive and casual play across individual scenarios and fully fledged campaigns.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Flesh and Blood: Welcome to Rathe
description:
After centuries of uneasy peace, the land of Rathe balances on the brink of war once again. Savage beasts emerge from the primordial jungle, clashing with noble warriors in increasingly violent skirmishes. A forgotten realm finds its ancient barriers eroding, exposing it to the greed of others, while a mystical land is faced with the history it once tried to bury, threatening to expose the secrets of their ancestors.

In a world plagued by conflict, you must fight for the chance to determine your fate. Will you succumb to the machinations of others, or will you choose your own path? Pick up your sword and prepare for battle; for no matter where you run, war will find you.

—description from the publisher

Synopsis: Lucha Libre, the spectacular combat sport from Mexico, combines traditional wrestling with unique cultural elements. Characterized by masked wrestlers (Luchadores), acrobatic movements, and strong symbolism, it reflects Mexican heritage and identity, drawing fans from around the world for its dynamism and flamboyant style.

In this game, you will experience a fight between four Lucha Libre celebrities: on one side El Santo and El Cavernario, and on the other side Blue Demon and El Bulldog! You will see that everything plays out as much in the audience as it does in the ring!

Gameplay: Simultaneously roll as many dice as your luchador's energy allows, and try to combine them to execute the best moves, which will earn you Fans and drain energy from both you and your opponent.

If you're not satisfied with your dice results, don't worry, spend 1 Fan to reroll as many dice as you wish... but be careful not to lose their support.

And remember, switch your wrestler at the right time, when they are rested enough to change the course of the game!

—description from the publisher

In Trash or Cash, players take on the role of scavengers in an underground world. Each turn presents a massive garbage puzzle, and players must decide whether to accept the potentially space-consuming puzzle piece. Declining incurs a compensation fee, while accepting grants the full compensation. Players must strategically manage their space, earning additional compensation for collecting pieces of the same series.

Various module cards are also provided to add more challenges for players, ensuring each game offers a unique experience. Balancing the accumulation of garbage with financial sustainability becomes crucial as players navigate this intricate underworld.

boardgame: Fates of Madness: An Adventure Card Game, 2nd Edition
description:
Fates of Madness is a single-player or cooperative role-playing adventure card game. Take control of a party of adventurers out to destroy the evil lurking in the wilderness. The party's skill and resilience will be tested by Scenarios and Monsters, and their determination will be rewarded with potions and treasure. Fate cards determine success or failure; Madness enters as the journey continues.

This horror-themed adventure uses art from Legendary Games' Gothic Horror Compendium.

For a better play experience, the revised Second Edition includes an Adventure Guidebook with updated Game Rules, glossary, tutorial, hints, and 7 replayable branching adventure modules written by game designer Jimmy Sanders. It's like having a game master in the box!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Harry Potter Miniatures Adventure Game: The Chamber Of Secrets Chronicle Box
description:
The first in our new range of Chronicle Expansions for the Harry Potter Miniatures Adventure Game, the Chamber of Secrets explores the cinematic saga like never before! Progress through a series of scenarios, and immerse yourself in the adventure.

The Chamber of Secrets: Chronicle Expansion includes new miniatures, rules, gameboards, cards and counters that not only introduce the new Chronicle Game Mode, but also expand and enhance your normal games.

—description from the publisher

Force of Virtue is a fast paced skirmish wargame set in Rome during the rise of the Borgias. Grow and shape your warband as you lead them in daring raids across the ruined landscape of the Eternal City.

Use cards to choose your troops equipment, training strategies and lay hidden traps for your opponent. Command your troops with the virtues of prudence, audacity, speed and fortitude and grow your warband as you triumph.

—description from the publisher

description:
In the contemporary world, mental development and problem-solving abilities are increasingly recognized as crucial skills. Consequently, a newly introduced game named "Viplex" serves to facilitate players in enhancing their cognitive development in an engaging manner. The primary objective of the "Viplex" game is to utilize the available numerical resources to both endeavor to obtain the highest possible numerical value on one's own board and thwart the opponent's pursuit of the same objective.

Gameplay:

The game consists of two 6x6 boards, with 28 green, 28 white, and 18 yellow pieces featuring numbers 1 to 9 and 2 X's. The game is divided into two parts, green and white pieces. Each player has 3 pieces corresponding to their color and 1 yellow piece of each number. White and green pieces can only be played on your own board, while yellow pieces can be played on both the opponent's and your own board. In the two-player game of Viplex, the objective is to achieve the highest possible number by using the numbers in hand, horizontally, vertically, and diagonally from left to right and top to bottom. "X" tiles are considered wildcards in the game and are the most important tiles. "X" tiles are placed on the opponent's board and have the ability to cancel all movements vertically, horizontally, and diagonally from top to bottom and left to right where they are placed, and each player has only one of them. Our game can also be played with a sack. In this case, numbers are randomly selected from the sack. Thus, the bags add a factor of chance to the game and test the players' strategic thinking skills.

boardgame: Hogwarts After Dark: The Werewolf Threat
description:
In a parallel universe to Harry Potter's wizarding world, Voldemort's power has been defeated, but a new threat is emerging - the Werewolf species under the leadership of Fenrir Greyback. At Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry, a dark time is approaching as students and teachers begin to transform into werewolves in the shadows.

Ordinary citizens, werewolves, and wizards must use all their skills and intelligence to triumph in the game of life and death. Trust, suspicion, and cooperation will be decisive factors in the battle against the dark power of the Werewolf species.

—description from the publisher

Gamelib Pocket is a pocket-sized board game library. Easy to carry, it is ideal for playing on the move, during trips or on holidays. With generic and minimal components, Gamelib Pocket is made of 100 game elements (54 cards, 40 wooden cubes, 2 wooden meeples, 4 dice), allowing you to play a multitude of different board games, both traditional and original.

Mindbug: Tag Team is an add-on pack for Mindbug introducing the ultimate 2vs2 experience. This add-on introduces 2vs2-specific cards to create combos with your teammate. It offers a fast-paced gameplay with only 3 cards in your hand and 3 cards in your deck.

Here is how it works:

     Mix the Tag Team 2vs2 pack with any other Mindbug set(s).
     Team up with a partner and share 3 life as a team.
     Each player gets 6 cards: 3 in their hand and 3 in their deck.
     Equip yourself with 1 Mindbug and 1 Mindfrog. Use your Mindfrog wisely to seize control of one of your teammate's creatures at a crucial moment for a tactical edge.


In Mindbug, you summon hybrid creatures and send them to battle against your opponents. But be careful: your opponents may use one of their Mindbugs to take control of it.

Lonely Ice is a solo game in which 60 colored pyramids of three different sizes are distributed randomly across the playing board, which consists of 20 cards placed at 90-degree angles to one another. The goal is to move pyramids across the cards such that each card contains exactly three pyramids of the same color. Movement is restricted via two factors: the size of the pyramids and the angle of the playing card on which they sit. In order for the board state to change, a pyramid on one card must "attack" (trade places with) a pyramid on a neighboring card. This swapping of places will only be successful if the pyramid initiating the attack is at least as large as the pyramid that it's attacking. In addition, attacks can only occur from the short sides of the card toward the long sides of the card.

There is also an advanced version of the game in which 25 playing cards are used to create the board, and one extra color's worth of pyramids is used (meaning 25*3=75 pyramids for the advanced game.)

There are also two Extra Challenges. In the first, the win condition is increased to having each row only allowed to contain one color; and the win condition is further increased to requiring exactly one pyramid of each size on each card (with the row restriction still in place).

A trivia games in which players have to decipher words and phrases from various dialects of the German speaking regions.

Cards come in four categories with a different task each: translate a word (multiple choice), find the right answer to a question (multiple choice), find the picture showing an item, and translate a sentence. All players answer the questions simultaneously, a correct answer yields one point. The player with most points after ten cards is the winner.

Lost in Translation is a cooperative game in which players interpret the meaning of unusual expressions from 35 languages. These are expressions that mean something entirely different from the sum of their parts. English examples (not included in the game) would be "to spill the beans" or "to beat around the bush".

The game consists of cards with one side showing the expression in the original language (and a transcription when non-Roman script is used) and a literal translation into German. The other side shows the actual meaning of the expression. One player draws seven cards from the deck, shuffles them under the table and reads a meaning, then shuffles the cards again and spreads out the cards with the literal translations on the table. The group now has to guess the phrase with that meaning, then flip that card. If correct, the group gains a point. If not, they will flip another card, etc. After flipping ten cards, the game ends and the group tallies their points - the more, the better.

There is also a competitive mode in which players read a literal translation and then write down explanations. A vote on the funniest/most original/most creative answer yields a point; the player with most points after 10 cards is the winner.

In Swanchyboy Studios Pirates players take on the roles of sailors during the Golden Age of Piracy. Through a series of turns, players will build their crew, acquire ships, trade goods, and complete missions, all in an effort to gain the most victory points by game’s end.

In this immersive world, players will encounter four powerful nations, The Spanish, The English, The French, and The Dutch—vie for dominance, each with its own ambitions and strategies. As you set sail, remember, the seas are not just home to merchants and adventurers but also to ruthless pirates who will stop at nothing to claim their bounty.

Each nation starts with NPC merchant ships laden with riches, tempting targets for daring pirates. These merchant ships sail the seas, carrying valuable cargo waiting to be plundered by those brave enough to attack.

The pirate NPC is a constant threat, lurking on the horizon, ready to pounce on unsuspecting ships. While they chase everyone, their primary targets are those who choose not to engage in combat, making them easy prey for the ruthless pirates.

Provoking the ire of a nation comes with consequences. Once a player attacks a ship of a particular nation, that nation will unleash its naval might. Naval ships from that nation will relentlessly pursue the aggressor, seeking retribution for their attacked brethren.

As a captain, you are not alone in this quest. You can recruit sailors at different ports, each with their own skills and abilities.

Combat between ships and crew is resolved by dice roll; the amount of dice will depend on the specific abilities of the ship or crew.

Ships also use dice roll for movement and the amount of dice will depend on the particular ship.  You have several options at ports as what ship to purchase and use in your adventure.

Your goal is simple: to become the most famous captain on the high seas. But achieving this goal will require more than just skill and cunning. You must be prepared to face challenges from all sides, whether it be battling rival ships, delivering precious cargo to distant ports, or searching for hidden treasure beneath the waves.

Navigate the treacherous waters, recruit skilled sailors, engage in epic battles with other ships, and embark on daring quests for gold and riches, Each player is a sailor looking to be the most famous in the Caribbean.

—description from the designer

A party game in which players need to recognize various books.

RULES

     The cards should be placed about the room, so that they can easily be seen. They must not be handled.
     Competitors are provided with a Scoring Card for writing down their solutions.
     The winner is the one returning the most correct card. In case two or more return correct cards the winner is the one who makes the first return.
     As the cards are handed in they should be numbered.

Fight off all usurping chipmunks and be the last chipmunk standing and win your new territory.

Chipmunks are extremely territorial - aggressively so.

These loners fight any invading usurper and chase them from their surrounding land. Each chipmunk has their own territory mapped out where they live, eat, patrol, and fiercely defend on a daily basis.

Your chipmunk has discovered a new territory, perfect for a homestead of its own - but what’s this? Other chipmunks are eyeing this territory too!

Now’s your chance!

The game is afoot. Not a word, grab your acorns and come.

Get in the game! It’s time to intimidate your opponents, send them fleeing in fear, and show the rest of these chipmunks what it means to be ... Territorial.

—description from the publisher

Collect wood to build different kinds of furniture. But watch out for woodworms. The first one to build all furniture wins.

On your turn your either build furniture with previously collected wood. Each type of furniture requires a different amound and kind of wood.

Or you go collect wood from the forest. The forest is represented by the face down cards spread over the table<

You flip over and collect cards until you decide to stop or flip over a special card.

When you flip a special card you handle the action of the sepcial card.

If you pull the worm, your wood has woodworm! you must discard all your collected wood and it is the next player's turn.
With poison you can neutralize the woodworms and you will not lose your wood.
Trucks allow you to steal someone else's cards. And the forest ranger causes you to discard down your collected wood.
Based on a famous and popular French TV show, the players compete as teams and the purpose of the game is to answer correctly to a series of questions about the show.

Slanguage, the party game of SLANG WORDS! Test your knowledge and be creative by guessing meanings of common slang words used over the last century. This unconventional party game creates tons of laughter when players make their best to convince the judge that they KNOW the right answer!

The judge rolls the die for one of the categories to play. They read the Slang and players will write their answer on their erasable sheet and add the decade they think the slang dates from. Once everyone wrote their answers, the judge mixes the cards and read all the answers and chooses which one they prefer, giving the slang card and 3 points to the player. The winning player reads the definition and the decade and players score if they got the decade right. Players play 5 to 10 cards and the player with the highest score at the end wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Falcons: The US Air Force Academy Board Game
description:
Welcome to the United States Air Force Academy! From now on, you are a Cadet! You will be tested physically and mentally, competing against other players to graduate at the top of your class. Be prepared to march, study, train, and compete head-to-head against other cadets. Join Doolie Training Sessions, walk Tours, and prepare your Capstone Project so you can score the most points and graduate as the Academy's Top Grad.

Falcons is a family-friendly board game where each player is a cadet at the United States Air Force Academy. Using a variable turn order, players march their cadets around the Academy’s Terrazzo to train and compete in a variety of academic, physical, and military competitions. Competitions involve simultaneous card selection and a variety of tactical action cards. Cadet time is always limited, so acquired cards are in short supply. Cadets must also develop their character by earning hidden character tokens. At the end of the game, character tokens are revealed, and the player with the least character is expelled before final scoring.

Falcons was designed by real academy graduates to be an authentic depiction of Air Force Cadet life. Prior Air Force knowledge is not required to play. Players may use the included companion glossary to learn more about the Air Force Academy's activities, slang, and traditions.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Sherlock Holmes: Mystères à Sorrowdale Manor
description:
A macabre evening at Sorrowdale Manor! Lord Horace Fairchild, the tea and coffee magnate, has just committed suicide. A few minutes later, his son-in-law is found poisoned. And finally his prestigious diamond - the Celestial Moon - mysteriously disappears. Are these events linked or completely independent of each other?

Play as Detective Holmes or Dr. Watson, tracking down clues, interviewing witnesses and using your deductive skills. Make the right choices to solve these three investigations, because you are the hero!

Sherlock Holmes: Mystères à Sorrowdale Manor is a comic combined with a game, one title in a series subtitled "The comic in which you are the hero". You embody a character in a immersive story in which your choices guide your progress. You have a character sheet akin to those used in role-playing games that tracks your possessions, your special abilities, your coins, and your victory points. In general, gameplay consists in following the comic's panels — which contain clues, riddles and traps — and making choices about where to go and what to do, while using your visual perception to collect clues and be smart enough to resolve the riddles. As in any game, you can lose — sometimes really badly!

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Age of Brigade (ボードゲーム)
description:
Age of Brigade is a card game modeled after the epic medieval militarized Europe.

Mercia was unified for the first time in 200 years by Ethelbert, the man of the generation, and just as the people's hearts stabilized, he suddenly disappeared during a hunting trip.

The king is dead! Who killed him? Rumors spread, and kings and feudal lords from all over the country rose up in the name of vengeance, and in an instant, the world of military forces returned.

You are one of the "pretenders" who have proposed to become the next High King. Tame more than 60 corps and units gathered near the capital and survive repeated battles large and small.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
publishers: Southern Cross Games (サザンクロスゲームズ)

description:
Swamp Monsters TCG is a satirical and strategic trading card game that humorously parodies contemporary political and cultural ideologies. Players select from six pre-established factions, each embodying exaggerated caricatures of real-world political personas: Est-Left, Est-Right, MIGA, Libertarian, Leftist, and Alt-Right. Set in a swampy political landscape, players choose a Champion, deploy Heroes, Operatives, Traps, and more in their swamp to strategically engage opponents. The game’s turn-based structure involves resource management, card deployment, and attacks, with the objective of diminishing opponents’ Champion Political Power (PP). “Swamp Monsters TCG” offers players a unique blend of strategic decision-making and humorous satire, inviting them to navigate a hilarious political arena while strategizing to achieve victory.

In a multi-dimensional universe a rift has opened between the Earth and Mars. From the other side of the Rift, humans of Earth are being manipulated by beings from the planet Devora. Devora has been at war for hundreds of years, and a few beings have learned powerful abilities that allow them to absorb energy from the rift between our world and theirs to gain amazing new powers. This metaphysical energy eminates from humans under great distress, and Devorans have learned they can increase this energy by telepathically connecting to humans to cause chaos on Earth.

This game is a 2-6 player deck building strategy card game, where players compete to be the "last man standing" in a turn base trading card game. To begin, players place a pre-selected Champion card this card is the leader of their deck. After turn order is determined, players take turn moving through the 5 phases of their turn(Draw,Resource,Deployment,Action, and End Turn). Players aim is to deploy Trap, Utility, Event,and Capitol cards to gain a tactical advantage. Persona cards (Heroes and Operatives) are used to defend their Champion and attack enemy Champions. This TCG utilized a three resource system (morale, influence, and crumbling fiat currency) to pay for deploying Persona cards, using abilities, or amplifying card actions. In a world twisted by chaos fight for control to regain balance for all mankind.

—description from the publisher

In SENH4 (code), players take on the role of hackers competing to be the first to unlock one of the codes in a digital matrix. Using colored code blocks, each player tries to recreate password patterns that are revealed and hidden in the matrix every moment to win the game.

The Game in Three Steps:

     Implant Secret Code Blocks in the Matrix: Start the duel of minds by positioning your blocks strategically, preparing the ground for cyber combat.
     Reprogram the Structures to Reveal SENH4 Patterns: Maneuver the code blocks with precision, changing the digital configuration to discover the secrets of the Matrix.
     Replicate SENH4 with Your Code Blocks and Definitely Control the Matrix!: Master the digital power game, using your cunning to replicate SENH4 and claim hacker supremacy.


—description from the designer (translated)


Em SENH4, os jogadores assumem o papel de hackers competindo para ser o primeiro a desvendar um dos códigos de uma matriz digital. Utilizando blocos de código coloridos, cada jogador tenta recriar padrões de senhas que se revelam e se ocultam a cada instante na matriz para ganhar a partida.

O Jogo em Três Passos:

     Implante Blocos de Código Secretos na Matriz: Inicie o duelo de mentes posicionando seus blocos estrategicamente, preparando o terreno para um combate cibernético.
     Reprograme as Estruturas para Revelar Padrões de SENH4: Manobre os blocos de código com precisão, alterando a configuração digital para descobrir os segredos da Matriz.
     Replique a SENH4 com Seus Blocos de Códigos e Controle Definitivamente a Matriz!: Domine o jogo de poder digital, utilizando sua astúcia para replicar a SENH4 e reivindicar a supremacia hacker.


—description from the designer (Portuguese)

Discover the exciting Misión Antártida expansion with the new Inclemencia cards! Immerse yourself in an even more challenging world full of unexpected events with these two new additions that promise to take the fun and uncertainty to the next level.

In Misión Antártida: Expansión Inclemencias, you will face extreme weather conditions that will test your strategy and your ability to adapt to the unexpected. From unforeseen blizzards to sudden avalanches, these cards add an element of surprise to every game, keeping the excitement and tension going throughout.

Are you ready to face the challenges that Antarctica has in store for you? With "Inclemencias", the excitement has never been so intense! Dare to explore this new territory and discover how far your skills will take you in "Mission Antarctica: Inclemency". Get ready for an even more exciting gaming experience full of unexpected twists!

Hexed combines the intuitive placement strategy of Dominoes, the tactical maneuvering of Chinese Checkers and the thematic allure of Rune Magic into a unique board game experience.

Designed specially for 2 players, it unfolds on an ever evolving board, shaped by double hexagonal tiles bearing the marks of six elemental runes: Fire, Earth, Air, Water, Soul, and Reality.

The objective is simple: Get one of your Power Nodes to an opponent's Foundation Tiles before they do the same.

Yet, the path to victory is anything but straightforward. With a dance of adaptability, tactical foresight and a smidge of luck, Hexed is a test of both your strategy and your ability to pivot with the whims of Fate.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: La Casa de Papel: Escape Game 3
description:
The Ultimate Heist!
Third part of the Escape Room type game from the Netflix series La Casa de Papel. The big end! After robbing the Spanish banknote printer, steal the gold reserves of the National Bank of Spain and liberate Rio. Again the teacher will guide you to escape from the Bank of Spain with 90 Tons of Gold.

The goal of Smocza jama is to reach the castle and, along the way, sneak past the pit of a voracious dragon. Monsters (on the board in large yellow squares) help the dragon capture the player. However, mysterious creatures help the player by giving him "lucky stars" (fields with a star), which allow him to summon a knight who will defeat the dragon. Dwarves (fields with dwarves) also help by giving items (sword, bow, invisibility hat) or revealing a spell that helps defeat monsters.
Players take turns rolling the dice and moving their pieces by the number of points on the dice. When a player stands on a square with a star, he or she receives a card with a star. When the player stands on the field with the dwarf, he receives a magic item on a cardboard in the color of the dwarf's hat. When a player stands on a space with a creature, he draws a card with that creature. It then moves to the circular track in the center of the board. Now he moves on it. When he stands on a green space, he is saved by a sleeping knight and goes to the space of the color indicated by the arrow on the creature card. When he stands on the red square, he is eaten by the dragon and returns to the start. If a player who stands on a space with a creature has a star, he gives it up and moves to the track with stars, crosses it, is then saved by a knight and moves to the space in the color indicated by the arrow on the card with the creature. If a player who stands on a field with a creature has a magical item, he defeats it, puts the item in the box and continues.

Munchkin is a satirical card game based on the clichés and oddities of Dungeons & Dragons and other role-playing games. Each player starts at level 1 and the winner is the first player to reach level 10. Players can acquire familiar D&D style character classes during the game which determine to some extent the cards they can play.

There are two types of cards: treasure and encounters. Each turn the current players "kicks down the door", drawing an encounter card from the deck. Usually this will involve battling a monster. Monsters have their own levels, and players must try to overcome it using the levels, weapons, and powers they have acquired during the game — or run away. Other players can chose to help the player or hinder by adding extra monsters to the encounter. Defeating a monster will usually result in drawing treasure cards and acquiring levels. Being defeated by a monster results in "bad stuff" which usually involves losing levels and treasure.

Munchkin Big Box contains the Munchkin base game, six expansions that were previously released on their own, and fifty new cards — more than 650 in all. Also included are a game board, a side board, standees, Kill-O-Meters, dice, other components required for play, and some stuff that's just for looks.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Cooperative and strategy game set in a post-apocalyptic future in the Crimean peninsula and Eastern Europe. It is the year 2225 and only 5 factions of warring people remain and their objective is to take their faction of allies to the sanctuary city of Sevastopol, avoiding various threats; attacks from enemy factions or forest creatures, while obtaining the necessary resources to continue their journey on the unexplored paths of the territory.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Bad Egg: Three's a Full Nest, Four's a Crowd
description:
A Card Matching, Dice Rolling, Point Salad Game
w/Press your Luck & “Take That” elements.

In a world of culinary eggs, you need full nests with the largest eggs for the most return for your labors. Don’t crowd your nests or you will downgrade their value and miss out on extra revenue. Filling special orders in a timely manner will gain you additional earnings. Make sure to avoid or discard any bad eggs or you may lose ground in building your egg empire. Fortified eggs have added effects that may assist or hinder you in hatching your plan for success. In an eggshell, you must roll with your chosen strategy in pursuit of an eggcellent income and take a chance, whisking it all in hopes of turning the largest profit in the egg industry.

Object: Have the most points at the end of the game.
Roll and place dice; competing to gather Nest Eggs and Fortified Eggs. Gather 3 of the same egg type and you have a Full Nest. More than 3 and your nest is crowded. Fortified Eggs may be good or bad depending on your situation in the game. Individual Egg Timer objectives will give you extra points based on the eggs you gather and the full nests you have. Avoid Bad Eggs or you may lose points.

Includes "Single Layer" Solo Play

—description from the designer

Whoever says Big Foot isnʼt real hasnʼt played Myth Fits — the family strategy game where mythical (wink, wink) creatures unite in the forest while evading roving photographers. Use special skills to slip through the trees, run fast or even fly across the tiles you play on each turn. Be the first to build a path to connect your creatures and you win the game. If you canʼt connect your creatures, work stealthily to collect photos of your opponentsʼ creatures as an alternate way to win. Will your sneaky moves bring you victory?

—description from the publisher

WordWeaver Adventures is a family friendly, co-op word building game where adventurers travel to beautifully depicted regions around the world. In each of these regions, they will be challenged by teachers to channel their energy through spelling words. Meet the goal and move to the next challenge with the teacher's praise. Fail and the teacher will make things more difficult. WordWeaver Adventures is highly modular and difficulty of play can be scaled to accommodate a wide variety of play groups and styles. A playthrough can be a breezy 15 minutes or a strategic 60+ minutes. Adventure awaits!

Theme: This game is set in the 18th century Philippines, Vietnam and Japan with animals from each region. The animals are either indigenous to or can be found in those regions. Whenever they are dressed, they are dressed in period specific attire. For example, the Court Musician is a tiger dressed in an 18th century Vietnamese dress (ao dai) and playing a Vietnamese instrument.

How to play: To play the game, players select a region with a corresponding Head Teacher and Assistant Teachers. Players will select an Adventurer board and draw seven tiles to put on their board.

Players will flip over one Assistant Teacher card at a time and be faced with a "Challenge Value" (CV) from that teacher. Each player will spell a word. Once all players have spelled a word, the point values are added up across all players and compared to the CV. If the total is greater then or equal to the CV, then the players will redraw back up to 7 tiles while placing all used tiles back in the tile bag. The next player will then flip over the next Assistant Teacher.

If the players do not meet the CV, the Teacher will "crack a tile slot" of the current player. All players must return all tiles back to the tile bag and then redraw. If a player has a "cracked tile slot" they draw one less tile for each cracked tile slot. For instance, if the current player has one cracked tile slot, they can only redraw 6 tiles instead of seven.

The next player then flips the next card and play continues until all Assistant Teachers have been challenged. The Head Teacher features a much larger challenge and multiple rounds to meet that challenge.

—description from the designer

Sangram is a Hindi word, often used to refer to India's 'struggle' for freedom against colonisers. This game gives players a taste of that struggle through simple mechanics, in just 30 minutes of game time. The aim is to build the strongest army out of all competing kingdoms. But beware....as history has shown us, infighting between the kingdoms makes it easy for foreigners to enter in the name of trade and slowly take over the entire subcontinent.

Players start with 3 warrior cards, each having a 'strength' number which they contribute to the kingdom's army. In each round of the game, first, players send one warrior each to battle, activating the special power of that card. Then, in the order of motivation of those cards, players can recruit 1 extra warrior from the market of volunteer warriors. Unfortunately, these recruited warriors attract some coloniser tokens. So the choice of recruited warriors depends on multiple factors - the coloniser count, motivation, strength and the special power of that card which could be useful later. Finally, in the training phase, all players gain 1 Army strength. However, if players have more than 4 coloniser tokens, they are weakened from within and don't gain that extra 1 Strength in that round.

This mechanic creates a constant tussle between choosing to activate warriors which attack other kingdoms, or warriors which keep the Coloniser tokens from piling up. This was a dilemma that rajas often faced at that time in India. Ultimately the winner is one who finds the fine balance between both paths. The first player to reach an Army Strength of 18, through smart recruitment, attacking opponents and consistent training, becomes all-powerful - the ruler of the entire subcontinent of Bharata!

—description from the publisher

Chappal (means Slippers) vs Cockroach is a speed-based light strategy party game for 2-10 players. It takes just 7 minutes to play, and people of all ages and languages are welcome to play it.

Players start with Slipper cards numbered 2 to 8. Open a cockroach card from the central deck, and the player who hits it FASTEST with a slipper of the same or higher number wins points! Keep adding points till the deck runs out and the player with the highest total points wins!

But there's a twist - On the back side of all Slipper cards are Night Slippers. So when a Night Cockroach opens up, you must flip your Slipper card and hit it using the Night Slipper side. And if a poop card shows up....well, don't hit it at all!

Choose to use your most powerful slippers as soon as you see ANY cockroach to get a guaranteed win, or save them for the big ones. Or maybe hold out till the other players run out of slippers? This game has more ways to win than one would think.

So are you ready for this fast-paced, colourful, cute, hilarious, slipper slapping fun game?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Terraforming Mars: City Parks Promo Card
description:
Promo card X71 given with issue 2/24 of Spielbox Magazine.

7M€ automated (green) project card requires that you have played 3 cities, but grants you with Plant tag and two Plant resources as well as 2 endgame VPs.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
UNO FLIP! flips classic UNO game play with twists like a double-sided deck, special FLIP card and tougher penalties that add a competitive edge…and with a theme inspired by the supernatural TV thriller Stranger Things, fans will really flip over this edition! There is a "Real World" side to the deck, which plays like the classic UNO game, and an "Upside Down" side where penalties are super-tough. Play the FLIP card, and all cards in the player's hand, the draw pile and draw deck are flipped over to reveal an entirely new set of numbers and colors on the opposite side. The holder of the FLIP card determines which side to play -- and when cards are switched back around. The "Upside Down" side of the deck includes action cards that really shake things up, like Draw Five and Skip Everyone. Strategize to rattle the competition and be the first to get rid of all cards to win. When players are down to one card, they still can't forget to shout "UNO!" This fun family card game is perfect for 2 to 10 players ages 7 years old and up, especially fans of the show. Colors and decorations may vary.

Inspired by the hit supernatural thriller, UNO Flip! Stranger Things adds twists to classic gameplay with elements that offer a competitive edge!

The collectible edition features a double-sided deck and special FLIP card designed with characters and images from the show!

Play the special FLIP card and all cards are turned over, including the draw pile, to reveal an entirely new set of numbers and colors on the opposite side!
The 'Real World' side of the deck plays like the classic UNO game; the 'Upside Down' side includes penalties that are super-tough, like Draw Five and Skip Everyone cards!

The first to get rid of all their cards wins, but only if they don't forget to shout 'UNO!'

—description from the publisher

Space is Time is Money is Power again.

Plutocracy: Passengers expands Plutocracy by introducing a captivating solo mode as well as the addition of passenger transport to multiplayer gameplay.
In the multiplayer expansion, new actions allow players to pick up passengers and fly them to their destination. When dropping them off, players can choose among a display of favors. These offer new surprising opportunities leading to even fiercer competition and introducing some randomness in a former deterministic universe.
In the new solo mode you are trying to maintain the passenger transport by carefully selecting the passengers you transport and the routes you are taking before time is running out. Ten planet constellations are waiting for you and only you can tell if Mission Impossible turns out to be really impossible.

—description from the publisher

Love cribbage? Love Yahtzee? Then Cribbage Dice was made for you.

Never heard of Cribbage but love Yahtzee? Add a fun twist and breathe new life into the beloved classic.

Got a group of more than four people that all want to play cribbage? Cribbage Dice can accommodate as many people as you can crowd around the table.

The goal of the game is simple: to roll the best cribbage hands you can and be the first player to reach 90 points.

—description from the designer
family: Versions & Editions: Dice Versions of Non-Dice Games

description:
In the heart of Copenhagen, you will find Tivoli. The beautiful amusement park is one of the oldest in the world. Inside the magical gardens, trees, flowers, and hundreds of thousands of colored lights welcome you to a world of wonder, exciting rides, balloons, and delicious treats.

Tivoli is a family-friendly board game with enough depth to keep adults engaged. On your turn, you roll 3 custom dice and use the results to move around the gardens, go on the different rides, stroll the quaint and artful paths, and acquire treats and toys from the card row to score points.

When the big clock strikes twelve, the gardens close, and you score extra points for having collected card sets as well as for your placement in 5 different races. E.g. who has tried the most ‘daredevil’ rides? The player with the most points wins.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Twilight Struggle: The Cold War Movies
description: One sheet of optional 007 movie playing cards, complementing the article "the Movies of the Cold War" in C3i magazine #37. 8 new cards titled after James Bond movies and featuring new events.

boardgame: Star Trek Ascendancy: The Final Frontier
description:
Star Trek Ascendancy: The Final Frontier is the ultimate collection of the most thematic 4x space game in the universe.

Star Trek Ascendancy: The Final Frontier is a big box collection of every Star Trek Ascendancy expansion ever made as well as:


    3 brand new civilizations: The Gorn, The Tholians and The Terran Empire. 
    2 brand new ways to play with:



        Campaign Mode, featuring unique Scenarios offering endless replayability.
        Non-Player Civilizations, allowing you to play solo or add even more players to your odyssey across the stars.



    An enhanced rulebook.

Welcome to the world of Messy! There is complete chaos in your house, garage and garden. Everything is lying where it shouldn't be - toys are in the kitchen, sheet music is in the bathroom, and someone has scattered keys and cards all over the house. Will you be able to restore order while spinning fascinating stories? Take up the challenge!

Everyone will find something for themselves in the "Bałagany" game, we have prepared 4 variants of great fun for you. In each variant, we play searching for various objects or animals in the house, garden and garage. You can play the cooperative, competitive or storytelling variant. Additionally, you can come up with your own rules and gameplay variants. You are only limited by your imagination!

Cho-Han Trick-Taking Game is a game based on Dinghan Tori-tei, a Japanese gambling game that was a combination of Ding-han gambling (which was played with dice) and Toriso-tei , and quickly took over Japan. However, as the people started gambling and stopped working, Nobunaga Oda felt a sense of crisis and issued a ban on Chohantori-tei, causing it to disappear.

Now revisited in Cho-Han Trick-Taking Game, the game was able to be revived in modern times, as it was only played for a short period of time and very little documentation of the original game remains.

GigaWatt – Energy Revolution! A fast and easy version of the original, that lets you race to transition the energy system against other players in just 45 minutes. Reach out to us to make this part of your company end-of-year gift.

Each turn in this game you decide how far you want to push your luck, while you prepare for the other players’ moves, and build up to a climactic ending of the game. The cards in your hand let you build wind turbines, upgrade the power grid, install batteries for flexibility or close down a coal plant.

—description from the designer

The German Version of this Promo card was given with issue 2/24 of Spielbox Magazine

Effect: If you lose this fight, move your units in an adjacent instead of removing them,

You are throwing a taco party, but you're broke. Luckily, the taco trucks in town are giving away free tacos, if you can get to them without your opponents getting in the way.

Players simultaneously choose which taco truck to go for.  If they are alone, they get all the truck's tacos and side dishes.  If two or more players run into each other, all the food gets wasted and discarded.

Which truck players want to go to will change as they try to build the perfectly balanced taco party.  When the game ends, if players have more tacos than side dishes, they have to discard the excess tacos before they count their score.'

—description from the publisher

TIMBER! is a unique stacking game that brings out both your competitive and creative sides! Simply combine the green “Leaves” and see how big the tree will grow!

Be careful though, as the tree grows taller it becomes more and more unstable. One wrong move and the whole thing comes crashing down!

TIMBER!

—description from the designer

Strategy card game of matching symbols to targets to complete challenges. Each player has a agent with a set of symbols and a special ability. Each agent ability allows the player to break the basic game rules in some way. Completing challenge cards allows you to upgrade your agent to improve their ability to match more symbols on future turns. Captured cards also count towards your final score.

—description from the designer

Each player becomes a chef, where they must demonstrate skill, dexterity, and speed to win. 
Throughout all the rounds, all chefs will roll their Ingredient Dice trying to complete Order Cards in their preparation area.  They can re-roll the dice as many times as necessary until completing an order. Once the dice are placed on the Order Card, they must quickly ring the Bell.

—description from the publisher

This box set contains:

An impressive full-color model, for you to construct from 16 A4 sheets of cards.
Two exclusive new Warzone miniatures - the Capitol Covert Operations Specialist and the Imperial Ordinance Officer.
A 32-page booklet with comprehensive new rules for fighting in-and-around buildings and structures, and for fighting underground.
New templates and counters.
A scenario set around the Imperial Bridgehead and exciting background text to bring the whole thing to life.

Players have to follow the stacking pattern and stack blocks on a rocking board trying to keep the balance. As the tower gets taller, each move becomes more challenging.  Working together you build the tallest towers or use strategy to force your opponents into making a mistake.

There are different ways to play, competitive and collaborative creating varied experiences.  The game rules ensure the stability of the blocks and encourage upward growth.  The game offers different ways to play, collaborative and competitive styles. The different game styles unlock a completely new feel to the game.

Building from a clear board means spending less time setting up after a game ends to play again. The block's color dictates where you can place it.  These move choices and options make the gameplay more interesting; picking a color is like rolling a die.

​Gameplay can be fast-paced or slow and strategic.  You choose how you want to play.

—description from the designer

Players cooperatively roll the die and flip cards to try and assemble a full train attaching cars behind the steam locomotive - however there are derailment and breakdown obstacles that must be overcome.

A perfect score is 12, but all games end with some measure of success.

The Greyhound Races is an Australian war years game. It is a table top race game where 4 metal greyhound dogs are pulled along by cord attached to a winder. The Greyhounds move by sliding along the table whilst being pulled. The first to the winning line is the winner.

Fabled: Eve of Times presents three unique Modules and two advanced Scenario sets. Each Module and Scenario uses a number of special components.

The Magnificent Allies, Obstacles and Guides, and Borderlands Modules may be added to the game in any combination and are compatible both with each other and with the core game of Fabled.

Edge of Worlds and Divine Intervention are advanced Scenario sets intended for the competitive play and for the Challenge respectively. Also, the Legendary level of Difficulty is introduced in the Divine Intervention Scenario set.

Oh So Fluffy is a game of fluff and fuss. It can be played by 2-5 players.

The objective of Oh So Fluffy is to build collections of table cards and while doing so also preventing the opponents from doing exactly the same thing by being mean, persuasive or just by pure luck.

A turn in Oh So Fluffy usually consists of three phases. Cards however, sometimes have the power to add both complete extra turns and other fluffy things to mess with this nice order. Usually all this is pretty straight forward and explained in a cute way on the cards themselves.

First player to achieve a collection with 5 table cards of the same kind wins.
Yes, it’s as easy as that!

—description from the designer

On the streets of Orcwood two bitter rivals end their feud in the only way they know how, a fight to the death. This two-player, fast-paced, sword fighting game has you slash, dodge, parry and thrust your way to victory.

Both players play their move at the same time and suffer the consequences accordingly, if both players are still alive then continue to the next round. Strategy and cunning will see you to victory in this epic duel.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Close the Case: Der Fall Hildenberg
description: As a team of investigators, the players are contacted to solve a cold case, a possible murder, that is considered hopeless by the police.

boardgame: Dead By Daylight: The Board Game – Malicious Expansion
description:
Enhance your Dead By Daylight: The Board Game experience with this expansion! Play as one of the new Survivors and utilize their unique perks to escape from a new cabal of terrifying Killers with horrifying abilities you've never seen before!

Play as 4 new resourceful Survivors with their own unique perks!
Play as 3 new Killers with horrifying powers that defy logic to hunt your prey!
Explore 2 new twisted realms within The Fog - Forsaken Boneyard and Withered Isle.
Bring the game to life with 10 hauntingly detailed miniature figurines!

—description from the publisher

The pettiness and greed of warring woodland factions has decimated their once-thriving farmlands. Cold, hungry, and ranks depleted from the harsh winter, their leaders reluctantly agree to an armistice. Exchanging weapons for farming tools, each faction strives to make amends with their ancestors by cultivating the war-torn soil before the next frost.

Over three seasons, players compete to establish their faction's dominance by drafting tiles and tokens to plant, grow, and harvest during the day while sabotaging their opponents from the shadows of the night. Using their workers, resources, and the help of supporters, they must overcome catastrophes to reap the benefits of each unique season and restore glory to their faction.

—description from the publisher

Dinosaurs roam the earth again in the latest twist on Ecologies!
⠀
This new game take things back to bygone eras such as the Cambrian ocean, Cretaceous forest, and the Pleistocene Tundra.  You will manage food webs not only across biomes but also across time periods!
⠀
It’s all about building healthy food webs on different biomes. If you create a healthy ecology you get the perks and powers of that Biome. Each organism card lists what it eats and what it’s eaten by. You arrange them with plants on the bottom, predators on top. There is a trade mechanic where you trade with others based on what you need and what they need. It is a race to get 12 victory points and different organisms give different points. There are factor cards that can affect opponents and different strategies such as focusing on one biome, going for perks and bonuses, or attacking other ecologies.
⠀
—description from the publisher

The French & Indian War is a two-player wargame of the conflict between the British and French Empires for control of North America during the wider Seven Years War. There are two opposing players: British Empire (Britain or British) and French Empire (France or French). Each player represents the high command for their respective forces in North America. There is also the possibility for intervention by American Indian and Spanish Imperial forces. The game has several scenarios, representing different starting situations. The game system models the effects of command control on campaigns conducted across vast distances of largely frontier terrain, and as part of the larger Seven Years War.

Each game turn represents a campaign lasting one year. Each hex represents 85 kilometers (50 miles) across. Each regular unit represents one or two battalions or equivalent-sized groupings of companies/squadrons. Provincial and militia units represent regiments or brigades. Some of the light units represent detachments of one or more companies. Indian units represent groups between 500 to 2000 warriors. Commanders are key leaders, their staffs, and guard troops. Riverine units represent flotillas of boats.

Initially scheduled for Modern War #68 (September 2023), Ukraine Battles is a grand tactical two-player simulation (battalion-brigade level) of the battles fought in the Donbass in 2014-2015. The game system incorporates the tactical and operational lessons learned from the operations in Ukraine, including tactical and operational drones, improved sub munitions, expanded range and lethality of artillery and MRLS systems, improved thermobaric ammunitions, Russian Battalion Tactical Groups, artillery radars, PSYOPs, Special Forces (Spetsnaz) as combat force multipliers, or networked Anti-Aircraft (AA) systems. The system simulates armor superiority, reactive armor, Shtora systems vs. Anti-Tank (AT) missiles and Rocket Propelled Grenades (RPGs), defensive positions and pre-arranged artillery fire, as well as the difficulties of command control and coordination of hybrid armies formed by regulars and militias.

Each game turn represents two to three days, each hex represents 10km or 6 miles, and combat units range from battalions to brigade sized units.

Eagles of Rome is a grand tactical two-player system for battles of the Roman Era, from the Republic through the Empire to the early Byzantine era. Caesar covers the battles of the Late Republic to the Early Empire (roughly 120 BC to AD 250). Players command armies of various types of forces, from Roman legions to barbarian hordes, with distinctions between melee and fire combat, plus special tactics. Morale is a critical factor. Caesar is played in scenarios. Each scenario represents a historical battle. The rules are in three sections. The first contains the Standard Rules common to all games in the series. The second is the Optional section, which gives rules that players can use at their option or not. The third is the Scenario section for specific battles.

Each hex on the map represents 100-200 meters across, depending on the battle. Each game turn represents 15 to 45 minutes, depending on the intensity of the action. Units represent three to five Roman cohorts, their equivalents, or amorphous groupings of irregulars (1,000 to 3,000 men, based on the era and efficiency of the army). Leaders represent single great commanders, war chiefs or the staff of a legion or other higher formation.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Build your Pop world with your hands and imagination!

In Abstratus Pop, players must represent their secret words through sculptures using colorful, transparent pieces and mirrors.
With special chips, players interact with each other, being able to limit, exchange, take or negotiate pieces.
Abstratus Pop invites you to dive into the POP universe in a totally innovative way, where your imagination, deduction and ingenuity will be put to the test.
Be the most Pop in the match

A mature humor party game.

Be the one that gets 5 white cards and win the game.

Reimplemented in spanish with some Mexican slang and latin american pop culture.

11 Days War is a two-player wargame, covering the final Central Powers offensive on the East Front, February to May 1918 (Operation Faustschlag), to include both the initial 11 days of offensive operations against the Bolsheviks and the ensuing pursuit in the Ukraine. The game pits the Central Powers (Germany, Austria-Hungary, their allies) against the Bolsheviks (or Reds, plus their allies). Additionally, there are third power forces called Greens which may come under the control of either player. There are three scenarios, representing different starting situations. The game system models warfare in the chaotic situation of the Eastern Front during the opening of the Russian Civil War. Further, the movement rules model the situation of operating in later Winter and Spring thaw, where railroads were vital.

Each game turn represents one to ten days of operations. Each hexagon (hex) on the map represents 60 kilometers (40 miles). Each unit represents anything from a brigade up to corps. Strategic markers represent specialized forces, such as airpower, and agitprop (agitation and propaganda).

You're playing as a tribe leader to gain the strongest community of animals. Over the course of 10 rounds, you'll play animals into your community and use actions to get ahead by playing extra animals or taking them from other leaders. Each round will end by facing a Predator as a group. The strongest fighter claims the defeated Predator as their trophy. But if the Predator wins, the weakest fighter loses an animal to the Predator. Which tribe will come out on top as the greatest in the land?

—description from the designer

CENTCOM is an operational one-player simulation of US-CENTCOM military operations in Afghanistan and Iraq during 2001 to 2003 and optionally 2005. The player controls the forces of the CENTCOM (US, NATO, Northern Alliance, and other Allies). The game system controls the opposing OPFOR forces (Al Qaeda, Taliban, and Iraqi). The objective of the game is for CENTCOM to destroy both the forces of Al Qaeda and Saddam Hussein thereby establishing the basis for a stable Persian Gulf.

The standard scenario covers the period of October 2001 (the initial US invasion of Afghanistan) to June 2003 (the anticipated conclusion of Operation Iraqi Freedom). The optional scenario extends the game to early 2005. The player mobilizes forces and conducts operations by expending Expeditionary Warfare Points (EP). When a rule calls for the OPFOR to take an action, you the player must execute that action.

OPFOR actions can be affected by the Persian Gulf Instability Index (PGII), which can shift throughout the game and generate events. CENTCOM can also be played by two or more players, controlling different parts of CENTCOM operations. This is explained in the Optional rules.

Each game turn represents from three weeks of intense combat to six months of logistical buildup and counterinsurgency operations. Each hex represents 43 miles (69.2 km). Units range in size from brigade groups, divisions, and corps.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Gates of Vienna 1683: Battles of the Age of Kings
description:
Gates of Vienna 1683: Battles of the Age of Kings system is a grand tactical two-player system for battles of the latter half of the 17th century to early 18th century. It is based on the 30 Years War Battles system with revisions to account for tactical developments of post 1648. Revisions include artillery mobility and an increase in tactical flexibility. The rules for Gates of Vienna are in three sections. The first contains the Standard Rules common to all games in the series. The second is the Optional section, which gives rules that players can use at their option or not. The third is the Exclusive Rules for each battle to include deployment, reinforcements, victory conditions, and special rules.
The game rules represent a combination of playability and realism and model the overall effects of the weapons and tactics of the great era of pike and shot, and include combat, leadership, and morale. Players command armies of infantry, cavalry, and artillery, each with their own special capabilities, as well as the great leaders who took the field in this era.

Each hex on the map represents circa 500 meters. Each game turn represents 45 minutes to 90 minutes, depending on the intensity of the action. Most cavalry and infantry units represent one or two regiments or battalions. Each combat strength point represents 100 to 300 men, depending on the quality of the army and assignment of light guns to the unit. Each artillery unit represents four to twelve heavy or medium guns.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Red Partisans: The War Behind Army Group Center, 1942-44
description:
Red Partisans (RP) is a two-player simulation of the brutal fighting that took place behind Army Group Center on the eastern front in 1942-44. The Partisan player, representing the Central Staff of the Partisan Movement, must destroy as many targets as possible, interdict the Axis armies’ lines of supply and control as much terrain as possible. The Axis player must keep open his side’s lines of communication, destroy as many Partisan units as possible and capture forced laborers for the German economy.

Each hexagon on the map represents 20 miles (32.4 km) from side to opposite side. The units of maneuver are Partisan “detachments” (company equivalents) and brigades. Axis units of maneuver vary in size from whole divisions down to
battalions. Each full turn represents two to three months, based on seasonality.

At the start of each round, 2 unique vowel cards and 6 unique consonant cards are laid out, then revealed simultaneously. Everyone has 30 seconds to write as many words as they can that use the revealed letters; using a letter more than once is allowed. When time is up, players check and score their words, 1 point per letter; the player with the highest total gets one game point.

Then the player with the Bonus Round card (which rotates each round), points to a letter card, thinks of a word starting with that letter, and writes it down. The rest of the players quickly take turns guessing the letter, with the one who guesses it getting one game point. If after 3 times around no one guesses, the player gives a clue and guessing continues.

The first player to 10 game points wins.

There is also a deck of Spice Up cards, and one is revealed each round, affecting how that round is played, e.g. more or less time, additional letter cards, double points for words that include certain letters, handicaps for certain players, team play, etc.

Words for Nerds contains: 
- 75 consonant cards
- 20 vowel cards
- 3 wild cards (shuffled into the consonant deck)
- 25 Spice Up cards
- 1 Bonus Round card
- 6 pencils 
- 1 pad of paper for writing words on
- An electronic timer

Dungeon Dice Online is a board game for 1 to 4 players with the theme of attacking dungeon in an online RPG game.
Dungeon Exploration × Deck Building × Dice × Battle with Monsters

—description from the publisher
designers: あんきろわーくす (Ankilo Works)
publishers: あんきろわーくす (Ankilo Works)

boardgame: Sounds of Fun: Barney Electronic Talking Board Game
description:
Actual character voices and realistic sound effects bring Barney and Baby Bop to life!

The wonder of sound - in a board game!

Listen to Barney and Baby Bop, then use your imagination to pretend you're a frog, or "moo" like a cow. You'll identify colors and numbers, and play other fun games. Lots of basic learning skills - wrapped up in an interactive, fun game... it's Super-dee-duper!

It’s the summer of 1986, you just graduated high school and you have one more week of summer before it’s time to head off to the real world.

Last Week Of Summer combines turn programming and worker placement with a variety of mini games. Each game, players set up their town using 4 or 5 of the possible locations: Pick up a movie at the Video Store, get high scores at the Arcade, grab some Fast Food, hang at the U-Mart, get the coolest (but not too cool) albums at the Music Store, or learn some tricks at the Skatepark. Each of these locations links 80's theme to a classic mechanism - set collection, tile placement, market manipulation, mancala, push your luck, dice rolling, and more.

Players plan their day and then simultaneously move from place to place as they compete to make friends with the coolest kids in town, get invited to the best parties, and complete location specific goals. As you complete goals you make memories by placing cassettes on locations (these represent the songs that bring you back to those summer days). Whoever has the most cassettes at the end of the week is the winner.

—description from the designer

In Comet, a solitaire game, you help allied airmen who are shot down in World War II escape from occupied Europe. There were several escape lines — networks of safehouses, forgers, and civilian guides — but the most famous of these was the Comet Line, running from Belgium through France and Spain to British Gibraltar. Real people along this line, many of them teenaged girls, are represented in this game, as well as the real challenges they faced. Given the nature of the work, infiltration by the Gestapo was unavoidable, and the threat of capture was omnipresent. More than eight hundred members of the Comet Line were arrested; about three hundred were executed, or died in concentration camps.

The game acknowledges this grim reality while celebrating the courage and victories of these brave civilians who risked so much in resistance to fascism. With a limited number of actions available to you each turn, you need to satisfy competing demands: finding new guides and safehouses, rooting out infiltration, and dealing with the impact of world events beyond your control.

At the end of each turn, you'll plan and execute movement of the men under your protection, risking their capture and your exposure. Losing your only guide for a route can bring the whole thing to a standstill. Through clever card play and careful use of your stretched resources, you'll have to rebuild and move forward — ever-optimistic in the face of incredible odds.

—description from the publisher

Four clans of warriors compete for the right to the throne. To do this, they will need to seize one of their rivals' keys and take it to the castle in the center of a labyrinth full of traps.

Llaverinto is an easy to play boardgame for all ages based on the simplicity of the classics;"A non-linear roll and move" in which players decide the best route to move their pieces across the board and the best tactics to defeat each other.

With your purchase, we will donate a portion to the Dibujando Un Mañana Foundation to support girls, boys, and young people in vulnerable situations.

In this in-hand solitaire mystical space voyage, commune with the stars as you search for a new home for humanity. Choose a star to guide your way, then heed or reject the compelling voices of other stars on your journey.

The star card you choose has a goal you must fulfill to find a home for your people. As you travel through the deck, choose between three star cards at a time, each with a number that represents a competing value. Each star card belongs to one of three suits, or sectors, and has two sides that represent heeding or rejecting that star's call. Cards may also have omens that allow you to take paths that you would otherwise miss.

Each choice you make is added to the course you chart through the heavens, a fan at the back of the deck, which will guide the remnant of humanity for millennia to come. Each card you do not choose spins to its opposite side, limiting your choices for the next turn. When all star cards have been chosen, flip the course and sort the cards by sector to score and see if you have fulfilled the goal of your guiding star.

Lead the way, Starspeaker. The future is in your hands.

This game was entered in the 2024 9-Card Nanogame Print and Play Design Contest, where it won several awards:

- #2 Most Thematic Game
- #2 Best Artwork
- #4 Best Grayscale Printed Game
- #4 Best Cards-Only Game
- #7 Best Overall Game

—description from the designer

An expansion for Black Rose Wars and Black Rose Wars: Rebirth. This expansion contains a new school of magic: Totentanz, a new type of Avatar with a brand new mechanic: The Invaders, 2 new avatars, 3 new evocations, 3 new Forgotten spells and 1 new mage.

boardgame: Rebellion without Rehearsal: Liberation – Part 2 (fan expansion for Android: Netrunner)
description:
Rebellion Without Rehearsal is the second half of the Liberation Cycle. It concludes the story began in The Automata Initiative, in which the passage of a law set to strip bioroids of personhood caused uproar throughout Brazil. As the protests turn violent, and only the President’s signature is needed for this law to be enacted, the activists striving to protect bioroid rights make unlikely alliances and decide how far they’re willing to go.

Rebellion Without Rehearsal contains full playsets of 65 new Netrunner cards, including three identities.

—description from the publisher
