The Word Games set has cardboard letter tiles in 4 colors -- red, blue, green, and yellow, with close to 100 tiles in each color. The bottom of the box has brief rules for several games.

The main game is "Word Making, Word Taking", which has also been known as Anagrams.  Central tiles are revealed one at a time, and any player may call out a word that can be spelled from the exposed tiles, and place that word in front of them. They can also call out words that can be made from one or more revealed tiles combined with other players' words, possibly rearranged. The first player to have 10 words wins.

That game mixes all the tiles together, the number of sets used depending on the number of players. The other games have each player use just their own set (color) of tiles.

- Words Galore: Form as many words from your set as you can in a given time.

- Sentences: Make a sentence using as many letters as possible from your set.

- Crosswords: Make a crossword using as many letters from your set as you can.

- Word Lines: Players take turns calling out a letter, and everyone takes that letter and places it in one of 6 lines of their letters, trying to spell words in each one.

Hexopolis is a 2-player abstract competitive strategy game. Both players control a chip and place a collection of paths, walls, and cells to create an expanding, evolving board. Their objective is to destroy their opponent, which can be accomplished either by jumping on them from higher ground or surrounding them directly with 4 or more walls.

The core mechanic of Hexopolis is "overriding", in which players can place one connector (a path or a wall) over another to redefine the logic of the board.

—description from the designer

Be it in taverns, parlours or field camps – many different dice games are played on the fantastic continent of Aventuria, in the world of »The Dark Eye«. With the rules, cardboard coins and dice contained in »Aventurische Würfelspiele«, these games can now be played in the real world.

All those little games of luck (and some finesse) are meant to function like quick card games do—as a way to win some copper coins when Phex, the god of luck, smiles at you.

Aventurien, ein Kontinent der Geheimnisse, der Abenteuer ... und der Würfelspiele!
Im Laufe der letzten Jahrzehnte wurden eine Vielzahl unterschiedlichster Würfelspiele aus der Welt
von Das Schwarze Auge beschrieben.
Jetzt gibt es endlich eine Komplettsammlung dieser Spiele, direkt spielfertig mit passenden In-Game-Würfeln und Spielmarken (Pappmünzen)!
Egal ob als Gimmick für den Rollenspielabend in der Taverne, zur ambientetauglichen Unterhaltung auf dem nächsten LARP oder einfach beim lustigen Beisammensitzen im Freundeskreis - mit der Aventurischen Würfelspielsammlung wird es garantiert nicht langweilig!

Both dice are rolled at the same time. The players move clockwise around the turntable according to the number rolled. A pawn is drawn according to the number on one die and another pawn is drawn according to the number on the other die, or the player adds the two numbers rolled and draws according to this sum with only one pawn.

If a space is already occupied by a cone, it is thrown out and must start again. If a double is rolled, that player must move the turntable clockwise or counterclockwise by as many spaces as a die shows. This can cause pawns to be knocked over and they have to start again. After a double the player rolls the dice again. The pawns are also allowed to take a detour through the hostel depending on the numbers thrown, if there is a free space there.

The winner is the player whose 3 pawns are the first to be in the target space of the same color.

—user summary

Players receive 2 tokens (plastic tiles) worth 50, 2 tokens worth 100, 2 tokens worth 500 and 1 token worth 500. In addition, each player has one pawn corresponding to one horse. Players roll the dice and move the horses. When the pawn (horse) hits the red square, the horse is relieved of its injury and is eliminated from the game. When the pawn hits the yellow point, the horse spooks and the player loses one turn. When a horse hits the green dot, it means the horse is in good condition and receives an additional throw. In addition to players moving horses, they can bet on which horse will win, and they can also bet on their own horse. You place bets by placing tokens symbolizing money on fields with pictures of horses. The money you have placed cannot be withdrawn, but you can add more until the horses pass the halfway point - the field with the letter P. The game ends when the first horse crosses the finish line. The player who predicts the winning horse wins all the money. If several players guess correctly, they share the winnings equally. Whoever has the most money at the end wins. The game can be finished after the first game or after each subsequent game. You can play as long as players have "money".
The game is intended for 6 people. If there are fewer people, you have to figure out how to handle all 6 horses running.

Origin's & Ends is the final game of The Blackbrim Trilogy, the sequel to Blackbrim: 1876 (Game I) and Reager's Return (Game II).

You've expertly foiled plot after plot, but each time Reager has slipped just beyond your grasp. Now, new information has come to light that may finally give you the upper hand. You return to the origin of your conflict to reveal hidden secrets that were previously just beyond reach. Along the way, you enlist the help of an old friend and embark on what you hope will be the final saga of this journey...

﻿﻿Origins & Ends contains one full storyline made up of two parts, with dozens of puzzles to decipher and clues to crack. It also contains a Blackbrim pin upon completion of the game! Play with a group or on your own for hours of fun.

While searching for spell components, an evil Witch has captured you and your fellow Wizards and turned you into foxes! You managed to escape while the Witch was deliberating what to do with you, but she can't be far behind! Welcome to the Magic Fox Hunt. In this one-vs-all game, one player will be selected to play as the Witch, and the rest of the players will play as multiple Wizards (turned into Foxes) hiding behind cover, moving through the valley attempting to escape the Witch.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Dustrunner: New Car Sections Promo Cards
description: Included in every copy of Tin Realm you will find three all-new cards for Dustrunner! These three new car sections will increase the number of possible combinations for car builds from 8 to a crazy 64.

A Promo Pack of 60+ cards for multiple games published by Grey Gnome Games.

This Promo Pack includes previously released promo cards, but it also includes an astounding 50+ all-new promo cards for all of most popular solo games.

The list of old and new promo cards this pack includes are:
Desolate (2 old cards and 8 all-new cards)
Dustrunner (3 old card & 5 all-new cards)
Gate/Gates (7 all-new cards)
Iron Helm (1 old card & 20 all-new cards)
StoneFire (1 old card)
Tin Helm/Tin Realm (6 old cards & 7 all-new cards)

Additionally, the following items are included:
Dungeon Oracle (6 double-sided oracle cards)
Instruction Cards (Pack includes 2 instruction cards)
Tracking Cubes (7 tracking cubes)

A fast-paced push-your-luck game about cats and poo.  Choose when to bank your poo and when to take the risk - lose poo if cats poo in an already dirty litter tray.  Draw cards or play from your hand, rolling 1 or 2 dice each time - the dice show which coloured litter tray to play poo in in what combination.  Decide when to risk another card and when to bank what you have so far.  First player to pass 10 triggers scoring and, after everyone has an equal number of turns, the winner is the person with the most poo banked on their player card.

—description from the designer

Hunters of the Kaladron Valley (formerly called Monster Hunter Quest) is a casual medieval fantasy solo card game. The main strategy involves managing your Reputation Points and choosing when to spend Actions to match Adventure Card requirements.

There are 6 different Quests with 3 Episodes each. Each Episode has different goals. The Player wins the game by achieving the goals for all 3 Episodes within a predetermined number of days.

There are 2 Heroes to choose from, the Hunter and the Huntress. They are the same except for the art work. Only one Hero is used to play the game.

Use Actions to match either a Success or Avoid Adventure scenario. Progress your Hero from Level 1 to 3. Earn Reputation and find Treasure as you strive to complete your goals. Experience a wide variety of scenarios using Modifiers.  Meet helpful Allies along the way. Suffer loss of Reputation from Gossip and loss of Treasure from Theft. Battle Orcs, Trolls, Giants, Dragons and other Monsters as you travel across your kingdom, purging the land of dangers.

Note: see General Forums for a link to BW PNP Version.

Artwork assisted by Bing AI.

—description from the designer

You have 9 sqares with 9 numbers on the front and 9 numbers on the back. Players place the squares down following the rules of Sudoku. There are squares in 4 colours. This allows 4 players to play simultaneously. The player to solve their Sudoku board first wins.

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Gnome Team – The Glimdwarrow Groundhogs
description:
Gnomes are something of an enigma in the world of Blood Bowl, with many fans having dismissed their existence almost entirely. While their tactics, and woodland creatures, are somewhat unorthodox, Gnomes are quite fond of the game. While individual players are hardly paragons of athleticism, their mastery of magical illusions and the befriending of forest beasts gives them certain advantages not enjoyed by less gloriously bearded teams.

This multipart plastic kit builds the Glimdwarrow Groundhogs, a Gnome team for use in games of Blood Bowl. The team comprises eight Gnome Linemen, two Woodland Foxes, two Gnome Illusionists, and two Gnome Beastmasters. You'll find four head options that can be used with any of the four different Lineman bodies, as well as two alternative heads for each of the other positions. The Beastmasters have a choice of staff toppers, as well as either goose or badger friends. In addition, there are two double-sided Blood Bowl coins, two turn and re-roll markers, four balls, and a transfer sheet.

This kit comprises 102 plastic components, and is supplied with 14x Citadel 32mm Round Bases and 1x Gnome Team Transfer Sheet. These miniatures are supplied unpainted and require assembly.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Gnome Team – Card Pack
description:
Gnomes are one of the most unusual teams to play Blood Bowl. What they lack in size, they more than make up for with tricks, illusions, and playing pranks on the unwary and unwitting.

Ready to build your dream-inducing team? This deck of 44 cards helps you focus on your game and keep track of your stats and special rules. Gnome team coaches have five positions available for hire, plus three Star Players, and these cards will ensure you have everything you need to hand during your games.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blood Bowl (Second Season Edition): Spike! Journal Issue #17
description:
This issue includes:

Trifling Tricksters
Vital background knowledge of Gnome teams in Blood Bowl, including a breakdown of the positions they play – Linemen, Beastmasters, and Illusionists, and the forest friends they bring to the pitch, including Woodland Foxes and Altern Forest Treemen.

Famous Gnome Teams
Thrilling tales of the Glimdwallow Geezers (not to be confused with the Glimdwarrow Groundhogs), Anti-Moot Meddlers, and more improbably-named teams.

Team Spotlight: Glimdwarrow Groundhogs
Hailing from one of the largest known settlements of Gnomes, this team has broken out of the bogs to play actual games across the Old World, becoming a firm fan favourite for their antics and plucky spirit. While highlights might be a stretch, you'll learn all about their history, team rating, and 2501-2502 lineup.

Star Player Spotlight – Rowena Forestfoot
Known as the Guardian of the Forest, this unlikely champion rides on the back of a stag. More focused and level-headed than many of her team-mates, she’s a dependable and agile asset to the Gnomes.

Dirt from the Dugout
Mindy Piewhistle exposes a scandal involving a Gnome team’s memorabilia, which may be too good to be true.

Star Player Spotlight – Rodney Roachbait
This Gnome is always angling for his next big catch. His mastery of the fishing rod proves quite handy, even if the balls end up punctured from time to time.

Gnome Garden Leagues
Fancy a casual game that’s big on magical hijinks, if not stunning feats of athleticism? Try your hand at using this set of rules for playing in the Gnome Garden Leagues.

Mystical Forest Blood Bowl Pitch
Includes rules for using the Mystical Forest Blood Bowl Pitch in your games, including special rules for a Forest Floor crawling with critters, a mystical Healing Glow, and Gnomish Portals. These rules are also included with the GnomeTeam - Double-sided Pitch and Dugouts Set.

Gnome Balls
Includes background and rules for 2 unique balls – the Oversized Acorn and the Gnomish Idol ball.

Coffin Corner
Spike! Journal's obituaries column commemorating those players who are no longer with us, whether trampled on the pitch, torn apart, or possibly eaten.

Chat with the Rat
Ace Skaven reporter Hackspit ‘the Scribbler’ Quillchewer tries to get to the bottom of an important question – what’s the difference between Gnomes and Halflings.

Pro Playbook: A League of Their Gnome
A thorough breakdown of what it takes to coach the Gnomes to an unlikely victory, including how to hire a team and develop them over time.

Star Player Spotlight – Akhorne the Squirrel
Don't make the mistake of underestimating this bite-sized bruiser. While the Gnomes are well-gnome for bringing their woodland pals to the pitch, this particular player puts the crit back in critter, racking up an impressive tally of casualties.

Dungeon Bowl Update: Gnome Teams
Provides a narrative and rules update for players to use your Gnome team miniatures in games of Dungeon Bowl.

Mighty Blow! – Oh Gnome You Didn't!
In this comic strip, Bob plumbs the confused shreds of his addled memory to piece together what little he knows about playing against a team of forest-dwelling Halflings.

—description from the publisher

Dungeons of Everternal transports players to the mystical world of Everternal, a realm filled with ancient secrets and mystical challenges. Here, the ruins of once-glorious civilizations hold the keys to untold powers and hidden treasures, making every dungeon a unique puzzle waiting to be solved.

In this print-and-play roll and write game, players adopt the roles of various heroes, each equipped with distinctive abilities and items. The gameplay merges classic dungeon crawling with innovative Tetris-style puzzle mechanics. Players navigate through meticulously designed dungeons by strategically placing tiles on their maps, which affects their progression and interactions within the game. The goal is to explore dungeons, defeat monsters, avoid traps, and collect valuable items and artifacts.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blood Bowl: Gnome Team – Pitch & Dugout
description:
The Gnome Team Pitch and Dugouts set includes a double-sided pitch depicting a magical forest in the day and at night, complete with rules for playing on it, and a pair of team dugouts.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Naval SITREP: The Journal of Naval Miniatures Wargaming #65
description:
The Naval SITREP is published twice a year by the Admiralty Trilogy Group. It supports the Admiralty Trilogy game system with scenarios, articles on history and technology, and rules expansions & corrections.

The Naval SITREP Issue #65 was published in October of 2023.

Features

Harpoon Scenario: Escape from Aden

Fear God & Dread Nought Scenario: Patmos

Naming Chinese missiles, Part 2

The Guns of SMS Emden

Command at Sea Scenario: Vorschlaghammer

Localizing a Submarine Contact with Sonobuoys

The Iranian Navy & Revolutionary Guard

Weapons & Sensors in Iranian Service

K-3 is a Museum

The Hwasal Missile

The Northern Fleet Incident

Rule Changes and Corrections

Harpoon Q&A: Air ASW

Book Reviews

Mao’s Army Goes to Sea, by Toshi Yoshihara

Black Shoe Carrier Admiral - Frank Jack Fletcher at Coral Sea,

Guadalcanal, and Midway, by John Lundstrom

—description from the publisher

description:
Successor: The Crown is Mine is a multi-player game that pits 8 royal siblings against each other to see who is worthy of the Crown. Create your strategies, form alliances, expect betrayal, prepare to either retreat or advance.
Play as one of 8 Royal heirs trying to earn the people's favor, either by collecting Peasants and Nobles into your court or stealing them from your siblings. Look out for multipliers, special events, or even your own allies, because in this game, alliances are made and broken as soon as the next card is drawn. 
Special events can turn the course of the game, granting one player a ton of points randomly or giving another player access to abilities unique to their Royal heir. But watch out, cause every card drawn brings the game closer to it's unexpected end.
Think you have what it takes to rule them all? Find out in Successor, The Crown is Mine.

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing (Second Edition) – Battle Over Endor Scenario Pack
description:
Players can relive the iconic battle for Endor with this new scenario pack for Star Wars: X-Wing! The Battle Over Endor pack contains everything players need to recreate this decisive battle on the tabletop, including rules for playing out the Battle Over Endor scenario as well as new Standard Loadout cards representing iconic Rebel and Imperial pilots who participated in the battle.

A new Scenario Pack that includes Standard Loadout cards featuring iconic pilots that participated in the Battle of Endor
The Standard Loadouts included in this pack are fully outfitted X-Wing ship cards, perfect for new and existing players to dive right into games of X-Wing.
Includes a new narrative scenario that allows players to reenact their own assault on the second Death Star

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

description:
DragonStrike is a fast and furious boardgame that will let you jump on your favorite type of dragon and take to the skies to perform daring missions while breathing fire (or lightning, frost or poison) on your enemies!

Equipped with beautifully pre-painted miniatures and based on simultaneous action selection, this intuitive game is truly easy to learn but deviously tactical, making it hard to master.

Whether you are looking for a casual game with friends, want to incorporate it into your rpg campaign or if you would like to build and customize your dragonwing to play competitively...if you love dragons this is the game for you!

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
Meta Worlds is a bluffing game from Japan, combined with silly improvs, players take turns to draw a card from the deck, which also includes the Grimoire cards secretly created by the players.

On your turn, draw a card, read its effect without revealing it to other players, and then read out the card effect, or improvise a fake effect and read it out. Other players may challenge and say “I doubt it” in which case the cards must be revealed. There is an advantage and penalty for the winner and loser of the challenge, respectively.

The game's box art cover mimics the style of Nintendo Wii box art cover, and the creator has received Nintendo's approval for it.

—description from the publisher
designers: Kenta Murayama (ムラヤマ　ケンタ)
artists: Kenta Murayama (ムラヤマ　ケンタ)

description:
Sneaker Heads is a game where players compete for popular brand sneakers at auction, sometimes reselling them, and competing for final points (satisfaction).

​This game consists of a purchase phase and a sales phase, and the key to success is maneuvering at the auction and anticipating resale. Find out which sneakers everyone wants, move around skillfully, and bid on the sneakers you want.

A pet's Life, the game where you must take care of your pet using the different cards you have in play, to give him a happy and dignified end with a death by old age... But do not think it is very easy, because your rivals will try to do everything to eliminate your pet before time, so do not hesitate... Take care of your pet... and you... Are you a Petlover or a Pethater?

In order to win the game you will have to create the history of your pet until you reach level 5 corresponding to the card "Death by Old Age".
In addition, you will have to leave many times the fate of your pet in the hands of the game dice!! where it will be decided if it falls in danger, is saved or eliminated!!!

—description from the publisher

A pet´s Life el juego donde deberás cuidar con todo a tu mascota usando las diferentes cartas que tengas en juego, para darle un feliz y digno final con una muerte por viejito... Pero no creas que es muy fácil, ya que tus rivales intentarán hacer de todo para eliminar a tu mascota antes de tiempo, así que no lo dudes... Cuida a tu mascota... y tu... ¿Eres un Petlover o un Pethater?

Para poder ganar la partida deberás crear la historia de tu mascota hasta llegar al nivel 5 correspondiendo a la carta "Muerte por viejito"
Además, deberás dejar muchas veces el destino de tu mascota en manos del dado de juego!! donde se decidirá si cae en riesgo, se salva o queda eliminado!!

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

"Priestess of Yin and Yang, let the ritual begin."

In karasuuri, visitors travel to a village to perform a ritual and pray for peace in the world. But some of the visitors are there to accomplish something very much the opposite. Two teams, sometimes deceiving and sometimes cooperating, struggle to achieve final victory for their side. The fate of the world depends on this ritual...

In this hidden identity board game, players secretly divide into two teams with the possibility of one player acting as the Mediator. The Yin team tries to use the ritual to plunge the world into darkness. The Yang team seeks to fulfill the true purpose of the ritual. Players play Prayer Cards against each other, trying to tip the balance in favor of Yin or Yang to win. Meanwhile, the Mediator exerts their own influence over the game, winning only if they can keep the score tied between the Yin and Yang teams.

—description from the publisher (translated)

"Dance in God's tiny garden"

DayLily is a battle game where girls from various factions — Human, Necromancer, Elf, CaitSith, Dragon, and Dwarf — compete for territory using magical soldiers and their special skills.

Make full use of your character's unique abilities to compete for the magic crystals sleeping in the God's Garden. When a territory is chosen by the roll of a die, players play larger and larger Magical Soldier Cards from their hands. Don't worry if you can't play; if you didn't use any cards or special powers in this territory, you can draw more cards for the next battle. The winner of the territory takes the magic crystals hidden beneath it, and the one who holds the most magic crystals at the end of the game will be the winner.

The territories where you compete and the outcome of the game depend on a moody God. Let's collect as many magic crystals as we can before God get tired of us!

—translated and adapted from the publisher's description

boardgame: Merchants of the Dark Road: Secret Villages
description:
As in years past, there is money and fame to be earned from buying and selling goods along the Dark Road. This winter, however, Highreach is alive with secrets and rumors... the clever merchant who takes notice can trade in something as valuable as any goods—information. Hidden paths to three secret villages have been discovered. In each village lies opportunity for intrepid merchants willing to engage the rangers, delvers, and pilgrims who choose to live in these small settlements off the beaten path. And everyone is talking about the latest news out of Farglen—it seems the Baroness of the Bards is missing! While on business in Highreach, merchants should keep their ears open for hints of what befell her, as these rumors are of great interest at the Ringway Inn.

This expansion contains two modules: Ringway Rumors and Secret Villages. These modules can be played together or separately. Some content—like the companion die, a set of wagon upgrades, and the all-new event types—can even be used with just the base game.

The Ringway Rumors module incentivizes visits to certain districts by providing “rumors” that can be bartered at the Ringway Inn for small favors, while the Secret Villages module offers three new travel destinations as well as “leads” that reward you for repeat trips.

All of this integrates smoothly with solitaire play thanks to the addition of a card system that allows the private concern to interact with and balance against these new gameplay features.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A game about sequencing screenshots of an old animation with 50 bits of writing by 50 writers.
description:
Try to place screenshots from Plane Crazy (1928) in the correct sequence.

Place 4 screenshots correctly to win!

Each card is double-sided; one side has a screenshot and a caption, whilst the other side will also show the timestamp of when the screenshot occurs in the animation.

The side with the timestamp also features a bit of writing, to hopefully spark thoughts and conversation about the nature of IP protection for art, animations, music, and game rules.

—description from the publisher

With this expansion, Prepare to be immersed in the rich tapestry of feudal Japan in Ninjas Unleashed, where the enigmatic power of celestial stones beckons. As a formidable Shogun, guide your courageous apprentices on treacherous missions, their success entwined with the fateful touch of these mystical artifacts. Witness the transformation of ordinary ninjas into awe-inspiring Elite Ninjas, wielding unparalleled abilities that shape the very fabric of destiny. Navigate the intricate dance of shadows, deceit, and strategic brilliance, as you fiercely compete for dominion and ascend to the pinnacle of greatness. Will you harness the true might of the Celestial Stones and emerge as the legendary ruler destined to mold the course of Japan's history? Brace yourself for an unparalleled gaming experience, where every move and decision holds profound consequences. Forge alliances, outwit rivals, and embrace the challenge that lies before you. Unleash your inner ninja, seize the reins of power, and etch your name into the annals of feudal legend. The path to triumph beckons—embrace your destiny and let the Ninjas Unleashed saga unfold.

The Deluxe version of the game contains additional cards and a rollable game board mat.
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play
Players: Games with expansions that change player count

boardgame: Ninjas Unleashed: Twin Shadows and Rise to Greatness
description: Introducing the Solo Mode and Co-Op Mode Edition, this is an Expansion Deck of Ninjas Unleashed.

An educational game for kids where the players roll dice to to reach a suffering organ in the human body and “heal” it. After an organ is cured, another one will need assistance. At each turn, the players also draw cards that may boost or hinder their movement.

No Pair is a small push-your-luck dice game designed to fit entirely on one side of a business card.

The rules are simple: roll three dice up to three times and observe the result.
If the result contains a pair, it's worth 0 points.
If the result is a straight, add the dice together.
If the result is a triplet, add the dice together and double the result.
Otherwise score the lowest die.

First player to reach 60 points wins.

—description from the designer
family: Series: Business Card Games (City Hare Studio)

description:
A board game inspired by the seminal work of philosopher of science Thomas Kuhn called The Structure of Scientific Revolutions.
Players come up with hypotheses by curating their hand of exemplar cards and run experiments to validate these hypotheses by drafting and rolling phenomena dice. If the experiment results support their hypotheses, player can get more influence on the paradigm board which represents swaying scientific community members to their framework for explaining natural phenomena. Watch out for paradigm-breaking anomalies in the results though, as they can easily lead to a paradigm shift! The game is played in a historical setting, during a transition period between two competing scientific paradigms, and ends once the paradigm shift is complete and one of the two paradigms is debunked by accumulating too many anomalies. Players who have influence on the paradigm board for the winning paradigm are victorious, with the player contributing the most support for the final paradigm crowned leader among the winners.

—description from the designer

Stack-a-Cash is 3-in-a-row style game played with coins.

The game was made as a challenge to see if a game could be made to fit on one side of a business card.

To play the game you need a 3x3 board and six coins.
You need 2 large, 2 medium, and 2 small coins. Denominations are irrelevant.

One player plays heads, the other plays tails.

All six coins are common to both players.

On your turn you can 

     place a coin
     move and/or flip a coin


Coins can be stacked big to small.
You cannot move or flip a coin that was just moved, flipped, or placed.

Game ends as soon as someone makes three in a row of the same side (heads or tails) on the topmost layer of coins.

Be careful you don't lay traps for yourself, the game is trickier than it looks!

—description from the designer
family: Series: Business Card Games (City Hare Studio)

description: A wealthy man is found murdered on his luxury yacht after a party. Players must examine all the evidence and look for clues in newspapers, witness statements, autopsy reports, phone records, maps, photos, surveillance videos, websites and more. Players must find suspicious details in the evidence and reveal the killer.

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Today the Rebellion Dies Squad Pack
description:
The Today the Rebellion Dies squad pack includes Iden Versio and the elite units of the Inferno Squad to create a strike team for the skirmish miniatures game Star Wars: Shatterpoint.

The Inferno Squad has proven its prowess in numerous clandestine missions, carrying out assassinations and lightning strikes to protect the Empire's most important secrets.


     Multilingual squad pack for the skirmish miniatures game Star Wars: Shatterpoint
     Mission team with Iden Versio, Gideon Hask, Del Meeko and Imperial Special Forces
     Beautifully modelled miniatures that are ideal for dedicated miniature players, newcomers to the hobby and for painting alike
     Contains 5 highly detailed, unpainted miniatures, 4 command cards, 4 battle style cards and 4 unit value cards


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Real Quiet Like Squad Pack
description:
A man once dismissed as an opportunistic scoundrel becomes a hero of the Rebel Alliance! More than just a pilot, Han Solo boldly leads a team of elite Rebel Commandos during dangerous missions, cementing his commitment to the Rebel cause. These troops are highly trained in reconnaissance and unorthodox tactics, making them valuable assets in the fight against the Empire.

Joined by the ferociously loyal Wookiee Chewbacca, who wields a powerful bowcaster and lends his ferocious strength to these dangerous missions, this group of Rebels’ goal is nothing short of restoring freedom to the galaxy…

The Real Quiet Like squad pack includes Han Solo, Chewbacca and a Rebel strike team to create a strike team for the skirmish miniatures game Star Wars: Shatterpoint.

Featuring Han Solo along with the fiercely loyal Wookiee Chewbacca, who wields a mighty bowcaster and uses his tremendous muscle power on dangerous missions, this Rebel strike force fights to restore freedom to the Galaxy.


     Multilingual squad pack for the skirmish miniatures game Star Wars: Shatterpoint
     Mission team with Han Solo, Chewbacca and a strike force of elite Rebel commandos
     Beautifully modelled miniatures that are ideal for dedicated miniature players, beginners to the hobby and for painting alike
     Contains 4 highly detailed, unpainted miniatures, 3 command cards, 3 battle style cards and 3 unit value cards


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Never Tell Me The Odds Mission Pack
description:
Players can embark on a new Star Wars: Shatterpoint mission with this mission pack! Featuring a new Mission card and 9 Struggle cards, Never Tell Me the Odds Mission Pack adds more variety to games of Shatterpoint with new objective layouts and new mission special rules.


What’s included
• 1 Mission Card
• 9 Struggle Cards
• 1 Punch Sheet

—description from the designer

Develop the imagination using various techniques of surrealism, developed at the beginning of the 20th century.

Retratos en Juego offers 5 gameplay modes:

Automatic Sequence

Deal five cards to each player and leave one face up in the center of the table. Participants must find a link between the letter on display and the portraits they received. Whoever discovers it first will place their card on the table and say it out loud. If the connection is accepted by the group, the game continues and the new search will have to occur with the last supported character. The first person to run out of cards will win. The sequence can be given by aspects linked to the characters (profession, nationality, affiliations) or by the elements that are part of the construction of each portrait. Associations linked to gender do not add up in this type of game.

Surreal Interview

The player on duty takes out a portrait and places it on the table for everyone to see. He must think of a question for said character and write it on a piece of paper, without the rest seeing it. Then he will fold the paper covering the writing and pass it, along with the letter, to the player on the right. Whoever receives the portrait will write the answer, as if they were completely certain about the question, taking the voice of the “interviewed” character. Then they will fold the paper again and pass it to the next player, who must now write a new question, always inspired by that portrait. The game will continue alternating the writing of questions and answers, as many rounds as the group wishes. At the end, whoever asked the first question will unfold the sheet and read the text created with everyone's contribution.

Poetic Metaphors

Place a portrait in view of the entire group. Each player must write a short poetic text or metaphor. They will be able to choose an element present in the portrait and cross it with an adjective or describe the character (without naming it) using what the construction of that portrait generates for them. For example, regarding Fontanarrosa, we could say: “His arguments were very sharp.” Everyone will write their metaphor on a piece of paper, fold the sheet so that the rest cannot see it, and leave it on the table. One of the participants will read the poetic texts aloud. Then, each player will vote for the one they consider most creative or original, taking into account that they will not be able to choose their own text. They will add one point for each vote obtained. In this game creativity wins!

Who am I?

A card is distributed to each participant. Without looking at it, they will place it on her forehead, leaving it visible to the rest of the group. Taking turns, each person will try to guess which portrait they got by asking questions about certain traits or qualities that she might have. The rest of the group will only be able to answer “yes” or “no” to the questions asked. If the answer is yes, you can continue asking until you receive a negative. In this case, it will be the turn of the player on the right to ask. The dynamic will be repeated until someone guesses the character.

Allegation

Based on a portrait obtained at random, one participant challenges another to substantiate or explain the presence of any of the elements with which the portrait is constructed. For example, someone proposes Edgar Allan Poe's “black cat” and both players have to explain what it means, arguing why it is there. The rest of the team will choose the argument that seemed most convincing to them. Players who spoke will receive one point for each vote obtained.

Combo Fighter is a card-driven tribute to the classic arcade fighting games.

Take on the roles of iconic fighters and string together cards to unleash powerful combos.

But be careful. Your deck is also your life bar. Every move you make and every hit you take drains your energy.

To become a true Combo Fighter champion you must make every hit count.

—description from the designer

Stories tell of the legendary c, a bird said to bring everlasting happiness and wealth beyond measure to those able to sate its hunger. If the rumors are to be believed, these rare creatures dwell on an uncharted island where they make their nests and rest their wings.
 Determined to seek your fortune, you track down leads and somehow manage to make landfall upon this virgin isle. To your delight, you quickly stumble upon the quarry you seek—the elusive joybeak!
 Before long, however, you realize your new feathered friend is far more demanding than anticipated. To satisfy its unending hunger, you have little choice but to wander the jungle with the joybeak perched upon your arm in search of the berries it desires.
 Do happiness and wealth await you at the end of this trial? There is but one way to find out...

—description from the designer

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C17 – The Last Stand
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C17 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for the original zombicide.

Designed by ARTUR RYMANOWSKI

Create and tell stories from the myriorama (panoramic scene) format with the cards obtained. It may be oral or written, individually, in groups or collaboratively.

Pasaje Alelí offers 7 gameplay modes:

Alelí Passage

Take cards from the deck and arrange them as desired to put together the construction and a story. To accompany the inspiration, there are some questions as an example: who appears in the scene? What is he doing? What is his name? Where does it come from? What will he do next? What sounds are heard? What do you like to do? Who are your friends? How would she meet another character? What would they do together? and many more.

Any day

Take five cards at random and place them face up on the table. Arrange them in the order you choose and tell the story of that day of the characters that appear there.

Neighbors

Take three cards and name the characters that appear. Introduce the neighbors who are there and tell a story where these characters meet.

The sounds of the neighborhood

In turns, each participant takes five cards and puts them face up on the table. Then he has to make a sound that he thinks is “heard” in one of the cards. The other participants have to point out which one. Whoever guesses first keeps the card and the rest are set aside. When the deck is finished, whoever has the most cards will be the winner.

In this room there is… (memory game)

Place the deck face down in the center. The first participant takes a card, says In this room there is... completes the sentence with an element that he sees on the card and leaves it face up on the table. The next player takes another card, says In this room there is, repeating what the first participant said and adds an element that he finds on the new card. He leaves her arranged next to the first one (above, below or to the side) creating a scene. So on, until a player forgets or confuses an element. If you want to make the game more challenging, each participant shows his card with the element but then keeps it face down in another pile.

The tallest building in the world

Shuffle the deck, deal three cards to each player and place the rest in the center of the table. In turns, each participant begins to assemble their building, starting on the sidewalk. If he didn't get a floor card, he takes another one from the deck and the next player continues until they can start. Once the first path is in place, you can continue up through the rooms. Lastly, those with a roof are placed. If on your turn you get another floor again, you will place it next to the previous one, and you must continue adding cards to your construction. Play continues until one player has completed a building or the deck runs out of cards. Whoever has the tallest building wins. Then each participant can tell the story of their construction.

And what happened before and what happened after

Take a card, look at it and tell a story that brought the characters to that scene and describe what could have happened next.

boardgame: Jurassic World: Camp Cretaceous Board Game
description:
Escape from Isla Nublar with the Jurassic World Camp Cretaceous Board Game.

Based on the Netflix original animated series Jurassic World Camp Cretaceous, this board game allows players to become a part of the world of dinosaurs. Role the dice and move across the board into different sectors of the map. Visit camp highlights along the way such as the botanical gardens and the T-Rex kingdom. Each sector has its own corresponding game cards which determine your fate. Will you be chased by a Velociraptor and have to run back 2 spaces? Or will you get a ride in the Gyrosphere and roll forward?

—description from the publihser

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

Anthony Browne, the renowned British author, invites us to imagine, create and tell stories. Put together short stories about the protagonist's tastes, readings or interests based on what the illustrations suggest.

The game book is made up of 50 cards: 45 illustrations and 5 game forms. The deck reflects different scenes that feature a monkey who enjoys playing and having fun.

New York, 1927:
Dear Cousin Otti. Welcome back to New York. It's been 13 years since your escape to Palermo and voices say the city has changed. The family is still going strong, but be warned. People never change and the dons of other families are after our money like never before. Times have gotten rougher and so have the methods. Keep your men ready, the big moment is just around the corner. - Don Carlo

Immerse yourself in New York in the 1930s and take on the role of one of eighteen different family heads in "Unfinished Business - Final Chapter" and do everything you can to create a financial legacy. Collect valuable possessions in your safe, speculate on winnings from the betting shop or network with influential personalities to get money from the bank. Be surprised by a variety of new events, new citizens and action possibilities that will take your previous Unfinished Business gaming experience to the next level. Take advantage of your new, unique character abilities that are available to you in three levels: Get extra money aside, steal from enemy safes, gain additional knowledge or always have the right "answer" up your sleeve. How will you earn money for your family? Will you challenge the bank at roulette, opt for honest investments or prefer an ambush? But be careful, if you brag about your wealth, you will quickly arouse the interest of the other dons and may be brought back down to earth with a loud bang!


     Use your basic game box as a new inlay for the new big box
     Enough space for base game, all expansions and promos
     Space for sleeves
     33 new events and actions
     18 playable dons with different abilities
     7 player expansion
     22 additional property cards
     Epic Game module
     Car bomb game module


—description from the publisher (translated)

New York, 1927:
Werter Cousin Otti. Willkommen zurück in New York. Seit deiner Flucht nach Palermo sind 13 Jahre vergangen und Stimmen behaupten die Stadt hätte sich verändert. Die Familie ist immer noch stark, aber sei gewarnt. Menschen verändern sich nie und die Dons anderer Familien trachten nach unserem Geld wie nie zuvor. Die Zeiten sind rauher geworden und die Methoden auch. Halte deine Männer bereit, der große Moment steht kurz bevor. - Don Carlo

Taucht ein in das New York der 30er Jahre und übernehmt in „Unfinished Business – Final Chapter“ die Rolle eines von achtzehn verschiedenen Familienoberhäuptern und setzt alles daran ein finanzielles Vermächtnis zu erschaffen. Sammelt wertvolle Besitztümer in eurem Safe, spekuliert auf Gewinne aus dem Wettbüro oder vernetzt euch mit einflussreichen Persönlichkeiten um Geld aus der Bank zu erhalten. Lasst von einer Vielzahl neuer Ereignisse, neue Bürger und Aktionsmöglichkeiten überraschen, die eure bisherigen Unfinished Business Spielerfahrungen auf die nächste Ebene bringen. Macht euch ab sofort eure neuen, einzigartigen Charakterfähigkeiten zu nutzen die euch in drei Stufen zur Verfügung stehen: Schafft zusätzliches Geld bei Seite, stehlt aus gegnerischen Safes, erlangt zusätzliches Wissen oder habt stets die richtige „Antwort“ im Ärmel. Wie wollt ihr das Geld für eure Familie verdienen? Fordert ihr beim Roulette die Bank heraus, entscheidet euch für ehrliche Investments oder doch lieber einen Hinterhalt? Aber Vorsicht, wer mit seinem Reichtum prahlt weckt schnell das Interesse der anderen Dons und wird unter Umständen mit einem lauten Knall zurück auf den Boden der Tatsachen geholt!


     Nutze deine Grundspielbox als neues Inlay in deiner neuen Big Box
     Genug Platz für Grundspiel, alle Erweiterungen und Promos
     Platz für Sleeves
     33 neue Ereignisse und Aktionen
     18 spielbare Dons mit verschiedenen Fähigkeiten
     7 Spieler-Erweiterung
     22 zusätzliche Besitzkarten
     Modus - "Episches Spiel"
     Spielmodul Autobombe


—description from the publisher (German)

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C21 – Let's Walk The Block
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C21 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for the original zombicide.

Designed by BILLY HAYES

The Disaster of 98. The Spanish-American War Game is strategic in nature and simply reproduces this conflict with a system that allows it to be played in a maximum of 45 minutes. It is designed for two players and can be played between experienced wargamers and beginners alike. To do this, the game system uses Event cards (one for each side to use in each of the 5 turns in which the game lasts) and wooden cubes of different colors that represent the troops involved in the conflict (Cubans, Tagalogs, indigenous people, Spanish and American expeditionaries).
The Event Cards simulate various political-military aspects of the years 1890 to 1898 and are divided into two separate decks of 8 cards for each side of the game (Insurgent and Spanish).

Golaço ("Great Goal" in Portuguese) is a fast paced game with simple rules for two to nine players.

The game plays in nine rounds.
Each round, place one field card and two team cards on the table. 
Then, roll the five custom dice.

Each die has a visual information that may match with the teams. It may be a color of the uniform, the model of the jersey, the shape of the team´s shield, etc. Each match scores a goal. And there is also a die with types of "Golaço" like "a bicycle", "little fish" (a diving header), "olympic" (a goal from a corner kick).

Everyone looks at the dice and try to solve the final score of that play. Whoever says corectly the total result of the play and the type of Golaço gets one game point.

After nine rounds, the player with more points wins the game.

—description from the designer

Golaço é um jogo com regras simples, veloz e muito divertido.
Todos ao mesmo tempo tentam descobrir o placar e o golaço da rodada.
Mas fique atento pois pode dar zebra!

Como é o jogo?
A cada rodada, um jogador escolhe a carta de campo com características específicas e compra duas cartas de times de várzea.
Um time é o da casa, o outro é o visitante.

Role os cinco dados:
Um dado mostra uma cor do uniforme; um dado mostra um formato do escudo; um dado mostra um modelo do uniforme; um dado mostra o time da casa ou o visitante ou o apito ou a zebra; e um dado mostra como foi o golaço da partida, de bicicleta, olímpico, de letra, de peixinho, de placa ou de mão! Cada correspondência dos dados com as cartas na mesa, junto com as características do campo, marca um gol para o time correspondente.
Todos tentam descobrir o placar simultaneamente. Quem souber primeiro cobre a carta de campo e fala o placar com o golaço em voz alta. Se acertar, ganha o ponto e a rodada termina. Se errar, está suspenso na rodada e os dados são rolados novamente.

Após nove rodadas, quem tiver acertado mais placares vence a partida.

—description from the designer (Portuguese)

Players are dealt hands of cards that display a black or white number or one of seven biomes (suits). In rummy fashion, players draw from the draw deck or from either the number discard pile or the biome discard pile to create melds (followed by an optional discard). Players must shed their entire hand at once with one or more melds. A meld consists of (i) a white number card with a matching number of similar biome cards or (ii) a black number card with a matching number of biome cards, all of which are different. Score a point for each card in the meld(s). Replenish to a hand of five cards and play until someone reaches 50 points.

PLAN - COOPERATE - ADAPT TO RISING TIDES

Sea level rise threatens homes, businesses, and infrastructure. If no action is taken, low-lying areas will face storm flooding and, eventually be permanently inundated.

The challenge is how to apply the variety of solutions to our landscape. Cooperate to protect homes, businesses, and most importantly, the health of the Island's five communities over three phases of sea level rise expected in the next 100 years.

—description from the publisher

Backstabbers is a battle royale game set in Solara, a new world discovered and corrupted by humans in their way to escape the total destruction they made on Earth.

In Backstabbers you're gonna use the power of more than 40 characters to eliminate your competitors. Eliminating players and being the last one standing will grant you coins, 5 coins will make you the Supreme, the most rich and powerful in the new world.

It is a game to 3-8 players within 45 min average where eliminated player still have a role in the game!

DON'T TRUST ANYONE, NOT EVEN YOURSELF!

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

boardgame: 7th Sea: City of Five Sails – Tooth and Claw
description:
All out war has broken out in the City of Five Sails!  The scheming Don Constanzo and his fate witches have successfully pitted each and every faction against the other.  Now, each desperate leader must fight tooth and claw to keep control of their district.

This 112 card expansion for 7th Sea: City of Five Sails introduces new characters, schemes, and other cards themed around conquest, crime, and combat.  Join us for our second competitive season, and help shape the City of Five Sails ultimate fate!

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
Let's all make a candy bouquet!

This is an action party game where you throw candies at each other and try to get more candies of your color in the candy bouquet.

Each player has a designated candy color. Players simultaneously reveal their chosen candy card, and players who match (resulting in "butting") try to throw their card onto the lace doily. Candy cards are worth various points, and the player with the most points of their candy color wins. But be careful, because cards that miss or overflow from the candy bouquet disappear!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Experience the aftermath of Factoria, where the game ended at 100G!

The Factoria: Forbidden Walkers expansion lets you enjoy the devastating expansion of an enlarged reproduction system with new complexity. Included are:


     Special abilities for owners of Legendary Gear, which increases the competition for Legendary Gear and providing a catch-up mechanic
     Addition of LV7 and LV8 relics
     Addition of overpowered, forbidden technical books


These additions created a dangerous balance that could go out of control at the slightest mistake mistake. The expansion is not recommended for first-time players, but once you experience it, it's so appealing that you won't be able to go back to regular play... This is a dangerous expansion set that everyone who considers themselves a gamer should try!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Deal all the cards face down. Cards have quadrants colored red, yellow, gray, and blue, respectively. Each quadrant will depict a rose, berry, whale, or sun. The middle of a card contains one of the color words that may or may not be written in the color that the word spells. Take turns revealing cards two at a time. When the color of the word on the first card (not the color spelled by the word) combines with the picture in the quadrant whose color corresponds to the color spelled by the word to create the phrase, "gray whale" or "red rose or "blue berry" or "yellow sun," slap the table. The slowest player takes the cards from both piles into his hand. Play until someone with no remaining cards is the first to slap the table and thus win.

As a new agent of the Culper Spy Ring your first mission s a search upon which many lives depend. Agent 711 has urgently requested that you locate three military facts believed to be a aboard a ship. But which vessel should you search in this crowded harbour? What of the news that several ships will set sail in just one hour? If you are late will our cause be lost?

Your fellow agents have hidden information in a local tavern that will guide you to a special ship. Do search for sets of numbers and sets of words that always appear in groups of three. Pay attention to related clues that indicate how the sets should be put in order.

—description from the publisher

Japan. Dawn of the Edo Era.
In the heart of the Land of the Rising Sun, young warriors are preparing to leave their master's Koryū, to embark on an initiatory journey across the country... to discover whether they possess the qualities required to become a true Samurai.
To do so, they will cross the territories of the 9 Bushidan, fearsome warrior clans, among whom they will have to find a Lord to pledge allegiance to, or risk remaining a rōnin, a renegade.

Competitive game in which players must achieve victory conditions through a strategic combination of cards collection and game actions, based on a mechanic of secret bids.

Goal: Be the first to fulfill 11 victory conditions in less than 12 rounds of play to be the first Samurai.

—description from the publisher

Deal every player seven cards, each of which is segmented into 9 sectors. Each sector is white, red, blue, green or gray. Seed a discard pile and then take turns playing cards onto the pile. Draw cards equal to the number of sectors on the played card whose colors overlap with the colors of the sectors on the top card of the discard pile. Ideally that number is zero. The first player to shed his deck wins.

Nebula Drifters: The Lost Voyage is a cooperative board game for 1-5 players, set on a vast, uncharted alien planet. Players assume the roles of survivors, marooned by a ruthless corporation that abandoned its colonization plans after failing to see quick progress. As one of the forgotten, you must rally your fellow survivors. Work together to gather resources, overcome obstacles, and tackle deep dungeons, deepening your bonds and enhancing your survival skills.

Each game session unfolds through a quest that challenges players to explore mysterious landscapes, discover ancient technologies, and adapt to the ever-changing environment. Choose your path wisely, as each decision impacts the group's overall success. With every day passing, survivors grow stronger and more adept, slowly transforming from castaways to pioneers of a new world. Whether playing solo or with friends, "Nebula Drifters: The Lost Voyage" offers an immersive experience of survival, cooperation, and adventure.

—description from the designer

Mud Run is a card game where players try to complete obstacles in a race to the finish line. Each turn, players get to take two actions to try to get ahead in the race. Players may draw an action card, play any number of action cards from their hand to complete an obstacle or the front player may reveal an obstacle card to extend the course. The game comes with more obstacles than you use each game, so every game is different.

There was more than warm hospitality extended to us as visitors to an unexplored land. As we speak to the master, the people around us stop and stare, showing expressions of interest, curiosity, suspicion, and disgust. But there's nothing to worry about. In my hands is a book left behind by a great linguist. Come on, let's learn all about the Numerous Rituals!

アマタノ儀式 (Amatano Gisiki / Numerous Rituals) is a communication game in which players perform rituals using the languages ​​of various cultures. In Interpretation (the cooperative game), players work together to perform as many rituals as they can in 10 minutes. The master speaks the language of another culture, the interpreter translates it, and the other players have to guess the ritual without looking at the language record. In the Study Session (competitive game), students compete to be the best at understanding different cultural languages. Players take turns as the master and exchange questions with each other. This mode tests both your ability to speak and listen to different cultural languages.

Both modes of play use only one language per game. Each language has various characteristics and ranges from easy to difficult to understand. There are 26 different languages ​​included in the Intercultural Languages ​​book. There is also a tutorial and a practice language sheet. As soon as you open the box, you can play and learn the game!

Transform your tabletop into a galaxy at your fingertips in Flickering Stars! Choose from two rival factions — the Children of Arha or the Terran Coalition — then take command of a powerful fleet, discover planets rich with resources, and battle it out with your spacefaring rivals in a quest to claim a piece of the stars!

1. FLICK your ships across the table.
2. FIRE missiles and plasma balls at your rivals to thwart their plans.
3. FINISH your objectives for the win!

With over one hundred custom-designed plastic pieces in the box, you can transport your table from the dining room to a war room in space as you face off in three scenarios. Start with an evacuation drill in scenario 1 to learn the basics of the game, then step it up in scenario 2 with a new ship and objectives. Scenario 3 brings it all together and more, adding refineries, outposts, and hubs. Each faction comes with five ships that serve different functions, including launching projectiles!

A reader reads a clue from a card. The first player to shout the answer, which will begin with the letter "p", wins the card, but only if that player adds the word "pickle" to his answer. Play an agreed-upon number of rounds. The player with the most cards wins. The game also can be played in Times-Up! fashion, in which clues are one-word long or in the form of charades.

Players take turns moving their playing piece according to a die roll in zig-zag fashion across a large hexagonal grid consisting of 70 hexagonal spaces. Each player chooses whether to place one of his three types of cactus tokens onto the space that he lands on or to give his opponents a chance to place a token there. Play continues until all the spaces are filled. At that point, the players score for each group --- that is, all adjacent tokens are of the same cactus type --- that they have created. The larger the group, the greater the score for that group. The person with the most points wins.

Each player places three one-story stacks, two 2-story stacks, and one 3-story stack of checkers (schmeckers) onto the board. Take turns moving in a direction indicated by the arrows in the starting square a distance of up to as many squares as the height of the stack. Capture opponents' pieces by bumping or jumping. The last player with schmeckers remaining on the board is the winner.

boardgame: Senjutsu: Battle For Japan – Daimyo's Castle
description:
Daimyo's Castle is a quad-fold XL board

An extra large quad-fold board for playing higher player-count games of Senjutsu.

Features:

     A stone courtyard area.
     Castle walls and broken Castle Walls area.
     The Castle gate area.


Fight on variable height surfaces and be careful not to fall to your doom!
family: Series: The Battle For Japan (Stone Sword Games)

description:
Expansion card for Sitting Ducks saying:

"Place any duck on an adjacent duck, these ducks are connected. The connected ducks are moved like a duck, placed in the crosshairs and removed as long as they are in the field of fire."

"Lege eine beliebige Ente auf eine angrenzende Ente, diese Enten sind verbunden. Die verbundenen Enten werden wie eine Ente bewegt, ins Fadenkreuz genommen und entfernt, solange sie im Schussfeld sind."

Start with a 5X5 grid with spaces numbered from 1 through 25. Roll three dice and use standard mathematical operations on the three numbers to arrive at one of the numbers in the grid. Cover that number with a marker. The first player to get five markers in a row horizontally, vertically, or diagonally is the winner.

UNO Flip! card game adds an exciting twist to the classic game that includes Autobots and Decepticons from Transformers! Play a Flip card and the deck FLIPS from the Autobots' side to the Decepticons' side, which has penalties that are extra tough. Battle to get rid of cards with the Might of Optimus Prime Rule OR fight to see who must draw cards with the Tyranny of Megatron Rule! Only another Flip card can change things back. Makes a great gift for players 7 years and older, especially Transformers fans.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
Soul Strain is a card game for 1-4 players, enhanced by dice rolls, perfect for quick 15-20 minute sessions. It's designed for easy setup, offering simple, stress-free rules, and a dynamic and flowing gameplay experience.

Soul Strain uses the mechanics of Pentaquest card game with some variations. Specifically, the target combinations on the cards are different, the player has a ticket that acts as a wildcard, the score is based on the combinations of cards retained and not on their number, there are 48 cards with combinations to beat instead of 42, there is no [+/-] symbol, and instead of having the two symbols Sun and Moon, there is only the Vinyl symbol.

—description from the designer

One page monster hunt is print and play, roll and write exploration, and puzzle game. For ages 8 and up, 30 minutes long.

Game objective: The players are exploring the map, gaining experience, collecting treasures, and trying to find altars where the enemy could be located and defeated.

Game components: One page game sheet per player, two d6 dice, and pencil.

Game modes: Solo with one hero, solo with band of heroes, competetive for 1 - ∞ players and cooperative play for up to 4 players

—description from the designer

The board game that rewards your knowledge.

In World Challenge Game, players will answer questions to conquer territories. Fight to conquer the world and show that you are the one who knows the most! Answer questions, carefully consider the territories you want to conquer, and learn while proving that you are the best in World Challenge Game.

World Challenge Game Asia is a new version set in the Asian continent, featuring a new map, new characters, and new thematic cards. It is played independently from the original World Challenge Game and can be combined with it.

Choose one of the two game modes:

Conqueror, where you conquer as many countries as possible before the game ends, or Attack, where you attack and win countries conquered by other players to take control of the entire continent. In this mode, you can conquer a country that another player has won.

Each turn, a player will draw a card and choose one of the two existing questions, which they must read aloud, along with the four answers. The other participants will write their chosen answer on their card, marking an "X" in options A, B, C, or D. When everyone has answered the question, the answers will be revealed, starting with the player to the left of the one who read the question. If the answer is correct, the player can place a conquest token in an available border country, and the same goes for the rest of the players.

The Conqueror game mode will automatically end once the last country on the continent is conquered. At that time, the countries conquered by each player will be counted, so the player with the most conquered countries will be the winner of the game. If there are two or more players with the same maximum number of conquered countries, tiebreaker questions will be read to them. Players who cannot answer the question lose.

The Attack game mode will end when only one player remains on the continent, who will be crowned the winner. If the question cards run out, the game will end, and the player with the most countries will win.

—description from the publisher

The Joker Expansion enhances Square One’s gameplay beyond its initial borders. It features three-reward and 4-point sequences, along with a versatile Joker piece that substitutes for any other piece. All together offering unique mechanics that can’t be found in the base game.

As an additional bonus with the Joker expansion, you can enjoy playing Square One with 5 or even 6 players!

—description from the publisher

This Kickstarter exclusive expansion brings in an additional unique mechanic with a special Joker black sequence and a special Joker piece.

The Joker Ambassador pack serves as a mini-expansion to Square One and you don’t need to have Joker Expansion to use it in your game.

—description from the publisher

A simple game of balance, strategy and luck. The game can be played at simple levels for any child 3+ or with more sophisticated rules for those with a strategic turn of mind. Simply pick a cube from the bag and place it on the playing board. If the board tips enough for the end to hit the table you lose.

The board is simple with four scoring zones, but the cubes have a hidden twist, just when you thought it was getting easy - the cubes are all different woods and therefore different densities, the heaviest might be double the lightest in weight, changing strategically where you can place them. As each turn brings a new piece selected blind from a bag you won't know until it is chosen how the weight of the cube will affect your strategy.

—description from the designer

Grotten is a minimalist dungeon crawler using physical tiles to generate and explore a dungeon, where you roll on random tables to fill the rooms with monsters, traps, loot, NPCs - and a final epic boss fight. The zine contains one-page rules and character creation, cut-out dungeon tiles and tokens, and lots and lots of random tables.

Grotten is perfect for both solo play, co-op, with or without a GM, as a quick and easy stand-alone game or to use as a dungeon in any fantasy system you are playing.

—description from the publisher

In October 1944, the Soviet 14th Army under General Meretskov launched the Petsamo and Kirkenes operation called the "Tenth Shock" by Stalin. Its aim was to throw the Germans out of northern Finland, an area of strategic importance for both its sea bases and for the nickel mines of Kolosjoki (today named Nikel). Following the Finnish capitulation in September 1944, preparations were already underway for General Jodl´s XIX Gebirgs-Korps to retreat into northern Norway; however, its fate hung in the balance when Meretskov launched his attack.

Polar Storm is taught in 30 minutes and easy to digest yet a challenge even for the more experienced players.

With the usual rules system of WWII Battle Series, its Special rules, Winning conditions and Events we try to depict this moment in time.
This is Game no 7.

—description from the publisher

Map scale: Each hex covers just under 4 miles [just under 5 1/2 kilometres].  Source:  Paul Shackleton, 29 May 2025.

description:
Stretch Goal Promo Cards from the Inventions: Evolution of Ideas Kickstarter.

Includes: 6 Promo Idea Cards:
Robots
X-Ray
Suspension Bridge

Board Game
Lacquer

—description from the publiher
