Böses Erwachen is a cooperative board game that takes place in a world that we enter every night, but to which we usually pay little attention - namely our dream world...

Are you really awake? Or are you still trapped in a dream? Throw back the covers, get out of bed and find out!

Dream Games lets you freely explore the game world with up to three other players. With each action you learn more about your surroundings and gradually reveal more playing field cards.

As you explore your dream world, you must always keep an eye on your supply of dream energy. Because when this runs out, you "wake up" in your bed again and have to start all over again - however, the skills you have acquired so far and the objects you have found remain with you and they are absolutely necessary in order to solve the many puzzles that the dream gives you will provide.

Little by little you will get closer to your goal: finding out why you are trapped in this dream and what lies behind your mysterious backstory - then you can finally wake up!

Welcome to your next investigation! Immerse yourself in the world of criminal investigation work to uncover a crime case using clues, clues, witness statements and internet research. The aim is to solve one or two tricky puzzles in order to expose the perpetrator.

Will you find out what really happened to Mary Amann and who the perpetrator is? Check all documents, find evidence and alibis and think outside the box if necessary.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Cold Case Detectives: Fall #1 – Tödlicher Sommer 1998
description:
If a murder cannot be resolved, one speaks of a cold case.

As Cold Case Detectives, you take over such cases and solve crimes that have remained unsolved for years.

This game is designed like a realistic investigation file.
View and analyse newspaper articles, evidence, photos, documents, digital materials and a lot more.

Case #1 - Summer 1998
It is summer 1998 in the small town of Rudensberg. The 19-year-old Bernd Zöller is found murdered in the outdoor pool.
Immerse yourself in the late nineties and find out who is responsible for the murder.

General:

     Use over 40 realistic documents and objects and work like real investigators to cooperatively find the murderer.
     With videos, soundtracks, special websites and many other interactive content.


Important: you need internet access. The story is only playable once, but the material is not destroyed and can therefore be given away to friends.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Turret Dice The Defenders is a Cooperative, Dice Placement, Tower Defense Game about defending your planet from a colossal swarm of meteors that threatens to destroy everything. Players must work together to shoot down all incoming targets as even one slipping through will be the end of their world.

Players will roll dice on their turns to determine which actions are available to take. They will then spend their dice to activate turret spaces in their Defense Array to damage and destroy incoming meteors and tower spaces that have a wide range of clever abilities like making other actions better, and mitigating dice rolls. Alternately, dice can be spent on Dashboard Actions to build more turrets and towers into the player's customized Defense Arrays, to upgrade turrets, to draw cards, or play powerful one-time-use event cards.

Meteors are generated by randomized "Spawn Decks" and have special abilities and tricks of their own, as well as pesky Alert Cards to throw players off and distinctively different bosses. These randomized elements will keep the game fresh and different for play after play! Not to mention optional Gameplay Modifiers that can change up the way the game is played or increase the difficulty substantially.

—description from the publisher

First Burger is a sequel or remake of Final Burger, a trick-taking game released in 2016.

This time, you're making burgers in a fast-paced memory game. The chef player tells the other players or customers which ingredient cards they should flip. For example, "Tomato cheese chicken onion sandwich!" Orders are placed using tokens, so the customers won't forget.

Every time you turn over a card with a correct answer, you receive points and may face greater challenges. If you can guess what the card will be when you flip it over, you receive additional points. What's more, if you flip the last piece of the order, you get a burger completion bonus! But if everyone tries and fails, it will be the chef who flips the final card.

You can stack the round ingredient cards in a staggered manner to create a fun-looking hamburger!

—description from the publisher (translated)

FOUR PETALS
Divulgative card game to recognize medicinal plants and relate them to their therapeutic properties and their daily use, based on the information collected in the Plantipédia de Remeiers and Remeieres de Girona.

—description from the publisher


            
        
    


CUATRO PÉTALOS
Juego de cartas divulgativo para reconocer plantas medicinales y relacionarlas con sus propiedades terapéuticas y su uso cotidiano, a partir de la información recogida en la Plantipedia de curanderos y medicinales de Girona.


            
        
    


QUATRE PÈTALS
Joc de cartes divulgatiu per reconèixer plantes remeieres i relacionar-les amb les seves propietats terapèutiques i el seu ús quotidià, a partir de la informació recollida en la Plantipèdia de Remeiers i Remeieres de Girona.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

boardgame: Through Ice and Snow: Big Box
description:
Playing on a modular board players captain their own ship in a bid to document species, build vital shelters and map treacherous landscapes. Each voyage brings unique challenges for your crew to face.

Succeed and become immortalised. Fail…and be lost to the ice forever!

The Big Box is a crowdfunding exclusive available to everyone who pledges Captain's Pledge or above, instead of receiving a box for the expansion and another for the base game - you can choose the Big Box. This will take up less shelf space than going for two boxes.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Lunaris 45: Promo Cards Solo Campaign Mode & Scenarios
description:
“The year is 2023 and the world is buzzing with excitement as a groundbreaking new project is announced. A leading corporation in space exploration has unveiled its plans to build a lunar complex unlike any other. The name of this project is LUNARIS 45, and its goal is to create a fully operational lunar colony that will be run entirely by artificial Intelligence. In this variant you’re
on your own...”

Use the official solo rules to play with these cards.

These 12 promo cards will give the player 2 new modes to play Lunaris 45:

6 Solo Campaign Cards - Play 6 solo sessions interlinked, with different challenges.

6 Scenario Cards - Solo\Multiplayer scenarios that offer a different challenge for the players

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Gods of Rome: Cards for Solo Campaign Mode & Scenarios
description:
The gods are divided. Rome has lost its way. We must restore its glory! With the favor of the gods, we shall lead our people to become the chosen tribe that will bring unity and strength to the empire once again.

These promo cards come with a link to the new official solo mode and also 10 new cards that can be used in 2 ways:


     Solo Campaign Mode - create a different path to glory, where you'll play 4 interlinked solo games, trying to achieve redemption.
     Scenario mode - use a single card to create a different setting for the solo\multiplayer game.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fado Duetos e Desgarradas: Promo Cards Solo Campaign Mode
description:
“The Great Day of Fado” is coming.

There is no memory of such an event, thousands of tourists and fado lovers from all corners of the world are expected. Managers from all over the country are working on the organization of the event. From north to south of Portugal, one, two and even three shows are planned simultaneously. It will be days, nights and, sometimes, even early morning parties!
Failure is out of the question, and to organize the perfect show, it is necessary to choose the best artist, the right alignment, the perfect melody and the most appropriate of the scenarios. It is the ideal time for some managers, hitherto unknown, to show how much they are worth.

No one will miss and this is your moment to shine!

These promo cards will come with a link to the official solo rule book, and also 4 new cards.

Solo Campaign Mode - Using the official solo rules, play 4 solo sessions interlinked with different challenges.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Moesteiro: Promo Cards Solo Mode, Solo Campaign & Scenarios
description:
Towards the end of the XIV Century, King D. João I ordered the construction of one of the most elegant Portuguese monasteries, the Monastery of Santa Maria da Vitória. During the construction period numerous prestigious architects, master craftsmen, and apprentices came from all over Europe motivated by financial rewards, prestige, and, importantly, the pursuit of knowledge.

In Moesteiro, players are architects in charge of teams of workers and master artisans and they compete for the glory of becoming the most prolific and important architect of their time.

A pack of 25 promo cards that will come with a link to the official solo rules and that offer the player new ways to play the game:

10 Automa Solo Cards - Now you can play Moesteiro in solo mode
6 Solo Campaign Cards - Play several solo sessions interlinked with different challenges
9 Scenario Cards - 8 of them can be played multiplayer, 1 is exclusively solo.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Lusitania XXI: Promo Cards Solo Mode, Solo Campaign & Scenarios
description:
Dreadful news reaches Rome; in the most remote part of Iberia, Lusitania, a people ungoverned and unwilling to be, has defeated the Roman Empire in another battle. Julius Caesar, Roman Emperor, is furious, he’s had enough.
"Alea iacta est" - the die has been cast.

He calls in his three best commanders with a clear message: travel across the Empire recruiting and equipping the most fearless Legionaries and Barbaric Mercenaries. Then march 4 Roman Legions into Lusitania to crush the heroic resistance. Caesar will lead one of the four legions himself and provides abundant funding to ensure success.

These 17 promo cards will come with a link to the official solo rules and also brings some new modes to play:

7 Automa cards - now you can play in solo mode
5 Solo Campaign Cards - play 5 different solo sessions with different challenges
5 Scenario Cards - Play Solo\Multiplayer with different challenges

—description from the designer

With your help, Inspector Karl Klugmann was able to successfully solve his first crime case in 2004. Now a bigger challenge is waiting for him - an unknown dead man who has not yet been identified. Who is the man who was found in the Vienna cemetery of nameless people in 2018? What happened to him? And who is responsible for it? This challenge is too big to be able to master it alone – joins Inspector Klugmann's team and helps him to solve this case.

You get the scant investigation file and access to the web-based police portal and you can get started right away! An envelope filled with helpful artifacts is included for your progressive investigation.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Mia Klein is a popular podcaster. With her channel "BloodCast" she wins the audience award in the highly competitive category "True Crime". But not everyone is happy with her success. When Mia is found murdered in Saalistaja, Finland, her fan base reacts shockedly. Rumors about the possible perpetrator are circulating quickly. An alarming number of people had a motive to murder Mia.

You take on the role of investigators to solve a crime with ingenuity, combination skills and detective work. Using a realistic case file, you have to uncover cross-connections, evaluate evidence and narrow the circle of suspects further and further.

Find a place with good lighting and internet access. Have pen and paper ready and an app for scanning QR codes and then open the main envelope. In addition to the first information, there are three other sealed envelopes. These remain closed at first and may only be opened if you receive instructions in the course of the game.

boardgame: Cold Case Detectives: Fall #2 – Vermisst In Paris 1967
description:
CRIME TENSION FOR HOME - Solve an exciting crime case together and reveal the murderer!

REALISTIC EVIDENCE - Use over 40 credible documents and objects and work like real investigators.

CASE #2: It was 1967 in Paris. Actress Elizabeth West disappears from her hotel room under mysterious circumstances. Can you find out what happened to her?

GET STARTED IMMEDIATELY - Forget long instructions! You can start playing this crime game straight away. Perfect for anyone who loves an escape game or cooperative crime game!

2 - 3 HOURS OF HIGH VOLTAGE - Important: you need internet access. The story can only be played once, but the material is not destroyed and can therefore be given as a gift to friends.

boardgame: Cold Case Detectives: Fall #3 – Der Fluch des Pharao 1923
description:
Solve a highly realistic interactive crime case together and find the murderer!

Use over 40 realistic documents and objects and work like real investigators to cooperatively find the murderer

Cold Case Detectives - Case #3: It's 1923 in the Valley of the Kings, Egypt. During excavations, archaeologist Sir John Williams is found dead in the burial chamber of Pharaoh Nebunenses II. Can you find out what happened to him?

Start straight away without lengthy instructions, perfect for escape game and crime game fans

1-4+ players, ages 14+, 2 - 3 hours of fun, Important: you need internet access, the story can only be played once, but the material is not destroyed and can therefore be given away to friends

boardgame: Cold Case Detectives: Fall #4 – Tod am Filmset 1986
description:
CRIME TENSION FOR HOME - Solve an exciting crime case together and reveal the murderer!

REALISTIC EVIDENCE - Use over 40 credible documents and objects and work like real investigators.

CASE #4: It's spring 1986 in Hollywood when 23-year-old Brian Decker is found murdered while filming a horror film. Can you find out what happened to him?

GET STARTED IMMEDIATELY - Forget long instructions! You can start playing this crime game straight away. Perfect for anyone who loves an escape game or cooperative crime game!

1-4+ PLAYERS - The Cold Cases Detectives game is perfect for detectives ages 14 and up.

2 - 3 HOURS OF HIGH VOLTAGE - Important: you need internet access. The story can only be played once, but the material is not destroyed and can therefore be given as a gift to friends.

boardgame: MonToons: Clash of the Coral Isles
description:
MonToons: Clash of the Coral Isles is a high-energy, competitive print-and-play, roll-and-write, arena combat game.

As MonToon warriors from rival factions, you compete in an epic clash to claim the Waystone and bring prosperity and power to your faction. Achieve victory by tactically positioning yourself around the map, defeating your rivals in combat, or solving a strategic puzzle to unlock the Waystone’s power.

The MonToons of the Coral Isles, accustomed to conflict, now face an unprecedented event, one which promises to fulfill an ancient prophecy: The Tidalmark. As the Waystones of the Chief’s Archipelago become supercharged, the most powerful MonToon warriors from all factions will traverse sand, sea, rock and reef to claim unimaginable power and etch their names into the island’s vast history.

The legendary thief Le Vent Rouge has challenged all scoundrels to rob the wealthiest man in France. Are you brave enough to enter his mansion, and skilled enough to escape with his treasures?

Le Vent Rouge is a dice roll & spend game. In this game, you will compete, negotiate, and team up with other players to take on increasing risks in a heavily guarded mansion full of gold and objet d'art.

The game ends in one of two ways. 

    Defeated guards: If a guard token is placed on a numbered clock space matching the number of players, the game ends.
    The boss is defeated: If the boss is defeated, the game ends during phase 6.


At the end of the game, whichever player has the most points across all their stolen goods is the winner.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Fist of the North Star x One Night Werewolf
description:
A collaboration product between “One Night Werewolf”, a very popular party game that allows you to play “Werewolf Game” with a small number of people in a short time, and “Fist of the North Star”! !
Play as the characters from “Fist of the North Star” and find out who is pretending to be “Jagi” or “Amiba” during the discussion!
family: Characters: Fist of the North Star

boardgame: Le grand jeu des affaires: Place de la Bourse
description:
With Le grand jeu des affaires, experience the crazy adventure of captains of industry!
Initially, a secret objective.
Equipped with a passport that makes you someone else and a few shares, you set out to attack the international markets: you speculate on the Wheat, Soya and Sugar markets; you then accumulate the financial resources which open the door to the stock market.
CANON. CLUB MED. DIGITAL, GILLETTE, LVMH, MATRA... Accompanying the rises, dodging the falls, you buy, you sell, you trade. Throughout the game, you treat yourself to the biggest names in the world economy. But beware ! The paths to glory are strewn with pitfalls: you will have to keep your objective secret, form and then betray alliances, counter the most unexpected takeover bids...
Near the top, you will soon create your own Holding company to list it on the stock market. In this strange and exciting world in which strategy always takes precedence over chance, you will feel the thrill of big business circles!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Avec Le grand jeu des affaires, vivez la folle aventure des capitaines d'industrie ! 
Au départ, un objectif secret.
Muni d'un passeport qui fait de vous un autre et de quelques actions, vous vous lancez à l'assaut des marchés internationaux : vous spéculez sur les marchés du Blé, du Soja et du Sucre ; vous accumulez alors les ressources financières qui vous ouvrent la porte du marché des actions.
CANON. CLUB MED. DIGITAL, GILLETTE, LVMH, MATRA... Accompagnant les hausses, esquivant les baisses, vous achetez, vous vendez, vous échangez. Au fil du jeu, vous vous offrez les plus grands noms de l'économie mondiale. Mais attention ! Les sentiers de la gloire sont semés d'embûches : il vous faudra garder secret votre objectif, nouer puis trahir des alliances, contrer les O.P.A les plus inattendues...
Proche du sommet, vous créerez bientôt votre propre société Holding pour l'introduire en bourse. Dans ce monde aussi étrange qu'exaltant au milieu duquel la stratégie prime toujours sur le hasard, vous allez ressentir le frisson des grands milieux d'affaires !

—description from the publisher

Tai Chui is an internalised martial art from China. This game aspires to be the gateway to the concepts and values practiced in Tai Chi.
During the game, Chi Gong or training cards are drawn with different point values. They can be exchanged for Taolu or form cards of different types and/or for Chi or energy tokens. The first player to collect 5 different form cards and two different energy tokens wins the game.

We journey now to the islands to the east – or west, depending on where you are relative to them. Here your title is Emperor. They tell you you’re just a figurehead, though you can still order whatever breakfast you want. They may be right; you did get that breakfast. Your ceremonial sword and armor are made of paper. The samurai never let you into their tea parties, and the ninjas are always tying your shoelaces together. And the epic poem they wrote about you is only 17 syllables long. Rice has been adopted as currency, and no-one seems to even be trying to get your face onto the grains. But when you wake up each morning and look out over the land, life doesn’t seem so bad. Now, what’s for breakfast?

This is the 16th expansion to Dominion.

It has 300 cards, with 25 new Kingdom card piles. There are Shadow cards that leap out from your deck, and Prophecies that will someday happen and change everything. Debt and Events return.

—description from the publisher

You are a doujin author. As you live your daily life, you work hard at writing and drawing a new book for the doujinshi sale, commonly known as "Pomike." Writing can be a solitary task, but writers come together at the convention! However...I don't think there will ever be a convention. Even the new book you have written cannot be distributed without "Pomiket!" Who will draw the “Pomiket is canceled" card?

In ポミケ中止 (Pomiket is Canceled), players manage the resources of their busy lives as they write their own doujinshi. Writing is a solitary job, but sometimes we all work together on a joint magazine, go on a reporting trip together, undergo a tax audit, and gather at a sales event! At the sale, line up new books in your own space and exchange cards with each other saying, "I'd love to buy the new book, please!" and "Thank you very much!" At the post-even celebration, draft cards and interact with other circles to get material for your next work! But watch out, when the "Pomiket is canceled" card is drawn, the game ends. The player with the most victory points wins!

If you know even a little about doujinshi sales, you're sure to have a great time. Experience the real frustration of being chased by a deadline and live the joys and sorrows of doujin activities!

boardgame: リトルアルケミア (Little Alchemia)
description:
This is Little Alchemia, a town full of apprentice alchemists. Learn alchemy and spread your fame by completing requests. When you gain enough fame, you will become a full-fledged alchemist. Who will be the first to graduate?

Players each become a different apprentice alchemist with their own special abilities. On your turn, you may gather materials, synthesize items, or fulfill a request. You may also go to the market to buy or sell materials and items. Gathering and synthesis actions involve rolling dice, so you'll need luck and planning on your side. Fulfilling a request gains you fame and money. The first alchemist to reach 30 points of fame is the winner!
publishers: Ever Forest Works (エバーフォレストワークス)

description: Arquilínea is a game that challenges observation, knowledge, strategy and memory with competitive and collaborative variants. The objective in all its ways of playing is to chronologically organize the works from different periods in the history of architecture.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Step into the vibrant world of Strip Mall— where entrepreneurs turn a tiny storefront into a thriving empire. 
It’s not just a game; it’s a crash course in hustle, creativity, and the sweet satisfaction of counting those virtual dollars!!

Great way for those who want to learn more about running a business. Learn the impact of advertising, rival businesses and random events which can make or break a fledgling business.

Take over three stores in the strip mall to expand your business empire and win.

—description from the publisher

A game for teaching young children to read an analogue clock.

In a player's turn, he or she draws a card, which shows either a clock face or a child holding a clock. In the former case, the player has to find a cardboard tile showing the same time, flip it over, and put it on the corresponding space on a player board (may be the player's own or another player). Then the player on whose player board the tile fit on takes the next turn. In the latter case, the player to the left of the active one says a time of day (e.g. quarter past nine) and the active player has to set a cardboard clock to that time. On a success, he or she draws a new card, on a failure each other player in turn has a chance to get it right. Then the player who got it right takes the next turn.

The winner is the player who first fills out all the spaces on their player board.

It's a simple tile placing board game using the hexagonal tiles.

Game setting:

Each player receive a special blank tile to put it in front as their starting tile.
The rest of tiles are put on a "pool" where we can take them at random (we use a cloth bag).
Then choose who will be the "first player", using your prefered method of your choice, who will keep the pool of tiles.

How a turn works:

The first player takes from the pool as many tiles as players plus one and place it in the middle of the tables. It will become the "market" where each player, starting by the first one, will chose and take a tile (this is compulsory).
Once the player has taken the tile from the market may choose to place it next the starter tile (or any other tile placed before) following the RULE OF PLACEMENT or to keep it in his reserve. There's no limit of tiles one can have in this personal reserve and can be used in any moment but, once the tile is placed in a "legal" position it can't be moved.
Once all players have taken their tile and placed (or not), "first player" moves to the next player who now will keep the pool and replenish the "market".

Rule of placement:

A tile can be placed only next to another tile while there's no contact with a marked side.

Game ends:

Game ends when there are no more tiles to take from the "pool" or when there's no room for more tiles on player's figures.

Scoring:

Each tile has a value equal to its marked sides. So, for us, red has value of 1, blue of 2, yellow of 3, green equals 4 and brown is 5.
So each player has to count how many tiles have of every colour and do the maths.
Also we count each marked continuous side and take the value of the lonqest one. If this is a "circular" one, surrounding the figure, its value doubles. In case of ties the figure with less tiles win.

Players create personal fantasy Family tree using the powers of your faction DNA.

At the beginning, choose your factions that will give you unique powers. Choose a couple of secret score cards objectives. Each turn, choose two adjacent cards from the Public Market and add them to the growing Genealogy Tree you create: producing as many descendants as possible, choosing their mates, or leaving some singles whose branches will be cut off in the name of tree purity. Use the supporting cards, your creativity and planning ahead in multiple generations to achieve as many of your secret goals as possible. 
Collect bonus points from public tasks, competing with all opponents. And most importantly, use your unique family DNA feature! The game ends when it runs out of cards. The winner is the player with the most points

As the sun sets on July 11, 1943, both sides prepare for the largest tank battle of World War II! Nearly a week of grueling slaughter has finally gained the 1st SS Panzer Division a few kilometers towards their objective: Prokhorovka. So close, yet so far!

The Russian 18th and 29th Tank Corps have been beaten back, but they are not defeated. Replenished with tanks, they will be the sledgehammer in their only planned counterattack. This has to work! It cannot fail!

Both enemies planned their offensives to begin on July 12th, but neither side is aware of the other’s plan. As dawn breaks, the roar of over 300 tanks signals the coming clash.

Now, YOU are in control. YOU are in command! How will your strategy succeed or will it fail in WW2’s greatest, epic, tank battle that history still talks about?

It is ALL IN YOUR HANDS!

—description from the publisher

In Lab Rats you control a group of researchers who must complete the different projects using the resources that you will acquire.
To achieve victory you must accumulate the maximum Research Points (PI), which you will obtain as you complete the Projects. The more difficult a project is, the more PI you will get, but the more resources it will cost to complete.
The main resources are glass cubes, which represent the material that can be found in a laboratory, and rats, which are used in some investigations.
The game time is measured in months, and each one begins with the Breeding Phase, where we will be provided with money and the rats will breed, followed by the Work Phase, in which the researchers are placed, and ending with the Maintenance Phase, in which everything will be prepared for the following month.
The main board has a calendar that marks the months, the locations where the actions are carried out and the project board.
The game ends when the year ends and that is when the finished projects must be presented. The winner will be the one with the most IP at the end of the game.

—description from the designer

In Tameri, you will experience everyday life in ancient Egypt, exploring a classic theme from an original angle that emphasizes agriculture, traditional life, and the high importance of weather cycles in the Nile Valley. In this game, you will farm the land, construct buildings, assign priests in different temples, build sphinxes, and so much more!

Tameri is a competitive 1-4 player medium-weight game that utilizes a unique mechanism of marble balls flowing through a pyramid on the game board to determine the weather, which in turn determines the crops and farming output of each player. Tameri blends classic Eurogame mechanisms, including resource management, tableau building, set collection, worker placement, engine building, and more in a fresh and highly thematic way.

Are you ready to outplay your opponents in ancient Egypt and rule the Nile Valley?

—description from the publisher

The goal of Eureka is to collect 4 Gold Nuggets before the other Miners. By digging in the Mine and stealing from sleepy Players, you will have to strategically find the best way to victory.

A core mechanic of the game is fatigue: by digging, you gain fatigue, and reaching 3 fatigues will make you fall asleep, allowing other players to steal from you.

This game contains 126 high-end playing cards (M31) and a rule book.

—description from the designer

The tea ceremony lies at the heart of Japanese culture, giving life the most essential and elemental concepts of hospitality, respect, and Zen philosophy. There, they believe that tea, which came from China in the 13th century, resolved the health problems of the Buddhist monks who drank it. Over the years, the tea ceremony evolved to offer the samurai a place of calm, far from the chaos of the battlefield.

In The White Castle: Matcha, you will have to deal with a new place in which to influence the court using new members of your clan. Having members of your clan in good seats within the Chashitsu (tea room) will help you achieve a greater level of influence within the court of Himeji. Also, you will have one additional action each round. Will that be enough?

The first printing includes The White Castle: Promo 4 packaged inside the expansion box. It is currently not available by itself.
To be played with the base game and the Matcha expansion.

Contains 3 Die tiles, each of which with Chasen on the back. When setting up the game, replace the 3 Die tiles that grant a Resource of your choice with these 3 new tiles.

—description from the publisher

The Desert Tribes introduces a new faction adept at survival in the harsh wasteland environments of the Neuroshima universe. This expansion adds a fresh tactical layer with innovative mechanics such as "Hide" and "Ambush," enhancing gameplay unpredictability and strategic depth.

—description from the publisher

Gnome Kingdom is a race through an enchanted forest, combining dice manipulation with strategic choices and a bit of luck.

Assume the role of one of the five clan leaders in the race to settle who should become the next leader of the Gnome Kingdom. Using the forest’s enchanted resources, unique clan powers and a lot of dice, each leader has to earn their skills to get through the forest.

The game ends when the hidden crown is discovered and the player who has collected the most valuable skills wins and becomes the new King or Queen of the Gnome Kingdom.

—description from the publisher
Played in teams. Players quickly memorize a hand of cards, then switch hands with their partners. Then each player chooses a card that he thinks will match one of his partner's cards.

boardgame: モノクロエンドゲヱム (Monochrome Endgame)
description:
A black and white storm, in a certain house there were two rumors that were being whispered among young girls. One is "White Lily", an older sister who guides troubled girls to the right path. One is "Kuroyuri no Kimi", an older sister who invites troubled girls into a path of vice. In a season where hearts waves like petals on the surface of the water, will the fate of these girls be black or white?

Monochrome Endgame is a trick-taking game where you "must not follow", Each player is given a team. When playing with 4 players, the "white camp" and the "black camp" will compete with 2 players each. When playing with 5 players, one neutral faction will be added here. The player who wins the trick exchanges his winning cards with those of other players 2:1. By doing this, you can control the difference in the number of white and black cards of cards that a player with a specific position ultimately acquired and aim for victory for your side.
publishers: スタジオカヤノナカ (Studio Kaya no Naka)

description:
Choose to play together as the Legends and save the world or take on the mantle of the Foe and destroy them.

The Legends
Take a Legend card and use your weapons, skills and abilities to work together to accomplish objectives and complete tasks.

The Foe
Use your barracks full of fighters to stop the Legends from completing their objective. Eliminate them to gain bonuses and conquer the world.

The Battleboard and Encounter Cards
Each battle take place on the Battleboard. the Encounter Cards explain how to setup the terrain and objectives.

Once the Battleboard is set, the players take turns moving and using actions to complete the objectives or eliminate all of the Legends.

—description from the designer

Bori Wars is a survey-based card game in which players or teams try to match the most popular answers to survey questions posed to Puerto Ricans. Split your group and compete to see who wins the most points.

Once, long ago, in another timeline, there was a storage-expansion for Millennium Blades called "collusion". The hubris and luxury of that gratuitous box was so great that the druid kings declared it could never be reprinted again. Thus, we changed the name and reprinted it again in this normal-sized box. Maybe they won't find out.

Millennium Blades: Draw Power is an expansion for Millennium Blades that includes the main gameplay components from the Collusion expansion. This gives you access to Team Mode, NPC's, updated rules, new characters, and, of course, more cards!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Is it unlucky that we released 13 mini-expansions for Millennium Blades over 8 years? That's a lot of games for one octade (which is like a decade, but shorter). While it would be worth it to use your valuable time travel opportunities to go back and collect all these, we decided to make things a little easier and just put them all in one expansion sized box.

Millennium Blades: Decked Out is an expansion for Millennium Blades that repackages and includes the items found in various mini-expansions. This includes rare Dice Tower promos, more characters, and more bosses.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Morderstwo Tomasa Blackwooda ("Murder of Tomas Blackwood") is a detective game in which players play the role of a detectives trying to solve, on the basis of the available case files, the murder mystery of Tomas Blackwood, known as Rocket Man, the eccentric chief engineer of CosmoZ, a leading company in the space industry, which in recent years has transformed the industry and allowed Americans to return to orbit in American manned spacecraft.

The case files contains the following materials (65 pages of documentation / 40 documents in total):

     10 photographs (victim, security guard at victim's apartment building and 8 suspects),
     8 profiles of the suspects,
     8 testimony transcripts,
     8 evidence protocols,
     1 autopsy report,
     1 photo of the victim from the crime scene,
     1 transcript of 911 call,
     3 screenshots from Twitter,
     3 screenshots of various websites,
     2 newspaper clippings,
     1 phone billings,
     1 email from a lawyer for one of the suspects,
     1 cryptocurrency wallet statement.


The aim of the game is to identify the murderer from among the 8 suspects (for this you receive 50 points), to indicate a motive (10 points) and the exact time of the victim's death (5 points). On top of this, an additional 14 questions about the overall events and the people involved are scored (2.5 points each).

—description from the designer

This expansion contains all the characters from the base game, the Council, and from the Depths of Damnation, the Old Gods, Mountain of Torments, and Forgotten Guardians expansions, all re-illustrated by artist Nicolas Roblin and each character slightly changed from their original. They may have different starting stats, and they will have one of their skills reworked! They come from an outer world, after all.

Beyond the Portal also includes two new characters: The Occultist and her soul syphon, and the Tinkerer and his skill to disarm traps!

Mortes Estúpidas ("Stupid Deaths") is a hilarious board game full of curious, clumsy, and surprising stories about the ways people have died. Every question card contains a Stupid Death, but is it true or false? Answer correctly and you will run away from the Death Pawn, but if you answer wrong the Death Pawn will approach. Be the last player on the board or collect all the letters of the word “MORTE” (death) and win the game.

King Arthur needs you! Dive into the legends of the Round Table as a Knight under the counsel of Merlin.

Will you slay Mordred? Save the knights from Morgan Le Fey? Build Camelot? Or will you try to accomplish the impossible and find the Holy Grail?

Make tough choices, finish quests in a tightly wound Roll-and-write game where players play simultaneously and using the same... we mean, almost the same dice as everyone. Merlin decided otherwise, and he's in charge while Arthur is battling Mordred.

Note: the game is not available separately, it is included in Dés-Papiers.

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

boardgame: High Noon: Expansion Loot – Kickstarter Exclusive
description: More loot for those that went along for the ride. All the cards are marked "KS Exclusive".

Remember the 80s arcade hit Snake? or the awesome lightcycle race from Tron???

2 Serpents is a Roll-and-Write game of these two, mushed together to make a quick dice-drafting game that's totally RAD!

Win by cornering your opponent and preventing them from moving. Collect power-ups that will allow you to bite off one of their 2 heads... Oh did I forget to say you had 2 heads on your Serpent? And you can also grow more with power-ups!!!

A very tactical game with push-your luck elements, Risk management and excitement. Bubblegum not included.

—description from the designer

boardgame: カケルカ　ケルカ (Kakeruka Keruka)
description:
A card guessing game in which players play by multiplying two cards in their hands!

Kakeru Kakeruka is a card game in which you fight with two cards in your hand.

Before the fight begins, multiply the two cards in your hand to form a large number!

The numbers on the cards are 0~7, plus -1 and -50.

You can collect the large number by relying on the positive cards, or you can risk it and try to collect two negative cards to guarantee a win!

Each card has an effect when placed on the field. Use them to make a better hand or to determine everyone else's number!

Cards with an identical number are stacked on top of each other, and when there are three piles of stacked cards, the game is over! Should you go out now or wait until your next turn...?

boardgame: Return to Dark Tower: Meshuggenah Promo Card
description:
Originally available from backing Secret Cabal 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

Corruption Card
If you would ever gain a card from the treasure market, gain the top card of the treasure deck instead.

A new hardback 7TV supplement to enjoy cinematic skirmishes inspired by all your fave 80s tv and films! Plus loads of new miniatures!

Everything you need to recreate your favourite 80s movie or show on the tabletop! 7TV comes from a love of action-adventure film and TV in all its variety. To capture this richness, our genre guides are themed around different types of TV shows and movies from different eras. Genre guides are a deep dive - exploring specific cinematic archetypes, tropes and shows - and this time we turn our attention to the 1980s!

You will need the 7TV Core Rulebook to play with this supplement.

Includes:
127 new profiles - a swathe of Neutral Stars and Co-Stars to reflect the 'blurred lines' of 80s anti-heroes. Plus 88 Extras to bolster your casts!

New script deck - 7TV: The 80s uses a deck of unique script cards to mark the countdown to the climax of the episode. Each script card contains an iconic quote and an in-game effect that benefits the player.

New props and maguffins - we add more buffs and iconic items to add to your games including BMX Bike, Fast Food, Demonic Box, Red Bandana and Time Travel Device!

New Episodes and Tropes - 12 Classic Episodes from the 8TV back catalogue offering a glimpse into the making of each production, with suggestions for using profiles and accessories from this genre guide to help you recreate key cinematic skirmishes from these classic episodes.

Originally available from backing Secret Cabal 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

Discard 2 cards from your hand to your discard pile and draw 4 cards from your deck into your hand.

Originally available from backing Secret Cabal 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

To use these cards, simply shuffle them in with the other cards before the game states.

Can be used with Keepers or Moments

boardgame: まっくらダンジョン (Makkura danjon)
description:
Explore the underground dungeon in this solo rogue-like game. Control the hero with up, down, left, right, A, and B commands. Collect relics scattered throughout the dungeon while defeating monsters lurking in the darkness.

The game of まっくらダンジョン (Dark Dungeon) comes with a clear file, multiple dungeons on grid paper, and a "dicil" (combination dice and pencil). By moving a transparent area of the folder over a map, the player has limited visibility as they explore the variety of ready-made maps. You can download "DLC" maps online and even create your own. Players also choose a difficulty level for each game and randomly assign locations for the relics and monsters, creating more replayability.

On each turn, the player writes down the combination of commands they will activate that turn, using the directional arrows (movement), A (Attack), and B (Defend). Combinations of commands allow you to shoot arrows or dash. As you take turns, you lose stamina points but gain skill points, allowing you to strengthen your skills or take more actions. You further increase your abilities by gathering relics, which function both as victory points and useful items, like tools and weapons.

Are you ready to challenge the Dark Dungeon? You can compete in a time attack, gathering as many relics as you can before your timer runs out. Or you can aim for a high score and try to gather all the relics before leaving the dungeon.

boardgame: Million Dollar Script: Setting Cards Promo Set
description:
Available from backing Secret Cabal 2023 Kickstarter campaign.

Set of 8 cards that allow you to choose (or draw randomly) a specific setting.

The front of the Setting card replaces the Heroic card during Character Creation and similarly, the back of the card replaces a Villainous card.

EN-NICHI (円日) aims to capture the fun of a Japanese festival as the players walk around the festival venue, visiting the stalls to play games, gain prizes and souvenirs, and catch the fireworks display.

Over the course of the game players walk among the 12 stalls, each hosting one of four card-based minigames as well as a specific way to gain or exchange minigame prizes or souvenirs. Minigame prizes can be scored at the end of the game or exchanged for high-value firework cards, while souvenirs can be exchanged for special omamori that can enhance the odds at specific minigames.

When the fireworks run out or the games empty up, the game ends, and everyone scores their winnings to determine the victor.

Serena, a girl who lives in an old Western-style mansion, hears mysterious voices at night when her parents are away. A group of people appear in front of her, claiming to be trapped in the mansion. They claim to be her allies and plan to find a way out together. Are they really her allies? While she still doubts, Serena joins their escape.

Recollection -リコレクション- is a semi-cooperative game of hidden identities. The main character of the story, Serena, and the five characters surrounding her act according to their own motives. The game will be different each time, so you can enjoy it even more by imagining the story as you play.

Players take on the roles of the characters, secretly divided into factions of allies aiming to escape and traitors trying to prevent an escape. Players also have a secret mission they must complete before the game ends. If players successfully complete quests in the revealed rooms, they can obtain items essential to their escape. But if they fail, there will be a penalty. Each character has their own physical strength and mental status, and if either of them reaches zero, they become incapacitated and will be unable to do anything for a period of time, so be careful!

Every 12 in-game hours, a story card will activate. Once all story cards have appeared, the time limit is reached and the game ends. Determine which players share your faction, work together, and achieve your victory conditions within the time limit!

In Class Craft (クラスクラフト), players take on the role of teachers, building their classrooms to ensure their respective classes score highest in the midterms and finals.

Each classroom consists of two rows, each with 4 seats, and the players play 1 student from their hand onto an empty seat on each of their turns. Most students are only good at certain subjects, and some have effects that can be activated when played in specific rows, such as switching the positions of other students or changing the subject being tested.

There are six teachers with different subjects that rotate in a specific order each game; memorizing the order and manipulating the flow of subjects is vital to scoring well at both the midterms and finals.

The most prized treasure of the Earth goddess Danu, the Cauldron of Blood was a sacred object that could offer up a never-ending supply of food. However, deep within its depths dwelt the spirit of the Goddess as Morrigan the Crone and her monstrous son Avagddu. Wielding the power of death, Morrigan could return a semblance of life to the fallen who were sacrificed to the cauldron. The Reborn were not truly alive but were more than capable of continuing the fight against their foes from beyond the grave.

Dedicated to Danu and to Lug the Sun God, Cathbad was the high druid of the Sessair and advisor to Sláine and his predecessor. Though many held him partly responsible for the capitulation of the Earth Tribes to the Fomorian tax collectors, he wielded considerable political and mystical power in the tribe.

—description from the publisher

You’re given a prompt and a pen. All you have to do is draw it. Except, there’s already something on your artboard. Some “thing” in fact. The only other rule is you must incorporate the “thing” into your drawing too. Get your teammate to guess the prompt in 30 seconds before the other team and see who has the dirtiest (or cleanest) mind!

—description from the publisher

Pin the tail on the donkey is so old school, am I right? If you're tired of the same old party games, try a different kind of donkey game. Get 4 matching cards and shout ""DONKEY!"" then hurry and grab a carrot or else slowly turn into a donkey. Lose one time, put up your left year. Lose two times, put up your right ear. Lose three times, and put on the donkey nose and give us your best ""HEE-HAW""! When the game is over, take a photo of the loser and share it with the folks at Brybelly. They'll sure get a kick out of it!

A prince among the alien Dark-Els, Elfric was an utterly depraved being that lusted after death and devastation. A being of magical energy, his mortal incarnation was almost impossible to destroy with normal weapons, and many were the battles where his body was pierced a dozen times and more by sword, arrow or spear without harm. Elfric’s magic also manifested itself in the form of a deadly gaze from a gem-like third eye in his forehead that could shatter armour and boil away flesh. Only with great effort and the aid of many allies was Sláine able to halt Elfric’s rampages across space and time, yet the demonic being was never utterly destroyed and will doubtless return to plague humanity another day.

Els were creatures from another dimension that exists at a right angle to our own. Dark-Els were evil beings, intent on spreading destruction for Crom Cruach. Sometimes these took the form of seductive human women to lure their prey into traps. They were spiteful, deadly fighters possessed of otherworldly power, often underestimated because of their appearance. Yet when slain their true forms manifested, as their bodies reverted into bug-like abominations.

Contains:

Elfric Serpent-Eye
3 x El-Women
1 x character card
1 x unit card

Of the children chosen for sacrifice to the Lord Weird, those that caught his eye in some way were spared and raised as Drunes. Once grown to adulthood these formidable warrior-witches serve as priestesses and bodyguards to Slough Feg, utterly dedicated to the Lord Weird and the worship of Crom Cruach. Three of the Badb, Nemon the Venomous, Fea the Hateful and Catha the Fury, fought Sláine when he was to be sacrificed to the worm god in a vast wickerman.

This boxed set contains the following Warlord Resin figures and plastic bases:

3 x Badb
1 x unit card

An ages-old enemy of the Earth Tribes inhabiting Tir Nan Og, the Fomorians were a race of sea demons from the icy waters of Lochlann to the north led by ferocious warriors such as Balor of the Evil Eye and Moloch.

Whether they were performing their own offensives against the Earth Tribes or working in conjunction with the Lord Weird Slough Feg's Drune forces, the Fomorians provided a stern test for Danu's warriors with many being slain or enslaved – Fomorians prizing the tears of humans as a delicacy.

This boxed set contains the following Warlord resin figures and plastic bases:

3 x Fomorian Sea Demons (hook & chain)
3 x Fomorian Sea Demons (harpoon)
3 x Fomorian Sea Demons (greataxe)
1 x Fomorian Sea Demons unit card
2 x Feat cards (Feint and Warding Way)

Under Balor were a number of other important Fomorians, charged with brutalising the population and leading the Fomorian armies. These foul creatures thrived on the misery of their victims, collecting human tears to savour. By their commands were many of Danu’s followers put to the blade, hanged or burned alive, simply for the joy of causing death and mayhem.

This boxed set contains the following Warlord Resin figures and plastic bases:

3 Fomorian Sea Demon Champions
1 x unit card

Worshippers of the Dark Gods such as Crom Cruach, the great time worm, the Drune Lords are evil priest-kings intent on wiping out Tir Nan Og, the Land of the Young. Committing a great number of slaughters and sacrifices in the name of their dark deities, the Drunes have already soured much of the land with their foul magics.

The armies of the Drunes comprise of the barbaric Skull Swords, Drune soldiers with hair breathing masks to protect themselves from the foul odours of their Drune Lord masters who rule the southern tribes.

This boxed set contains the following Warlord resin figures and plastic bases:

1x Skull Sword Champion (axe & shield)
1x Skull Sword Champion (great axe)
1x Skull Sword Champion (sword & shield)
3x Skull Sword (repeater crossbow)
3x Skull Swords (axe & shield)
1x Skull Sword Champion character card
1x Skull Swords unit card
2x Feat cards (Stubborn Block and Moon-torn)

Crom Cruach is the Time Worm, master of entropy and decay. With the power of the great worm the dead do not have to lie still but can be roused from their inanimation by Sour magic. They remember little but the most basic need to fight and kill, and their twitching limbs are of little skill compared to their prowess in life, but warriors of the Drunes can serve again as Half-Dead when summoned by a Drune Lord.

This boxed set contains the following Warlord Resin figures and plastic bases:

6 x Drune Half Dead
1 x unit card

Supreme leader of the Drune Lords, the Lord Weird Slough Feg, a now insane twenty-thousand year old former Horned God laid waste to swathes of Tir Nan Og in the name of his foul master Crom Cruach. Leading his armies of Skull Swords and his wicked priestesses against the tribes of the Earth Goddess, Danu. One such priestess was Medb, a young girl taken from her village as sacrifice but showed great promise to Slough Feg, becoming a Druness instead of perishing in a wicker man conflagration.

Following several clashes with her nemesis, Sláine, she returned under the guise of Megrim, casting a glamour over the King of the Sessair tribe and bending him to her will. Aided by her dwarf assistant, Robym, she caused untold trouble for the Earth Tribes.

This boxed set contains the following Warlord resin figures and plastic bases:

1x The Lord Weird Slough Feg
1x Medb
1x Megrim
1x Robym the Dwarf
3x Character cards

Facing the twin threats of the Drune Lords with their twisted Sour magic blighting the Land of the Young and invasions of Fomorian sea demons intent on enslaving mankind, the tribes of the Earth Goddess, Danu, are by necessity fearsome warriors.

Comprising many different tribes such as The Growling Shields, Tribe of the Shadows and the savage Sessair, the Earth Tribes often fight amongst themselves for dominance but are ultimately united in their devotion to Danu. Among the Sessair are a sect of warriors capable of undergoing a warp-spasm – a mystical battle frenzy allowing them to channel the power of the earth through their body and turning them into berserk, raging beasts, feeling almost impervious to wounds.

—description from the publisher

Amongst the Celtic Earth Tribes are mighty warriors who inspired their tribes to glory against the Fomorians and the Drune Lords. Sláine's former wife, Niamh, whom he was exiled from the Sessair for having a dalliance with, was an accomplished warrior and inventor. Mongan Axehead was Sláine's foster father, taking the place of the exiled Roth Bellyshaker.

The Tribe of Growling Shields was led by King Rudraige who had taken Niamh as his wife. Rudraige wielded one of the great treasures of the tribes – the Silver Sword of the Moon, whose terrible wounds could not be healed. Wielded by King Gann of Finias, the Flaming Spear of the Sun was a bloodthirsty treasure that returned, shrieking, to the hand of the thrower by command after passing through its target.

This boxed set contains the following Warlord resin figures and plastic bases:

1x Queen Niamh
1x Mongan Axehead
1x King Rudraige with the Silver Sword of the Moon
1x King Gann with the Flaming Spear of the Sun
4x character cards

boardgame: Sláine: Balor of the Evil Eye
description:
Possessed of a deadly, baleful gaze that scorched all before it, Balor was the warchief leading the Fomorian Sea Demons against their enemies in Tir Nan Og, the Earth Tribes. Often seen sat atop a ferocious war elk, Balor proved a fearsome enemy for the people of the Earth Tribes. Laying waste to swathes of the Land of the Young as they swarmed south from the icy waters of their home in Lochlann the Fomorians also allied with the Drunes against Sláine's people.

This boxed set contains the following Warlord resin figures and plastic bases:

1x Balor riding war elk
1x Balor of the Evil Eye character card

The god of the animals and the forest is Carnun, Lord of Beasts. Just as nature is both beautiful and cruel, Carnun cannot be considered as good or evil, but an elemental force that must be reckoned with and appeased. Both the Earth Tribes and the Drunes offer worship to Carnun, and in reply the god may send his bestial minions to do the bidding of a petitioner. However, while Carnun may despatch bears and great-antlered stags to gouge and trample the foe, there are many smaller creatures such as wolves, badgers and porcupines that can present quite a threat, or rats, snakes and toads that overpower their victims in a vicious swarm.

Contains:

Bear
Wolf
Porcupine
Wild Boar
1 x unit card

Each round begins by giving yourself a weird NICKNAME, like Katie The Hammer, or Captain Chris.

Each player then gets some DESCRIPTION cards and distributes them to the other players.

Then, each player reveals all of the descriptions about themselves and picks their favorite one.

The player who "wrote" it reveals themselves and collects a point.

Hope you have a good sense of humor about yourself!

—description by the publisher

The Masquerade expansion adds a masquerade ball to the ballroom during the third week of the Season Phase. In order to attend you have to collect a mask on the Hive Board. They are clear tokens that you may set on your Player Mat character. There are clear cards that overlay the Ton and L&C cards in the ballroom during the third week. They mask the people on the cards and they provide both a wild crest and allow you to select the influence of your choice to gain on your influence track. Additionally, the expansion adds a a new card to the L&C deck with a masquerade specific end game scoring goal.

description from kickstarter campaign

The dragon is dead and with your companions you stand in the hoard looking at unimaginable wealth. Knowing that you can’t possibly carry it all, and still battered from the epic fight with the dragon, you've agreed you’ll leave as soon as you hear the approach of enemies. With gold and silver all around you each of you sifts through the hoard for items of special value before returning to the group to divide and claim what you’ve collected. As each of you will undoubtedly leave with more wealth than you can spend in several lifetimes, you’re competing to collect the most impressive loot from this seemingly endless hoard.

Split the Hoard is strategic card game for 2 - 5 players and takes about twenty minutes to play.

In the game you take on the role of an adventurer who has achieved the pinnacle of any character’s career, the slaying of an ancient dragon. You and the other players are attempting to claim the best rewards from the hoard. You score points for treasures specific to your adventurers need and secret agendas. Negotiate, steal or bluff to ensure you get the loot you desire! The game ends when all the treasure has been split and the players with the highest score wins.

boardgame: Polarity of Power: The Game of Chaos
description:
Polarity of Power is a dynamic real-life-based card game that immerses players in a world of political intrigue, societal upheaval, and personal ambition. Navigate the complex web of power dynamics as you vie for control, but beware, as power always comes at a cost. Dive into the shadows of power and discover its true weight in this captivating game.

The game utilizes two main decks: the Tactics Deck and the Eco Deck.
Tactics Deck: Focuses on political actions and maneuvering. Includes:
Action Cards: Represent political actions with various effects.
Policy Cards: Enact social policies with lasting impacts.
Trick Cards: Allow for cunning tactics and manipulation.

Eco Deck: Focuses on economic development and industry management. Includes:
Industry Cards: Represent different economic sectors and generate national power.
Basic Attack Cards: Used to attack opponent's industries.
Basic Defense Cards: Used to defend against attacks.
Community Cards: Provide strategic actions with cooldown periods.

Additional Components:
Opportunity Cards: Introduce unexpected events and challenges.
Dice Cards: Used for random outcomes.
Role Cards: Define player (Hero) abilities and objectives.
National Power (NP) Tokens: Represent a nation's stability and strength.
Popularity (PT) Tokens: Represent public appeal and influence.
Action Point (AP) Tokens: Used to perform actions during turns.
Polarity Cards: Used in specific game modes.
Round Tracker Card: Tracks cooldowns, production, and industry strength or counting AP used per round.

Polarity of Power - A Game of Political Maneuvering and Economic Mastery
Step into the dynamic world of Polarity of Power, where cunning political tactics and shrewd economic strategies collide. Command two unique decks - the Tactics Deck and the Eco Deck - to outwit your opponents and claim victory.
The Tactics Deck is your arsenal for political maneuvering. Play Action Cards to influence the political landscape, enact sweeping changes with Policy Cards, and outsmart your rivals with clever Trick Cards. Will you sway public opinion, forge alliances, or orchestrate a cunning power play?
Meanwhile, the Eco Deck fuels your economic engine. Construct a thriving industrial empire with Industry Cards, engage in economic warfare with Basic Attack Cards, and defend your assets with Basic Defense Cards. Cultivate the support of communities through Community Cards and watch your nation prosper.
Choose your path to power in one of three captivating game modes:
1) Polarity of War: Form teams and protect your designated "polarity" player while aiming to dismantle opposing teams. The last polarity standing reigns supreme as the ultimate superpower.
2) Brawl Survivor: Every player for themselves! Navigate the treacherous political landscape, strategically utilize your cards, and outlast your rivals to emerge as the sole survivor.
3) 1 on 1: Engage in intense head-to-head battles, adapting to new roles every three rounds as you strive to outmaneuver your opponent and claim victory.

With unexpected twists and turns introduced by Opportunity Cards and the ever-present element of chance with Dice Cards, Polarity of Power guarantees an engaging and unpredictable experience. Manage your resources wisely, leverage the unique abilities of your Role Card, and forge your path to political and economic dominance.

Opportunity Cards in Polarity of Power
Opportunity Cards inject dynamism and unexpected twists into Polarity of Power, ensuring each game is a unique experience. These cards represent unforeseen events, challenges, or opportunities that can dramatically shift the political landscape.
Drawing Opportunity Cards:
Timing: Opportunity Cards are drawn at the beginning of each round, before any player takes their round.
Who Draws: In Polarity of War and Brawl Survivor modes, players take rounds drawing Opportunity Cards, following the established term order. For example, if Team A starts the round in Polarity of War, they draw the first Opportunity Card. In the next round, Team B would draw, and so on. Similarly, in Brawl Survivor, Player A would draw first, then Player B in the following round, etc.
1 on 1 Mode: Players alternate drawing Opportunity Cards at the start of each round.
Executing Opportunity Cards:
Immediate Effect: The effects of Opportunity Cards are resolved immediately upon being drawn. Players must adapt their strategies and actions accordingly.
One-Time Use: Once an Opportunity Card is drawn and resolved, it is removed from the game for that particular playthrough. This ensures that each game presents unique challenges and prevents repetition.

Examples of Opportunity Card Effects:
1)Sudden Economic Boom: Grants bonus National Power to all players with Industry Cards in specific sectors.
2)Political Scandal: Forces a player to discard cards or lose Popularity Points.
3)Natural Disaster: Damages Industry Cards or disrupts resource generation.
4)Technological Breakthrough: Provides a temporary advantage or unlocks new strategic options for a player.
5)Shifting Public Opinion / Issues: Influences Popularity Points for players based on their Role Cards or recent actions.

Opportunity Cards add a layer of excitement and unpredictability to Polarity of Power. They force players to think on their feet, adapt their strategies, and capitalize on sudden opportunities or overcome unexpected challenges. This dynamic element keeps players engaged and ensures that each game is a thrilling and unique experience.

Player Actions on Their Turn in Polarity of Power
A player's turn in Polarity of Power is a strategic dance of managing resources, playing cards, and maneuvering for political and economic dominance. Here's a breakdown of the actions a player can take during their turn:
1. Draw Cards:
Choose a Deck: At the start of their turn, the player chooses to draw from either the Tactics Deck (Action, Policy, Trick cards) or the Eco Deck (Industry, Basic Attack/Defense, Community cards). This choice allows players to tailor their strategy based on their current goals and the game state.
Draw Two Cards: The player draws two cards from their chosen deck, adding them to their hand.
2. Play Cards:
Card Selection: The player can choose to play any number of cards from their hand, as long as they have the required Action Points (AP) to activate them.
Card Types and Effects: Each card type has unique activation conditions and effects.
Action Cards: Immediately trigger political events or actions.
Policy Cards: Enact lasting changes to the game's rules or conditions.
Trick Cards: Played face-down and activated under specific circumstances to manipulate the game.
Industry Cards: Placed on the field to generate National Power and represent economic ventures.
Basic Attack/Defense Cards: Used to attack opponents' industries or defend against attacks.
Community Cards: Provide strategic actions and have cooldown periods.
3. Manage Resources:
Action Points (AP): Players start each round with a set number of AP (typically 5-7). Each card played has an AP cost, which is deducted from the player's available AP pool or Use the round tracker to mark the AP spent and keep track of your remaining AP for the turn.
(Players recover their Action Points (AP) at the beginning of each new turn. This means that any unspent AP from the previous turn do not carry over)
National Power (NP) and Popularity (PT): Players need to carefully manage their NP and PT levels, as reaching zero in either results in elimination. Industry Cards and certain card effects can increase NP, while Policy Cards and other actions can influence PT.
4. Other Actions:
Negotiate and Form Alliances: Players can engage in discussions and negotiate deals with other players, depending on the game mode and their current objectives.
React to Opponents: Players can respond to their opponents' actions by playing cards or utilizing abilities on their Role Card.
5. End Turn:
Discard Down to 2-3 Cards: At the end of their turn, the player must discard cards from their hand until they have a maximum of cards (typically 2-3)remaining. This ensures a dynamic flow and prevents players from hoarding cards.

The combination of card play, resource management, and strategic decision-making makes each player's turn in Polarity of Power a captivating and impactful experience. Mastering the various card types and their interactions is key to achieving victory in this dynamic and engaging game.

Game End and Victory Conditions in Polarity of Power
The climax of Polarity of Power arrives when specific conditions are met, leading to the game's end and the crowning of a victor. Victory conditions and the path to success vary depending on the chosen game mode, adding to the game's replayability and strategic depth.
1) Polarity of War:
Game End: The game concludes when only one "polarity" player remains on the stage. This means all other teams have had their polarity player eliminated by having their National Power (NP) or Popularity (PT) reduced to zero.
Winning: The team with the last surviving polarity player is declared the winner. Their ability to strategically protect their polarity while dismantling opposing teams leads them to triumph and the prestigious title of "Superpower."
2) Brawl Survivor:
Game End: The game ends when only one player remains on the stage. All other players have been eliminated by having their NP or PT depleted to zero.
Winning: The last player standing is crowned the victor, having outmaneuvered and outlasted all rivals in the fierce battle for political survival.
3) 1 on 1:
Game End: Similar to Brawl Survivor, the game concludes when one player's NP or PT reaches zero, resulting in their elimination.
Winning: The remaining player, having successfully navigated the challenges of shifting roles and strategic card play, emerges as the champion of this intense head-to-head battle.

General Gameplay Flow:
While the specific victory conditions differ across game modes, the core gameplay loop involves players strategically utilizing cards from the Tactics and Eco Decks to influence the political landscape, develop their economic power, and ultimately, ensure their survival or their team's victory.
1) Turns and Rounds: Players take turns within each round, drawing cards, playing cards from their hand, managing resources like Action Points (AP), National Power (NP), and Popularity (PT), and reacting to opponents' moves.
2) Opportunity Cards and Dice Cards: These elements introduce unexpected events and chance, forcing players to adapt their strategies and adding to the excitement.
3) Card Interactions and Abilities: Players leverage the unique effects of different card types and their Role Card abilities to gain advantages and outmaneuver their opponents.

In essence, Polarity of Power is a game of strategic decision-making, resource management, and adapting to a dynamic and ever-changing political and economic landscape. Players must carefully choose which cards to play, when to play them, and how to best utilize their resources to achieve victory in their chosen game mode.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Recollection -リコレクション- 新たなる来訪者 (Aratanaru Raihosha)
description:
"I'll repeat this over and over again, just to get you back..." A repeating world, an unchanging ending. New visitors appear in this closed world. Are they friends or enemies?

-リコレクション- 新たなる来訪者 (Recollection: New Visitors) adds new quests, new characters, new special items, and expanded rules to Recollection, a semi-cooperative game where allies and traitors attempt to escape from a Western-style mansion.

All New Content included through the Canvas Big Box Kickstarter campaign. (Trilogy Booster Pack not included with the Big Box and must be pledged separately.)


     10 New Art Cards illustrated by Luan Huynh
     4 New Scoring Cards
     4 New Award Cards
     1 New Signature Style Card
     4 New Painter Cards



    You must own all 3 Canvas games to use all the Trilogy Booster content.


—description from publisher

boardgame: Recollection Story -リコレクション ストーリー- 君に捧げる物語 (Kimi ni Sasageru Monogatari)
description:
Will she reach hope or despair at the end of this repeated story? "Hey, do you really think it's right to leave this world...?" The one who resembles me gently whispers in a sweet voice that if I run away from here, everything should be over. In front of the door that is blocked yet again, the one who resembles me laughs. “Where do you really want to be?”

Recollection Story -リコレクション ストーリー- 君に捧げる物語 (A Story Dedicated to You) adds new non-player characters, new special items, and gameplay mechanics to Recollection, a game where players attempt to escape a Western-style mansion.

This expansion allows Recollection to be played fully cooperatively and without the use of traitors. All characters work together to defeat the Familiar of Delusion lurking in the mansion within the time and obtain the Fragments of Truth, which will allow you to escape. However, if you fail to defeat the Familiar of Delusion within the time limit, Fragments of Ruin will accumulate. If you cannot collect all the "Fragments of Truth" within the time limit or if you collect 10 "Fragments of Ruin", the game will be over.

The ending of the story will change depending on whether you succeed or fail in your escape and how many Fragments of Truth you have collected. Each character reveals their own personal truth as well, which also changes depending on the ending you achieve as a group.

Take on the roles of witches and wizards and challenge each other to a broom race!

Each turn, draw a number of movement cards — which include actions like advancing, taking mana, and rolling the die — equal to the number of players and line them up in a row. Players then choose a card from their personal deck of numbered cards and reveal them simultaneously. These played cards are lined up in order beneath the movement cards, and each action is resolved from the highest-numbered card to the elowest. However, if two players play the same number (resulting in "butting"), they do not get to take an action and receive mana instead. Mana, which can be obtained in several ways, allows the player to roll the movement die to advance. This functions as a catch-up mechanism, with the first-place player paying 6 mana to roll and the fourth-place player paying 1 mana. The player who reaches the goal fastest wins.

In addition to the basic rules, there are advanced rules where player characters each have unique abilities as well as special actions on each space of the race course. There are also cooperative rules where players work together to escape the dragon!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
For those who like math! For those who are a little weak! Here we go!

Equal= is a trick-taking game with must-follow and trump cards suitable for children's learning, and equals spring. Place the cards you took and the four arithmetic tiles in your place, and the number calculated at the end of the game will be your score.

Welcome to your first day working for HowToDoAnything.biz. Our goal (well, our shareholder's goal) is to definitively build the world’s largest collection of "100% accurate" How-To guides.

Each player gets a How-To PROMPT, like "How To Make Money Fast", or "How To Survive an Animal Attack". The object of the game is to pitch your article to the CEO as a worthy addition to the site.

The twist? You'll be given an assortment of items (some you choose, some of which are random) and you must utilize this STUFF in your pitch. When it’s your turn, simply explain to the group how you could accomplish the task in your prompt using only the objects on your cards.

If the CEO likes your article the best, you get published! Will it make sense? Probably not! But we've got a site to build!

—description from the publisher

One thousand years after Theseus defeated the Minotaur, a mysterious hanging garden appears in Crete. People are disappearing, and there are rumors the legendary Minotaur has returned. A group gathers to enter the labyrinth. One seeks his lost compatriots, another of the treasure sleeping beneath Crete. Yet another seeks the reward offered by the king to slay the monster. Crete has truly become an island of desires.

In Minotaur Corridor, players travel the labyrinth of the crumbling hanging garden and find treasures with the help of Guardian Cards. The guardians also help fight monsters, defeat the Minotaur, and survive events. Cards may be traded between players, but a battle ensues if the other player refuses the trade.

You may intentionally call the Minotaur for battle, or it may attack when the monster spots a player or the Minotaur Roars card is revealed. Defeating the minotaur ends the game and awards a large treasure. The game may also end when the deck runs out, resulting in no one needing to face the minotaur, or if only one player is left alive.

— translated and adapted from the translated rulebook
designers: かいじゅうプリン (Kaiju Pudding)
artists: かいじゅうプリン (Kaiju Pudding)

description:
PowerPlay Pop is a new tabletop board game designed to immerse gamers in the captivating world of hockey team management and thrilling head to head gameplay. It combines strategic decision-making with rapid-fire competition, challenging players to assemble their dream team and dominate the ice.

In the game, players strategically acquire Player Cards with varying skill levels and salaries, while engaging in head-to-head Pop-games with rapid-fire action. The goal is to lead your team to victory through shrewd roster decisions and mastering the strategic elements of the game. A little puck luck doesn't hurt either.

Roll the 2 game dice to start your turn. Move your trophy token ahead on the board in the amount shown on the dice. Each team on the board has a 1, 2, 3, or 4 star rating that corresponds to the 4 poppers on the board. Pop the corresponding popper to represent the opponent's team score. Pop the corresponding one represent the player's team score. For every pop game points are awarded as follows: Win = 2 points for the winning team, TIE = 1 point for each team, LOSS = 0 points for the losing team.

With its innovative mechanics and immersive experience, PowerPlay Pop is a must-have addition to any sports fan’s gaming collection, inviting players worldwide to join the excitement and become part of the PowerPlay Pop team.

New missions and new rules for new dragonriders such as spellcasters and archers.

Hex tokens to play with four new dragons.

Extra 3'x3' mat compatible with the core mat to expand the play area for larger battles.

Components for four additional players, increasing player count to eight.

boardgame: Army of the Potomac: Campaigns of 1862 and 1864
description:
Army of the Potomac: Campaigns of 1862 and 1864 is the second volume in the Civil War Heritage series and the follow-up game to the innovative and acclaimed Rebel Fury. Army of the Potomac uses the same core rules as Rebel Fury, so players familiar with Rebel Fury will be able to jump right into the action. Each battle in Army of the Potomac is quick-set-up, quick-playing, and deeply interactive. The density of counters in each scenario is low, allowing you to see and experience the big picture of the battle.
