Army of the Potomac covers the battles of Spotsylvania II, North Anna River, Cold Harbor, and the entire Seven Days battle (McClellan vs Lee), including the prelude Seven Pines (McClellan vs Johnston) when Johnston's wounding brought Lee into the command of the Army of Northern Virginia. Each battle places you, the player, in the role of the Army Commander (Grant, McClellan, Lee, Johnston). You maneuver your army to find the enemy’s flanks, concentrate your forces for an attack, and determine where to commit your artillery assets.

Units are portrayed at the Infantry/Cavalry division level. The Civil War Heritage series game system features a new Zone of Influence/Zone of Control mechanic that controls unit formation (March/Battle) based on their proximity to your opponent. As your units close with the enemy, your forces naturally break into battle formation, where they then maneuver the last distance to engage. Unlike most hex and counter wargames, this system allows you total freedom to move units in any order multiple times, unleashing the full range of historical tactics in a simple and clean format.

Combat features a differential combat system with few but significant factors that capture the key features of Civil War division level combat. Units are rated for quality, artillery support, supporting units, and terrain. The game features artillery duels with the occasional Exploding Caisson result. The Civil War Heritage series combat system accurately depicts the ability of units to launch multiple waves of attacks on the same position, capturing the back-and-forth nature of many famous combat duels.

Army of the Potomac also includes a set of campaign rules that allow you to conduct operations from Spotsylvania to Cold Harbor featuring logistics and attrition. Also included are two bonus scenarios to allow owners of Rebel Fury to also fight Spotsylvania II and begin the Campaign scenario from Wilderness to Cold Harbor using their original Rebel Fury map.

Components:
    Five Paper Maps
    Three Countersheets
    Two Player Aids
    Two Off Map Displays
    One Turn Record Track
    Two 10-sided dice
    One custom 6-sided die
    One Rulebook
    One Playbook
    One 2" Box

-description from publisher

Carnival of Shadows: Sinister Showdown is a thrilling fusion of tableau-building, worker placement, action selection, and deck building mechanics. In this game, players immerse themselves in the dark art of crafting and manipulating custom carnival attractions, where every aspect contributes to the chilling atmosphere. From the haunting melodies of eerie music to the menacing presence of larger-than-life mascots prowling the grounds, players have the opportunity to create an immersive and unforgettable experience for their unsuspecting attendees.

Your objective is to construct the most captivating and sinister exhibit, earning the favor of Mr. Shadow by executing his nefarious commands in any way possible. With a diverse cast of asymmetrical characters, players construct and oversee their own tableau, recruiting followers and enticing townsfolk to achieve their individual or sometimes shared objectives. Whether your aim is to claim souls, sow chaos, or even protect the innocent, players must utilize their unique abilities and strategies to outmaneuver their rivals and establish dominance over the fairgrounds, all in the pursuit of appeasing their enigmatic master.

—description from the designer

Perpend is a connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white.

Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place one or two friendly stones on empty points. If you place two, they must be orthogonally adjacent to each other. Next, place enemy stones on all secluded empty points. A point is secluded if there is no free path along the lines of the board between that point and the edge of the board.

At the end of your turn, for any two diagonally adjacent stones of your color there must be another stone of your color adjacent to both, and there must be no 2×3 patterns of two empty points, two black stones a knight's jump away from each other and two white stones a knight's jump away from each other. Passing is not allowed, but, if you have no legal moves available, your turn is skipped.

You win if there is a chain of orthogonally connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

-description from designer

Nakatta is a drawless connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white.

Hard corners and naked attachments are illegal 2×2 patterns. A hard corner is made up of two diagonally adjacent stones of the same color, one stone of the
opposite color, and one empty point. A naked attachment is made up of two orthogonally adjacent empty points, one black stone, and one white stone.

Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty point without forming any hard corners or naked attachments. Passing is not allowed, but, if you have no legal moves available, your turn is skipped.

You win if there is a chain of orthogonally (horizontally or vertically) interconnected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

-description from designer

boardgame: Evil Dead 2: The Board Game – Deluxe Edition
description:
The Deluxe Edition combines the base game, the Evil Dead 2: The Board Game – Extras Pack, some quality of life graphical improvements, and new rules for solo and co-op game modes.

The cooperative version of the game lets you play either by yourself or with others in true cooperative fashion without the possibility of a traitor.
family: Movies: Evil Dead / Army of Darkness

description:
37 cards, 3 suits, each with the same number repeated only once. These card suits wind around each other like a DNA double helix.

LOVE(LA)PLACE is a deck of cards that includes the numbers 0 to 19 in 3 suits. However, each number only exists in two of the three suits (with the exception of 0, 10, and 19, of which there is only one of each, with 0 being an unsuited card). Rules for many games were available on the publisher's website following the LOVE(LA)PLACE's release.

Triangular Situation, the rules that shipped with the deck, is a must-follow trick-taking game for two or three players. As for each turn, Players must play a card that matches the led suit, with the 0 card acting as a wild that may be played at any time. Regardless of suit, the highest card wins, with ties going to the later card played. In a 2-player game, a player sets aside their winning card where it can be seen, because the round ends when a color has won three tricks. The final winning card of the round is set aside and the other cards are returned to the deck, and when a color has won three rounds the last player who won with that colors wins the entire game.

The rules for a 3-player game, except the lead passes to the left instead of alternating, the winning card for each round is recorded and mixed back in with the other cards,

Before each round begins, players may play one face-down card for a maximum of three throughout the game, which will work toward a tiebreaker. If no color wins three times before players run out of cards, players compete in a round-long tiebreaker involving any cards turned face-down.

Ward: Trading Card Game is a dice-driven fantasy trading card game that combines strategic deck-building with fast-paced, tabletop-style combat. Players summon creatures, equip them with weapons and magic, and engage in dynamic battles where attacks are resolved using six-sided dice. Designed for both casual and competitive play, Ward emphasizes direct creature combat without relying on traditional resource systems.

Set in the war-torn realms of Ward, the game features a deep, evolving storyline centered around ancient cosmic powers, invading empires, and legendary champions. Through major releases such as World of the Orcs and Undead Invasion, players explore conflicts involving the Cabal, the Empire of the Damned, and the mysterious Ancient Ones who manipulate life and death through the Soul Sphere. Each expansion adds new cards, mechanics, and narrative elements to the growing universe.

Gameplay focuses on building 30-card decks, managing card interactions, and adapting to changing battlefield conditions. The dice-based combat system introduces probability, momentum, and dramatic swings into every match, rewarding players who combine tactical planning with risk management. Deck construction supports thousands of possible combinations, encouraging experimentation and customization.

Unlike many traditional trading card games, Ward booster packs are designed for immediate “tear and play” use. Because the game does not rely on strict card combinations or mandatory resource engines, players can purchase three booster packs, build a complete 30-card deck, and begin playing right away. This design encourages accessibility and experimentation, allowing new and casual players to enjoy full matches without requiring extensive deck optimization.

Ward supports a wide range of play modes and player preferences. Players can compete in one-on-one matches, team rumbles, and organized tournaments, cooperate in solo and team-based boss raids, or progress through an RPG-style campaign mode inspired by tabletop role-playing games. Rather than forcing a single format, Ward allows players to use their cards to play their way, whether competitively, cooperatively, or casually.

Key Features:

• Dice-based combat using six-sided dice
• Trading card game with deck-building and customization
• No traditional resource or mana cards
• 30-card decks with up to three copies per card
• Hundreds of collectible cards across multiple expansions
• Competitive, cooperative, and solo gameplay modes
• Solo and team boss battles
• RPG-style campaign and progression system
• Team rumbles and multiplayer formats
• Deep fantasy setting and connected storyline
• Retro-inspired comic and fantasy artwork
• Official organized play and tournament support
• Accessible for new players with long-term strategic depth

Created in northern Minnesota by the Casey Brothers, Ward was designed to capture the excitement of classic 1990s fantasy and comic-book games while offering modern flexibility and replay ability. By combining storytelling, strategy, and dice-driven combat, Ward delivers a trading card game experience built around player choice.

Turn the Tides of Ward.

It is the witching hour. You should order your kunoichis to invade the homes of villains! Earn kobans (large Japanese golden coins) as a prize for the heads of villains killed by your kunoichis. Each night, you must launch one assault after another until the end of the game. But the key to winning may be deciding when to hold back...

In 10 Kunoichis, each player has a hand made up of the ten ninja cards. Villains are drawn and laid out, and players may choose one of their ninjas to attack. Choose carefully, because only certain ninjas can attack the different villains. When all players who chose a card are ready, everyone calls out "Seibai!" (Punish!) and reveal their cards. If only one player plays a card, they will defeat all the villains they can. If multiple players are involved, ninjas act from fastest to slowest, with lower numbers being faster. Each ninja defeats all the villains they can before the next ninja begins. Play continues until all ninjas are played or the villain deck runs out.

To keep everyone on their toes, each player may also declare "Ninpo!" once per game. Declaring Ninpo reverses the speed of the ninjas, with 10 becoming the fastest and 1 the slowest. The effect remains until another player declares "Ninpo!" and the speeds return to normal.

At the end of the game, the player with the most kobans is the winner. Keep an eye out for the round where the Evil Merchant appears, because he doubles the value of all villains currently in play!

— adapted from the publisher's description

Jamboree is a game all about crafting jam and filling orders for profit. It's a simple to learn money management, bag drawing, dice rolling, food themed game set in the Renaissance era. It's great fun created for the casual and family gamers alike.

Players take on the role of a Jam merchant who competes to fulfill customer orders. By rolling dice and gathering produce from the supply bag to fill jam jars as efficiently as possible without busting a jar, to gain lucrative profit and benefits for filling the orders. After three orders are fulfilled players go one more round. Add up the total of coins and VP on orders. Highest score wins.

-description from designer

OVERVIEW
Collect sets of flower cards to create ‘gardens’. This attracts colorful butterflies which count as points. The flowers and butterflies are all native to the Philippines.

It’s a family friendly card game for 2-4 players, which takes about 20 minutes to play. Featuring hand painted artwork created by local Filipino artists.

HOW TO WIN
At the end of the game the player with the most butterflies wins!

SETUP
Deal 3 flower cards to each player. Then place the remaining deck of cards to the side and reveal the top three flower cards, placing them in a line as cards that may be drawn.

Place the butterfly circle in the middle of the table and the butterfly deck on it. Reveal the top three butterfly cards and place them around the circle matching to their color.

Left of the dealer goes first.

ON YOUR TURN
Draw a flower card from one of the three revealed or the top of the deck.

Create a ‘garden’ by laying down 3 of the same color flower cards.

Flower cards come in 7 colors of the rainbow. There are also ‘double-color’ flower cards which may be one of two colors. And there’s also a ‘wild’ flower card that can be any color.

Collect any revealed wild butterflies that are the same color as your garden by laying them on your garden. 
In addition, you may STEAL all butterflies from an opponent’s garden IF it is the SAME COLOR and WEAKER. Determine a garden’s strength by counting the total number of ‘butterfly symbols’ on the corners of each flower that match your garden’s ‘color’. (solid color flower cards are ‘stronger’ than double-color or wild-color flower cards).

At the end of your turn reveal a new wild butterfly from the top of the deck. A player with a matching color flower garden may immediately collect this butterfly. But they must pay attention because if they miss it before the next person’s turn then another player may steal it for their themselves!

The game ends when there are no wild butterflies around the wild butterfly circle. This usually happens when atleast one garden of every color has been made. The game may also end when there are no butterfly cards in the deck.

There are also 7 ‘sparkly butterflies’ which have a special ability that activates when they are collected by a player. See the reference cards for these. Example: “draw 2 flower cards”, or “reveal a flower card and steal this color butterfly”.

-description from publisher

In 惨劇の夜会 (Tragic Soirée), you and your friends work together, using your different abilities to escape from a Western-style mansion.

It is a game of one versus many, in which a single player acts as the wandering killer and his murderous maids, while the other five players hide and try to escape. The team fighting to escape should explore each room of the mansion and look for the items necessary for escape. The killer player must hunt the others until they are too injured to escape.

In the additional Last Night mode of play, the escaping team may add another player in the role of a detective, raising the maximum number of players to seven. To win in this scenario, the escaping players must open a safe before fleeing the mansion.

Sibling is a game of creating larger groups of pieces on the board.

Except for the first move, players place two pieces at a time. These two must be on the same line on the board.

When no more pieces can be placed, the second largest groups are compared to each other and the owner of the larger group wins. Therefore, you need to grow your two large groups so that they do not merge with each other.

This game is included in Generic Board "Hexagonal" set, and won 2nd place in the BEST COMBINATORIAL 2-PLAYER GAME OF 2024.

Snaketrail is a loop-building game.

Players add shared pieces to the board on their turn, but they must always be added next to the piece placed by the opponent just before. When a loop of shared pieces is formed, the player with more pieces of his or her color inside the loop wins.

This game was inspired by Bill Taylor's Slimetrail and Don Green's Snailtrail.

This game included in Generic Game Discs Color Pack.

The Weakness Expansion includes 18 alternative weakness cards that can combine or replace the core game Weakness cards to change the difficulty of the game.

Weakness cards make the game more interesting, affecting your hand management. The crew members are not superhuman, so they can be affected by the horrors of the incursion. Combine different weakness types to increase the immersion of the game.

—description from the designer

Nuts Sorting is a game using six colored pieces representing nuts.

Each player is responsible for three colors each and moves a piece to an empty cell or removes a group of any of their pieces. The player who has a single group per his/her color wins the game.
 
This game is included in Generic Game Discs Color Pack.

Fruits Platter is a game in which players put together six colored pieces representing fruits according to their colors.

The direction of movement is determined by the color of the piece, and the pieces jump in a straight line toward the corresponding edge. Pieces that are jumped over are moved to the empty cell as a row.

The player who has all three colored pieces in his/her charge in one group per color wins.

This game is included in Generic Game Discs Color Pack.

Hunt the most wanted enemies and complete objectives as a team and your crew will obtain additional benefits during the raid stage.

The Bounty expansion introduces and additional game mode where players work together as a team to complete an objective.

The bounty cards are reversible cards with an objective in front, and a supply type on the reserve. When the incursion team completes the objective, flip the card. At any time any during their turn, any player can claim the reward with benefits for all the team.

—description from the designer

Candy Chain is a game of six colored pieces representing candies.

Each player is responsible for three colors and removes one or two pieces from the board per turn. However, all pieces on the board must remain connected as a single group.

The first player to remove all two of his or her colors from the board wins.

This game is included in Generic Game Discs Color Pack.

Sign your own contract to complete individual objectives and obtain large amounts of resources and credit on the Nova Nasau Black Market.

The Contracts expansion introduces and additional game mode where players can choose their own individual contract in a push your luck mechanic. When the incursion step ends the players can check in the black market phase if they completed the contract, to obtain resources and loot.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Long Dark Sea: Dark Side Expansion
description:
The Dark Side Game Mode introduces 52 alternative copies of enemies, locations and events with the dark back cards to make even more difficult and unpredictable the core game.
 The dark back card mechanic makes the game more unpredictable because you don’t know what are in the other side of the card and also makes the enemies more hard because they are affected by eclipses on the Luminara System.
 This expansion is designed for those who are not afraid of the dark.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Lagobond Nails and Lagobond Hoppers
description:
This is a set of three promo cards, that came with the Usagi miniature, which itself is not part of the game.

The set contains two gear cards and a glossary card that explains their use.

The Lagobond Hoppers allows a survivor to "hop" around the battlefield, while the Lagobond Nails are a weapon that provide some control over future attacks.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Lordsruin Promo Cards
description:
Lordsruin - Titus is packaged in a graphic box that includes:

1 x Lordsruin - Titus hard plastic miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and scenic insert
1 x Origin Branch imdomitable King monster resource
1 x Lordsruin pattern
1 x Lordsruin pattern gear
1 x Install guide
1 x Large matte art print

In the strobing darkness, Titus struggled up the dizzyingly forking limbs, groping blindly, testing the unyielding branches with his wrenching grip. As he tugged at the frozen bark, a bolt of soundless lightning arced, lurching downward, striking the joint beneath his grip. It exploded soundlessly, impossibly bright, sending him flying. Titus sailed through the charged air, clutching his prize in one fist and swinging wildly with the other. As he crossed from within to without, a lucky blow collapsed the monstrous entity into a sizzling heap, the peal of its crown against the ground breaks the oppressive silence.

The indomitable monsters that stalk the darkness unchallenged tend to grow features not normally found in younger specimens of the species. Survivors brave and lucky enough to survive encounters with these monsters gain indomitable resources. These unusual components inspire new configurations of gear worthy of these hard won treasures.

Arkade is based on a simple and intuitive concept of dice rerolling with an Alien attacker seeking to reach the enemy defense lines, and a defender aiming to eliminate all opposing ships. Two game modes exist, a strategic mode with bonus tiles to unlock, and a frenetic arcade mode where both players will play at the same time.

The final chapter in the Euthia trilogy, Cruel Frost brings the epic adventure that is Euthia to a chilling climax.

Euthia is an open world fantasy RPG board game for 1-4 players you can play co-op, competitively, or solo. The Cruel Frost expansion introduces challenging new scenarios, hair-raising new enemies, and unique new heroes.

The story centers around a malevolent Spider Queen who’s seized control of forbidden ice magic, and now threatens to plunge Euthia into frozen darkness. Take on the challenges as one of the existing heroes, or become one of the new heroes introduced in Cruel Frost, each with their own branching path.

As well as new replayable scenarios, Cruel Frost introduces icy new mechanics to your base game. Playable with almost any scenario, the Ice Claws module brings raging ice storms to Euthia, transforming your modular map with new frozen tiles and paving the way to fresh challenges and new opportunities.

Moving through ice storms will cause frostbite, but will also grant access to powerful ice magic, changing the way you play. Out in the snow, you’ll encounter the new Ice Entity, offering tantalizing rewards to the brave.

Luckily, with the Ice Claws module comes the arrival of the Ice Clans. These guardians of ancient wisdom will share their knowledge with your heroes to help them withstand the cold, revealing new ways to gear up, heal, and learn new abilities.

Speaking of gearing up, Cruel Frost introduces an entire new stock of items for traders, adding even more depth to equipment upgrades. Not only are there more levels and options (thanks to new items and four new gem types), but some armor can also now be upgraded with elemental essences, introducing powerful new effects in the advanced stages of the game.

Aqua Tofana is a strategic 18-card game set in 17th-century Italy, themed around the famous poison that gives the game its name. In the game, players simulate a tense dinner between a married couple, with each player taking the role of either poison or antidote. The goal is to have the most cards of their role visible in the playing area by the end of the game. The game's premise revolves around Giulia Tofana, a famed cosmetologist who secretly elaborated and sold lethal poison to abused wives seeking a way out of violent marriages. The game's setting and design evoke a sense of historical tension and social commentary on gender violence and the resilience of women in patriarchal societies.

The game is played with 18 cards, each with poison on one side and antidote on the other. Players then take turns placing cards orthogonally adjacent to existing cards, applying the flipping effect represented on the card played. These effects can cause cards in the playing area to flip, changing the visible role and impacting the game's outcome. The gameplay symbolizes a confrontation between doses of poison and antidote.

Through its historical theme and social commentary, Aqua Tofana brings attention to the enduring fight against gender violence, promoting solidarity and resilience among women. The game’s design and artwork, alongside the unique gameplay mechanics, offer a thoughtful and engaging experience for players while underscoring an important message about equality and justice.

—description from the designer

On this occasion, with the help of our publisher and Pascal Quidault, who illustrated the game, we are happy to offer you a completely free SOLO MODE, to download, print and cut yourself at home.

I am only a fan of solo modes if they meet 3 requirements:


     the game must not turn into a gas factory


(everything must remain as simple as possible)

     The gaming experience should be analogous to playing against real opponents


(I hate when a single player mode ultimately leads to playing - another game, different from the one I like in “real” games

     you have to be able to lose against the game


(I hate when a single player mode simply asks the player to try to beat his record from previous games. To have the pleasure of winning, you have to run the risk of defeat. Not simply making a lower score than the previous time )

It is in this direction that Charles and I know how to work.

And I don’t think we messed up too much?
Try it and tell us what you think!!

—description from the designer

A cette occasion, avec la complicité de notre éditeur et de Pascal Quidault, qui a illustré le jeu, nous sommes heureux de vous proposer un MODE SOLO complètement gratuit, à télécharger, imprimer et découper vous-même à la maison.

Je ne suis adepte des modes solos que si ils répondent à 3 impératifs:

le jeu ne doit pas se transformer en usine à gaz
(tout doit rester le plus simple possible)
L’expérience de jeu doit être analogue à celle du jeu contre des adversaires réels
(je déteste quand un mode solo conduit finalement à jouer à un autre jeu, différent de celui que j’aime en « vraies » parties
il faut pouvoir perdre contre le jeu
(je déteste quand un mode solo se contente de proposer au joueur de tenter de battre son record des parties précédentes. Pour avoir le plaisir de gagner, il faut courir le risque de la défaite. Pas simplement faire un moins bon score que la fois précédente)
C’est dans cette direction qu’avec Charles nous savons travaillé.

Et je pense qu’on s’est pas trop plantés ?
Essayez et dites nous ce que vous en pensez !!

—description from the designer

In the Свечеварение("Candlemaking") board game, players will cook candles from a variety of components, including wicks, colored wax, fragrances and molds.

In the game there are candle cards and ingredient tokens. At the beginning everybody gets 5 cards with candles. Players create candles by taking component tokens from the game table.

The fastest and most accurate player wins by collecting all the candles in exact accordance with cards.

—description from the designer

変幻トリック ("Protean Trick")is a trick-taking game that features constantly changing trump suit and rank (strongest card). The way you play your hand is may follow, and you score points by predicting how many tricks you will win in one round.
publishers: クードゥーラパン (Queue de Lapin)

description:
On the swaying ball. Both the elephant and the rules are shaking! While trying to get rid of all the cards in his hand as quickly as possible, Elephant on Ball must also think about leaving a valid hand in order to win as many mini-games as possible. There are three types of rules when playing cards, and it is important to use the cards in your hand so as not to be thrown out by the rules that change with each mini-game.

Elephant Ball Balancing Act is a shedding/trick-taking games where you try to win the mini games as well get rid of your cards. The trick to not falling off the top of a swaying ball is to not rush and move unnecessarily.

boardgame: ゲームニャーケット99 (Game Marcat 99)
description:
A group of cats decided to create a board game each and distribute it on Game Marcat. In order to widely distribute your work: the game system and theme match, artwork is excellent, and you increase your awareness through advertisement.

Game Marcat 99 is a trick-taking game for 3 to 4 players. The winner is the cat who can create an excellent game and distribute it the most on Game Marcat.
publishers: 三ツ星まかろん (Three Star Macaron)

description:
Using a standard deck of playing cards, players manage their shop's inventory and negotiate trades with customers who arrive with specific demands and offers. Each interaction involves four steps: assessing the customer's offer, crafting a suitable counteroffer from the available items in the shop, verifying the trade's legitimacy, and restocking the shop shelves.

Successful trades earn positive feedback from customers, enhancing the player's standing within the guild. However, failure to meet customer demands or declining trade offers can lead to negative feedback, resulting in a loss of guild standing. If a player's standing falls too low, they face immediate removal from the guild.

—description from the designer
The special 'Mickey's Movie Madness' rule forces players to name 4 characters from a chosen Disney movie or they must draw 4 cards!

boardgame: 箱庭少女 / Deep Forest (Hakoniwa Shōjo / Deep Forest)
description:
In a miniature garden world created and closed off by a witch's curse, 13 girls whose memories were stolen fell into darkness and became puppets of the witch. Work together, recover the girls' memories, escape the witch, and save everyone!

In the cooperative game 箱庭少女 / Deep Forest (Miniature Garden Girls / Deep Forest), players take on the role of birds in a miniature world. At the start of the game, only the players and the witch move around the board. Girls may appear as themselves when their memories are revealed, or they may enter the game as marionettes, still under the witch's control. Shadows serving the witch may also appear to threaten the players. The witch and her allies roam the map to capture players, and the players lose if they are all caught.

Once a girl has her memory back, players may lead her to the church, the one safe space. Rescuing a girl grants the player special abilities that will help as the game continues. Once all the girls have been rescued, players must escape through the Gate of Liberation. But be careful: If you are captured and the other players have escaped, there will be no one to rescue you!

If players are captured, or if a player is captured after all the other players have escaped, players receive the game's bad ending. If the players rescue all the girls and escape the miniature garden world, they receive the normal or the good ending depending on their score.

boardgame: Sea of Legends: Legendary Rewards Pack
description:
A bonus for Kickstarter Backers for backing Sea of Legends: Vengeance of the Empires campaign.

The normal crew gets shuffled in with the other crew and lovers get shuffled in with the other generic lovers. The legendary crew are held off to the side to only be used when the app asks you to use them.

Portici Magica is a two-player card game that combines elements of strategy, luck and set collection into a fast-paced and addictive challenge, making it perfect for short but intense gaming sessions.

In a magical city, players must collect orbs and activate locations to accumulate victory points. At the start of the game, the 18 cards are shuffled and laid out on the table (partly covered, partly not) in four rows that form the City. Each row is arranged offset from the previous one.

During the game, players take turns picking a free card, i.e. with no other cards underneath. Each collected card can be either placed in the player's inventory, to add orbs and amulets, or activated if you have the necessary orbs in your inventory.

The game ends when all City cards have been collected. Players add up the points from the activated cards and additional points based on the type of amulet most present in their inventory. The winner is whoever has the highest score.

—description from the designer

A solo statistical replay game covering the events of the 2016 Olympic Games in Rio de Janeiro. The game uses charts and dice along with a set of rules for each individual sport and a data sheet with the competitor ratings.

The events covered are: Diving, Swimming,, Open Water Swimming, Synchro Swimming, Water Polo, Archery, Athletics (all track and field,, Marathon, Walk, Decathlon, Heptathlon, Badminton, Basketball, Boxing, Canoeing, Canoeing Slalom, Cycling Road and Track, Mountain Bike, BMX, Equestrian, Fencing, Field Hockey, Football, Gymnastics, Rhythmic Gymnastics, Trampoline, Handball, Judo, Modern Pentathlon, Rowing, Rugby Sevens, Sailing, Shooting, Table Tennis, Taekwondo, Tennis, Triathlon, Volleyball, Beach Volleyball, Weighlifting, Wrestling.

This expansion contains 3 new scenarios related to the main story. You will embody Victory, Javier, and Diego, three brothers and skilled veterans who have to complete a crucial mission to fight the alien invasion. This task requires skills and military tactics.

Features:
Customize weapons - Through the craft action, you may customize your weapons, unlocking special attacks.
Companions - Characters may have a companion, each one provides a unique ability to be used during the game.

Contains 1 rulebook, 30 additional cards, 3 storybooks, 55 additional map cards, 15 story & rule cards, 1 chronicle diary, 1 utility punchboard, and 2 secret boxes.

Sight is a game played on an Alquerque board using the line of sight mechanics.

Players place their pieces on the available intersection, and if there is friendly pieces on the "line of sight" of this placement, another pieces are placed on top of those pieces to form stacks.

If the player already has his or her own stacks at the beginning of their turn, then must move a piece from one of the stacks to the next available intersection. The player who has no legal move loses the game.

This game is included in ''Generic Board Alquerq'' set.

Alquad is a pattern building game using the Alquerque board.

Players attempt to create a regular square by adding pieces to empty intersections and placing their own pieces at their vertices.

However, even if you complete a square, you lose if your opponent creates a larger rectangle on your next turn.

This game is included in ''Generic Board Alquerq'' set.

Collapse is a game inspired by block breaker games.

Players capture pieces by moving their piece in a straight line and colliding it with the opponent's piece. The player who cannot capture the opponent's piece first loses the game.

This game is included in ''Generic Board Alquerq'' set.

The seasons are changing, and the event that determines the tone of school life has arrived ─ yes, it's time for a house change! You gather the students to start a new life in the dormitory you oversee. Students who want friends with similar hobbies, students who want someone with similar passions, students who want more variety in their community...  Build a sisterhood that satisfies all their requests and build the reputation of your dormitory family!

In Planetary Sisters, players draft a set of cards representing students with a variety of interests and contributions. Each player has a personal deck that they draw their hand from, with their hand representing the dormitory full of students. The cards in their hand are used to acquire cards from the central tableau. Players must decide which cards they would like to get rid of, but those cards may be taken by other players. Players must also take into account how the cards they use and acquire relate, because the difference between them affects who will be start player for the following round.

After players have drawn all their cards and drafted their full dormitory, cards are scored. Cards show one or more elements; there are common elements, such as Food and Animals, and less common elements, like Axe and Shield. There are also rare, unique elements, like Knight and Queen. Points are scored using special formulas involving the number of elements and the number of cards containing those elements. Bonuses are rewarded based on how the player's dormitory related to the cards left in the tableau. The 2-player rules add variety to the way a player's final hand and the tableau create a score.

Game set including a board with the front side for Arquelque and the back side for Fanorona.

Also included are the rules for Alquerque and Fanorona, as well as the original games Alquad, Sight, and Collapse that can be played on these boards.

Set of additional colored pieces for the Generic Board series, including rules for the four original games Snaketrail, Nuts Sorting, Fruits Platter, and Candy Chain, which use pieces of three to six colors.

Reigning Unicorns  is a game where players race to be the first to get rid of all of their cards. This is done by performing the card's actions with other players that are performing the same action. The first player to get rid of all of their cards is the Reigning Unicorn and gets to wear the Unicorn Headband. The first player to win 2 rounds wins the whole game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Monopoly: Star Wars – Return of the Jedi
description:
Imagine traveling to Tatooine, Dagobah, Endor, and the second Death Star with the Monopoly: Star Wars Return of the Jedi Edition game! Players must secure as many locations, transports, and droids as they can in an epic battle against the Galactic Empire. The more property a player secures, the more credits they'll be able to collect from other players. Players can use Speeder Bikes and X-Wings to strengthen their position. But beware the might of Darth Vader and the Emperor! The last player with credits when all other players have lost theirs, wins!

—description from the publisher

Gather ’round, Bridgerton fans! This edition of the Monopoly game features artwork, gameplay, and components inspired by the series on Netflix.
In this twist on classic Monopoly gameplay, players move around the board buying as many properties as they can to raise their social standing in the Ton.

—description from the publisher

Danger! Moon mission astronauts-- race to safety first to win!

A galactic spin on the beloved game. Race against your fellow opponents, to save your astronauts. Roll die to avoid losing fuel, oxygen, and your rover. Be the first player to get all your astronauts into the airlock!

In the year 2133, Earth is ravaged following "The Collapse". Humanity finds itself scattered amongst the Solar System. A lone planet orbits into our reach, Eden. A strange planet that houses mysterious ruins and an astonishing new element, code named "Edenite".

The Council of Humanity implores 6 of the most renowned factions, each with the task of serving as stewards of this new marvel.

'EDEN' is a strategic wargame. You, the player, control 1 of 6 said factions. Station your Ark on the map, and protect it at all costs. Players will fight to control territories on the map using their Battle Deck, waging the lives of their units. Spend credits to build structures and use Hero Abilities that will grant you special perks. Manage your resources well, and time your offenses right.

Each turn you will move your forces, utilize your assets, and 'BATTLE' your opponents.

Wield your Battle Deck against your foes and refine it. Evolve your Hero by equipping Hero Abilities, and negotiate with allies and enemies.

Be the last player with your Ark intact.
Or
Be the First to Capture 3 others.

UNITE, DIVIDE, CONQUER.

—description from the publisher

Your girlfriend exists in a highly probabilistic, quantum mechanical state. Whether she is good at studying, what she does after school, and her age are all uncertain. You must identify your girlfriend's attributes within the quantum fluctuations and make them converge on a single person..

In 量子カノジョ ("Quantum Girlfriend"), each player has one of six heroine cards on a stand, and they are the only player who cannot see which card it is. Each heroine card has three attributes: how good they are at studying, what she does after school, and her age. As the round progresses, players try to decide if the attributes in the center of the table apply to the heroine card that they cannot see. On a player’s turn, they either place an attribute card in the center of the table or call out “Observation!” If more than one attribute answering the same question are placed, they are stacked, creating the possibility that any of those attributes could be true.

Once “Observation!” is declared, all players look at their heroine card. If more than one attribute card of the same type are in the center of the table, those cards are shuffled and a random one is chosen to be included in the final attributes. If the final attributes apply to only one of the heroine cards in front of the players, that player receives 1 point. If the cards apply to more than one heroine card in play, then no player receives points. When a player receives their second point, they are the winner.

boardgame: 魔王様のお引越し (Maō-sama no o Hikkoshi)
description:
Moving into the Demon Queen's castle began with the Demon Queen's reckless behavior. However, her goblin's stats include Smartness 3, so they stack their luggage vertically. Will the baggage pass through the door or not? If it doesn't pass, will the goblins be burned by the Demon Queen's hellfire!?

Players take on the role of the Demon Queen's goblins, and it's their job to decide if items in the real world around them will pass through the castle's doors. At the beginning of each round, a door card is drawn. A corresponding colored string shows the physical size of that door opening, and each player may examine that string for 5 seconds. Then a command card is drawn, with an item such as the top of a desk or the length of a curtain. The designated player chooses an item in the real-life environment that matches the item. If no item matches the command card, the turn ends and the next player begins by drawing a new command card.

Players vote whether the item or the door opening is larger. If all players vote the same way, the turn ends. If the decision is split, the item is measured and the correct players each receive a point. If only one player is correct, they gain extra points depending on the round. The game ends once round 4 is completed, the command cards run out, or a player scores their seventh point. The player with the most points at the end of the game is the winner.

Jump into the driver’s seat and shift into gear. This is your chance to outlast your rivals and end the day in Victory Lane!

Victory Lane is a quick-playing, push-your-luck dice game. Players must balance upgrading their car while trying to maintain position on the track.

Object of the Game: Be the first to complete three laps while navigating dangerous corners, managing your car’s resources and planning for the perfect Pit strategy.

In this two-player strategy game, each peg placed on the board has an "Aura" which influences the spaces and pegs around it. By means of careful placement, an empty space can be claimed, or an occupied space can be turned into extra points. As more areas of the board come under your control, you approach the end game with confidence.  Auras is an abstract game which requires visualization of many possible patterns, and deciding which is best, defense or offense, plan ahead or take control?

—description from the publisher

Desperate Rescue  is a 2-player abstract peg game similar to Norse tafl games. The players have asymmetrical forces and different goals. One player is attempting to rescue a prisoner from a dungeon, and the prison guards are out to thwart their efforts. The circular board adds a further twist to the game play as the rescuers attempt to open a path of escape for the unfortunate inmate of the castle keep. The guards, however, need to ensure that this doesn't happen. The basic moves include the traditional jump-and-capture variety but with some restrictions.

—description from the publisher

For those who stumble into the Cave of Djinns, there is only one way out. Should you fail to solve the djinns’ riddles, you will surely suffer their wrath.

Cave of Djinns is a solo-only 6-card expansion to the Mysticana deck. In this evolving puzzle, the player must solve the djinns’ riddles through careful card placement. Each of the 6 double-sided djinns are placed in a loose pyramid shape, with space around each djinn to play a card in each orthogonal direction. The player draws 2 cards and plays them both in sequence each turn. Each djinn shows a specific arrangement of cards around them which must be fulfilled to solve their riddle and earn an immediate one-time bonus. Solved djinns are covered by an adjacent Mysticana card. Complete all 6 riddles to escape the cave with wits intact!


Cave of Djinns is a solo tile-laying puzzle with action-chaining.

Mysticana is a Foundation Deck: an 18-card deck that includes multiple core games using just the base cards with even more games added through small expansions. The deck features cards with 6 ranks across 3 suits (elements) which have a “rock-paper-scissors” hierarchy. While each game might make use of the deck’s design in different ways, the flexible foundation enables new games to fit in seamlessly alongside previous entries.

The world is full of long-forgotten items of power. Will you harness their enchantments or gamble on your own fortunes to prevail?

Arcane Artifacts is a multiplayer 6-card expansion to the Mysticana deck. In this lightweight game of chance and skill, each player receives 2 cards and hopes to have the best combination for the round. After the initial deal, players may discard 1 of their cards to draw a replacement, then each player decides whether to stay in the play or resign for the round. Once all players have committed, a new card is flipped; this determines scoring for the round and also changes the value of each Avatar (rank ⟐). Players reveal their hands to see who holds the best score. Each round also features one of 12 Artifacts, which offer a bonus to the player's hand who meets their criteria most effectively. Rounds are cumulative, and the first player to win a round and have 30+ points is declared the final victor!


Arcane Artifacts is a game of luck and skill to make the best hand using powerful artifacts.

Mysticana is a Foundation Deck: an 18-card deck that includes multiple core games using just the base cards with even more games added through small expansions. The deck features cards with 6 ranks across 3 suits (elements) which have a “rock-paper-scissors” hierarchy. While each game might make use of the deck’s design in different ways, the flexible foundation enables new games to fit in seamlessly alongside previous entries.

Duckit is a trick taking card game for three players that uses part of a standard 52 card deck.  Each round, a duck rank and trump suit is established.  The 4's are penalty cards, and players lose points for each one they capture UNLESS they can capture all of them.  In that case, they lose nothing and their opponents are penalized.  The first person to capture a 4 gets the quacker - a bonus card remaining after the deal.  This card might be useful in getting all of the 4's, or it might help you avoid them.

Original and fun game that will test the degree of knowledge and complicity that exists in your relationship and that of your friends. If you win, you are the ideal couple, if you lose you will have had a good time. The two of you answer a question determined by the dice and if you fail you will advance one square on the board. The goal is not to move forward. What we guarantee you is a time of fun.
To find out what type of question you have to answer, each pair will roll the dice. The game contains pencils and paper to write down the answers within a time that will mark the hourglass. The pawns are heart-shaped, and each pair must choose a different one. If you win, you are the ideal couple, if you lose you will have had a good time. Contains 386 question cards. Recommended for +18 years.

Note the box player count of 2-5 is for teams of two, so the actual player count is 4-10.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Original y divertido juego que pondrá a prueba el grado de conocimiento y complicidad que existe en vuestra relación y en la de vuestros amigos. Si ganáis, sois la pareja ideal, si perdéis habréis pasado un buen rato. Responded los 2 a una pregunta determinada por el dado y si falláis avanzaréis una casilla del tablero. El objetivo es no avanzar. Lo que os garantizamos es un rato de diversión.
Para saber qué tipo de pregunta te toca responder cada pareja tirará el dado. El juego contiene lápices y papel para anotar las respuestas dentro de un tiempo que marcará el reloj de arena. Los peones tienen forma de corazón, y cada pareja deberá elegir uno diferente. Si ganáis, sois la pareja ideal, si perdéis habréis pasado un buen rato. Contiene 386 tarjetas de preguntas. Recomendado para +18 años.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Red Tides: A Tale of New Caribbea
description:
Red Tides: A Tale of New Caribbea is a co-operative boss-battling naval strategy game. You and your fellow pirate captains begin with small ships, basic cannons, and a humble crew. Over the course of ten rounds, you hunt merchant ships, gather plunder, upgrade your vessel, and face increasingly powerful foes — including the dreaded Pirate Lords. Your ultimate goal is to take down the tyrannical pirate king John Davies and his monstrous flagship, The Dead Drifter.

Combat features tactical movement and dice-driven volleys at different ranges (long, medium, close), with opportunities to board and seize enemy ships. Plunder gains you cannons, gold, and upgrades, while faction-specific powers and fate dice add strategic flair.

The game escalates across three acts from scrappy survival to epic naval warfare, culminating in a final showdown. Win, and New Caribbea is free; lose, and you sail again another day.

—description from the designer

How to achieve balance in complicated situations?
A story about a group of characters who found it difficult to find balance. 
How to play? You have to accompany the characters in 3 moments:

     Identify when emotions appear
     Understand what happens to our body
     Seek to regulate the intensity of the emotion


In competitive mode, one character is selected per player and the cards are arranged on the table so that they are placed from highest to lowest emotional intensity. In the middle of the route, the "emomemo" cards are placed that will be used at that point in the route for a memory game about bodily manifestations of emotions. Players take turns answering each card, either through questions about themselves, about others, exercises and self-regulation activities.

Whoever reaches the end of the route first, that is, who has resolved the card with the least emotional intensity, accesses the final card where it is confirmed that they have reached "balance."

It is a psychoeducational game to help children manage their emotions.

Includes MINDFULNESS and COGNITIVE BEHAVIORAL THERAPY tools.

The peaceful town we lived in became a dangerous area overflowing with zombies overnight. When we hurriedly escaped from our homes, all we could grab was an almost empty backpack. We need to gather items scattered around and pack them into our backpacks to survive until help arrives. What happens to those who can't use the items effectively... I don't even want to think about it.

Backpack Hazard is a competitive puzzle game in which players draft item cards and stuff them into backpacks in a world where a zombie pandemic has broken out. Stack or rotate guns, herbs, and ammunition to fit them into your backpack. A zombie-themed puzzle game without any actual zombies.

LOB2 is a solitaire-oriented boxing simulation using dice and charts. It is an evolution of the 20-year old Legends of Boxing with new development inspired by other Stone Mountain Press games, such as Stone Cold Hockey and Dice United 2nd Edition.

LOB2 has also been balanced based on data from LOB for Windows. More depth has been achieved with the new High Action chart, which activates certain ratings in interesting ways, bringing more twists and turns to the action. Game play has been streamlined. Twenty heavyweights have revised ratings, including new versions of Ali in the 70s, and Foreman in the 90s. Fighters are now presented in a quality, magazine-style catalogue, including a section describing why the fighter is rated a certain way in the context of the game.

NOTE: LOB2 is completely backwards compatible with 1st Edition fighter cards, with the exception of three fighters who have Mode Cost Traits (Locche, Padilla, and Hamed).

—description from the designer

Tatsu grew up in the Sengoku period, a time of constant war. A descendant of a noble clan, she was raised in Konomine Castle, and became one of the court ladies there.

Her life of privilege was interrupted when a large army led by Ouchi Teruhiro attacked the castle. With their lord and his army off on raids of his own, the defense of the castle fell to Lady Ichikawa. Knowing her few remaining warriors would not be enough, Lady Ichikawa armed Tatsu and the other court ladies and prepared them to defend the castle against Teruhiro's army.

Tatsu was only 17, and this was the first time she raised a sword. But to her surprise, she felt no fear in battle. Instead, a thrill energized her and she fought ferociously. After 10 long days of battle, Teruhiro's army fled, but not before Tatsu suffered a vicious gut wound.

The days that followed were pure agony as her wound festered. This continuous intense pain brought about her awakening in a thunderous blast of sound and force that left several dead and a hole in the castle wall.

Unaware of her sorcerer’s heritage, Tatsu was confused and frightened. She fled the castle, and was presumed dead.

In the years that followed, Tatsu was irresistibly drawn to the thrill of combat. The combination of an intense training regiment and the sorcerer’s power running through her veins made her a force to be reckoned with. In battle after battle her reputation grew, and she became a famous Onna-bugeisha.

Drawn by visions and nightmares she didn’t fully understand, Tatsu eventually left Japan. She met Li Xian, a Chinese sorcerer, and they sought out ancient artifacts connected to the Jade Emperor. On their centuries long quest, Tatsu found many items of great power and mastered long forgotten enchantments and alchemy.

Tatsu’s Character Deck is full of deadly attachments with the new “Concealed” mechanic that allows them to be played face-down. As a tactic, you can pay the attachment’s cost and reveal it, unleashing a powerful effect.
This pack is a complete Domain Deck for use with Sorcerer, containing the Bloodcursed Ship domain skill card and 10 cards for your grimoire.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
This expansion for the Judge Dredd Miniatures Game introduces a whole new level of play to your tabletop – the (in)famous block wars of Mega-City One!

Each player builds their own block with unique characteristics, gathers their residents together, and then unleashes them on the self-satisfied citizens of a neighbouring block - who have had everything their own way for far too long! Included are new rules for use in the Judge Dredd Miniatures Game; new characters and units, new Armoury cards to add to your deck, and a brand-new Block War deck that brings the sheer violence and chaos of a block war to the tabletop – along with a lot of Judges!

The block war campaign allows players to marshal their forces, from common citizenry to the (self-proclaimed) elite forces of the Citi-Def, and then play through a number of battles that show the key moments of the block war. As the war escalates, new scenarios are unlocked, damage can be done to the enemy block, and with enough of a rival effort, you may just be able to destroy the physical foundations of a block and send it crashing into City Bottom in a fiery cloud of dust, rubble, and falling citizens.

The streets of Mega-City One have just become a lot more violent… Judge Dredd: Block War Expansion contains:

80-page Block Wars A5 book
Pad of 100 City Block datasheets
15-card Block War deck
Three new Armoury cards
Two rampaging resident figures (in Warlord ResinTM) – exclusive to this set!

boardgame: Sorcerer: Forlorn Opera House Domain Pack
description: This pack is a complete Domain Deck for use with Sorcerer containing The Forlorn Opera House Domain skill card and 10 cards for your grimoire.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: A Wayfarer's Tale: The Journey Begins – The Lonely Isle
description:
A Wayfarer's Tale: The Journey Begins-The Lonely Isle is a game of adventure and discovery. Players navigate their own individual maps, collecting treasure and avoiding monsters as they explore new locations. Turns are taken simultaneously as players chart their own course racing to discover new worlds.

As an introduction to A Wayfarer's Tale, this lite version available for download. This game offers an opportunity to grasp the basics of movement and scoring, serving as a gateway into the core mechanics.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Judge Dredd: Justice Department Pat Wagon
description:
After the Lawmaster, the Pat Wagon is the most commonly sighted Justice Department vehicle seen on the streets of Mega-City One. Designed to be flexible enough to bring the Law to citizen riots and armoured perps, as well as fulfil the grunt work of catch and meat wagon teams, the Pat Wagon is well respected amongst Judges for being able to take just about any punishment the streets can give.

Contains one resin/Warlord ResinTM vehicle and five game cards.

This is Valen's story: Valen moved around his neighborhood, he had friends who lived near his house and liked to do the same activities. One day he met someone from a different place who didn't share the same tastes.
Do you know what happened? Join us in this game to find out!

With attention and speed we are going to put together groups of friends based on their differences or similarities. Can we get along with people similar or different to us?

OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME:
Create groups of friends based on their differences or similarities:

How to play? We will find cards with combinations of 3 characters, 3 different places to live and 3 different interests or activities. Based on certain rules and without taking turns, players must assemble groups of characters. At the basic level, the person who has the most cards will win; at the advanced level, other elements are included to reward the greatest social diversity.
 Includes a guide for adults with tips to work on diversity and enhance the game's resources.

In Dream you must complete your dream by playing Dream and Rem cards, completing the sequence from 1 to 6 and you will try to hinder the success of your opponents’ dreams, thanks to the Nightmare cards.
Each turn you will draw cards or play them, in your sequence or in that of an opponent, until a player completes their dream by winning the game.

The game takes place in several turns, until a player completes their Dream sequence, thanks to the Dream and/or Rem cards, or when all players have finished their cards in their hands.

—description from the publisher

Elementary’s vision is to close the gap between scientists and non-scientists, by providing a game that is fun for both.

For scientists, Elementary is an interactive, scientifically correct and quick game. Great to play with colleagues, but also the perfect way to share your scientific passion with your friends and family.

For non-scientists, Elementary shows that physics is not as scary as it seems. You’ll be able to play around with elementary particles and energy-mass conversion within minutes, requiring zero physics knowledge.

Elementary can be played in two different ways, depending on your level of physics enthusiasm. Easy Mode is meant for the novice player: get to know the elementary particles by combining them into sets. Classic Mode is for those looking for more interaction: it involves anti-particles, technique and sabotage.

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – Hunting for Answers: Parallel Investigator
description:
Hunting for Answers is a print-and-play rules set for Arkham Horror: The Card Game that provides an alternate version of Rex Murphy, previously released in The Dunwich Legacy Investigator Expansion.

In order to play this investigator, a copy of the Arkham Horror: The Card Game core set is required, as well as a set of curse chaos tokens featured in The Innsmouth Conspiracy Campaign Expansion.

—description from the publisher
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Game: Arkham Horror TCG – Challenge Scenarios & Parallel Investigators
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Pericle: Labyrinth is the second release from Long Dog games. Pericle is a bold new tabletop RPG that combines a fantasy RPG world with a ground-breaking app called Loremaster. App-driven gameplay presents the campaign, which takes away the need for a Game Master. Loremaster sets the scene with music and context, and indicates how to set up the tabletop when combat is inevitable.

In the second Pericle product but the first of its kind, embark on a perilous journey into the legendary Labyrinth of Porta, where daring adventurers face the ultimate test: a rite of passage by choice, not chance. This contained tabletop RPG plays like a board game with physical maps and dice, assisted by our Loremaster app, which acts as the game master. You and your band of heroes must navigate a gauntlet of cunning traps and monstrous denizens. Amidst the dangers lies the promise of untold wealth, a tantalizing reward to beckon adventurers.

Each journey through the Labyrinth is a uniquely  generated adventure consisting of 5 encounters, ensuring endless replayability. Conquer each encounter to earn XP, loot, and prestige, empowering your heroes for future challenges. The Labyrinth utilizes the core Pericle rules and game mechanics found in Pericle: Gathering Darkness, creating a familiar yet thrilling experience.

—description from the publisher

Immerse yourself in the beauty of North America's coastlines in the Coastal Expansion for Keystone: North America. Encounter 28 captivating new species and explore exciting new game mechanics that will challenge even the most seasoned biologists. Dive deep into the narrative with a brand-new field journal containing 10 solo and co-op story scenarios, each offering unique challenges and opportunities for discovery.

Expand your Keystone experience and discover the wonders of coastal ecosystems in this essential expansion. Whether you're a seasoned player or new to the game, the Coastal Expansion contains hours of exploration and enjoyment.

-description from publisher

Are you ready for sun and fun? In the Greatest Beach Vacation, you and your fellow players compete to experience the best beach vacation. Spend 5 days snorkeling, surfing, shopping, playing volleyball, jet skiing and building the best sand castle!

In this roll & write game, you will roll your dice and attempt to score the highest in your choice of six beach-vacation themed activities. Each activity will present its own challenges and rewards. After five rounds players compare their scores to see who had the best beach vacation ever.

Looking for even more vacation fun? Check out the other Greatest Vacation games, like Game Vacation and Vegas Vacation. More themes to be announced soon!

Are you ready for the best game vacation ever? In the Greatest Game Vacation, you and your fellow players compete to experience the best vacation at a game convention. Spend 5 days playing your favorite games, eating delicious food and making the best cosplay costume!

In this roll & write game, you will roll your dice and attempt to score the highest in your choice of six game-vacation themed activities. Each activity will present its own challenges and rewards. After five rounds players compare their scores to see who had the best game vacation ever.

Looking for even more vacation fun? Check out the other Greatest Vacation games, like Beach Vacation and Vegas Vacation. More themes to be announced soon!

Boxing: the Game is a simultaneous-play 1v1 game that simulates a boxing match. Punch, Block, & Feint your way to victory as you Master the Sweet Science.

Just like with real boxing, there are three ways to win: Score more points, exhaust your opponent, or land an epic KO!

Built with a passion for both board games & combat sports, B:tG puts fun first with a simple yet action-packed gameplay loop featuring deckbuilding, bluffing, character progression, plus an endless variety of tactics & strategies.

You don't need to buy any expansions or additional content for the full experience.

Boxing: the Game is designed with a balance-first approach. Our hope is that competitive & hobby gamers alike can enjoy an ever-evolving local metagame, similar to games like: Dice Throne, 7 Wonders: Duel, Star Realms, Radlands, or Mindbug.

However, unlike other classic 1v1 dueling games, B:tG is *simultaneous-play,* meaning you won't be taking turns or waiting around for your opponent. Just like in a real fight, you and your opponent are making decisions at the same time!

Whether you're hardcore boxing fan or not, you'll appreciate that every mechanic is dripping with flavor: stance-switching, clinching, balancing speed & stamina, styles making fights, & more.

B:tG is simple enough that a child can learn the game on their first playthrough, yet offers enough strategic depth that you can continue improving your fight game for many bouts to come.

—description from the publisher

You are a Warlord!

We have two planets: one known as TerraFyte and the other, Bitpaþ. These two planets have been locked in combat for countless years and things are bleak on both sides. The leaders of TerraFyte approach you. You are a little-known hero from a place called “Earth.” They think you have what it takes to help save their planet from total annihilation. Being a kindhearted soul, you are happy to help this poor planet and its people. There is only one problem, though: due to the war, the economy has fallen into shambles. The inhabitants had to change their currency into a crypto-currency called Banana Bitz. Using Banana Bitz is a challenge, as there are not many who are willing to work for them. Other well-known heroes won’t even think about taking Banana Bitz as payment or even as a gift! Fear not! There are those who are willing to work for this crypto-currency: monkeys and heroes that really, really need the work. You can ask heroes such as Chinchebula or Star Fish to help defend TerraFyte. They are willing to take any form of payment. Will you use military might to destroy the other planet? Or will you use diplomacy to end this war?

TerraFyte, created by the small indie group Arkidogo Interactive, is a customizable card game featuring a 6x2 board for intense 1v1 battles. Think Magic the Gathering meets Hearthstone with a space twist—imagine monkeys in spaceships battling it out, inspired by old RTS games and featuring charming pixel art. Players take turns at the same time switching priority to see who plays next. The game has a rhythm that most card games do not have. The gameplay strikes a balance between accessibility and depth, offering easy-to-learn mechanics with strategic depth. Season 0 launched with two prebuilt decks that players can mix and match.

—description from the designer

boardgame: アリスは１番前がいい (Alice ha ichiban maegai)
description:
You've been invited to a parade in Wonderland! You were excited to attend, but it looks like they haven't settled on the order of the parade. If you leave it up to the residents of Wonderland, there's no telling when the parade could start. "We'll never get anywhere like this!" shouts the Queen of Hearts. "Let's ask a third party to decide it fairly!" And just like that, you're in charge. Choose how you want to organize the parade, and let's get this show on the road!

In アリスは１番前がいい (Alice should be in front), five Row Cards showing residents of Wonderland (the Queen of Hearts, the Mad Hatter, the Cheshire Cat, Alice, and the White Rabbit) form a parade line. There is a deck of Resident Cards showing these same residents, with each card displaying a priority letter from "A" to "H" and a majority value of 1, 1.5, or 2. All of a character's Resident Cards show the same action, but they will have different letters and values. Each player has a hand of two resident cards, and there is a common area with as many face-up resident cards as there are players.

Each turn, all players choose one of the cards in their hand and reveal it simultaneously. Players then acquire a card from the common area in order of priority. The played card's ability then activates, which rearranges the Row Cards in the parade, and the played card is placed in a column of matching cards in front of the player. After a pre-determined number of rounds have passed, the Row Cards in the 2nd and 4th positions are "awakened" and flipped over, revealing additional scoring rules.

After a number of turns have passed based on the player count, players receive victory points. A player receives 1 victory point if they have the highest value of Resident Cards for a character. The character at the front of the parade line is worth 2 victory points. Players also receive bonuses based on the awakened Row Cards. If any player has reached 10 victory points, the game ends, and the player with the most victory points wins. If that condition isn't reached, the game continues until someone reaches 10 points, with the ties going to the player who scored the most points in the final round.

You have come to the Rooted Soul, the massive tree that stands on the border of the Anwyn Valley, in response to a crisis. For years a poisonous fog has filled the Valley. Soon it will expand and swallow up the rest of the land. Some of you have a nearby hamlet or village you call home. Some of you have heard that there are great and ancient secrets hidden in the Valley. Some of you long for a chance to prove your prowess. But all of you agree that the fog must be stopped before it poisons the world.

Echoes of the Sundering is a 1-5 player cooperative, open-world, deck construction adventure taking over 30 hours to complete. Players will create a party of heroes and explore the valley through a beautifully illustrated map book. Along the way, they will face dynamic, narrative-driven encounters and should they succeed, will grow in power gaining new cards to add to their deck and new items to complement their play style! The story reacts to players' decisions and no two play-throughs will be the same!

—description from the publisher

Once upon a time, there was a girl who was blind. Four girls who were her friends wanted to teach her about the colors. The four girls decided to show their friend the nature of color by acting out the colors themselves.

This is a competitive card game where you compare the strength of the numbers written on the cards and reduce your opponent's "life" from 10 to 0. Each card has a color, and each color has a special power. Use the power of colors and the abilities of the cards to defeat your opponent.
publishers: ヴィレッジ・ブリッジ (VillageBridge)

description:
You have built an airship company to acquire wealth and fame. The world of airships is still in its infancy. Who will grasp the trend, overcome obstacles, and win the title of best in the industry? Embark on an airship delivery game!

In a normal delivery game, you would load customers and cargo onto your own airship from the space where your piece is located, transport them to their destination, and earn victory points and money! So far it's just as you can imagine!

However, to move this airship in Air Express, you don't play a card from your hand or roll a die! Manipulate rolling balls that represent your crew — the board tilts as if you were steering it! — and try to drop them in the right space! You can truly feel the uncertainty of operating an airship! And without a license!

How many balls do you use? Will you modify the airship to increase its payload? Which course will you take to your destination? There are so many details to consider!

Choose the request card for the square you are in and load it with your luggage. Decide which marbles to use and roll them well by maneuvering. When you arrive at your destination, get victory points or money equal to the reward value! The player who earns a certain number of points based on the number of players wins!
publishers: るりるりゲームズ (ruri ruri games)

description:
Tricks, traps and pandas… so many pandas! BamBoom is a strategic card game for 2-4 that pits armies of pandas against each other in a frantic nuclear arms race. Featuring cartoon style violence, unexpected twists and more panda puns than you can shake a stick of bamboo at, BamBoom is recommended for players aged 10+.

Grow your panda army and defend your camp against the tricks aimed at you by your opponents. Go on the offensive to become the first to gather four nuke parts and one nuke shell and establish nuclear dominance–but beware! Traps lurk in the deck, ready to be sprung at the most inopportune time. Each turn starts by triggering cards you already have in your camp, making a long-term strategy a must. When you draw a new card, you have the opportunity to attack another player. If victorious you can take a nuke piece from them, but be warned–if you lose, they will be taking one of yours. Players are limited by a certain number of cards they can hold, forcing you to employ strategy and cunning to secure your victory.

In a fast-paced game of twists and traps, only one thing is certain: nuclear war has never been this cute.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: All Time Wrestling: A New Dawn
description: Expansion for All Time Wrestling that includes 4 new personas, 10 attack cards with special abilities and 10 new wrestling abilities to existing legends in the base game: Kurt Angle, Randy Savage, Andre the Giant & The British Bulldog.

"War on "Petrel's Paradise"! Five Companies vie for control to restart "Project Clone-a-Saurus" and corner the market on the production of dinosaurs! Little do they know, it's open season and the island's dinosaurs are doing the hunting."

Clone-a-Saurus: The Miniatures Game is a tabletop skirmish game that allows players to simulate the thrilling clash between Predator vs Prey! Players can play as either Humans or Dinosaurs and take part in fun and dynamic battles 66 million years in the making! Supporting both Dino v Human and Dino v Dino combat, Clone-a-Saurus: TMG is the tabletop game for dinosaur enthusiasts and wargamers alike!

—description from the designer

Whether their raison d’être is to protect their block, experience the thrill of petty lawlessness or become involved in organised crime, the average citizen and Justice Department alike. When a block war breaks out the block gangs are more often than not to be found taking the fight to the enemy blockers by fair means or foul.

Block champions are often little more than a bully who likes to think they take care of their own and let the rest of the drokking city go to hell. They are at the forefront of escalating tensions between blocks and often responsible for outright block warfare backed up by their cohort of gangers, often armed with heavy weaponry when inter-block warfare erupts.

Box contains five Warlord Resin figures and four game cards.

—description from the publisher

You will be the leader of a cult that believes in "The Elder", an ancient evil god, and aims to bring the Elder back to full life.
All you seek is the favor of the Elder. There is no time to be lost. Use everything you can. Exploit the believers, offer offerings, pray earnestly, and one day, go to the Elder's knee and offer yourself to him!
The Elder may give you something unconsciously, but don't get too close. Your body will not be able to hold it. If you are going to approach, be prepared.
Make your wish come true in a world of danger and witness the moment of the Elder's descent!

Amidst the radiation, small pockets of civilisation attempt to develop their communities and survive this harsh world by harvesting the last of earth's untouched resources. But beware, thereʼs always someone who wants what you have and will do anything to take it.

In Residuum players seek to explore the game map while expanding their community, exploit the resources available and interact with other players through trade deals and conflict. Conflict does not result in player elimination or the ʻdeathʼ of your character, instead you lose ground, lose assets and lose resources, so be careful who you trust and who you deal with!

Residuum is an open world. Areas of the map allow different resources to be harvested and different technologies to be discovered. Each turn is only limited by the number of resources you have available to spend, so considerable expansion is possible through building a robust engine for resource generation.

At the end of the game, points are awarded for the size of your community, for the discoveries you have made, and for your stockpile of resources. Whoever has the most points at the end will be declared the winner.

—description from the publisher
