description: A simple game where you score points according to the rules by placing tiles with cats on them.

boardgame: ㅊㅅ게임왕 훈민정음 실종사건 편 (King of initial consonant game)
description: A simple game where you hide two cards with Korean initial consonants on them and guess the corresponding initial consonant by saying the word.

A set of four promo tiles for Lanterns: the Harvest Festival, released in 2019 by White Goblin Games in the Netherlands (different from the 2015/2016 promo tiles).

The tiles provide more aesthetic variation in the platform artwork. Before you add all four new tiles to the game, you are encouraged to first remove one tile of each platform type from the base game.

An "escape room" style game, with multiple puzzle cards that need to be solved and then combined to solve a meta puzzle that connects them all.

The pack contains a series of puzzles which connect to create an answer to a master puzzle you must solve to unlock C.L.E.D. (Captain’s log Entry Device) which has been password protected in an alien language and discover the whereabout of Captain Landon Plannet.

It’s a super game and takes experienced puzzlers around an hour to solve. For those who aren’t as experienced in puzzles, you may need a little longer – average solve time 90 minutes for less experienced puzzle solvers.

You can play the game in a small team, as the puzzles are printed on individual cards, meaning you can work together on different puzzles at the same time, and combine answers to create the master password.  There's no time limite either, so you can do 2-3 cards, stop, then do another 2-3 later.

If you have a larger group of friends, you can buy multiple copies and run the game in groups simultaneously and see who solves it first?  It can easily become a “team escape experience”.

—description from the publisher

description:
Love Escape Games?  Enjoy fiendish puzzles?

This game is packed full of puzzles and fun and great for solo play or with a small group.

A fascinating escape room style adventure set in the 1940s. Challenge yourself as you attempt to access a secret black market organisation!

A great game to enjoy whether you are a solo player or part of a small group.

Find the people behind the Blackmarket threatening the Homefront

You must solve the puzzles and find a secret password in order to be admitted to the criminal underworld.

It’s a super game and takes experienced puzzlers around an hour to 90 minutes to solve. For those who aren’t as experienced in puzzles, you may need a little longer – average solve time 2-3 hours for less experienced puzzle solvers.

You can play the game in a small team, as the puzzles are printed on individual cards, meaning you can work together on different puzzles at the same time, and combine answers to create the master password.  If you have a larger group of friends, why not buy multiple copies and run the game in groups simultaneously and see who solves it first?  It can easily become a “team escape experience”.

From 2020 to 2024 there was a series of murders along the I-90 between Cleveland and Louisville.   Over the course of 3 years, police investigated 28 homicides which all conformed to the pattern.  Each victim was staying in a hotel or motel close to the I-90, and was killed with a single bullet from a Colt 45 shortly after retiring to their room.  A feather, later determined to be from a dream catcher, was left at each murder scene.

Can you solve the case and catch the serial killer?

There are a variety of puzzles in the box as well as narrative, combining the best of both worlds to create an exceptional self contained game. You need to engage with both the puzzles and the narrative in order to eliminate suspects as you progress through the case.

This game does not require access to a computer and is designed for solo play, or for a small group.

This is a promotional Item card for Level 7 [Escape].

The text is "Pass a 5 Intelligence challenge to move any player, guard, or clone one tile.  This counts as a challenge action."

This is a promotional Adrenaline card for Level 7 [Escape].

The text is: Gain 2 Fear at any time (Shield +3) / Gain 3 Fear at any time.  Any player can reroll any number of dice from one roll of his choice.  / Discard at any time to raise or lower your fear by 1.

There's no better way to enjoy a spring afternoon than with a walk through the woods. Your relaxing stroll quickly spirals out of control however when you find yourself trapped within the gates of a most sinister-looking garden. You just can't shake the feeling that something strange lurks within the long grass. You need to find your way out, and fast!

—description from the back of the box

boardgame: Dune: War for Arrakis – Desert War
description:
Desert War is an expansion for Dune: War for Arrakis that introduces new units for both the Harkonnen and Atreides factions, further detailing the harsh reality of the conflict fought on the burning surface of planet Dune.

Expert Fremen Sandriders summon giant sandworms and ride them into battle with unparalleled skill. Fremen Rocket Launchers emerge from their Fremen Camps to fire at Harversters and Ornithopters. Suspensor Troopers scout ahead to make surprise attacks, withstanding the desert hazards like few can. Escort Ornithopters not only help move Legions around but offer direct support to them in battle.

—description from the publisher

In Diversity, players take on the role of recruiters for large companies committed to diversity and inclusion. The aim of the game is to build a team of employees with different skills and experience for great vacancies, reflecting the diversity of our society. The game is challenging and fun, and is a great way to learn more about the importance of diversity in the world of work.

—description from the publisher's website

A number of companies are revealed, each with job vacancies for different professions.
During the game, each player acquires possible employees of the most diverse classes, genders and professions, placing them in a puzzle in front of them. Once a player has managed to gather orthogonally adjacent possible employees on their table, fulfilling the requirements of one of the companies with current vacancies, they can "hire" one of these, which will generate points at the end of the game, and reveal a new company, with new job vacancies.
At the end of the game, each person employed generates points and the categories most in demand by the market, additional points.

This fan expansion allows you to use a new resource (Cargo) to take an entirely new kind of turn, a Trade turn.

With 2 new civs, 6 territory tiles, 2 landmark cards, 5 Tapestry cards, 7 Tech cards, and 53 Trade Rout cards, this is a full expansion for Tapestry, and works well with all the official expansions.

This is an unofficial, print and play, fan expansion for Tapestry.

boardgame: HALMETTE: An interesting Game for Two Players
description:
A unusual variant of Halma that is played on a uniquely rectangular fold out board. The board is very small and folds in half, making it pocket-sized. There are 60 spaces (6 by 10) for play with 12 starting pieces for each side.

Based on game components and construction, it is estimated to have been published sometime between 1885 and 1900.

—user summary

Construction is booming in Tokyo! But there's a Kaiju on the loose, smashing any building in its path!

Players buy property cards to build towering skyscrapers. The higher you build, the more blocks cost, but the more rent you collect!

However, a rampaging Kaiju (represented by a windup monster toy) might knock over your buildings. You choose the monster's path—its unpredictable movements can be helpful or harmful!

Fortunately, you can insure your buildings to reap great rewards if they are knocked over. You can also buy sushi cards that provide big pay-offs!

The player who earns the most points from completing objectives wins the game!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Japanime Tactics: Granblue Fantasy – Djeeta Starter Set
description:
In Japanime Tactics, you can assemble a team of your favorite Granblue Fantasy characters like Djeeta and Io by combining their ability cards into a deck, then battle your opponent's team on a modular board of interlocking map tiles.

Each round, players use their cards to attack and defend with their characters. Any card can be used by any character to provide bonuses for matching colors and action types, but characters can perform powerful unique abilities by using their specific cards.

During a battle, each player has a unique objective such as rescuing a hostage or destroying supplies and must use their team to attack their opponents and interact with the terrain to score the most points before all players run out of cards.

Japanime Tactics: Granblue Fantasy – Djeeta Starter Set contains the characters Djeeta, Katalina, Io, and Vira, and it's playable on its own in a simplified 2v2 mode. By combining this set with other Japanime Tactics sets or boosters, you can play the full 4x4 game.

The object of Jaw Breaker is to match cards in order to knock out an opponent's tooth from their playing surface. Your goal in the end is to knock out all players teeth and have the last tooth or teeth standing.

The game Murder in Palermo is a card-based role-playing game in which the players take on secret roles. Now it is up to them, the residents of Palermo, to act together or each pursue their own plan to ensure justice at the end of the night.

A dreamy stroll through the warm nights of Palermo. But the romantic facade is deceptive. The Cosa Nostra has the Sicilian port city firmly in its grip and a Mafioso may already be lurking around the next corner. If a citizen of the city doesn't defend himself, he may be... dead the next night.

Lords of the Frontier is a great tactical and economic strategy where you have to try on the roles of great rulers, governors and treasurers. In this game, players will lead one of four warring factions: Knights, Vikings, Beast Lords and Necromancers.

The game equally combines both the economic aspects of government, where you have to develop your city, train troops, and monitor the general state of affairs in your kingdom, and military strategy, where you will need to competently direct your fighters into battle, discover new territories and capture foreign cities in order to lead your people to victory!
The goal of the players is to score the maximum number of victory points, which are counted from the achievements of the player's Hero and his faction, or destroy other players by defeating the armies of their Heroes and capturing Cities. Each round consists of three phases:


     City Phase;
     Adventure Phase;
     The Battle Phase;


In the first, players will have to rebuild new buildings in the City, train new troops from Recruits, calculate resource revenues and losses.

In the second, players move Heroes with their armies around the map, opening new territories, capturing mines, killing robbers, and most importantly, capturing new villages that will bring new Recruits and bring the player closer to victory.

And the third phase unfolds when the Hero encounters another Hero or a band of robbers. The battle takes place in a separate Arena in the center of the playing field, where players will command their troops, trying to destroy the opponent's army. By winning, the Hero player receives experience points, with which you can develop the characteristics of the Hero, which will directly affect the course of battles and your advantage in the game.
The game is designed for two or four players, lasts 4 game weeks (12 rounds), on average the game takes from 3 to 5 hours.

—description from the designer

boardgame: ブラッククリスマスポーカー (Black Christmas Poker)
description:
ブラッククリスマスポーカー (Black Christmas Poker) is a card game using mah-jong/poker combinations

36 cards of three suits : red, green and black. Cards go from A to 7, with two of each card in red and green, and one of each in black, plus one special black card.

On your turn, you draw a card either from the facedown deck or from a faceup discard, and then you discard.

You win if, after drawing from the deck or the discard pile, you have a complete set. Sets are composed of three combinations : run of 3 cards of the same suit, 3 cards with the same number or three cards, 2 cards from the same number. To have a complete set, you must have at least two combinations of 3 cards and one of 2 cards.

You also must announce when you only need one last card to form your complete set.

At the end of the round, the winner earns some points depending on how you completed your set. You also can win some bonus points.

With two games, you can play up to seven players.

boardgame: マタタビおかいものゲーム (Fur Furtive Alley)
description:
Players try to have the most money at the end of the game.

On your turn, you, as a buyer offer some money face up, and other players try to convince you to take their card(s) in exchange. When you chose someone, you take their offer facedown, and they become the next buyer.

Player(s) not selected by the buyer receive some tokens that can be offered to the buyer, or can be used to see if the transaction is fair when you're the buyer. If it's not, the seller has to give you tokens until the deal is fair. If they can't, they're out of the game.

The game ends when someone is out of cards, the player with the most money (and still in the game) wins.
A card-based cooperative game where you play as one of seven cats and use their individual abilities to find food and shelter.

Quattromania is a strategic board game for two players, offering an innovative twist on the popular game Connect Four. The game rules allow for easy immersion while still providing room for strategic thinking and enjoyment. Below, I'd like to highlight some of the game's strengths:

Sustainability: The entire game, including the board, game pieces, and packaging, is made from recycled plastic, promoting not only environmental friendliness but also awareness of sustainable gaming practices.

Fun and Strategy: Its resemblance to Connect Four allows players to quickly grasp the game, while the rules and longer playtime of 10-20 minutes ensure a tactical and entertaining experience.

Practicality: Quattromania impresses not only with its gameplay but also with its practicality. The game pieces are taken out of the board at the start, and the game tidies up itself during play. It's highly durable and easily transportable in its game sleeve.

Aesthetics: With its striking graphics and colorful game pieces, Quattromania not only provides enjoyment but also boasts aesthetic appeal. After the game, the remaining pieces create new patterns, making it suitable as a decorative element in the living room, perhaps as a "coffee table game."

—description from the designer

Dreadful mutations have taken the world by storm. Some are more resistant to its effects, while others fall victim to the primal instincts of the animals their DNA was spliced with. As one of the survivors who has limited control over their mutations, you and the rest of the colony that has banded together must research the mutant's DNA for a cure if humanity has any hope at survival. While gathering DNA samples, players must also withstand the treacherous wastelands, craft vital items, and secure locations to build back a civilization worth fighting for. Each player must prove their capabilities by earning the most Reputation Points in hopes of becoming the leader of the colony. Dare to enter a world of mutants to save humanity?

—description from the publisher

Cosmic Conquest is a fast-paced 2-4+ multiplayer dice rolling game filled with combat and strategy. It features a unique combat system using a mixture of dice, cards, and Contender abilities.

Players will assemble a team of three Contenders to fight for galactic supremacy! With incredible art, repeatable gameplay, and collectible miniatures, players will not want to miss out on the fun!

Etch your name into the history books as the champion of the upcoming Alpha Clash in Cosmic Conquest!

GAME SYSTEM

    
    
         This entry is to allow for discussion/rating of the game system as a whole. It is not for a specific product or release. Versions will appear on the individual item pages.

    


Alpha Clash is a superpowered, lore-driven, trading card game filled with fast-pace action and a unique comic book art style.

Based on the characters from the Alpha Clash universe, players will be pitted against one another, leading the fight with their Contender. Players will build a 50-card deck to support their Contender where the last one standing is crowned the victor!

The course of history can be steered by powerful leaders. Achieve their goals alongside other major milestones to make the most of their singular talents.

Leaders - Set III introduces 6 renowned leaders from ancient times and the Middle Ages : Nefertiti, Croesus, Hannibal, Chares of Lindos, Genghis Khan, Nebuchadnezzar II and a rules card.

The course of history can be steered by powerful leaders. Achieve their goals alongside other major milestones to make the most of their singular talents.

Leaders - Set II introduces 6 renowned leaders from major eras in history: Artemisia II, Pharoah Khufu, Hammurabi, Rameses II, Xenophon, Nero.

One leader takes control of your civilization at setup, adding a new scoring requirement that must be met at the end of the game. Another is used for its milestone. Milestones are events which happen during play; on completing a milestone, you are bound by a new rule that impacts your society for the rest of the game. Meet your leader's goal and complete your milestone to secure your civilization's enduring legacy!

Guide the growth of your beanstalk while diverting the plants of your neighbors. Tile placement is crucial as you must balance maintaining control over your plant’s direction with ensuring you have the right structure to support optimal bean growth.

The light engine-building tile game Vine leverages simple yet strategic gameplay, challenging players to develop the highest-yielding garden of pole beans. Prune your plant to create more options later in the season, close off sections to prevent unwanted growth, and let your vines climb high to maximize points by the end of the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Spider-Foes Affiliation Pack
description:
Players can begin assembling their own team of sinister Spider-Foes with Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Spider-Foes Affiliation Pack, which brings together the Green Goblin originally found in CP21, the Doctor Octopus originally found in the Marvel: Crisis Protocol Core Set, along with the Kraven the Hunter and the Lizard originally found in CP58 in one convenient package to give players the characters that will form the foundation of their Spider-Foes rosters. These characters are accompanied by stat cards for all four characters, ensuring players have everything they need to use their Spider-Foes in Marvel: Crisis Protocol.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Avengers Affiliation Pack
description:
Players can begin assembling their team of earth's mightiest heroes with Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Avengers Affiliation Pack, which repackages the Avengers-affiliated characters found in the original Marvel: Crisis Protocol Core Set, including Captain America, Captain Marvel, Black Widow, and Iron Man in one accessible box. Players looking to get into the game or expand their Avengers roster will find the miniatures and Stat Cards for these characters along with copies of the Avengers Assemble, Ricochet Blast, and Second Wind Team Tactic Cards featuring new art, giving them everything they need to form a battle-ready Squad straight out of the box.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Cabal Affiliation Pack
description:
Players can begin gathering their own Cabal in Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Cabal Affiliation Pack, which repackages the Cabal-affiliated characters from the original Marvel: Crisis Protocol Core Set, including Red Skull, Crossbones, Baron Zemo, and Ultron in one accessible box. Players looking to get into the game or expand their Cabal roster will find the miniatures and Stat Cards for these characters along with the Cosmic Invigoration, Dark Reign, and The Age of Ultron Team Tactic Cards from the original Core Set featuring new art, giving them the foundation for their Cabal squads.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Old Biddies is a cat themed bidding game. It's not important to be an old woman, or to even like cats. You just need to win.

The Aha Moment - Vizzini would be proud.

You are playing Old Biddies and a lot of cats come by. You want those cats so you bid your highest card. But wait, aren't the other people going to bid their highest card too? If you bid your lowest card and let everyone else fight over the cats, you will have the advantage on the next bids until you spend your highest card. But everyone knows that, and they may bid low also. Perhaps you should play a middling card and beat their low cards? Or just put out a Porchlight and wait to see what they bid? Ah heck, just make it rain!

Old Biddies is a very simple mechanic with a few power cards thrown in to make it interesting. Additionally, if you win the most days in a round, you earn a round bonus so even small numbers of cats can have an impact. And lastly, he who controls the Catnip bag, controls the ties. No matter who ties, the player with the Catnip bag decides who wins.

—description from the publisher

Founders of Reyvick is a 3-5 player economic game of speculation and competition on the newly settled world of Reyvick. Each player controls a company just starting out on the planet that is attempting to amass the most wealth and craft the greatest legacy. Players deploy their partners to collect resources from their assets and complete projects that further research, exploration, and building across the planet.

A game of Founders of Reyvick revolves around assets, which include the districts on Reyvick as well as special assets that differ in each game. Companies compete to own these assets and take advantage of the resources and revenue that they provide, while maximizing their profit by buying low and selling high. Each game round begins by resolving a draw of event tiles. Most tiles place new population, resources like flora and fauna research, or the mysterious "Unobtanium" into the various districts of Reyvick, but some events present setbacks for companies to manage.

While the promise of rich resources have drawn many to Reyvick, the economic climate there is unique. Unlike other planets, Reyvick's Ruling Council uses a radical approach to managing the many companies seeking profits on Reyvick. Every game round, each player must set the price at which they would be willing to sell each of their assets to another player. Players are then obliged to respect their own valuation, so the price must be chosen carefully; if you set it too low, someone else will take over your asset and make a profit, but at the end of the turn you must pay taxes on the price you've set on every asset, so you also don't want to set it too high.

Once prices are set, the Council distributes whatever taxes were collected in the previous turn. Some portion of those taxes will fund public projects, which are placed in districts for the welfare of its citizens — and companies — while the rest is distributed to players. Rights to the resources and profits from a district or special asset are always for sale; any company can buy them by paying the price set by the current owner — and every asset on Reyvick has a price!

Once you purchase an asset, you must immediately set the price as at the beginning of the turn. You can set it to any amount that is higher than what you paid, but the Council will tax you based on what you claim your assets are worth, and those taxes are distributed as incentives in a public/private partnership, so your tax money can end up benefiting your competition.

At the end of the game, the player with the greatest combination of wealth, assets, and projects wins. Founders of Reyvick is based on the economic theories of Nobel Prize-winning economist William Vickrey, as well as the writings of Eric Posner and Glen Weyl, especially their book Radical Markets.

—description from the publisher

Bitterest Day recreates the fighting between the American and Japanese troops on Okinawa during May 1945.

Contents:

     9 Scenarios
     Campaign game rules
     1 countersheet with 180 1/2" counters and 64 5/8" counters
     1 27x35" historical map of Sugarloaf Hill.
     Terrain description and special rules.
     Chapter divider and quick reference.


Scenarios:
BD-1: Opening Probe
BD-2: Limited Gains
BD-3: Western Anchor
BD-4: Thicker Than Flies
BD-5: Ultimate Insult
BD-6: Bitterest Day
BD-7: Crescent Carnage
BD-8: We're Dead Anyway
BD-9: Good Luck Charm

Ownership of American and Japanese OOB required to play.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Delve into the dungeon to defeat pairs of Crystalline Creatures to steal their treasure.
Players press their luck by taking numerous turns Delving into the Dungeon: rolling dice against increasingly difficult odds to collect gems and treasures without busting.

—description from the designer

Shuffle the deck and deal out 8 cards to the table and to yourself.

You start off the usual way by matching from your hand with a card on the table, followed by drawing one from the deck and matching it or placing it on the table. (drawn cards never go into your hand.)

In this version you are playing against the deck. After you go, the deck draws one card to play as if from their hand, matching any cards that are available or place it on the table. Then draw a second and repeat the previous option.

Play like this continues until you or the deck gets a valid combo, you may choose to koi koi, but the deck never does and immediately ends the round with the points. For added fun you can allow the deck to koi koi on some possible combos like 10 chaff and/or 5 seeds as these ones are easier to score in the coming turn and adds a challenge for the player to try and score something before they do.

Place all cards the deck claims across from you on the table to keep track, just like you do on your side.

Play a set of 3, 6, or 12 rounds like the traditional game. And keep track of both sides trying to beat the deck. For a hard mode give the deck a starting 10 points and beat them by the end of all rounds

House rules that can add to the possible combos.
Full month: getting all four cards of one month, either at the start when dealt or during the game. scoring 6 points.
Joined hand: getting a full hand of 2 cards each from four different months in hand, immediately ending the round. scores 6 points.

boardgame: Corps of Discovery: Mega Foggy Map Pack
description:
These maps are all for the new mode we unlocked where you can play the Fog concept in each of the other chapters. Adds 4 more per Chapter for a grand total of 20 more maps! To be clear - you NEED the Fog Expansion to make use of these maps!

4 Fog in Fauna (6103, 6104, 6105, 6106)
    4 Fog in Flora (6203, 6204, 6205, 6206)
    4 Fog in Insecta (6303, 6304, 6305, 6306)
    4 Fog in Vameter (6403, 6404, 6405, 6406)
    4 Fog in Maldonado (6503, 6504, 6505, 6506)

boardgame: Corps of Discovery: Mega Map Pack

description:
Fruit Island is a fast-paced, interactive design by Reiner Knizia centered on timing, risk assessment, and tactical positioning.

Players take on the role of agile monkeys gathering fruit across a shared island landscape. Each turn presents a familiar Knizia dilemma: press your luck to collect more, or secure what you’ve gained before the roaming gorilla disrupts your plans. Movement and collection are simple, but the shared space creates constant tension as players influence one another’s opportunities and risks.

The rules are approachable, yet the decision space is sharp and dynamic. Incremental gains can compound quickly — or unravel just as fast — depending on timing and table interaction. As in many of Knizia’s designs, the depth emerges not from complexity, but from player psychology and calibrated pressure.

Designed for 2–4 players and playing in about 20 minutes, Fruit Island offers a compact, highly interactive experience that highlights Knizia’s enduring ability to pair elegant systems with meaningful decisions.

In Antarctica, penguins are invited to a Christmas party. This journey poses challenges for the penguins as they have to climb towering icebergs, cross wide rivers, and navigate through floating ice sheets while occasionally encountering polar bears blocking their path. To brave the bitter cold, they decide to wear beautiful and warm scarves before setting off determinedly.

—description from the publisher

Keep your eggs close and fight with dragons.
Once the deck runs up the game is over and whoever has the most dragon eggs- wins!

Boat is a small dice game in the Yacht family designed to be playable with just three dice.

On your turn you roll all three dice and may optionally roll any number of dice up to two more times.

Then you write the value of the rolled dice in the category that fits the rolled dice the best.

If nothing fits, you simply write a 0 in a category of your choice.

Once the score sheet is full, the player who did the best wins.

The game can be played solo as a score attack game or with up to six players.
family: Series: Business Card Games (City Hare Studio)

description:
A 2-player, 18-cards game about sitting guests at your own wedding.

Players take turns to place one of the three available double-sided guest cards in a shared tableau, with each guest having their own preferences regarding their neighbors. The game ends after the table is full. The winner is the player whose guests scored more points total (whose guests had more fun at the wedding)!

Solo mode with beat your own score type included.

—description from the designer

For the PnP game of Propaganda Wars you will need 5 six-sided dice, two pencils and the game board in the instructions. It is a Yahtzee-inspired head-to-head battler. It was created during the Erasmus+ project Gamify+: Enhancing Organizational Capacity through Hybrid Gaming during activities introducing participants to the PnP games principles and how to theme your design with media literacy education in mind. The following focuses on propaganda. Players represent two sides of an ongoing information-based conflict. Their possible attacks are defined by the well-known combinations of dice values, in this case, named after the most common propaganda mechanisms. To win one needs to drop the opponent's HP to zero.

Across the World by Air is an Australian wartime game where players race from 4 different starting place on the globe and race towards Melbourne, Australia.

Players follow a coloured path across the world, moving by the roll of a dice. Players many encounter obstacles along the path that may hinder their progress.

As the players travel along their route to Melbourne they will encounter other cities which the player must land on by the exact number on the dice. First to reach Melbourne is the winner.

A somewhat identical game titled "The Air Race game" is listed elsewhere on BGG. It differs in certain graphics such as some destinations, the title and no rules printed on the board

In Heated, players aim to reach a specific score to win, with temperature-themed cards ranging from +1 to +5 and from -1 to -5. Each turn involves drawing a card to either aid or sabotage the player, playing a card from hand to increase or decrease temperature score, and drawing a new card from deck.

Players must avoid reaching a freezing negative score or burning from an excessively high one. Action cards add a twist to gameplay.

—description from the publisher

The world shivers as the vengeful Carthage launches a devastating war against Rome, a conflict raging across Italian and Iberian peninsulas, Corsica, Sardinia, Sicily, and North Africa. Stand with some of the greatest generals in history – Hannibal Barca or Scipio Africanus – and lead your empire to victory and glory!

Hannibal is an award-winning asymmetrical card-driven wargame with rules and cards upgraded from previous editions. Use Strategy Cards to maneuver armies and famous generals of the time, raise new troops, gain political control of provinces and regions across the Mediterranean, and play powerful historical events. Achieve victory by capturing your enemy's capital, holding more provinces at the last round or by removing all your opponent's PC markers from the map.

Compared to the previous Golden Geek edition, the Classic Edition: 

     Revives the classic Second Punic War scenario as the core of the game.
     Has an updated rulebook that reflect this, and addresses all known outstanding questions and errata.
     Adds a Playbook with a series of learn-to-play tutorials.
     Replaces the plastic miniatures with cardboard standees.
     Adds the Sun of Macedon expansion General and strategy cards as a newoptional rule.


—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
Who shall rule the Mediterranean Sea? A nascent land-based empire with no naval traditions or the world’s premium trading and maritime power? Stand with Rome and lead mighty legions to expand its power beyond the confines of the Italian peninsula, or take Carthage’s side and lead its battle-hardened fleets and generals to victory and glory!

Hamilcar is an asymmetrical card-driven wargame for 2 players with updated rules and cards. Use Strategy Cards to maneuver armies, fleets, and generals, raise new troops and build warships, gain political control of provinces and regions across the Mediterranean, and play powerful historical events. Achieve victory by capturing your enemy's capital, holding more provinces at the last round or by removing all your opponent's PC markers from the map. Carthage can also win if Roma controls less than 3 provinces at the end of any turn.

Hamilcar Silver Edition is the first version of the game to be released separately from Hannibal. Compared to the previous Golden Geek edition, this Silver Edition:

     Has an updated rulebook and player aid to address all known outstanding questions and errata.
     Changes some Land Battle rules
     Changes the Naval Interception rules
     Adds the Price of Failure expansion General and Strategy Cards as an optional rule.
     Replaces the plastic miniatures with cardboard standees and corresponding counters for the Generals
     Adds a new Fleet counter/standee for each side to help track Naval Movement and Interception.


—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
This is a promotional Event card for Level 7 [Escape].

The text is:

Spawn a guard on every tile that has a player with 3 or more threat.  "Attention all personnel: Escaped subjects reported in Gamma sector."

Spawn a clone on every tile that has a player with 6 or more feat.  "If you don't calm down, they'll find you."

Each player rolls as many dice as his current Strength and discards five cards minus one for every Strength icon rolled.  "This place is falling apart."

This is a promotional skill card for Level 7 [Escape].

The text is: Gain 1 fear to reroll one die.

In Patinhos de Bo’Racha (loosely translating to Rubber Duck Races) competitors score points with their trio of rubber ducks by moving them as far down the water slide as possible. But all the ducks in the race are identical, so it doesn’t take long to lose track of whose are whose when they start bumping and jumping each other! If you don’t take time during the race to discover where your rubber ducks are, you’ll probably end up helping your opponents by moving theirs!

—description from the publisher

In Pounce, players become cats aiming to catch prey totaling 8 food value. Each round, a new prey is turned over and player simultaneously selects Sleep, Stalk, or Pounce. Players who Pounce must place their Pounce action card on one of the revealed prey. If only one player chooses it, they can catch it by discarding Energy cards from their hand. If multiple players Pounce on the same prey, they must fight over it using Intimidation and Hiss cards. Sleeping lets you refill your hand of cat cards, and Stalking gives you an advantage in your next fight.

The cat deck is filled with many different cards, split between Energy (used to catch prey), Stars (e.g. Hiss, Scratch, Catnip, which can be played once at a specific time), and Skills (e.g. Fishing (allows you catch fish), Intimidation, Sharp Vision, which can be played multiple times). The game is fast-paced and highly thematic.

The game also comes with a mini-expansion that adds event cards to the prey deck.

Rock Paper Scissors Wizard Croc is a fast-paced and strategic twist on the classic game of Rock, Paper, Scissors. In this card game, players engage in epic battles – multi-turn competitions where Rock, Paper, and Scissors meet Wizards, Crocodiles, and Wild cards (oh my!).

How does this evolution make the game more fun?


     New elements, like the Wizard and the Crocodile, expand battle options and outcomes, providing players with more choice and helping to make game play different each time.
     A back-and-forth play structure replaces the classic one-and-done game, elevating the importance of strategy and longer-term thinking, while adding an educational component to the game. 
     Wild cards make for wild times, with unexpected surprises that can change battle momentum on a moment’s notice.
     The Rock Paper Scissors roots make this new variation easy to learn (rock still beats scissors, paper still beats rock, etc.) and the familiar base facilitates cross-generational appeal.
     There are multiple ways for 2-4 people to play. The variations – one-on-one, multi-round battles; tournament-style play; and group play – enable players to mix things up based on personal preference, number of players available, amount of time available, and age range of the players.


—description from the publisher

Legend of Gonia is a strategy game where your objective is to destroy your opponent's Akrios or annihilate their Tribe.

Deploy your Warriors into the Gonia arena and take control of the tiles. With the powers of Spirit Cards, attack from a distance, cast magical spells, or perform special movements to win the battle! Release the Djinns to exert constant pressure on your opponent and turn the tide in your favor!

Craft your Deck with finesse and dominate the Battlefield in an epic duel, where synergy between Spirits and your Warriors will be the key to victory.

Unleash the power of cards and become the Master of Gonia.

—description from the publisher

In Vector, you'll race against the stars as you gather resources to save your civilization from your galaxy's dying star in the polyomino engine and deck builder.

Each turn, players use their deck of cards to perform vector movements in order to draft triangular polyominos into their ship, or collect resources for future purchases and/or end game scoring.

The player with the most Valor before at the end of the game, is declared the winner.

—description from the publisher

Jurassic Park meets Ghosts in this Co-operative boardgame about a theme park gone mad! It's opening day at Ghostland, a theme park that contains actual ghosts! However, havok has immediately broken loose, and the ghosts are attacking guests left and right. It's up to you and your friends to stop the ghosts, build Field Generators to contain them, and finally face off against the most sinister ghost in the park, the infamous Rex Garrote!

Each players picks a character that is unique and contains their own stats and abilities to help you not only survive the theme park, but hopefully save the day as well! The game is played in rounds where players move arounds the park, fight ghosts, buy items, or try to build a Recurrence Field Generator.

Once players have successfully built three Recurrence Field Generators they will summon the big bad himself, Rex Garrote, for them to defeat! But if the park's human population reaches zero, it's Game Over!

—description from the designer

Dive into a living, unpredictable dungeon! In Yum Quest, you play as a team of heroes venturing through rooms filled with monsters, traps, and challenges. Together, you’ll solve dice-puzzle encounters, manage your energy resources, and hold back the rising Threat before facing the final Boss.

Each room is a cooperative puzzle where players must assign colored dice, play Heroic cards, and combine their powers to overcome danger. Success earns gold and upgrades; failure raises the Threat and tightens the pressure. Every decision matters.

Heroes wield five types of Energy — Physical, Magical, Divine, Natural, and Technical — which fuel their powers and act as their life force. Purple dice are wild but unstable: they can save the team or doom it instantly.

When the dungeon deck runs dry, the Boss appears! To win, the heroes must complete three escalating trials in a single round — a thrilling finale that tests teamwork and luck to the limit.


Plongez dans les profondeurs d’un donjon vivant et imprévisible ! Dans Yum Quest, vous incarnez une équipe de héros unis contre les monstres, pièges et défis qui s’y cachent. Ensemble, vous devrez résoudre des puzzles de dés, gérer vos énergies et contrer la montée de la Menace avant d’affronter le Boss final.

Chaque salle du donjon propose un casse-tête coopératif : il faudra combiner vos cartes Héroïques, vos dés colorés et vos pouvoirs pour triompher. À chaque réussite, vous gagnez de l’or pour améliorer vos capacités ; à chaque échec, la Menace grimpe et les ennemis se renforcent. Une tension constante entre risque et coordination.

Les héros se distinguent par leurs cinq énergies — Physique, Magique, Divine, Naturelle et Technique — que vous utiliserez autant pour combattre que pour survivre. Les dés violets, véritables jokers instables, peuvent sauver la partie… ou précipiter votre chute.

Quand la pioche s’épuise, le Boss surgit : il faudra enchaîner trois épreuves consécutives dans un même tour pour remporter la victoire. Un défi collectif haletant, où chaque dé compte.

boardgame: Legend of the Galactic Heroes: Reinhardt Joraku 銀河群雄伝説 ラインハルト上洛
description:
This is a doujin game produced for sale at Japanese game shows. In order to get one, you needed to order the game in advance and then it could be picked up at the show. These were not available to the general public.

Ginga Gunyu Densetsu Ichi or "Reinhardt Joraku" (Reinhard takes the Capital) reproduces the Legend of the Galactic Heroes conflict using the "Sengoku Gunyuden" system. It is a campaign game with a map that stretches the galaxy. The game comes with an 8-piece 34"x 45" map, rules, two sheets of counters, and four tables/charts. You need to assemble the map and mount the counters.

Scenarios include “Twilight of the Gods” and “Mar-Adetta.”

There is also an expansion for the game called "Frontier Conquest."  However, both can be played as stand-alone games.
family: Setting: Legend of the Galactic Heroes

description:
Deep in the heart of Feline City lies an alleyway that has been the center of a long war waged between two clans of cats who follow the way of the ninjas. At stake is the supremacy and control of the alley. With so much blood spilled and no end to the war in sight, both clans have agreed to engage in a duel of throwing stars. Whichever clan is the most accurate at throwing, will have sole rule of the alleyway. The losers with lick their wounded pride and find another place to live. Which Alley Cat Ninja clan will be the rulers of the alleyway?

Alley Cat Ninjas is a two player, table top dexterity game where you will flick or push your throwing stars to land on your opponent’s targets (coins) and score points. Players will sit directly across each other and alternate turns launching their throwing stars. Be careful though, if you throw it off the table, you will lose an opportunity to score. With each hit, you will move your points marker accordingly. Whoever reaches 12 points first, wins the game!

—description from the designer

In Kobbbrix, you take control of robotic space snakes in a desert world, where you need to consume dimensional energy vortices to survive.

The game offers a challenging experience, with 3 levels of difficulty, where 1 to 4 players must work as a team to strategically and efficiently place pieces, creating the best combinations, avoiding unwanted pieces, and preventing snake collisions. As the game progresses, the challenges increase and players need to enhance their cooperative skills, managing pieces and data as best as possible to achieve victory.

Besides the cooperative mode, Kobbbrix can also be played competitively in a 1-on-1 versus mode. In this mode, each player must consume more dimensional energy vortices than their opponent while making the best combinations and avoiding collisions.

—description from the publisher

Escape From Zombie City is a race to get out of the city as zombies are taking over. Players will fight zombies and find weapons, quick cures, backpacks and other fun items along the way. As players fight zombies, they will earn points as they kill the zombies.

If a player gets bit by a zombie 4 times, they become a zombie and chase everyone else. They do lose all of their points and any cards they are holding. This player does not have a chance to win the game, but they can stop the other players from winning the game as well.

The city is a bit of a maze and does have blocked roads. Players are trying to get to an extraction zone that's on the opposite side of the city. When a player reaches the extraction zone, they start a four turn counter and hover above the city in a helicopter that allows them to shoot zombies as they wait for more players to get to the extraction zone. When the four turn counter is over, any player that didn't make it to the helicopter gets left behind and automatically loses the game. Points are added up at the end of the game to see who the winner is.

—description from the publisher

Ah, the school bell goes off, for the last time in the school year, classes are over. Vacation, the magic word that echoed through the heads of all young students stops being a vague term and finally gains meaning; Time to close your notebooks and open your imagination to the possibilities.

Four friends already had everything planned: A cabin, a weekend, and everything that came to mind. The journey of several kilometers to the heart of the forest amidst the mountains, where screams echo, and yet are not heard.

In Terror na Cabana, players compete against each other, trying to survive, while being chased and eliminated by classic monsters from horror stories. The last player left alive is the winner.

boardgame: Offshore AWA Sydney: Hobart Yacht Race Game
description: Offshore simulates one of the world's great ocean yacht races - the AWA Sydney - Hobart Bluewater Classic. Using charts, dice, instruments, cards and calculator, each player tries to get his or her yacht to Hobart first. This game has a combination of skill and luck. And just like the real Sydney Hobart there is a line honours winner - the first boat to cross the finishing line, and a Corrected Time Winner - the winner on handicap. The game may be played at two speeds - standard and fast.

The long war has finally come to an end.
However, the day when the people of this frontier territory can return to their peaceful lives is still far away.
For their sake, we must rebuild the devastated city as soon as possible.
Don't forget to fulfill your true purpose...

Cultus is an abstract strategy games in which players will take turns moving around the board to collect funds, rebuild the city with their building cards and monuments while score victory points from various point source such as the monument abilities or their plan card.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
An easy to learn card game in which you have to match colors and numbers in an ascending or descending manner. Play strategically, change direction or color, finish your cards and win the game!

—description from the publisher (translated)

A strategic game that gives great importance to the harmonious shape of the design. Whoever closes the most circuits wins!

—description from the publisher (translated)

A classic bluffing game with picture cards!

The practical pocket format is perfect for bringing the fun always with you!

Play cards, deceive other players and don't get caught!

Doubt everything and everyone!

—description from the publisher (translated)

An irreverent game with lots of black humor to laugh in company.

The game contains many cards and scorecards.

The black humor and minimal design make it the perfect game for carefree evenings with friends with a bit of self-irony.

Liven up the evening with ironic, embarrassing and irreverent questions to really get to know your friends.

—description from the publisher

Back in medieval times, Capybaras discovered "Match 4" and immediately knew they could do better. After tireless research and scientific experiments, they had finally made it - MATCH 5!

In CapyBattle, the goal is to dominate five quarters in a row alongside your trusty Capybara knights or fortified allied structures.

The core of this two-player game lies in the 10 randomly placed actions. During each turn, the capybara monarchs, pivotal pieces, move from capybara to capybara to activate corresponding actions aligned with the column and row of each capybara.

These actions can allow you to do things such as moving capybaras, baiting enemy capybaras, building structures, using secret passages, or simply taking a nap. With new structures, more knights will heed the call of their leaders until the whole Capytal will be flooded with chivalrous rodents and the new Heir to the throne will be finally decided!

Life has been extinguished across the universe. Starbeast has destroyed any life he has come into contact with. Now the resistors, a band of humans, elves and orcs must use their unique talents to stop Starbeast and return peace back to the Universe.

Starbeast is a game for 4 players with a unique 1v3 set up. One player takes the role of Starbeast and the others of the 3 Resistors. This game uses strategy, resource management and cooperation during timed turns. Players create and battle units, strategically set up bases, recruit characters and trade resources.

—description from the publisher

Your IT department is under attack! Server crashes, security breaches and unplanned absence lurk around every corner. Build an army of real senior consultants—each with their own unique set of skills and abilities—and fight back in this tabletop collectible card game.

The players take turns playing as attacker or defender, managing their budget. The loser of each skirmish depletes part of the budget.

Will you go bankrupt—or be crowned King of Consultants?
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Runner Tactics is a game for two players that combines elements of tactics games like Final Fantasy Tactics and field goal games like American Football or Rugby.

A player wins either by crossing into the opponent’s end zone, or by attacking and removing all of the opponent’s pieces.

There are 10 unique pieces.  Large command tiles are used to assign movement and attacks in secret from the other player, while the small game tiles will execute those moves on the game board.  Each player places a blue cube for movement and a red cube for attack on each command tile.  After both players are ready, the vision guards are removed, and the moves are executed simultaneously.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Super Snipers: Light and Shadow Mini Expansion
description:
This expansion for Super Snipers introduces two new Snipers:

J@[\]u5 is a mind-hacker capable of shutting down their opponent's powers for short periods of time and stealing their basic ability when enraged!

SUBHUTI is a monk with a Basic Ability that becomes less potent the more hits he lands but an Adrenaline power that becomes more powerful with every shot he takes.

This expansion comes with 10 Cards (1 Basic Ability, 1 Adrenaline ability, 1 AI Basic Ability, 1 AI Adrenaline Ability, and 1 Reference Card per character), and 6 acrylic Hiding Discs.

Impostor Syndrome is a hilarious game of bluffing, blending in, and BS! Each round, players will have to pick a person and modifier card from their hand to compete in a randomly selected challenge. The twist? One of you is an Impostor, who doesn’t know the challenge when they pick their competitor! The challenge is revealed once everyone has selected their cards, and now everyone must present their argument for why their competitor is the perfect candidate for this challenge. Keep a careful eye on your opponents! You’ll only score points for correctly identifying the sneaky Impostor. And if you’re the Impostor…? Don’t let anyone know that you know nothing!

—description from the publisher

Players pick 1 to 3 tower cards (each with a grid of 7 blocks) and draw a hand of 6-12 gooblet cards for each tower, taking turns adding cards to their towers to harvest resources, add to the tower's defenses, or attack an opposing tower. The goal is to be the first to destroy all an opponent's towers.

HoHoHo is an asymmetric dueling strategy game with paper-and-pencil mechanics, moving through the territory and building a path that reproduces the Christmas atmosphere.

On his turn, Santa can move 1, 2, or 3 spaces to get to houses with children, give gifts, and get to the South Pole as soon as possible. Grinch, in his turn, prevents Santa - he plants giant super-fast-growing Christmas trees, green like himself, which block Santa's way. Who will win in this competition for Christmas cheer?

There are also other marks on the map - mountains, different types of houses, and Wormhole tunnels that can help Santa fold the space-time continuum.
Options for placing children in houses and poles on the map make each game unique and create many possible combinations.
The game contains 2 expansions, which will also allow you to diversify the next games. 
And what symbols to use on the map will not let the players forget the name of the game!

—description from the designer

ХоХоХо — це дуельна асиметрична стратегічна paper-and-pencil гра на переміщення по території та побудові шляху у сеттінгу та атмосфері Різдва. 
У свій хід Санта може походити на 1, 2 або 3 клітинки, щоб дістатись до будиночків із дітьми, подарити подарунки і якнайшвидше дістатись Південного полюса. У свою чергу Грінч перешкоджає Санті, а саме садить супер-швидко-ростучі велетенські зелені, як сам Грінч, ялинки, які перешкоджають шлях. Хто переможе цьому двобії за різдвяну радість?

На карті є також інші позначки - гори, різні типи будиночків та тунелі-червоточиниб які допомогають Санті скласти просторово-часовий континуум.
Розташування різної кількості дітей у домівках та полюсів на карті додає реграбельності кожній новій партії. Є варіанти доповнення та розширення карти. А якими позначками користуватися гравцям на карті не дасть забути назва гри!

—description from the designer (Ukrainian)

boardgame: Rangers of Shadow Deep: A Gathering of Heroes
description:
With the evils of the Shadow Deep clawing at his borders, the King of Alladore sent out a call to every realm, a summoning of heroes to come and stand against the darkness. In truth, he had little hope that his plea would be answered, and it is true that no large armies have come to Alladore’s aid, but help has come! Mainly they came in ones and twos, or small unassuming groups, but they came. Spellcasters, mercenaries, knights-errant, desert nomads... heroes from the lands surrounding Alladore have come to stand with their brothers and sisters. Individually, they seem an insignificant force, but with their skills and special abilities added to those of the rangers, they might just tip the balance!

The 192-page hardcover Rangers of Shadow Deep: A Gathering of Heroes features:


     Advanced Character Creation including new Traits and Limitations that players can use to bring greater tactical and narrative variation to their rangers.
     Ten ‘Archetypes’ that players can use to create new characters, representing the heroes that have come from the lands around Alladore to join the fight against the Shadow Deep.
     Collects five previously stand-alone supplements for the game: Blood Moon, Temple of Madness, Ghost Stone, Incinerator, and Menagerie.
     Fifty-two unique enchanted weapons.
     A bestiary featuring all the creatures from the core rulebook and all the collected supplements.
     Copious new artwork from the visionary of the Shadow Deep, Barrett Stanley.


—description from the publisher

Players work together to recreate a visual story by using illustrated cards. Players can freely talk (without showing their cards to the others) and try to elaborate a coherent story by adding cards to the middle of the table. The main appeal of the game is the narrative creativity it initiate, the emotional bonds players experience when listening to each-other.

Vieux Chêne (Old Oak)

As we walk, I understand why this activity is essential to him. I believe that for him, hiking is about offering a simple encounter with yourself in the present!

In Plantje Boom you collect as many animal species as possible with your tree cards. The more different species you collect, the more leaves grow on a tree. The winner is the player with most leaves on their tree.

The most played party game throughout history around the world has evolved. Meet Fluttr, a hilarious reimagined version of Spin The Bottle, designed to bring people together in unexpected ways. A few minutes into playing Fluttr, strangers become friends and sparks start flying.

Fluttr is the only game that makes you feel butterflies!

First, you grab a Challenge card and read it out loud. Chances are you’re about to get very close to another player, the question now is, which one of them?
That’s when the bottle comes in and the butterflies start fluttering.

How racy are the Challenges? Just enough to test your chemistry with other players. Also, they’re very interpretative, which means that you decide how far you take them, and this depends a lot on who the bottle picked for you. That’s what makes Fluttr unique. You’re not really playing against anyone. Instead, everyone is having a great time and enjoying a good dose of feel-good chemicals derived from human physical interaction, without the awkwardness that it could generate under other circumstances. It’s all part of the game.

Now you can also bring Fluttr with you wherever you go, with the Fluttr Spin The Bottle app.

Gunslingers each have 3 hearts (shells)
They are dealt 5 cards from the main deck.
Each Gunslinger looks at their hand for an Attack or Item card.
(Any card can be played face down as a "Shoot" card)
The Gunslinger holds their chosen card up, at eye level, facing themself so as not to let other Gunslingers know what they will be playing. All Gunslingers must have Steely Eyed Glares on their faces.
When the Dealer (a chosen Gunslinger or non player) says "GO!!!" all Gunslingers throw their cards at their targeted Gunslinger. Attack cards are thrown at opposing Gunslingers. Item cards are thrown in front of the Gunslinger throwing the card. 
If a Gunslinger is attacked they may play Defense cards on top of the attack cards. A Gunslinger may play as many Defense cards as they like in response to an attack.
Gunslingers may play Special cards, if the requirements are met.
Gunslingers lose hearts for any damage that is not negated.
All damage is dealt at the same time.

Gunslingers with hearts remaining at the end of the round are dealt 2 cards. Continue rounds like this until 1 or No Gunslingers remain.

Things to know:
There is no maximum hand size. You can have as many as you are dealt.
The deck is never reshuffled. If you run out of cards, you run out of bullets.
You can't Bleed a Barrel.
If there are Gunslingers alive and no one has cards in their hand, the Gunslinger with the "Knife" card equipped wins (Because they brought a knife to a gunfight).
Fastest game is 13 seconds.

—description from the publisher

In the tiny world of insects, a rainy day can unleash maximum chaos.
Run to protect yourself and compete to get the most bugs in the shelters while the rain ends!


En el diminuto mundo de los insectos, un día lluvioso puede desatar el caos máximo. 
¡Corre a protegerte y compite por lograr el mayor número de bichos en los refugios mientras la lluvia acaba!

Lines is an exciting abstract strategy board game that will test your intelligence and strategic skills. Lines is perfect for the whole family, with games lasting from 15 to 30 minutes, and can be enjoyed by 2 to 8 players, ages 8 and up.

In Lines, every decision matters. You start the game with 5 cards and 2 or 3 different wildcards in your hand, so use them with cunning and cooperation to form lines of 5 chips on a meticulously designed board, where luck is minimal and victory is achieved through your skill and strategy.

••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••

Líneas es un emocionante juego de mesa de estrategia abstracta que pondrá a prueba tu inteligencia y habilidades estratégicas. Líneas es perfecto para toda la familia, con partidas que duran de 15 a 30 minutos, y puede ser disfrutado por 2 a 8 jugadores, a partir de los 8 años.

En Líneas, cada decisión cuenta. Empiezas el juego con 5 cartas y 2 o 3 comodines diferentes en tu mano, así que úsalos con astucia y cooperación para formar líneas de 5 fichas en un tablero meticulosamente diseñado, donde la suerte es mínima y la victoria se alcanza gracias a tu destreza y estrategia.

In this simple card game, be the first to empty your hand of cards by playing them into the central "elevator" pile.

Cards have to either be played in ascending, or descending order but this can change depending on the cards played!

Cards left in your hand once another player empties their hand become penalty cards. If you keep the same numbered penalty card across 3 rounds you'll lose!

The game is fully language independent.

—user summary

ルールは簡単！「上昇／下降を繰り返す場札」に手札からカードを出して手札０枚を目指そう！
特殊効果カードを使って途中で反転！上昇／下降の流れを変えて他人を妨害しよう！
残った手札の数字がペナルティになる！優先的に捨てないと脱落するかも…

—description from the publisher

In this tactical game with deckbuilding elements, 1-4 players  become one of the Jomsvikings - an elite mercenary group capable of  accomplishing even the most impossible tasks. You set off to different  planets of the Nine Realms to gain glory while figthing anything your  employers want you to fight!

The game consists of 3 rounds, during  which players fight the chosen monsters (each of them separately), but  can either help or hinder other players. Each round takes us to a  different planet, with various conditions, that can impact combat. By  investing in new cards and new equipment it can become much easier to  defeat them, but the ultimate goal lies in collecting the glory points  (received for defeating the enemies, finding the valuable artifacts, and  using the abilities skilfully).

Jomsviking can be played in 2-4 player free for all mode, solo mode or in 2-4 player coop mode.

—description from the designer

Fatal Encounters is a murder mystery board game where you find a predetermined killer hidden in a selection of suspect profiles. 
It’s a story made up of crime scenes, text messages, photos and more which you pieces together in an intuitive and tactile way to pin a killer back to the scene of the crime.

Yes our stories are immersive and twisted, BUT what really sets us apart is our cases are made by people with experience in criminal investigation.

From the stories to mechanics, Fatal Encounters emulates the psychological nuances of crime solving to make you feel like a detective.

All of our puzzles & features are gamified techniques and protocols of what we did in the police to investigate crimes. We just removed all the boring paper work so you just experience the thrill of catching the bad guy!

—description from the designer

Meanwhile in Veracruz … a variety of pineapple, once thought extinct, has recently been rediscovered. This golden pineapple is a true concentrate of energy, known to increase strength and agility. Luchadores are crazy about it and would do anything to get their hands on this vitamin-packed fruit !
To get slices of this golden pineapple, the 2 teams of luchadores have to guess what's in the Piñata.

Modern Dudo with competitive Teams and no elimination. Two teams of Luchadores try to estimate how many candies, of one type, are inside the Piñata.

To adjust your estimations you have : 
• your cards which you can consult at any time
• the card you gave to your teammate after consulting
• the backs of your teammates and opponent's cards

At your turn you can denounce the previous player or announce a superior number, by adding +1, of one candy of your choice : Papagayo, Cocodrilo or Chihuahua ! And, obviously, you can bluff !

The team of the player who is right gets 10 pineapple slices points for the first round then 20 for rounds 2,3,4 and 30 points for rounds 5,6,7,8. First team to have 100 win the game !

70 cards (45x90mm) :
• 53 Piñata Cards (27 single-color backs, 18 two-color backs,8 three-color backs) 
• 8 Team Cards
• 8 Point Cards
• 1 Memo Card

—description from the publisher

Bulbous: Bael’s Grotto is a Roll & Write adventure where players assume the role of ancient guardians of hidden forests. In these forests, little creatures can be tamed by grabbing their favorite treat: bulbs.
It’s a game for 2 players, that takes around 20 minutes to play, from ages 10 and above.

Game Setting

Bael’s Grotto is a mysterious place located in the forbidden region. Elder legends tell stories of mysterious creatures and succulent bulbs from the depths. For the fearless Baelfungious, there’s no room for idle chatter! Embark on the exploration of the grotto and lead a group of four Baelfungious to find and savor the irresistible bulbs.

—description from the publisher

A micro-deck-building screw-your-neighbor race to the gate.

Make That Flight is a fun social game that involves building your best deck to get you to your flight first. Over three rounds you will trade, steal, and give away cards to get the quickest time so you are the first to Make That Flight. Navigate the four phases (Transportation, Check-In, Security, and Gate) of your journey best to win the game!

The Aha Moment

After Round 1, you're sitting pretty with 150 minutes and no bad cards. Then in round 2 your "friend" trades you a Missed Flight card for your "No Worries" card in Transportation! Now what?!?! Luckily you have Round 3 and you pass the "Missed Flight" card back to your "friend"...success!! Unfortunately now you have to draw a Gate Card to finish your story.....what will it be? Golf Cart Ride, No Worries, Bad-Timed Bathroom break.........whether you make it or not all depends on this card. And the card you flip over is................

—description form the publisher

An educational life science board game for kids and their families. Find and raise monarchs and learn about their life cycle. Backyard Butterfly is a game played in 4 stages:

Stage 1: Hunt for eggs. Roll the leaf shaped die and flip over 1-3 leaf tiles until you find an egg. Place an 'egg tile' on your milkweed board. Move on to stage 2 on your next turn. 
Stage 2: Feed your hungry caterpillar. Action sequence:

     Roll leaf die to populate leaf tiles onto your milkweed board.
     Roll both caterpillar dice. Move your caterpillar meeple around the milkweed board twice with the goal of landing on leaf tiles to eat.
     Place any eaten leaves onto your leaf tracker board. 


Continue with this stage until your leaf tracker board is full. 
Stage 3: Chrysalis Coin. Flip the coin (twice per turn). 
Green side- continue to wait. 
Orange and Black side- your monarch butterfly is ready to emerge! Move on to stage 4!
Stage 4: Your monarch has emerged and flys away! Place a monarch butterfly token in the sky above the leaf tracker board. Butterfly tokens represent points earned. Begin your next turn back at the egg stage.

There are two additional gameplay variations: 
'BB Helper' walks parents through how to teach even the youngest gamers to play a game with multiple actions and stages in a way that is intuitive and fun.
'Field of Milkweed' is a competitive version of the base game where all 4 milkweed boards are placed together to form a field. All resources are up for grabs and nature can strike at any time. Nature being an additional d12 rolled at the beginning of the caterpillar stage causing havoc.

boardgame: L'Ordre de Veiel: Les Miraculés D'Alenmur
description:
The Les Miraculés d'Alenmur expansion is made up of 7 heroes, 6 of whom feature French content creators.

Etrigane, Bob Lennon, Sheshounet, Pressea, Benzaïe and Ganesh have all agreed to join the Order of Veiel!

Each new hero you will discover has been designed in close collaboration with each of the participants and according to their respective wishes and expectations. Everyone has already tried ODV and enjoyed it!

Of course, as well as adding more and more heroes, it's also important to us that each creation offers a new way of approaching the Order of Veiel, with new mechanics.

—description from the publisher

Birds of a feather sit on wires together!

Utilize the elements of the scenery to make matches and gain control over the telephone wires. Have the highest number of birds at the end of the game to win!

Watch out for hazards like squirrels and downed wires. Strategize to cover up your opponents birds. Stake your claim to the telephones wires in this fast-paced and infinitely re-playable area control matching game!

—description from the publisher

You want to live in nature, so you decided to start a goat farm. First you bought a large plot of land full of grass, and then you built a fence. Now the real work begins. Barter for goats, breed them, and ship them out. Your goal is to become a full-fledged goat rancher.

What is Ziegen jagen? This game is a two-player abstract game with a ranch theme. The primary mechanic resembles Kalah or Mancala, which have been played since ancient times. However, in this game, the Mancala has evolved into something unusual and mysterious, creating elements that feel like worker placement, expanding options, area majority, and set collection. It is truly the "future of fun."

—description from the publisher

Your goal in Dragon & Unicorn Collector is to collect the most exotic...dragons and unicorns. Yes, exactly what the title promises. The harder the creature is to collect, the more points you get for capturing it. You also earn extra points for collecting a dragon and unicorn from the same region.

How do you capture these creatures? Roll the super magical sparkle dice and match the symbols to collect the dragon or unicorn.

Dario Massa's board game, (for 3-7 players), will immerse you in an epic challenge to conquer the title of Duke of Tristerra. You will have to wander through the lands of the kingdom, showing off in the eyes of its inhabitants.

Every corner of the duchy is full of challenges (80 to be exact), each more extravagant than the last, ready to test and evaluate your talent and composure in surprising ways.

You will be called to face a variety of tests, from the most unusual to the most challenging, discovering one after another the extravagant characters who populate this incredible world. Gather followers along your journey and prepare for the ultimate confrontation: challenging the game's powerful Bosses. Only by overcoming every obstacle and beating your opponents to the punch will you finally be able to reach your goal and reclaim the coveted throne.

36 Characters
80 Challenges
5 Bosses to face
very high replayability, thanks to the different options for each challenge
The manual is printed in full color and thread bound.

The game system of Tristerra: La Grande Baldoria stands out for its versatility, adapting to both the world of board games and that of role-playing games, with a fluid integration of game design elements. Thanks to a skilful synergy between game mechanics and narrative, both games use the same Deck of Cards, offering an engaging and satisfying experience for both tabletop players and role-playing game lovers.

—description from the publisher
The legacy of your grandfather is waiting for you. But you will only find this if you can solve the mysterious puzzles that hide in the Jass cards. If too tricky, hint or SOS cards will help you.

boardgame: The Hunters A. D. 1492: Wasserburg Dockers
description: Take on the dockers and their infamous leader Jonas "Stronghand" Ebmer in this mini-campaign featuring new scenarios, enemies, events, and equipment, which can be played as an extension of the main campaign or as a standalone experience.

Two new playable characters are called to join the hunt: Chloe the Witch and Guido the Crossbowman. Chloe is a highly perceptive and experienced caster who uses her magic to detect and anticipate incoming attacks, aid her allies with long-lasting beneficial effects, or curse her enemies. Guido specializes in dealing large amounts of damage from a safe distance and rallying his party to victory.

Contains:
1x Guido miniature
1x Chloe miniature
1x Guido board
1x Chloe board
45x Guido action cards
45x Chloe action cards

“The Greatest Motoring Game of Skill Ever Invented”

An antique Car Racing Game dating from the early Twentieth century c.1910’s.

The game plays on a large, two-fold game board and uses six Racing Car pieces and a dice. It is stated that “the cars move by an entirely new method, no dice or mechanical device being employed.” It therefore appears to be an early ancestor of the better known Formula 1 by Waddingtons.

The Racing Circuit depicted on the board has corners marked “Mountjoy” and “Gough”,  indicating that it is based on the Phoenix Park Circuit in Dublin, Ireland.

Made in England by H. P. Gibson & Sons, London, later known as Gibsons.

Similar to:

    Formula 1

Embrace the shadowy underbelly of society with two new bandit-themed playable characters: Franziska the Brawler and Siegfried the Cutthroat. Franziska is a former noblewoman-turned-rogue who relies on manipulation and dirty tricks to lower the enemy's morale and support her fellow party members. Siegfried is a deadly assassin who specializes in dual-wielding daggers and other short weapons to deal quick and decisive blows to his prey.

Contains:
1x Franziska miniature
1x Siegfriedminiature
1x Franziska board
1x Siegfriedboard
45x Franziska action cards
45x Siegfriedaction cards

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Enclave: Assault Force
description:
This set is part of the CAPITAL wave for Fallout: Wasteland Warfare!

Even the technological superpower that is the Enclave has a need for foot soldiers. Men and women loyal to the President of America, issued with the finest pre-war arms and armor the Enclave has to offer its footsloggers and Special Operatives. Consummate professionals with a pride in their work and training unrivalled across the wastes, an Enclave assault team is a threat few can hope to counter.

—description from the publisher

Suite Dreams is a cooperative game in which the players will have to rearrange dream cards, which are in six suits numbered from 1 to 11.

A number of cards (which varies depending on the chosen difficulty) is placed in a face-down row on the table. The players each have several actions (the amount of which varies depending on the number of cards present and the player count) to secretly look at a card and replace them face-down somewhere in the row, trying to place them in ascending order. When all players used all their actions, the cards are all revealed and if they are in ascending order, the game is won.

The game comes with several variants, including a training mode, the Nightmare Master mode in which they have to rearrange as many cards as possible, and a hidden traitor version when one of the players is secretly the black sheep, trying to sabotage the row.

Embark on a prehistoric adventure where speed, strategy, and excitement collide! DinoCourse offers a frenetic race through prehistoric landscapes, where dinosaurs mounted on quads compete for victory.

With dynamic gameplay mechanics, strategic choices at every turn, and modular terrain tiles, each game is a unique and thrilling experience.

Throughout the race, players must collect food, herbs, and meat to purchase upgrades for their vehicles! Use these resources skillfully to unlock powerful vehicle upgrades and gain an advantage over your opponents!

DinoCourse offers fast-paced races of about 20 minutes, accessible to everyone from 7 years old. Choose from six unique dinosaurs, use a hand of numbered cards to advance. Explore a variety of game modes suitable for different group sizes.

Get ready to experience unforgettable prehistoric adventures with your friends and family.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Las Ligas de la Ñ: Los Juegos de la X
description:
King Alfonso X the Wise has organised an exceptional cultural contest - the Games of the X, similar to the Olympic Games of Ancient Greece in Olympia and the twelve challenges of Caesar in the great Colosseum. Which contestant will get the most medals to obtain the magnificent X of eternal wisdom?

During his life, Alfonso X has sponsored and translated works of different disciplines in his court, such as law and politics (Fuero Real de Castilla, Espéculo, Setenario and Siete Partidas), history (Estoria de España and General estoria), art and music (Cantigas de Santa María and Cantigas de escarnio y maldezir), games (Libro de los juegos, that includes chess & variants, alquerque, dice and tables games, nine men’s morris or the mil game…), science and magic (such as Tablas alfonsíes and Libro complido en los judizios de las estrellas, both about astronomy and astrology; Lapidario, about natural properties of the stones; and Picatrix, about hellenistic magic), and sapiential literature (Sendebar or Libro de los engaños, Calila e Dimna, Liber Scale Machometi and Bocados de oro).

Las Ligas de la Ñ: Los Juegos de la X (The Ñ Leagues: The X Games) is a board game played by 2 to 8 players that contains 15 playable characters of the Middle Ages and Renaissance (Alfonso X, Wallada, Ramon Llull, Christine de Pizan, Maimónides, Urganda the Unknown…).
