boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Electro & Sandman & Shocker & Vulture
description:
Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Electro & Sandman & Shocker & Vulture features a sinister line-up of some of Spider-Man's most noteworthy foes.

Electro, Sandman, Vulture, and Shocker give players even more options when building their Spider-Foes and Criminal Syndicate squads. Supplementing these characters are Sandman's two Sand Constructs that act as Grunts on the battlefield. In addition to miniatures and Stat Cards for each character, players will also find 6 Team Tactic cards that give them even more ways to accomplish their nefarious schemes.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Do you know that wallabies are small kangaroos, right? There you go!
These little wallabies just found a field full of tasty plants, with only an old battered scarecrow to protect some ... they jump & bounce all over, to see who eats the most!

The power of the Shogunate is in shambles, and Japan has fallen into the chaos of the Sengoku Jidai (warring states). Daimyos lead their clans into battle, fighting to become the next Shogun, while peasants rise in revolt. But those are the problems of lords and generals, yours is to lead your men, and achieve honor and glory for your house!

Hard Cover, 166 pages

New rules, characters, traits and equipment

Army lists for 9 major clans of the late Sengoku period and also for Ikko Ikki, Pirates, Ming Chinese, Joseon Koreans, and the Iga uprising

Includes an extensive historical outline of the period by renowned expert Terje Solum author of the Saga of the Samurai books.

—description from the publisher

The Awakening serves as Alpha Clash TCG's first set! Due to recent events on Earth, the Awakening has transformed humans into Alphas, creating a fight for supremacy. Leaving nothing in their wake, the Alphas continue to battle until one remains.

This set features 5 Contenders including Magnate, Moxie, Clarity, Torque, and Mean-Streak. Each Contender offers a unique play style and thrusts players into the action. Who will come out on top as the champion?

Clashgrounds is the second set of the Alpha Clash TCG featuring a new comic book art style and brand-new support.

The Harbingers have arrived! As servants of the Progenitors, they have come to test the Alphas and lay waste to Earth.

Adding seven new Contenders into the mix with Conquest, War, Pestilence, Death, Haven, Machina, and Mean-Streak gives players new options and play styles.

Haven introduces a new game win condition through her Backpack - this set put its stamp on the Alpha Clash!

boardgame: Alpha Clash: Year of the Dragon Draft Box
description:
Year of the Dragon is the first Draft Box for the Alpha Clash Trading Card Game featuring 197 different cards and 20 new alternate arts. Each draft box supports 8 players and contains prizing, Contenders, tokens, and packs for each participant.

This Draft box serves as a functional reprint of Sets 1 and 2. The Rising Empire Studios team hand selected all 197 cards and placed them into the draft box for an incredible draft experience!

Unrivaled is the third official set for the Alpha Clash TCG featuring all new support!

The Discarded have arrived to wreak havoc on the Clashground with their Ambush abilities, led by the gruesome leader, Lord Krung.

Other Contenders such as LINN, Cao Ling, Magnate, and Flare also enter the fight and give support to new and existing characters, affiliations, and colors. This set is a game-changing set to the Alpha Clash TCG.

Power Brawl is a print-and-play, single-player card game. It consists of only 7 cards, and is played entirely within one hand. Each round, the player will rearrange the cards within their hand to set up the best offensive strikes, supporting moves, and surprising combos.

Each card, representing one super-powered character, can be used in 8 different ways, depending on whether that character is a heroes or villain, healthy or wounded, and attacking or defending. Once all villains in your hand are removed, the heroes win!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Village Pillage: Dark Arts and Crafts
description:
Life in the village has always revolved around turnips. Growing them, hoarding them, stealing them...some say they can even be eaten!

But recently this simple life has been disturbed by rumors of magic, of dragons, and of a SECOND type of vegetable. Some call it heresy, others call it a pumpkin. You call it opportunity.

Harness the power of these lumpy orange gourds to conjure beasties, transmute turnips, and show those others villages what TRUE villagery looks like!

Dark Arts and Crafts adds a whole new deck of spell cards to Village Pillage, each with a powerful effect. These cards can only be cast by using a brand new resource: Pumpkins! To help gather these powerful veggies, you start with a brand new set of starter cards.

In addition to the above, a new collection of Market Cards can be shuffled into the deck to expand the variety of villagers.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Battered Bastards of Bastogne: Panzer Dice 4, in the Bulge
description:
The Siege of Bastogne was a confrontation between American and German forces in the Belgian city of Bastogne, which was part of the larger Battle of the Bulge during World War II. The siege lasted from December 20 to 27, 1944, when the besieged Americans were relieved by elements of General Patton's Third Army.
For their incredible feats, courage and endurance, the soldiers of the 101st Airborne Division were called by the press of their time “The Battered Bastards of Bastogne.”

Panzer Dice 4, In the Bulge, is the fourth part of the classic Roll and Write solitaire game, Panzer Dice. This time set in the Battle of the Bulge and is made up of four titles, of which two are German missions and two are American missions about this chapter of the Second World War.

—description from the designer

Players develop their colony by laying tunnels, chambers, and harmful tiles. First to finish wins.

Players take turns selecting and laying new tunnel tiles or play a harmful tile on a player’s colony, or removing a harmful tile from their own. Over time each player will develop a Colony of tunnels and chambers full of eggs, larva, food, and more. Each tile placed will have some kind of impact on the players Colony during the game. The game will end when one player finishes off their Colony by placing all 7 chambers and closing off all open tunnels.

boardgame: What do you Meme?: Mostly Uncensored
description:
The What Do You Meme? Adult Party Game is fun for friends from the social media generation. This is the Mostly Uncensored version.  Contains no profanity.  However due to mild adult themes and language, viewer discretion is advised.

This party card game is simple to teach and quick to play. Every player selects one of the caption cards from their hand to accompany that round's image and plays it in secret. The judge then selects which caption they think is the funniest, and that caption's player gets a point. You can play to whatever score you like.

Contents: 360 caption cards, 75 photo cards, 1 easel, and Bonus Rules

Vie for control of Jerusalem and the supremacy of the Middle East as one of the four major powers of the medieval world: the Latin Christian Crusaders, the Orthodox Christian Byzantine Empire, the Shia Fatimid Caliphate, or the Sunni Seljuks. Refight and change the history of the two first Crusades!

Lords of Heaven is an area control and card placement game. You, the leaders of each of the four asymmetric factions, will play action cards to take your actions such as build units, fortresses, and temples, move troops, fight battles, and develop your Middle East holdings.

The game is a combination of rivalry and cooperation between two blocks of factions – Christian and Islamic – where combat and conquest are not always the best ways to achieve success. Politically nuanced and steeped in its early crusades setting, Lords of Heaven will offer a couple of short scenarios and a campaign.

—description from the publisher

Pandamonium is a lightweight board game with card drafting and set collection mechanics. It's played over 4 rounds of simultaneous play where you'll be drafting cards to collect tributes for the Panda Lords.

In Pandamonium, you will bribe the Panda Lords and Usurpers to secure your place of influence within the Pandapire. Select your Tributes wisely and navigate the chaos of the Pandamonium Cards to ensure that when the dust settles, your influence remains supreme.

Gather your tribute and bribe your way to victory by gaining the favour of as many of the Panda Lords and Usurpers as you can. But don't get too comfortable with your hand it won't be yours for long.

At the end of the 4th round, the winner is the player with the most unique Favour Tiles.

—description from the designer

UPO the cat is seeking a tycoon to accompany him. Are you clever enough to become the greatest, wealthiest, and most successful financial manager?

In UPO, players will try to earn money to fulfill their achievements and to pay all their debts. Earned achievement and money will score points. However, your score will be reduced by the remaining debt you still have.

At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher

Xấp Sắc is a game inspired from traditional Vietnamese Culture. The game is simple but very deep, easy to teach but hard to master.

The starting player begins to play one or more cards to create a column of a row that is one of these combinations:  A pair, three of a kind, four of a kind, 3 cards straight, or 4 cards straight.

In the clockwise order, players have to play card(s) to create a row or a column that is equal to higher in rank than the previous play. If they don’t want to or can't, they must pass. If all players but one passes, that player becomes the leader and can play any combination. Then the game keeps going.

Everyone will continue to play until one player remains with cards on hands, then the game ends and they score points.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Holy Moly: New Testament 홀리몰리 신약성경
description:
Let's match the order of the books of the Bible.
A game played using the order of the New Testament and card colors.
It uses a very simple and familiar mechanism.
As long as you can read, you can play, and the player who first lays down all their cards to have an empty hand wins.

The front of the card has the book name, chapter, and verse of the Bible, and the back has the corresponding Bible verse.
The goal of this game is not to memorize Bible verses, but to get familiar with playing with the Bible.

A Bible verse deeply engraved in your heart during childhood
can be a great strength during the most difficult times of your life.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Holy Moly: Old Testament 홀리몰리 구약성경
description:
Let's match the order of the books of the Bible.
A game played using the order of the Old Testament and card colors.
It uses a very simple and familiar mechanism.
As long as you can read, you can play, and the player who first lays down all their cards to have an empty hand wins.

The front of the card has the book name, chapter, and verse of the Bible, and the back has the corresponding Bible verse.
The goal of this game is not to memorize Bible verses, but to get familiar with playing with the Bible.

A Bible verse deeply engraved in your heart during childhood can be a great strength during the most difficult times of your life.

—description from the designer

In 街コロライフ / Machi Koro: Life, the newest title in the Machi Koro series, instead of building up cities, players will use engine-building cards to collect "hearts", which represent different aspects of life such as hobbies or jobs, in an effort to ultimately exchange these hearts for "dream" points depicted on another kind of cards on display.

The core mechanisms of rolling one or two dice and activating blue/red/purple cards remain essentially the same, and one can either buy another engine-building card or a card with "dream" points after rolling the dice and obtaining the corresponding amount of hearts.

The first player to obtain 8 dream points will have attained their ideal life and wins the game.

The Suprématie ("Supremacy") box contains additional factions for Diktat, such as the Edification Directorate, capable of building Complexes on stack slots, or Harmony, which uses the fear instilled by Others in the minds of citizens as a source of power.

In addition, the Others faction is reinforced with specific counters and three "Aberrations", specific Others with special abilities coming into play as soon as their counters are revealed.

—description from the publisher (translated)

La boîte "Suprématie" contient des factions additionnelles pour Diktat, comme la Direction de l'Édification capable de construire des Complexes sur des emplacements de pile ou l'Harmonie, qui utilise la peur instillée par les Autres dans l'esprit des citoyens comme source de pouvoir.

En outre, la faction Autres se voit renforcée de pions spécifiques et de trois "Aberrations", des Autres spécifiques aux capacités particulières entrant en jeu dès que leurs pions sont révélés.

—description from the publisher

Gifts Galore is a festive group gifting game that levels up the fun of opening holiday presents together with your friends and loved ones.

Work your way around the board answering festive trivia questions as you open presents and mystery gifts, on your way to winning the Surprise Prize!

Includes alternate rules for playing at parties, with coworkers, and throughout the holiday season!

—description from the publisher

This game has the same rules as Gifts Galore: Christmas, but with a different theme and a different set of questions.

Collect as many complete sets of different kinds of mushrooms as possible.
On your turn you will roll the die and gather mushrooms of one single colour.

Mages allow you to manipulate the die or the display in order to collect more mushrooms or for more variety to choose from.
Their support is very valuable, but it only lasts until you cover them up with more mushrooms.

When everyone is familiar with the game, you can add the optional Miraculous Mushrooms Module to spice the game up a bit.

—description from the designer

With Startup High, you're not just playing a game – you're living the startup journey. Picture yourself as the founder writing your own startup's destiny. From the first spark of an idea to the sizzle of success, every move you make counts.

This is a strategic board game having elements that reflect the experiences and challenges of a startup founder in a fun way. Players take on the role of an aspiring founder and make decisions relevant to being entrepreneurial.

 The players have been incubated with their startup ideas and to make their dreams into reality, the founders face an interesting dilemma: what is more important to them; time or money? Using the saved-up resources, the founders have to hire team members across different divisions, leverage their network, and navigate through market forces, to grab the right opportunities!

Startup High isn't just a board game; it's a gateway to understanding the entrepreneur's world. Feel the adrenaline of uncertainty, the thrill of innovation, and the sweet taste of success. Will you rise to the top through calculated risks and bold decisions? The future of your startup is in your hands.
Get ready to roll the dice (not literally), make strategic moves, and let your entrepreneurial spirit soar in Startup High. Unleash your inner founder, and ‘dive deep’ into the game!

—description from the designer

A board game that makes reference to the addresses described in the book The Interior Castle, by Santa Teresa d’Ávila and, thus, consists of advancing through the mystical stages of the soul to reach heaven. Divided into a maximum of 3 teams, with at least 2 players in each, the houses advance as letters are answered through mime, drawing, quiz, tips... guaranteed fun with lots of instruction and spirituality for family and friends , catechesis.

The game contains: 1 board, 750 cards, 1 easy/hard dice, 1 card dice, 1 numeric dice, 3 pawns, 7 special cards, 1 rule manual and 1 sheet with answers. Each team will consist of 2 to 8 players.

With these solo rules you can play Karvi alone in two different ways using the same core mechanic.
Both variants let you play the game using the normal rules, but against a simplified opponent.
Neither variant requires any additional components.
The Highscore Mode is a beat-your-own-score for you to learn the game and improve your
score. It uses simple rules where the opponent blocks spots and removes some cards and tiles.
The Battle against the Völva adds some opponent rules and is intended to give you a challenge
where you have to beat the opponent‘s score.

—description from the publisher

S.O.B. is a dynamic card game filled with Bandits, Duels, and a Few Sons of Bandits.

Players take turns acting out cards from the Bandit deck. Guess the word, and earn the Bandit.  But beware....some of the outlaws are S.O.B.s!

Strategically Duel opponents to steal Bandits or become the new Sheriff in town.

Goal is to collect the most Bandits before time runs out.

Can be played in either teams or individuals.

—description from the publisher

Want to play with children under 8? Gifts Galore has you covered with an expansion deck!

Whenever your Малыш (Little One) lands on a question spot, draw from this deck to ask them an age-appropriate trivia question so they can fully join in on the fun.

Want to play with children under 8? Gifts Galore has you covered with an expansion deck!

Whenever your Little One lands on a question spot, draw from this deck to ask them an age-appropriate trivia question so they can fully join in on the fun.

boardgame: The End of a Reign: The Battle of Naseby, June 14, 1645
description: The End of a Reign is volume 7 in Roberto Chiavini's set of games on famous battles of the English Civil War.
family: Series: English Civil War (High Flying Dice)

boardgame: Montrose Triumphs: The Battles of Auldearn and Alford
description: Montrose Triumphs is the 8th game in a series of ten games by Roberto Chiavini on famous battles fought in the English Civil War.

Welcome to Patas Arriba, where the pond is total chaos, and nothing is in its place! In this quacky, fast-paced game of reflexes, you'll throw cards to knock down rival ducks while defending yourself from flying flip-flops, axes, and anything else that comes your way. Duck Up and cover—no one is safe!

The game ends when a duck has been knocked down five times, but the real champion is the one whose duck has avoided the card storm the best. Are you ready to Duck Up into this pond and emerge as the last duck standing?

- description from designer

¡Bienvenido a Patas Arriba, donde la charca es un caos total y nada está en su lugar! En este juego de velocidad y reflejos, lanzarás cartas para derribar a los patos rivales mientras te defiendes de chanclas, hachas voladoras y cualquier cosa que venga. ¡Nadie está a salvo!

La partida termina cuando un pato ha sido derribado cinco veces, pero el verdadero campeón será aquel cuyo pato haya soportado mejor la tormenta de cartas. ¿Estás listo para sumergirte en esta alocada charca y salir como el último pato en pie?

- description from designer (spanish)

The Mona Lisa has disappeared! Who could have stolen the most famous painting in the world? Detectives, it’s up to you to find the culprit!
Investigate using the clues given by your information. Chase down suspects and arrest them before other players. Decode the testimonies to find the real culprit!

Use your clue cards to determine the profile of the suspects spotted by the informers, locate them on the map and interrogate them before the other players by moving around the board: the first to have interrogated a suspect from each of the 3 clans wins the game! But it's not over: a cooperative phase allows you to decode the information left by the 3 suspects to unmask the real culprit together!

boardgame: The Binding of Isaac: Four Souls – 6th Anniversary Edmund Booster Pack
description:
To celebrate the sixth anniversary of Four Souls, Edmund McMillen and Maestro Media joined forces to craft three new booster packs for The Binding of Isaac: Four Souls, with the 6th Anniversary Edmund Booster Pack being one of them.

Enjoy fifteen new cards inspired by Edmund's other games.

Welcome to the Triora School of Witches and Wizards!
In Ritus you are students of the ancient arcane arts of this school of magic.
The tests are difficult and require a lot of wisdom from the apprentices!
So take control of the classroom, cast these tests and become an expert in the arcane arts.

In Ritus players compete to see who will be the great sorcerer, you will have cards that will help you complete the orders, or the "RITUS", and as the round monitor, try to block the other player from being able to do so. 
Download all the cards in your hand to earn a prestige point, be the monitor and if a player is unable to download a card, they win the prestige point.

—description from the publisher

Quest number 16: is your party ready for Twilight Souls?

A bizarre eclipse has revealed a hidden land, one suspended far above the surface. Inhabited by all manner of strange beings, it is governed by the Lightkeepers, an ancient society founded by a sun deity to watch over the world below and ensure that the light remains for all. But all that is thrown into chaos as a new danger has arrived with dark powers that threaten to devour the light of every soul in this realm, sending it plummeting to the ground and the world into perpetual darkness. You must rise to the challenge, Champion. Or we may all fall with you.

—description from the publisher

A new expansion for one of the best Deck Builders of all time! Quest number 17: is your party ready for the All-Consuming Hive?

Not since the Eruption has the land around Thunderstone Keep felt such violence as massive worms broke forth, shattering mountains and upending entire towns, climbing high into the sky before hardening to form towering citadels in the surrounding countryside. But no one could have been prepared for when these new structures opened across their sides, unleashing terrifying insectoid creatures to seize what life they could find and drag it back to their homes. Now you must venture inside these living towers to rescue the captives and bring an end to their queen’s influence upon your world. The belly of the beast awaits!

—description from the publisher

Engage in this thrilling, strategic dice game where role-playing steps aside for a mix of strategy and chance. Friends turned rivals face off in intense battles, rolling and scoring dice. Defenders navigate the treacherous effects of the 7 Deadly Sins and epic mythical clashes, while attackers aim to sabotage their opponents’ high scores. Experience the rush of dice-driven excitement—get ready to roll!

Unveil your darker side and get ready to battle your nemeses in Vices and Vengeance—a simple, yet thrilling dice game where heavenly virtues are nowhere in sight. In this perilous world, the Seven Deadly Sins reign, and your goal is to outmaneuver and out-roll your opponents while keeping your score as low as possible. Silver Bullets and Mirrors may be your key to success, but be careful, though; reaching 70 points, often the number of forgiveness, could spell your doom!

The Seven Sins have Colors! You will play with:

     7 larger 6-sided Sin dice (16mm): Lust (Blue), Gluttony


(Orange), Greed (Gold), Sloth (Light Blue), Wrath
(Red), Envy (Green), & Pride (Purple).

     7 smaller 6-sided Sin Dice (14mm).
     1 special 6-sided Pride Die: Purple metal.
     7 "Nemesis” dice: 20-sided & sin-colored.
     6 Mythical Match-up 6-sided dice pairs: Mirror & Medusa, David & Goliath (Maroon - 25mm & 12mm), the Silver Bullet & Werewolf.
     3 Spoiler dice: 12-sided (black), 10-sided (gray), & 8-sided dice (white).
     2 rolling trays with a removable foam insert.
     7 sin-colored "tracker" tokens.


THE GAME
Assign the Sins

     Determine the first player by the highest roll on any 20-sided die (d20). Play goes clockwise.
     The first player rolls the "Mirror" die along with the 7 (16mm) sin dice. That player chooses from any sins matching the mirror die. If none match, roll again. The next rolls 6 sins, etc. until all players have a sin.
     Each player receives their 16mm sin die, the matching d20 (Nemesis die), and sin token.
     A player with Pride also gets the metal purple "Pride" die.


Everybody Rolls!

     The player whose turn it is is called the Defending Player (DP). The DP rolls all 7 of the 14mm sin dice, the Mythical Match-up dice, the 12, 10, 8-sided dice, and their d20 Nemesis die to start a turn (* See the example below).
     The DP does not roll their 16mm Sin die on their turn.
     All other players (Attacking players-APs) roll their 16mm Sin die and their d20 Nemesis die every roll. APs are trying to match the DP's 14 mm sin die of the same color as well as trying to match any Nemesis dice the DP rolls. Upon a match, the DP faces an Effect roll detailed below.
     At least one die must be removed/scored each roll. The DP repeats rolls until the end of their turn. Their turn ends after all dice have been scored/removed.


† APs only need to roll dice they can still match to DP's!

Die Removal & Scoring

     The DP must remove and score at least one die each roll.
     When they have removed all the dice they want to score on a roll, they roll unscored dice again, and the APs roll their dice, too.
     Mythical Match-up pairs must be scored in pairs. (See below)
     A Matching Nemesis die from any AP must be taken by the DP and rolled next roll. It cannot be scored the turn it matches. The DP may still remove any nemesis die/dice that they rolled. For example, if an AP rolls any 19, and the DP rolls 19, the DP can only remove the 19 that they rolled.


Scoring

     The DP wants to score the lowest total in each turn. The way to score low is to roll Max Rolls on dice or match specific dice.
     Max Roll: Any die that rolls its maximum number can score 0.
     Non-Max Roll: Points add up by 1 for each pip less than the max roll. For Example, an 8 on a 12-sided die scores 4.
     Mythical Match-up: If a Mythical Match-up pair matches (e.g., Medusa and Mirror), they both score 0 points! If the bullet lands upright, it is wild and matches any number on the Werewolf die.
     DP's Matching Nemeses or Sins: Any DP rolled Nemesis d20s that match, or any matching penalty Sin dice score 0 points.

Magician's Challenge is a card game where "you don't take tricks, you PERFORM them!"  
   Each player holds a hand of magic props cards; six different types of props, including birds, balls, rabbits, rings, ropes, and wands.  Each card has a point value of 1-6.  Collect sets of props to "perform" 9 different Tricks. For example, playing your Rabbit Props to get points for the Rabbit Trick.  
   Players take the role of one of six magicians, each having special powers to make one of the prop types wild.  So, the magician, Wanda Wands, may use her wild Wands Props to add points to her Rabbit Trick.
   Magician's are sneaky and may play SNEAKY cards to gain minor advantages over their opponents, such as pick pocketing Props cards from another magician's hand. 
   Perform each trick once and add up the scores to see who will win Magician's Challenge.

Following a series of expeditions—some successful, others not—into the frigid Siberian tundra, corruption has spread from animals and people to a much larger threat. New heroes, better prepared than their predecessors, gather to face the prospect of a mechanical monster, but the changing landscape brings greater uncertainty as they explore Tunguska with fresh eyes.

The Gears of Corruption expansion to Expeditions adds elements of risk and danger in the form of secret bonuses on map tokens and a corrupted mech that roams the land. To counter these threats, the expansion offers 2 new mechs and 4 new character/companion pairs, as well as components for a 6th player. Gears of Corruption also accelerates early gameplay by giving players starting resources and a hero worker that can act as any type of worker.

—description from the publisher

description:
Simple actions yield deep strategy in the sacred and enchanted lands of Grunndavell. Vie to build a hallowed village within the secluded forest glades under the watchful boughs of the sacred tree.

Within simple and easy to learn mechanics lies deep strategy, myriads of opportunity costs, and layers of agonizing trade-offs. Think, plan, and build a superior village, understand the mysteries of the Ciphers, and become one with the sacred tree.

—description from the designer

Put your people skills to the test in this easy to learn, thrilling, and deceptively engaging game of people-reading, deal-making, and politicking.

Make deals to buy votes to further your agenda while limiting the success of your opponents’ agendas. Negotiate, deceive, deal, and betray. All is fair when furthering your agenda.

Now onward, you diminutive miscreant! For party, for self, and for the agenda!

—description from the designer

Showcase your command of the language with Similarities! In the time allowed, find as many synonyms for the chosen word as you can. List as many as you can and get points for matching the synonyms listed.

Or, you can take a risk and list a single antonym. If you match this, then you get the most points, but beware, if you miss on the antonym, its points count against your total.

So are you excited? Elated? Trepid? Nervous? Ambitious? Get ready for a robust game that engages and exercises your brain.

—description from the designer

Band Wars is a card game for 3/4 players in which the aim is to form your own band and play at concerts of different importance.
The Musician cards depict the members of the Band and must be placed in the Tour Bus of the Board.
When you draw a Musician, you must decide which instrument to make him play and place him, accordingly, on the corresponding Tour Bus space on your board.
Try to limit the competition from other bands, to win silver and gold records up to the platinum record.

Become the mighty knight, embark on an epic solo journey in Dice Tales: Knight's Adventures. In this immersive sandbox game, you’ll put on the armor of a valiant knight, venturing across the kingdom lands. Your brave action in one location will echo in the others, shaping your destiny and changing the course of your adventure. Dice Tales: Knight's Adventures is a game where every choice carves a path to greatness. Will you rise to become the realm’s most celebrated hero? The dice are cast, and your tale awaits!

The choice is yours, decide where you want to go in each round to gain fame and glory. Compete in grand knight tournaments, rescue ladies in distress, explore evil dungeon, embark on adventure tract, try to make your path to fight the fearsome dragon, or come to one of the towers to increase your fame with one of the adventures.  Throughout your travels gather a fortune in gems and gold, and use them to your advantage. Encounter a cast of characters including merchant, ranger, and knight. Sharpen your skills in combat and defense, and gather a quiver of arrows to aid you in your noble adventures. Embark on a knightly adventure and shape your destiny.

Dice Tales: Knight's Adventures is a Roll and Write game created specifically for solo players. You will roll the dice and decide where you want to go each round trying to write your story and gain fame. The game offers diverse paths to victory.

—description from the publisher

La Squadriglia degli Assi simulates biplane battles during the First World War. Are included all the planes that flew on the Italian front during the First World War.
it is a new edition with more modern rules of the 1994 edition.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Arrivano i Francesi! (The French are coming! ) simulates two battles in Italy during the Napoleonic wars: Montebello (1800) and Campotenese (1806).
This game uses a rule system derived from Caldiero 1796 (Para Bellum II) and Voi Bravi ed Infelici (Para Bellum VIII).

Components:

    A2 double-sided map
    Two sheets of 118 die-cut counters
    Rule booklet
    Two game tables


This is Para Bellum magazine "Speciale 2024".
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Quella Volta Che... is an engaging narrative party game with cards, winner of the Italian Game Jam in Bergamo 2023. This game is perfect for fun-filled evenings with friends, where laughter and creativity are always in the spotlight.

How to Play:
Players take turns drawing a card from the deck, each containing unique prompts to create a story. The narrator of the turn must use these prompts to tell an intriguing and surprising story. Meanwhile, the other players listen carefully and try to predict the outcome of the story.

Game Objective:
The main goal is to have as many stories guessed correctly by the other players as possible. Each time someone correctly guesses the outcome of the narrated story, both the narrator and the player who guessed earn points. The game continues until all stories have been told or until players decide to end the session.

Key Features:

Creativity and Improvisation: Each card offers unique prompts that stimulate players' imagination and improvisational skills.
Social Interaction: Perfect for breaking the ice and creating a fun, participatory atmosphere.
Suitable for Everyone: Simple and intuitive rules make the game accessible to people of all ages.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Der Hobbit: Kampf um den Arkenstein – Eine unvorhergesehene Gesellschaft
description:
A variant on a card for The Hobbit: Battle for Arkenstein (Love Letter) in which more than four players can take part.
You will need 2 base games.

Whakaari is an exhilarating party game for 3 to 8 players, and the proud winner of the Italian Game Jam in Naples 2022. Set on a treacherous lava lake, players must jump from platform to platform in a thrilling race to the finish line.

How to Play:
Players take turns to check the status of the platform they are on. Depending on the state of the platform, they may need to draw an event card and resolve its effects. If the player survives the event, they can then play one of their cards to jump to another platform. The length of the jump is measured using the sides of the card.

Special Abilities:
Each player has a unique special power that can be activated to regain ground on their opponents. These powers add a strategic layer to the game, allowing for dramatic comebacks and exciting turns of events.

Game Objective:
The goal is to be the first player to reach the finish line, navigating the perilous platforms and outsmarting opponents with clever use of cards and special abilities.

Key Features:
Dynamic Gameplay: The combination of event cards and strategic jumps keeps the game fast-paced and unpredictable.
Special Powers: Unique abilities for each player add depth and strategy to the game.
High Replayability: The variety of event cards and special powers ensures that no two games are the same.

—description from the publisher

As the lead investigator, you are summoned to unravel the mysterious death of Isabella Novakova, a renowned socialite found dead under puzzling circumstances. The Elite Grand Enigma challenges players to navigate through a labyrinth of clues, conflicting alibis, and hidden agendas.

Amidst a cast of six unique suspects, each with their own secrets and connections to the victim, your task is to dissect alibis, analyze forensic evidence, and scrutinise witness statements. From a disillusioned bartender with dreams beyond the hotel, to a high-profile politician caught in a scandalous web, each character brings a layer of complexity to the case.

"Porca Vacca!" is an exciting card party game for 3 to 8 players, with game sessions lasting between 30-45 minutes. The goal is to be the last player standing by eliminating all your opponents through strategic card play and careful hand management.

How to Play:

Shuffle the deck of cards and place it in the center of the table.
Each player draws a card on their turn.
If a player draws a green, yellow, or blue card, they must immediately apply its effect.
If the drawn card is of another color, it is added to the player's hand.
If a player ends their turn with 5 or more cards in hand, they are eliminated from the game.
Game Objective:
The objective is to outlast all other players by managing your hand and using the effects of the cards wisely to eliminate opponents.

Key Features:

Quick and Easy Setup: Just shuffle the deck and start playing.
Strategic Depth: Players must balance between applying card effects and managing their hand size to avoid elimination.
Interactive and Dynamic: Constant player interaction keeps the game engaging and unpredictable.
Fast-Paced Fun: The game’s quick rounds and simple rules make it perfect for parties and gatherings.

—description from the publisher

Ayrumã is a trick-taking game with an astronomy theme from the native Brazilian people. Even ahead of the knowledge used by Europeans in the 17th century, the Tupinambá people used observation of the skies in their daily lives with a precision compatible with current scientific knowledge, such as the influence of the moon on the movement of the tide.

This inspired our game where each participant will take on the role of leader of a tribe, progressing in different aspects of their daily life, and thus earning victory points. After 3 lunar cycles, the chief of the tribe who best managed his tribe's knowledge is the winner of the match.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Creatures: Nukalurk King
description:
This miniatures set is part of the Nuka World wave for Fallout: Wasteland Warfare!

‘A hail of gunfire for the king, baby.’
Of the Nukalurk Royal Family, the King is the least imposing, but far from the least dangerous. Far more agile than his blushing bride, the King possesses a bestial intelligence and a domineering will that allows him to keep his queen’s progeny in check.

Though they tend to let the more common Nukalurks deal with the messy work of catching and eviscerating their prey, the Kings are more than capable of supporting them from range with a sonic scream that can turn your brain to jelly and melt out of your ears. Makes you feel for misbehaving Nukalurk hatchlings, my dad couldn’t scream like that and my ears still hurt.

This miniatures set is part of the Nuka World wave for Fallout: Wasteland Warfare!

‘At the very least, they’re easier to see coming now…’
Mercifully unique to the advertisement plastered ruins of The World of Refreshment, Nukalurks are a bizarre mutation to the ‘standard’ Mirelurk, suffused with the delicious hue of Nuka-Cola Quantum and a healthy blue glow.

Somehow, Nukalurks are more violently territorial than their original-flavour cousins, and more than one Nuka-enthusiast has been dragged below the surface of the Nuka-World river by these crustacean terrors.

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Call to Arms – Hagraven Coven
description:
For some, the pursuit of power is worth any price. A monstrous blending of human and bird, Hagravens are the result of a ritual to sacrifice their very humanity for increased magical prowess. Twisted and vicious, they shy away from civilisation in the forsaken corners of Skyrim. Adventurers should be wary of them, for they wield deadly magic and fight with terrible talons.

For use with The Elder Scrolls: A Call to Arms Miniatures Core Rules and Chapter 4 Card Pack. Expand your game with other Starter and Expansion Sets also available now.
family: Video Game Theme: The Elder Scrolls

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Call to Arms – Draugr Lords
description:
The oldest and most powerful of all their brethren, the DRAUGR LORDS are the leaders of the undying legions:

     RED EAGLE, the ancient hero of the Reach who led an army to defeat an Empire.
     SIGDIS GAULDURSON is the powerful wielder of the legendary Blackbow.
     CURALMIL, the legendary Alchemist who guards a fabulous treasure. 
     OLAF ONE-EYE, an ancient, legendary, dragon-slaying High King of Skyrim. 
     THE GATEKEEPER, a guardian of an even greater foe, she stands between Adventurers and the secrets of the ancient Dragonborn.
family: Video Game Theme: The Elder Scrolls

description:
Awesome expeditions through uncharted territory!

It is said it is the journey rather than the destination which is more important and Achtung! Cthulhu: Unexplored fills in the blank spaces on the map, agents often simply “redline” through before their next epic encounter.

No corner of the globe is safe from the unholy alliance of cultists and the Mythos, and agents should be prepared to traverse all kinds of dangerous terrain to carry out their assignments.

From the dark depths of perilous caverns to the sweltering jungles of the tropics, from the peaks of snow-capped mountains to the merciless sands of the desert, Unexplored allows you to traverse six separate biomes, randomly generating missions, challenges and adventures, with exciting expeditions through unknown territory.

Create endless forms of wilderness adventure, either on the fly, in preparation for a game session, or as inspiration for adventures of your own.

Discover how weather, hazards and environmental conditions will affect each expedition, explore creative uses for Threat, detect new NPCs and deploy dangerous denizens like panthers, cougars and alligators!

Each chapter concludes with four detailed locations with a main mission and a host of adventure seeds to use as the climax for each journey, or provide an entertaining diversion along the way.

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Raiders: The Disciples
Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Raiders: The Operators
Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Raiders: The Pack
The Elder Scrolls: Call to Arms – Dragonborn Heroes
description:
The path of the Dovahkiin has many branches, and one wielder of The Voice may be very different to another:

     The DRAGONBORN RANGER excels at use of bow and stealth, while their opposite is the DRAGONBORN BERSERKER, whose attacks are unmatched in their ferocity.
     The DRAGONBORN SPELLSWORD matches arcane and martial talents, whilst the DRAGONBORN THU’UM WEAVER focuses on mastery of The Voice.
     The DRAGONBORN CHAMPIONS OF BOETHIA and MEHRUNES DAGON use the powers of The Voice to further their patron’s mysterious goals.
family: Video Game Theme: The Elder Scrolls

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Robots: Starlight Theatre Staff
Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Robots: Space Sentry

description:
Cryptborn is a solo roll-and-write dungeon crawler that is inspired by Darkest Dungeon and the souls-like genre.

You awake from your eternal slumber in the crypt of the castle, something or someone has awoken you from death to purge the castle of the dark forces that have befallen it.

In Cryptborn, you'll design a dungeon by rolling a D6 and then populate it with encounters. Once that's done, you'll run the dungeon and try to survive.

Each dungeon will have a boss but the goal isn't always to kill the biggest, baddest thing in the dungeon but instead to simply survive to get through to the next stage. In this game, 'winning' means staying alive.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Call to Arms – Companions
description: The Companions are an ancient kinship of the greatest and bravest warriors, that also hides a dangerous and savage secret. The Companions are led by the veteran KODLAK WHITEMANE, ageing, but still deadly. Shield-brother to the Dragonborn, FARKAS excels with heavy armour and is a stalwart companion. Second only to Kodlak, SKJOR leads the secretive organisation called The Circle. AELA THE HUNTRESS embraces her savage side and is without equal as a hunter. The secret of the Circles is their ability to transform into either the terrifying WEREWOLF BRUTE or SAVAGE.
family: Video Game Theme: The Elder Scrolls

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Unaligned: X-01 Power Armor
The Elder Scrolls: Call to Arms – The Silver Hand
description:
The Silver Hands have but one purpose - to eradicate the curse of lycanthropy, by whatever means necessary:

     KREV THE SKINNER is their uncompromising, battle-scarred veteran.
     The SILVER HAND WEREWOLF HUNTER specialises in fighting the savage beasts backed up by the SILVER HAND WARRIORS.
     The SILVER HAND HEALER uses potions and herbs to bolster her fellow warriors and keep them free from deadly diseases.
family: Video Game Theme: The Elder Scrolls

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Creatures: Ghoulish Remnants
The Elder Scrolls: Call to Arms – Arnbjorn, Dark Brotherhood Predator
description: Exiled from the Companions because of his “unorthodox” methods, ARNBJORN, DARK BROTHERHOOD PREDATOR is a bestial and savage agent of the Night Mother. As a former member of The Circle, he is able to transform into the fearsome ARNBJORN, WEREWOLF and savage his enemies with his fearsome claws and deadly bite. In either form, he is a formidable foe, or a steadfast ally.
family: Video Game Theme: The Elder Scrolls

description:
In Wild Wild Pattern, you race to create a pattern in the wheel of cards before you. Do so first, and you claim one or more of those cards that will later cash in at the bank. However, if you make the right pattern, you can win the game instantly!

To set up, lay out a wheel of eight cards, then place the deck in the center of this wheel. As on a clock, a card to the right of another card is considered to be "after" that card, and a card to the left is "before". Each card features one of eight objects, one of five colors, and one of two suits.

On a turn, everyone draws a card from the deck and looks at it simultaneously. Whoever thinks that they can create a new pattern plays their card on top of another in the wheel, then identifies their pattern, such as after every red card is a gun or before every dynamite is a bullet. A pattern must include an object, and it can occur one or more times.

If a pattern is true for all instances — for example, a gun is indeed present after every red card — then the pattern creator claims one or two cards from the wheel depending on whether the pattern includes a color or a suit. If the pattern relates to all eight cards, the player wins instantly. (If the pattern is invalid, then everyone else claims a card from the wheel.)

Refill the wheel, then continue taking turns until someone has claimed at least five cards, at which time everyone cashes in their cards at the bank, earning from 20 cents to $3. If someone has exactly five different colors or exactly five of the same object, they win instantly. Otherwise, if someone has a total of at least $3, they win. If no one has this much money, play another round.

Alternatively, you can play the whoever has the least money at the end of a round is eliminated. Keep playing rounds until one player remains.

boardgame: Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Cult of the Mothman: Eclipse
Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Cult of the Mothman: Followers
Fallout: Wasteland Warfare – Cult of the Mothman: Clergy
What On Earth Are You Talking About?: VS. Expansion
description:
VS is an out-of-this-world expansion to What on Earth Are You Talking About?, and this time it’s you versus your opponent in a real-time space race!

With two similar earthly observations on each card, teams race each other to guess their concept first without accidentally guessing the opposition’s concept!

Think you can translate BAPTISMS into something an alien can understand, without anyone guessing DROWNING? Prove it in VS!

—description from the publisher
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

boardgame: What On Earth Are You Talking About?: NSFA Expansion
description:
Warning!!! If you observe humanity for any length of time, you’re sure to see something that is not safe for aliens.

This NSFA expansion for What On Earth Are You Talking About? compiles all the dirtiest, raunchiest and grotesque concepts found on Planet Earth. We've also included a select number of red herring concepts - because once you start thinking like a filthy human, it's impossible to stop.

In What On Earth Are You Talking About?, you are an alien competing against a rival research ship to be the first to locate intelligent life amongst the stars. Instead, you've found Earth. You will be describing “quintessential” human concepts to your teammates, trying to have them guess each concept as quickly as possible. But there’s an extraterrestrial twist! You can only use words that begin with the letters on the Translator Cards, making you talk like an alien!
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
In Robby Solo, you have been inspired by the eponymous thief to rob banks on your own for as much money as possible.

To set up, lay out as many colored bank cards as the number of players, plus one. Each player takes heist cards matching these banks.

The leader for the round lays out one face-up loot card on each bank, with loot showing an item or a value from $1-9,000. Each player places a heist card face down matching the bank they want to rob, then the leader names either the color of the bank they will rob or a color that they won't rob. All other players can then change their heist card if they wish. Finally, reveal all the heist cards. If a color has been chosen only once, that player takes all of the cards from that bank. Everyone takes back their heist cards.

Pass the loot deck, and start a new round. If at the end of a round, four cards remain in a bank, the police arrive, returning all of these cards to the bottom of the deck. Additionally, whoever has the most cards must return their most valuable card to the bottom of the deck.

When not enough loot cards remain to place a card in every bank, the game ends. Players tally their holdings, with items varying depending on how many you have; a single item is worth only $1,000, while two are $10,000 and five are $60,000. Whoever has the highest sum wins.

To score in Purrfect, you must collect all nine poses of the same cat...while imitating a cat at the same time.

To prepare, choose one set of nine cat cards for each player, shuffle them into a single deck, then deal each player a hand of nine cards. Lay out cards showing the numbers 1-4, then place a random "meow" card next to each number from the deck of 29 cards, such as "miyu" or "mrraoo".

Playing simultaneously without turns, players can propose trading cards with one another by making the sound of the number of cards they want to trade. All cards in a single trade must be of the same cat. (Cats are identified by color, name, and an indexed letter.) If someone else meows the same way, you each place face down the appropriate number of cards, swap them, then place the new cards in your hand.

As soon as a player has all nine cards for a single cat in hand, they win the round, earning two pawprint tokens. Among the remaining players, whoever has the most cards of one cat wins one pawprint. Play multiple rounds until someone collects at least five pawprints and wins.

Candies, fruit, popsicles, stars! Take the tile that will help you the most! Make it fall and rotate on your slide to form the biggest group of the same-colored candies.

Don't leave spaces in Pentris as you need to fill in rows as well. Watch for special symbols: Popsicles and stars can get you big points if you can place them well. After three rounds, the game ends. Did you make the best slide and score the most points? If you did, you win.

Get ready to step into the limelight and become the next big thing in Wild Gardens with the community pack! The content inside was voted on by our fans as the most requested ingredients and recipes. The Community Expansion Pack includes 8 new guests, tantalizing recipes, a new location, and a new solo adventure.

—description from the publisher

Prepare for an electrifying cast of characters packed into this expansion! 8 beloved personalities from the tabletop world have arrived in Wild Gardens, each bringing their own unique abilities to the table. With 18 brand new cards, including new guests, tantalizing recipes, a new location, and a new solo adventure, the Media Expansion pack is bursting with exciting new content.

—description from the publisher

Hold onto your sun hats, because the team behind Wild Gardens is here and ready to shake things up! Get ready to unlock new combos and tantalize your taste buds with delectable recipes as you discover more about the people who helped bring Wild Gardens to life. With 18 brand new cards, including new guests, tantalizing recipes, a new location, and a new solo adventure, this pack is bursting with exciting new content. This is an expansion to Wild Gardens, base game not included.

—description from the publisher

Put yourself in the shoes of a contestant from the hit television game show with the official BOOM game. An exciting game in which a team of contestants must defuse bombs by answering several questions correctly within a certain time. Discard the wrong answers and prevent the bomb from exploding.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Ponte en la piel de un concursante del exitoso concurso de televisión con el juego oficial de BOOM. Un trepidante juego en el que un equipo de concursantes debe desactivar bombas respondiendo a varias preguntas correctamente dentro de un determinado tiempo. Descarta las respuestas incorrectas y evita que explote la bomba.

—description from the publisher

"At first, the cries of survivors were few and far between. They clung to hope, and fought, and wept. There was no one to bury. Nothing to see. Just roots, everywhere..."

"DEADHOLT:Don't Stray!" is a moderately competitive pick-up-and-deliver game with character-building elements, set in a gritty post-apocalyptic universe. In this alternate reality, an alien Life-Seed crashes onto Earth, quickly overgrowing into a planet-wide, titanic forest. Survivors call this strange new environment "The Holt".

While playing, you assume the role of a Guide: A veteran pathfinder with unique scores and abilities. 
Your main objective is to ensure that, by the end of the game, you will have collected the most Metal Scrap(in-game currency). HOW you do that, though, is entirely up to you! In DEADHOLT, there are many ways for players to earn Metal Scrap: Escorting Clients to their destinations, hunting Beasts, fighting other Guides, doing profitable trades or even scavenging around for coins! Players are free to explore and find their preferable play-style. This "Sandbox" nature of DEADHOLT paired with its high variability motivates players to make meaningful decisions and carve a different, personalized path each time they play the game.

Asymmetric and interactive, DEADHOLT promotes smart movement, enforces player agency and tactical thinking while also bringing in small doses of controlled randomness in order to deliver a fun and exciting experience. With over 120 mechanically unique cards, custom dice and tokens in the box, players will often find themselves in tricky situations, while still having the tools to come up with a way out.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Tower of Trivia: Race to the Top!
description:
It's time to aim high in this unique trivia game!  With 520 general knowledge questions to play from, teams take turns to try and climb up the tower by correctly answering the questions on the cards.  The questions get trickier the higher up the tower you go.  With just two minutes on the clock, will you be able to reach dizzying heights, or will you get stuck on a level as the time ticks down?

Played over three rounds, teams score points based on the difficulty of the questions they are able to answer before the timer runs out.  Higher difficulty questions can only be unlocked if the lower level questions are successfully answered, thus climbing the tower.  An incorrect answer drops you back down to the easiest tier of questions, which are worth fewer points, forcing you to climb back up if you want to increase your score faster (at the risk of dropping back down again).

boardgame: Blueprints of Mad King Ludwig: Promo Card Pack 1
description:
What kind of monarch could you possibly be without a proper room to celebrate the glory that is you? Fear not for the Lords of Bezier have delivered room schematics of such divinity to make you weep with antici...

CONTENTS
1 Wine Tasting Room
1 Book Nook
1 Time Out Room
1 Sax Serenade Room
1 Ruby Ring Room

Rooms from the Promo Card Pack 1 were designed by Tantrum House, the Game Boy Geek, and 2 gamers from the community: Bailey Osborne & Griffin.

...pation

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Castles of Mad King Ludwig
Misc: Watch It Played How To Videos
Versions & Editions: Roll- or Flip-and-Write Versions of Non-Writing Games

description:
The gravity game made from recycled fridges.

Stack 'm - don't drop 'm!

Rockeees are nestable plastic blocks.
Each block has different 17 sides - so they can be stacked in many ways.

Rules:
Each player takes his own set of Rockeees. You start building one pile. You go on turns. You drop the pile, you lose.  (A bit like Jenga).

Or you could have two players or two teams and set the challenge who builds a tower of 6 Rockeees in the fastest time. You can make it more difficult by placing the first Rockeee for both teams in one of 15 positions.

Embark on a cosmic adventure in the rugged world of Magneterra, a distant planet characterized by its towering mountains, deep valleys, and flowing rivers. This unique terrain, enriched with rare elements, is the birthplace of the mystical Tochon Magnets—ancient artifacts infused with immense magical energies. Drawn by the allure of these powerful entities, human Mage Warriors have come to Magneterra, driven by a thirst for magical knowledge, each aspiring to become the most powerful mage.

Magneterra introduces an innovative game mechanic centered around the fascinating properties of magnetism. Players begin the game with the ability to attract Tochon Magnets from the shortest pillars on the game board, utilizing specially designed figurines that interact with magnetic fields. As players gather more magnets, their magnetic powers increase, enabling them to draw from increasingly taller pillars. The ultimate challenge lies in being the first to claim the Tochon Magnet from the tallest pillar, a feat that not only requires strategic maneuvering and skillful play but also crowns the winner as the supreme Mage Warrior of Magneterra.

However, the quest for magnetic supremacy is fraught with danger. The native Thians—part metal, part flesh giants—and their loyal Ankylocat companions fiercely defend the Tochons. These formidable opponents roam the landscape, determined to thwart the Mage Warriors and preserve the natural balance of their planet. As tensions rise, players must decide whether to confront these monsters directly or find more cunning ways to navigate the perilous terrains and claim the ultimate magnetic prize. With every move fraught with peril and opportunity, who will emerge victorious, and at what cost? The battle for Magneterra's Tochon Magnets is about to begin!

—description from the publisher

Cognizant is a solo or co-operative tactical deck-builder. Play as giant biomechanical mecha through a series of encounters crafting a unique deck and unlocking other powerful equipment along the way.

Players will choose from one of four playerboards known as "Echoes" and build an initial deck called a configuration indicated on the back of the Operations and Configuration card. This initial deck is built from a mix of basic action cards and signature action cards that are specific to each "Echo"; These are used to attack, move, defend, or trigger other abilities in combat to defeat enemies. Next, players will choose the starting stage map and populate it with encounter tokens based on the chosen difficulty. Players win by completing the three stage maps and defeating each stage boss enemy.

A secondary Endless mode is available to play, during which players will complete ever increasingly difficult encounters gaining points from unlocking cards and other upgrades. Each stage completion adds additional difficulty for players to overcome. The goal of this mode is to play until you are inevitably defeated and see how high a score you can accumulate.

—description from the publisher

Fósil takes us into the depths of the desert and turns us into true paleotologists in search of the most amazing animals in the history: the Dinosaurs.
Be careful, you won't be the only one on this journey... avoid the traps of other researchers and use everything at your disposal to become the number 1 paleontologist.
Collect the different fossil pieces of the 6 types of dinosaurs, use support and special cards to facilitate your game and sabotage your rivals; and of course, gain the Jurassic power of the dinosaurs by controlling the space as you wish.

BioForge lets your creativity shine in a fast-paced Creature Creator Card Game for 2+ players. Over the course of the game, you will draft and build your creature and face off against your rival's own challenger. Mixing in-depth strategy as you combine different animal attributes with luck of the die roll will leave you never playing the same game twice.

Galaxy Hunt Activate: Galactic Hegemony is a space war themed dexterity game. On a player's turn, they spin the wheel to determine which spaceship piece need to be taken out. The player has to choose carefully to get points, as there are also negative-scoring pieces. When a player accidentally dislodges a piece, the player receives negative score if the piece/s is negative-scoring. Player with most points at the end of game wins.

—user summary

A simple and fast abstract game for one player about some of the problems posed for military, government and non-government organizations when they are tasked with assisting large numbers of people on the move. The people shown in this abstracted model are moving away from some kind of threat - a battle or invasion, a disaster, a violent mass movement, some kind of monster protected by copyright legislation, etc.. The United States Department of Defense calls these people Dislocated Civilians or DCs; the Department of State calls them Internally Displaced Persons, and other organizations variously call them refugees, evacuees or other descriptive terms. 

The game is played in turns. In each turn cards representing groups of DCs will be drawn from a deck of ordinary playing cards and placed at the top of a notional grid of spaces arranged in rows and columns, while other card groups move downwards towards the bottom of the grid. Think of columns of people moving down the Lines of Communication, away from whatever started them moving.

The player will move their unit counters representing organizations and services to place them with groups of cards, and then roll dice to obtain a score that will allow all, some or none of the cards in the groups to be removed from the game (signifying that their primary needs have been addressed, so that in the short term at least they may be considered safe or healthy or settled).

But the needs and demands to be satisfied are larger than the resources and services available, so some cards will exit off the bottom of the Field; these cards are “lost”. The game is over when all the cards have made their way through the Field, and the Player’s level of success depends on the total values of cards that were lost.

—description from the designer

Hexer is a dice management game. In it, you are a wizard who must obtain the golden discord apples from the goddess Eris. You must use your magic to move 5 souls along her temple to achieve the goal, before the other wizards can do it. Beware! winning a battle will not always lead to victory.

Eris´s temple is the main board of the game. On predetermined spaces will be placed 8 golden apple and 10 colored souls (5 of each color per player).

Each round, players will roll 5 dice and then reroll any number of them up to one time. Then, each of them secretly place one by one over each soul symbol on he/she personal board behind a screen.

When all dice are placed, each player remove his/her screen. Each souls´s willpower (dice value) will be compared one by one with its color counterpart. The one with a greater value will move forward and the one with the lowest will be move backwards. The willpower of 1 it´s a wild, so it can be move on step to any direction. If there is tie between souls´s willpower, neither of them can be moved (even with 1).

If a soul reach a space with a golde apple, it is placed over the player´s board on that soul space. That player´s soul can´t be moved for the rest of the game, but it´s will power will keep affecting the movement of it´s opponent soul counterpart.

After all the souls will power are compared, a new round begins.

When a player reach up to 3 golden apples with his/her souls, game ends immediately.

Here in Hexer the Eris temple it´s full of surprises. Areas that can´t be reached by a wild will power, wormholes that teleport your soul to another corner of the board and much much more.

Take on the role of a detective, tasked with solving the murder of construction company owner Neil Collins in a local park in Mercia-upon-Tame. With multiple suspects to investigate, players must use their deductive skills to sift through physical evidence, witness statements, and red herrings to determine who is responsible for the crime. From a drug dealer with a history of violence, to a business rival with a hidden agenda, to a homeless man with a mysterious past, every suspect has their secrets and motivations that players must unravel.

—description from the publisher

Benefits and Burdens is an immersive detective game set in the shadowy corners of Amberwell, where players are thrust into the heart of an enigmatic disappearance. Players take on the role of detectives tasked with solving the perplexing case of Liam Davis’s sudden vanishing.

Structured as a non-linear narrative, the game empowers players with unrestricted access to a wide array of evidence from the get-go. Sift through digital footprints, comb through various types of communications, and analyse a collection of items and documents that could hold the key to unraveling the mystery. Analytical skills and attention to detail will be paramount as you navigate through the evidence, piecing together clues and drawing connections.

At the core of the investigation lie four suspects, each with their own complex web of motives and secrets. It’s up to players to delve into their backgrounds, scrutinise their alibis, and determine how they fit into the bigger picture of Liam’s disappearance. The game challenges players to think critically and question everything, as appearances can be deceiving, and the truth is often hidden beneath layers of lies and misdirection.

—description from the publisher

At the beginning of each turn, players select two Victory Goal cards. A player can win the game by fulfilling the requirements specified on either of the selected cards.

boardgame: 新信長風雲録 (Shin Nobunaga Fuun-roku)
description:
Operational level wargame covering for battles of Oda Nobunaga over Kinai region. It was published as Game Journal Issue 87. Shin Nobunaga Fuun-roku means New Chronicles of Oda Nobunaga.
The game takes its name and theme from the old Tsukuda Hobby game 信長風雲録 (Nobunaga Fuun-roku), but is not a reprint or update of that game, but rather a completely new design.

The basic rules use the later version of the Series: Sengoku Gunyuden.
Players organize forces around their leaders and select actions for each force at each stage, such as movement, battle, siege battles, reorganization, and morale recovery.

It includes 5 scenarios based on campaigns that took place between 1568 and 1573.
Victory conditions are set for each scenario, but generally involve comparing the accumulated victory points gained by capturing castles and eliminating units.

Compared to other games in the Sengoku Gunyuuden series, there are many short-span scenarios and solo scenarios for practice are also included, making this a good introductory game to the series.

Contents

    Number of counters: 252 pieces
    Map: 1
    Game Period: September, 11th year of Eiroku era (1568) / June to December, 1st year of Genki era (1570)



Note: 新 means new
信長 is Nobunaga.
風雲録 (Fūun-roku) means "brave records". See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brave_Records_of_the_Sanada_Clan.

boardgame: Galactic Renaissance: Astute Diplomats Game Up
description:
This tiny expansion for Galactic Renaissance adds the astute diplomat specialist card and minis to your game, as well as a new planet.

Kettii  is a 2-dice Strategy Board Game developed and published under Anileconn, whose gameplay follows that of classic-board games like Pacheesi. 
The game can be played by 2,3 or 4 players each moving 3 pieces around circular rings until they reach their homes in the center of the board. 
The objective of the game is for a player to reach his/her home first.

Board Layout
The board consists of 4 player bases/yards at each corner coloured red, blue, yellow and green, each with 3 slots where each player's pieces start off. 
There are 3 concentric rings that players must go through to reach their home - the large circular area in the center of the board divided into 4 colors for each player.The board has 2 colored lanes for each player that connect the player's base and home that are safe zones for every player. The white lane in-between these safe lanes with arrows pointing inward is the rising lane and allows players to move to an inner ring.

Game Play
Players roll the 2 dice while taking clock-wise turns for example in the order red, blue, yellow, green – if the chosen starting player is red. 
The player who rolls a 6 on any of their two dice can start and move any of his/her pieces onto the starting tile. At this point, the piece is said to be in play. They can then move the value of the other dice on the piece in play. 
After rolling, a player can move the value of any dice on any of the movable pieces in play until all the values on the 2 die have been used/moved. 
If a player has no pieces in play after rolling, the game's turn moves to the next player.

Players keep moving their pieces clockwise until they find their rising lane at which point they can move to an inner/higher ring and continue moving clockwise until they reach their home.

If a player's piece lands on an opponent's piece when done moving, the opponent's piece is displaced and sent one ring back/outside maintaining the lane the piece was in i.e the opponent's piece moves exactly 40 steps back.  
If an opponent is displaced from the outer most ring (the ring containing the starting tiles), the player return to his/her base.  
However, opponents cannot be displaced from safe zones. Any of the colored tiles on the board are safe zones for all players i.e no piece can be displaced from these regions. Additionally, the rising lane of a specific player is a safe zone for that player alone e.g. a blue player cannot be displaced from the blue rising lane, but can be displaced from any other player's rising lane.

Players can reroll in any of these cases;
- After rolling a double i.e the same number on all the die.
- After a roll that enabled a player to leave their base.
- After a roll that enabled a player to reach their home.
- After displacing an opponent's piece.

—description from the designer

One day, the fox family decided to go on a picnic. It's worth preparing well for such a trip! You will need a blanket, toys and snacks so as not to play on an empty tummy. The mother fox asked her children to pack everything they would need. However, you need to do it as soon as possible, because the adventure begins in a moment! Liski is a quick card game that develops reflexes and observation. Includes 5 additional variants, designed to both educate and entertain.

There are 5 variants of the game, here is a quick summary of each:

     Each player receive the same set of items on the cards. When the task card is flipped everyone has to choose matching cards from their hand and place them in a grid 2x2 with correct orientation to fulfill the task. The first player to do it receives the task card and the round is over. The first player to win 5 rounds wins the game.
     At the beginning of the game the item cards are placed at the table in a grid 3x3. The active player has to flip two cards that are the same on the other side. Almost like classic memory... but! The cards are not onesided, both of the sides show items, so when the player reveals non-matching cards they do not flip them again and just leave as they are. The game ends when there are no cards to refill the grid and whoever collected the most is the winner.
     Only task cards are used. The players place the cards from their hands to cover the card on the table. Each matching symbol provides 1 point. After all the cards are played whoever gathered most points wins.
     Four task cards are placed in the middle in a grid 2x2. Now all the players together have to build the same configuration but with a item cards. This should be done three times and the game is over.
     Two task cards are revealed and the players quickly count the most present symbol. Then they need to play a matching item card(s) from their hand. The first player to do it receive the task cards. After the task cards are depleted the game ends and whoever gathered most wins.


—description from the publisher

The fox family is looking for a cozy place for themselves. Will you help them build a beautiful hole? This is quite a challenge. Tools and colorful paints will be used. Each room is unique. Be careful not to let your structures collapse like a house of cards. Once you feel confident, you can introduce new challenges to the game that will make each construction adventure unique.

In the game each player receives the same set of 5 room cards, 2 floor cards and 1 entrance to the hole. When the task card is flipped everyone build their hole according to the task card. The first player to build correct hole receives 3 points. Others may gain 1 or 2 depending on the number of levels they finished. After fifth round the player with most points is the winner.

Advanced players may want to use tool cards which define the way players may use their hands.

—description from the publisher

Valdivia

EN:

Valdivia is a roll & write / print & play game in which you will have to rebuild the city of Valdivia, south of Chile, after the largest earthquake in human history.

You will manage resources to rebuild the city while providing shelter and food to the people trapped in the flood that subsequently covered the area.

You will manage resources to rebuild the city while providing shelter and food to people trapped in the flood that subsequently covered the area.

To play Valdivia you'll need 3d6, a pencil and an eraser. 
The game is divided into 4 rounds, each with 3 phases. In phase 1, you collect money using dice. In phase 2, you invest that money in resources and, in phase 3, you gain or erase - depending on your actions and decisions - damage points.

You will rebuild the city with workers and investments, build emergency structures for the people using wood, send them food in boats while the streets are underwater, and fix the flood by gathering resources between rounds to overcome the crisis and reconnect the city with the region once again.

Depending on your investments of money and resources, the number of rebuilt sites in the city, the level of needs met in the community, whether or not you fix the flood, and the amount of damage the city has suffered, you will earn points and, at the same time, by the end of the game, you will get a total score that you will compare with a score board.

ES:

Valdivia es un juego de mesa en el que deberás reconstruir la ciudad homónima situada al sur de Chile luego del mayor terremoto en la historia de la humanidad. Para ello deberás gestionar tus recursos, proveer refugio y alimento a damnificados, reconstruir la ciudad y lograr La Hazaña del Riñihue.

Es un juego en solitario (para 1 persona), sin embargo, puede jugarse en simultáneo para ver quién logra obtener más puntaje. La primera partida puede durar aprox. 40 minutos y luego, ya sabiendo jugar, duran entre 20 y 30 minutos. Es un juego recomendado para personas desde los 12 años hacia arriba.

Es un juego tipo Roll & Write, es decir, usarás dados y lápiz mina. Necesitarás 3 dados de 6 caras y recomendamos tener una goma a mano también.

Por otro lado, es también un juego Print & Play, es decir, que para jugarlo simplemente debes imprimir las hojas de juego y listo.

Si tienes alguna duda, escríbenos a eterna.jdms@gmail.com o a nuestro Instagram @eternagames

Esperamos que lo disfrutes.

Juega la vida :)

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Third World War: Alternative Naval Ruleset
description:
"The Third World War: Alternative Naval Ruleset", published by Compass Games in Paper Wars #106, is an alternative set of naval rules developed by Dominic Warren, Greg Warren, and Eric Farago.

Some rules include adding and adjusting naval zones, a map overlay depicting the new Danish Straits naval zone, allowing the Soviets to send raiders to interdict NATO supply lines (affecting the number of NATO stacking points that are unsupplied), and having the Soviet and Polish naval divisions break down into regiments.
